LOCAL AND PRIVATE ACTS
OF THE
STATE OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE REGULAR SESSION OF THE GENERAL
ASSEMBLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN AND HELD IN
THE CITY OF FRANKFORT ON MONDAY ,
THE TWENTY -EIGHTH DAY OF NO .
VEMBER , EIGHTEEN HUN-
DRED AND EIGHTY-
ONE .
LUKE P. BLACKBURN , Governor .
JAS . E. CANTRILL , Lieut. Gov'r and Speaker of Senate.
W. C. OWENS , Speaker of the House of Representatives.
JAMES BLACKBURN , Secretaryof State.
P. W. HARDIN , AttorneyGeneral.
CHAPTER 740 .
AN ACT to consolidatecertainturnpike companies .
WHEREAS , The stockholders in each of the following
named companies , incorporated in the county court of
Nicholas county, Kentucky , pursuant to chapter 56 of
the General Statutes,viz: The Carlisle,Rogers Mill, and
Mt. SterlingTurnpike Road Company ,and the Carlisle,
Concord ,and East Union Turnpike Company ,have peti-
tioned the Legislature of Kentucky to consolidate said
companies ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That said companies be,and are hereby ,authorized
and empowered to enter into articlesof consolidation on
such terms and conditionsas they may agree upon as to
LOC L.,VOL . II- 1

2 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the corporate name ,the construction of their respective
roads,the collection, management , and appropriation of
theirrespectivefunds,and the number of directors: Pro-
vided, Nothing therein contained shall conflictwith the
Constitution or laws of Kentucky .
§2 . Said articlesof consolidation shall be signed and
acknowledged by the directors of each of said compa-
nies,and set forth their terms of consolidation, and be
recorded in the county clerk's officeof the county in
which the principal part of the proposed pikes are lo-
cated, as required by section 3 of said chapter 56 ; and
said articlesmay be amended ,from time to time ,by said
directors, as may be necessary, so as to carry out the
intent of this act : Provided, Said amendments shall be
acknowledged and recorded as the original articlesare
required to be by thisact.
§ 3. The new management formed after the said con-
solidation is effected may extend the lines of the pike
which said companies now propose to construct; and said
new management shall be entitled to all the benefits of
the law , as itnow exists,authorizing subscriptionsby the
county court to the capital stock of turnpike companies ,
and may receive subscriptionsof stock from private indi-
viduals to make such extensions,and collectthe same ;
may make contracts,sue and be sued,acquireand hold
and transferproperty as a natural person may , and may
establishby -laws , and make all rules and regulations
necessary for the lawful management of itsaffairs; and
said turnpikeroad companies, when consolidated,shall
not be allowed to charge a greater rate of tollthan is
now ,or may hereafterbe, fixedby the general laws of
Kentucky .
§4, This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
W. C. OWENS ,
Speaker of theHouse of Representatives.
JAMES E. CANTRILL ,
Speaker of the Senate.
LUKE P. BLACKBURN .
Approved April 1, 1882.
By the Governor :
JAMES BLACKBURN , Secretary of State.

CHAPTER 742 .
AN ACT toauthorizeW. O. B. Ratliff, R. M. Ferrell,and R. H. Ratliffto
erecta boom acrossShelbycreek, in Pikecounty.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That W. O. B. Ratliff, R. M. Ferrell, and R. H.
Ratliff,be,and they are hereby,vested with authorityto
erectacrossShelby creek,inPike county,Kentucky ,upon

which may be adriftand floatingupon said stream ,and
coming against said boom , or which may be driven by the
owner or owners thereof against said boom .
§2 . The said incorporators erecting and maintaining
said boom are allowed to charge the owner or owners
thereof fifteencents for each saw -log caught and har-
bored by them by said boom , and ten cents for each locust
log caught and harbored ,which charges shallconstitute
a lien upon the timber so caught and harbored ; and if
the owner or owners of such caught timber shall fail
for twenty days to claim said logs, and pay the charges
thereon ; as fixed by this act,the said persons named as
incorporatorsmay proceed to sellsaidtimberinpayment
of the charges thereon . Before a sale is made under this
act,the time ,terms ,and place of sale shallbe advertised
by a constable of Pike county ,by written notice,one at
the court-house in Pike county ,and one near the place
of sale, for the period of ten days before said sale. The
sale shallbe made for cash in hand , and said sale shall
vest the absolutetitleto the propertysold in the pur-
chaser under said sale .

Approved April 1, 1882.

CHAPTER 743 .

of Kentucky :

Story,John Reidling,John Uny ,T. C. Tucker ,and Jacob
Eclinger ,theirsuccessors and assigns, are hereby created
a body-politicand corporate,by the name of the " East
End Ferry Company ," and by that name may contract
and be contracted with, sue and be sued ,plead and be
impleaded,answer and defend,in allcourtsand placesas
a naturalperson; and may have a common seal,and alter
thesame at pleasure; and saidcompany may have all
otherpowers usual and incidentto corporations,and may
purchase and own allsuch property,real and personal,
as may be necessary to enable said corporation to carry
on the business of ferrying freight, passengers,and live
stock,and animals and vehicles,from or near the point
where the north end of Clay street, in the city of Louis-
ville,county of Jefferson,strikesthe Ohio river,across the
said Ohio river, to an accessible point on the Indiana
shore,and generally to do and perform allsuch acts as an
individualmight do in the premises .
§2 . The capitalstock of said company shallbe divided
into shares of one hundred dollarseach ,and may consist
of any number of shares,not to exceed two thousand ,that
may be decided upon by the board of directorsof the
company ; and the board of directorsmay adopt by-laws
and amend and repeal the same at will, and provide how
the stock shallbe voted at corporate meetings , and the
form of voting by proxies,the transferof the same , and
the mode and manner of conducting and transacting
business of the corporation,and forthe meetings of stock-
holders; and shall elect five directorsfrom among the
stockholders,who ,when so elected,shallelectone of their
own number president of the company ,who shallserve
for one year , and until their successors be elected and
qualified; and the presidentand directorsshallhave the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 5
management , government , and controlof the property,
business,and affairsof thecompany . The presidentand
two directorsshallbe a quorum of the board .
ers .
§3 . The directorsof thecorporationare hereby author- Directors' pow-
ized and empowered to establisha schedule of charges
for ferryage,which shall be posted in the officesof the
company ; to appoint a secretaryand such otherofficers
and agents as they may deem necessary,and provide for
their compensation ; and the powers hereby conferred on
the board of directors,or the corporation,may be exer-
cised by the corporators, or a majority of them ,until the
stockholders may elect a board of directors.
§4 . The directorsmay purchase or lease real estate,
steam ferryboats,theirfurniture,tackle,and apparel from
individualsor corporations; may consolidate with corpo-
rations created by or under the laws of another State ;
may acquire by purchase or otherwise the franchisesand
propertyof otherferrycompanies or corporations; may
purchase or construct wharves and docks , and build

goods ,wares,merchandise,and grain,and receivecom-
pensation forthe storage of allarticles; and may issue

its warehouses or elevators; and may issue its coupon
bonds ,not in excessof the amount of capitalstockhere-
by authorized, upon such terms and conditionsas the
directorsmay stipulatein the body of the bonds, and
securethe payment of said bonds by executing one or
more mortgages, thereinconveying the franchisesof the
corporation,and allof itscorporateproperty,or so much
thereof as may be therein named and described,to one
or more trustees,for the common and equal benefitof all
holders of the bonds so issued .

sage .
Approved April 1, 1882 .

CHAPTER 744 .
AN ACT to prevent the sale of spirituous, vinous,or malt liquorswithin
two miles of Crossland,in Calloway county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shallbe unlawful for any person to sell
spirituous, vinous,or malt liquors,or any mixture there-
of,in any quantity,in the village of Crossland, or within
two miles thereof .

sectionof thisact shall,upon conviction thereof,be fined
not lessthan twenty nor more than fiftydollarsfor each
offense,to be recovered in any court having competent
jurisdiction.
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 1, 1882.

lects the State revenue ; and he shall have the same

14 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .

CHAPTER 752 .

of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Harrison county , a ma-
jority of the justices of the peace being present and

county , to any turnpike company which have been , or

afterconstructed,and shallnot be paid over to the officers
of the company ,exceptupon thecompletionof each mile,

are in the hands of the company to insure itscompletion
derivedfrom bonafidesubscriptionsof stock.

and mode of constructionof the proposed road,and un-
lessitappear from such statementthattheproposed road
is to be a well constructed and durable turnpike road ,

scribe stock therein. Before the amount of any such
subscriptionof stock by said county to any turnpike
company is actuallypaid to such company ,said county
court shall cause the road to be fully and carefullyex-
amined , and said subscriptionsshall not be paid over
unlessthe road,for the lengthof which subscriptionis
made , has been completed in a workmanlike manner ,

herein .

in which such is subscribed shall issue a certificate of

so subscribed and paid in by her, and said county , acting

and management of any such road as any other stock-
holder therein .

nearlyjust to every portionof the county as possible,
not giving preference to one part of the county to any
other part through which a road isbeing built,or one is
proposed to be built.
§5 . All proceedingstouchingsubscriptionof stockby
said county shall be kept of record in the county court
clerk'soffice, and the county court clerk shall keep a

16 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
book , in which shall be recorded the date and amount
of each subscriptionof stockby the county of Harrison,
what company ,and when the subscriptionof stockwas
paid .
§ 6. All dividendsreceivedby the county of Harrison
on the stockheld by itin any turnpikecompany shall
be paid intothecounty treasuryas othercounty revenue.
§7. If said subscriptionof stockare made as author-
izedby thisact,the said county court,a majorityof the

have power , and itshallbe theirduty,to levy a tax upon

revenue purposes, sufficientfor the purpose of paying

the county revenue is now collected.
§ 8. If thecounty courtshalldeem itbest,it may ,in-
stead of levying a tax to pay said subscriptions,as herein

nominations of $ 100 and $ 500 , payable to bearer, due

four years,bearing 5 per cent.interestfrom date,payable
semi-annuallyon January 1stand July 1stof each year,

whom bought , and at what price,and when .

sufficientto pay the annual intereston said bonds , and
to create a sinking fund for the redemption of the same ,

with the similar provisions as stated in section eight of

uary 26 , 1882 .

preceding his electionor appointment ,or who isless than

sagacious,or who cannot write the English language in-
telligibly, or who has been convicted of a felony,or who

ing any of the policeforcefor cause, for causes,or for

said charter, approved March 21st, 1871: And provided
further, Thatupon dismissalforcauseor causestherein
by the Mayor ,the party so dismissedshallbe ineligible
as a member of said force for one year thereafter.
§ 4. Any policeman or policeofficer,aidingor assisting
any candidatefor officeby advice or counsel or other-
wise ,or counseling or advising against such candidate ,
or advocating or discussingthe merits or demerits of
such candidates,or solicitingany person to vote for or
againstsuch candidate,shallbe guiltyof interferingin
elections within the meaning of this act .
§5 . The petitionof each applicantto become a mem-
ber of thepoliceforceshallbe signed by the petitioner
himself,accompanied by the followingoath,wholly writ-
ten and subscribed and sworn to by the applicant : " Ido
solemnlyswear thatI am a citizenof the United States
and of the State of Kentucky ; that I have resided,for
threeyears next preceding my applicationto become a
member of the policeforce,inthe cityof Louisville; that

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 19
I am not under twenty -four years of age ; that I can read
and writethe Englishlanguage intelligibly; thatI have
never been convicted of a felony. I further swear that
I will not at any time ,while a member of the police force,
interferein any election furtherthan to vote. I further
swear thatI willat alltimes do and perform my duty as
a member of the police force of said city, so long as I
remain a member thereof,to the best of my ability. I
further swear that my habits are moral and sober, and
that this obligationhas been wholly written by me , with-
out aid or assistance- so help me God ." Which petition
and oath shallbe filedwith the Mayor , and the names of
allsuch petitionersshallbe published in at leastone issue
of some newspaper published in said city, including the
papers published on Sunday , before the commissioners
shall act on the same , and it shall not be necessary to
referthe same or have the same read before the general
council .
§6 . The Board of Police Commissioners shall, from
time to time , subject to the provisions and regulations
herein contained , elect policemen to fillsuch vacancies
as may occur, but such electionsshallbe only for the
unexpired time from the date of such electionto the next
quadrennial electionof said police force.
§ 7. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved April 1, 1882 .

CHAPTER 755 .
AN ACT in regard to the condemnation of privateproperty for publicuse
in Hardin county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . Whenever Hardin county ,in this Commonwealth ,
shall,by a majority of itsjusticesof the peace , deem it
advisable for the public good to drain water from any
county or public road in said county , or from lands adja-
cent to said road , or to turn water from its natural course
upon the lands of individuals,or to cutditchesupon said
lands, it may ,by order of record, direct the county at-
torneyto instituteproper proceedingsto carryout said
object, whereupon it shall be the duty of said county
attorney to filea petitionin the county court of said
county,in which said county shallbe the plaintiff,and

24 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the partiespresumably to be affectedby the proposed
measures shall be the defendants , in which petition he
shall,in general terms ,set out what issought to be done
by thecounty.
§2 . When said petitionis filedas aforesaid,itshall be
the duty of the clerk of said court to issue summons or
warning order against the defendants named in said peti-
tion,subject to the provisions of the Civil Code in regard
to the same , except that the defendant ,when a warning
order is issued,shallbe warned to appear at the firstday
of a regular term of said court ,which does not commence
within thirtydays afterthe making of the order,and said
defendants against whom warning order is issued , as
aforesaid, shall be deemed to have been constructively
summoned on the tenth day after said order was made ,
and an attorneyappointed by said clerkto defend pur-
suant to the Civil Code of Practice ; but if said defend-
ants, or any of them , are absent from the State, and
from any cause a warning order cannot be obtained ,
then the provisions of section 56 of the Civil Code of
Practice shall apply , except that the word " thirty" shall
be substitutedfor the word " sixty" in said section,and
said partiesshallbe deemed to have been constructively
served whenever the requirements of said section shall
have been complied with; but personal service shall be
had in all cases when it can be obtained .
§3 . When said defendants shall have been summoned
as aforesaid,said court may appoint at least three suita-
ble persons, one of whom shall be a surveyor, and allof
whom shallbe over 21 years of age ,to act as viewers ,
whose duty itshallbe to examine intoand reportwhat
improvement is the most practicable; but before they
proceed to act,they shallbe sworn to faithfullyand im-
partiallyexecute the duties assigned them . The viewers
may examine other improvements than the one proposed
by the county court, and may report in favor of those
which they prefer,with theirreasons for theirpreference ,
and will show what effectthe proposed improvements
will have upon the property of any person affectedthere

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 25
by ,and willreportthe conveniencesand inconveniences
which will result as well to individualsas to the public
from the proposed work .
§4 . Should the report of the viewers show that other
partiesthan thosenamed in the petitionare interestedin
the proposed improvements ,and have lands which will be
affectedthereby,itshall be the duty of the court to have
said partiesbrought before the court, pursuant to the
previous sectionshereof.
§5 . When allthe parties shown to be interested are
duly beforethe court,ifthe court have enough beforeit
to enable it to fix upon a just compensation to the pro-
prietors and tenants whose lands are to be affected, and
they are willing to accept what the court deems just, and
by written answer consent to the same ,itmay determine
the matter without a writ of ad quod damnum .
§6 . If a writ of ad quod damnum be desired, then the
provisions of sections 8, 9, and 10 of chapter 94 of the
General Statutesare hereby made applicabletothesame ,
except as modified herein.
§ 7. It shall be the duty of said jury, after being duly
sworn by the officerto view the lands of the proprietors
and tenants designatedin the reportof the viewers,and
ascertain what will be a just compensation to each for
the amount of damages that he will sustain by the pro-
posed improvements ,and of what said damages will con--
sist,and the benefitswhich may accrue to said defendants
from said acts .
§ 8. The court shallupon the report, inquest, excер-
tions of the defendants , and the evidence , determine
whether the improvements shallbe made pursuant to the
report of the viewers or otherwise,and fixthe amount of
damages done each of said defendants , which amount
shall be paid or tendered said defendants before their
lands shallbe so used , unless said defendants consent , in
writing, filedin court, that they may proceed with said
improvements and work before payment of the money .
§ 9. All of the provisions of chapter 94 of the General
Statutesapplicableto the casesherein providedfor,and

26 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
not inconsistentwith the provisions hereof, are made a
part of thisact,and shallso govern such proceedings .
§ 10. In case any of the defendants are infants, the
provisions of the Civil Code of Practice in regard to
infantsshallgovern these proceedings .
§ 11. In case any of the non -resident defendants fail
to answer in person ,pursuant to the fifthsection hereof,
then the court may order the improvements to be made ,
but provision shall be made for the payment of the
amount due such defendant when demanded .
§ 12. Itshallbe the duty of the county attorney,when-
ever the factsjustifyit,to make the requisiteaffidavitto
obtain a warning order.
§ 13. Appeals under thisact shallbe regulated by sec-
tion 43 , chapter 94 , of the General Statutes, from the
decision of a county court condemning land , or the right
to ditch the same ,or to exercisewater privileges,or refus-
ing the same ,to be taken by the party aggrieved pursuant
to said section .
§ 14. Nothing in this act shall be construed to affect
any litigationnow pending .
§ 15. This act to be in force from and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 1, 1882 .
CHAPTER 757 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the Hustonville and Bradfordsville Turn-
pike Road Company .
WHEREAS ,It is represented to this General Assembly
that many persons are in the habit of hauling heavily
loaded wagons over several miles of the Hustonville and
BradfordsvilleTurnpike Road ,and then evading the pay-
ment of tollby leaving the pike ,either by a road con .
necting with said pike,near the house of Wm . M. Dodd ,
or by a road near the Narrows ; for remedy whereof ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshall be lawful for said company to exact
and collect tolls, at the intersection of either of said

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 27
roads , from all persons driving wagons over said turn-
pike, and turning offfrom or enteringon said pike at
either of said points.
§ 2. This act shallbe in force from and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 1, 1882.

Approved April 1, 1882 .
CHAPTER 759 .
AN ACT for the benefitof the county roads in Henry county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That forthe purpose of this act Henry county is
divided intoseven districts, viz : the magisterial districts
as now located .
§ 2. It shall be the duty of the officersof the election
in August , 1882,to open a poll at each voting place in
Henry county , and to ask each voter whether he votes
for or against working the county roads in the districtin

28 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
which he votes,by taxation,and to record the votes, and
if a majority of those voting upon this question in any
districtvote in favor of working the roads by taxation,
then the provisions of this act shall be in force in said
district; otherwisethe presentlaw in relationto public
roadsshallbeinforce,and in any districthot votingthe
tax,the question shallagain be submittedto the voters
thereof, as herein provided ,at the next general election.
§3 . In every districtvoting to work the road by taxa-
tion,the county judge of Henry county shall, at the first
term of the county court after the tax has been voted ,
appoint a superintendent of county roads in said district,
who shallhold his officeduring the pleasure of the judge ,
and whose duty it shall be to oversee all county roads
within his district,keep them in good repair,remove all
obstructionswhich may be placed upon them ,and open
or change any road that the county court may direct, as
providedin chapter94,article1,of General Statutes.
§ 4. The superintendentshallhire as many hands as
may be necessary to work ,in a proper way ,the county
roads in his district, and oversee them while at work ;
shall pay the smallest wages possible to secure good
hands , and require them to work nine hours every day ;
may hire the necessary teams , wagons , and plows , and
purchase all tools and implements necessary to aid in
working the road , which , when purchased , shall belong
to the district,providedthatthe amount expended in any
districtin one year under the provisionof thisact shall
not exceed the amount raised in that districtby taxation
for thispurpose .
§5 . The superintendentshallannually make to the
county judge a fullreport, showing the amount of money
received by him ,the roads he has worked ,the number of
hands employed,and wages per day paid,the names of
those who worked out part or allof theirtaxes,the char-
acterof toolsand implements purchased,and pricespaid
therefor, the amount expended for hire of teams , & c.,
the number of days he was actually employed , and all
other expenses actually incurred by him in the discharge
1

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 29
of hisduty,and he shallbe paid a reasonablecompensa-
tion forhis labor,to be fixedby the county judge .
§6 . The levy court shall, when they fix the county
levy,assessa tax of ($2) two dollarson each male per-
son over the age of twenty -one , and a tax of five cents
on each ($ 100) one hundred dollars' worth of taxable
property in every districtvoting the tax.
§7. This tax shallbe collectedby the sheriffat the
same time and manner as the county levy,and he shall
pay the tax collectedunder this act, as soon as collected,
to the superintendent of the districtin which itwas col-
lected, and shall be liable on his officialbond for all
failuresto collector pay over, as herein provided, the
same as in actions against him concerning the collection
and payment of the county levy, and shall receive the
same pay as forcollectingStaterevenue.
§8 . Each superintendent, before entering upon the
discharge of his duty ,shall execute, with good security,
to the county judge,a bond conditionedfor the faithful
performance of allthe dutiesimposed upon him by this
act . Said bond shall be of the same character as the
bonds executed by the sheriff, and shallbe recorded by
the county clerk in his office, and he and his sureties
shallbe liableon said bond to any persons aggrieved by
a breach of its conditions .
§9 . The superintendent shall, in working roads,give
the preferencefirstto turnpikesthat have been turned
over to the county , then to other leading roads in the
order that they are the most traveled and necessary for
the convenience of the public.
§ 10.It shallbe the duty of the superintendent of each
districtto give the refusal of employment upon any of
the roads he works to the persons residentin the voting
precinctwhere the work is being done , and who have
been assessed for taxation,by virtue of the provisions of
this act,provided such persons can be employed at the
same price as other hands of equal efficiency; and the
certificateof the superintendent given to such persons
shallbe taken by the sheriffin payment of or as a credit

30 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
upon the road tax due by such persons. The certificate
shallshow the number of days he has labored, and price
paid per day . He shall also hire teams , & c., to aid in
working roads,from tax-payers under thisact,provided
he can get them as cheap as from other persons , and
shallgive them a certificatein payment of such hire,
which certificateshallbe taken by the sheriffin payment
of or as a creditupon the road tax due by such persons.
The certificateshallshow what was hired,and price paid
therefor .
1
§ 11. The superintendent shall not buy or speculate
directlyor indirectlyin claims due persons for services
or articlesfurnished under the act ,and he shall take from
every person to whom he pays money a receipt showing
the actual amount paid ,and saidreceipt shallaccompany
his report to the court .
12. The county levy court,before levying the county
levy in any year ,shallfirstascertain the amount neces-
sary to be raised for county purposes ,exclusive of road
claims, and shallonly levy on persons living in districts
that have voted the road tax a sufficienttax to pay their
pro rata part of the necessary expenses of the county ,
exclusive of road claims .
§ 13. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 1, 1882.

CHAPTER 761 .
AN ACT to incorporate the Owensboro and Grissom's Landing Gravel
Road Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1. That a company be,and the same is hereby,in-
corporated to construct a road ,on the macadamized or
gravel plan , from Owensboro , Daviess county , to Gris-
som's Landing ,in said county , upon the road known as
the Buzzard Roost road,whenever practicable,between
said points above named , under the name and styleof
the Owensboro and Grissom's Landing Gravel Road
Company ; and by that name and style may sue and be
sued ,contract and be contracted with,plead and be im-
pleaded , and use and have a common seal, and alter or
amend the same at pleasure.
§2 . The capitalstock of said company shallbe twenty-
five thousand dollars, to be increased or diminished at
the pleasure of the company , and to be divided into
shares of fiftydollarseach .
§ 3. That A. C. Tompkins,M. P. Mattingly, James
Herr , W. H. Perkins,T. J. Monarch , Alex . Hill, and L.
P. Little are hereby appointed commissioners , whose
duty it shall be to open books for the subscription of
stock at such places as said commissioners may desig-
nate ; and as soon as five thousand dollarsis subscribed,
they shallgive ten days ' notice, at the most public places
in the vicinityof the line of road, of a meeting of the
stockholders,for the purpose of electing a president and
fivedirectorsfor said company ,a majority of whom shall
be competent to do business, and to do allthings author-
ized by thisact to be done by the president and directors
of said company ; and the management of the fiscaland
prudential affairsof said company shall be confided to

point,and who shallcontinuein officefor one year,and
untiltheirsuccessorsare elected and qualified.

34 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 4. That no person shall be eligibleas president or
director of said company who is not the owner , in his
own name ,of one or more shares of stock in said road .
§ 5. The president and directors shallhave the power
of appointing a treasurer, gate-keeper , and all other
officersor agents deemed necessary to effectthe purposes
of thisact, and to remove the same at pleasure ; they
shall have power to require of the treasurer, and all
other officersor agents appointed by them , bond and
securityin such penaltiesas they may require, condi-
tioned for the faithfulperformance of the duties incum-
bent on them as such .
§ 6. The commissioners herein appointed shallprocure

book , viz : We , whose names are hereto subscribed, do
promise to pay the president and directorsof the Owens-
boro and Grissom'sLanding Gravel Road Company the
sum of fiftydollars for each and every share of stock in
said company set opposite our names , in such manner
and proportion,and at such times as shallbe by them
required under the law incorporating said company , to
be collected as other debts . Witness our hand this
day of— , 188– .
§7 . That said road shallnot be lessthan thirty(30)
feet wide ; the width of the road -bed shallbe sixteen (16)
feetwide, and the metal not lessthan sixteen(16) feet
wide , and shall be graded to an elevation not exceeding
four(4°) degreesin any part thereof; thatwhenever two
and a half (24) continuousmiles of said road are com-
pleted, the company may erect a toll-gate, and charge
tolls thereat at a rate of toll not exceeding that author-
izedby chapter110,General Statutesof Kentucky : Pro-
vided, They may erect such gate within one and a half
miles of the limits of Owensboro .
§ 8. That the president and directorsof said road shall
have the rightand power to acquire the right of way for

construction, by voluntary concession and release, and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 35
by privatecontractwith the land-holders: Provided,how-
ever, If the right of way , and if material cannot be had
as above indicated, it shall be obtained in the manner
and mode prescribedby the laws now in forceregulating
the application for and establishment of public roads ;
and the county court of Daviess county may , by order
entered of record,permit the said corporation to locate
theirsaid road on and occupy for that purpose any part
of the publicroad lyingbetween the cityof Owensboro
and Grissom's Landing ,in which case the said corpora-
tion shall be liable for any failureto keep that part of
said road so used by it open and in good condition, in

now liablefor such failure: Provided,That nothing here-
in shall give said road the right to run over the line of
the Owensboro and Buzzard Roost Gravel Road Com-
pany , approved April 14th, 1880.
§9 . When the stockholdershallhave made fullpay-
ment of his stock,it shallbe the duty of the president,
over his signature , to issue certificates of stock to all
persons entitledto the same , attestedby the secretary of
said company .
§ 10. The county court, when a majority thereof deem
itnecessary,may ,by orders made at any regular term of
the majoritycourt,take possessionand maintain posses-
sion of said road for the benefitof the people , provided
they shallat the same time provide for the reimbursement
of the company for the cost of constructing said road ,
and the improvements thereon, and to that end the said
company shallkeep a faithfuland full account of said
expenses .
§ 11. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .

AN ACT forthe benefitof Jos.M. Kendall, of Morgan county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

36 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
is a person of honesty,probity, and good demeanor ,to

cuit judges , they may examine said Kendall , touching

sible for all his contracts, and the performance of his

CHAPTER 763 .
AN ACT for the benefit of G. W. Noble , of Breathitt county .

ury not otherwiseappropriated.

CHAPTER 764 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Campton Institute, in Wolfe county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That S. S. Combs , G. T. Center, Z. T. Hurst, W.
F. Elkin , and W. W. Steel, and their successors,be , and
they are hereby ,created a body -politicand corporate,by
the name and styleof the Campton Institute,and by that

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 37
name shallhave perpetual succession; may contract and
be contracted with, sue and be sued ,plead and be im
pleaded ,in all courts and places ; have a common seal,
and alterand break the same at pleasure ; may make all
necessaryby-laws forthe transactionof the businessand
government of said company ,not inconsistentwith the
laws of the State or United States .
§2 . The capital stock of said company or institute
shall be divided into shares of twenty dollars, subscribed
and paid for in such manner as said board of directors
may , by itsby -laws ,prescribe.
3. The stockholders of said instituteshall annually
elect a president, secretary,treasurer, and fivedirectors,
who shallhold theirofficefor one year ,or untiltheirsuc-
cessors are duly elected. Said elections shall be held
annually in April of each year, and the officersand
directors so elected shall,before entering upon the dis-
charge of the duties of theiroffice,take upon themselves
an oath before a magistrate that they will,to the best of
their ability,discharge the duties imposed upon them .
§ 4. Said electionshall be held at the school-building.
All voting shall be by ballot,and the individual receiv-
ing the highest number of votes cast shall be declared
elected to the office for which he was voted for . The
secretary shall superintend and hold each election, and
make known the result .
§5 . Any vacancy that may occur in any of the offices
by death ,resignation,removal , or refusal to act, shallbe
filledby the remaining directorsfor the unexpired time .
§6 . The board of directors,when so elected,shallhave
power ,and itishereby made theirduty,to open a book
for the subscriptionof stock to said institute. Said shares
of stock shall be divided into twenty dollars each , and
when one hundred (100) shares are so subscribed, said
book shallbe considered closed,and they shallreceive no
furthersubscription; which said sums ,so subscribed and
paid, shallbe used by said directorsforthe benefitof said
institutein such a manner as a majority of the directors
so elected as aforesaidmay think best.

38 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§7 . The board of directors may receive donations of
money or property for the purpose of assisting in the
erection of school buildings,for the purchase of libraries,
chemical apparatus ,for the employment of teachers,or
forother purposes connected with said institute.
§8. They may purchase lands,not exceeding ten (10)
acres,joining or near the town of Campton ,and lay out
grounds to commence and carry on a school for the ad-
vancement of education .
§ 9. That said officers herein declared to be elective
shallbe electedby the stockholders of said company , and
none others shall be legal voters ; and each stockholder
shall be entitled to one vote for each share of stock .
§ 10. That said board of directorsshallhave fullpower
to hold and controlforthe purposes aforesaidany lands as
set forthin sectioneight of thisact
§ 11. Said directors shall have the full control of and
management of the school and all property belonging
thereto .
§ 12. Itshallbe the duty of the president to preside at
all meetings of the directory, and perform such other
dutiesas may be assignedby the by-laws of said com-
pany . It shallbe the duty of the secretaryto keep a
faithfulrecord of all proceedings had , and perform such
other duties as may be required of him by the by -laws .
It shallbe the duty of the treasurerto collectand receive
allmoney connected with said institute, and pay same out
by order of the directory.
§13 . The said company , when fullyorganized, shall
cause a book to be opened ,subject to the inspection of
any member of the board of stockholders, which shall
contain the names of all stockholders of said company ,
and the number of shares owned by each ; and the said
sharesmay be transferredon saidbooks in the manner to
be prescribedby the by -laws of said company .
§14 . This act shall take effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved April 1, 1882 .

CHAPTER 765 .
AN ACT to amend and revisethe act incorporatingthe Falmouth Cemetery
Company ,approved February 5th, 1866 .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That from the time this act shall take effectall
persons owning lots in the Falmouth or Beech Grove
Cemetery (as itis commonly called),Pendleton county,
and their successors,be ,and they are hereby ,constituted
a body -corporate,under the name of the Falmouth River-
side Cemetery Company ,by which they shallhave power
to sue and be sued ,have and use a corporate seal,and to
do allother acts necessary for effectingthe object of their
incorporation,which is to provide a place of repose for
the dead .
§2 . This corporation shall be managed by a board of
directors of six persons ,to be electedby ballot; and any
one owning a lotin said cemetery shallbe eligibleas a
director , and entitled to one vote ,but no member to more
than one . The first election shall be held at a time and
place to be designatedby C. H. Lee, W. C. Kennett,and
D. E. Redman , who shallbe commissioners to reorganize
the company under this act. One , two , or allof said
commissioners may act, and who shall designate from
the said personswho may be chosen at the firstelection
two of said six to serve one year ,two to serve two years,
and two to serve three years, so that at each election
thereafter, which shall be annual ,two of said directors
shall retire,and two be elected to filltheir places. In
case of death or resignation before the annual election,
the remaining members may appoint some one to fillthe
unexpired term . A quorum of the board shall be three
members . The first annual election shall be on the
fourth Saturday of February next, and on the fourth
Saturday of February of each year thereafter; and if
from any cause there should be no electionheld at the
stated times therefor, the directors remaining in office
shallfillthe placesof the two retiringdirectorsby ap-
pointment .

40 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§3 . The said board shall elect from their number a
presidentand a secretary,and any lot-owner they may
choose as treasurer. But they shallrequire of the treas-
urer,before he shallenter upon the duties of his office, a
bond stipulatinga faithfulperformance of his officialob-
ligations, and furtherthat he will,by each annual meet-
ing , while he is in office, render to the board a tabular
statement showing his receiptsand disbursements for the
current year , which statement the board shall require;
and for their failure to do so, or for failure to take from
the treasurera good and sufficientbond ,or for any failure
or neglect of duty on the part of the directors,by which
any loss may be sustained by the company , or by any
lot-owner ,they shall be personally liable therefor. The
directorsshallreceive no remuneration for theirservices,
except the secretary , whose remuneration for work actu-
ally done by him shall be fixed by the board , but this
shall not prevent the secretary from receiving any fees
that may be allowed him under the by -laws from the lot-
owners . The board may in theirdiscretioncompensate
the treasurer for his services,but not to exceed twenty-
fivedollarsa year. They shallhave power to compel
the treasurerto renew hisbond ,or they may remove him
at pleasure .
§ 4. This corporation is vested with the titleof all the
lands heretoforepurchased by the trusteesof the Fal-
mouth Cemetery Company under the act incorporating
the same ,approved February 5, 1866,to which thisact is
amendatory ,and repeals any part or parts of said original
act that may be in conflictwith this act; and the board
of directorsare authorized to purchase additionalgrounds ,
not exceedingfiftyacres,forthepurpose hereinspecified,
and lay the same offintolotsof convenientsize,and lay
off suitable avenues , subdividing the same ,allof which
cemetery grounds shall be included in one common
boundary , and shallbe known by the name " Riverside "
Cemetery ; and all money arising from sale of lots or
otherwise shall be devoted exclusivelyto improving the
said grounds .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 41
§5 . The board of directorsshallhave power to im-
prove said grounds , as they shall think best, from all
moneys coming to theirhands , and to controlthe charac-
ter of improvements of persons owning lotstherein,and
to pass such needful rules and regulations as they may
think best to control the same . They shall have power
to erect a suitable building for a superintendent, to ap-
point a superintendent,and otherofficersifitbe deemed
by them necessary. They shallhave power to make by-
laws for the government of the corporation at any regular
meeting of the board ,and they shallkeep a record of all
their proceedings .
§6. When a lotis purchased,the board shallcause a
certificateto be given the purchaser ,showing the number
of the lot,and thetitlethereofmay be transferredin such
manner as may be prescribed in the by laws of the cor-
poration. It not transferred,it shall descend or may be
devised as real estate ; but no certificateshall be issued
to anyny lotowner untilthe purchase priceof saidlothas
been fullypaid .
§7 . The board of directorsshallappoint some suitable
place within the town of Falmouth where theirmeetings
shall be held on the firstTuesdays of March ,June , Sep-
tember , and December of each year . But they may ap-
point and hold other meetings ,should business require it.
They shall call before them the present officersof the
Falmouth Cemetery Company , the " trustees" and other
officers,and make with them a fulland complete settle-
ment of the affairsof the company , which settlement,
together with the reports of the several officers,the said
board of directors will cause to be entered on the records
of thiscorporation ; and the said trustees and other offi-
cers appointed by them willturn over to the board of
directorsallthe books and papers, together with any
money they may have belonging to said Falmouth Ceme-
tery Company ; and the said board of directorsare au-
thorizedto collectby suitor otherwise any money or
claims that may be due said Falmouth Cemetery Com

42 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
pany, whether in the hands of the officersof the Com-
pany or of collectingofficers,or that may be due from
lot -owners .
Approved April 1, 1882.

mine , through the counties of Caldwell , Trigg , Lyon , and

CHAPTER 769 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the town of Earlington .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the trustees of the town of Earlington , in
the county of Hopkins, may at any time institutean

44 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
actionin the Hopkins circuitcourt, for the purpose of
closingup and vacating any of itsstreetsand alleys,
dividing any of its squares or lots thereof, and to any
such action all the owners of ground or lots abutting on
the streetsor alleys sought to be closed and vacated or
discontinued , shall be defendants . And if all such de-
fendants are competent to act for themselves, and shall
consent to the closing up , vacating, and discontinuing
prayed for, then the court shallrender a judgment ac-
cordingly; but without such consent , such court shall
hear the proof made by the parties,and if satisfiedthat
the closing up , vacating , and discontinuing would be
beneficialto said town , and not injurious to any party
not consenting, shall render a judgment closing,vacat-
ing, and discontinuing such streetsand alleys.

Approved April 1 , 1882.
CHAPTER 770 .

of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the capitalstockof theKentucky River Mills
may , by a majority vote of two thirds of the stock, be
increased from time to time to any sum not exceeding
$500,000,and for that purpose the corporationmay sell
shares to the amount of the increase ; but no share shall
be sold to any person other than the stockholders at the
time of the increase until each stockholder shall have had
the privilegeof purchasing an amount in proportionto
the stock then held by such stockholder.
§ 2. That saidcorporationshall,through itssecretary,
on or before the firstday of October of each year ,by a
statement in writing, sworn to by said secretary, report
to the Auditor of Public Accounts the number of shares
of stock in said corporation issued to share-holders there-
in ; and on filingsaid statement with the Auditor obtain
his permit to pay into the State Treasury the sum of fifty

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 45
centson each shareso reported; and on the payment of
said sum into the Treasury ,the same shall be in lieu of
alltaxes against said corporation and the stock therein.
And alllaws or parts of laws , so far as they conflictwith
thisprovision, are hereby repealed.
§ 3. At their January and July meetings of each year ,
the directorsmay declare such dividends out of the earn-
ings as they may deem right.
§ 4. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved April 1, 1882.

CHAPTER 773 .
AN ACT for the benefitof the Louisvilleand TaylorsvilleTurnpike Road
Company .
WHEREAS ,It is made to appear that the toll-gate now
on the Vandyke Mill Branch Road of the Louisvilleand
TaylorsvilleTurnpike Road is so locatedthatallof the
tollproperty collectableon said branch road cannot be
collected at the toll-gate now on said road , and without
additional facilitiessaid toll cannot be collected ; there-
fore ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . Said Louisvilleand TaylorsvilleTurnpike Road
Company be authorized and empowered to erect at and
across said branch road , at a point thirty-five feet on
the opposite side of the railroad from the toll-gate now
on saidbranch turnpikeroad,an additionaltoll-gate pole,
to be under the management and control of the keeper
of the toll-gate on said road ; and said company shall

48 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of toll on said branch road shall be in force and shall

CHAPTER 774 .
AN ACT to prohibit the selling,giving,or loaning spirituous, vinous,and
malt liquors at Forks of Straight Creek , in Carter county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be unlawful for any one to sell,give,
or loan any spirituous,vinous ,or malt liquors,or mixture
thereof,at the Forks of Straight Creek ,in Carter county ,
Kentucky ,or within a radius of two miles thereof. Any
person so offending shall be deemed guiltyof keeping a
tippling-house,and shallbe subjectto the pains and pen-
altiesimposed therefor: Provided,however, That any per-
son may give to a neighbor ,when visiting his or her
house , or a physician administer when needed , a drink
of eitherof said liquors without incurring the penalties
above imposed .
§2 . After the approval of this act no licenseshallbe
granted to any person to sell any spirituous,vinous ,or
malt liquors within the boundaries specifiedin the first
section of this act .
§ 3. This act shall be in force from and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 1, 1882.

Lexingtonand Big Sandy Railroads,inCartercounty.

of Kentucky :

pains and penalties imposed therefor: Provided,however,
Any person may give to a neighbor when visitinghis
or her house , or a physician administer when needed ,

penalties above imposed .
§2 . After the approval of this act no license shall be
granted to any person to sellany spirituous, vinous , or

section of this act .
§3 . This act shallbe in force from and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 1, 1882 .

AN ACT to amend an act forthe benefitof the tax-payers of Lyon county,
approved February 6 , 1878,and to authorizesaid county to compromise
itsdebts,issuebonds ,and levy and collecttaxes topay the same .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the " Financial Board of Lyon County,"
elected or appointed pursuant to the act to which this is
an amendment ,may ,by the decisionof a majorityof its
members , and for the purpose of more effectuallyand
speedilyaccomplishingthe purposes of said act, direct
that the bonds of said county be issued by the county
court of said county ; said county court shall forthwith,
after such action is taken by said board , make an order
directingthat the bonds of said county shallbe issued for
an amount sufficientto carry out the purposes of thisact,
and no more .
§2 . Said bonds shallbe made payable at such time or
times as said county court may direct, not more than
LOC . L., VOL . II- 4

50 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
thirty(30) years from theirdate,shallbear such rateof
interestas said county court may direct, not exceeding
six(6) per cent. per annum ,payable semi-annually,and
having coupons attached to represent such interest. The
said bonds shallbe signed by the presiding judge of the
Lyon county court, and countersignedby the clerk of
said court , and shall have the seal of said court affixed
thereto; and the said coupons shallbe signed by the said
clerk alone ; said bonds and coupons shallbe payable at
theofficeof the "Treasurerof Lyon County," appointed
pursuant to the act to which thisisan amendment ,which
officeshallbe kept in the town of Eddyville,the county
seat of said county .
§3. Said bonds may be exchanged for the bonds of
Lyon county , executed and made payable to the Eliza-
bethtown and Paducah RailroadCompany ,the coupons
attached to and representing the interestthereon ,and in
payment of any other indebtednessof Lyon county ex-
istingat the time of the issuing of said new bonds ; said
exchange shallbe made under such rules and regulations
as may be prescribed by said county court. In making
such exchange the said bonds issued under this act shall
be estimatedat theirpar value. Any bonds,coupons,or
other evidences of indebtedness received in exchange for
thebonds issuedpursuanttothisactshallbe immediately
canceled , and shall be burned in the presence of said
county judge and county clerk.
§4 . The bonds issuedpursuantto thisact may be sold
for cash at not lessthan par ,and the money received for
same shallbe paid to the treasurerof Lyon county ap-
pointed under the act to which this is an amendment ;
and he and the suretiesupon his bond as such treasurer
shall be responsible for said money to the same extent
and in the same manner as forthe money received by said
treasurer arising from taxation .
§5 . The money realizedfrom the sale of bonds issued
pursuant to thisact,and the money thathas been or may
be realized from taxes levied and collected under the act
to which thisisan amendment ,and the money thatmay

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 51
be realizedfrom taxes that may be levied under this act,
shallbe treated as one fund , and shallbe used or applied
to no other purposes than in the purchase of the prin-
cipalof the bonds executedand given by Lyon county to
the Elizabethtown and Paducah Railroad Company as
providedin the acttowhich thisisan amendment ; inthe
payment of the principaland interestof the bonds issued
under thisact, as herein provided ; in the payment of the
costs and expenses incident to the issuing,sale, and ex-
change of said bonds ; and in the payment of the costs
and expenses incident to the levies and collection of
taxes under this act and under the act to which this is an
amendment . Said moneys mentioned in thissection are
set apart as a sinking fund ,to be used alone for the pur-
posessesnamed above,and shallnot be appropriatedto or
used for any other purpose by any character of order or
proceedings whatever ; and said moneys shall not be
attached ,or the persons holding the same garnisheed at
the instance of any creditor of said county of Lyon ,
other than the holders and owners of bonds and coupons
issued under this act .
§6 . The said treasurer shall, with the money realized
under thisact,or the act to which this is an amendment ,
pay off the coupons upon the bonds issued under thisact
as the said coupons mature , out of any of said money
then on hand , and not otherwise appropriated, for the
purposes named in last section. But if at any time the
amount of money in the hands of said treasurer,or levied
and in process of collectionunder the act to which thisis
an amendment ,should be insufficientto pay off the cou-
pons upon the bonds issued under this act at that time
due and owing ,or to pay off said bonds issued under this
act at their maturity,it shall be the duty of the county
court of Lyon county to levy a tax on allthe real estate
and personal property in Lyon county subject to taxa-
tion under the revenue laws of thisState,including the
amounts owned by residentsof Lyon county which ought
tobe given in under the equalizationlaws,which levy
shallbe sufficientto pay allthe coupons upon saidbonds

52 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
issued under this act that are then due , or that will ma-
turewithinsixmonths thereafter,or topay thesaidbonds
issuedunder thisact aftertheirmaturity,togetherwith
the costs and expenses of the collectionand disbursement
of same . The leviesprovided for in thissection shallbe
made if the contingencies named should occur, although
the " Financial Board " may not have given theirconsent
thereto .
§7 . The levieslaid under the provisions of lastsection
shallbe collected and paid over under the provisions of
the act to which this is an amendment , and the duties of
the sheriffor collectorwho may collectsame ,and of the
treasurer,and the rightsand privilegesof the tax-payer
and all other persons with reference to said levies and
the collectionthereof,shall be governed by the act to
which this is an amendment and the laws therein referred
to,except as modified herein. The liabilityof the sheriff
or collectorwho may collectsaid tax, and of the sureties
upon his bond for the collectionthereof, and the liability
of the treasurer and the suretiesupon his bond ,shallbe
as prescribedin the act to which thisis an amendment .
§ 8. The sheriffof Lyon county , after he receives no-
ticethat a levy has been laid under this act or the act to
which thisis an amendment ,shall have thirty(30 ) days
within which he may execute bond for the collection
thereof; and if he should failto give bond within that
time,with good and sufficientsecurity,he shallnot by
reason of such failure forfeithis office of sheriff, but shall
forfeithisrightto collectsaid tax,in which case itshall
be the duty of the Lyon county court to appoint a col-
lectorof said tax, as provided in the act to which thisis
an amendment .
§ 9. The said county court may , with the consent of
said " Financial Board ," cause the bonds ,or any of them
issued under this act,to be redeemed at any time with
any money in the hands of the treasurer,at not more
than theirfacevalue and accrued interestat the time of
redemption; and saidcounty shallhave the rightthus to
redeem any of said bonds before theirmaturity .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 53
§ 10. No more bonds shall be issued under this act than
will be sufficientto redeem and satisfy,in the way and
manner provided herein,allthe bonds ,coupons ,and other
indebtedness of Lyon county outstanding and unpaid at
the time said new bonds shall be issued ; and if the bonds
firstissued are not sufficientfor that purpose , others.may
be issued from time to time until that purpose is fully
accomplished. The clerkof the Lyon county court shall
keep a record of allbonds issued under thisact,showing
the date of each bond ,when payable,the amount there-
of,and the number of coupons attached to each ; also the
number and amount of bonds sold,and the number and
amount exchanged ,as provided for in thisact; said bonds
shall be of the denominations of fifty, one hundred , and
five hundred dollars .
§ 11. The county court of Lyon county is hereby au-
thorized and empowered to make and enter into any
contract or contracts with any person , persons ,or cor-
poration for the compromise ,purchase ,or redemption of
any and allbonds ,coupons ,or other indebtedness of said
county, by the exchange of new bonds therefor, issued.
under this act,or for the sale of such new bonds or pur-
chase of old bonds, under such limitationsand restric-
tions as are provided in thisact and that to which thisis
an amendment ,and providedsuch contractor contracts
shallbe sanctioned by said " Financial Board ."
§ 12. In the eventjudgment shouldbe renderedon any
bond or bonds which may be issued by Lyon county
under the provisions of this act,or any coupon of any
such bond or bonds , such judgment shall constitute a
lienupon all real and personal property in said county
subjectto taxation under the revenue laws of the Com-
monwealth .
§13 . If the court rendering such judgment should be
of opinion that such serious obstruction is likely to be
offeredto the enforcementof any such judgment as will
materiallydelay it,itshall,on motion of plaintiffthere-
in, referthe same to a commissioner , with instructionsto
ascertain and report on a day to be by it named the

54 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
amount which it will be necessary for the owners or
holders of any such property to pay , in order to raise
promptly,in proportionto the value of such property
owned by each , a sum sufficientto pay the said judg-
ment and allcostsof said suit,and the costs of collecting
the same ,making due allowance for probable delinquen-
cies .
§ 14. Upon making any such reference,the said court
shallmake an order,and if need be ,enforce itby process
of contempt,directingthe officershaving charge of the
books containingthe last assessment for purposes of
State taxation to deliver them to the said commissioner ,
to be by him used in making out his report.
§ 15. If, forany reason ,such assessment books shallnot
be promptly forthcoming,the courtshallprovideformak-
ing a new assessment under the directionand supervision
of the said commissioners .
§ 16. Upon the completion of said report, the said
commissioner shall file the same in the clerk's office of
said court, and publish in a newspaper published in the
town of Eddyville,once a week for three weeks ,a notice
of the filingof said report, and that same will be open
for exceptionsto be filedin said clerk'sofficeuntila day
to be mentioned in said notice,which day shallnot be less
than thirtynor more than fortydays from the lastday on
which said notice is to be published. If no newspaper
shallat the time be publishedin said town , then the
notice shallbe given by posting printed notices at three
or more public places in each election districtin said
county for at leastthree weeks .
§ 17. On a day to be named by the court in the or-
der referringsaid judgment to the commissioner , or on
the firstday of the next regular or special term of the
said court after the expiration of the time for filingex-
ceptions,the saidexceptionsshallstandforhearing; but
the hearingthereofmay be continuedfrom time to time,
forgood cause shown ,or to suit the convenience of the
court .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 55
§ 18. When , after such correction as the court may
deem proper ,said reportshallbe approved and confirmed ,
judgment shallbe rendered againsteach of the parties
namedin the report forthe amount each ,according to said
report as so corrected, should be required to pay , and for
costs; and at the expiration of ninety days from the date
of said judgment ,several executions shall issue against
allpersons who have not then paid to the sheriffor other
officerwho collectsexecutions from said court, the sums
adjudged to be due by them respectively,which executions
may be leviedupon any property held by defendants
therein respectively,or which was owned by them at the
date of the judgment .
§19 . The court shall make proper orders for the safe-
keeping and dispositionof the money to be collected
under the provisions of this act; and it the amount col-
lected shallbe more than necessary to pay the judgment
and costs,the residue shallbe paid over to the said treas-
urer appointed under the provisionsof the act to which
this is an amendment , or to such other officeror person
as the county court of said county may ,by order,direct
to receive same ,and same shall be used as provided in
regard to other funds collected under thisact and the act
to which this is an amendment .
§20 . The sheriffor other officerwho may collectsuch
sums as shallbe voluntarilypaid to him under such judg-
ment , shall be responsiblefor same on his officialbond ,
and shall be entitled to such compensation as sheriffs
shallbe entitledto for collectingtaxes due thisCommon-
wealth .
§21 . The bonds issued under this act shall on their
face stipulatethat the holders of any of them , or of any
coupon thereof, shall be entitledto the remedies for the
collectionof same herein provided for.
§22 . The county judge of Lyon county , sittingas a
court, shall have and exercise all the powers of the
county court of Lyon county, within the meaning and
purview of this act, and possess and exercise all the
powers conferredupon the Lyon county court by this
act .

56 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§23 . For the purpose of more effectuallyaccomplish-
ing the objects and purposes of this act,and the act to
which this is an amendment , the said treasurer shall
depositsuch portionsof the money at any time in his
hands with any person ,persons,corporation,or corpora-
tions appointed as agents for said county ,as he may be
directed to do by the order of said county court,provided
said order shallbe sanctioned by said " Financial Board ."
Said treasurer,in his settlementswith the county court,
shall be required to report under oath the amount of

§24 . This act shall take effect from its passage .
