CHAPTER 777 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Shawnee Turnpike Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That D. J. Curry , R. H. Cecil, W. W. Goddard ,P.
B. Thompson ,jr.,Ben . C. Hardin, H. Clay Davis,and J.
M. Tebbetts, theirassociatesand successors,be , and they
are hereby ,created a body -politicand corporate,under
the name and style of " The Shawnee Turnpike Com-
pany ," with perpetual succession; and by that name are
hereby made capable ,in law and equity, to sue and be
sued,plead and be impleaded,in allcourtsof law and
equity; to contract and be contracted with; to make ,use,
and have a common seal,and to alterthe same at pleas-
ure; and said corporation shall have such powers and
authority as are usual and incident to turnpike corpora-
tions in Kentucky , and as are necessary to carry out the
purposes of thischarter.
§2 . The object of said corporation is to construct and
maintain a turnpike road in Mercer county , commenc-
ing on the pike leading from Harrodsburg to Lexington ,
opposite or near the residence of Martin Shehan , and
running thence nearly due north across Shawnee Run ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 57
between the lands owned by J. M. Tebbetts and R. H.
Cecil to the Mundy's Landing Turnpike , or as near on
said described line as may be found convenient and expe-
dient; said road to be as nearly straight as practicable,
and to be not lessthan fortyfeetnor more than fiftyfeet
in width . Said corporationshalldefinitelyfixand locate
saidrouteforthe constructionof said pike,prescribethe
grades and the width and depth of the metal placed
thereon ,and the mode and manner of constructing the
same , and designate the place for a toll-gate . After one
mile of said road shall be completed , they shallhave the
right to charge proportionate toll, and apply the same to
the completion or repairing of said road , and so with
each additionalmile to be completed .
§3 . The capital stock of said company shall be ten
thousand dollars, to be increased or decreased at the will
of said company ,divided into shares of twenty-five dol-
lars each .
§4 . All the affairsand business of said company , not
herein speciallydelegated to the stockholders, shallbe
managed and controlled by a board of directors, com-
posed of fivestockholders,one of whom shallbe presi-
dent of the board ,to be chosen by the members thereof;
and fiveof the corporatorsnamed in thisact,to be chosen
among themselves ,shallconstitutethe board of directors
for the period of one year from and after the organiza-
tion of said company under this act,and until their suc-
cessors are elected and qualified; and thereafter the
directorsare to be chosen annually by the stockholders
at such times and places as said board shallappoint, and
shallcontinue in officefor one year ,and untiltheir suc-
cessorsare elected and qualified. At the annual election
the stockholdersshallhave the power ,ifthey see fitto
do so, to prescribe and fix the salary of the president;
and in the election of said board , and in the fixing of
said salary, each stockholder shall have a vote for each
share of stock held by him . Any vacancy occurring in
said board shall be filledby the remaining members
thereof, until the next annual meeting of the stock

58 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
holders,and untiltheirsuccessor or successorsshallhave
been elected and qualified. The board shall have the
power to appoint a treasurer, secretary, and all other
officersand agents deemed necessary by said board , and
to remove them or any of them at pleasure, and to re-
quireof saidofficersuch bonds as they may deem nec-
essary to secure the performance of their respective
duties, and to protect the interestsof the company ; and
said board shallhave power and authority to prescribe
and establishrules, regulations, and by -laws fixing the
duties of the officersand agents of the company , and the
mode and manner of their performance , and also to
establishby laws and rules for the transaction, control,
and management of the business and affairs of the com-
pany .
§5 . Said company shallhave power and authorityto
purchase ,lease,buy ,sell,and convey any real or personal
estateitmay deem necessary for the interestsof the com-
pany .
§6 . Said board may establishthe rate of tollto be col-
lected, and may ,in such manner as they deem best, reg-
ulate the travel on said road , and charge tolls therefor,
and collectthe same ; but such tollsso charged and col-
lected shall not exceed in proportion the rates of toll
allowed by the General Statutes. Said board may issue
to persons traveling said road permits for any length of
time,not exceeding one year ,at such charge and price as
may be agreed upon ,and such permit may be for the per-
son alone to whom it is granted, and his or her family,
and servants and employers , as may be agreed .
§ 7. Itshallbe lawful for the officersand employes of
said company to enter, with their tools and appliances,
upon lands over and contiguousto which the intended
road shallpass, firstgiving notice to the owners or occu-
pantsthereof. The company shallhave power totake and
receive the right of way over and through the lands
where said road may be located; and if in any instance
the rightof way cannot be procured by agreement with
the guardian of any infant, or any executor , or owner or

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 59
owners of lands through which the road is to pass,then
saidcompany shallapply to the Mercer county courtfor
a writ of ad quod damnum to assess the damages which
may be sustained to the owner or owners of said lands;
and said court shallissue such writ by itsclerk,directing
thesheriffof saidcounty,on a day to be named insaid
writ, to meet upon the lands through which the way is
sought to be established, with a jury of twelve discreet
householders of said county not living within two miles
of said road ,which jury , upon being sworn ,shall view
and assessthe damages sustainedby the establishmentof
therightof way forsaidroad. Said jury shalltake into
account the advantages and benefits accruing to the
owner or owners of said lands , as well as the disadvan-
tages and value of the lands,in making up theirverdict;
and upon payment of the damages , if any shall be as-
sessed, or in case no damages shall be assessed and
awarded by said jury, after a fullhearing of the case,it
shallbe lawful for said company to open and make said
road , and to do allthe work pertaining thereto; and the
writ of ad quod damnum ,with the action had thereon ,shall
be returned to the office of the clerk of the court whence
itissued; and all proceedings thereon shall be the same
as are prescribed by the general law when said writ is
issued , so far as is not inconsistent with this charter .
§8 . The county court of Mercer county is hereby au-
thorized to take stock in said turnpike company ,not to
exceed the amount per mile which said county court now
has authorityby law to subscribeto turnpikeroads; and
the towns of Harrodsburg and Burgin and respectively
authorized to take stock in said road .
§ 9. Said corporators,or any two of them ,may open a
book for subscription to the stock of said company ,at
such time and places as they may designate, of which
due notice shall be given by posting the same at the
court -house door in Harrodsburg ; and the subscribers to
the stock shallsign therein an obligation,in substance as
follows : We , whose names are hereunto subscribed,re-
spectively promise to pay the Shawnee Turnpike Com-

60 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
pany the sum of twenty-fivedollarsfor each shareof
stocksetoppositeto our names ,in such proportions,and
at such times,as shallbe determined by the directorsof
said company .
§ 10. This act shalltake effectand be in forcefrom and
afteritspassage .
Approved April 1 , 1882.
CHAPTER 778 .
AN ACT for the benefit of the Cincinnati and Southeastern Railroad .
WHEREAS , The Commonwealth of Kentucky ,in token
of her sympathy with allenterprisesintendedto develop
her natural resources and increase her material wealth ,
has exempted from taxationsuch property within her
borders fora period of years ; and whereas ,such a policy,
at once generous and wisely selfish, has attractedinvest-
ment of capital and opened the way for a speedy and
successfuldevelopment of our unlimited mineral wealth ;
and whereas , the Cincinnati and Southeastern Railway ,
without aid of local subscription,is engaged in the con-
structionof a standard gauge railway from Newport , in
Campbell county,toPound Gap ,on the linebetween Ken-
tucky and Virginia,traversingin itscourse a wide extent
of unproductiveland,which willbe made productiveand
valuable by the construction of said road ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Cincinnati and Southeastern Railroad ,
from the cityof Newport to Pound Gap ,itspropertyand
effects,be,and the same are hereby,exempted from State,
county,or municipaltax fora period of ten years: Pro-
vided,That if said railway company shallfailto complete
saidrailway,and put thesame inoperationtothecoal
and iron fieldsof Southeastern Kentucky within three
years from the firstday of March ,1882,then thisact shall
be nulland void,and saidcompany shallbe liableforall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 61
taxes that have accrued during the three years interven-
ing between March 1st, 1882,and March 1st, 1885.
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 1, 1882.
CHAPTER 779 .
AN ACT to empower the county court of Mercer county to appropriatepart
of the jailand jail-yard for work -house purposes.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of the county of Mercer be
hereby empowered to appropriate and use the upper
story of the old jail building which is not in use and a
part of the jail lot,by 100 feet in width and 75 feet in
length, off the west end of said lot,for work -house pur-
poses and for confinement of personssentby processof
law to serve a term in said work -house .
§ 2. That thisbe in force from and afteritspassage .
Approved April 1, 1882 .
CHAPTER 780 .
AN ACT to incorporateDavid Lodge No. 222, Independent Order of Odd
Fellows, Union county
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That James W. Cruse , F. B. Sigler, George H.
Myers , and W. E. Sutherland , and their associates, be ,
and they are hereby,created a body-corporate, by the
name and style of David Lodge , No. 222 , of the Inde-
pendent Order of Odd Fellows; and they and theirasso-
ciatesand successorsshallso continue,and have perpetual
succession; and by that name are made capable in law
as natural persons to sue and be sued ,plead and be im-
pleaded ,contract and be contracted with, answer and be
answered,in allcourtsof law and equityin thisCom-
monwealth ; to make ,have , and use a common seal,and

62 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the same to break,alter, or amend at pleasure. They
may make and ordain regulations and by -laws for their
government ,and those now in force in said lodge to alfer
when deemed proper ,and may change and renew the
same at pleasure : Provided,They be not in contravention
of the constitution,laws , and regulations of the Grand
Lodge of the Independent Order of Odd Fellows ,incor-
porated by an act approved February the 16th, 1838 , nor
in contravention of the Constitution and laws of the
United States or of this State. The said corporation
shallhave power and authority to acquire and hold real
and personal estate not exceeding ten thousand dollarsin
value, and from time to time , if deemed expedient ,sell
and convey the same ,or any part thereof, and to reinvest
and dispose of the proceeds . The right to alter, amend ,
or repeal this act is hereby reserved to the General As-
sembly .
§2 . The individual property of the members of this
lodge shall not be responsible or liablefor the debts of
the corporation.
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 1, 1882.
CHAPTER 781 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Kinniconick Creek Turnpike Road Company ,
and to repealso much of section6 of the act,entitled" An act to incor-
porate the Vanceburg , Dry Run , and Kinniconick Creek Turnpike Road
Company ," approved February 18th, 1869, as conflictswith thisact.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That L. A. Muleer , Wm . Hardy ,Jonathan Esham ,
Samuel McEldowny ,and Joseph D. Redden , and their
associatesand successors in office,are created a body-
corporate and politic, under the name and style of the
"KinniconickCreek Turnpike Road Company ." The
capitalstock shallbe fiftythousand dollars; each share of
stock shall be twenty -five dollars. Said company shall
have power to sue and be sued , contract and be con

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 63
tracted with ,hold, enjoy, possess, sell, convey , and dis-
pose of real and personal estate,not exceeding the sum
of fiftythousand dollars .
§2 . The officersof said company shallconsistof a
president, treasurer, secretary, and three directors; and
the officeof secretary and treasurer may be filledby one
and the same person ; and the president shallbe one of
the directors. The said officersshall be chosen annually
by the stockholders in such manner and at such time as
said company shallprovide for by its by -laws ; and said
officers shall hold their offices until their successors are
elected and enter upon the discharge of their duties.
The treasurer shallgive bond to the president and direct-
ors of said company , with good and sufficientsecurity,
for the faithfulperformance of his duties as such ; and
for any breach thereof the company , or any person ag-
grieved or injured thereby,may have his or theirproper
action or remedy at law upon said bond ,and against said
treasurer and sureties .
§3 . The company may , by appropriateproceeding,
cause to be condemned for the use of said road the right
of way ,sitesfor tollgates,residence of the keeper , and
forsuch other purpose as said company may need , land
for the use of said road; they may also in like manner
condemn stone quarries , gravel beds , and all necessary
and proper materials to build and keep in repair said
road, and allnecessary bridges on the same . Said turn-
pike road shallcommence at the present terminus of the
Vanceburg ,Dry Run and Kinniconick Creek Turnpike
Road ,near the residence of Thos . B. Harrison ,formerly
the residence of J. W. Rand ,and run thence up said Kin-
niconick Creek ,keeping the route of the present county
road so far as practicable, passing near the mouth of
theHolly Fork,and continuingup thevalleyof saidKin-
niconick Creek ,with said county road so far as practi-
cable , to the crossing of said creek with the road leading
up the Indian Fork of said creek; and thence up either
fork of said creek,Indian or Elk , as said company may
hereafter determine by a majority of its stockholders,

64 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
selecting the most practicable route for the location of
said road as the company may deem best and proper .
They shallmake proper provisionfor publictravelover
the present county road wherever the same interferesor
conflictsduring the construction of said turnpike road .
The saidcompany willnot be requiredto builda bridge
acrossthe HollyFork of saidcreek,but shallprepare the
proper approachesto and from. The buildingof bridges
along the route of said road shall be left to the best
judgment and discretionof said company ; the saidroad
to be locatedby the saidcompany ,or by commissioners
under its direction and management ; and when said
road is finished and in condition forpublic travel a dis-
tance of one mile,said company may erect a toll-gate
and charge forsaid mile thus traveled in proportion and
at same rate as charged for likedistance on the Vance-
burg ,Dry Run and Kinniconick Road ,and so on for each
mile so finishedand traveled; but one toll-gate shallbe
erected for each five miles so finished and traveled .
§ 4. That Thos . B. Harrison , Wm . Hardy , and Sam'l
McEldowny , any one of whom may act,are hereby ap-
pointed commissionersto receivesubscriptionsof stock
to said turnpike road company at such times and places
as said commissioners or commissioner so acting may
from time to time deem expedient and proper ; that such
of said commissioners as shall act shallprocure one or
more books , and the subscribers for stock shallsign an
obligation in substance as follows: " We , whose names
are hereto subscribed, do promise to the Kinniconick
Turnpike Road Company the sum of $25 for each share
set oppositeour respectivenames , in such proportions
and at such timesas shallbe determinedby the president
and directorsof said company ."
§5 . That so soon as two thousand dollarsis subscribed
in stock to said company ,itshallbe the duty of the com-
missioners , or the one or ones acting , to give notice to
those who have subscribedstock to said company of a
meeting of stockholders at the residence or storehouse of
Thos . B. Harrison for the purpose of choosing the officers

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 65
of said company . Each stockholder shallbe entitledto
one vote for each share of stock owned by him .
§6 . That so soon as the officersare elected and the
company organized,said company shallpossessallthe
powers ,authority,rights,and privileges,and may do all
acts and things necessary to enable the company to build
said turnpike road , the erection of gates, collection of
tolls, appointing all necessary engineers, surveyors, and
agents as shallbe necessary for the building and success-
ful occupation , use, and operating of said road ,and for
keeping the same in repair.
§7 . That said president and directors shall fix and
regulate the elevation and grade of said road; the width
or part thereofto be covered by stone or gravel,and the
width or part thereof to be elevated and graded , and left
uncovered with stone,& c. They shalldesignate the place
for the erection of gates,not exceeding one for every five
miles of said road : Provided,The width of said road shall
not exceed fiftyfeet, and the part covered with stone or
gravel shallnot be lessthan twelve feet,and part graded
feet, and the stone or gravel to be twelve inches
deep ,and they may use such stone or gravel as shallbe
accessible and convenient thereto .
§ 8. The president and directorsshalldeliver a certifi-
cate, signed by the president, and countersigned by the
treasurer,to each stockholder for the shares by him held,
which certificatemay be transferable on the books of
said board according to the order of such stockholder;
but no share shall be transferred until all calls or arrear-
ages thereon are paid .
§9 . That the president of the board may callmeetings
of the board at such times and places as he may think
proper,and a majorityof the directorsshallbe necessary
for the transaction of business. Said board shallkeep a
recordof itsproceedings,to be entered in a book to be
furnished for that purpose ; and each day's proceedings
shallbe signed by the president or presiding officer.
§ 10. The presidentand directorsmay callon the sub-
scribersof stock for any portion of their stock subscribed,
LOC . L., VOL . II- 5

66 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
not exceeding one fourth of the total amount at a time ,
nor on shorter periods than three months after the first
call(the firstcallof one-fourthmay be calledfor, and
payment enforced at any time after said board is organ-
ized),and untilallthe stock subscribedshallbe paid in
by the subscribersof stock.
§ 11. That in order to enable said company to build
theirroad as speedilyas possible,and equalizethe bur-
thens thereof, there ishereby levied on all property, real,
personal, and mixed , subject to taxation for State rev-
enue purposes,withintwo milesof saidturnpikeroad as
surveyed and located,and on eithersidethereof (not now
taxed to build any other turnpike road),an ad valorem tax
of one dollaron each one hundred dollars'worth of prop-
erty,said tax to be levied and collected each year until
said turnpike road is finishedfrom itsbeginning point to
its terminus, in said county , either on the Elk Fork or
Indian Fork of said creek,as shallhereafterbe deterinined
by said company .
§ 12. That said company may appoint an assessorto
assessthe estate, as aforesaid, subject to taxation, along
the route of said road so surveyed and established, of all
persons and property subject and liable therefor. Said
assessmentshallbe returnedto the May or June term of
the Lewis county court,in each year , which shallbe sub-
ject to revision and correction by said county court,
and said court shall list said tax lists with the sheriff of
Lewis county for collection; and said sheriffshallreceive
and collectsaid taxes and pay same over to the treasurer
of said turnpike road company on or before the firstday
of December in each year . For any defaultof saidsheriff
under thisact,he and his securitiesshallbe liableto suit
or suits on his official bond as sheriff. Said suit or suits
shall be brought in the name of the president of said
" KinniconickTurnpike Road Company ," for the use of
saidcompany ,in the Lewis circuitcourt. Said company
shall recover in said suit or suits the amount of taxes
collectedby said sheriff, or what he might have collected
by due diligence,with same interest,costs, and damages

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 67
now allowedby law againstsheriffsforfailingto collect
and paying over the county levy.
§13 . The tax-payers who pay taxes under thisact
toward building said turnpike road shallbe stockholders
in saidturnpike road company for the amount of taxes
they shallthus pay ; and proper certificatesthereofshall
be deliveredto them by said company thereforwhenever
they shallhave paid an amount equal to twenty-fivedol-
lars, and so on untilthe road is completed .
§ 14 That itshall be the privilegeof each individual
through whose land said turnpike road may run or pass,
who shall subscribe stock , to work out the value of his
said stock through his said land at the estimated price for
labor done by others on said road ,or he shall have the
preference at the lowest bid, if made known by him
before the work is commenced by the other party or the
company. Allsubscribersofstockshallhavethepriv-
ilege of working out their stock so subscribed,under the
direction of the president and directorsof said road , at
the customary price for labor,instead of paying the same
in money . They must , however , work under the direc-
tion of the president and directorsor their agent .
§15 . That whenever the resourcesof said company
are such that, with the aid of the county subscription of
one thousand dollars per mile, said company will be
enabled to build the road one mile, or its entirelength
from the beginning point aforesaid to the intersectionof
the county road at the crossingof Kinniconick creek,
near the mouth of the Indian Fork (five miles more or
less),and when said road shallhave been actuallylaid
out and locatedby a competent engineer,and the right
of way therefor obtained, and proof of these facts satis
factorilymade to the county court of Lewis county ,said
court shall be authorized and directed to make the sub-
scriptionof one thousand dollarsper mile,and so on for
each mile so made and ready for travel and use by the
public .
§16 . That sections twenty -third and twenty -fourth of
an act,entitled" An act to incorporatethe Vanceburg ,

68 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Salt Lick ,Tollsboro, and Maysville Turnpike Road Com-
pany , in Lewis county ,and to establisha sinking fund
board for said county ," approved February 13th, 1867, so
far as the same are applicable,are hereby adopted and
re-enacted as part of thisact.
§ 17. The commissioners appointed to open books for
the subscriptionof stock are hereby authorized to receive
subscriptionsto the capital stock of said company in lieu
of taxes,and the persons so subscribing and paying their
stockshallnot be compelledto pay the specialtax levied
by section 11 of thisact untilthe entire amount of taxes
assessed against him or them shall equal the amount
which he or they may have subscribed and paid on their
said subscriptionof stock to the company ; theirsubscrip-
tion shallbe considered and treated as taxes paid in ad-
vance, and allthe tax-payers being stockholdersto the
amount of taxes they thus pay to assistin building said
road .
§ 18. That itshallbe unlawful for any person teaming
on said turnpike road to rough -lock,or lock with a chain ,
any wagon while going over said road ; and allpersons
teaming on said road shall be required to use the rub-
lock, if any ; and for any violation of this section the
offender shall be fined the sum of five dollars for each
offense,to be recovered upon conviction before a justice
of the peace by warrant in the name of the presidentof
said road .
§19 . If any person shallgo around or turn offsaid
road with the intent to avoid the payment of tolls, or
shallpass through refusingto pay toll, he and he ,she, or
theyshall,on convictionthereofbeforeany justiceof the
peace,be finedfivedollarsforeach of saidoffenses,to be
collected as other sums under the jurisdictionof such
officers .
§20 . That so much of sectionsix(6) of the act,entitled
" An act to incorporate the Vanceburg , Dry Run , and
Kinniconick Creek Turnpike Road Company ," approved
February 18th, 1869 , as comes in conflictwith thisact,or
gives that company the right and power to build and ex

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 69
tend theirsaid road up Kinniconick Creek to the mouth
of the Indian Run Fork, and thence to same point in
Rowan county,as contemplated by said sectionof said
act as a branch road, be, and the same is hereby, re-
pealed .
§21 . This act shalltake effectand be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved April 1, 1882 .
CHAPTER 782 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Union County River Bottom Land Fence
Company .
WHEREAS , The section of land in Union county ,Ken-
tucky,lying in the bottoms oppositeShawneetown ,Illi-
nois, is subject to overflow from the Ohio river; and
whereas ,thelaterisein the Ohio riverhas swept away
most of the division, cross, and otherfencing on these
lands. Whereas ,it isdesirable,in point of convenience ,
economy ,and necessity,that one common fence should be
built and kept up by the land -owners around theirfarms
in said bottom ; therefore,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That George Huston , Henry C. Dixon , John C.
Atkinson , W. B. Henshaw , John Young Brown , Mrs.
Mary F. Burbank ,John M. Lambert ,and John C. Atkin-
son , executor of George Atkinson , deceased , and such
other persons in their stead as may hereafter succeed to
the ownership of the lands now owned by them (same
lying and being in the county of Union , below the ferry
opposite Shawneetown , Illinois,and extending down the
Ohio river to the lower line of George Huston's farm ,
lyingnext to the Brown farm),be,and are hereby,con-
stituted a body -politic and corporate, under the name
and style of the " Union County River Bottom Land
Fence Company ," with power to sue and be sued,to con-
tractand be contracted with ,for the specificpurpose as
setforthherein,to-wit: To build and re-build,and keep

70 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
in repair, from year to year, one common fence (post-
plan, plank, or rail) inclosingtheirlands hereinbefore
mentioned:Providedfurther, That they shallhave power
to contract with other parties,so far as may be necessary,
forthe privilegeof building a fence across their lands so
as to inclose those mentioned .
§2 . They shall elect of theirnumber a chairman , sec-
retary, and treasurer, a majority of the parties above
named to constitutea quorum for such purpose ,or forthe
transactionof any business whatever . In the event of a
division and sale of the lands inclosed by the common
fence,increasingthe number of partiesin thiscorporation
by virtue of the firstsection of this act,it shallstillre-
quirea majorityof allthe partiesto constitutea quorum
forthe purpose of business, and said partiesmay vote on
allmatters by authorized proxies.
§3 . They shallhave power to pass any rulesand regu-
lations,and to impose finesand penaltiesthey may deem
proper, to insurethe successof the objectof thisact.
§ 4. It shall be the duty of the secretary to make all
contracts involving the expenditure of money , but no
such contract shall be binding upon the company until
submitted for their approval , and countersigned, under
their order, by the chairman , a record of the yeas and
nays being kept by the secretary; and when itbecomes
necessary to meet the expense incurred by any contract
of the company ,it shall be the duty of the secretary to
assess a specifictax upon the partiesnamed in thisact,
or hereafterbecoming partiesby virtueof thisact, in
proportion to the number of acres of cleared land on the
tractsowned by each,sufficientin the aggregateto cover
said expense ,and to delivera copy of such assessment or
assessments over his officialsignature to the treasurer .
§ 5. It shall be the duty of the treasurer to collectall
assessments made and delivered to him by the secretary
from the parties against whom such assessments have
been made ,and he ishereby grantedthe power to collect
the same by law ; and to secure the payment of any such
assessment the company shallhave a lienupon the lands

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 71
inclosed by the common fence against which the assess-
ment has been made , which lien may be enforced by any
court of competent jurisdictionin the county of Union
It shall further be the duty of the treasurerto collectall
fines imposed under the power granted in section third of
this act; and he shall have the power to collectthe same
by legal proceedings .
§ 6. It shallfurtherbe lawful for thiscompany to con-
tract for the purchase and sale of any fences or parts of
fences that may be already built upon the route of the
proposed common fence,such purchase or sale to be in
keeping with the spiritof thisact.
§7 . The firstannual election in this company shall
take place on the 11thday of April,1882,at which time
there shall be chosen a chairman , secretary, and treas-
urer ,each of whom shall hold officetilltheirsuccessors
are qualified, and the next election shall thereafterbe
held on the second Tuesday of October , 1882 , and there-
after annually at such time as may be prescribed by reso-
lution of the company . Any vacancy occurring in the
office of chairman ,secretary, or treasurer,shall be filled
at any fixed or called meeting by regular election, time
of holding meetings to be fixed by the by -laws of the
company . The chairman may at any time calla meeting
of the company by notice to itsmembers . The secretary
shallkeep a record of the proceedings of all meetings .
§ 8. Be itfurtherenacted, That thiscompany may ,by a
vote of a majority of its members ,consolidate with that
known as the " Raleigh River Bottom Land Fence Com-
pany ," a majorityof the lattercompany assentingthere-
to; and the consolidatedcompanies shallbe known by the
name of this company , and shall have all the powers ,
rights, and privileges set forth in their respective char-
ters .
Approved April 1, 1882 .

72 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 783 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe CatlettsburgCemetery Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That George N. Brown , M. F. Hampton , John
McDyer , John C. Hopkins, Colbert Cecil, jr., W. Ο .
Hampton ,Robert H. Killgore,Silas Patton , and Thos .R.
Brown , and their associates, be , and they are hereby ,
createda body -politicand corporate,under the name and
styleof the CatlettsburgCemetery Company ,and by that
name saidcompany shallhave perpetualsuccession,and
shallbe able and capable in law to have and use a com-
mon seal,to sue and be sued ,plead and be impleaded ,
defend and be defended , in all courts of this Common-
wealth and elsewhere . They shall have power to pur-
chase any quantityof land in the county of Boyd ,not
exceeding one hundred acres,and receive a conveyance
therefor, with such covenants of warranty as they deem
proper. The lands and appurtenances,when conveyed
tosaidcompany,shallbe heldsolelyand exclusivelyfora
cemetery , and ornamental grounds connected therewith,
and shall never be alienated, sold,or used for any other
purpose whatsoever; but the company may permit their
superintendent or other officersto use any portion of
theirgrounds not sold for burial lotsforhorticulturalpur-
poses,but the same shallnot be used in a manner incon-
sistentwith the reverence and respectdue to the cemetery
of the dead . The grounds ,fixtures,shrubbery,and every-
thing grown therein,shall be exempt from taxation; and
afterthe grounds shallbe fullypaid for no part thereof
shallbe subject to saleby virtueof mortgage , judgment ,
execution, or decree,for any cause whatever . No road
or passway shall ever be opened through said grounds .
unlessby consent of said company , and shallnot in any
way or in any event pass over or interferein any way
with any lotwhich may have been soldby saidcompany
for burialpurposes. The said company may take and
receive,by giftor devise,any land or other property,to be
held or appropriated to the ornamenting ,improving , or

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 73 .
extending the grounds of the company , subjectto the
restrictionsaforesaid, and may vest in State bonds or
stock, or loan any spare fundsthey may at any time have ,
but shallnever exercise any banking power .
§2 . That within one year afterthe passage of this act
the persons named in the preceding section, or any three
of them ,shall cause books to be opened in the town of
Catlettsburgforthe purpose of receivingsubscriptionto
the stockof saidcompany ; saidbooks shallbe opened at
the law office of Thomas R. Brown in said town and
such other places as may be designated . The shares of
said company shall be each ten dollars; and said books
shallbe kept open untilat leastone hundred shares shall
have been subscribed,when they may be closed : Pro-
vided, however, That any person may thereaftermake
private subscriptionsupon the books of said company
with the consent of the company .
§3 . That so soon as the books of saidcompany shall
be closed the said corporators,or such of them as may
act, at such time and place as they may appoint , call a
meeting of the shareholders,who shallelectfivetrustees,
who shall continue in officefor three years, and until
theirsuccessorsshallbe elected and qualified; said board
of trustees shall elect one of theirnumber as president,
and shall have power to fillallvacancies in their body .
Three trustees shallconstitutea quorum for the transac-
tion of business ; death , resignation , or removal from the
county of Boyd shallvacate the officeof trustee. Elec-
tion of trustees shallbe held every three years ; and if,
for any cause ,an electionshall not be held at the proper
time , then said trusteesshall appoint some other time for
the election; and the election shall be held under the
direction of the board of trustees then in office, and a
majority of the shareholders who vote shall elect,each
stockholder voting one vote for each share,which may
be done in proper person or by the written proxy filed
with the board or officers of the election . Reasonable
public notice shallalways be given of the time and place
of holding the electionby written or printed notices in.

74 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
four public places in the town of Catlettsburgfor ten days
next before the election, shall be deemed reasonable.
Every owner of a lot or lots of the value of ten dollars
shallbe a shareholder, and each shareholder shallbe en-
titledto one vote for each share he may own . Each trus-
tee,before he enters upon the duties of his office, shall
make oath before some judge or justiceof the peace that
he willfaithfullyand impartiallydischargethe dutiesof
his office .
§ 4. That the trusteesshallhave power to appoint a
superintendent, clerk, treasurer, and such other officers
as they deem proper,and may regulatetheirterms of
office,and may take from any or allof them bond , with
good security, for the faithfuldischarge of their duties.
They shall keep a regular record of their proceedings ,
and of allsales,transfers, and disbursements, and shall
prepare and keep an accurate map and survey of the
grounds and lots, and have the same recorded in the
clerk'sofficeof Boyd county . They shallhave power to
make by -laws for the regulation and sale of lots, and for
the control, care, and management of the grounds and
graves , and the mode of ornamenting the same , and es-
tablish rules in relation to the interment of bodies, and
make such other by -laws and regulationsas may be nec-
essaryforthepurpose of the company . They shallhave
power to enter upon any of the lots and remove any
monuments , fixtures, or shrubbery that may be placed in
said lotsagainst the by -laws or regulationsof said com-
pany .
§5 . If,from any cause ,the electionis not held for trus-
tees as herein required, the board of trustees in office
shallremain in officeuntil the electionand qualification
of theirsuccessors, as herein provided .
§ 6. The trustees, or those authorized by them , subject
to the approval of the board ,shall have power to sell
buriallotsat eitherpublic auction or privatesale; and
when sold the president of the company shallexecute to
the purchaser a certificatethereof, under the seal of the
company ,which shallvestthe purchaserwith title,which

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 75
certificate shall be recorded in the clerk's office of the
Boyd county court; and the seal of the company being
annexed to said certificate,shallbe sufficientauthentica-
tion to admit it to record; and for recording such certifi-
cate of title, together with his own officialcertificate
appended thereto,the clerksof said courtmay charge a
fee of fiftycents,and no more . All subsequent transfers
shallbe required to be proved or acknowledged , and re-
corded in the manner requiredby the laws of thisState
in relation to conveyance of real estate. The titleto
lotsheld by individualsshallpass by sale, devise, or
descent , as in case of other real estate; but they shall
never be used forany otherpurpose than buriallots; and
if applied to any other purpose,the titleshallrevertto
the company .
§ 7. It shallor may be lawful for the corporate authori-
tiesof the town of Catlettsburg,and for the county court
of Boyd county ,to subscribe to the stock of said com-
pany to an amount of fivehundred dollarseach,not to
exceed said sum ; and in the event of such subscription
being made and paid,or secured to be paid,itshallbe
the duty of said company to assign to them proper lots,
accordingto the amounts by them respectivelysubscribed
and paid ,and give certificatesof titletherefor; and said
lotsso assigned and conveyed shallbe foreversetapart
as a place of burial for paupers ,strangers, or sojourners
who may die in the county of Boyd ; and to allsuch the
same respect shall be paid as to those who may be the
owners of lots; and the money to pay such subscription
shallbe raised by said county court and town authorities
in the same manner in which they raisetheirtown and
county revenue . It shall also be lawful for alllodges of
the Independent Order of Odd Fellows ,of allchapters of
Royal Arch Masons ,of alllodges of Free and Accepted
Masons ,and of divisionsof the Sons of Temperance ,or
other temperance organization in said county , each to
subscribe for and hold stock in said company to an
amount not exceeding three hundred dollarseach , or to
purchase and buy lotsof said company suitableand for

76 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
burial purposes as aforesaid,each not to exceed the sums
aforesaid, and shall thereby become stockholders to the
amounts of their said purchase as authorized in individ-
uals to become stockholders,and shallhave representation
in the electionof trusteesby theiragents duly authorized
to cast theirvote. The county court,and the corporations
or associations above named , if they subscribe to the
stock of said company , shall be entitled to the value
thereof in lots, and to all the rights and privileges, and
subject to the same rules and regulationswhich may be
adopted for the government of individualstockholders.
§ 8. That those who may become subscribers to the
capitalstock of the company shall,out of the proceeds
of thesaleof lotsor otherfunds of thecompany ,be enti-
tledtohave the sums they may respectivelypay repaidto
them by the company as the means can be spared for
that purpose ; and if not paid within one year afterthe
organization of the company , interestshallbe paid after
one year on any sum that may be due . The value of the
lotsrespectivelytaken by stockholdersmay be a payment
of the stock subscribed . The shares of the subscribers of
the capitalstock shallbe diminished by payment as made ,
and when the whole sum shallbe paid, then the value of
the lots held shall fix the shares . In determining the
shares of stock the value of the lots, without regard to
the improvements or monuments that may be placed
thereon , shall be the criterion.
§9 . That if any person or persons shall forcibly, and
without lawful authority,violateany of the graves of the
dead , or any vault which may be erected on the grounds
of the company ,or willfullyinjure any of the ornaments ,
shrubbery ,fencing, fixtures, or buildings, or in any way
damage the grounds or other property of the company ,
such person or persons so offending, and those who may
aid or abet therein,shallbe punished by finenot lessthan
ten,or imprisonment in the county jail not exceeding six
months,or both,which may be by indictmentin the Boyd
circuitcourt, or by a warrant before the police judge of
the town of Catlettsburg , and shall also be liable to the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 77
company in an action for damages in any court having
jurisdictionthereof, which damages ,when recovered ,and
fines shall be for the company , and shallbe applied by
the company to the repairingthe injurycommitted,and
the residue,if any ,to be used upon the roads,streets,and
alleyspassing through or around the grounds of the com-
pany , or on the inclosure thereof.
§ 10. Should said company acquire any land deemed
by them indivisibleforthe purposesabove described,they
may exchange or sellthe same ,and apply the proceeds
to any debts, advancements , or improvements they may
deem desirable. The said trustees shall have power to
levy a tax on the lot-owners in said cemetery to raisethe
necessary fund for keeping said grounds in said repair.
The said trustees shall have power to forfeitany lot or
lotsin said cemetery grounds ,taken up by any person or
persons who shallfailto make payment forsuch lotor
lots; and when any interments have been made on any
such lot or lots,the trustees shall have power to enter
upon the same , and remove , or cause to be removed ,the
bodies to the public grounds in said cemetery or some
other one : Provided, That the said trusteesshall,before
forfeitingany such lot or lots,give written notice to the
person or persons in whose name such lotsare entered of
at leastthirtydays before such action; and if such person
or persons cannot be found , or residesout of the State,
then said noticeis to be posted on said lotor lotsfor sixty
days before such action .
§11 . If two or more persons shall own one or more
lotsin partnership,or jointly,ifthey shallnot agree as
voting the stock, as herein provided,the firstone named
in the certificateof titleshall have the right to vote the
said stock .
§ 12. This act shallbe in force from and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 1, 1882.

78 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 784 .
AN ACT to amend and reduce into one the acts in relation to the town of
Harrodsburg .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the corporate limits and boundary of the
town of Harrodsburg shallbe as now establishedby law.
§2 . That the fiscal,prudential,and municipal concerns
of said town , with the government and control thereof,
shall be vested in seven trustees,four of whom shall form
a quorum to transact business, and all of whom shall
have resided in said town one year next preceding their
election .
§3 . That said trustees and their successors in office
shallbe a body -politicand corporate,and be known by
the name and style of the " Board of Trustees of the
Town of Harrodsburg;" and by thatname shallhe capa-
ble in law of contracting and being contracted with, of
suing and being sued ,of pleading and being impleaded ,
of answering and being answered , and of defending and
being defended ,in allcourts and places; and may use a
common or private seal.
§ 4. That said trusteesshallbe elected on the firstSat-
urday of April, 1882, and biennially thereafter, by the
qualified voters of said town who shall have resided
within the limits of the town , as herein defined , for the
period of twelve months previous to said election, or in
the State of Kentucky two years ,and in said town for
sixty days previous to said election . Said trustees shall
hold their officefor two years, and until theirsuccessors
shall have been duly elected and qualified, or until a
majority of their successors so elected shall have quali-
fied. Before entering upon the duties of theirofficethey
shall take an oath before some officer authorized to ad-
minister the same that they will, without favor or affec-
tion, faithfullydischarge the duties of trustees of said
town during theircontinuance in said office.
§5 . Said election shall be held at the court -house in
the town of Harrodsburg,or at such otherplace as the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 79
trustees of said town may designate by written notice
posted at the court-house door for one week previous to
said election . It shall be held between the hours of 8
o'clock,A. M. , and 5 o'clock,P. M.
§6 . That when two or more candidates at any election,
for any electiveofficein saidtown ,shallhave received an
equal number of votes,the election shallbe determined
by lot in such manner as the board of trustees may
direct, and in the presence of the said board and at least
three other persons .
§7 . That the board of trusteesshall, before every such
election, appoint suitablepersons as officersthereof, viz :
two persons as judges , one to be of one politicalparty,
and the other of the opposite party, and a like difference
shall exist between the'sheriff and clerk of the election,
which said trustees are hereby authorized to appoint in
like manner : Provided, That there shall be a sufficient
number of the members of each politicalparty as afore-
said to fill said offices who will consent to act . That
said officers, before they proceed to hold said election,
shall be duly sworn to faithfullyperform their respective
duties as officersof said election, and they shall perform
similar services,and for the same receive like pay ,and
be governed in their duties so far as is consistent here-
with , and be under the same responsibilities, and subject
to the same penaltiesas officersof the State elections
under the laws of this Commonwealth .
§ 8. That alllaws in forcein thisCommonwealth , pro-
viding for the punishment of illegalvoting at the State
elections,shallapplyto illegalvoting at the town elec-
tionsin the town of Harrodsburg, subject, however , to
the provisionsof the act touching the qualificationsof
voters .
§ 9. The board of trusteesshalljudge of the qualifica-
tions, elections, and returns of its members , and deter-
mine who have been elected to fill the several offices
provided for in thischarter.
§10 . That the poll-books of allelectionsof officersof
said town shall be certifiedby the officersappointed to

80 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
hold said election , with the names of those elected , and
returned by the clerk or sheriffof said election, and de-
posited immediately with the clerk of the board of trus-
tees of said town , to be preserved by him . The clerk
of said board shall record the result of said election in
theminute-book of the board,and notifythe severaltrus-
tees of their election .
§ 11. That the legal titleto allthe streetsand alleysin
saidtown,and to allrealor personalpropertywhich now
belongs or which may hereafter belong to said town ,is
vested in said board of trustees, with authority to hold
realand personalestate,by purchase,donation,or other-
wise , for the use or ornament of the town ; and they are
authorized to use, lease, or sell and convey the same as
they may deem expedient .
§ 12. That said trustees, after their qualification,shall
have power to fillany vacancy occurring in their own
body . It shall be their duty to elect one of their own
number chairman , who shall preside at all their meet-
ings,and in case of the absence of the chairman , they
shallappointa chairman pro tempore. In case any trus-
tee shallremove from the town ,or failor refuse to per-
form his duties as such trustee, or to attend the meetings
of the board for three months successively, or in case of
the death of a member , the board may , by resolution
passed to that effect,declare his seat vacant ,and fillthe
vacancy so created .
§ 13. Said trusteesshallhave legislativepower to make
by -laws and ordinances for the carrying into effectof all
the powers herein granted for the government of the
town , and to do allthings properly belonging to the po-
liceof an incorporatedtown ; also-
1. To regulate the times and places of their meetings ,
and change them at pleasure,and to determine theirrules
of procedure ; to enact laws to compel the attendance of
members at any regular meeting or any meeting called
by the chairman or any two members of the board .
2. To appoint or elect a chairman , clerk, treasurer,
assessorof the taxes,collector,sexton of the cemetery ,and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 81
1
alsoa competent policeforce,and to fixtheirfees and
salary,and to pay the same out of the treasuryof the

and collector of taxes bond with good security for the

vacancies occurringin any of the officeswhich, under

pointment, and allvacancies of officerselectedin any
manner to serve said town .

good order,decency ,and decorum of the town , for the

the inhabitants,and to enforcethe same by appropriate
finesand penalties.
5. To adopt and enforce sanitary regulations,to pre-
vent the introduction and spread of epidemics and conta
gious diseases, and to establisha hospitalor pest-house .
6. To clear the streets, alleys, and pavements of all
obstructions; to erectand sink cisterns, wells,and pumps ,
and keep open allsprings within the town .
7. To ornament the grounds and buildingsbelonging to
thetown ; tomake and keep in repairalldrains,ditches,
and guttersthat they may deem necessary to promote the
health and cleanliness of the town .
8. To cause to be removed and abated all nuisances,
and to pass all necessary by -laws for the suppression of
the same under penalty for the regulation, abatement ,
and removal of the same , within the town limits.
9. To prohibitand to regulate the storage of any com-
bustible, explosive,or unwholesome material within the

10. To cause any chimney ,flue,stove-pipe, fire-place,

11. To cause the owners and occupiers of property
fronting on streetsand alleys,or having streetsor alleys

out license, and gaming of every description, whereby

or ball or pin alleys of any kind , and of shooting gal-
leries .

20. To restrain,suppress,and punishindecent,obscene,

24. They shallhave power to appropriatesuch sums as
theymay deem proper to construct,or aid in construct-
ing, pavements, plank roads or walks, rock walks or

lation of any of the ordinances of said town in such

to exceed fiftydollarsfine and ten days imprisonment,

26. To provide for the purchase and erection of lamp-
posts,lamps,and othersuppliesnecessaryforlightingthe

ing, and keeping in repair and good running order of a

§ 14. That itshallbe the duty of said board of trus-
tees-
1. To keep a journalof theirproceedings,and at the
requestof a member the ayes and nays on any question
shall be recorded . In the meetings of the board no mem-
ber shallvote on any question in which he has a pecu-
niary interest.

ble to " The Board of Trustees of the Town of Harrods-
burg ," and may contain such stipulationsand conditions

any obstructions to the free flow of the water in the town
branch , or any of itstributarieswithin the town , and to

ings of the board , and record allitsacts and resolutions,

He shallperform allthe dutiesproperlyappertainingto

§ 15. That the said board of trusteesshall have power
and authority annually to assess,levy, and collect a tax
on all the real and personal estate within the limits of

erty; also--

dollaron allmale persons residentin saidtown over the

erty sold under execution, or by order of court, or by

within the town limitswithout licenseby appropriate

That no such license is to take effect until the State tax
is paid and the bond executedwhich isrequiredby the

sessorof the town the trusteesshallappoint a committee
as a board of supervisors, who shall carefullyexamine

uation of property as made by him , whether the same

vided,and reportto the saidboard withinfifteendays
aftertheirappointment .

portion and levy an ad valorem tax on the propertyso
returned, and a poll-tax on each male inhabitant over

shall deliveran alphabeticallyarranged copy of said

sons and property by whom and upon which taxes are

9. That alltaxes and assessments levied or assessed by

commissions incident to the collection thereof , shall be a
lien upon all the real and personal estate of the owner
within the town , whether the tax be levied,voted , or as-
sessed, untilthe same be paid, and no sale or transfer
of real estate shall affect the lien thereon
10. That the marshal or collector,immediately on the
receipt of said tax-book and lists,shallgive notice there-
of,to be publishedin some newspaper of said town if
there be any ,for two consecutive weeks ,or by posting
the same in four conspicuous places in said town for the

be ready at his office, between the hours of nine and
twelve in the forenoon , and two and five in the afternoon ,
forthe periodof sixdays,to receiveand receiptforsaid
On the failure of any tax-payer to pay his taxes

sonal propertyowned by the person againstwhom the

prima facieevidence that said publicationwas made as

the purchaser .
12. That there shall exist in all sales of real estate
for taxes in said town a right of redemption , for the
period of two years from the day of sale,on payment ,
by the original owner ,of the fullamount forwhich the
property may have been sold, with ten per cent. per
annum interest,and alltaxes accruing subsequent to the
sale: Provided, That if the owner at the time of the

mind ,said rightto redeem shallexist for a like number
of years after the removal of such disability. If not
redeemed as above specified, the marshal , on presenta-
tion of the certificateof purchase, and upon affidavit
thereon that the same has not been redeemed according
to the provisions of thisact, shall make to the purchaser
or his assignee a deed for said property .
13. When personalpropertyis soldby the marshal or
collectoraforesaid to pay town taxes, the same shall not
be subject to redemption .
14. The board of trusteesof said town may from time

of lots on any street or alley of the town to cause the

width and of such materials,in such manner and at such

nished with sidewalks within the time or manner pre-
scribed by said trustees,it shall be lawful for the said

fants owning such property. Said improvements shallbe

viceofsaidnotice,which noticeshallbe servedby a copy,
and have the serviceduly indorsed thereon and preserved

cessiveweekly publications,give notice to the partiesto
have said work done ; and a copy of said order or direc-

this act,may be recorded in the officeof the clerk of the

§ 16. The treasurer appointed by the board shalltake
an oath to faithfullyperform the duties of his office, and
shallexecute a bond in a penaltyof not lessthan five
thousand dollarsfor the faithfuldischarge of the duties of
his office, and conditioned that he will,at the expiration
of histerm of office,deliverallfundsand propertyin his
hands belonging to said trusteesto his successor in office,
and disburse any funds received by him according to the
order of the said board of trustees. It shallbe his duty
to receive and receipt for all moneys paid to the board ,
and pay no money withouta certifiedcopy of the order.
of the board making the appropriation, signed by the
clerk; and he shallfilesuch copy as a voucher in his set-
tlement with the board . His books shall at all times be
open to the inspectionof the officersand citizensof the
town . The treasureris authorized to receive and receipt
for all money due the town for licenses,fines,penalties,
and taxes collected by the marshal , or money due the
town from any source. He shall, on or before the first
day of April in each year ,report to the clerk of the board
a truelistof allthe taxesand moneys receivedand paid
outby him . He shall,when requiredby the board,report
to them the condition of the treasury, and shall at all
times,on reasonable notice,be ready for a settlement of
his accounts with the board , who shall,at any time ,have
power to demand a settlement of him .
17. That there shall be appointed annually by the
board of trustees of said town an assessor . No one shall
be eligibleto said officewho is not qualifiedto vote for

92 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
trustees of the town . He shall take an oath before en-
tering upon the discharge of his duties similar to that
required of the county assessor. It shall be the duty of
the assessorto callupon allpersons liableto be taxed ,or
theiragents, if known to him ,and make out a true listof
theirtaxable property,with the value thereof, which list
shall be made upon the oath of the owner or his agent ,
to be administered by the assessor. Said listsshallbe so
taken as to include all real estate and every species of
personal property subject to taxation for State revenue
purposes situatedin said town ,exclusiveof household fur-
niture,owned on the 10th day of January of the year for
which the assessment is made , and all male residentsof
the town at that date over twenty -one years old. If any
person shall refuse to give a statement of his property ,
under oath , the assessor shall make out a list from the
best information he can procure . If there be any estate
within the town , the owners of which are absent or un-
known , or have no known resident agent , the assessor
shallreportthe factsspecificallyon his lists, and the
board shallfixthe valuation of such property ,and cause
itto be appended to the assessor'slists. Two complete
copiesof saidlists,arranged in alphabeticalorder,are to
be returned by the assessorto said board on or before the
firstday of May ,with the certificateof the assessorto
each . Upon the return of the assessor'sliststhe trustees
shallgive notice that any person or persons who may feel
themselves aggrieved by an excessive valuation may ap-
pearbeforea board ofsupervisors,who shallbe appointed
each year to supervise the lists,at some time on or before
the firstday of June of each year ,and upon satisfactory
evidence presented to them they may change the valua-
tion. Said supervisorsshallalsochange the valuationof
any other property which they may consider erroneous .
§18 . That the police court of Harrodsburg shall re-
main . That thereshallbe electedon the firstMonday in
August , 1882, and every fourth year thereafter, by the
qualified voters of said town , a " Police Judge of Har-
rodsburg." That saidpolicejudge shallbe commissioned

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 93
as such ,and before he enters upon the discharge of the
duties of his office, shalltake an oath faithfullyand im-
partiallyto dischargethe same ,togetherwith such other
general oathsas likepublicofficersare requiredby stat-
ute to take . His term of officeshall begin on the first
Monday in September afterhiselection. No person shall
be eligibleto the officeof police judge unless he be over
twenty -one years of age ,and shallhave been a citizen
of the town for one year next preceding his election.
1. Said policecourt shallbe holden quarterlyin the
town of Harrodsburg , at such times and places as the
judge thereof shall appoint , for the trialof civilcases,
and shall be known as the " Police Court of Harrods-
burg ;" and itshallbe always open for the trialof crimi-
nal and penal cases, and cases for the breach of town
ordinances .
2. Said court shallbe governed in itsrules of pro-
cedure and practice in civiland criminal cases by the
Code of Practice of Kentucky , and the amendments
which have been or shall be made thereto; and appeals
from itsjudgments may be taken as prescribed in said
Code to the circuit court .
3. Said court shall have jurisdictionwithin the limits
of the town of Harrodsburg of allcauses,civil,criminal,
and penal, in which justicesof the peace or county
judges have jurisdictionin theirrespectivecourts. In
allpenal and criminalcasesthe saidpolicejudge shall
have thejurisdictionof two justicesof the peace. In all
misdemeanors and violationsof by -laws and ordinances
of said town ,said court shallhave exclusivejurisdiction.
Said court shall be a court of record, and the police
judge shallact as clerk of said court, and issue allneces-
sary precepts, writs,and summons . He shallkeep a cor-
rect record of the proceedings of said court,and a duly
certifiedor attestedcopy of the same ,and of any record
in said court,shallbe evidence in all the courts of this
Commonwealth .
4. Said courtshallhave power to punish contempts by
fineor imprisonment,not exceeding fivedollarsfineand
one day'simprisonment.

94 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
5. The process of said court shall be directed to and
executed by the marshal or any peace officerof Mercer
county, or any other county within the State. It shall
issue in the name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
and may go intoany county of the Commonwealth ,and
the officerto whom itmay be directed,in any town or
county withinthe State,may executeit,or give authority
to so execute itby indorsement on the process. The per-
son so authorized shallhave the rightto execute it,and
to have the same fees thereforthat are allowed by law
to the marshal .
6. That all fines and forfeitures recovered in said court
are hereby granted to said town , and shallbe collected
by the marshal or otherofficerto whom capiasprofineor
execution isdirected, and paid by him to the town treas-
urer. But before execution or other finalprocess issues
the defendant may pay the fine or forfeitureto the judge
of said court,who shall pay the same to the treasurerof
the town .
7. That the defendant shall have the rightto replevy
for three months any judgment ,fine, or forfeiturein said
police court before execution or other finalprocess there-
in,by giving bond ,with good security,in the same manner
as allowed by law in similar cases within the jurisdiction
of other courts of this Commonwealth .
8. That a return of " not found " on a capias pro fine,
and of " no propertyfound" on an executionissued on
any judgment of the police court, shall authorize an
equitableproceeding in favor of the trusteesof Harrods-
burg against the estate of the defendant ,for the purpose
of enforcing satisfactionin the same manner that a return
of " no property found " authorizes such proceedings in
civil cases under the Code of Practice of the State .
9. That any person convicted of a breach of an ordi-
nance of the town of Harrodsburg , and adjudged to pay
a fine,in default of payment shallbe confined in the jail
or work -house , and kept at hard labor at such work as
the board of trustees may prescribe, on the streets of
Harrodsburg , in the work -house , or elsewhere , under

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 95
such regulations as the trustees may prescribe,until said
fine and costs are paid ,at the rate of one day's confine-
ment and labor for each dollar of the fine. All persons
sentencedto imprisonment by thejudgment of saidcourt
for breach of the ordinances of the town may be assigned
to labor in like manner as those imprisoned for non -pay-
ment of fines: Provided,That under thisact a day's labor
shall not exceed eight hours.
10. That the fees of the jailerfor persons confined in
jail by order of the police court of Harrodsburg shallbe
paid by the county of Mercer .
11. That the fees of the police judge of said town ,
whether acting as judge , clerk, or otherwise, except as
herein speciallyprovided ,shall be the same ,and be col-
lected in a similar manner as the fees of similar officers
under the statute law of the State as it now exists, or
may hereafter be in force; and fees and costs shall be
taxed in said court to the same extent , in the same man-
ner, and under the same regulations, as other courts of
thisState having similarjurisdiction.
12. That all prosecutions for a violation of the town
ordinances shall be in the name of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky ,and inure to the board of trusteesof Harrods-
burg ; and said town shallbe entitledto allfinesand for-
feituresrecovered in such cases, and have the same right
to enforce the collectionof the same ,by due processof
law and equitableproceedings,on a returnof no property
found , as is provided above in other cases.
13. That the said police judge shall have the same
power to issueallwrits,tryallcauses,requireand take
all bonds , and enter alljudgments in civil, penal , and
criminal causes , as is given to police judges in similar
cases by the Civiland Criminal Codes of this Common-
wealth as they now exist,or may hereafter be amended ,
though saidpowers be not expressly statedin thischarter.
14. That the chairman of the board of trustees of said
town shallhave power toperformthedutiesof thepolice
judge in allcriminal,penal ,and civilcases,wherein from
vacancy , absence , or any other cause ,the police judge

96 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
does not or cannot act,and he shall be entitledto the
same fees for his services .
15. That the trusteesof said town shallnot be respon-
sible for costs in any penal cases in said court,except
such costs as are collected from the defendant in a case .
16.That ifany person be brought before said police
court in a drunken and disorderlycondition,it shallbe
the duty of the court to refuseto try him in that condition,
but the court may make an order sending him to prison
not lessthan fivenor more than twenty -four hours before
trial. If a person be arrestedat night in a drunken and
disorderlycondition,the marshal of the town shall,with-
out process,placehim in jail,the watch -house or work-
house,during the nightforsafe-keeping,untilhe can be
brought beforethe policejudge on the ensuing day for
trial .
17. In criminaland penal casesthe policejudge shall
be entitledto charge the following fees,viz: for a war-
rant for a violationof the criminal and penal laws of the
Commonwealth , or the by -laws and ordinances of the
town ,one dollar; for swearing a jury and presidingat
the trialin any such case, fiftycents; for a recognizance
or other bond ,tortycents; for a recognizance to keep the
peace,to be paid by the applicanttherefor,and taxed as
costs against the defendant , if convicted, fiftycents;.for
an order of commitment in any case,fiftycents; for issu-
ing a subpœna , twenty cents; for swearing witnesses,
fivecents each . All other fees of said judge shallbe the
same as are by law allowed to the circuitcourtclerkor
judge of the quarterlycourt, or the county judge, for
similar services .
§ 19. That there shall be electedfor said town , every
four years,and at the same time and place and in the
same manner thatthe policejudge iselected,a prosecut-
ing attorneyfor the town ,who shalltake an oath to
faithfullydischarge the duties of his office. He must be
a regular practicingattorney, a residentof the town one
year next preceding his election, and shallhold his office
fouryears,or untilhissuccessorisduly electedand qual-
ified .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 97
1. It shallbe the duty of the town attorney to perform
allprofessionalservicesincidenttohisoffice; toprosecute
all offenders against the by -laws and ordinances of the
town ,and all statutoryoffensesfor which warrants have
issued from the policecourt, and to give written opinions
upon allsubjectssubmittedto him by the board of trus-
tees, or by any of its committees . It shall also be his
duty to instituteproceedings for the enforcement and for-
feitureof recognizances and bail-bonds ,and the enforce-
ment and collectionof alljudgments against offendersof
record in said court ; and for his services he shall be enti-
tled to thirty-three per cent. of what may be collectedon
said fines and forfeitures: Provided, however, That in all
jury trials,in lieu of said attorney receiving a portion of
the fine, there shallbe a fee of fivedollars taxed against
the defendant for the benefit of said attorney in every
case where there is a conviction .
2. In addition to the fees above allowed to the town
attorney,he shall receive such additional compensation
from said town as the trustees thereof shall consider him
entitled,to be paid out of the treasuryof saidtown .
§20 . There shallalsobe electedby the qualifiedvoters
thereof,in the manner and at the time prescribedby law
for the electionof sheriffsin thisCommonwealth , a mar-
shal for said town ,who ,beforehe entersupon the dis-
charge of the duties of his said office,shalltake the oaths
usually required by law for similar officers,and shallgive
bond with sufficientsecurity,to be approved by thetrus-
teesof said town ,conditionedfaithfullyto discharge all
the dutiesof said office,and to pay over to such persons,
and at such times as they may be respectivelyentitledto
the same ,allmoney that may come to his hands as mar-
shal. This bond may contain any otherconditionwhich
the board may thinkproper to require,and shallbe made
payable to the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
1. It shallbe the duty of the town marshal to collect
all taxes due said town , and all executions and other
demands which may be put into hishands forcollection,
LOC . L. , VOL . II- 7

98 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and pay over the same to whoever shallbe legallyen-
titledthereto,under the same rules and regulations re-
quired by the statuteof sheriffsand constablesin the
collection of taxes and other demands . For a failure to
discharge any of the duties of his office,he shall be sub-
ject to the same proceedings which by law may be had
in thisCommonwealth against sheriffsand constables in
similar cases .
2. All bonds executed by said marshal for the faithful
performance of his duties,or those executed by all col-
lectors appointed by the board , shall be filed with the
archives of said board ,and recorded in full on the minute
book of said board of trustees .
3. The marshal shall be entitled to the same fees and
commissions for collecting the town tax which are al-
lowed to sheriffsfor collectingthe county levy,and they
may be increasedby order of the board ifthey think
proper . The marshal shallin allother cases be allowed
the same fees which are allowed to constables for similar
services .
4. That the town marshal , or any collector of taxes
who may have been appointed ,may , with the consent of
the board of trustees,appoint a deputy or deputies,who
shall take the oath of office, and thereby be invested
with allthepowers and authorityof the saidmarshal or
collector, and the said marshal or collectorso appointing
him shall be liable on his official bond for all the acts of
his deputy .
5. That the town marshal shall be chief of police of
said town ,and shallrender allrequired police services.
§ 21. That all laws, ordinances, regulations, and by-
laws now in force in said town , and not inconsistent
with this act, shall remain in full force until altered,
modified, or repealed by the board of trustees,and those
inconsistentherewith are hereby repealed.
§22. That thepresenttrustees,and allotherofficersof
the town ,shall continue in officeuntil their successors
are duly electedand qualifiedunder thisact, and they
are hereby investedwith allthe powers,rights,and priv-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 99
ilegeswhich the trusteesand other officersto be elected
under thisact will possess.
§ 23 , That this act shall not invalidate any legal act
done by the board of trusteesof Harrodsburg,or by its.
officersprior to itspassage .
Approved April 1 , 1882.
CHAPTER 785 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Raleigh River Bottom Land Fence Company .
WHEREAS , The section of land in Union county , Ken-
tucky,lying in the bottoms oppositeShawneetown ,Illi-
nois, is subject to overflow from the Ohio river; and
whereas ,the laterisein the Ohio riverhas swept away
most of the division, cross, and other fencing on these
lands; whereas,itis desirable,in point of convenience,
economy ,and necessity,that one common fence should
be builtand kept up by the land-owners around their
farms in said bottom ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That John M. Lambert ,Wm . B. Henshaw ,George
L. Dixon ,J. V. Cromwell ,and Mrs. Mary F. Burbank ,
and such other persons in their stead as may hereafter
succeed to the ownership of the lands now owned by
them (same lying and being in the county of Union ,on
the Ohio river,in the Stateof Kentucky ,above the ferry
opposite Shawneetown ,Illinois, and extending nearly to
Raleigh,in said county),be,and they are hereby,consti-
tuted a body -politicand corporate ,under the name and
style of the " Raleigh River Bottom Land Fence Com-
pany ," with power to sue and be sued ,to contract and
be contracted with, for the specificpurpose as set forth
herein,to-wit: to build and rebuildand keep in repair,
from year toyear ,one common fence (post plan ,plank or
rail),inclosingtheir lands hereinbefore mentioned : Pro-
videdfurther,That theyshallhave power to contractwith
otherparties,so faras may be necessary,forthe privilege

100 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of building a fence across their lands so as to inclose
those mentioned .
§2 . They shallelectof theirnumber a chairman,sec-
retary, and treasurer, a majority of the partiesabove
named to constitute a quorum for such purpose ,or for
the transaction of any business whatever . In the event
of a divisionand sale of the lands inclosed by the com-
mon fence,increasing the number of partiesin thiscor-
poration by virtue of the firstsection of thisact,it shall
stillrequire a majority of all the parties to constitutea
quorum forthe purpose of business,and saidpartiesmay
vote on allmatters by authorized proxies.
§3 . They shallhave power to pass any rules and regu-
lations,and to impose finesand penaltiesthey may deem
proper,to insure the success of the object of thisact.
§4 . It shall be the duty of the secretary to make all
contractsinvolvingthe expenditure of money , but no
such contractshallbe binding upon the company until
submitted for theirapproval, and countersignedunder
their order by the chairman , a record of the yeas and
nays being kept by the secretary; and when it becomes
necessary to meet the expense incurred by any contract
of the company ,it shall be the duty of the secretary to
assess a specifictax upon the parties named in this act,
or hereafter becoming parties by virtue of this act, in
proportion to the number of acres of cleared land on the
tractsowned by each ,sufficientin the aggregate to cover
said expense ,and to deliver a copy of such assessment or
assessments,over his officialsignature,to the treasurer.
§ 5. It shall be the duty of the treasurer to collectall
assessments made and delivered to him by the secretary
from the parties against whom such assessments have
been made ,and he ishereby grantedthe power to collect
the same by law ; and to secure the payment of any such
assessment,the company shallhave a lienupon the lands
inclosed by the common fence against which the assess-
ment has been made , which lien may be enforced by any
court of competent jurisdictionin the county of Union .
It shallfurther be the duty of the treasurer to collectall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 101
finesand assessmentsimposed under the power granted
in section3 of thisact,and he shallhave the power to
collectthe same by legal proceedings .
§ 6. Itshallfurtherbe lawful forthiscompany to con-
tract for the purchase and sale of any fences or parts of
fencesthat may be alreadybuiltupon the routeof the
proposed common fence,such purchase or sale to be in
keeping with the spiritof thisact.
§ 7. The firstannual election in this company shall
take place on the 11th day of April, 1882,at which time
there shall be chosen a chairman , secretary,and treas-
urer,each of whom shall hold officetilltheirsuccessors
are qualified, and the next election shall thereafter be
held on the second Tuesday of October , 1882,and there-
afterannually at such time as may be prescribed by reso-
lutionof the company . Any vacancy occurringin the
officeof chairman ,secretary, or treasurer,shallbe filled
at any fixedor calledmeeting by regularelection,time
of holding meetings to be fixed by the by -laws of the
company . The chairman may atany time calla meeting
of the company by notice to itsmembers . The secretary
shallkeep a recordof the proceedingsof allmeetings.
§ 8. Be itfurtherenacted, That thiscompany may ,by a
vote of a majorityof itsmembers ,consolidatewith that
known as the " Union County River Bottom Land Fence
Company ," a majorityof the lattercompany assenting
thereto; and the consolidatedcompanies shallbe known
by the name of the lattercompany ,and shallhave allthe
powers,rights,and privilegessetforthin theirrespective
charters .
Approved April 1,1882 .
