CHAPTER 787 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof Eastern Kentucky Railway Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the charter of the Eastern Kentucky Railway
Company ,approved January 18th,1870,be,and the same
is hereby , amended as follows : The said Eastern Ken-
tucky Railway Company shallhave power ,and itishere-
by authorized,to extend itsmain line of railway from or
near its present terminus at Willard , in Carter county ,
by any practicableroute,to the Lickingriver,at or near
West Liberty,in Morgan county, or Salyersville,in Ma-
goffincounty .
§2 . The said Eastern Kentucky Railway Company is
hereby authorized and empowered to issueincome bonds
to an amount not exceeding three millions of dollars,
which bonds may bear interestat a rate not exceeding
seven per cent. per annum , non -cumulative , payable
semi-annually; saidinterest,ifearned,to be paid before
the payment of any dividend upon the preferred or com-
mon stock of said company .
§3 . The said Eastern Kentucky Railway Company is
furtherauthorized and empowered to issuepreferredstock.
to an amount not exceeding three millionsof dollars,upon
which dividends not exceeding six per cent.per annum
may be paid beforethe payment of any dividend upon
the common stock of said company .
§4. The said Eastern Kentucky Railway Company
may ,by vote of a majorityof itsstockholders,issueany
part of said income bonds and preferredstock author-
ized by the foregoing sections,and may use the same in
settlement of any bonded or other indebtedness of said
company : Provided, The bond -holders or other creditors
of saidcompany shallagree to and with said company
in such exchange or settlement.
§5 . Any extensionof the liensof said Eastern Ken-
tucky Railway Company , made and completed within
fiveyears from the passage of thisact,shallbe exempted
from allState and localtaxation fora period of ten years

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 105
from the time when each sectionor part of sectionof ten
milesshallbe completed and open forregulartraffic.
§ 6. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 6, 1882 .
CHAPTER 788 .
AN ACT to authorizeGrant Green , Fayette Hewitt,James W. Tate, James
M. Withrow , and Samuel B. Churchill, to examine and correcterrors, if
any , in a former settlement with Newton Craig .
WHEREAS , An act was passed by the Legislature,ap-
proved March 7th, 1868,by which the Commissioners of
the Sinking Fund were authorized and directed to inves-
tigate and audit and settlethe accounts of N. Craig , late
Keeper of the Penitentiary, and to reportthe resultof
such investigation to the Legislature at itsnext meeting ,
all of which was fullyand carefullydone to the entire
satisfactionof all concerned ; and whereas , at the next
sittingof the Legislature,another set of commissioners
were appointed to reinvestigatethe same accounts ,who ,
after a careful and full examination of said accounts ,
reportedthat they fullyagree with the Commissioners of
the Sinking Fund in their report on the matters referred
to both of said commissioners ; and whereas ,itisclaimed
thatthe lastset of commissioners, afterthey had fully
concurred with the Commissioners of the Sinking Fund
in theirreport,erredin bringingintotheirlastsettlement
several items which had been paid and settledten or
twelve years previous,and of which payment they were
not apprised, the papers in the case not being before
them ; for remedy whereof ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the following commissioners who did investi-
gatesaidaccounts,namely : Grant Green ,FayetteHewitt,
James M. Withrow , James W. Tate , and Samuel B.
Churchill,be, and they are hereby , re-appointed ,and are
authorized and directed to examine said settlement ; and

106 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ifitisascertainedthatan errorhas been committed,they
are authorizedand directedto correctthe same . The
said commissioners are hereby re-appointed and vested
with the same powers given by said act,and to proceed
in allrespectsin the premises as directedby said act,ap-
proved February 22, 1870: Provided, Said Craig pay all
expenses incurred by the investigation contemplated by
the operations of this act.
§2 . This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 6 , 1882.
CHAPTER 789 .
AN ACT to suppress the sale of intoxicatingliquorsin voting precincts
numbers 5 and 8 ,in Boyd county.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the question whether spirituous, vinous , or
malt liquors,or any other intoxicating beverage ,shallbe
sold within the territorycomprised in voting precincts
numbers 5 and 8 ,in Boyd county ,including the cityof
Ashland ,shallbe submitted to the qualifiedvoters of said
districtsin the following manner : The county court of
Boyd county shall,upon a written petition signed by at
least twenty legal voters, being not less than ten from
each precinct,at the next or same term of court at or
after he receives said petition, make an order on the
county court order-book , directing that an election be
held at the voting places in said precincts on the third
Saturday after the date of said order, for the purpose of
taking the sense of the legally qualified voters therein
upon the question aforesaid.
§2. The courtshall,at the same time,appointforeach
precinct two discreetvoters as judges , and a clerk,who
shallbe qualifiedas required by section 4 ,article3, chap .
ter 33 ,General Statutes,before entering on the duties of
theiroffices,whose dutiesand powers in takingthe vote
under thisact shallbe the same as thoseprescribedfor

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 107
like officersby said article3,chapter33,General Stat-
utes ,so far as applicable,except that in the case of disa-
greement between the judges , the clerk of the election
shall act as umpire .
§3 . It shallbe the duty of the county court clerk to
give to the sheriffof the county a certifiedcopy of the
orderof thejudge of thecounty court,asitappears upon
his order-book , within two days after said order is made ,
who shall,withinfivedays thereafter,notifyeach of said
officersappointed as aforesaidof theirappointment by a
written notice. It shallalso be the duty of the sheriffto
have the order published in all the newspapers that may
at the time be published in Ashland for at leasttwo weeks
before the election .
§4 . The clerkof the Boyd county court shallprepare
two poll-books,one for each precinct,with one column
headed " For liquor," and another"Against liquor," and
deliver same to sheriff, who shallhave the same ready at
the places of voting for recording the votes.
§5 . It shall be the duty of the clerksof the electionto
propound to each voterwho may vote thequestion,"Are
you in favor of the sale of spirituous, vinous , or malt
liquors,or otherintoxicatingbeverage,in precinctsNos .
5 and 8,of Boyd county,includingthe cityof Ashland,"
and his vote shall be entered for or against it, as he
directs .
§ 6. No person shallvote at said electionwho does not
reside within the territorycomprised within the precincts
aforesaid,and who would not be a legal voter at a gen-
eral election under the Constitution and laws of this State .
§7 . The pollsshallbe open for voting from six o'clock
in the morning untilseven o'clock in the evening .
§ 8. The sheriffshallattend at the place of electionby
himselfor deputy,keep order,and receivefrom the offi-
cersof the electionthe poll-books,and returnthem with-
in twenty -four hours thereafterto the clerk of the county
court of Boyd county ,who shall keep them on filein his
officeuntilcompared ,as directed in the next succeeding
section .

108 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 9. Said poll-books shallbe examined and compared
by the county judge, county clerk, and sheriffof Boyd
county , in the manner and time prescribed by section 1,
article5,chapter33,of theGeneral Statutes,in reference
to elections .
§ 10. If it shall be found that a majority of the legal
votes cast at the election hereinbefore provided for were
given against the sale of spirituous, vinous , or malt
liquors,or otherintoxicatingbeverages,within saidvot-
ing precincts,by summing up the votesof both precincts
for and against, it shall be the duty of the examining
board to certifythat fact,which certificateshallbe deliv-
ered to the clerk of the county court,and by him safely
kept until the next regular term of the county court,at
which term the judge thereof shallhave the same spread
upon the order-book of his court; and said entryof the
certificatein the order-book,or a certifiedcopy thereof,
shallbe prima facieevidence in all proceedingsunder
this act .
§ 11. After the entry of the certificateof the examin-
ing board in the county court order-book , as above pro-
vided for,itshall be unlawful for any person to sellany
spirituous, vinous, or malt liquors,or a mixture of either,
or any intoxicating beverage , within the territorycom-
prised in said voting precincts five and eight; and any
person who shallsellany such liquorswithin said pre-
cincts shall,upon conviction,be fined a sum of not less
than twenty -five dollars nor more than one hundred
dollarsfor each offense; and each separate sale shall be
a separate offenseunder this law .
§ 12. This act shallnot be consideredas affectingany
of the provisions of the general laws of the State or the
charter of the city of Ashland in reference to the giving
to or procuring intoxicating liquors for a minor or an
inebriate, and allpenaltiesthereinprescribedfor said
offenses,and allrightsof suitgiven to others for a viola-
tion of said laws ,shallstillbe in force as if thisact had
not been passed .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 109
§ 13. After the entry of said certificateof the examin-
ing board in the county court order-book,as provided in
section ten of thisact,any shiftor device for the purpose
of evading the provisions of this act,and the establish-
ment of resortsfor the purpose of procuring or drinking
any intoxicatingliquors,such as club-rooms , or by any
other name ,within said precincts,shall be deemed to be
unlawful ; and every sale or deliveringto,or handing out
of such intoxicating liquorsby any club-room keepers or
tender to any person shall constitute a violationof this
act, and subject the offender to the penalty announced in
section eleven .
§ 14. After the entry of the certificateof the resultof
the election,as provided in sectionten,the keepers or
tender of any resort wherein intoxicating liquors are
kept ,for the purpose of vending , giving away ,keeping
for, or issuing to,others, shall be deemed a violationof
this act, and each day or part of a day or night he so
keeps same shallbe a separate offense,and subject him
to the penalty prescribed in said section eleven thereof.
§ 15. After the entry of said certificateof the election,
as specifiedin sectionten,itshallbe unlawful for any
person to rent hispropertyto anotherfor the disposition
of the inhibitedliquors,or to use his own propertyfor
that purpose; and such use of the propertyby a lessee
shallwork an immediate forfeitureof his rightsunder the
lease; and shouldany landlordpermithispropertyto be
so used within said precincts,afterknowledge of the fact
has been brought home to him ,or his agent ,if a non -res-
ident of the State or county ,and he has a residentagent ,
without taking immediate steps in good faith to dispos-
sess such, tenant,he shall be deemed a violatorof this
act, and shall for each week he allows such tenant to
carry on such business after such knowledge , and with-
out honest effortto dispossess him ,be subject to a fine
of fiftydollars,which ,when assessed,shall be a lien on
the real estatewhere such violationoccurred,enforceable
by appropriate action in the name of the Commonwealth
in the Boyd circuitcourt.

110 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 16. If the owner of the property is a non -resident of
thisState, serviceof process upon his agent shallbe
sufficientto bring him before the court for a violation of
this act .
§ 17. The provisionsof thisact shallnot apply to the
wholesale dealer who , in good faith,and in the course of
trade,sellsby the wholesale ,nor to druggistswho qualify
themselves to do so by taking out license as provided for
them by an act,entitled" An actto furtherregulatethe
retailtrafficin spirituous,vinous ,and malt liquors,and
nostrums used as a beverage," approved May the 5th,
1882 .
§ 18. After the entry of the certificateof the examining
board on the order-book of the county court, as provided
in section 10, no license shall thereafterbe granted by
any court or body to establishsaloons or taverns, with a
privilegeof sellingsuch inhibitedliquors,or to authorize
any one to sell such inhibited liquors in any manner
within the said precincts Nos . 5 and 8 ,except as drug-
gists, as herein before provided .
§19 . This act shall be construed as cumulative legisla-
tion to suppress the sale of intoxicatingliquors,and shall
not be construed as repealing any existing law enforce-
able within the territoryembraced in said precinctsNos .
5 and 8,which has been enacted for the same purpose,
but shall stand together, and leave the election of the
remedy to the prosecution.
§20. The sheriffof Boyd county shallbe allowed
twenty dollars for the services required of him under
this act, and the county clerk of Boyd county shall be
allowed fifteendollars for his services, and the county
judge of Boyd county shallnot make the order for the
electioncontemplated under thisact untilthe persons
signingthe petitionhave depositedwith him a sufficient
sum of money to pay said officers' fees as aforesaid.
§ 21. The jurisdictionof thecourtsfor tryingoffenses
against this act shall be as regulated by law for other
violationswhere likepenaltiesas to amount of fineare
prescribed.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 111
§ 22. Should any of the officersappointed by the county
judge to hold the election herein provided for failto at-
tend for thirty minutes after the time commencing the
election, or refuse to act, the sheriffshallappoint a suit-
able person or persons to act in his or theirstead for that
election .
23. The election provided for under this act may ,
upon the requisitepetition,occur every two years there-
after, with the same effect as to this law as the firstelec-
tion thereunder. But a term of two years shallelapse
between each election .
§ 24. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 6 , 1882 .
CHAPTER 791 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Vanceburg Public School and Seminary , and to
repealtheactincorporatingthesame,entitled"An acttoincorporatethe
Vanceburg Male and Female Academy ," & c .
WHEREAS ,The white citizens and tax-payers residing
in the city of Vanceburg and common school district
number eight,Lewis county,Kentucky ,have,by means
of taxation under the provisions of the act incorporating
the Vanceburg Male and Female Academy ,referred to
above , and the severalacts amendatory thereof, pur-
chased a valuable and suitablelot of ground in the lower
end of Vanceburg ,fronting on the Ohio river and Salt
Lick creek, consistingof fiveacres, more or less, and
have built thereon a large and commodious school build-
ing , at the cost of about seven thousand dollars,besides
paying forthe lot,and being in debt thereon about six
thousand dollars, for which the trustees thereof issued
theirbonds,under the provisionsof the act,entitled"An
acttoamend an act incorporatingtheVanceburg Male
and Female Academy ," approved April6,1880,and now
being desirousto pay offsaid bonded indebtednessand
raisingan adequate fund to keep saidschoolbuildingand
ground in good order and make the same attractiveand
desirableforthepurposesintended,and furtherdesiring
to perpetuate said school building and certain reserved

112 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
portionsof the aforesaidlotforcommon schoolpurposes,
as well as the higher degrees of education ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the property heretofore known and desig-
nated as the " Vanceburg Male and Female Academy "
be ,and the same shallhereafterbe ,known and styledthe
" Vanceburg Public School and Seminary ," and shallbe
used for the purpose of teaching the common school in
and for said school district, as well as a high graded
school . The same shall be under the control and man-
agement of the trustees elected in and for said school
districteach year ,and subject to the common school laws
of the State,except as provided herein.
§ 2. The board of trustees aforesaid shall have power
and authorityto appoint a principalor presidentof the
school,and,with the adviceof said principal,such other
teachers or professors as may be necessary to instruct
the pupils of said school and seminary in the arts and
sciences,and in all necessary and useful branches of a
thorough and liberal education ; the object, aim , and
design being not only a common school under the gen-
eral laws of this Commonwealth for the education of all
itswhite children within the school ages residingin said
district,but also to enable the trustees and teachers there-
of to raise the standard of education , and to teach all
personsor childrenthatmay attendsaidschoolthe higher
branches of education not required by the common school
law of the State to be taught in schoolsexclusivelyunder ,
and regulated by , said school law .
§ 3. The trusteesof said school and seminary , together
with theprincipalteacherthereof,shallhave power and
authority to regulate the course of studies,and grade the
school children in departments and classes, as to them
may seem best; and may , with the other teachers or pro-
fessors, confer upon the graduates of said school and
seminary such diplomas,honors,and degrees,as evidence
of scholarship,as are usualin the high schoolsand col-
leges in this Commonwealth .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 113
§ 4. The trusteesof said school and seminary , together
with the advice and assistanceof the principal or presi-
dent thereof, from time to time, as they may deem ex-
pedient, prescribeforms, rules and regulationsfor the
government thereof, as also a uniform system of text-
books and course of study for the scholars attending the
same . The said trustees shall have the right and au-
thoritynot only to employ the principaland subordinate
teachers , as indicated above , but also to discharge any of
saidteacherswhen such discharge shall,in theiropinion
and best judgment ,be demanded for the best interestsof
the school .
§ 5. All white childrenwithin the school ages prescribed
by the common school law of this State, and residing
within said schooldistrict,shallhave the rightand privi.
lege to attend said school and seminary free of charge
forat leastfivemonths in each year ,the said school being
entitledto and receiving the public school money coming
to said school districteach year under the provisions of
the common school law of the State ; and that after said
free school term shallexpire,the said trusteesand teach-
ers may carryon in said school buildinga continuation
of the school and seminary, at which allpersons who
desire so to do may attend the same , under such rules,
regulations,and terms as may be prescribed by said trus-
teesand principal,they having the rightto regulatethe
term ,pay , and kinds of study as to them may seem best
and proper : Provided, The same shallnot be of an in-
fidel or denominational character .
§ 6. That the lot of ground purchased of the Cains
heirs,situatein the lower end of Vanceburg ,and by them
conveyed to the trusteesof the Vanceburg Male and Fe-
male Academy by deed recorded in deed -book " Z ," page
406 , Lewis county court records,be ,and the same is now ,
set apart and dedicated for educational purposes , and
shall belong to said city and common school districtin
which same issituate,and so much of said lot as includes
the buildings now being inclosed by fence,together with
LOC .L., VOL .II- 8
,

114 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
at least sixtyfeet of ground on the river bank to Salt
Lick creek, and thence down to low water -mark , shall
forever be held and dedicated for the purposes aforesaid,
and shallnever be soldby the trusteesaforesaid,in whom
the titleisnow vested, without an enabling act hereafter
of the Legislature of Kentucky ,and to them made mani-
fest by petition or otherwise,anything in any other act
heretofore passed in relationto the " Vanceburg Male and
Female Academy " to the contrary notwithstanding ; but

heretofore made ,or that may hereafter be made ,of such
portions of said originallot,not reserved herein , as was

emy ," approved February 14, 1873,the object now being

Second street to the Ohio river, with the two alleys on
eitherside,and the frontage aforesaid,allalong the river
bank to Salt Lick creek, and thence to low water -mark
on said river .
§7 . That a tax of one dollar on the one hundred dollars'
worth of taxablepropertysubjectto taxationfor State
revenue purposes,situateand being within said cityof
Vanceburg and common school districtNo. 8,owned by
white tax-payers ,be , and the same is hereby , authorized
to be levied, assessed,and collectedforthe year 1882,and
each year thereafter,untilthebonded debt createdby the
trustees of said school district under the amended act
aforesaid,approved April6th, 1880,be fullypaid offand

edness,and which taxation shall then cease. The said
tax shall be ascertained by the trustees of said school
districtfrom the county assessor'sbooks made out and
returnedeach year to thecounty courtclerk'soffice,and
they may listand forcethe payment of said taxes as
county and State taxes are made and collected; and the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 115
§ 8. That said trusteesshallhave the rightto retain
from fines,liquorlicense,or other sums of money paid
over to them each year,by virtueof certainlocalactsor
laws ,enough of said finesor fund to pay for the keeping
said school buildings in good order and condition,as well
as to beautifyand keep clean and in good conditionthe
lotand grounds belongingthereto; and theyshallgive an
account of same in theirannual reportand settlement.
§ 9. This act shalltake effectand be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved April 6, 1882 .
CHAPTER 792 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof G. H. Albright, sheriffof Rockcastlecounty.
WHEREAS ,G. H. Albright,sheriffof Rockcastle county ,
did, in May , 1880,convey Grove C. Kennedy from the
Madison county jail to the penitentiary, and it was be-
lievedthat therewas danger of the said Kennedy being
rescued from the sheriffwhile being transportedto the
penitentiary, and that the Rockcastle circuitcourt was
not then in session to allow said Albright a guard for
Kennedy ; and whereas , the said Albright furnished a
guard at his own expense forthat purpose,and thathis
expenses, amounting to said sum of fortydollars, was
paid by said Albright; now ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditorof PublicAccounts be,and he is
hereby,directedto draw hiswarrant on theTreasury in
favorof saidG. H. Albrightforthe sum of fortydollars,
payable out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise
appropriated,being forthe expenses so incurredby said
Albrightforfurnishingthe guard and transportingGrove
C. Kennedy from the jailin Madison county to thepeni-
tentiaryin Frankfort.
§2 . This act shalltake effectand be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved April6,1882.

uary 27, 1880,be ,and the same are hereby ,declared legal

CHAPTER 795 .
AN ACT to amend and reduce into one the variousacts in regard to the
town of Whitesville, in Daviess county,Kentucky .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
ARTICLE I.
§1 . That the town of Whitesville,as laid off and de-
scribedin theplat of said town recordedin the Daviess
county clerk'soffice,shallbe known , and is hereby de-
clared,the extent and limitof said town ; and is hereby
incorporatedand declareda body -politic,with thepowers
and privilegesvested in said town by thisact, under the
name and styleof the " Town of Whitesville."
§2 . The prudential,fiscal,and municipalconcerns are
hereby vested in five trustees, who shall be elected annu-
ally,on the firstMonday in August,and shallhold their
officesfor one year ,untiltheirsuccessorsare elected and
qualified. Said trusteesshallqualifyand enterupon the
discharge of theirduties on or before the firstday of Sep-
tember succeeding their election . Said trustees shall
qualifyby takingthe oath of officeas prescribedby the
Constitution of Kentucky .
§ 3. Should any member of the said board of trustees
die,resign,or remove outsidethe corporate limitsof said
town during the term for which he was elected , the re-
maining members shall elect a successor to fillthe va-
cancy occasioned by such death ,resignationor removal .
§ 4. No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof trustee
of saidtown who isnot a housekeeper in saidtown ,and
been a residentthereof for twelve months preceding the
election .
§ 5. All qualifiedvoters of the State of Kentucky who
have resided in said town for six months preceding the
electionat which they offerto vote,and allqualifiedvoters
of Daviess county who own real estatein said town ,are
declared the qualifiedvoters of Whitesville: Provided,
That no such person shallbe entitledto vote who cannot
exhibithistax collector'sreceiptfor all taxes assessed
againsthim under the provisionsof thisact.

118 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§6 . Said board of trustees shall elect one of their num-
ber chairman of said board , who shall hold his office for
the term for which he iselected. He shallbe styledchair-
man of the board of trustees of Whitesville .
§7 . The board of trustees of Whitesville are vested
with power to make and receive all necessary convey-
ances in relationto said town . They are hereby vested
with power and control over the squares, streets, and
alleysin said town ,or any which may be opened here-
after. They shall direct the opening , improvement , or
repairingof the same in such manner as they may deem
best. The said board of trusteesshallhave allnecessary
sidewalks,with suitablecurbing, constructed, improved,
or repaired in said town , and shallassessthe cost thereof
against the owner or owners of the lot fronting on side-
walks thus constructed, improved,or repaired, and the
cost thereof shall be a lien on the lot .
§ 8. Said board of trustees are vested with power ,
first,to make all necessary ordinances for the regulation
and good government of said town ; second ,to pass all
necessary ordinances for the suppression of riots, routs,
and breaches of the peace ,affrays, unlawful assemblies,
riotous or disorderly conduct , and drunkenness in said
town ,and to provideforthe punishment of personsen-
gaged in saidoffenses,and fixthe penalties; third,to fix
penaltiesfor the violationof any of the ordinances of
said town in such sums as they may deem best,not,how-
ever,in any case to exceed one hundred dollars for any
one offense ; fourth , to declare by ordinances what are
nuisances within said town , and by their order to direct
same to be abolished; and they may ,by ordinances,im-
pose finesupon persons guilty of creating or continuing
nuisances in said town .
§ 9. It shall be the duty of said board of trustees to
appoint a treasurer, clerk, assessor,and an attorney for
saidtown ,and to provideby ordinancesforthe compen-
sation of each . They shall require bond from clerk,
treasurer , and assessor for the faithful discharge of their
respectiveduties,in additionto which said officersshall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 119
take the oath of office,as prescribedby the Constitution
of Kentucky .
ARTICLE II .
§1 . The board of trustees of Whitesville have power
to levy and collecta tax upon the property in said town ,
not to exceed in any one year fiftycentson the one hun-
dred dollars worth of property so assessed, and to levy
and collect a poll-tax on the male inhabitants of said
town , not to exceed three dollars on each male inhabi-
tant of said town over twenty -one years of age .
§2 . Said board of trustees are vested with power to
license and tax hotels, coffee-houses , and saloons, or
houses or places wherein spirituous, vinous , or malt
liquorsare sold or given away in said town ,or within
one mile of corporate limits: Provided, That the tax to
keep hotels, coffee-houses, saloons, or houses or places
wherein spirituous, vinous, or malt liquors are sold or
given away ,shall in no case be less than one hundred
dollars , nor more than two hundred dollars in addition .
to the State tax : And provided further, That the license
contemplated in thissectionshallnot apply to druggists
or pharmacists selling drugs and medicines under the
existing laws of the State of Kentucky . But all per-
sons claiming to sellas druggist or pharmacist ,who may
sell, give, or oause to be given any spirituous, vinous ,
or malt liquors, unless it be upon a prescription of a
regular practicingphysician, shall forfeitand pay to
the town of Whitesville any sum that the board of trus-
tees may fix by ordinance , if not less than twenty nor
more than thirtydollarsforeach offense; and any person
or persons claiming to sellspiritsformedical purposes
shallkeep a correct register of all prescriptions, giving
number and date of same . This registershall, on de-
mand of the board of trustees,be subject to theirinspec-
tion. Any person or persons refusingto comply herewith
shall be fined any sum the board of trustees may desig-
nate by ordinance ,if not lessthan twenty nor more than
fiftydollars. The said fines and forfeituresshallbe re-

120 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
coverablebeforethe policejudge of Whitesville,as other
fines and forfeitures are collected .
§3 . The chairman and board of trustees are vested
with power to suppress all tippling-houses and allretail-
ing of spirituousliquors without license, and to fine all
who may violatetheirby -laws in relationthereto in any
sum not exceeding fiftydollars nor lessthan twenty -five
dollars for each offense,which may be recovered before
the policejudge of said town . They shall have the
power , at any time, to suspend indefinitely, or for a
limited time , any hotel or coffee-house or other license
they may grant, whenever they are satisfiedthat the
hotel, coffeehouse keeper ,or other licensed whisky ven-
dor ,has permitted unlawful gaming in his house or upon
his premises, or has allowed any disorderlyor indecent
conduct to be practiced or committed in his house or
upon his premises : Provided, That no license shall be
suspended until said hotel or coffee-house keeper , or
whisky vendor shall have at least fivedays' notice, and
have been permitted to show cause, if any , why his
licenseshould not be suspended .
§4 . Said board of trustees are vested with power to
licenseand tax,and to provided by ordinance forlicensing
and taxing ,within the limits of said town , all sporting
galleries, places of public resortfor sport or pleasure,
shooting galleries,all auction sales, shows , and exhibi-
tions,liveryand sale stables,omnibuses ,hacks ,carriages,
and allother vehiclesrun in saidtown forpay or hire, and
shall by ordinances fix the tax for such licensein such
sums as they may deem proper. They shall, by ordi-
nance , enforce the taking out said licenseby fixingfines
and penalties against allpersons failingor refusing to do
so : Provided,No licenseshallbe granted for any games or
gaming inconsistentwith the laws of thisCommonwealth .
All shows which exhibit within one half mile of said
town shallpay tax hereinprovidedfor.
§5 . The assessor shall be annually appointed on or
before the firstday of March ,and shallimmediately qual-
ify and enterupon the dischargeof hisduty. He shall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 121
callupon each resident of said town for a listof his,her,.
or their property. He shall administer an oath to each
person similar to the oath administered by the county
assessor as to the truth of his,her,or theirlist,and shall
value the property so listed. He shall make out the lists
of property in said town owned by non -residentsthereof,
calling on their agents, if they have any , and if none ,
then from such information as he possessesor may obtain ;
or,if thereis no agent ,he may make such listsfrom the
assessor'sbooks of the preceding year , and said property
shall be valued in the same manner as the property of
residentsof said town ; but the name of the agent, if
there is one ,shallbe placed upon the assessor'sbook , as
well as the name of the owner .
§6 . The listsof property and the valuation thereof
shallbe made as of the 20th of April of each year ,and the
assessorreturn his list,together with a complete listof all
the male inhabitants of said town over the age of twen-
ty-one years, and the number of dogs owned by each ,on
or beforethe firstday of June of each year.
§ 7. Immediately upon the return of said listby the
assessor,the board of trusteesshallcarefullyexamine the
listas returned by the assessor, and correctthe valuation
of propertyas made by him ,whether the same be valued
too high or too low ,and make such correctionsas may be
found needful; and any personwho feelsaggrievedby the
valuation of his or her property as made by the assessor
may apply to the board of trustees,who shallhear and
determine the matter , and grant such reliefas isproper .
§ 8. Immediately after having examined and revised
the assessor'slists,the board of trustees shallapportion
and levy an ad valoremtax on the propertyreturned,and
a poll tax on each male inhabitant returned,and a tax
on each dog returned,to be paid by the owner : Provided,
That the tax on each dog shallnot be lessthan fiftycents:
Provided further, Should the owner or owners failto pay
the said tax when demanded ,the dogs shallbe killed by
the tax collector .

122 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 9. The board of trusteesshallprescribeallnecessary
rules and regulations for obtaining annually a fair and
equal assessment of all property subject to taxation in
said town ,and for the collectionthereof.
§ 10. The clerk of the board of trustees, as soon as the
assessor'sbook is approved of by the board,shallmake
out,in a book to be providedforthat purpose,an alpha-
beticallistof the tax-payers of said town ,together with
the amount of property owned by each , and the amount
of tax due from each ,and place,the same in the hands of
the marshal or tax collector of said town on or before
the 10th day of August in each year .
§11 . The marshal of Whitesville,or the tax collector,
shall proceed immediately to collectthe taxes on said
book,and shallfurnishto each tax-payer a receiptfor
the amount of taxes paid . Should any tax-payer failor
refuseto pay his,her,or theirtaxes on demand ,the mar-
shal or tax collector shall tender to the delinquent a
receiptforthe amount due ,and again demand payment ;
and if the tax -payer then failsor refusesto pay same ,the
officershallimmediately distrainfor same by levying
upon and sellingfor cash in hand any or allproperty of
the delinquent .
§ 12. All property owned by the person against whom
the taxes are assessed shall be subject to distraint, and
may be levied on and sold for the taxes due and costsof
sale,including cost of advertising,and six per centum on
the amount due as commission to the officerfor making
the levy and sale. The marshal shallfirstseizethe per-
sonal estate of the tax-payer ; if none ,he shall levy on
and sellthe real estateof the tax-payer ,or the real estate
against which the assessment is made , as the case may
be .
§ 13. All real estate in Whitesville may be sold for
taxes due upon said real estate,or due to the town from
persons owning same . The marshal or tax collectorshall
levy upon the same by indorsingthe levy upon the tax
receipt,and shallsellthe same at the post-officedoor in
the town of Whitesville on the firstSaturday in each

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 123
nonth . Before sellingthe officershalladvertisethe time ,
terms , and place of sale,together with a descriptionof
the propertyto be sold,forat leastfifteendays preceding
the sale, by printed handbills posted at the post-office
door and at least four other public places in said town ;
and if a newspaper be publishedin saidtown ,then by
two insertionsin said newspaper . Said sale shall be
made for cash in hand ,and shall be for a sum sufficient
to pay the taxes due and cost,as provided in the lastsec-
tion. If the lot sold is susceptibleof division,the officer
shallsellonly so much as isnecessary to pay the tax and
costs; but if not susceptible of division,then the whole
property shallbe sold,and the excess over and above the
taxes and costs shall be paid over to the delinquent
owner . It is provided, however ,that the owner of the
propertysoldshallhave the right,at any time withintwo
years afterthe sale,to redeem said propertyby paying
the tax due ,ten per cent. interestthereon ,and the costs
of sale. If said property is not redeemed within said
two years,then the marshal or tax collectorshallconvey
the propertysoldto the purchaserthereof.
§ 14. If there isno marshal of said town ,or if the mar-
shal fails or refuses to take the tax -book and collect the
taxes,then ,and in that case,the board of trustees shall
appoint a collector of taxes for said town , who shall
execute bond and take the oath of office, and shall be
invested with allthe rights,powers , and privilegesof the
marshal in the collectionof taxes,and shallbe subject to
likepenalties.
ARTICLE III.
§1 . There shall be elected on the firstMonday in Au-
gust, 1882, and every four years thereafter,by the legal
voters of Whitesville, a judicial officerto be styled the
policejudge of Whitesville, who shall qualityas re-
quired by the Constitutionand laws of the State of
Kentucky , and enter upon the discharge of his duties
on or before the firstday of September succeedinghis
election .

124 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 2. Said policejudge shallpossessthe same qualifica-
tions as county judges .
§3 . Said policejudge shallhold hiscourtsin Whites-
villeat such times as the board of trusteesshall,by ordi-
nance, prescribe, and his court shallbe known as the
policecourtof Whitesville.
§ 4. Said court shallhave exclusive jurisdictionwithin
the limitsof saidtown ,and withinone half milebeyond
said limits,of allprosecutionsfor a breach of the ordi-
nances of said town ; and concurrentjurisdictionwith
the quarterlycourtand courtsofjusticesof the peace of
all civilsuits where the matter in controversy does not
exceed one hundred dollars, exclusive of interestand
costs .
§5 . Said policecourt shallhave concurrentjurisdic-
tion with justicesof the peace of allcriminalprosecu-
tions within the county of Daviess,except as a courtof
inquiry,in which he shallhave the same jurisdictionas
the county judge .
§ 6. Said judge shall act as clerk of said court, and
shallissueall necessaryprecepts, writs,and summons .
He shallkeep a correctrecordof the proceedingsof said
court,and a certifiedcopy of same shallbe evidence in
all the courts of this Commonwealth .
§7. All prosecutionsin saidcourt,fora violationof the
ordinancesof Whitesville,shallbe upon warrant eitherof
arrestor summons ,as the policejudge may deem proper,
issued in the name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
for the benefit of the town of Whitesville; and , in said
warrant ,the offensecharged shall be succinctly stated;
and for allfinesdue said town , a capiaspro fine may
issue .
§ 8. The said police judge is vested with power to
issuesubpœnas forwitnessesto appear and testifyin all
matterspending insaidcourt,and tocompel theirattend-
ance by attachment ,and fineand imprisonment for con-
tempt of court. He shallhave power to fineand imprison
forcontempt to thesame extentthatjusticesof thepeace
are permittedto fineand imprisonfor likeoffenses. He

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 125
shallhave power to ordera jury to be summoned and
empaneled in allcaseswhen ,by law,juriesarepermitted
in courts of justicesof the peace .
§9 . The police court of Whitesville shall be always
open forthetrialof persons charged with the violation
of the ordinances of Whitesville, or any criminal offense
cognizable in said court.
§ 10. Said police judge shall receive the same fees,
collectable in the same manner ,as allowed by law to
justicesof the peace for similarservices.
§ 11.Appeals may be taken from judgments of said
court in the same manner and on the same terms that
appeals are allowed from judgments of justices of the
peace .
§ 12. If, from any cause ,the police judge shall failto
attend and hold his court,or being in attendance should
failor refuse,from any cause ,to hold his court,or should
there be a vacancy in said office,then,and in that case,the
chairman of the board of trustees shall hold said court;
and in case of a vacancy in said office,said chairman
shallissue allprocesses, writs,and executions,which said
policejudge couldby law issue.
ARTICLE IV .
§1 . There shall be elected by the legal voters of.
Whitesville,on the firstMonday in August , 1882, a mar-
shal, who shall qualify as constables are required to
qualify,and hold his officefor two years,and untilhis
successoris electedand qualifies, and entersupon the
discharge of his duties,which shall be within one month
after his election . The said marshal shall execute to the
board of trusteesof Whitesville a bond of security,to be
approved by them,forthe faithfuldischargeof hisduties
as marshal of said town .
§2 . Said marshal shall execute all processes from the
policecourt of Whitesville, and collectall executions
from said court. He shallcollectalltaxes due said town ,
and pay the same over to the treasurer. He shallcollect
allfinesand forfeituresdue said town ,and pay same over
to the treasurer. He shallannually ,on or before the 20th

126 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
day of July,pay over to the treasureralltaxesdue said
town ,and on or before said day make settlementsof his
accounts as marshal with the clerk of the board , and ob-
tain a quietus for alltaxes placed in his hands for collec-
tion,and forallmoneys collectedby him ,or which ought
to have been collectedby him , on account of fines and
forfeitures,which settlementsshall be examined and ap-
proved by the board of trustees.
§3 . The marshal of Whitesville is vested with the
same powers as are conferred by law on sheriffsin the
collection of taxes and the execution of processes,and
shallreceivethe same compensation,payable and col-
lectablein the same manner ,and he shallbe liableto
like penalties: Provided, He shall receive one dollar for
serving each warrant of arrest, and five cents for each
summons for violations of the ordinances of the town of
Whitesville,to be paid by the town and taxed as costs;
and each defendant arrested or summoned shall be con-
sidered a separate warrant . He shallreceive in addition
theretosuch salary for policing said town as the board of
trusteesmay ,by ordinance ,fix.
§ 4. The marshal of Whitesvilleisvested with the same
power , in all case throughout the county of Daviess,
which is given by law to constables. He shall perform
such other duties as are not inconsistentwith thischarter,
and make such other returns and reports of his acts as
may be providedby the board of trusteesof Whitesville.
He shall be entitled to the same fees and commissions for
collectingfines and forfeituresas the constables are in
like cases . He shall have the same fees as constables for
like services .
§ 5. Said marshal shall have power to summon to his
aid as many citizensof the town of Whitesville as may
be necessary for the enforcement of any ordinance
adopted by the board of trustees of said town ; and
any citizenthus summoned and failingto obey,shallbe
liableto such fine as the board of trusteesmay ,by ordi-
nance ,fix.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 127
§ 6. The board of trusteesmay ,from time to time,ap-
point policemen for the town of Whitesville,not exceed-
ing four at any one time,and they may remove any or all
of them at pleasure. Said policemen shalltake the oath
required by law of constables,and are invested with all
the powers of sheriffsor constablesin suppressingriots,
arrestinglaw -breakers,or to preserve the peace of the said
town . They are authorizedto executeallcriminalpro-
cesses issued by the police judge of Whitesville. The
board of trusteesshall,by ordinance,provideforthe pay
of said policemen when on duty .
§7 . When any drunken, riotous,or disorderlyperson
is arrestedby any peace officerwithin said town or
within one half mile of the corporate limits,itshall be
lawful for such officerto confine said drunken ,riotous,
or disorderlyperson in such place of confinement as the
board of trusteesmay provide,untilhe can be brought
before the police judge or some magistrate,to be dealt
with accordingto law ; but such personshallnot be con-
fined for a longer period than twenty -four hours , unless
the day succeedingthearrestbe Sunday ;then saidperson
shallbe taken before the police judge or magistrate by
twelve o'clock,M. , on Monday .
§ 8. Should a vacancy occur in the officeof marshal ,
then the board of trusteesshall appoint some suitable
person to fillthe officeduring the remainder of the term .
ARTICLE V.
§1 . The treasurerelectedby the board of trustees,as
provided in thisact,shallbe the custodianof the funds
of said town ,which he will pay out on the orders of the
board of trustees,and will render a quarterly statement
of the amounts receivedby him , togetherwith proper
vouchers for all disbursements ; and will render such
other statements as the board may require from time to
time , and turn over allfunds and property of the town
in his hands to his successor .
§ 2. The clerk of the board shall keep a correct record
of allthe proceedingsof the board of trusteesin well-
bound books to be providedforthat purpose,at the cost

128 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of the town , and shall be the custodian of the records
and papers of said board .
ARTICLE VI .
§1 . The board of trusteesof Whitesville have power
to work allable-bodied partieson any of the streetsof
said town who may be convicted for violation of ordi-
nances ,when the said defendants fail or refuse to pay
over or replevy the same . Said work is to be under the
supervisionof the marshal, or some other person ap-
pointed by the board of trusteesof Whitesville, at the
rateof one dollarper day,untilsaidjudgment and costs
are satisfied .
SCHEDULE .
§1 . The board of trustees and other officers of said
town ,in officewhen this act takes effect,shall continue
in office,with allthe powers and privilegesunder thisact,
untiltheirrespectivesuccessors are elected or appointed
and qualified. They shallreceivethecompensation fixed
by law or the ordinances of said town in forceat passage
of thisact. The by -laws and ordinances now in force
in said town shall continue in force under this charter
untilrepealed, or othersadopted in theirstead by the
board of trustees .
§ 2. All laws or parts of laws in conflictwith this act
are hereby repealed .
§3 . This act to take effectand be in force from and
afteritsadoptionby a majorityof allthe qualifiedvoters
within the boundaries herein ; and to ascertain the will
of said voters, an election shall be held on the second
Saturday in June,1882,in the manner prescribedby law .
And to determine the number of voters within said
boundary ,the trusteesshall cause a fulllistof the quali-
fied voters in said boundary to be returned to them by
some competent and reliableperson by them appointed ,
who shall also certifythe number thereof to the clerk of
the Daviess county court. Notice of said election shall
be given by writtenor printedpostersplaced at ten dif-
ferent public places within the boundary , at leastthree

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 129
of which shall be on the main business street of the
town ,and thequestionpropounded toeach votershallbe:
" Are you foror againstthe new charter? " and a certifi-
cate of the number of votes cast for and against said
charter shall also be filed in the clerk's officeof the Da-
viesscounty court.
Approved April 6th, 1882 .

fortheyear 1877,defaultedforthe revenue due the State

CHAPTER 820 ,
AN ACT to prohibitthe sale of spirituous, vinous,or malt liquors within
the corporatelimitsof the town of Dixon , in Webster county , or within
one mile thereof .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That it shall not be lawful for any druggist or
other person to eitherdirectlyor indirectlyvend or sell
any spirituous,vinous ,or malt liquors,or a mixture there-
of, within the corporate limits of the town of Dixon ,in
Webster county ,Kentucky , or within one mile thereof.
§ 2. For any violationof thisactthe person or persons
so offending shallbe finedtwenty dollarsfor each offense,
to be recovered by warrant issuedby policejudge of
said town ,judge of Webster county court,justice of the
peace forsaidcounty,or by indictmentby the grand jury
of said county , such warrant to be issued in the name of
the Commonwealth ; and fullpower is hereby given to
any such officerto try any violation of thisact that may
occur within said corporate limits, or within one mile
thereof, and the same proceeding shall be had upon a
judgment rendered as if rendered by a criminal or circuit
court for a similar offense .
§3 . All finescollectedunder the provisions of thisact
shallbe paid into the treasury of said town .
§4 . This act shallnot affectany one now sellingunder
a licenseuntilthe expiration of said license.
Approved April 6 , 1882 .

and board of directorsmay adopt by-laws,in which they
shall provide for a transfer of shares of stock, and the

ings, and mode of calling meetings of stockholders, and
the transaction of business at such meetings , and amend
or repealthesame at pleasure; and shallhave the man-
agement , government , and control of the property and
affairsof the company ; and the president and three di-
rectorsshallconstitutea quorum of the board .
§3 . The company may purchase, hold, and selland
convey real estate; may buy and sellreal estate on com-
mission ; may encourage immigration by the circulation
of maps , books , pamphlets , circulars, or other printed
matter; may make contracts for the settlement of colo-
nies of immigrants on any land in Kentucky , and for
that purpose may lay offand plat towns , districts, or
villagesin any county of the Commonwealth ; may loan
money or negotiable paper in any manner not prohibited
by law . In salesmade by it,it may receivein payment
thereforcurrentmoney ,stocks,or bonds of other corpo-
rations; or the payment of money due iton any and all
sales may be upon time ,evidenced by billsof exchange
or promissory notes, and may secure the payment of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 155
debts due it upon sales of its property by reserving a
vendor's lienupon the property sold and conveyed by
it, and it may secure the payment of any debts due by it
by mortgage; and the company may issueitscoupon
bonds ,and securethe payment thereofby executing one
or more mortgages upon itscorporate property and fran-
chises to one or more trustees for the common benefit of
the holders of bonds : Provided, Said bonds in the aggre-
gate shallnot exceed the amount of capital stock herein
authorized .
§4 . The company shallestablishitsprincipalofficein
some town or cityin Kentucky ,to be determined by its
board of directors; and branch officesand agencies in
such towns and places as it may deem necessary. The
corporators herein named shall have and exercise all the
powers and functions of the board of directorsuntilsaid
board shall be elected .
§5 . This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 6 , 1882 .

CHAPTER 828 .

land,located in Breathittcounty ,Kentucky ,the survey of

therefore ,

of Kentucky :

patent to James C. Crow to correspond to the survey
made to said Crow by M. J. Amyck , and now on filein
his office .
§ 2. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 6, 1882.

CHAPTER 829 .

Company ,in Washington county,and to authorizethe Washington county
court to take stock in same .

of Kentucky :
§1 . That the persons who may subscribe stock for the
purpose of constructing a turnpike road from a point on
the Springfield and Maxville Turnpike Road , near Thos .
R. Brume's gate, via Beechland,to the Springfieldand
BloomfieldTurnpike,in Washington county, Kentucky .
be, and they are hereby, created a body -corporateand

Lincoln Run Turnpike Road Company ; and by said
name they may sue and be sued ,and shallhave perpetual
succession .
§2 . Said road shall be under the management and
control of five directors, one of whom shall be chosen
president by said directors, and another shall be , in like
manner ,chosen secretary and treasurerof said company .
The directorsshallbe elected annually on the firstSatur-
day in May ,except as hereinafterprovided .
§3 . The capital stock of said company shall be ten.
thousand dollars,or such sums as may be necessary to
build said road , to be divided in shares of twenty -five
dollarseach ,and each stockholdershallbe entitledto one
vote for each share owned by him or her.
§4 . The road shall be builtwith a grade not lessthan
twenty feet wide , with an elevation of not less than
twelve inches in the center, with a ditch on each side of
and outside the grade of sufficientwidth to drain the
road . The metal shall not be less than fourteen feet
wide and ten inchesdeep,of sound hard rock,the bottom
rock to be five and a half inches deep , and brokers so as
to pass through a five-inch ring,with a top dressing of
rockbrokersso as to pass through a two and a half inch
ring. The top dressingmay be of sound hard gravel,in
the discretion of the board of directors.

erned by the provisions of chapter 110 of the General

man ,Ab ,Walker, are appointed commissioners to open

cientamount is subscribed to pay forbuilding said road ;
theymay also receiveconveyances to said company of

zion .
of the company ,surveythe route of said road. A ma-
jorityof the board concurring,shall determine the route

CHAPTER 833 .
AN ACT to confer on courtsof justicesof the peace, mayors ,and police
judges, in Bourbon county, jurisdictionin prosecutionsfor petitlarceny,
trespass,and injury to personalproperty, and to regulatethe proceedings
insaid prosecutions.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of theCommonwealth
ofKentucky :
§1 . That whenever any person may make or filean
affidavit, in writing, before any justiceof the peace,
mayor,or policejudge,in Bourbon county,statingthat
any person has committed the offenseof petitlarceny,
trespass, or injury to personal property in said county ,

166 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
then itshallbe the duty of such magistrate before whom
said affidavitis filed to issue his warrant of arrest against
the person charged to have committed the offense, sub-
stantiallyalleging, as provided in section 122, subsec-
tion 2 , of the Criminal Code , the nature of the offense
charged .
§2 . Said warrant may be directed to any sheriff, con-
stable,marshal ,or policeman ,in thisCommonwealth ,and
shallbe indorsed as provided in section 28 of the Crimi-
nal Code : Provided , That when the arrest is made in
Bourbon county ,any officermaking ,and having authority
to make ,the arrest shall be authorized and empowered
to take the bail in the sum indorsed .
§3 . Said warrant shallbe returnable on such date ,and
at such place in the county , as may be in the warrant
directed .
§4 . On the returnday of the warrant,ifthe accused
has been apprehended,the magistratebeforewhom the
warrant has been returned shall arraign the accused for
trial,cause him to plead to the charge against him , fixa
time on that or another day for the trial, issuesubpœnas
tor witnesses, and have a jury summoned .
§5 . The sheriffor constableshallsummon a jury of
twelve (12) sober, discreet, and intelligenthousekeepers
to attend, at the time and place fixed for the trialof the
accused ,and they shallbe sworn well and trulyto trythe
case. If any juror so summoned shall failto attend,or
be objected to or excused , others shall be summoned in
theirstead. The justice, mayor , or judge shallpreside
at the trial, and decide all questions of law arising in
the case,and preserve order.
§6 . The jury shallconsistof twelve (12) persons,un-
less the accused and the prosecuting attorney agree in
open court that it may be composed of a less number ,
and each party shallhave the same rightof challenge
now provided by law .
§7 . The trialshall be had in the justices' districtin
which the offensewas committed , and the warrant shall
be returned before one of said officersin said district;

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 167
but any one of saidofficersmay have jurisdictionin any
districtin the county , and a failureto try in said district
shall in no way affectthe verdict or judgment of the
court. The defendant may have a change of venue , as
provided in articlethree(3),chaptertwelve (12),General
Statutes , in civil cases .
§8 . The trialshall be conducted in all respects as if
had on an indictment in the circuit court, except as
herein provided . The magistrate holding the trialshall,
as clerk, do whatever the clerk of the circuitcourt should
do in such trial,keeping in a book ,to be provided for
that purpose,a correctrecordof allproceedingshad in
each case .
§ 9. The proceedings had in each case, afterjudgment
isrendered , shallbe in allrespects the same as proceed-
ingsupon a judgment renderedby a circuitcourt.
§10 . An appeal may be taken from thejudgment of the
saidjustices',mayor's ,or police court to the circuitcourt,
ifitbe for more than twenty dollars,or forconfinement in
the jailor work -house for more than ten days ; or itbeing
for both fineand imprisonment, eitherthe fineis over
twenty dollars,or the term of imprisonment is over ten
days : Provided, however, That beforetakingsuch appeal
the defendant shallsupersede such judgment by executing
before the magistrate in whose court the judgment was
rendered ,a bond , with good and sufficientsurety,condi-
tioned that the defendant will appear in the circuitcourt
to prosecute his appeal ,and not depart therefrom without
itsleave,and willperform itsjudgment by paying any
finewhich may be assessed against him ,or by surrender-
ing his body for confinement ,one or both, as the judg-
ment may require. If any forfeitureshallbe taken on
said bond ,the judgment thereon shallbe for the fine,
costs, and two dollars for every day of confinement
which had been adjudged ; and , in addition, upon failure
of the accused to appear , the circuitcourt shallenter the
judginent appealed from against the accused , which he
may be required to satisfy,and for that purpose the judg-
ment shallbe affirmed,and a capiasissuedthereon.

168 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 11. On allappeals to the circuitcourt,the case shall
be triedde novo; but in case of such appeals no indictment
of a grand jury shallever be necessary.
§ 12.The county attorney, when so requested, shall
prosecute all such cases in the court of the magistrate ;
and for his services he shall have the same fees and com-
missions in every case of the Commonwealth's attorney
in like cases prosecuted by him in the circuitcourt.
§ 13. The justices', mayor's and police courts,for the
purpose of exercisingthe jurisdictionhereby conferred,
shallalways be open ,except on Sundays and legal holi-
days .
§ 14. Nothing in this act shallbe held to interferewith
the jurisdiction of the Bourbon circuit court to exercise
concurrent jurisdictionin prosecutions for the offenses
herein named ,provided the prosecutionis institutedby
indictment of a grand jury .
§ 15. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 6, 1882 .

tucky riveror any of itstributarystreams .
§2 That sectionsecond of said act,to which thisis an
amendment ,be so amended as to read as follows: That
it shall be unlawful for any person or persons to use a

CHAPTER 836 .
AN ACT to establisha road law for Lewis county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
ofKentucky :
§ 1. That it shall be lawful for the county court of
Lewis county ,at itsregular term in February ,March , or
April,in each year,to appoint three road commissioners
forsaid county ,one of them to reside on the waters of
Kinny Creek, another in or near Vanceburg , the other
between Tollesboro and Concord ,in said county . The
commissionersso appointed shall,ifpresent at the time
of such appointment ,or if not present,at the next county
court thereafter,before entering upon his or theirduties,
take an oath forthe faithfulperformance of their duties;.

170 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and said commissioners shall, at the same term of the
court,give bond with such securitypayable to the Com-
monwealth as the county court may deem sufficientfor
the faithful discharge of their duties as commissioners ,
on which bond any person considering himself aggrieved
may sue; and one recovery shall be no bar to another
suit or suits ; and no commissioner shall act as such until
he shall have taken the oath and executed the bond as
aforesaid .
§ 2. The aforesaidcommissionersshallproceed to lay
off the county into road districts,and from time to time
may make any alterationor change as may be required,
taking care that the districtsdo not clash, and appoint
one overseer over each road district, and him remove at
pleasure; and said commissioner , at the time he delivers
to said road overseer his road order , shall administer to
him an oath that he willfaithfullydischarge the duties of
road overseer as required of him by thisact.
§3 . The said commissioners shall be made hereby
a body-corporate,and by the name of " Road Commis-
sionersof Lewis County " may sue and be sued,plead
and be impleaded ,defend and be defended ,answer and
be answered , and by the name aforesaid contract and
be contracted with in relation to any of the objects for
which they were appointed ; and they and theirsuccessors
shallbe considered in law as a body existing in perpe-
tuity; and they may receiveby giftor otherwise any
estate, real or personal, choses in action, security, or
other thing for the use and benefit of the public roads ;
and shallin allcases,when the purposes of the donation ,
subscription,devise,or contract issetforthor manifested,
appropriate the thing thereby received according to such
direction: Provided,however,That any person injuredmay
and shall, for the malfeasance or nonfeasance ,trespass,
or conduct incompatible with the powers granted by
thisact of incorporation,on the part of any one or more
of said commissioners ,have the right to sue such com-
missioner or commissioners , and his or theirsecurityor
securities,in theirindividualright, and in that character

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 171
shall they be alone liable for said wrong hereinbefore
enumerated .
§4 . That said commissioners shall keep a book in
which they shall enter all the appointments of, and
changes made of overseers, and the amounts in their
respectivedistricts,in each year ,to be paid for in labor
and tax , and the amount due the road fund from each
overseer ,and such other matters in relation to their duties
as shall be deemed necessary .
§ 5. It shallbe the duty of said road commissioners to
make out and filewith county court clerk a correct listof
the names of each overseer in his portion of county , and
also the respectivenumber of each district.
§ 6. It shall be the duty of the commissioner to settle
with each road overseerby the 20th day of December of
each year ,for the taxes,labor, fines,and the amount of
road fund due and collected in his district , and the dis-
bursement thereof, which he shallshow by proper re-
ceipts and vouchers ; and if any overseer shall failso to
settle, or to collect taxes, the said commissioner shall
report him to the county court,which shallcause a rule
to issue against him ,to be collectedfor road purposes in
his road district,and ten per cent. thereon, and a capias
pro finemay issueagainst him ,from time to time ,untilthe
judgment issatisfied; said rule shallissue in the name of
said commissioners ,who shall be a competent witness to
prove any fact on the trialof said rule; and taxes so col-
lectedshallbe expended upon the road districtso taxed .
§7 . In motions or suitsbrought by the commission-
ers under the provisions of thisact,the attorney for the
Commonwealth , if in the circuitcourt, and the county
attorney,ifin the county court,shallex officioprosecute
the same ,and the clerks,sheriffs, and other officersshall
charge no fees, unless judgment be given against the
defendant or defendants ,and then the cost in the execu-
tion shallbe for the benefit of the officersrendering ser-
vices,and for the witnesses and others who have a right
thereto; and the officerscollectingthe same shallpay the
same over to the persons entitledthereto.

172 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 8. It shall be the duty of the county court clerk of
Lewis county,withinten days afterthe returnto him of
commissioner's books of assessments for revenue for that
year,and when calledupon by the road commissioners,
to make out a correcttranscript,or so much of saidbooks
as embrace allpersonsand propertyassessedin saidcom-
missioner's portion of said county , showing in appro-
priate columns the amount of road tax to be paid by each
person so assessed. Said commissioners shallfurnishthe
county court clerk with a book ,or a necessary amount of
writing paper , for the purpose as aforesaid; and said
clerk shall also make out a correct list of the names of
allthe overseersin said county,which listshall,on the
firstday of the term of each circuitcourt,be handed to
the foreman of the grand jury .
§9. The tax to be leviedin Lewis county forroad pur-
poses shall be six cents on every one hundred dollars'
worth of property taxed for State revenue purposes , and
shall all be expended in the road districtto which the
tax-payer belongs,except ten per cent.of said tax,which
shallbe used in defraying other expenses belonging to the
road system.
§ 10. All persons between the ages of eighteen and
fiftyyears shall be required to work on roads,except
thoseexempted by generallaw .
§ 11. Any person may work out their taxes at the rate
of one dollarper day ,under the directionof the over-
seer, and by his order , or two dollars for team .
§ 12. The law as to the number of days any given road
shallbe worked yearly,shallbe that found in the General
Statutes .
§ 13. The commissioners shall be allowed , each , fifty
dollarsannually for his services,to be paid out of ten per
cent.above appropriatedto pay expenses.
§ 14. The overseer of each road shall have the power
and right to collectall taxes belonging to his road dis-
trict, and must proceed as a sheriffto obtain the same .
§ 15. As to obstructingroads or opening new roads,the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 173
same course shallbe pursued as authorizedby general
law .
§ 16. The county court shall order two hundred copies
of thislaw printed for the information of overseers and
citizens of the county ,to be paid out of county levy.
§ 17. All laws in conflictwith this act are hereby re-
pealed .
§ 18.This actto be in forcefrom itspassage.
Approved April 6 , 1882 .

CHAPTER 842 .

to vote a tax to build turnpike roads in said precinct.

of Kentucky :

between the hours of 7 o'clock, A. M. , and 6 o'clock,P.

person voting whether he votes for or against the tax to
build turnpikesin saiddistrict; and if a majorityof the

at once notify,in writing,the county clerk,but he shall
have no power to pass upon the legalityof any of the
votes cast .

county court, in marking out the tax-listsfor Henry
county,shalladd to the listsof taxesfor Port Royal pre-
cinctfiftycents on each one hundred dollars' worth of

property ,as shown by the assessor'sbooks in said pre-
cinct .

account of all moneys received and disbursed by him ,

entitled" An act to re-enact and amend an act,entitled

sage .
