extent , that the board of education of said town and
districtare liable, including the bonds issued by said
board for erecting school buildings, and the annual in-
terest thereon .
§7 . The board of education shall elect one of their
number president, and shall also elect annually, at their
firstmeeting , a secretary and treasurer,who may or may
not be of the board ,and may allow them reasonable com-
pensation for theirservices, and remove eitherof them at
will. They shallalso elect an assessoras now provided

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 185
by law ,or may assign that duty to the city assessor, and
allow him compensation for the additionallabor required
of him in performing the duties required.
§ 8. The assessorso elected or designated by the board
of education may appoint as many sober,discreet per-
sons, not under the age of twenty-four years,to assist
him in the discharge of hisduties as he may deem neces-
sary,by consent of the board ,and he may remove them
at pleasure .
§9 . The assessor and each assistant shall,before enter-
ing on the duties of the office,take the oath prescribedby
the Constitution, and , in addition thereto, the following
oath:" You do swear thatyou willfixa fairand fullvalu-
ation on allproperty listedby you for taxation for school
purposes in the city of Hawesville , without favor or par-
tiality; that you willdiligentlysearch and inquire so that
no person shallbe passed over or failto have an opportu-
nityto give in hislistof taxable property; that you willre-
quire each person to swear that he willrender you a true
and fulllistof all property he holds or controls subject
to taxation for school purposes in the city; that you will
take a fulland complete census , as far as possible,of all
white children within the school ages , and return itin a
separate list,with the age and sex of each child,and the
name of parent ,guardian ,or other person having charge
of each of them ; and that you will trulyreport all per-
sons who shall fail or refuse to give in a listof their tax-
ableproperty,under oath, afterbeing duly calledon for
that purpose ,or who have given in a falseor fraudulent
list, or refused to give in the amount of their residuary
estate as required under the equalization law : so help
you God ." He shall also,before entering on the duties Bond.
of his office,give bond to the board of education ,to be
approved by them , for the faithfuldischarge of the duties
of the office, which shall be recorded by the secretary,
and the originalfiledaway .
§ 10. All propertyshallbe listedfortaxation,whether
forgeneralcitypurposesor forschoolpurposes,as of the
tenth of January each year ,except stores,which shall be

186 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
returned at the time and in the manner now required by
law for taxation for State revenue ,or otherwise ,as herein
provided for.
§ 11. The electionfor allthe cityofficesand school
trusteesshall be held at the court-house ,by one set of
officers, and in one book , by two judges, a clerk and
marshal , who shall,for the firstelection,be appointed by
the trustees of the town of Hawesville , and each year
thereafterby the council; and said elective officersshall
qualifyand be governed in every respect,except as oth-
erwise provided herein,by the general election laws of
theCommonwealth . They shall,at the closeofthe polls
carefullyexamine them to see that no mistakes have been
made ; seethat they are properlyrecapitulated,properly
certifythem , make out for each officerelected a certifi-
cate of the fact, and seal up the poll books ; allof which
itshall be the duty of the clerk to take charge of, and
within twenty -four hours deliverto the cityclerk the poll-
books , and to each officerso elected his certificate, if in
thecity; otherwise,deposititwith the cityclerkfordeliv-
ery when demanded .
§ 12. All electionsin said cityshall be held between
the hours of six o'clock in the morning and six o'clock in
the evening .
§ 13. All laws in force in thisCommonwealth punish-
ing illegalvotingin Stateelectionsshallapply to illegal
voting in the cityof Hawesville ,and may be enforced by
information and warrant of arrest in the mayor's court,
or by indictment in the Hancock circuitcourt.
§ 14. Should the council failto appoint officersof an
Mayor- powers. election,or when appointed,should such officersallfail
to attend and open the pollsfor thirtyminutes after six
o'clock in the morning , when such electionshould have
been begun , or if attending, failor refuse to act,then the
mayor ,or in his absence such of the councilmen as are
present,or any of them ,shallappoint such officersas are
herein required to act,or at the firstelection under this
act,any of the town trusteespresent shallmake the ap-
pointments ; or should such officershave been appointed ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 187
and part of them failto attend or act,then the officeror
officerswho do attend shall fillsuch vacancies ,and the
officersso appointed ,afterbeing duly sworn , shallconsti-
tute the board of officersfor that election,and proceed
to hold such election .
§ 15. It shallbe the duty of the mayor to preside at all
meetings of the council, and in allcases of a tiehe shall
have a vote ,and not otherwise . He shall observe a strict
supervision over all the affairsof the city; see that the
ordinances of the city are enforced and observed ; see
that each officerof the citydischarges the duties of his
officepromptly and properly,and cause any officerwho
may prove derelictor in defaultto be impeached or other-
wise dealt with, as the case demands . He is empowered
with due authority, and it shallbe his duty ,to suppress
all riots, insurrections, or disorderly conduct , and may
arrest offenders against the penal laws of the city, and
against the penal and criminal laws of the Common-
wealth in view , and may summon the posse comitatusto
his aid if necessary. He shall sign all licenses,commis-
sions, and permits granted by authority of the city, and
perform allotherduties,and exercisesuch other powers
as may from time to time be requiredof him by law ,or
by ordinances of the city.
§ 16. The officeof policejudge of the town of Hawes-
ville is hereby abolished, and in lieu thereof the mayor
of said city shall hold a court therein, which shall be
styled" The Mayor's Court of Hawesville," and the same Mayor'scourt.
ishereby declared a court of record, and shallbe pro-
vided with and have a seal; and copies or transcripts
from the proceedings of said court,properly attestedand
signed by the mayor ,shall be evidence in all courts in
the Commonwealth .
§ 17. The said court shall have concurrent original Jurisdiction.
jurisdiction of all causes arising from violations and
breaches of the ordinances of the city, and concurrent
jurisdictionwith the circuitcourt in all criminal cases
occurring in said city,except felonies; and in civilcases,
where the amount in controversy shall not exceed one

188 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
hundred dollars, exclusive of interest, he shall have
the same jurisdictionas justices of the peace , and the
power of two justicesof the peace in holding examining
courts, inquiring into charges of felony, and in com-
mitting, bailing, and recognizing offenders under said
charges; and he may grant,hear,and receivereturn of
writs of injunction, attachment , and habeas corpus.
§ 18. Said court may award all such process as may
be deemed necessaryand proper for the efficientadmin-
istrationof justicein said city. All originalprocess shall
be directed to, and executed by, the city marshal , and
mesne and finalprocess may be directedto said marshal ,
or any sheriffor constable,in any part of the Common-
wealth ,which officersshallreceive and execute said writs,
and make returns under the same rules and liabilities as
they are required to do of like process from the circuit
and justices' courts. The said court shall have all the
powers conferred on the board of trusteesand theirclerk
of the town of Hawesville ,by an act, entitled" An act
for the benefit of the town of Hawesville ,in Hancock
county," approved March 1, 1854,in proceedingsto open
streets, as defined in the eighth sectionthereof; and said
section is hereby amended so as to extend to the whole
city. All other powers conferred by said act on the board
of trusteesof said town , except such as conflicthere-
with, shallbe exercisedby the council.
§19 . For the trialof civilcauses the mayor's court of
Hawesville shall be held once in two months ,at such times
as the council shall appoint by ordinance ; and the court
may set the causes for any particularday,and continue
the term from day to day tillthe business shallhave been
disposed of; and the process shall be returned upon the
firstday of the term to which it is returnable.
$ 20 Said court shallbe open at alltimes for the trial
of penal offensesand for infractionsof the ordinances of
thecity; and may proceedtohear and tryallsuch causes,
and render judgment thereon , without the intervention
of a jury ,unlessdemanded by the accused ,in which case,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 189
if the jury shallconvict,the court shalltax fiftycents for
each juror in the billof costs.
§21 . The mayor shall, on the firstday of January ,
April,July,and October,in each year,and at the end of
his term , when he goes out of office, make out, swear to,
and return to the board of education a list of all fines
and forfeituresfor which judgments have been rendered
in his court, which , under the law establishing public
schools , and amendments thereto , are directed to be so
appropriated; and if any have been paid into court,he
shall pay the same to the treasurer of the board , at or
before the time of making said reports; and upon his
failureto do so,he may be proceeded against in the quar-
terlyor circuitcourt of Hancock county,by petitionor
motion on his bond , and judgment rendered for double
the amount of the sum or sums so in default , and the
costs of the proceedings .
§ 22. Appeals from the mayor'scourtmay be taken to Appeals.
the circuitcourt of Hancock county in all cases where
the amount in controversy exceeds ten dollars.
§23 . The mayor shall act as clerk of his own court,
and issueallprocessreturnablethereto,but may appoint
a deputy clerk. He shallhave all the powers and au-
thoritynow conferredby law on the policejudge of the
town of Hawesville ,and alllaws pertaining to that office
shallremain in forceand apply to the mayor's court,ex-
cept such as conflictwith or arequalifiedby thisact. As
soon as the said mayor shallbe qualified, the dockets,
files,and allbooks and papersbelongingor pertainingto
the officeof policejudge of the town of Hawesville,
shallbe,by the said policejudge,deliveredto him ,and
he shallhold and actupon the same as a justiceof the
peace acts upon the docket of his predecessor.
§24 . The mayor shallbe entitledto the followingfees: Fees.
for issuing warrant and presiding at a trialof a breach
of the peace or any misdemeanor ,or violationof a city
law,to be taxed againstthe defendant,if found guilty,
$ 2; for execution, including indorsements thereon and
recording return thereof, sixty cents,to be charged and

190 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
included when issued ; and for other services rendered he
shall be entitled to receive and charge such fees as are
allowed to circuitclerks, county clerks, and justices of
the peace for like services. He may issue fee-billsas
clerksof courts are allowed by law to do , and they shall
possess the same distrainableforce.
§25 . The inayor shall have the same power to take
depositions that examiners and justices of the peace
have , and shall be allowed the same fees. He shall be
entitledto charge fiftycents for affixingthe seal of the
cityto any document .
§26 . The mayor shallhave power to take recognizances
for appearance in his court on any day named therein,in
cases cognizable in said court, and also recognizances to
keep the peace and be of good behavior for one year.
Forfeitures,for any recognizance for appearance in said
court,may be enforced in said court,and the penalty col-
lected as in the circuit court .
§27 . That in all cases of fines imposed by judgment
of the mayor's court, he may directin the judgment that
the defendant be committed to prison untilsuch finesand
costsare paid or replevied,or,afterwards,issuea capias
so ordering; and the council may provide by ordinance
forthe employment of such prisonerson the streets,roads,
or any work being done by order of the city, and may
compel such services,for which a rate per day shallbe
provided in such ordinance , which shall, when rendered,
liquidatesaid fine and costs. The council may appoint
a suitable person to supervise such labor, and make a
suitableallowance out of the citytreasury.
§ 28. The mayor shall have power , at any session of
the council or of his court,to enforce good order as is
now given by law to presiding judges of county courts.
§29. All legislativepowers herein granted to the city
Council-powers. of Hawesvilleshallvestin the" citycouncil," and itshall

additionto othergeneral and specialpowers,they shall

no case beyond the firstday of the next May succeeding
theirissual: Provided, That the council may , for the pur-
pose of encouraging manufactures in said city, exempt

for a period not exceeding ten years .
§30 . The council shallhave power to make and repair
all necessary wharves , piers,docks , landings, and other
improvements along the Ohio riverwithin the city limits,
and to lease or rent any part of the landing ,to be used
by wharf-boats, coal-yards, or for other purposes de-
manded in carryingon the trade and commerce of the
city; and they may fix by ordinance the rates of wharf-
age , commission , storage, or other fees to be charged and
received by the keepers of such wharf -boats, and may
impose penalties for carrying on such business on any

taining a license therefor, and the council shallhave the

rent fees shall, untilpaid, be a superior lien on the boat,
float,or other craft,itsfurniture,tackle,and appendages

and if not paid when due , afterdemand ,such boat , float,
craft, or other property may be proceeded against by

the State authorizing the seizure of boats and other

a longer time than fiveyears,nor in any case in such a
manner as to be exclusive , or so as to interfere with the

§31 . The council may erect all buildings which they
may deem necessary, when the public good shallrequire.
They may erect a city prison or work -house , and regu-
latethe management thereof : Provided, That until such
prison shallbe ready for the receptionof prisoners,the
city shall be allowed the use of the jail of Hancock
county for the confinement of all such persons as may
be convicted before the mayor ,or who shall be liable to

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 193
imprisonment under the laws of the State or the ordi-
nances of the city; and persons so imprisoned shall be

jailin such manner as shall be prescribed by law or the
ordinances of said city; and when arrests are made for
drunkenness ,riots, or disorderly conduct , or breaches of
the peace in the city,between the hours of 9 P. M. and
6 A. M. ,itshallbe the duty of said jailerto receive and
confine persons so arrested without requiring a warrant ,
order, or mittimus from a judicialofficer; but in no case
shall he continue such confinement later than noon with-
out proper authority .
§32 . The councilshallhave power to levy and collectTaxes.
all the taxes which the trustees of the town were author-
ized to collectbefore the passage of this act,not other-
wise directed herein : Provided, That in lieu of an ad
valorem tax they may levy a special tax each year on
each house for the sale of merchandise of any kind,

of them ,or for the purchase and sale of country produce
or other thing in any part of the city, or on any boat
used or occupied for any of the above purposes,a sum
not less than $ 5 nor more than $ 50,the amount to be
governed , as near as may be ,by the amount of business

authorized by thisact, and ordained by the council.
§33 . The council shall, at theirfirstmeeting ,electone Mayor,protem.
of their number mayor pro tem. for one year ,and such
member shalltake the oath required of the mayor , and ,
in the absence of the mayor ,presideat the meetings of
the council; and in case of the death or resignation of
the mayor ,his absence from the city,removal or inability,
from any cause ,to perform the duties of the office,said
mayor pro tem, shall,during such absence, inability, or
vacancy ,perform allthe duties,and have and exercise all

of both the mayor and mayor pro tem , the council shall
electa chairman to presideat theirmeetings; and the

ttorney .
said councilshallhave power to fillany and allvacan-

§34 . The council shall,at their firstmeeting in 1881,
Marshalandcityand at the firstmeeting of the new council every two
years thereafter,electa marshal and cityattorney,who
shallhold theirofficesfor two years,and untiltheirsuc-
cessors are chosen and qualified. The marshal shall,
withinthirtydays afterhiselection,executeto the citya

The councilshallalso requirethe marshal to executea

and conditions thereof. They shall require good and

by the council. Said bonds shall be filedwith the clerk
of the city, and a true copy of each spread upon the

of them ,the marshal shallbe liablein the same manner ,
and to the same extent,as sheriffsor constables are now
liableby law on theirbonds,in the same penalties,to be
recovered by law in the same manner before the mayor's
courtor any courthaving jurisdictionin similarproceed-
ings against sheriffsor constables.
§35 . Before enteringon the duties of his office, the
marshal shall take an oath, to be administered by the
mayor ,thathe willwell and trulydischargethe dutiesof
his office,and he may appoint deputy marshals ,who shall
take a similar oath . Should the marshal fail to execute

shall,by ordinance, declaresaid officevacant,and pro--
ceed to fillthe vacancy .
§36. Allwarrantsand processissuingfrom themayor's
court,tobe executedin Hancock county,shallbe directed
to,and executedby,the marshal,exceptwhen he may be

absent,sick,or under some disability,or when the office

constable or policeman . The marshal may execute any

§37 . The marshal ,in person or by deputy ,shallattend

council, and perform allduties required of him by either,

constables. He shallbe allowed the following fees,viz :
for arrestingeach person charged with violatingthe ordi-
nances or laws of the city, one dollar; summoning each
witness,twenty fivecents; summoning a jury,when or-
dered to try an offender, one dollar; for other serviceshe
shall be allowed the same fees as sheriffs or constables
for likeservices; and the council may allow him compen-
sation for any extra duties required of him by them , and
provide for itspayment .
§ 38. It shall be the duty of the marshal especially to
exerthimselfto suppressallfights,riots,breachesof the
peace ,and infractionsof the penal laws , and to appre-
hend without warrant , and take before the mayor , all
rioters, disorderlypersons,disturbersof the public peace ,
persons found drunk and uncared for or exposed in the
city,and allpersons in the act of committing any offense
indictableor punishable under the penal or criminal laws
of the State, or fleeingfrom justice aftercommitting any
such offense; and if he deem it necessary for that pur-
pose, he may callto his assistanceallpersons in the
vicinityor in the city,and persons so summoned by the
marshal, any of his deputies, or any policeman, who

or'scourt; and persons arrestedfor drunkenness, riot,

thejailof Hancock county or the cityprisonuntilmorn-

ing,and reported as early as convenient to the mayor or
some magistrate of the county to be dealt with .
§39 . The council may elect a streetcommissioner,
whose duty it shall be to see that the ordinances in
reference to the streets, alleys, roads,wharf , and public
grounds are enforced, and generallyto perform allthe
duties,and exercise such powers as the council may or-
dain; and for his services he shall receive such compen-
sation as shallbe fixedor regulated by the council; until
such officerisappointedallsuch dutiesand powers shall
devolve on and be exercisedby the marshal .
§ 40. It shall be the duty of the marshal to see that
good order is preserved in the city; and when public
occasions, holidays,and other extraordinaryhappenings
make it probable that he alone cannot preserve the
peace,or protectproperty or persons, he shallapply to
the mayor ,who may appoint or order the appointment

necessarytothat end,whose duty it shallbe to preserve
order and peace , with authority to arrest without war-
rant any one shooting fire-arms , making tumultuous
noises, mischievouslytearingdown , carryingoff, or in-
juring in any way , any signs, gates, fences, or other
property,privateor public,or in any way violatinglaw
or the ordinances of the city. Persons so arrested shall
be taken before the mayor or other magistrate to be dealt
with : Provided, That such prisoners,if arrested between
9 o'clockP. M. and 6 A. M. ,may be committed to jailor
cityprison untilmorning, as elsewhereprovided herein,
in case of arrest by the marshal for drunkenness , riot,
disorderlyconduct,or breach of the peace. Such police-
men shall be allowed a reasonal amount for their ser-
vices by the conncil,and orders drawn on the treasurer
forpayment .

book has been supervisedand equalized,make outa copy

takinghisreceipttherefor,in which shallbe specifiedthe
aggregate amount of taxes so due ; and the clerk of

quired to be paid ; otherwise,the treasurer is to be gov-

CHAPTER 847 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Green Meek , of Johnson county.
WHEREAS ,It appears that Green Meek ,deputy sheriff,
Johnson county, did,in January,1882, pursue and cap-
ture one Joseph Ward ,charged in the county of Johnson
with murder ,and bring him to justice; and it appearing
that said Ward was convicted of said charge ,and isnow
confined in the penitentiary for life; and it appearing
furtherthat the court failed to allow said Meek's claim,
because there had been no affidavitfiledbefore the jus-
tice issuing the warrant for Ward's arrest; therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be , and he is
hereby , directed to draw his warrant on the Treasurer in
favorof Green Meek forthe sum of ninety-eightdollars,
to be paid out of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise
appropriated .
§ 2. This act to take effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 7, 1882.

of Kentucky :
§1 . That the present term of officeof the assessorof

firstTuesday in December , 1885, and until his successor
qualifies; and the term of officeof the assessorof said
cityshallthereafterbe four years.

Approved April 7 , 1882 .

CHAPTER 850 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled" An act to amend and reduce into one
the severalacts relatingto the city of Frankfort," approved April 24 ,
1880 .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the severalwards of the city of Frankfort
shallbe the same as at present constituted.
§ 2. The citycouncil of Frankfort shallconsistof nine
members ,three to be elected,as hereinafterprovided,from
each ward . Said councilmen ,to be eligible,shall be resi-
dents in the wards from which they are returned, and
bonafide freeholders,who shallhave paid theirpolland
other citytaxes sixtydays prior to the day of election.
§ 3. Electionsforcouncilmen shallbe held biennially,
on the firstSaturday in December , beginning with the
currentyear. Every person qualifiedto vote for Repre-
LOC . L , VOL . 11-15

226 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
sentativesin the General Assembly shall be qualified to
vote for councilmen: Provided, That he shall,sixtydays
precedingthe election,have paid allpollor other taxes
due to the city. For the purpose of ascertainingwho
have so paid their taxes,the city marshal or collector
shall,upon payment of taxes,give to each person so pay-
ing a receiptor certificateto that effect,and shallenter
the name of each person so paying sixtydays prior to the
day of electionin a registerto be kept forthat purpose ;
those residentin each ward to be kept separately.
§4 . It shall be the duty of the cityclerk, thirtydays
next preceding the election,to make a copy of said reg-
isteredlistof persons entitledto vote as provided in the
preceding section, and cause the same to be published
four times in a newspaper of the city of Franktort. It
shallalso be the duty of said clerk to furnish the officers
appointed by the citycouncil to hold said electioncopies
of said listduly certifiedby him as clerk.
§ 5. No receipt or certificateof payment of taxes shall
be issuedexcept to such persons as have actuallypaid
their taxes , and a marshal or collector who shall antedate
any receiptor certificateshall be deemed guilty of mis-
feasance of office, and upon indictment and conviction,
shallbe finednot lessthan fiftynor more than fivehundred
dollars .
§ 6. The next electionfor cityattorney shallbe held on
the firstMonday in August , 1884, and each succeeding
electionfor the same shallbe held on the firstMonday in
August quadrennially thereafter,the same qualifications
being required for voters for thisofficeas forcouncilmen .
The councilmen and cityattorneymentioned in thisact
shallenterupon the dischargeof theirdutiesat the time,
and theirterm of officeshallbe as now prescribedby
law .
§ 7. It shall be unlawful for any member of the city
council to contractfor the performance of any work to be
letor authorized by said council,or to be directlyor indi-
rectlyinterestedin any contract,payment for which isto
be made from the citytreasury. Nor shall any member

of the citycouncil take or receive any fee or reward as
attorney ,counsel,or agent for the city,or the transaction
of any business which may come before the council.
§ 8. Any councilman violating the provisions of the
foregoing section shall be liable to indictment in the
Franklin circuitcourt,and upon conviction,shallbe fined
not lessthan fiftynor more than fivethousand dollars, or
imprisoned in the county jailnot less than ten days nor
more than one year ,or be both so fined and imprisoned .
§9 . All proceedings of the citycouncil shall be with
open doors, accessible to the public, and the journal
thereof shall be at all times open to public inspection,
subject only to such regulations as willguard against its
defacement or mutilation .
§ 10. All acts or parts of acts in conflictwith this act
are hereby repealed .
§ 11. This act shall take effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved April 7, 1882.
CHAPTER 851 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe sellinggiving or lending of spirituons, vinous,or
malt liquorsin Pleasurevilleprecinct, in Henry county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shallbe unlawfulfor any person to sell,
give,or loan,in any quantitywhatever,spirituous,vinous,
or malt liquors,or the mixture of either,to any person in
Pleasurevilleprecinct,in Henry county ,
§ 2. Any person violating the firstsection of this act
shallbe finedforeach violationfiftydollars,to be recov-
ered in the name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,by
warrant before a justiceof the peace,or by indictment
of a grand jury.
§ 3. All laws and parts of laws ,special or general, in-
cludingthose passed at thissession,in conflictwith this
act,are hereby repealed .
§ 4. It shallbe the duty of the officersof electionin
Pleasurevilleprecinct,at theAugust electionin 1882,to

228 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
open a poll,and ask each person votingwhether he votes
for or against sellingwhisky in Pleasurevilleprecinct,and

forcefrom and afteritspassage.
Approved April 7 , 1882.
CHAPTER 852 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof the town of Mayslick , in Mason county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the trusteesof the town of Mayslick ,in Mason
county ,shallhave power to impose a tax of from fiftyto
one hundred dollars,at theirdiscretion,upon allhotel and
saloon keepers ,as a licenseto retailspirituous,vinous,or
malt liquors within the corporate limits of the town of
Mayslick , and itshall be unlawful for any person or per-
sons to sellat retailspirituous, vinous,or malt liquorsin
said town without firsthaving obtained a license from
said trustees .
§2 . Any person or persons violatingthe provisions of
thisact shallbe fined twenty dollars for each and every
offense,upon convictionbeforethe policejudge of said
town .
§2 . This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 7, 1882 .
CHAPTER 853 .
AN ACT to prevent personsfrom injuring the streetsand crossingsin the
town of Salyervilleby drawing saw -logsor other heavy draftsthereon.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That any person who shallhereafter injureor de-
stroyany part of the streetsor crossings in the town of
Salyervilleby drawing saw -logs or other heavy drafts

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 229
thereon ,and who shallfailor refuse to repair said streets
or crossingswithin a period of three days afterhaving been
notifiedby the street commissioner or surveyor of the

erableby warrant in the Salyervillepolicecourt,or by

sage .

CHAPTER 855 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe town of Sharon Grove , in Todd county , Ken-
tucky .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Sharon Grove ,in Todd county,be,and the
same is hereby, declareda body -corporateand politic,

230 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
with allthe rights,privileges, and duties conferred upon
towns by chapter 107 of the General Statutes, entitled
" Towns ."
§2 . That said town may , if they see proper , elect a

vided, Itshallnot extendbeyond the limitsof saidtown .
§3 . The term of office of said judge and marshal

§ 4. The limitsof said town shallbe as follows,to-wit :

CHAPTER 857 .
AN ACT to abolish fencing in Logan county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That afterthe adoption of thislaw in said county ,
itshallnot be lawful for the owner of any horse, mule ,
ass,cow ,hog ,sheep, or goat, voluntarilyto permit any
such animal togo at largeoffthepremisesof such owner ;
and the owner of any such animal so permittedto go at
largeshallbe liableto the partyinjuredforalldamages

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 231
done to the fruitor shade trees,or ornamental shrubbery
or crops of any person ,to be recovered before any court
of competent jurisdiction,and the judgment of the court
shallbe a lien upon the stock causing the same .
§2 . Be it further, enacted, That any person in said
county who isthe owner ,or lawfullyin thepossessionof
any land , or his agent , on which there is growing or
planted,or has been grown during the year,a crop of
any kind , shallhave the right to take possession of any
animal named in the preceding section, if found at large
or uncontrolled on the premises of such person; and when
so taken up ,such person,or his agent,so taking up such
animal,shallforthwithnotifypersonally,or by leavinga
written notice at the usual place of residence of the
nearest justice of the peace in his districtof the same ,
or the owner of such animal , within twelve hours after
the taking up .
§3 . Be itfurtherenacted, That ifany person takingup
such animal on his premises shall take such animal off
his premises before he shall have previously or in writ-
ing by himself or agent notifiedeitherthe owner thereof
or the nearest justice of the peace of the said district,he .
shallbe guiltyof a misdemeanor ,and be fined in any
sum not exceeding one hundred dollars .
§ 4. Be it furtherenacted, That it shall be the duty of
any person or his agent taking up any animal as in sec-
tion second to make affidavitbefore the justice of the
peace eitherthat he knows the owner or that he does not
know the owner thereof , and if the former affidavit is
made ,then the said justiceof the peace shall issue a no-
ticeto the owner of the same or his agent ; which notice
shallbe served on the owner or his agent , or leftat his
house or usual place of residence,eitherby the person
taking up such animal or by the constable of the district,
or by some other person appointed by the saidjustice
for that purpose as the justice may order; and upon
applicationof the owner or his agent ,the justice shall
give him an orderforhis saidproperty,he firstpaying to
the justicehisfees and the fees hereinafternamed for the

232 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
takingup and keeping such animals :Provided, That the
owner or his agent shallhave the rightto the order with-
out paying any fee for taking up or keeping such animal ,
if he fileswith the justice an affidavitsetting forthany
fact showing that the person had no right under thisact
to take up such animal; and ifthe taker-up makes affida-
vit that the owner of such animal is not known ,then such
person shallproceed in accordance with the laws provided
inthe case of estrays,and no feesshallbe charged either
by the justiceof the peace or constablefor the affidavit
and serving notice eitherto the taker-up or owner of such
animal .
§5 . Be itfurtherenacted, That when the owner makes
affidavitand procures an order,as in section fourth, for
his animal ,the taker-up shalldeliverto the owner ,or his
agent,said animal, in accordance with such order,and
may then,if the fees for the taking up and keeping such
animal have not been already paid ,have the right to sue
for and recover such fees,for the taking up and keeping
such animal , from the owner thereof in any court of
competent jurisdiction; and ifthe judge or justicebefore
whom the same is triedis satisfiedof the justiceof such
claim,accordingto the provisionsof thisact,under the
same rulesand regulationsas is applied to the trialof
civil causes in said courts, he shall render judgment
for the same , and the judgment rendered shall be a
lien on the property doing the damage or going at large:
Provided, If the owner or his agent shall not claim as
aforesaidhis animal in ten days afterhe or the justice of
the peace is notifiedof the taking up of such animal ,
then the same shall be considered as an estray, and the
taker-up ,or his agent ,shall proceed in relation to such
animal in the same manner as in the case of an estray
when the owner is unknown .
§6. Be itfurtherenacted,That for taking up and keep-
ing such animals the following fees shall be allowed for
the use of the person taking up , viz: for taking up each
horse, mule , or ass, one dollar; cow or hog ,fiftycents;
goat or sheep,twenty-fivecents; one halfthe above sums

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 233
every day each such animals are kept by the takerup
Young animals not capable of doing damage to crops
shallnot be charged for,provided the taker-up or his
agent shall not be liable for injuriesto animals ,or for
escapes ,except in cases of willfulneglect, or where such
damage was intended .
§7 . Be itfurtherenacted,That the rightof appeal shall
be had in allcases as in other civilcauses ,and that liens
shallbe lostunder the provisionsof thislaw ,unlessthe
plaintiffin such judgments proceeds, without delay, to
enforce such liensby alllawful means .
§ 8. Be itfurtherenacted,That judgments may be ren-
dered by justices of the peace for amounts within their
jurisdictionon ten days' noticeto defendants,and exe-
cution may issue thereon after five days from rendition;
and the justiceof the peace of the districtwhere the
damage is done by any animal , or where the fees for
taking up and the keeping of such animals are incurred,
shallhave jurisdictionin such cases,notwithstanding the
defendant may livein another districtor another county ,
and for any amount not exceeding one hundred dollars:
Provided, That where there is no justiceof the peace
residing in such district,or where the justices in such
districtare disqualifiedby interest or any other lawful
cause, then any justiceof the peace in any adjoining
districthas jurisdictionof such cases in accordance with
the provisions of thisact.
§ 9. Be itfurther enacted, That this act shall not take
effectuntil it shall be accepted by a majority of the
qualified voters of the county by a vote to be taken at
the firstgeneral electionhad afterthe passage of thisact.
And to ascertain thisfact the sheriffof said county shall
cause a column to be opened in the poll-books at said
election, and the question propounded to each voter:
" Are you for or against the act to abolish fencing in
Logan ," and his vote shall be recorded accordingly; and
if a majority of those voting at said election shallbe in
favorof itsadoption,thisact shallgo intoeffectin sixty
days thereafter .

234 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 10. Be it furtherenacted, That should a majorityof
the votersof thiscounty decideagainstitsadoption,any
two or more adjoiningdistrictsshallhave the right,by
permission of the county court,to have an election in
saiddistrictsas to whether or no they willavailthem-
selvesof the provisionsof thisact,the expenses of said
electionto be paid by the districtsso desiringsuch elec-
tion, and collectableas other taxes.
Approved April 7, 1882.

CHAPTER 858 .
AN ACT to authorizeprovisionforthe payment of therailroadindebted-
ness of Clark county.
WHEREAS , The bonds issuedby Clark county in pay-
ment of itssubscriptionof $200,000to the capitalstock
of the Lexington and Big Sandy RailroadCompany will
mature on the 15th day of April, 1883,and there will
then be outstanding a considerable.number thereof, which
the sinking fund of said county willbe insufficientto pay ;
now ,in order to make provision for the payment of said
bonds when they mature,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky:
§1 . That thecounty courtof Clark county (a majority
of the justices thereof concurring therein) be ,and it is
hereby ,authorized and empowered to issuethe bonds of
said county for the balance due on said outstanding
bonds at or after the date of their maturity as aforesaid,
to be of the denomination of not less than five hundred
nor more than one thousand dollars($ 1,000) each,paya-
ble at such place and on such terms as the said court may
determine ,and to bear interestat a rate not to exceed six
percent.per annum ,payable semi-annually, or at such
lower rateas the courtmay deem advisable. Said bonds
authorizedtobe issuedby thisact shallbe signedby the
presidingjudge and clerkof saidcourt,and have the seal
of saidcounty; and eitherthey or theirproceeds,as said
courtmay direct, shallbe used in redeeming or paying

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 235
said outstanding bonds that will mature as aforesaid on
the 15thday of April, 1883,or such of them as may then
remain unpaid . Said bonds authorized by this act may
run for a period not exceeding fifteenyears,at the discre-
tion of the court,and the obligationto pay interestthere-
on shallbe evidencedby coupons attachedtheretosigned
by the clerk of said court.
§2 . That forthe purpose of providingforthe punctual
payment of the intereston the bonds herein authorized to
be issued,and for the finalliquidationof same and of the
other indebtedness of said county incurred by virtue of
itssubscriptionto the capitalstock of the Elizabethtown ,
Lexington and Big Sandy Railroad Company and to the
capitalstock of the Kentucky Union Railway Com-
pany , the sinking fund created under an act, entitled
" An act to establisha sinkingfund forClark county,"
approved March 4, 1856, and the amendment there-
to, entitled " An act in relation to the sinking fund
of Clark county ," approved March 1, 1860, is hereby
continued, and the provisionsof said acts are hereby
continued in full force and effect until all of said in-
debtedness is satisfiedand discharged : Provided, how-
ever, That said county court shall have power to levy
a greater tax than twenty -five cents on each hundred
dollars' worth of taxable property in said county ,but not
to exceed fiftycents on same in any one year .
§3 . This act shallbe in force from and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 7, 1882.
CHAPTER 859 .
AN ACT to incorporateBethel Hill and Jessamine County Turnpike Com-
pany .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That a company may be formed and created a
body-politic,by the name and styleof the " Bethel Hill
and Jessamine County Turnpike Road Company ," forthe
purpose of building a turnpike road from a point on the

236 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Danville, Lancaster and NicholasvilleTurnpike Road ,
near Robards' Chapel,in Jessaminecounty,viathe hillat
Bethel meeting -house and Glass's Mills,to intersectthe
Woodford County and Clover Bottom Turnpike Road , at
Wilmore Station, on the Cincinnati Southern Railroad .
§2 . The capitalstock of said company shallbe eight
thousand dollars,tobe dividedintosharesof fiftydollars
each .
§3 . Books forthe subscriptionof stock shallbe opened
by John Wilmore ,Robert Scott,J. H. Glass,Geo . Over-
street, Wm . Combs , and Melvin Land ,who are hereby
appointed commissionersfor said purpose; and the sub-
scribersto the stock of said company shallenter into an
obligationas follows in the books of said commissioners :
" We ,whose names are hereunto subscribed,promise to
pay the president and directorsof the Bethel Hill and
Jessamine County Turnpike Road Company the sum of
fiftydollarsfor each share of stock by us subscribed and
set opposite our respective names , in such proportions
and at such times as shall be required by said corpora-
tion ." When the sum of six thousand dollars shall have
been subscribedto the capital stock of said company , it
shallbe the duty of any two or more of the commission-
ers named in this section to give notice,in such manner
as they may deem expedient,for a meeting of the stock-
holders,at such time and place as the noticemay specify,
for the purpose of electing a president,four directors,and
a treasurer . One vote shall be allowed for each share of
stock , and the officers so elected shall continue in office
for one year , or until their successors are elected and
qualified. The manner of giving the notice, and the
place of allelectionsafter the first,shall be fixedby the
presidentand directors; but the day of electionshallbe
thesame in each year : Provided, The stockholdersmay
change the time of theirannual meeting,a majorityof
all the votes concurring .
§4 . Every presidentand directorof saidcompany ,be-
fore he enters on the duties of his office,shallswear or
affirm,beforesome personauthorizedto administeroaths,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 237
that he will well and truly discharge the duties of his
said officeto the best of his judgment , without partiality
or prejudice.
§ 5. As soon as saidcompany isorganizedby the elec-
tion of officers, the president and directors shall be a
body -politicand corporate,in factand in law ,under the
name and styleaforesaid; and by that name shallhave
perpetualsuccession,and privilegesand franchisesinci-
dent to a corporation; shall be capable of holding their
capitaland the increaseand profitsthereof,and of taking
and holding , by purchase or gift, all such lands, tene-
ments ,hereditaments ,and real and personal property ,as
may be necessary for the prosecution of their work or
the objectsof the corporation; they shallhave power to
contract and be contracted with ,sue and be sued ,plead
and be impleaded , answer and be answered ,in any court
of law or equity, or elsewhere ; also to have and use a
common seal, and , generally, to do any act,matter, or
thing which a corporationmay lawfullydo to effectthe
objects for which thiscorporation was created.
§6 . The president and directorsshall fixand regulate
the grade and elevation of said road , which elevation
shallnot exceed four degrees; the width of the part to be
covered with stone; shall designate the place for the
erection of gates; may fixthe rates of toll,regulate and
change the same ,but the toll shall not be greater than
the law permits on other turnpike roads in this State.
They may enlarge the capital stock to such an amount
as they may deem necessary,and open subscriptionthere-
forin such manner as they may direct. Whenever said
road shallhave been completed to Glass'sMills,they shall
have power to erect a toll-gate and charge tollthereat,
and to erect another gate at which tollmay be collected
when the remainder of said road shall have been com-
pleted .
§7 . All the dividends at any gate on said road shallbe
applied in aid of the constructionof said road , untilthe
same is completed ; after the road is completed ,the net
profitsthereofshallbe paid pro ratasemi-annuallytothe
stockholders in said road .

238 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 8. The presidentand directorswith theirsurveyors,
engineers,artists,and chain-carriersare hereby author-
ized and empowered to enter in and upon the lands and
inclosures, public roads and highways , in, through , and
over which the intended road may pass,and to examine
and surveythe ground most proper for that purpose; to
examine quarries or beds of stone,gravel,and other ma-
terialnecessary for the constructionof said road .
§ 9. Itshallbe lawfulforthe presidentand directors,
with theirsuperintendents,engineers,and workmen with
their tools, instruments, carts, wagons , and other car-
riages, and their beasts of draught or burthen , to enter
upon the lands in and over ,contiguous and near to,which
said road shall pass, having firstgiven notice of their
intention to the owners or occupiers thereof, or their
agents: Provided, That if the presidentand directors
shall not agree with the owners of said land through
which said road is to pass as to the damages the owner
or owners may sustain by the road passing through their
land,thepresidentand directorsshallapplyto thecounty
courtof Jessamine county fora writof ad quad damnum
to assess the damages which may be sustained by the
owner or owners of said land; and upon payment or
tender of the damages assessed,itshall be lawful for the
president and directors to open and make said road ,and
dig and carry away any stone,gravel,or other material
necessary for the construction or repairing of said road .
§ 10. The president and directorsmay require payment
of the stock subscribed at such times and in such install-
ments as they shalldeem necessary; and any stockholder
failingto pay the amount of the call upon him for thirty
days afterthe same shallbecome due and payable,shall
pay in addition thereto one per cent. on said amount ,
and two per cent.per month for every month itremains
unpaid thereafter.
§ 11. The presidentand directorsshalldelivera certifi-
cate, signed by the president and countersigned by the
treasurer,and sealed by the seal of the corporation, to
each share-holder foreach share by him or her subscribed

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 239
and held,which certificateshallbe transferableon the
books of said company ,in person or by attorney,but no
share shallbe transferreduntilallarrearages thereon are
paid . The original certificateof the share or shares
transferred shall be surrendered , and a new certificate
given to the purchaser ,who shall be entitledto all the
benefitsand privilegesthat would have accrued to the
original owner .
.
§ 12. The treasurer of the company ,before he enters
on the dutiesof his office,shallgive bond and security
as the presidentand directorsmay require, payable to
them , conditioned to pay over any amount in his hands
to the order of the president and directors.
§ 13. The county courtof Jessamine county shallhave
power , a majority of the justices concurring therein, to
subscribe a sum not exceeding one thousand dollarsper
mile to the capitalstockof said road,to be regarded as
other stock .
§ 14. If any person shall go around or turn off from
said road with the fraudulentintent of avoiding payment
of the toll, as fixed by the president and directors,he or
they shall,on conviction thereof before any justiceof the
peace of said county ,be fined five dollars and costs, to
be collected as other sums within the jurisdictionof the
Commonwealth .
§ 15. Itshallbe lawful for the president and directorsof
the said company ,for the more thoroughly good govern-
ment of the same ,to make any set of by -laws not incon-
sistentwith the provisionsofthisactand the Constitution
and laws of Kentucky .
§ 16. That no person or persons over whose land the
saidroad may run shallbe charged,in theirdailyoccu-
pation,forgoing through the gates,in passingfrom one
part of the farm to another; nor shallany person be
charged tollfor going to or from church on said road on
the Sabbath day .
§ 17. No person who isnot a share-holderin said cor-
porationshallbe allowed to vote at any electionor hold
any officein the company , nor shall any stockholder

240 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
vote,or be entitled to any right of a member of said
company ,unlessthe whole amount due and payable, as
aforesaid, on each share by him held, shallhave been
paid, agreeable to the requisitionsof the president.
§ 18. That for failureto keep said road in repairit
shall be lawful for the county court of said county to
enter an order that tolls for travel on said road should
not be paid; then,insteadof and penaltywhich the law
imposes for such failure,the company shall be forbidden
to charge tollat theirgates; and ,should they offend,they
shall be finable in any sum not less than one nor more
than fivedollars,beforeany justiceof the peace of said
county,in actionof debt by any person aggrieved: Pro-
vided, The presidentof saidcompany shouldhave notice
of such order of the county court. After such order no
tollsshallbe charged until said court is satisfiedthat the
road has been sufficientlyrepaired, or untila justiceof
the peace , from personal inspection of the road , shall
certifythat it has been repaired,and that, in his judg-
ment ,tollsought to be charged , which certificateshallbe
filedin the county clerk'soffice, and then be recorded by
the clerk,forwhich the lattershallbe paid twenty-five
cents by the company ; and as soon as so filedtollsmay
be charged and collectedon said road.
§ 19. If the subscribersto the company hereby author-
ized shallnot become so far organized as to electa presi-
dent and directorswithin two years from the passage of
this act, and within one year thereaftermake bona fide
contracts for the construction of said road as far as the
saidGlass'sMills,the privilegesof thiscorporationshall
cease,and thisact be void .
§20 . This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved April 7 , 1882.

CHAPTER 860 .

§3 . The said company shall have power to purchase , Powers.
acquire,and hold such real estate,in the State of Ken-
tuckyand elsewhere,by gift, lease,or purchase,as may

or otherwise, not exceeding in amount one half of its
capitalstock; to buy ,erect,conduct, and controlany or
allbuildings and warehouses for manufacturing or depot
purposes,and allmachinery,fixtures,and personalprop-
ertydeemed necessaryor proper in carryingon itsbusi-

ness; to sell, convey , or transfer, by deed or mortgage ,
any part of,or any interestin, itsestate,real and per-
sonal,corporealor incorporeal. It shallhave power to

erty, and evidenced and transferredas may be prescribed

forthe faithfulperformanceof theirdutiesastheymay

proper ; it may receive cereal grains and other personal

CHAPTER 867 .

and a half east seventy-four poles to a beech at a ledge
of rock; thence south thirty-nine east thirtypoles to a
beech in R F. Barr's field; thence north sixty-three east
sixtypoles to a stake in R. B. Robinson'sfield; thence
north thirteen west ninety-two poles to the beginning ,

day in June , by the legally qualifiedvoters, citizens of

succession, who ,in their corporate name aforesaid,shall

248 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
have power and capacity to sue and be sued ,implead or

ityof them may act.

may purchase and hold lotsfor a graveyard,church,and

said town , a police judge and town marshal ,to be styled

mansville ; the policejudge to have the same jurisdiction.
power ,and capacity,entitledto the same feesand emol-
uments,and subjectto the same liabilitiesand responsi-
bilitiesas a justiceof the peace for Todd county. The

ities,duties,and responsibilitiesas a constablefor Todd

proved by the court, with the same conditions and penal-

pointedby the Todd circuitcourtor any judge thereof.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 249
§ 5. All officersherein mentioned shallbe firstduly

$ 6. One half the amount of all fines and judgments

ertybought by,or donated to,said corporation,not dis-
posed of,shallrevertto the originaldonor or vendor .

CHAPTER 871 .
AN ACT forthebenefitof thecommon schoolsof Lincolncounty.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth.
of Kentucky :
§1 . That any trusteesof a common school within the
county of Lincoln ,who may desireto increase the school
fund of his district,ishereby authorized and empowered
tolevyand collectan annual ad valoremtaxon allpatrons
of said school who ,in the discretionof said trustee,may
be pecuniarilyableto pay such tax.
§2 . Each patron shall be taxed in proportion to the
number of children he may send to said school, and said
tax shall be levied on all the lands,goods , and chattels
included within the boundary of said district: Provided,
That saidassessmentshallbe made only upon patronsof
said school .
§3 . The said trusteeshallappoint a suitableperson to
assess said tax , who shall act under oath , and make a
true and correct report to said trustee of the names of
each patron in said school district, and the amount of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 253
taxable propertyin said districtowned by them . Said
person ,so appointed ,shall be possessed of all the privi-
leges and powers now conferred on county assessors.
§4 . The said trusteeis alsoempowered and authorized
to appoint a suitableperson to collectsaid tax when
levied, and before entering upon his duties as such col-
lectorhe shallexecute to the said trusteea bond ,payable
to said trustee,for the benefit of said school district,in
double the amount of tax required to be collected. Said
collector shall be vested with all the powers and privi-
leges now given to sheriffsfor the collectionof the county
revenue , and shall be entitledto the same fees and per
cent . as sheriffs are now entitled to for similar services .
§5 Said act shall not operate so as to deprive any
person , not able to pay taxes under the provisions of this
act, from sending their children to any common school
within his or her district,which districtmay have availed
itselfof the provisions of thisact.
§6 . The trustee so ordering, levying, and collecting
the tax as herein provided , when the same shall have
been collectedand paid over to him as such trustee, shall
use the same in connection with the tax now raised by
the State, and due said district,in employing and paying
a competent teacher for the teaching of said school.
Said school shallbe taught and governed by and under
the laws now prescribed for teaching a common school
in this Commonwealth .
§7 . It shallbe the duty of each trustee,at the terminus
of his office,to make to the incoming trusteea full and
complete statement of the amount of tax collected, of
whom collected, and how expended , and report such
surplusremaining in his hands (if any), and pay over
the same to such incoming trustee,which statement shall
be recordedin a book kept forthat purpose,which book
shallbe kept by the trustee,but shallat alltimes be sub-
ject to inspectionby the school commissioner, or any
patron of the school.
§8 . Said school shallbe taught for at leastfivemonths

254 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
in each year,and no patronshallbe taxedin any one
year more than fivedollars on each pupil sent to school.
§9 . This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 7, 1882 .
CHAPTER 872 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Mutual Life Aid Associationof Mayfield, Ken-
tucky .

of Kentucky :
Incorporators .
Style .
§ 1. That D. G. Smith , P. L. Henson , L. L. Henson ,

Neale , and othersthat they may associatewith them ,

of Mayfield ,Kentucky ; and in said name and styleshall
have the rightto contract,purchase,hold,and sellprop-
erty, to sue and be sued in all the courts of this State,
for the purpose of operating the business hereinafter de-
scribed .
Purposes
§2 . The object of this association is to unite all ac-
ceptable white persons between the ages of fifteenand
sixty-six years into a benevolent associationfor mutual
protection, to promote morality, temperance , industry,
economy , benevolence , and charity, and to ameliorate
theconditionof sufferinghumanity by providinga bene-
ficiaryfund on the plan of mutual assessment and en-
dowment in which the living and the representatives of

the executing board of the association shall determine .
§3 . That said association shall have the power to
make and adopt a constitution,by-laws and regulations,
not contrary to the Constitution and laws of this State
or of the United States,for the government and regula-

associationshallbe exempt from the debts of the associa

pany organized, said company shallpossessallthe au-

258 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
thorityand power and rightsand privileges,and may do
allactsand thingsnecessaryto enablethe saidcompany
to build said turnpike road ,erectionof gates,collection
of tolls, appointing all necessaryengineers, surveyors,
and agents as shallbe necessary for the building and suc-
cessful occupation ,use, and operating of said road ,and
forkeeping same in repair.
§ 7. That said presidentand directorsshallfix and reg-
ulate the elevation and grade of said road ,the width or
part thereof to be covered with stone or gravel, and the
width or part thereof to be elevated and graded and left
uncovered with stone or gravel. They shall designate
the place for the erection of gates not exceeding one for
every five miles of said road : Provided, The width of
said road shallnot exceed fiftyfeet,and the part covered
with stone or gravel shall not be less than sixteen feet
wide and twelve inches deep ,and the part graded not
lessthan twenty -two feet.
§ 8. The president and directors shall deliver a cer-
tificatesigned by the president and countersigned by the
treasurer to each stockholder for the shares by him held,
which certificate shall be transferable on the books of
said company according to the order of such stockholder;
but no share shall be transferred until all calls or arrear-
ages thereon are paid.
§ 9. That the president of the company or board may
call meetings of the board at such times and places as
may be deemed proper and a majorityof the directors
shall think necessary, for the transaction of business.
They shallkeep a record of itsproceedings,to be entered
in book to be procured by them for that purpose; and
each day's proceedings shall be signed by the presiding
officer,afterbeing approved by the board .
§ 10. The president and directorsmay callon the sub-
scribersof stock for any portion of theirstock subscribed,
not exceeding one fourth of the total amount at a time ,
nor on shorter periods than three months after the first
call. The firstcallof one fourth may be called forand
payment enforcedat any time aftersaidboard isorgan

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 259
ized,untilallthe stock subscribedshallbe paid in by the
subscribers of stock .
§ 11. That in order to enable said company to build
their said turnpike road as speedilyas possible, and
equalize the burthens thereof, there is hereby levied on
allproperty,real, mixed,and personal,subjectto taxa-
tion forState revenue purposes,lyingand being within
two miles thereof from its commencement at Vanceburg
to itsterminus at Concord , and between the Ohio river
and parallel line thereto from said termini as aforesaid,
the sum of fiftycents upon each one hundred dollars'
worth of taxable property as aforesaid each year , com-
mencing with year 1882,and continue until said road is
builtand paid for, which tax as aforesaidshallbe as-
sessed and collectedas hereinafterprovided, and shall be
expended in buildingsaidroad as collectedeach year.
12. That said company may appoint an assessor to
assess the said property owned by persons subject to tax-
ationas aforesaidforthe buildingand completionof said
road . The assessment shall be returned to the May or
June term of the Lewis county court in each year ,which
shallbe subject to revisionand correction by the Lewis
county court, and said county court shallcause the same
to be made out and listedwith the sheriffof Lewis county
for collection . Said sheriff shall receive and collect said
taxes, and pay the same over to the treasurer of said
turnpike road company on or before the firstday of
December in each year ; and for any default of said
sheriffunder this act,he and his sureties on his official
bond shallbe liableto suitor suitsfor the recovery there-
of. Said suit or suits shall be brought in the name of
the president of said Vanceburg and Concord Turnpike
Road Company for the use of said company in the Lewis
circuitcourt. The judgment shallbe for the amount of
taxes collectedby said officer,and which he might have
collectedby due diligence, with the same interest and
costsand damages now allowed by law against sheriffs
for failure to collectand pay over the county levy and
revenue . The clerk of the county court shallbe allowed.

260 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and have such fees for what he may be required to do
under thisact as allowed to him forsimilarservices per-
formed by him for the county or other similarcorpora-
tions, to be paid by the company out of funds in the
hands of their treasurer .
§ 13. All persons who pay taxes under thisact towards
the building of said turnpike road shall be stockholders
in said company for the amount of taxes they shallthus
pay , and proper certificatesthereof shall be delivered to
them thereforwhenever they shall have paid an amount
equal to twenty -fivedollars, and so on until the road is
completed .
§ 14. That it shallbe the privilegeof each person
through whose land said turnpike road may run or pass,
and who may have subscribedstock, to work out the
value of his said stock on the road -bed through his land,
at the estimated price for labor done , or he shallhave the
preference at the lowest bid therefor; and all subscribers
of stock shall have the privilege of working out their
stocksubscribedby them under the directionof the presi-
dent and directorsof the road at the customary prices
forlabor. They must ,however , work under the direction
of the president and directorsof said company , or their
authorized agents .
§15 . That whenever the resources of said company are
such that, with the aid of the county subscriptionof one
thousand dollarsper mile, as anthorized by acts hereto-
fore passed , it will be enabled to build its road one mile
or more ,and when the same shallhave been actuallylaid
out and located by a competent engineer , and the right
of way secured, and proof of these facts satisfactorily
made known to the county court of Lewis county ,said
court shallbe authorized to make the subscriptionof one
thousand dollars per mile, and so on for each mile so
made and ready for travel by the public untilthe entire
road iscompleted .
§16 . That persons who shall subscribe stock to said
company ,and shallhave paid the same ,eitherin money
or work as aforesaid,shallnot be compelled to pay the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 261
specialtaxes levied by section eleven of thisact untilthe
entireamount of taxes assessed against them respective-
lyshallequalthe amount which theymay have subscribed
and paid in stock as aforesaid. Their subscriptionsshall
be considered and treated as taxes paid in advance ,and all
the tax-payers being stockholdersto the amount of taxes
so paid by them towards the building of said road ; the
intentionbeing to equalize allpersons along the route of
saidroad subjectto the payment of taxes thereon; and
the taxes against persons who may have subscribed and
paid their stock as aforesaid shall be canceled , and not
listedfor collectionuntilequalized as aforesaid.
§ 17. The county court of Lewis county ,in additionto the
subscriptionof one thousand dollarsper mile,shall sub-
scribe stock in one half the cost of building a bridge over
Sycamore creek,near Concord ,on the line of said road ,
and shallissuethe bonds of said county for that purpose
upon the same terms as otherbonds authorized tobe issued
by saidcountyforbuildingbridgesinsaidcounty;and upon
the subscriptionand issualof the bonds by said county as
aforesaid,forthe building of said turnpike road and the
bridge across Sycamore creek,the county shallbecome a
stockholder in said company to the amount thus sub-
scribed. The stockof Lewis county shallbe represented
and voted at any and allelectionsof officers,& c., of said
company by the county judge of said county. That to
pay the subscriptionsthus made by county judge to
thecapitalstock of said company of one thousand dol-
larsper mile,saidcounty judge ishereby authorizedand
requiredto issuethe bonds of Lewis county,payable at
not longer date than ten years,to bear interestat six
per cent. per annum , which bonds may be in such ,
and principal and interest payable at such place or
places as the county judge may direct. Said bonds
shall be delivered to the president of said company ,
who shall execute a receipt for same in the name of
the company , and upon the deliveryof said bonds to
said president,the county of Lewis shallbecome a stock-
holder in said company to the amount of one thou

262 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
sand dollarsper mile. The officersof saidturnpikeroad
company shall execute a bond or bonds in the Lewis
county court, upon the deliveryof the county bonds as
aforesaidto the president of said company , for the faith-
fulexpenditure of said bonds or publicfunds to the build-
ing of said turnpike road as provided by the act of the
General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
approved April 9 , 1873.
§ 18. The compensation of the president and directors
shallbe fixedby the company in theirby-laws,and shall
not exceed two dollarsper day forthe presidentand one
dollar per day for the directors for each day's service
actuallyperformed by them respectively; and afterthe
road isfinished,the same may be wholly discontinued or
reduced to a nominal consideration .
§19 . The company shallhave the power and rightto
pass by -laws prohibiting " dead -locking " on said road ,
trespassing,or injuriesdone to the same ,and the evading
of paying of tollsfor traveldone thereon, and to recover
damages or finesthereforby proper proceedingsin the
name of itspresident,before a magistrate of the district
in which the same shall have been committed or done .
§20 . That allacts and parts of acts in conflictwith this
act are hereby repealed.
§ 21. This act shall be in force from and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved April 7, 1882 .
CHAPTER 875 .
AN ACT to incorporate the Daviess Gravel Road Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That a corporation ishereby created in the county
of Daviess ,to be known as the Daviess Gravel Road
Company ,and by thatname shallhave perpetualsucces-
sion, and sue and be sued ; and may have a seal, and
alterand change the same at pleasure.
§2 . The business of said corporation shallbe to grade ,
construct,gravel, macadamize , and otherwise make and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 263
,
improve roads leading from the eastern part of the city
of Owensboro to the Daviess county line, and to erect
gates and receive and collecttollsfrom persons,vehicles,
and animals passing over such roads when constructed
as hereinafter provided; and shall have all incidental
powers to carry out said objects.
§3 . The capital stock of said company shall be one
hundred thousand dollars,divided into shares of fiftydol-
lars each ; and J. W. M. Fields, William P. Baker ,J. H.
Bill,and M. V. Monarch are appointedcommissionersto
open books and receive subscriptionsfor stock in said
corporation, any one of whom may act. When ten
thousand dollars of stock shall have been subscribed,
said company may organize by electingfivedirectors,
all of whom shall be stockholders in said corporation;
one of whom shall be elected president of the board of
directors,and said board of directorsand the president
so elected shall hold their officesfor one year from the
ime of their election, and until their successors shall
have been duly elected. The directorsshall be elected
by the stockholders, each share entitlingthat holder to
one vote,which may be castby proxy or in person; and
when so organized ,may begin the construction of said
road . The president and directors,when organized ,may
adopt such by-laws and regulationsfor the conduct of
thebusinessof saidcorporation,consistentwith the laws
of the State, as they may deem fitand proper. They
may appoint such subordinate agents ,including a treas-
urer, as they may deem proper , and require bond and
security for the performance of their respective duties.
The said corporationmay acquirethe rightof way by
grant or gift,and the county court of Daviess county is
hereby empowered to grant to the said corporation the
rightto construct said roads in and over any part of the
publicroadsleadingfrom the easternpartof saidcityof
Owensboro to the county lineof Daviess county ,by orders
entered upon the order-book of said court, so long as
such roadsso authorizedshallbe kept in good condition
for travelthereon. And the president and directors of

264 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
said corporation shall be liable to be proceeded against
and fined in the same manner that surveyors of public
roads now are liablefor failingto keep said roads in
repair and good traveling condition .
§ 4. The road or roads to be constructed under this act
shallbe of such grade and such width,and such thickness
of metal , as the president and directorsmay determine
and fix. And when said road shallbe completed as much
as two miles from the limits of the city of Owensboro ,
said corporation may erect gates and charge tollsat the
rates hereinafter prescribed; and may erect other gates
at the distance of fivemiles from each other,and charge
tollsas prescribed in thisact.
§5 . When said corporation shall desire to acquire the
rightof way for such road over the lands of any person
or corporation, may filea petition in the clerk'sofficeof
the county court of Daviess , stating that such right of
way is necessary to the proper location of such road ,
describing the parts of such land which they desire to
pass over,and asking the court to appoint commissioners
to assess the damages which will result from locating
said road thereon to the owners of the land whose names
shall be given, and shall be made defendants to such
petition, and thereupon the clerk shall issue a summons
on said petition, summoning the landholders to appear
and answer such petitionon a day to be fixedin the sum-
mons ,which day may be either in vacation or in term
time , or enter a warning order, if the landholder be a
non -resident,warning such defendantto appear on such
day ,not less than twenty days from the time of making
said warning order; and on the day so fixedin the sum-
mons ,the said county shall,by order entered by the clerk
on the order-book , appoint these commissioners to go
upon the land over which the right of way may be
sought,and after being duly sworn assess the damages
of every descriptionwhich willresultto the lands and
persons by the appropriation thereof to the use of said
corporationand itsroad,who shallreportthe damages.
which will result to the landholder as tenant or in fee ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 265
and at the time of court to which said reportshallbe,
the court shall have said report, and if not shown to
be unjust to eitherparty, shallcompare the report, order
that said corporation may establishitsroad thereon upon
paying to the persons to whom the same may be due the
damages so assessed; and upon paying such damages ,
said corporation may proceed to use and occupy said
lands by constructing theirroad thereon . Either party
may appeal from the judgment of the county court to
the circuit, so as to raise the question to whether the
amount of damages assessed were correct,but the judg-
ment of the county court shall be final as to all other
questions
§6 . The said corporation have power and authority to
charge and collect such tolls, on such roads or parts of
roads as they may construct, as prescribedin chapter
110, General Statutes .
§7 . The county court, when a majority thereof deem
it necessary, may ,by order made at a regular term of
themajoritycourt,take possessionand maintain control
of said road for the benefit of the people,provided they
shallat the same time provide for the reimbursement of
said company for the cost incurred in the construction of
saidroad and the improvements thereon, and to this end
the said company shallkeep a faithfuland full account
of said expenses .
§ 8. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 7, 1882.
