CHAPTER 880 .
AN ACT to amend an act and to reduce into one the severalarts incorpo-
ratingtheMachpelahCemeteryCompany ,of Montgomery county.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That A. T. Wood , J. D. Wilson, Fred. Senieur,
Harry Campbell, and W. S. Thomas , directorsof the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 267
Machpelah Cemetery Company , who were electeddi-
rectors of said company by Watson Lodge , No. 32 , I.
O. O. F., and theirsuccessors, assigns, and associates,
be, and they are hereby, continued and constituteda
body -corporate and politic,under the name and styleof
Machpelah Cemetery Company ; to have perpetualsuc-
cession; may sue and be sued ,contract and be contracted
with.plead and be impleaded , defend and be defended
against,in allthe courts in thisCommonwealth and else-
where ; may have and use a common corporateseal,and
thesame alter,break,and renew atpleasure. Said board
ofdirectorsand theirsuccessorsin officemay establishsuch
by-laws,rulesand regulations,as they may deem expedi-
ent for the goverment , arrangement , and disposition of
the property and effectsof said company ,and to alterits
by -laws ,and do all other acts and things necessary and
proper for effectingthe object of theircorporation,which
is to provide a place of repose for the dead : Provided,
however,They do not act inconsistentwith the laws of the
State of Kentucky or the United States.
§2 . That Watson Lodge . No. 32, I. O. O. F., of Mt.
Sterling, Kentucky ,itbeing the owner of allthe stock in
said cemetery company , shall,on the firstmeeting night
of said lodge in April, 1882, and every two years there-
after,elect,by ballot,fivedirectorsfrom among the mem-
bers of said lodge who are in good standing therein, to
serve for two years,and untiltheirsuccessors are elected;
and said directorsshallelectfrom among theirnumber a
president, secretary,and treasurer,to serve during their
term of office. Said directors, or any of them ,shallbe
subject to removal by said lodge for any misconduct in
office .
§3 . That the president and directorsof said company
shallkeep a recordof allof itsproceedings,and an ac
count of allreceiptsand disbursements of said company ,
and make a report to said lodge at the close of each
officialyear . A majority of said board of directorsshall
constitute a quornm to do business; and in case of a
vacancy in said board of directors, such vacancy shall

268 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
be filledby said lodge as itmay directfor the unexpired
term of said office .
§ 4. That said corporation shall have power to pur-
chase ,take by gift,hold,transfer,and sella tractor tracts
of land in additionto what they already own , not ex-
ceeding seventy-five acres, within one mile of Mt. Ster-
ling,Kentucky , to be used as a burying-ground,which,
togetherwiththe grounds they now own ,shallbe forever
freefrom any interferenceof turnpikes,railroads,or other
public improvements ,unless by the consent of the direct-
ory. Said directory may improve the same by suitable
avenues ,walks ,embellishments and adornments ; may sub-
divide the grounds into lots suitable for graves or vaults ,
and may sellthe same atpleasure(not inconsistentwith
the by -laws ), which lots shall be used exclusively for
burial purposes and no other, except such portions of
said grounds as the directorsmay see fitto appropriate
to horticulturalpurposes , and to beautifying and adorn-
ing the grounds ; and said tractsor lotsof land are hereby
foreverdedicated and set apart, to be held by said corpo-
ration and their successors and assigns for the purpose
aforesaid, and no other. Said cemetery grounds , and all
lots in said cemetery , shall be forever free from State,
county , or other taxes.
§5 . The board of directorsmay have power to erect
buildingsforthe generaluse of the corporation,and for
the keeper of the grounds , and to prescribe rules for
inclosing , adorning , and erecting monuments in the cem-
etery lots, and to prohibitany use,division,improvement ,.
or constructionof the grounds which ,in theirjudgment ,,
may be objectionableor improper .
§ 6. That no debt shallbe contracted in anticipationof
futurereceipts,except for layingout,inclosing,and em-
bellishingthe grounds and avenues and building vaults,
for which a debt or debts may be contracted,not exceed-
ing fivehundred dollars in the aggregate ,to be paid out
of futurereceipts; but thatsaidcorporation,in the pur-
chase of other land for the extension of its cemetery
grounds ,may contract debts not to exceed one thousand:

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 269
dollars, to be paid out of future receipts; that no lots
shallbe soldby the corporationon a creditof more than
sixtydays ,with approved security. Said board of direct-
ors shallhave power to appropriate burialpermits forsuch
poor and meritoriouspersons as they may see proper;
and that the lotin said cemetery heretoforeset apart and
appropriatedby Watson Lodge,No. 32,I.O. O. F.,for
the burial of Odd Fellows shall forever remain under its
control, and should said Watson Lodge surrender its
charter,then said lotis to be controlled by the Odd Fel-
lows of Montgomery county ,who were in good standing
in said lodge at the date of such surrender; and the lot
heretofore set apart to Montgomery Lodge , No. 23 , Free
and Accepted Masons ,shallbe under the control of Mt.
SterlingNo. 23,Free and Accepted Masons ,and should
said Masonic Lodge surrender its charter or otherwise go
out of existence,then said lot is to be under the control
of the Masons of Montgomery county .
§7 . The originalconveyances of lots from the corpo-
ration to individuals shallbe evidenced by a certificate,
signed by the president and countersigned by the secre-
tary, under the seal of the corporation, specifyingthe
number of thelot,and thatsuch person is the owner of
such a lot,and such certificateshallvest in the purchaser ,
hisor her heirsand assigns,a rightin fee-simpleto such
lot, which lot shallbe exempt from execution, attach-
ment ,taxation,or any other claim ,lien,or process what-
ever for the sole purpose of interment , under the rules
and regulations of the corporation; and said certificates
shall have the same force and effectas deeds duly exe-
cuted in other cases, and said lots,or such portion thereof
as may be prescribed by the board of directors,and with
their assent, may be conveyed by deed in due form of
law for the sole purpose of interment as aforesaid; but
no originalcertificateshallbe grantedto any person who
does not produce a receiptfrom the treasurer that he has
paid for his lot in full,nor shallany person own more
than two lots at the same time unless by the consent of
the directors. All lotsheretoforesold and conveyed by

270 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the committee of Watson Lodge , I. O. O. F., or Mont-
gomery Lodge ,No. 23,Free and Accepted Masons ,or by
the board of directors of said corporation, are hereby
legalized .
§ 8. Any personwho shallwillfullydeface or destroy
the tombs , monuments , shrubbery , adornments , or any
thing pertaining to said cemetery or its avenues , or who
shallhunt or shoot game of any kind on said grounds ,or
turn horses or stock of any kind on said grounds , shall,
upon convictionof saidoffense,or any of them,be fined
not less than five dollars nor more than twice the value
of the propertyinjuredor destroyed, and such amount
shall be expended in repairing said damage , or for the
improvement of the grounds ; and any court of compe-
tentjurisdictionshallhave jurisdictionin such cases.
§9 . That for the purpose of keeping said cemetery
grounds in repairin the future, itshallbe the duty of said
cemeteryboard to setasidetwenty per cent.of allgross
receiptsof saidboard from the saleof lotsor otherwise,
to be invested by them each year in some safe securities
untilthe fund for the purpose of keeping said cemetery
grounds in repair (principaland interest) shallreach the
sum of eight thousand dollars, and that after said sum
shall reach eight thousand dollars, the interest alone
of said sum shall be used in keeping said grounds in
repair, and purchasing other grounds . All the balance
of the receipts shall be controlled by the board of di-
rectorsof said company with the advice and consent of
Watson Lodge .
§ 10. All acts heretofore passed incorporating said
cemetery company ,or amendatory thereof,that are in-
consistentwith thisact,are hereby repealed.
§ 11. This actshalltake effecton itspassage.
Approved April 7 , 1882.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 271
CHAPTER 881 .
AN ACT creatingand incorporatingthe Lebanon Jockey Club , of Marion
county .
Be it enactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
§1 . That Ben .Wilson,James Howe ,J.G. Phillips,jr,
and O. G. Kelly,together with their associates and suc-
cessors,be, and they are hereby, createda body-politic
and corporate,under the name and styleof the Lebanon
Jockey Club , and in that name may sue and be sued ,
plead and be impleaded ,in allthe courts of thisCommon-
wealth and elsewhere ; to make and use a common seal,
the same to alter and renew at pleasure ; and to have
perpetual succession.
§2 . Said club may receive,by subscriptions or other-
wise , capital stock to an amount not exceeding fifty
thousand dollars; but in addition to said capital stock,
may acquire, by gift, purchase , exchange ,or otherwise,
real estate not to exceed two hundred acres,and such
otherpersonalpropertyor estateas may by the club be
deemed necessary to carry out the ends for which said
club is created .
§ 3. Subscription to the capital stock of said club shall
be in shares of not less than ten or more than one hun-
dred dollars; and when shares to said capitalstock to the
aggregate amount of two hundred dollars shall have been
subscribed and paid in, the subscribers shall meet and
organize by electing from among their number the fol-
lowing officers,to-wit: a president,a vice president,sec-
retary, treasurer,and fivedirectors,who shall hold their
officesrespectivelyfor one year ,the date of the annual
election of said officersto be fixed by the board of direct-
ors in theirrulesand regulationsto be provided for here-
inafter .
§4 . The board of directorsshallbe presided over at its
meetings by the president of the club, which meetings
shallbe held on the third Saturday in each month ; but
calledmeetings may be held at any ime when business
may requireit,upon the callof the president,or upon

272 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the call of two members of the board of directors ; and
a majority of the members shall constitute a quorum at
the meeting of said board .
§5 . Said board shall have the power to enact such
rules and regulations for the government of the said
board and club as may be necessary and do not conflict
with the laws and Constitutionof the Commonwealth ,or
the Constitutionof the United States. All contracts,pur-
chases,leases,& c., of the club shall be by action of the
board , and nothing shall be binding on the said club ex-
cept by the action of its board of directors. Said board
shall,by itsrules and regulations,prescribethe duties of
the officersnamed herein,and affixpenaltiesforthe non-
performance thereof. Said board shall have power to
create and abolish such other officesas it may deem nec-
essary,and prescribethe duties and emoluments thereof.
§ 6. Said club may have such public meetings and fairs
at such times and places,within or near the town of Leb-
anon , as it may deem necessary or expedient for the en-
couragement of the stock breeders and stock men of the
country, and at such meetings and fairsmay offersuch
premiums and prizes as it may deem necessary for the
improvement of the speed ,blood, action,& c.,of the stock
of Marion and other counties. The mode of competition
for prizes shall be determined by the board of directors.
The regulationsand programmes ,and allotherrulesand
orders necessary for successfullycarrying out said meet-
ings or fairs,shallbe prescribed by the board of directors
and enforcedby such officersas shallbe designatedby
said board .
§7 . The board of directors shall have the power of
appointing any number of special policemen as it shall
deem necessary to enforce law and order and the rules of
the club, at the meetings ,races, & c., of said club; and
saidspecialpolicemen shallbe sworn,priorto entering
on the discharge of their duties, by the city judge of
Lebanon or the county judge of Marion county ,and shall
have the same power and authorityon the grounds ofthe
club,and during the races,& c.,of said club, as the mar

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 273
shalof Lebanon ,said policemen to be paid by the said
club .
§ 8. At all the meetings and general or special elec-
tions of the club , each share -holder shall have a vote for
each share he may own . Each share-holder may vote
eitherin person or by proxy; ifby proxy,it must be in
writing .
§ 9. The saidjockey club isempowered hereby ,through
itsboard of directors,to rent or lease any amount of real
estate, not to exceed one hundred and fiftyacres, and for
any length of time , not exceeding ten years for one
period .
§ 10. This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
age .
Approved April 7, 1882.
CHAPTER 882 .
AN ACT to erect a districtin Butler county, and to regulate the saleof
spirituous, vinous, and malt liquorstherein.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That for the purpose of taking a vote under an act,
entitled" An act to regulatethesaleof spirituous,vinous,
and malt liquorsin the Commonwealth , approved Jan-
uary 26th, 1874, the town of Morgantown , in Butler
county , and allcountry within one half mile of the cor-
porate limitsthereof, be , and the same is hereby , erected.
into and establishedas one district; and said districtis so
established is hereby authorized to do all the acts that
may be done under said act by any town or civildistrict,
and in the manner therein prescribed.
§2 . That shouldsaiddistrictorganizedby thisactvote
against the sale of spirituous,vinous , or malt liquors,in
the manner provided in an act approved 26th January,
1874, it shall be unlawful to sellspirituous, vinous, or
malt liquorsin said town of Morgantown ,or withinone
half mile thereof,under the pains and penaltiesprescribed
in said act,or any act in aid thereof: And providedfurther,
LOC . L., VOL . II- 18

274 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
That if the districtprovided for and erected in thisact
should at any time vote against the sale of spirituous,
vinous, or malt liquors,this act shallnot be construed as

tofurtherregulatetheretailtrafficin spirituous,vinous,
and malt liquors,and nostrums used as a beverage ," ap-
proved May the 5th, 1880,but the provisions of said act
shall be in full force in said district herein erected .
§3 . That a vote. may be taken at any time after the

the question of the adoption or rejection of the act of
January 26 , 1874, and the general laws in aid thereof,
which election shall be conducted as provided therein,
except at the firstelection after the passage of this act.
The county judge may ,at a called or regular term , sub-
mit said question without a petitionfrom any one . He
shall, at a called or regular county court,make an order
upon the order-book directingthe sheriffto open a poll
at the court-house in Morgantown ,at a time to be fixed
by him and stated in the order. The county court clerk
shall immediately deliver to the sheriffa copy of said
order , and the sheriff shall give notice of the time and
place of the electionby posting written or printed notices
thereof,one at the court-house door in Morgantown , and
one each at not lessthan four otherpublic places in Mor-
gantown , and within one half mile of the corporate
limitsthereof, for at leastten days beforetheday of the
election. The county judge shall also, at the time he
makes said order, appoint two discreet persons to be
judges ,and some discreetperson to be clerk of said elec-
tion,which electionthey shallconduct as providedby
the general laws ,to-wit: the act of January 26 ,1874 ,and
return the pollas therein provided, and the pollshall
be compared and certified, and the certificateentered of
record as isprovided in the act of January 26 , 1874,ex-
cept that the certificatemay be entered eitherat a called
or regular term of the county court.
§4 . After the firstelectionunder thisact, any sub-
sequent electionin said districtshall be governed in all

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 275
respectsby the general laws of the Commonwealth ,and
allamendments to,or actsin aid of,the localoptionlaws
of the Commonwealth shall affect and be in force in the

city, town ,or civildistrict.
§5 . This act shall be in force from and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 7, 1882.

276 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 886 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the town of Munfordville,Hart county.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That at any time when itoccurs that there are no
legally qualified trustees in the town of Munfordville ,
that the police judge of Munfordville shall appoint some
suitable person as overseer of the streets, alleys, and
roads within the corporate limits of said town , whose
duty itshallbe to keep the same in repairand in good
order, or such of them as may be speciallydesignated by
said police judge ; and for that purpose all the able-
bodied male persons in said town over the age of sixteen
and under fiftyfiveshallbe required to work on said
streets, alleys, and roads, under the same penalties for
refusing to work when notifiedas far as applies to per-
sons refusingto work on public highways in Hart county ,
and said overseer shallbe under the same responsibilities
and duties, and liableto the same penalties, as overseers
of public highways in said county .
§2 And be itfurther enacted, That allfinesfor misde-
meanors committed in said town shall be applied to the
working and repairs of said streetsby said police judge
of the town of Munfordville; and it shall be his duty to
appointa treasurerforsaid town ,whose duty itshallbe
to take charge of and receiptfor all money collectedfor
the use of working the streetsof the town , and pay out
the same under the orders of the police judge of said
town .
§3 . Should a vacancy occur in the officeof police
judge , then the judge of the Hart county court shall
make the appointments provided for in this act.
§ 4. And be it furtherenacted, That the police judge of
said town shallhave concurrent jurisdictionwith the jus-
ticesof the peace of Hart county .
§5 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 7, 1882.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 277
CHAPTER 887 .
AN ACT to amend an act,entitled" An act for the benefitof Joseph T.
Benton , of Ohio county," approved May 5, 1880.
WHEREAS , The Legislatureof Kentucky , by act ap-
proved February 3 , 1870 , released the suretiesof Q. C.
Shanks ,latesheriffof Ohio county ,from payment of all
damages included in the judgments against the said
Shanks and his sureties; and whereas , it was further
provided in said act that the purchase of alllands made
by the Commonwealth in satisfactionof saidjudgments
should be released upon payment of said judgment , less
said damages ; and whereas , it appears from the act to
which this is an amendment that said debts were paid
before the sales of land referred to in the act of Febru-
ary 3 , 1870 , were made ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
ofKentucky :
§1 . That an act,entitled"An act for the benefit of
Joseph T. Benton, of Ohio county," approved May 5,
1880,be so amended thatitsprovisionsshallapply to all
salesof land made under executions issued on the judg-
ments against Q. C. Shanks in the Franklin circuitcourt,
referred to in the act to which this is an amendment , and
allsaid salesare hereby declared void, and said damages
in said executions are released .
§ 2. That this act shall be in full satisfactionof all
claims of the State against these parties for revenue of
years 1858, 1859,and 1860,and said partiesshallhave no
claim or claims against the State for any money which
has been paid upon these sales,or any claims for any
payments made in the past for damages , interest,costs,
& c.,this act squaring said accounts in full as between
said parties, Q. C. Shanks and his suretiesand the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky .
§ 3. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 7, 1882.

278 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 888 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Hazel Green Academy ,in Wolfe county.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 .That G. B. Swango ,J. T. Day , Harrison Swango ,
W. O. Mize, Joseph M. Kash , and their successorsin
office,be,and they are hereby,createda body-politicand
corporate, by the name and styleof the Hazel Green
Academy ,and by that name shall have perpetual suc-
cession; may contract and be contracted with,sue and be
sued , plead and be impleaded ,in all courts and places;
have a common seal, and alter and break the same at
pleasure; may make all necessary by -laws for the trans-
action of the business and government of said company ,
not inconsistent with the laws of the State or the United
States .
§2 . The capitalstock of said company shallbe divided
into sharesof twenty-five dollarseach, subscribedand
paid for in such manner as said board of directorsmay
by its by -laws prescribe. Said capital stock shall not
exceed ten thousand dollarsat par value.
§3 . The stockhollersof said company shallannually
electa board of fivedhectors, a president and secretary,
also a treasurer,who shall hold their officefor one year ,
or untiltheir successors are duly elected. Said election
shallbe held on the fifteenthday of June in each year ,
and in event such day shall be Sunday , then,to be held
on the Saturday before.. In event of a vacancy occur-
ring in any of the officesmentioned , either by death,
removal ,resignation, or refusal to act,then the board of
directors , or the remainder of said board of directors,
shall appoint to fillthe unexpired term of office. Said
officersand directors,before entering upon the duties of
their office,shall go before a magistrate, and take upon
themselves an oath that they will, to the best of their
ability, discharge the duties imposed upon them with
honesty and without favor. They shallalso give a good
and sufficientbond ,with surety, which proceedings shall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 279
be lodged with the clerk of the county court of Wolfe
county forrecord.
§ 4. Said election shall be held at such building in
Hazel Green as the stockholders may designate, and
each stockholder shall be entitled to one vote for each
share of stock he or she may own at the time , and the
individual receiving the highest number of votes cast
shall be declared elected to the office for which he was
voted for. The secretary shall hold each election and
make known the result, except in the firstelection, in
which the incorporatorsshallselectthe remaining officers
in such manner as they may deem proper .
§ 5. The board of directors, when elected, shallhave
power ,and itis hereby made theirduty,to open a book
forthe subscriptionof stock to said academy ; and when
one hundred and twenty shares are so subscribed and
paid up ,saidboard may proceed to acquirelands,by pur-
chase or donation , whereon to erect suitable buildings
forthe purposes of advancing the cause of education,and
in such manner and as said board may deem proper .
They may continue sale of stock, receive donations of
property or money ,untilthe capitalstock does not exceed
ten thousand dollars, and may use and manage the same
as,in theirjudgment ,the interest of the institutionde-
mands , in conformity to the by -laws . The titleto real
estate, buildings, equipments, and everything of value
belonging to said institution, shallvest in the board of
directors, as trusteesfor the stockholders, and they shall
not dispose of any part of said property to the detriment
of said stockholders,unless so ordered by a majority of
saidshares. The board of directorsshallhave fullpower
and controloverthe management of the propertyof the
company ,and management and government of the school.
They may lease or rent said schoolbuildings,parapher-
nalia,real estate,& c ,for a period not exceeding five
years, for the purpose of conducting therein a school,
which contractmay be declarednulland void upon fail-
ure of either party to comply with contract Said leas-
ing may be done from time to time , and under such
contractas the board of directorsmay deem proper.

280 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 6. It shallbe the duty of the presidentto calland
preside at allmeetings of the directory,and perforınsuch
other duties as may be designated by the by -laws of the
company . Itshallbe the duty of the secretaryto keep a
faithfulrecordof allproceedings had,and perform such
other dutiesas may be designatedby the by-laws. It
shall be the duty of the treasurer to collectand receive
all money , and pay same out by order of the directory,
and perform such other duties as may be designated by
the by -laws. The company ,when fullyorganized,shall
cause a book to be opened,subjectto the inspectionof
any member of the board of stockholders,or any stock-
holder of said company , which shall contain the names
of all stockholders and the number of shares owned by
each ; and said shares may be transferredon said books
in the manner to be prescribed by the by -laws of said
company . Any dividends which may accrue to said com-
pany may be disposed of or used in such manner as the
board of directorsmay deem advisablein conformityto
the by -laws of the institution.
§7 . The board of directorsshallhave the power ,upon
the consent and by the advice of the principalof said
academy ,to issue a diploma to any student graduating
at said school, which diploma, when approved by the
State Board of Education , may serve as a certificate
entitlingthe holder thereof to teach in any common
school in this State without any further certificate: Pro-
vided, The person to whom it is issued has the further
requirementsof morality and sobrietyrequiredby law
in relation to teachers of common schools in this Com-
monwealth . Said certificatemust be signedby the presi-
dent of the board and by the principalof the school,and
attestedby the secretary. This certificateshall be good
and valid during the lifeof the holder thereof, and not
transferable .
§ 8. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 7, 1882 .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 281
CHAPTER 889 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Henderson Female BenefitAssociation.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1. That Jackson McClain ,Campbell H. Johnson, С.
T. Starling, Henry L. Reigler, A. T. Dudley , Marion
Duncan ,P. H. King , and C. Bailey , and theirsuccessors,
be , and they are hereby ,created a body -corporate, under
the name of " The Henderson Female Benefit Associa-
tion;" to have perpetual succession, with power to sue
and be sued,contract and be contracted with ; to have the
right to acquire and hold property, not exceeding one
hundred thousand dollars in value ; to confer such chari-
ties as are herein provided for; to have and use a cor-
porate seal,changeable at pleasure; and may make all
the by -laws necessary for its government and manage-
ment consistent with this charter and with the laws of
the land .
§2 . The principalofficeof this association shallbe in
Henderson ,Kentucky , and the persons named in the first
section,to whom this charter is granted ,shallconstitute
the firstboard of directors,who , or a majority of whom ,
shallmeet at any time afterthe passage of thisact and
organize,and from theirnumber electthe officersof the
society, viz : a president, vice president, secretary, and
treasurer, and other officers, if they deem it necessary ;
and the officesof secretaryand treasurermay be heldby
one person if the board so elect. They shall hold their
officesfor two years, or during good behavior, and until
their successors are elected and installed . The election
of a new board shall take place two years afterthe or-
ganizationunder the directory,and annuallythereafter.
Each member shallbe entitledto one vote in allelections,
which shallbe cast in person or by written proxy . The
board of directorsmay fixthe salariesof the officersand
employes , and prescribe their duties, and the amounts
and conditionsof such bonds as they may require of
them ; and should a vacancy occur in the board , a ma

282 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
jorityof the remaining have power to choose a fitperson
to fillit.
§ 3. The board of directorsmay receive as members of
this associationsuch female persons as they may think
proper,who are of sound healthand good standing,who
shallpassa satisfactoryexaminationand be recommended
by the examining physician, and be between the ages of
eighteen and sixtyyears; but said board may rejectany
applicationfor membership . Members of this associa-
tion may be divided into divisionsof two thousand each ,
and theaccountsof each divisionmay be kept separately;
and when a vacancy occurs in a completed division,it
shall be filledby a new member ,thus keeping each di-
vision filled to its maximum . The members of each
division shall be divided into classes according to their
several ages; those from eighteen to twenty -five shall
constitute one class, from twenty -five to thirtyanother ,
thirtyto thirty-five another, thirty-five to fortyanother,
fortyto forty-fiveanother,forty-fivetofiftyanother, fifty
tofifty-five another ,and from fifty-five to sixty another .
The board of directors shall fix by a by -law the assess-
ment dues from the members of each class in a division
upon the death of a member .
§4 . Each applicant shall pay into the treasury of the
association the sum of ten dollars as a membership fee,
and the further sum of two dollars as an annual fee,
which annual fee is to be paid at every anniversary of
the membership . Upon the payment of these sums ,each
member shall secure a certificateof lifemembership of
such device as the board of directorsmay adopt , which
shall express on itsface the obligationsof such member ,
and the benefitsto accrue to the legal heirsor beneficiary
of the same in the event of death . The board may , if
they think proper,change the amount to be paid as mem-
bership fee or annual due as experience may indicate,
but such change shallnot affectany person then insured.
§5 . The board of directorsshallregulate the form and
manner of proof of death of members by a by -law , and
upon notificationby the secretary of the death of any

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 283
member ,each survivingmember shallpay intothe treas-
ury, within thirty days from the date of the notice,the
mortuaryfeestipulatedin thecertificateof lifemember-
ship,out of which fund thereshallbe paid by the associ-
ation,in sixtydays from the date of the proof of death
of such member , to the beneficiary of the deceased mem-
ber ,such an amount as isshown to be due and owing to
the beneficiary by the by laws of the association. If
legal proceedings are not institutedagainst thisassocia-
tion within five years afterthe death of a member , any
right of recovery against itshallbe forfeitedand barred.
§6 . Upon the death of a member of this association,
the fund shallbe paid to the person or persons designated
in the certificateof lifemembership , or to the person or
persons to whom the said certificatehas been assigned ;
this being changed by death , or otherwise impossible,it
shallgo to the legal heirs or beneficiaryof the deceased
member ; and if the beneficiaryhas no legal heir, itis to
be held by the association, and transferred to the fund
mentioned in section one ; and the fund , or any part
thereof, due any person by the death of any member ,
shall not be subject to the claims of creditors for any
debt or liability, and shallnot be reached by attachment
or garnishment ,or other process of law , so as to divert
itsbenefitsand use from the persons intended to be bene-
fitedby it.
§7 . Any member tailingto pay his annual fee or mor-
tuaryfeewithinthirty(30) days from date ofnotice,shall
forfeithis membership , or any benefitsarising therefrom ,
but may be reinstatedby the board upon payment of all
arrearages, if applicationbe made in writing, accom-
panied with medical certificateof continued good health,
within twelve months from date of forfeiture. A notice
addressed to the member at post-officenamed in the ap-
plicationfor membership ,or other addresssubsequently
given in and entered on the books of the association,
shallbe considered a legal notice for allpurposes herein
§ 8. The board of directorsmay establishagencies and
branch officesanywhere for the purpose of facilitating

284 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the objectsof the association,which , being of a benevo--
lent character , shall be free from all licenses and taxa-
tion,except as to real estate and personal property; and
the said board may by by -laws ,regulate the time of all
called and regular meetings , and all other business not
inconsistentwith this act, and may also fixthe rules and
principlesby which the associationmay try and expel a
member of the association .
§ 9. The president and directorsshallcause to be made
annually a fullreport of the business of the association,
subject at all times to the inspection of its members .
The private property of officersor members shall not be
liablefor any debts of the association.
§10 . This act shall take effectand be in power from.
and after its passage .
Approved April 7 , 1882 .
CHAPTER 890 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Kentucky Home Seminary , of the city of.
Louisville .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That R. A. Robinson ,J. M. Robinson , W. George
Anderson ,John B. Temple , James W. Tate , Stephen E.
Jones, Joseph B. Kinkead , Noble Butler, and Belle S.
Peers, and their successors,are hereby created a body-
corporateand politic, under the name of the Kentucky
Home Seminary , and by that name may sue and be sued ,
contract and be contracted with, in any court of law or
equity; may have a common seal,and alter the same at
pleasure; may have perpetual succession; and may make
allby -laws necessary for the transaction of the business,
and forthe government of said seminary ,not inconsistent
with the laws of this State or of the United States .
§2 . That the said corporation may receive or acquire
or hold,by gift,grant,or otherwise,for educational pur-
poses, only as hereinafter expressed, property, real and
personal,in fee or for a term of years,not exceeding fifty

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 285
thousanddollarsinvalue,withinthecityofLouisville,and
may ,from time to time ,sellor transferthe same at pleas-
ure,and invest the proceeds of sales on like terms and
conditions .
§3 . Since the object of this corporation is to establish
an institutionof learning of a high order for the educa-
tion of girlsand young women ,the saidcorporation shall
have power to confer such honorary degrees and diplo-
mas as are usual in the colleges of this Commonwealth .
§4 . The said corporators and their successors shall
have power to elect a board of directorsand such other
officersas they may deem proper to perform such duties
as may be imposed consistentlywith the purposes to be
accomplished by thisact.
§5 . This act shall take effect from its passage .
Approved April 7 , 1883 .
CHAPTER 891 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Patrick Henry Gold , Silver, Mining and Re-
duction Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
§1 . That W. H. Lape , Thomas B. Youtsey , Robert
Waring ,W. F. Johnson,and Edward W. Lape ,theiras-
sociates,successors, and assigns,be ,and they are hereby ,
constituteda body -corporateand politic, by the name ,
title, and styleof the Patrick Henry Gold ,Silver,Mining
and Reduction Company , and by that name , title, and
styleare hereby made as natural persons to contract and
be contracted with, to sue and be sued ,to plead and be
impleaded in this Commonwealth and elsewhere ,in all
courts of law and equity; to have a common seal; to
ordain, establish, and put in execution all such by -laws ,
ordinances ,and regulations as shall seem necessary and
convenient for the government of said corporation, not
being contraryto the laws of the State of Kentucky or
of the United States. The capitalstock of said company
shall be three million dollars, in shares of ten dollars

286 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
each , and may be increased or diminished fiftyper cent.
by a voteofthe majorityof the stockholders,at any time,
upon notice of ten days ,by publicationin any newspaper
of general circulation,of fiveinsertions,said paper being
publishedat or near the home company's office. Said
company may , however , commence operations as soon
as fivethousand dollars stock is subscribed and fiftyper
cent,of said amount is actuallypaid up in cash. The
remaining portion of said stock may be issued and sold
by the board of directorsof said company at such times
as may seem to them best .
§ 2. The said company shallhave power to work ,pur-
chase , lease, and negotiate for and hold, as much real
estate as they may deem necessary for the successful
prosecution of itsbusiness,to-wit: to explore, bore, ex-
cavate ,mine , and dig for gold,silver,copper , iron,lead,
and allotherproductsof the earth,and in the reduction
and manufacture of the same , on all lands now owned
by them , or either of them , or that may be hereafter
owned by them , or either of them , in this State, or any
State or Territoryin the United States. The company
shall also have power to build steam and other boats,
bridges, roads, tramways , or railroads, to connect its
mines with other roads or navigable waters ; also to pur-
chase rightof way , mining rights,franchise, or to con-
solidate itsinterestswith other corporations; to put up
buildings for machinery and improvements that may be
deemed necessary for the successful prosecution of its
business,and to sellthe same ,and re-purchase or other-
wise dispose of the same ,as the interestsof the company
may require .
§3 . For conducting the affairsof said corporation, a
board of fivedirectorsshall be chosen . The fivecorpo-
ratorsherein named may constitutesaid board of direct-
ors for one year after said company is organized. Said
board shall choose one of its number , who shall be the
president of said company . In case of failure of the
company to electa board afterthe firstyear , said board
may hold over until their successors are elected and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 287
qualified. Notice of saidelectionshallbe made by three
insertionsin some newspaper of general circulationat
or near the home company's officetwenty days before
said election takes place. The president and board of
directorsshallhave power to appoint allofficers,agents ,
and laborers under them ,and at pleasure remove them .
In all elections each share of stock shall entitle the holder
thereof to one vote,and stockholdersmay vote by proxy .
The stockof saidcompany shallbe deemed personal
estate. In the absence of the president,the board may
appoint a presidentpro tem.
§4. Books forthe subscriptionof stockmay be opened
at any time ,and closed again at pleasure : Provided,how-
ever, That the stock at no time shall exceed the maximum
amount herein named .
§5 . That itshallbe lawful forthe president and direct-
ors of said company ,from time to time , and at alltimes,
to borrow or obtain loans and sums of money ,on such
terms as they may deem expedient, for the use of said
company ,and to pledge or mortgage allor any part of
the estates, improvements , privileges,effects, and assets
of said company for the re-payment of said sums of
money so borrowed , at such time and terms as have been
agreed upon .
§6 . In the event of failureon the part of any stock-
holder to pay up his or her stock,the shares of said stock-
holder may be forfeitedby the president and board of
directors,or they may sellsame ,or ifthey so elect,sue
and recover the same before any court of record in this
Commonwealth or elsewhere ; notice of said forfeiture,
sale, or suit,to be given said stockholder ten days pre-
vious to said proceedings. The stock of thiscompany
shall be deemed non -assessable .
§7 . The home officeof the company shall be es-
tablishedin Newport , Kentucky . The company may ,
however , if deemed expedient, establishbranch offices
elsewhere .
§ 8. It is expressly understood that nothing in thisact

288 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
shallbe so construed as conferring banking privilegeson
this corporation .
§ 9. This actto take effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 8, 1882.
CHAPTER 892 .
AN ACT to authorizethe Grayson county court to borrow money to pay
off the indebtedness of said county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Grayson county is here-
by authorized to borrow money in any sum sufficientto
pay off the indebtedness of Grayson county .
§2 . To enable the said county court to borrow the
money mentioned in the preceding section,itis hereby
authorized to issue the bonds of said county , in denomi-
nations not less than one hundred dollars, and not more
than five hundred dollars, to be signed by the county
judge and attested by the county clerk of said county ,
and the coupons thereon shallbe attestedby the clerk
alone . The said bonds shallbe negotiable and payable
to bearer in the city of Louisville,Kentucky ,at not more
than twenty years from theirdate , and redeemable at the
pleasure of said county at any time afterfive years from
theirdate, and they shall bear interestat the rate of six
per cent.,payable semi -annually in the cityof Louisville,

delivered to any purchaser thereof, as hereinafter pro-
vided, for lessthan itspar value .
§3 . That whenever the said county court shall issue
any bonds under the provisions of thisact, itshallbe the
duty of said court annually thereafterto cause to be
levied and collecteda tax sufficientto pay the semi-
annual interest on the bonds so issued , and the cost of
collecting said tax, and paying the interest on all the
realestateand personalpropertyin said county subject
to taxation under the revenue laws of this State , includ-
ing the amounts owned by residents of said county ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 289
which ought to be listedfor taxation under the equali-
zation law .
§4 . That whenever the county court of said county
shallfailor refuseto levy a tax sufficientto pay the
semi -annual interest on said bonds , and the cost of col-
lecting the tax, and paying the interest as required by
the preceding section,each member thereof shallbe lia-
ble to a fineof one hundred dollars,and imprisonment
in the county jail of said county for fiftydays ,to be re-
coverable and punishablein the circuitcourt of said
county on indictment by the grand jury , or the sworn
written information of any resident tax payer of said
county , filedin the clerk'sofficeof the circuitcourt of
said county , on which a summons shall be issued by the
clerk of said court in the name of the Commonwealth
against the several members of said court,against whom
the information is filed, to be executed and returned in
allrespects similarto a summons in a civilaction in said
court,and on convictionthereofthe saidpenaltiesshall
be enforced by the proper ordersand processesof the
said circuitcourt: Provided, however, Though no tax be
levied, any member of said court who shall,at the annual
levy term thereof, for the purpose of levying said tax,
cause his vote to be entered of record on the order -book
of saidcounty court in favorof levyingthe tax required
by the provisionsof thisact,shalltherebybe exempted
from allthepenaltiesinflictedby thissection.
§ 5. That whenever the said county court shallfail or
refuse to levy the tax required by the thirdsectionof this
act,then itshallbe the duty of the county judge of said
county to levythe tax in the manner providedforin said
section,and on hisfailureor refusalto levysaidtax,he
shallbe liableto allthe penaltiesprescribed in the pre-
ceding section,and he may be proceeded against in the
manner provided for therein. And on the failureor re-
fusalof the said county court and the county judge of
said county to cause said tax to be levied,then the judge
of any circuitcourt in the State of Kentucky , on the

written request of one or more residenttax-payers of said
county ,ishereby authorized to levy said tax as provided
for by the third section of thisact ; and it shall not be

ent in said county at the time of levying the said tax,but
the written order of said judge making said levy and

lic, under his officialsignature and seal of office,shall

and itshallbe the duty of the clerk of said county court

and on his failure or refusal to do so ,he shall be liable to
allthe penalties prescribed in the fourth section of this
act,and he may be proceeded against in the manner pro-
vided for therein .
§6 . That whenever a tax shall be levied as provided
for in the third section of thisact,itshall be the duty of
thecounty courtof saidcounty to appoint threeresident
tax-payers of said county , who shall be styled the board

The commissioners , before they proceed to discharge
their duties,shall be sworn ,in the presence of the court
appointingthem,to faithfullydischargetheirdutiesac-
cordingto the bestof theirskilland judgment ; and they
shallholdtheirofficeat thepleasureof thecourtappoint-
ing them . They shallimmediately appoint one of their

the Commonwealth ,with such securityas shallbe ap-
proved by thecourt,for the faithfulkeeping and disburs-
ing of allmoney coming into his hands as treasurerof the
board,on which bond suitmay be brought,from time to
time,by and in the name of said county , by any person
injuredby a breach of hisbond ,in any courtofGrayson
county having jurisdictionof the amount claimed in the

suit. Such treasurermay at any time be requiredtogive
a new bond; and any suretyon hisbond shallbe entitled
to the same remedies for procuring additionalor counter
securityas are now given to guardians , administrators,
& c. Said treasurershallbe allowed by the said court
one per cent.of the money receivedand paid out by him
as a compensation for his services. In case a vacancy
shall occur in said board of commissioners , it shall be
filledby said court.
§ 7. It shall be the duty of the treasurer of the board
of commissioners of the sinking fund , by and with the
consent of his co -commissioners , to borrow any money
necessaryto pay offtheindebtednessof Grayson county,
whenever he has the opportunity of liquidatingthe same ,
or any part thereof; and in payment of the money so

court,who shallkeep a recordin his officeof thedate,

the name of the person or persons to whom delivered, to

under theirofficialsignatures.

ers, as provided in the preceding section. The treas-
urer of said board of commissioners shall be allowed

paid out by him in pursuance of the preceding section,

annual settlement with the county court for all his acts
under the provisionsof thisact.

§ 9. The county court of said county shallconvene

And providedfurther, That they may appoint any day
previous to said date to make the levy, and may so con-
vene and levy said tax.
§ 10. The taxes levied under the provisions of thisact
shallbe collectedby the sheriffof Grayson county , who ,
before collectingany part thereof,shall execute a bond
in said county court, with such suretiesas shall be ap-
proved by the court,conditionedthat he willfaithfully
collectand pay over to the proper person, within the
time prescribed by law , all the taxes levied under this
act which may be placed in his hands for collection.
§ 11. That the sheriffof said county ,having for collec-
tion taxes levied under thisact, shallhave allthe powers
of distrainingand sellingpersonal property which he
now has by law in the collectionof the State revenue ;
and whenever the said sheriffshallbe unable to find per-
sonal propertyliableto sale for the unpaid tax of any
individual,he may levy the same on any real estate of
such person situated in said county , and sellthe same
under the regulationsprescribedby law for sellingreal
estate under execution; and all taxes levied under this
act shallbe a lien on all the real and personal estateof
the person taxed situated in Grayson county ; but the
owner of any real estateso sold may redeem the same
within fiveyears aftersaid sale by paying to the pur-
chaserthe purchase-money and ten per cent.per annum
interestthereon,with alltaxesof every descriptionpaid
by the purchaser and ten per cent. per annum interest
thereon .
§ 12. That the sheriffsellingreal estatefortaxes levied
under thisactshallgivethe purchasera certificateof his

purchase,which shalldescribethe real estatesold, and
state the amount for which it was sold and the date of
the sale, which certificateshallbe lodged by the pur-
chaser with the clerk of the said county court,who shall
record the same in his officein a book to be kept for that
purpose, for which the said clerkmay charge a fee of
fiftycents,to be paid by the purchaser.
§ 13. That the sheriffcollectingthe taxes levied under
thisact shallreceivethe same compensation as isallowed
by law for collectingthe State revenue .
§14 . That one half of the taxes levied under thisact
shallbe collected and paid over to the treasurer of the
board of commissioners of the sinking fund by the sher-
iff,withinone hundred and twenty days afterthe same
shallbe placed in his hands ,and the residue within six
months after the same ought to have been paid ; and
if the sheriffshall fail or refuse to execute bond for the
collectionof the taxes levied under thisact, within thirty
days afterthe said tax shallbe levied,he shallforfeithis
office; and thesaidcounty courtshallthereupon appoint
a collector, who shall execute bond with sureties, which
shallbe approved by the court,and have allthe powers
and subject to all the duties and liabilitiesof the sheriff
in collecting taxes under this act .
§ 15. That ifthe sheriffor collector,having in his hands
forcollectionthe taxes levied under thisact,shallfailto
collectand pay over the same ,within the time prescribed
by the preceding section,the said sheriffor collector,with
his sureties,shallbe liablefor the amount not paid and
tenper cent.thereon,to be recoveredby motion,on ten
days ' notice,in any court of said county having jurisdic-
tion of the amount ; and any executionissued on said
judgment shallnot be replevied,and shallbe indorsedby
theclerkor officerissuingitthatno securityof any kind
is to be taken .
§ 16. Itshallbe the duty of the board ofcommissioners
of the sinking fund to see that the sheriffor collector
pays over the taxes accordingto the provisionsof this
act,and to institutelegalproceedingsin theirnames as

294 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
commissioners for the benefit of Grayson county against
the sheriffor collectoron his failureto do so. They shall
appropriate said money to the payment of the semi -an-
nual intereston the bonds issuedunder the provisionsof
thisact,and any surplusremaining in theirhands from
time to time may be used by them in the redemption of
said bonds at not exceeding theirpar value .
§ 17. The said county court may annually ,when mak-
ing the levy provided for in the third section of thisact,
include an additionalsum in the levy,not exceeding fifty
cents on every one hundred dollars' worth of property,
for the purpose of creating a sinking fund for the re-
demption of the bonds issued under the provisionsof
this act, which tax shall be collectedby the sheriff or
collectorof said county , who shall collectand account
forthe same ,and have the same powers ,and be subject
to the same duties and liabilitiesprovided for the collect-
ing and paying over of the othertaxesmentioned in this
act, and said sheriff or collector shall be liable for the
same on his bond , and shallpay the same over to the
treasurer of the board of commissioners of the sinking
fund,who shallbe responsibleforsame on hisbond,and
he shallexpend the same in the redemption of the bonds
issued under thisact at not exceeding theirpar value .
§ 18. This act shalltake effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 8 , 1882 .

day of

CHAPTER 895 .
AN ACT to authorizethe collectionof taxes in Webster county.
WHEREAS , It isrepresentedto the General Assembly of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky thatR. T. Wallace,who
was sheriffof Webster countyfortheyear 1880,has paid
the State revenue ,but has failedto account for the county
levy for that year; and whereas, itis representedthat
there is yet due from many of the tax -payers of said
county State and county revenue for 1880, and that said
Wallace has leftthe Statewithout making any provision
forthepayment of saidcountyrevenue or dues,and with-
out any provisionfor the collectionof said State and
county revenue yet due and unpaid to said sheriff; there-
fore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Webster county, upon
the motion of any one of the suretiesof saidWallace,on
his county levybond ,may appoint some person or per-
sonsto collectthe same . Such person so appointedshall
executea bond ,with suretiesapproved by saidcourt,for

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 297
the faithfulperformance of thedutiesimposed upon him .
He shallhave allthe powers to distrainand sellthat said
Wallace would have ifpresentand actingin person,and
shallbe subjectto the same penaltiesas said Wallace
would be .

shall be paid when collected .
§3 . The person so appointedmay agree with thesure-
ties of Wallace as to his commissions .
§ 4. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 8, 1882.

CHAPTER 897 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Cynthiana Leaf Tobacco Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Wm . Addams ,Paul King , John W. Mattox ,
James T. Hedges ,Hugh N. Garnett,and John K. Lake ,
and their associates and successors, be , and they are
hereby,createdand constituteda body-politic,under the
name and styleof the Cynthiana Leaf Tobacco Com-
pany , and by that name shallhave power to contract
and be contracted with ,to sue and be sued ,plead and be
impleaded; to take,hold and convey both realand per-
sonal property,the real estateheld by said corporation
not to exceed twenty thousand dollarsin value ; to make

298 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
allnecessary by -laws and regulationsforthe government
of the corporation and the management of itsaffairs, and
to do all other acts that may be necessary for carrying
on the business of buying and sellingleaf tobacco , grain,
hemp , wool , and other merchandise or produce ,and stor-
ing same for others,in which businessthe corporationis
empowered to engage .
§2 . The principal officeand place of business of the
corporation shallbe at or near Cynthiana ,Kentucky .
§3 . The capital stock of the corporation shall not be
less than six thousand dollars,divided into shares of one
hundred dollarseach ,but may be increased,by a vote of
two -thirds of the stock , to not more than one hundred
thousand dollars; but should such increase of stock be
made , the holders of stock at the time shall have the
right to take said additional stock in proportion to the
amount of stock then held by each .
§4 . Each stockholdershallbe entitled,eitherin per-
son or by written proxy , to cast one vote for each share
of paid-up stock held by him; and the holdersof two-
thirdsof the capital stock of the corporation shall con-
stitute a quorum for the transaction of business at all
meetings of the stockholders.
§5 . The stockin the corporation shallbe personal prop-
erty, and a record of the ownership thereof shall be kept
by the corporation, and no transferthereof shallbe valid
untilrecordedupon the books of the corporation. Any
stockholderdesiring to sellhis or her stock in the com-
pany must give the chief officerof the corporation ten
days' notice of such desire; and should such chief officer
not purchase said stock or furnish a purchaser for the
same on as favorable terms as the same could be sold in
open market , then the said stockholder shall have the
rightto sellthe same in open market .
§6 . The selectionof officersof the corporation, and
theirpowers and duties in the management of the affairs
of the corporation,shallbe prescribedby the by-laws,
§7 . The privatepropertyof the stockholdersshallbe
exempt from the debts of thecorporation,and the pay

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 299
ment into the Treasury of thisCommonwealth during the
firstweek in each year,by the chiefofficerof the corpo-
ration,of fiftycents on each one hundred dollarsof capi-
tal stock held in said corporation,shall be a part of the
revenue proper,and be in fullof alltaxes against this
company ,except the real estate owned by said corpora-
tionshallbe subjectto citytaxation,as other likeprop-
erty in the town of Cynthiana issubject.
§ 8. Said corporation shall have power to make ad-
vances on tobacco and other products of the country ,
charge and collectany rate of interestwhich may be
agreed upon by the partiesin writing,not exceedingthe
maximum rate allowed by law .
§9 . The corporation shall continue for the term of
ninety-nine years from the passage of thisact,but itmay
be dissolved at any time by a vote of two thirds of the
capital stock.
§ 10.Should any subscriberto the stockof the corpo-
rationfailto pay his subscription,or any part thereof,
the governing board of the corporation may forfeitthe
same , or they may order a sale of the same at public
saleafterpublicadvertisement. Any surplusof the pro-
ceeds of the sale,over and above the amount due upon
said subscription,shallbe paid to such delinquentsub-
scriber; but in case of forfeitureof said stock, allpay-
ments theretofore made shall inure to the benefit of the
corporation .
§ 11. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 8 , 1882 .
CHAPTER 899 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Phoenix ContractCompany .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Jas. B. Dunlap , Jas. P. Helm , and John L.
Dunlap ,theirassociates,successors,and assigns,be,and
they are hereby,createda body-politicand corporate,by
the name and styleof the Phoenix Contract Company ;

300 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and by that name shallhave perpetualsuccession,and
capable in law of contracting and being contracted with ,
of suingand being sued,pleadingand being impleaded,
in all courts and elsewhere as a natural person ; may
adopt and use a common seal,and alteror renew the
same at pleasure.
§2 . That said company shallhave the power to con-
tractwith individualsor corporationsforthe construction
and equipment of railroads, streetrailroads, turnpike
roads,elevators,wharves ,canals,and other like internal
improvements,and may take in pay thereforany of the
stocks,bonds ,real estate, or other property of the indi-
viduals or corporations with whom itcontracts,and hold,
sell, transfer,convey , and enjoy the same as a natural
person .
§ 3. That the affairsof said company shallbe con-
ducted by a board of directorsof not lessthan three nor
more than seven,one of whom shallbe electedannually
president. Said board of directors shall be elected at
the annual meeting of the stockholders,which shall be
held on the second Monday in May of each year by the
stockholders,after due notice to the stockholders of the
time and place given,as may be prescribedby the by-
laws . At all the meetings of the stockholders each

of stockheld by him .

sum not exceeding fivehundred thousand dollars,divided
into shares of one hundred dollars each , which shall be
transferableon the books of the company as may be pre-
scribed in the by -laws . The incorporators above named
may open books for subscriptionsto the capitalstockof
the company , after giving such notice of the time and
place as theymay deem proper. And when one hundred
sharesof the capitalstock shallhave been subscribed,
they shall calla meeting of the subscribers, afterdue
notice to each subscriber,and organize by the election
of a board of directors, who shall serve until the next

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 301
annual meeting , or until their successors are elected and
qualified.
§5 .The board of directorsshall have power to adopt
such by -laws ,rules and regulations, as may be necessary
for the government of itsofficersand affairs,not incon-
sistentwith the laws of thisState or of the United States,
and may appoint allthe officers, agents, and servants
necessary, and take from any of them such bond as may
be required,and do any and all acts for the carrying out
of the purposes of thisact not inconsistentwith the laws
of thisState. The principalofficeof said company shall
be located in the city of Louisville. The subscriptions
to the capital stock shall be paid for upon such terms as
may be agreed upon between the board of directorsand
the subscribers .
§ 6. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 8 , 1882.
CHAPTER 900 .
AN ACT to incorporate Riverside Male and Female Seminary ,at Vance-
burg .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Henry K. Taylor ,as principal,and Samuel
J. Pugh , A. H. Parker , H. C. Bruce , L. B. Piersal, T. B.
Harrison ,Dr. J. M. Wells , A. L McKay , and F. M. Tay-
lor, as trustees, and their successors appointed by the
principal,shall be a body -corporate, under the name of
the Principal and Board of Trustees of the Riverside Male
and Female Seminary .
§2 . That the principaland corps of instructorsshall
have power todeterminethecourseof studyand discipline
tobe adoptedin said institute,provideditbe coextensive
with that prescribed by the best institutionsof the same
order. That the principal and corps of instructorsshall
conduct semi -annual examinations , and shall, with the

attendthe examinations,conferupon such young ladies

302 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and gentlemen as shall have completed the prescribed
courseof study,and passed a reputableexamination,the
literaryhonors of the institution.
§3 . That there shall be a course of science and mod-
ern literature,and a more extended course of scienceand
ancient literature,taught in said institute; and upon those
who shallhave pursued the former the degree of Maid of
Scienceor Bachelorof Science,and upon thosewho have
pursued the latterMaid of Arts or Bachelor of Arts shall
be conferred. Further,that upon those upon whom the
second mentioned degree has been conferred, and who
shallforthree years pursue a literarycourse,the honorary
degree of Mistressof Arts or Master of Arts shallbe con-
ferred .
§4. That should saidinstituteat any time pass out of
the hands of the present principal,his successors shall
have allthe rightsand privilegesspecifiedin thisact.
§5 . This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved April 8 , 1882 .

CHAPTER 909 .
AN ACT to incorporate the Cincinnati and Charleston Railway Company .
Be it enactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
§1 . That B. J. Peters, A.L. Greer , John Gray , W. F.
Simrall, and W. H. Adams , and their associates, suc-
cessors, and assigns, who may become stockholders, be ,
and they are hereby,createda body-politicand corporate,
by the name and style of the Cincinnati and Charleston

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 315
Railway Company , and by that name shallhave perpet-
ual succession,and be capable of suing and being sued ,
pleading and being impleaded ,defending and being de-
fended , in law and equity,in allcourts and elsewhere , in
like manner and as fully as natural persons may be .
And said corporation may have and use a common seal,
and set aside and renew or alter the same at pleasure;
and by said corporate name may contract and be con-
tracted with, and by that name shallbe ,and are hereby ,
invested with all the powers , privileges,immunities ,and
franchisesof natural persons .
§2 . The capitalstock of said company may be any
amount not exceedingthirtythousand dollarsper mile,
for each mile of railroadwhich said company shall build
or own in this State or other States , divided into shares
of one hundred dollarseach , and may be subscribed for,
purchased , owned , and controlled by individuals in and
out of thisState, and railroad companies , and other cor-
porationsin Kentucky or elsewhere.
§3 . The corporators named in the firstsection, or such
of them as may act, shall constitute the firstboard of
directors of said company , and may serve as such until
theirsuccessorsare duly elected and qualified. The said
corporators, or a majority of them , shall meet at such
time and place as they may agree upon and designate,
and from theirown number , or from such persons as they
may associate with themselves , choose a president, and
appoint such other officersand agentsas may be neces-
sary; they shallhave power to adopt such by-laws for
the government of the company ,its officers,agents, and
employes , in the general conduct and management of
the affairsand business of the company ,as may be need-
ful and proper , and alter, amend , or repeal the same at
will .
§ 4. The general officeof the company shallbe located
in the cityof Covington , Kentucky . The directorsshall
have power and authority to locate and establishbranch
officesor agencies at such other place or places as may ,
in theirjudgment ,be found necessary.

316 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§5 . That the incorporators,or firstboard of directors,
shallprovide in the by -laws for an annual electionof
directors, consisting of seven in number , who shall be
stockholdersof said company ,and fixthe time and place
and determine the manner of the firstand succeeding
electionsof directors. The directorssucceeding the first
board shallseverallyhold theirofficesforone year,and
until their successors are elected and qualified. Each
board of directors may fillany vacancy occurring in
their number , and shall choose from their own number
a president. It shallrequirea majority, includingthe
president,to constitutea quorum of the board of direct-
ors for the transaction of business .
§ 6. At the electionof directors, and allother meetings
of stockholders in said company , stockholders shall be
entitledto one vote for each share of stock so held,
which may be cast in person or by writtenproxy. Pro-
vision shallbe made in the by laws for annual meeetings
of the stockholders,and such called meetings as are nec-
essary .
§7 . The president and directorsshall have the man-
agement and control of the property,business,and affairs
of the company ; may appoint all such officers, agents ,
and employes as are necessary,prescribetheirduties and
compensation ,and take from any one of thein bond , with
security, forthe faithfulperformance of his duties.

said company to open stock-books for subscriptionto the

open forsuch length of time as may be deemed necessary.

be personalproperty,and may be issued,certified,trans-
ferred,and registeredin such manner ,and at such places,
as may be ordered and provided by the by-laws of said
company .

pany shallbe,and are hereby,authorizedand empowered

such number of tracks and lines of telegraph as said

ton or the cityof Newport ,Kentucky , thence by such

road with all necessary side-tracks, turn-outs, switches,

ing,and conveying and transferringallreal,personal,and

the carrying out of the objects of this act, to the same

CHAPTER 911 .
AN ACT forthe benefitand protectionof the owners of sheep in Nicholas
county, Kentucky .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe the duty of the Nicholas county
court clerkto keep in his office, and furnish to the appli-
cants therefor, as hereinafter provided , metallic collars,
with a number and the seal of his said county court
thereon ; and itshallbe the duty of each owner or keeper
of a dog in Nicholas county to apply to the said county
court clerk,and procure from him one of said collars,to
be placed upon and continuallyworn by each dog be-
longing to,or in the possession or under the control of,or
stays upon his or her premises .
§2 . The county court clerk shall charge and receive
for each collarso furnished two dollars, and shallkeep a
record showing the date the same was furnished , and the
number of the collar, the color of the dog , and the name
of the person to whom he furnished collars; and shall,on
the firstMonday in January of each year ,make out and
record in the order book of Nicholas county court,in tab-
ular form ,showing the number of collars,the date,when
furnished,the color of the dog , and the name of the per-
son to whom he furnished collars under this act , the
amount expended in procuring said collars, and the gross
and net amount he has received therefor ,and the amount
in his hands January 1st of each year.
§3 . Any person who has had sheep killedby dogs shall
make out a statement , in writing , showing the number
and value of the sheep so killed subsequent to the first
day of January preceding said statement, which state-
ment shall be sworn to by said owner or his employe ,
whose duty and business it was to take care of said

326 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
sheep,and accompanied by the affidavitsof at leasttwo
creditablepersons,to the effectthat said statement and
account isknown by them to be true. Said statement
and account shall be presented to the magistrate of the
districtin which said sheep were killed, and he shallgive
to said owner or employe a certificatethat said owner
or employe had proven to his satisfactionthe following :
the date when the sheep were killed, the number and
value thereof, and the amount he is entitledto as pay
therefor, which certificateshall be filedwith the county
court clerk on or before the firstday of January follow-
ing the year in which the sheep were killed.
§ 4. The county court clerk shallfileand preserve said
certificates, and by the fifteenthday of January each
year make out a statement showing how much money is
due to each person for sheep killed during the preceding
year ,and pay the same to the persons entitledthereto out
of the funds in his hands from the sale of said collars,
provided there be enough money to pay the same ; if not,
he shall distributeand pay pro rata the amount in his
hands to the persons entitled thereto ; and if at the end of
any year thereis any money leftin the hands of said
county clerk, afterpaying for the loss of sheep as herein
provided , he shall distribute said balance among the
school districtsof Nicholas county , according to the
amount of public school tund received by each school
district. He shall pay the same to the trustees of the
various school districts,and they shall appropriate it to
the public school in said districts.
§5 . Any personor personswho shallown or keep upon
his or her premises or harbor a dog or dogs without a
collar, as provided in this act,or in any way evade the
provisions of thisact,shallbe fined not lessthan fiveand
not exceeding twenty-five dollars, which shallbe enforced
by warrant before any justice of peace having jurisdic-
tion, or by indictmentby the grand jury of Nicholas
county .
§ 6. Any person who loses a collarfurnishedhim under
thisact shall apply to and receive from the said county

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 327
courtclerka duplicateof the one lost,and shallpay said
clerk therefor twenty -five cents. Before the clerk shall
furnishany duplicatecollars,the person applying there-
for shall make oath before said clerk that he desires said
collarto be used in lieu of one theretofore procured and
lost .
§7 . Any person or persons using a collarlostby any
otherperson,and not properlyhis own ,or one furnished
fora previousyear,shallbe finednot lessthan fivedol.
lars or more than twenty -five dollars, said fine to be
entorced in the same manner as for other violations of
this act .
§ 8. It shallbe the duty of each owner or keeper of any
dog or dogs in Nicholas county to provide the same with
collars,as requiredby thisact,on orbeforethefirstday of
January of each year ,except for the year 1882,for which
they shallprovide the same within thirtydays afterthe
passage of thisact.
§ 9. Itshallbe theduty of allthe justicesof the peace,
constables,and sheriffsof Nicholas county to see that the
provisions of thisact are enforced; and the judge of the
Nicholas circuitcourt shall instructthe grand juries at
each term thereof as to the provisions of this act.
§ 10. The county court clerk of Nicholas county shall
purchase and pay for fiftycollarsout of the money in his
hands belonging to the county revenue ; and the collars
thereafterpurchased , and the same used to purchase the
said fiftycollars,shallbe paid out of the money received
for the collars distributed under this act . Said collars shall
not cost exceeding twenty -fivecents each ; and the clerk
is allowed for his servicesunder this act ten per cent, on
the amount received by him .
§ 11. So much of the laws of this Commonwealth as
impose a tax upon dogs in Nicholas county is hereby
repealed .
§12 . This act shall take effectand be in force from
and afteritspassage .
Approved April 8, 1882.

AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act to incorporatethe town of New
Columbus , in Owen county," approved February 23, 1874,and all sub-
sequent amendments thereto.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act, entitled"An act to incorporate the
town of New Columbus , in Owen county," approved

to,be so amended as to allow and authorize the trustees
of said town to charge and collect a license of not ex-
ceeding one hundred dollars each on all hotel keepers ,
saloon-keepers,and druggistswho are now ,or may here-
after be , engaged in selling or furnishing spirituous,
'vinous , or malt liquors in the corporate limits of said
town .
§2 . This act shall take effect from and after its pas-
sage .
