CHAPTER 915 .
AN ACT toamend an act,entitled"An act to incorporateThe Knights
of Honor Mutual Aid Association, " approved April 26th, 1880 .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That section eighteen be so amended as to allow
the officersof said associationto deduct ten per cent.
from the gross amount received on each assessment .
Said ten per cent.shall be deposited in bank at interest
or invested in bonds ,or loaned upon mortgage at interest,
until the board shall determine to use said fund to pay
assessments, and decrease the amount of assessments
that may be needed in any one year in case of an epi-
demic or other great emergency .
§ 2. That said section eighteen be further amended so
as to read: " and if said assessmentis not paid within
thirtydays from the date of said notice,the member shall
be ," & c.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 329
§3 . Section nineteen be amended by adding the
words : " and any notice of an assessment having been
sentby mail to the post-officeof the member ,as given in
his application,or as directedin his change of post-office,
shall be deemed as sufficient notice to the member .
§ 4. These amendments not to be in force untilap-
proved by a vote of the majority of the members voting
at any election held for the purpose of taking the sense
of the members for or against the approval of these
amendments ,
Approved April 8, 1882.
CHAPTER 916 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe selling, lending, or giving of spirituous, vinous,or
malt liquorsto any person or personswithin the corporate limitsof the
town of New Castle, in Henry county, Ky ., and within the distance of
three miles of the court -house in said town .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That it shall be unlawful for any person or per-
sons to sell,lend, or give to any other person or persons
spirituous,vinous,or malt liquors,in any quantity what-
ever, within the corporate limits of the town of New
Castle, in Henry county , aforesaid, or within the limits
of three miles of the court-house in said town , except as
hereinafterprovided.
§ 2. Any person or persons who shallbe found guilty
of selling,lending,or giving to any other person or per-
sons, spirituous,vinous, or malt liquors,in any quantity
whatever , within the corporate limits of said town of
New Castle, or within three miles of the court-house in
said town ,except as hereinafter provided , shallbe fined
sixtydollarsfor every such offense,and every such sell-
ing,lending,or givingshallconstitutea separateoffense.
§ 3. Spirituous,vinous , and malt liquors may be kept
and prescribedas a medicine when necessaryas such,
but for no other purpose,by a physicianor physicians
within the limitsaforesaid; but such physicianor physi-
ciansshallrecord,in a book or books to be kept by him

330 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
or them for said purpose ,every prescriptionso made and
given , with the date thereof, the quantity prescribed, and
the name of the person to whom given ,and such book or
books shallbe open to the inspection of the grand juries
of Henry county . A failureto keep said book or books ,
or to record therein the date of every such prescription,
and the name of the person to whom given , and the
quantity prescribed by such physician or physicians with-
in the limits aforesaid,shallbe a misdemeanor , and such
physicianor physiciansso failingshallbe finedsixtydol-
larsfor every such offense, and every such failure shall
constitutea separate offense.
§ 4. So much of the charter of said town as confers
upon the trustees thereof the power to grant license to
vend spirituous,vinous ,or malt liquors therein, ishereby
repealed
§ 5. All general and specialacts, or parts of acts, au-
thorizing the county court of Henry county to grant
license or privilege to vend spirituous,vinous , or malt
liquors to any tavern or hotel now within or hereafter
established within the town and limits aforesaid , are
hereby repealed .
§ 6. All laws of a local or general nature inconsistent
with or repugnant to thisact are hereby repealed, so far
as they may be applicable to said town , and within the
limits of three miles of the court -house thereof , and no
construction shall be given to any such law or laws as
shallin anywise permit the selling, giving,or lending of
vinous,spirituous,or malt liquorswithin said corporate
limits, or within the limit of three miles of the court-
house in said town .
§ 7. This act shall not be construed as prohibiting the
giving of said liquorsby a parent to his or her child, or a
guardian to his or her ward , or the use of wine at the
sacrament .
§ 8. The circuitcourt of Henry county and the justices'
court held in the limits aforesaid , shall have concurrent
original jurisdiction of offenses under or infractionsof
thisact Such prosecutionsshallbe by warrant from the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 331
justices' court in the name of the Commonwealth , or by
indictment by the grand jury in the circuitcourt afore-
said. The finesimposed may be collectedby executions
offierifaciasor capiasprofine,or both,or such otherpro-
ceedings as are now allowed by law for the collectionof
finesor satisfactionof judgments ,or it shallbe the duty
of the county attorney of Henry county to prosecute
said actions in the justices' court aforesaid,and he shall
for his servicesreceive thirtyper cent. of so much of the
fines so imposed as are collected. Appeals from the
judgment of saidjustices' court to the Henry circuitcourt
may be taken in the manner now provided by law in
cases of misdemeanor .
§ 9. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 8 , 1882 .
CHAPTER 917 .
AN ACT to authorizethe county court of Pulaski county to take stock
in turnpike roads .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . An electionshallbe heldin the county of Pulaski
on the firstMonday in August , 1882,for the purpose of
taking the sense of the qualified voters thereof as to
whether the county court shalltake stock in turnpike
roads in said county . Such election shall be held, and
return thereof made , as now provided by law for holding
county elections,except that the certificatementioned in
section 2 of article5 ,chapter 33 ,of the General Statutes,
shall be delivered to the county judge , who shallcause
an order to be entered upon the records of the county
court,at the next term thereof,showing that such election
was held, and the result thereof. It shallbe the duty of
the sheriffto cause printed notices of said electionto be
posted in at least four public places in each voting pre-
cinet in said county fifteendays before said election.
The question to be propounded to each voter at said
election shallbe , " Are you in favor of the county court

332 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of Pulaski county subscribingstock in turnpike roads
within the county ? " The costs of said electionshallbe
paid as in case of other county elections.
§ 2. If, at the election provided for in section one of
thisact,a majority of the votes shallbe castin the affirm-
ative,then the county court shallsubscribe stock in every
mile of turnpike road to be built in said county to the
amount of one half the cost of building said roads ; said
costs to be ascertained by estimates of a competent en-
gineer : Provided,No part of said stock shallbe subscribed
or paid untilthe other half of the stock of any proposed
road shallbe secured by bona fide subscriptionsof citi-
zens of the county , or other persons .
§3 . That the said court may execute the bonds of the
county ,of such denomination as the court may choose ,
not lessthan fiftydollarseach ,in printed torm , under the
seal of the county , signed by the presiding judge of the
court,and countersigned by its clerk, payable when and
where the court may elect, with annual or semi -annual
interestcoupons , at a rate of interestnot to exceed eight
per cent. per annum ,and to be sold for the purpose of
raisingmoney to pay for said turnpike stock.
§4. That to provide for the payment of theprincipal
and interest of the said bonds , the said court is hereby
authorized to levy an ad valorem tax, not to exceed
twenty cents to a hundred dollars, on allthe property in
said county subject to taxation for county or State pur-
poses, for each and every year that such tax may be
necessary. And the proceeds of said tax,together with
the dividend accruing to the county on itsstock in said
turnpike roads, shall be paid to the treasurer of the
county,who shallapply it in payment of the principal
and interestof the said bonds , under the directionsof
said court .
§5 . That the court shalldesignate the county treasurer,
or, if there be no such officer, a special treasurer, to
receive and pay out the funds mentioned in sectionthree,
and such treasurer shall, before receiving any of said
moneys , execute a good and sufficientbond ,with good

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 333
surety,approved by the court,for the faithfulperformance
of his duties .
§6 . That the sheriff or collector of taxes of said
county , and his sureties, shall be responsible on their
bonds for the collectionof the tax herein authorized, in
the same manner they are responsible for the ordinary
county levy; shallhave and exercise the same powers in
the collectionthereof as they have by law for collecting
the county and State taxes; and shall, under the same
penalties,pay over the same to whom the court may
appoint treasurer, and at such times as the court may
require .
§7 . That the presidingjudge of said county shallbe
ex officioa member of the board of directorsof any turn-
pike road in the county in which the county owns stock,
and shallrepresent the county ,and cast the vote or votes
to which it may be entitledin all the meetings of said
board .
§8. Thisactshalltakeeffectfromitspassage.
Approved April 8 , 1882 .
CHAPTER 918 .
AN ACT to authorizethe Ohio county court to fund her bonded indebted.
ness at a lower rate of interest .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the outstandingbonds heretoforeissuedby
the Ohio county court, and known as the jail bonds ,
togetherwith the interestdue and unpaid thereon,may
be funded into new bonds , at a rate not exceeding five
per cent.per annum ,and redeemable at any time within
ten years , at the option of said court, and to be sold at
not lessthan theirpar value .
§2 . The bonds aforesaidshallbe signedby the judge
of the Ohio county court, and countersigned by the clerk
of said court , and shall not exceed in value the amount
of the outstanding bonds , with theiraccrued interest.

334 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§3 . The proceeds arising from the sale of the bonds
authorizedby thisactshallbe used in the redemption of
the jailbonds and interestoutstanding, and for no other
purpose .
§ 4. The bonds authorized by thisact may be redeemed
at any time within ten years, at the option of said court,
which shall have power to call same in for redemption
by giving thirtydays ' notice to the party or partieshold-
ing said bonds,if known to said court; ifnot,then by
four weekly insertionsin some paper published in said
county .
§ 5. The county judge of Ohio county is authorized to
sellsaid bond , and take up the outstanding bond and
interest now due , and shall be liable on his officialbond

CHAPTER 920 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act to incorporate the Frankfort
Lumber , Brick, and Impiement Manufacturing Company ," approved
March 3 , 1869 .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1. That the titleand name of the company created
and incorporated by said original act be , and the same
ishereby, amended and changed from " The Frankfort
Lumber , Brick, and Implement Manufacturing Com-
pany " to " The Kentucky Lumber ,Brick,and Implement
Manufacturing and Mining Company ."
§ 2. In addition to its other business now authorized
by saidoriginalact of incorporation,saidcompany shall
have power ,and is hereby authorized,to engage in and
carry on the mining , manufacture ,purchase , and sale of
coal and other minerals and fuel,and all kinds of build-
ing material; and itmay engage in and carry on allthe
differentbranches of business authorized by said original

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 335
act and thisamendment thereto,or in any one or more of
them ,as itmay ,from time to time ,elect.
§3 . Any threeof the individualsnamed as incorpora.
torsin said originalact shallhave power ,and are hereby
authorized,to organize the company and call the stock-
holders together for the election of a regular board of
five managers , and for the transaction of other business ,
as provided in sections three and four of said act ; and
untilsuch election is held and fivemanagers appointed ,
they shallconstitutethe firstboard of managers .
§4 . The company may at any time increaseitspur-
chase ,holding, or lease of real estate to any amount not
to exceed three hundred thousand dollars, and it may
cultivate or lease the same , and selland dispose of its
products for the benefit of the stockholders; or it may
sellthe real estateso purchased ,or any part thereof, and
convey titleto the same ; and the company may further
increaseitscapitalstockto any amount not to exceed
two and one half million dollars .
§ 5. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 8 , 1882
CHAPTER 921 .

of Kentucky :
§ 1.That AlbertLeet,W. W. Meadows ,Jno.A. Wyatt, Corporators.

their successors and associates,are hereby constituteda
body-corporateand politic,by thename of the " Manufac- Style.
turingand Builders' Association,of Fulton,Fulton County ,
Kentucky ," with perpetual succession, with fullpower to
manufacture all kinds of building material, furniture,
spokes,hubs,felloes, and agriculturalimplements of all
kinds; and by said name may sue and be sued ,contract Powers.
and be contractedwith, acquire, hold, and dispose of
property,real and personal; may have a seal,and may

Principal office.
Capital stock.
make such rules , regulations , and by -laws , and enforce
the same for the government of the company and pro-
tection of its property as the board of president and
directorsmay deem proper. The principalofficeof said
company shallbe at Fulton ,in Fulton county , Kentucky ,
and they may have such other places of business in Ful-
ton county ,and agencies,as the board may think proper .
§2 . That the above named corporators,together with
such others as may be associated with them , shallhave
power , and are hereby authorized,to elect, at such time
and places as may be convenient , a president,secretary,
treasurer, and such other officersas they may deem need-
fulfor the transaction of theircorporate business.
§3 . That the capital stock of said associationshallbe
fiftythousand dollars, which shall be divided into shares
of one hundred dollars each .
§ 4. That said corporation shall have power , and they
are hereby authorized,to issue certificatesof stock to the
amount of fifty thousand dollars; and they shall have
power to purchase and hold such real estateas may be
necessary for the transaction of its business ; also to take
and hold any real estate, as security,to secure the pay-
ment of any debt due the association, or to purchase real
and personal estate at any sale made in virtue of any
judgment at law , or decree in equity, in favor of said
corporation.
§5 . That said corporation shall have power to erect
buildings of any kind by contract, and that for and in
consideration of the price agreed upon for such building,
a lien is granted and retained by said corporation on
said property until the amount due the same shallhave
been paid .
§6 . That the corporators aforesaid, or a majority of
them , may meet at any time in Fulton , Kentucky , of
which due notice shall be given ,at which meeting they
may open books and receive subscription to the stock of
aid institution: Provided, That said company shall not
commence business untilthe sum of twenty dollarsto the
share shallhave been paid on allthe stock taken .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 337
§8 . And whenever fiftyshares of stock shall have
been taken and paid in, said associationmay go into
operation; other shares may subsequently be subscribed,
until the whole stock is taken . The officers shall be
elected on the firstMonday in January , and hold their
officesone year . In the electionsfor officersand transac-
tion of other business,each member present shallbe en-
titled to a vote for each share owned : Provided , That
married women and infants, members of the association,
may vote by proxy or by theirhusbands or guardian .
§ 9. Said associationshallhave power to issue and sell Mayissuebonds
or hypothecate its bonds , with coupons or interestwar-
rants attached,for any sum not exceeding two thirdsof
the sum of capital stock paid up , and make the same
payable at such time and place as may be deemed most
advisable ; but such bonds shall not bear a rate of interest
exceeding six per cent. per annum ,to be paid annually
or semi -annually , as said corporation may direct: Pro-
vided,however, That no bonds shall be issued without the
consent of two thirds of the stockholders .
§ 10.No stockholdershallbe individuallyliableforany
debts or engagements of the said association ; neither
shall his or her private property be subject to the pay-
ment of the debts of the association .
§ 11. No member shallbe elected to the officeof presi-
dent of the association who does not own as much as
fiveshares of stock in said association, and the president
shallbe the manager of allthe buildings and machinery
of the association; and no bill or account against the
same shall be paid by the treasurer untilithas been ex-
amined and approved by the president.
§ 12. The buildings,machinery ,and manufactured pro-
ducts of the association, shall be free and exempt from
the payment of allcorporation or citytaxes whatever for
the space of ten years .
§ 13. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
-sage .
Approved April 8 , 1882 .

enter his motion in the Louisville chancery court for a
writ of possession,commanding the marshal of said court
to put him in the possession of the land or improvement
described in said deed ,and on his filingsaid deed ,or an
attested copy of the record thereof, the chancellor shall
award , the clerk of said court shall issue,and the marshal

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 349
of said court shall execute such writ. The person enter-
ing such motion shallpay to the clerk of said court a tax
of fiftycentsforthe benefitof the State, and shallpay to
the officersof the court allcostsincurred by him in such
proceeding . The notice provided for in thissection shall
be given by at least one insertion in one of the news-
papers printedand publisheddailyin the cityof Louis-
ville, and shall be in form and substance as follows :
" Notice is hereby given that I will,on the - day of -— ,
18- , enter a motion in the Louisville chancery court for
a writ requiring the marshal of said court to put me
intothepossessionof (here describetheland or improve-
ment ). A. B." One of the issuesof the newspapers
containing the notice shall be filed when the motion is
entered , and shall be evidence that the notice was given .
No action shall be instituted or maintained to recover
the possession of lands or improvements from the person
or those claiming under him to whom such land or im-
provements were conveyed by the clerk of the board of
councilmen under the authority of the eleventh section
hereof , unless such action be instituted within six months
from the date of such deed , nor shall such action be in-
stitutedunless the person institutingthe same pays and
tenders into court for the defendants to such action the
purchase price of such land or improvements at the sale
thereof made by the receiver of citytaxes, with interest
thereon at the rate of twenty -five per cent.per annum
from the date of such sale; also the sum of all taxes
on and assessmentsagainstsuch land or improvements
paid by the defendants to such action, or those under
whom they claim , with interest thereon at the rate of
twenty -five per cent. per annum from the date of such
payments ,and the fullvalue of any and allimprovements
or additionsput on or added to such land or improve-
ments by the defendants to such action , or those under
whom they claim. The city of Louisville shall, in all
cases where it is a purchaser at sales made by the re-
ceiver of city taxes,be entitledto the same rights and
remedies as any other purchaser .

350 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 13. In addition to the remedies herein given ,the city
of Louisville shall have the right to institute an action in
the Louisvillechancery court for the recovery of the
amount of any unpaid tax billor tax billsand interest,
and the enforcement of itslien,and may in such action,
without bond or affidavit,sue out a general attachment
similar to that provided for in chapter three of title
eight of the Code of Practice in civil cases .
§ 14. The general council shallannually ,by ordinance ,
fix and determine the amount or rate of tax to be levied
on each one hundred dollars of the assessed value of
the lands,improvements,and personalpropertyassessed
under the provisionsof thisact in the year in which such
taxes are levied, and the rate of taxation thus fixed shall
be uniform on all the classes of property assessed,pro-
vided that there shallbe levied annually , for the benefit
of the treasury of said city, a tax of two dollarson every
person listedunder the third section hereof: Provided,
That the rate of taxation annually levied under this act
shallnot exceed the sum now authorizedby law .
§15 Said receiverof city taxes shall, on or before the
31st day of December , in the year 1882, and in every
year thereafter, state and settlehis accounts with and
receive a quietus from the finance committee of the gen-
eral council, and in such settlements said receiver of city
taxes shallbe allowed no creditfor any uncollected tax
bill, unless such committee believe, from the evidence
offered,that such uncollected tax bill could not have been
collected by such receiver of city taxes, and for the
amount of every uncollected tax bill for which such
committee refuses in such settlement to allow a credit,
said receiverof citytaxes,and the suretieson his official
bond ,shall be liableto account for and pay to said city,
the same as if such tax billshad been collected; but such
receiverof citytaxesshallbe subrogatedtothe rightsof
said cityas against the persons owing such uncollected
tax bills. In the event that such receiver fails to obtain a
quietus at such settlement, such failureshallbe reported
to the board of aldermen at their next meeting thereafter,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 35
whereupon such board of aldermen shallat once declare
the officeof receiverof citytaxes to be vacant , and order
the treasurerof said cityto immediately take possession
of allthe moneys ,books ,bills, registers,accounts,& c , of
the officeof such receiver of citytaxes,and at the next
meeting of the general council thereafter there shall be
elected in joint session, on viva voce vote,a receiver of
city taxes for the unexpired term of such defaulting
receiverof citytaxes.
§ 16. The general councilmay , by ordinance not in
conflictherewith , regulate the officeof the receiver of
city taxes, the officeof assessor, and the collectionof
taxes, and may by ordinance fix the compensation of
any of itsofficersor employes ,and define theirstudies.
§ 17. In all cases when land or improvements pur-
chased by the cityof Louisville,at the salesthereof made
by the receiver of citytaxes, are redeemed , the redemp-
tion money shall be paid to the treasurer of said city,
and in allsuch cases allunpaid taxes assessed and levied
by said city on such land or improvement shall be re-
garded as having been paid by said cityon the day of
and prior to such redemption .
§ 18. All courts in the county of Jefferson shall take
judicial notice of the passage , approval , contents, and
publication of each ordinance of the city of Louisville,
§19 . The generalcouncilof the cityof Louisvilleshall
have power by joint resolution to adjourn for not longer
than thirtydays at any time ,but shall be subject to be
convened at any time by notice from the mayor .
§20 . That no principalof any school,and no principal
or teacher of any private sectarian or other school, shall
admit to any such school any child or minor who shall
not have been vaccinated within five years next preced-
ing the admission or application for admission to any
such school of such child or minor ; or shall any such
principalor teacher retainin, or permit to attend any
such school,any child or minor who shallnot have been
vaccinated within five years next preceding the taking
effect of this statute . The evidence of such vaccination

352 LAWS OF KENTUCKY
to be presented to any such principal or teacher,as is
mentioned above , shall be a certificatesigned by the
health officeror any practicing physician.
§21 . The healthofficeris hereby empowered to visit
any and allpublicand privateschoolsin the city,and to
make ,or cause to be made ,any examination of the chil-
dren and minors in attendance therein as often as he
may deem necessary to secure compliance with the pro-
visions hereof .
§ 22. Any principal of a public school, or principal or
teacher in any private or other school, who shallviolate
any of the provisionsof this statute, or shall in any way
prevent or attempt to prevent the health officerfrom
exercising the power conferred by thisact, shall be fined
for each offense not less than five dollars nor more than
one hundred dollars,recoverable upon ordinance warrant
in the city court, and be paid into the citytreasury.
§23 . The first eighteen sections of this act will take
effectfrom on and after the 1st day of September , 1882 ,
and sections 19 , 20 , and 21 hereof will take effect and be
in force from and afterthe passage of this act; and all
laws and partsof laws in conflictherewith are hereby
repealed .
Approved April 8, 1882.

CHAPTER 930 .
AN ACT todispensewith fencing, to prevent the running at large of cattle
and for the benefit of owners of land on Green River Island .
WHEREAS , Green River Island is separated from the
main land of the State of Kentucky by the channel of
the Ohio river, and the great scarcityof timber on said
island renders it impracticable for land-owners there to
fence their lands ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That so much of article1, chapter 55 , of the Gen-
eral Statutes as requires certain fences or inclosures for
the legal protection of land against depredations of cat-
tle, shallnot apply to Green River Island.
§2 . Owners ,tenants,and occupants of land on Green
River Island shall keep all their cattleand live stock of
every descriptionwithin theirown inclosures; and the
boundaries of the several tracts,parcels,and lotsof land
on said island are hereby declared and shall be lawful
fences to allintents and purposes .
§ 3. Itshallbe unlawful for cattle or live stock of any
descriptionto run at large on said island, and for all
depredationsof cattleor livestockof any descriptionon
said island,the occupants of land who may be damaged
thereby shallhave allthe rights, privileges,and remedies
prescribed by chapter 55 of the General Statutes; and in
addition thereto,they shallhave the rightto take up and
confine any animal found depredating upon their lands,
and to recover of the owner of such animal the damages
sustained, and the cost and expense of keeping such ani-
mal , for which they shallhave a lien thereon .
§4 . Any person taking up any animal , as allowed in
the preceding section,shallprovidefor itthe necessary
food and water,and shallgive to the owner ,ifknown ,
notice thereof within two days thereafter ; and if such
animal is not reclaimed by the owner , and all damages
and charges paid within two days aftersuch notice,the
taker-up may procure from any justice of the peace ,on
affidavitspecifyingthe factsof the case,and describing
the animal , a warrant forthe sale of such animal ,which

358 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
sale shallbe for cash ,and shall be made by the officerto
whom said warrant is directed, after written advertise-
ment of the time,terms,and place of saleforfivedays
preceding , unless such animal is sooner reclaimed ,and
alldamages ,costs,and expenses paid . The proceeds of
such sale,afterpaying the costsof the proceeding,shall,
to the extent of the damages and expenses ,be paid to
the taker-up of the animal ,and the balance shallbe paid
to the owner , if known , or if not known , then to the
trusteeof thejury fund of Henderson county . But noth-
ing herein shallbe construed as limitingthe recovery by
the taker-up of any animal to the value of such animal .
§5 . A copy of the order of saleshallbe served upon
the owner of any cattleor otheranimal taken up under
the provisions of thisact,and he shall have the right at
any time beforesaleto reclaimthe same by payment of
all damages ,expenses , and costs,or by executing bond ,
with sufficientsecurity,that he will diligentlydefend the
action of the plaintiff, and perform the judgment of the
court. Upon the execution of such bond , the officer
taking the same and holding the warrant shall deliver
the animal or animals to the defendant , and return the
warrant and bond to a justice of the peace , and shall
notifythe partiesof the time and place of such justice's
next succeeding term of court, at which the case shall
stand for trial as other cases .
§6. Upon trialof the case,judgment shallbe rendered,
and if in favor of the plaintiff, itshall be against allthe
partiesto the bond , subjectto appeal as in other cases.
§7 . If the owner of any animal sold under this act be
unknown , he may at any time within two years after
such sale,upon his motion made in the Henderson cir-
cuit court,after ten days notice to the Commonwealth's
attorney, and upon proof of his ownership, be allowed
the amount paid into the jury fund ,which shallbe paid
by the trustee thereof upon the order of the court,after
deducting the commissions allowed upon other moneys
receivedand paid by him.
§ 8. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 11, 1882.

of Kentucky:

CHAPTER 938 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Falmouth , Willow and Powersville Turnpike

§2 . That the business of said company shallbe to

364 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
road from some point on the Falmouth and Milford Turn-
pike, near Brandywine creek, in Pendleton county, by

or on such eligibleroute adjacent to that indicated as its

ment or condemnation under a writ of ad quod damnum ,
§3 . That those persons who have heretofore subscribed

dred shares of stock . The notice shall be written or
printed, and postedconspicuouslyat the company's prin-
cipal office,and at each toll-gate on itsroad .
§5 . That the board of directors shall, on their own
election,immediately electone of theirnumber president
of theboard ,who shallpresideat theirmeetings ,and be the
executive of said board . The board shallhave the power
to selectfor said company a secretary and treasurer, and
such otherofficersand agents as they may deem neces-
sary, to prescribe their duties, and fix their compensa-
tion .
§ 6. That itshall be the duty of the secretary to keep
the books and accountsof said company ,and to attend

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 365
the meetings of said board , and keep in a substantial
book an accurate record of allitsproceedings.
§7 . That the treasurer of said company shall be the
custodian of allmoney belonging to it,and shallpay the
same on the order of the board of directors, certifiedto
him by the secretary. But before enteringon hisduties,
he shallexecute a bond to the company , with sureties
approved by the board of directors, conditionedthathe
will faithfullyperform allthe duties of treasurer, and pay
over as directed by the board of directors any money in
his hands belonging to the company ,and will make full
settlement of his accounts when required by said board ;
and on going out of office, will deliverto his successor
allbooks ,papers , property,money ,or other thing in his
hands belonging to said company . This bond shall be
recorded in his record-book and preserved by the secre-
tary .
§8 . That said company shallhave the right to estab-
lishtoll-gates on said road , and charge and collect tolls
as authorized by the General Statutes.
§9 . That the capital stock of said company shall be
divided into shares of fiftydollars or twenty -five dollars
each , as the first board of directors elected hereunder
shall determine , which determination shall be entered on
their record -book .
§ 10. That said board of directorsshallhave power to
make allby -laws ,rules and regulations, for the govern-
ment of themselves and the offices and affairs of said
company ,not inconsistentwith public law .
§ 11. That at any general election, the stockholders
may change the time of holding the regular election for
directors .
§ 12. That R. S. Fishback , Park Peddicord , and Elisha
Galloway are hereby made commissioners to organize
said company ,and they,or any of them choosing to act,
may open books for the subscription of stock, call an
election for the election of the firstboard of directors ,
and do such other acts and things as may be necessary to
completely organize said company .
§ 13. This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved April 11 , 1882 .

366 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 939 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act to amend and reduce into one
allthe actsin regard to the town of Columbia ."
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the poll-tax and ad valorem taxes levied by
the board of trustees of said town be a lien upon the
property listedby any person or corporation in said town
to the extent of the taxes due from each listuntil said
taxes shallhave been paid ,and no sale, mortgage , trans-
fer, gift, or devise shall affect the lien thereon . The
taxes levied by said board of trustees shall be due on
the firstday of June of each year ,and if the same be
not paid on or before the firstday of October following,
thecollectorappointedby saidboard of trustees,afterhe
shall have executed bond , and taken the oath of office,
may proceed to distrainfor said taxes, and the said col-
lectorishereby empowered and authorized to distrainfor
saidtaxes; he may advertiseand sellthe personalprop-
erty for taxes due thereon,or so much as may be suffi-
cientto pay said taxes,the cost of levy, and collectors'
commission . If there be no personal property out of
which the taxes of any person or corporationmay be
made ,and that person or corporation has listedfor that
year's assessment any real estate,the collectormay levy
upon said real estate, and sellso much thereof as willbe
sufficientto pay said taxes and cost of sale. The laws
laiddown in the General Statutes of Kentucky in regard
to the sale of lands for revenue and county levy taxes
shall be made applicable to the sale of real estate for
taxes leviedby the board of trusteesof the town of Co-
lumbia . The collector shall be vested with the same
powers and authority,shallbe entitledto the same fees
for services,subjectto the same penalties, and have upon
him the same duties imposed in regard to the collection
of said taxes that a sheriff has under the General Statutes
of Kentucky in regard to the collectionof the State reve-
nue and county levytaxes,and allthe laws applicableto

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 367
1
a sheriffin regard to the collectionof taxes are hereby
made applicable to said collector.
§ 2. That thisact shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 11, 1882.
CHAPTER 940 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof D. S. Trimble, of Menifee county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

draw his warrant upon the Treasurer in favorof D. S.
Trimble for the sum of one hundred and seventy -five
dollars, payable out of any money in the Treasury not
otherwiseappropriated,forkeeping ElizaEllenTrimble,
a pauper lunatic,fortwo years and four months : Pro-
vided, That thisamount shall be in fullfor allclaims for
the care of saidlunaticup to thisdate.
§2 . This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved April 11, 1882 .

AN ACT to constituteWabash Island,in Union county, a common school
district in said county .

of Kentucky :

AN ACT for the benefitof Hiram Newton Sanford,son of J. N. Sanford,
of Ballard county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Hiram Newton Sanford ,son of J. N. Sanford ,

CHAPTER 944 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act to amend the charterof the city
of Henderson ."
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That in addition to the tax now authorized to be
levied by the city of Henderson ,the said city of Hender-
son is hereby authorizedand empowered to levy and
collect taxes , at the rate of not exceeding fifteen cents
on the one hundred dollars'worth of property,upon the
tax-payers of said city,taxable under the revenue laws
of this State : Provided, however, That said taxes shall
not be levied for more than five years after the passage
of this act : And provided further, That the amount real-
ized from said taxation shall be used for, and devoted
exclusively to, the repair and improvement of the gas-
works belonging to said city, and such other public im-
provements as the council of said city may determine .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 369
CHAPTER 945 .
AN ACT to authorize the commissioners for the county of Kenton to sub-
scribeforstockin turnpike roads in said county , and provide means for
the payment thereof.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the commissionersfor Kenton county,at any
regular meeting of theirboard ,allof such commissioners
being present and concurring therein,shall have power
to make subscriptionto the capitalstockof any turnpike
road company which may hereafterbe incorporatedunder
the laws of this Commonwealth for the construction of a
turnpike road ,the termini of which isin Kenton county ,
or the boundary lines thereof, not exceeding the sum of
seven hundred and fiftydollars per mile for each mile of
said road : Provided, That such subscriptionsshallnot be
made until said board of commissioners are satisfied that
an amount of stock sufficient,with the aid of such county
subscription,to complete each mile of road to which such
county subscriptionsapply, has been taken by private
subscriptions.
§2 . That said commissioners are hereby authorized to
issue bonds in sums not less than one hundred dollars,
and not exceeding five hundred dollars,bearing any rate
of interestnot exceeding six (6) per cent, per annum ,
the principal of which bonds shall be payable in ten
yearsafterdate,having coupons attached theretoforthe
payment of the interestsemi -annually on the firstdays
of January and July in each year , at the branch of the
Northern Bank of Kentucky at Covington . The board
of commissioners shall prescribe the form of the bonds ,
and when issued they shallbe signed by the three elected
or appointedcommissioners,attestedby the county clerk,
having the seal of the county attached thereto, and in-
dorsedby thepresidingjudge of saidcounty.
§3 . The commissioners shall prescribethe time ,man-
ner,and place at which such bonds shall be offeredand
sold; but no sale thereof shall be made for less than their
face value .
LOC . L.,VOL . II- 24

370 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 4. Itshallbe the duty of said commissionerstopro-
vide for the payment of the intereston saidbonds,and
for theirfinalpayment when due .
§ 5. To enable the board of commissionersto comply
with this act, they are hereby authorized to levy an ad
valorem tax,not exceeding twenty -five cents upon each
one hundred dollars in value of the taxable property in
said county of Kenton , outside of the corporate limitsof
the city of Covington , which shall be collected by the
sheriffof said county , who shall be responsibleupon his
officialbond for the faithfulperformance of the duties
hereby imposed .
§6. That saidboard of commissionersshallhave power
to make all orders necessary for carrying into execution
the provisions of thisact.
§7 . The bonds hereby authorized to be issued shallnot
be subject or liableto county or municipal taxation.
§ 8. Before the said commissioners of said county shall
subscribestock or exercise any of the powers herein con-
templated ,the question shallbe submitted to the qualified
voters of said county outside of the corporate limits of
the cityof Covington , whether or not they are in favor of
empowering saidcommissioners to subscribeto the cap-
ital stock of turnpike roads constructed in said county ,
at an electionto be held at each of the voting places of
said county,outsideof thecitylimitsof the saidcityof
Covington, on a day to be fixedby the county judge
of saidcounty,and ifthe affirmativeof said proposition
shallreceive the majority of the vote cast,the said com-
missioners shall be authorized to exercise the powers
conferred by thisact.
§ 9. It shallbe the duty of the sheriffof said county to
open a pollateach of thevotingplacesin saidcounty,as
provided in the eighthsectionof thisact,and said elec-
tion shallbe conducted ,and the returnsthereof made and
canvassed , and the result declared, in the same man-
ner and by the same officersas in election of county
officers. The sheriffshallgive ten days' notice of the
time,place,and objectof saidelection,by printednotices

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 371
posted in at leasttwo publicplaces in the neighborhood
of said voting places,and by two insertionsin a news-
paper printed in said county .
§ 10. The judge of the said Kenton county court shall
cause the result of said election to be entered on the
order-book of saidcounty court,and provideforthepay-
ments of said election .
§11.The countymay,byan orderdulyenteredonits
order book , again submit the question to the qualified
votersof said county,but not oftenerthan once in any
year afterthe vote shallhave firstbeen taken .
§ 12. This actshalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 11, 1882.
CHAPTER 946 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Moorehead, West Libertyand Salyersville
Railway Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That D. D. Sublett and Joseph Gardner , of Ma- Incorporators.
goffincounty,and William Mynhier,John P. Salyer,W.
W. Cox , and John T. Hazelrigg , of Morgan county , and
Warren Addison, A. J. McKinzie , Cyrus Alley, Z. T.
Young ,and A. J. Thurber ,of Rowan county ,and J. W.
Perry, of Owen county, Kentucky, or any four of them
who may act, are hereby appointed commissioners ,and
they, theirassociates and successors, are created a body-
politicand corporate,for the purpose of constructing a
railway with a single or double track, beginning at a
point on the Elizabethtown , Lexington and Big Sandy
Railroad, between Licking river and Tripletttunnel,
thence by West Liberty, Morgan county , to Salyersville,
in Magoffin county ,and to the Virginia line, at a point
between the northern boundary line of Harlan county
and the southern boundary line of Martin county ,Ken-
tucky .

372 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Style.
Capitalstock.
§2 . The name and style of the corporation hereby
created shall be the Moorehead , West Liberty, and Sal-
yersvilleRailway Company ,and by thatname shallhave
perpetual succession, and be capable of suing and being
sued ,pleading and being impleaded ,defending and being
defended ,in law and equity,in allcourts and elsewhere ,
in likemanner and as fullyas natural persons may be ;
and said corporation may have and use a common seal,
and 'set aside, and renew or alter the same at pleasure ;
and by said corporate name may contract and be con-
tracted with,and by that name shall be , and are hereby ,
invested with allthe powers ,privileges,immunities , and
franchisesof natural persons. The capital stock of said
company may be any amount not exceeding fivemillions
of dollars, divided into shares of one hundred dollars
each,and may be subscribedfor,purchased,owned ,and
controlled by individuals in and out of this State, and
railroad companies and other corporations in Kentucky
and elsewhere .
Directors .
General office.
§3 . The corporators named in the firstsection,or such
of them as may act, shall constitutethe firstboard of
directorsof said company ,and may serve as such until
theirsuccessorsare duly elected and qualified. The said
corporators, or a majorityof them , shallmeet at such
time and place as they may agree upon and designate ,
and from theirown number ,or from such personsas they
may associate with themselves ,choose a president, and
appoint such other officersand agents as may be neces-
sary; they shall have power to adopt such by -laws for
the government of the company ,its officers, agents,and
employes,inthe generalconduct and management of the
affairsand business of the company as may be needful
and proper,and alter,amend ,or repealthe same at will.
§4 . The generalofficeof thecompany shallbe located
in the town of Moorehead , Rowan county, Kentucky .
The directorsshall have power and authority to locate
and establishbranch officesor agencies at such other
place or placesas may ,in theirjudgment ,be found nec-
essary .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 373
§5 . That the incorporators or firstboard of directors
shall provide in the by -laws for an annual election of
directors, consisting of seven in number , who shall be
stockholders of said company ,and fixthe time and place ,
and determine the manner of the first and succeeding
electionsof directors. The directorssucceeding the first
board shallseverallyhold their officesfor one year , and
until their successors are elected and qualified. Each
board of directorsmay fillany vacancy occurring in their
number ,and shallchoose from theirown number a pres-
ident. It shallrequire a majority,including the president,
to constitutea quorum of the board of directorsfor the
transaction of business .
§ 6. At the electionof directors,and allother meetings Proxies.
of stockholders in said company , stockholders shall be
entitled to one vote for each share of stock so held ,which
may be cast in person or by writtenproxy. Provision
shallbe made in the by -laws for annual meetings of the
stockholders,and such called meetings as are necessary .
§7 . The presidentand directorsshallhave the manage-
ment and control of the property,business, and affairsof
the company ; may appoint all such officers,agents, and
employes as are necessary; prescribetheirduties and
compensation , and take from any one of them bond with
securityforthe faithfulperformance of his duties.
§ 8. It shallbe lawful for the president and directorsof
said company to open stock-books for subscriptionto the
capitalstock at such time and places, and continue them
open for such length of time , as may be deemed neces-
sary. The capitalstock of said company is hereby
declared to be personal property,and may be issued, cer-
tified,transferred,and registered in such manner and at
such places as may be ordered and provided by the by-
laws of said company .
§ 9. The said Moorehead , West Liberty, and Salyers- Powers
villeRailway Company shall be , and are hereby , au-
thorized and empowered to locate, construct,build, and
complete a railroad, with such number of tracks and
lines of telegraph as said corporation may desire, and

374 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
for that purpose shallbe entitledto acquire titleto a
continuous line of one hundred feet in width of land,
with such additionalground as may be necessaryforthe
use of saidrailroad,from a pointon the said Elizabeth-
town ,Lexington and Big Sandy Railroad,between Lick-
ing river and Tripletttunnel, thence by such route as
said company may select, to any point on the eastern
boundary line of the State, between the northern line
of the county of Harlan and the southern line of the
county of Martin,in the Stateof Kentucky ,and to con-
struct,build,and furnish said railroad with allnecessary
side-tracks,turn-outs, switches, depots, stations, and all
necessary buildings, erections, and constructions for the
convenient working of said railroad, and carrying on its
business,and with allthe rolling-stock of every sort and
kind, and all other machinery, implements, and prop-
erty the company deem necessary and proper for the
proper prosecutionof itsbusiness; and said company
shallhave power to operate and maintain the same ;
and for said purpose said company , in its corporate
capacity,is hereby vested with all the powers necessary
for purchasing , acquiring titleto, holding, selling, and
conveying and transferringallreal,personal,and mixed
propertysaidcocompany may deem necessaryto the carry-
ing out of the objectsof this act, to the same extent as
any naturalperson or persons,or any other corporation
may exercise such right of acquisition, holding, or dis-
posing of his,their, or itsproperty.
§ 10. That portionof said Moorehead , West Liberty
and SalyersvilleRailway which may be constructed from
a pointon the Elizabethtown,Lexington and Big Sandy
Railroad to the eastern boundary line of the State of
Kentucky ,with itsappurtenances and property belonging
thereto,shallbe,and arehereby,exempted freefrom tax-
ation, State, county ,or municipal , as follows,i, e.,each
sectionof ten milesshallbe exempted forfiveyears from
itscompletion from all taxation: Provided, That ifthe
road be not operated afterthe lapse of fiveyears from
itscompletion,thisexemption from taxation shallbe in

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 375
operative ; but in no event to be exempt from taxation
for more than eightyears from the passage of thisact.
§ 11. Said corporationis hereby authorized, by its
agents , surveyors, and engineers, to cause such ex-
aminations and surveys of the differentroutes as are
necessary to determine which is most suitablefor the con-
struction of said railroad ; and it shall be lawful for said
company to enter upon ,take possession of, and use all
such real estate as may be necessary for the construction,
operation, and maintenance of said railroad,its depots ,
side-tracks,water -stations,engine -houses ,machine -shops ,
and other buildings and appendages necessary for the use
of said railroad; and said corporation may also take and
use any earth,gravel,stone,or other materials necessary
for the construction, maintenance , and repairs of said
railroadand itsappendages and works ; and may , by its
authorized agents , agree with the owner of the land ,
earth, gravel, stone, or other materials wanted for the
purchase of the whole , or the use and occupation of the
real estate,the owners thereof being competent to con-
tract; but if the owners , being incompetent , refuse to
contract,or any of them are femes covertor non compos,or
out of the county in which the property wanted is situ-
ated, applicationmay be made by the company or its
agents to the county court of any county into or through
which it is proposed to construct said railroad. Such
court may appoint three commissioners , who shall be
residents of the county , and freeholders,in which the
property is situated, who shall be sworn to discharge
theirdutiesunder thisact justlyand impartially,to the
best of their skilland judgment ..It shall be the duty of
said commissioners to view the land or materialsrequired,
and fixthe amount of compensation to which the owner
or owners may be entitled,and make out and return to the
officeof thecounty clerkof saidcounty a report,in writ-
ing,particularlydescribingthe land and materialsvalued,
and the amount of damages , if any ,assessed. The clerk
shallreceive such report and filethe same , indorsing
thereon the time of its being filed; and if the owner

376 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
residesin the county ,he shallforthwith issue a summons
eommanding the owner or owners of said land or mate-
rialsto appear and show cause why the reportshallnot
be confirmed ,which summons shallforthwith be executed
by the sheriffor other officerin whose hands it may be
placed . The report shall be docketed in the county
court,and stand for trialat any term of said court com-
mencing not lessthan ten days afterservice of the sum-
mons , unless the service is had in another county ,when
it shall be twenty days , it being understood that the
county judge may act in all cases where the county
court is mentioned in this chapter. If the owner be a
non -resident of the State , the court shall, at the firstterm
afterthe report is filed, appoint an attorney to defend for
such owner ,who may fileexceptionsat any time after
sixtydays from the date of his appointment ; and upon
exceptions being filedby the owner or his attorney,or
by the company ,the court shallforthwith order a jury to
be empaneled to try the question of compensation and
damages ,unless,for good cause ,time is given for prepa-
ration. If no exception be filed,the report shall be con-
firmed. The commissioners and jury shall,in estimating
damages , allow the owner the full value of the land
taken or to be taken by the company ; but in estimating
incidentaldamages ,or damage to the land of the owner
adjoining the land taken ,may take into consideration the
advantages to accrue to the adjoining lands of the owner
by reason of the building of the road . Said company
shallnot be delayed in itswork of construction by pro-
ceedings provided in thissection,but may , afterfirstgiv-
ing bond with sufficientsecurityto pay such damages and
costsas may be finallyawarded , proceed with itscon-
struction, taking and using such land and material as it
may require and be entitledto take under thischarter.
§ 12. That said company shallhave power to purchase
and hold any road in this State,or in any other State,
and may subscribe stock in,or aid in building any other
railroadin or out of thisState, and may consolidate itself
with any otherrailroadcompany in or out of thisState,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 377
upon such terms and conditions as may be agreed upon
between itand the other company or companies ,so as to
secure a continuous line under one management , from
two or more certain termini,or so as to secure branch
roads and feedersfor the main line, and may establish
and take a suitableand proper name ,to be approved of
by the consolidatingcompanies,and may leaseand oper-
ate any railroad or branches connecting with it or its
branches ,upon such terms as may be agreed upon , and
forthat purpose fullpower ishereby given said company
to make and execute any and allsuch contracts as may
be necessary to carry into effectany such agreements for
the purchase and sale or leasing of any such roads or
branches , or for the consolidating of such companies :
Provided, however, That it shall not be lawful for said
company to purchase or lease, or in any way acquire
control of any parallelor competing railroad,or to con-
solidatewith any railroadcompany owning or operating
any parallelor competing railroad.
§ 13. That said company is hereby authorized to bor-
row money to complete ,equip, furnish, operate , or main-
tain its said railroad, and to issue bonds , at a rate of
interestnot exceeding six per centum per annum ,with
such time to run as it may deem proper , and negotiate
and dispose of the same as it may deem proper ,and to
mortgage itscorporate property and franchisesto secure
the same ; but it shall not be lawful for said company to
issue,discount, or dispose of a greater amount of bonds
at their face value than five millions of dollars .
§14 . That copies from the minutes of proceedings of
the board of directors of said corporation, certifiedby
the secretary,and copies of the by -laws adopted by the
board , when so certified,shallbe received in alltribunals
in the State as evidence,as fullyand to the same extent Evidence.
as would be the books of the company .
$ 15. That it shall be lawful for said company ,by its
officersand agents,to receivesubscriptionsto itscapital
stock,payable in real estatesituatedin the State of Ken-
tucky,and take deed or deeds thereforin fee-simple,and

378 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
issue certificates of stock thereon ; and the real estate
thus acquired may be held,used,and conveyed by said
company as a naturalperson. But itis expresslypro-
vided that any conveyance of lands to said company , or
to any person in trustforsaidcompany ,or to any one for
the use and benefitof saidcompany or itscorporators,or
any of them ,in payment of any subscriptionto the cap-
italstock of said company ,or as a bonus or giftto induce
the building of a railroad,shalloperate only as an escrow
inthe hands of whomsoever it may be lodged untilsuch
time as a railroadshallactuallybe constructed under this
charterto saidlands,or to a point on the contemplated
line of said road nearest to said lands,and cars actually
run thereon; and shouldno road be actuallyconstructed
under thischarter to said lands, or contiguous thereto as
aforesaid, within ten years from the passage of thisbill,
then any such conveyance of such lands shall be and
become absolutelyvoid, anything in the conveyance to
the contrary notwithstanding ,and the grantee shallhave
no right to enter upon or take possession of said land
untilthe conditions aforesaid are complied with .
§16 . That it shall be lawful for said company to re-
ceive,lease,purchase ,or otherwise acquire,hold,use,and
enjoy allsuch real estateand personalpropertyas may
be convenient and desirable for constructing,operating,
orequipping said railroador itsbranches,and a lineof
telegraphalong the same ,or which may be conveyed to
it in payment of stock subscriptions, or which may be
purchased by it, and may bargain,sell,and convey the
same as a natural person .
§ 17. That said company may make contracts with any
corporation or person for constructing or equipping said
road and branches thereof,or any part of the same , and
pay therefor,in whole or in part,in the stock,bonds , or
other property of said company .
§ 18. That itshallbe lawful for the corporate authori-
tiesof any incorporatedcityor town through which said
railroad may be located to grant to said company the
right of way through said city or town to construct its

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 379
saidrailroadon,upon ,or under any publicstreetin such
cityor town ; such grantof the rightof way to be made
upon such terms and conditionsas may be agreed upon
between said company and the corporateauthoritiesof
such city or town .
§19 . That the board of directors and officers of said
company shallhave the power to establishsuch rates of
tollsforthe conveyance of persons and propertyon the
said railroadand itsbranches as it may deem proper ,not
however , to exceed the rates charged by other similar
roads for like services .
§20 . That the Legislatureof Kentucky , in granting
thischarter,reserves the rightto control and regulate the
rates of freight on the roads to be built under this char-
ter in common with the other railroadsin Kentucky ,and
to alter or amend this charter .
§ 21. That the said company shallbe, and hereby is,
authorized and empowered to build branch roads from its
main ,or to and from any pointon it,but no such branch
road shallexceed seventy-five miles in length, and in
the construction of said branches , and in maintaining
and operatingthe same , the company may , as to such
branches , have , exercise, and enforce any and all the
rights, privileges, powers, and provisionshereinbefore
granted , as touching the main line.
§ 22. That this act shall take effect and be in force
from and after its passage .
Approved April 11, 1882.

will; and itshall have allother lawful powers usual and

CHAPTER 948 .
AN ACT to amend an act to amend and reduce into one all previousacts
incorporatingthe town of Owenton , Owen county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That hereafter when a party to a civilsuitor other
proceeding, or any person charged with a violationof
any of the criminalorpenal laws of thisCommonwealth ,
or with having violatedany of the ordinancesor by-laws
of the town of Owenton ,Owen county,Kentucky ,shall
be brought for hearing or trialbefore the police judge of
LOC . L.,VOL . II- 25

386 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
said town of Owenton , or other officerof said town hay-
ing jurisdictionto try any of said enumerated class of
cases; and when any such party or person about to be
tried in said court shall make and file before said court or
judge his affidavit, in writing,that he believeshe cannot
have a fairand impartialtrialof such cause or prosecu-
tion before said judge or court, it shall be the duty of
said judge or court to immediately transfer such cause ,
proceeding ,or prosecution for trial,to the county judge
or some justice of the peace , or other officerof said
county having jurisdictionto try the same ; and shall
give to such judge or court any and allpapers,books,
and records, for the use of said judge or court for the

of such cause ; and any judge ,justice,or court,assuming
or acquiring jurisdictionof any cause or prosecution
under the provisions of this act,shallproceed to try the
same ,under the same rules,pains,and penalties, as are
now provided by law . And if any fines should be as-
sessed against any person tried under this act by any
judge or court fora violationof the ordinances or by -laws
of said town , such fine shall inure to, and be for the
benefit of, said town , and may be collectedin the same
way as now provided by the charter and by -laws of said .
town .
§ 2. That in allcivilcauses and penal prosecutions,or
prosecutions for the violation of the ordinances or by-
laws of said town , triableby the police judge thereof,or
which may have been transferred to the county judge or
justicesof the peace , or other court,under the provisions
of thisact,either party,if a civilsuit,or the defendant ,
if itbe a penal prosecution,or a prosecution for a viola-
tion of the ordinances or by -laws of said town , shall
have the rightto be tried by a jury when desired or de-
manded by him ,regardlessof the amount in controversy
in a civilsuit,or of the amount of fineor penaltyin a
penal prosecution or a prosecution for a violationof the
by -laws or ordinances of said town .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 387
§3 . That if any judge ,justice of the peace , or other
court having jurisdictionto try any such cause so trans-
ferredshall, without good cause ,refuse to take jurisdic-
tion of and try any such cause so transferredto such
court under the provisions of this act, or if the police
judge of said town or other officerhaving jurisdictionto
try any cause or prosecution under the provisions of this
act,and under the charter of said town to which thisis
an amendment , shallfailor refuseto comply with the
provisionsof this act, he shall, upon indictmentby a

victionthereof,be finedin any sum not lessthan twenty-
five dollars nor more than fiftydollars,said fine to be
collected in the same manner as now provided by law ,
which fine,when collected, shallbe paid to the trusteeof
the jury fund of said county , and by him applied to the

§4 . That all acts and parts of acts heretofore passed

tees of said town the right, or which might be construed

§ 5. That the police court of Owenton shallhereafter

Approved April 11, 1882.

392 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 950 .
AN ACT to authorizethe county courtof Henderson county to issuebond
for the purpose of purchasinggravel roads and constructingof new gravel
roads .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the county court of Henderson county is
hereby authorizedand empowered ,the presidingjudge
and a majorityof the justicesof the peace being present
and concurring therein, to submit to the qualifiedvoters
thereof, at the next or any ensuing general election,the
proposition to issue bonds of said county ,in any sum not
exceeding one hundred thousand dollars,to raise funds
for the purchase of gravel roads already built in said
county , and to build others.
§2 . Before any such electionshallbe held,thirtydays'
notice thereof shall be published in the several news-
papers of said county ; and if a majority of allthe legal
votes cast be in favor of such proposition,then itshallbe
lawful for said county ,the presidingjudge and a majority
of the justicesof the peace being present and concurring,
to issue bonds as aforesaid, to-wit : in any amount not
exceeding one hundred thousand dollars, payable in not
exceeding thirtyyears from theirdate, but to be redeem-
able, at the option of the county ,in five years; in size
not larger than five hundred and not less than one hun-
dred dollarseach , as they may deem proper , and to bear
interestat any rate,not exceeding six per cent. per an-
num , payable semi -annually .
§3 . The presiding judge of the court shall sign the
bonds , and the clerk shall countersign them , and prefix
the seal of the court. Coupons signed by the clerk for
said interest shall be attached to the bonds .
§4. The courtshallhave power todesignatesome suit-
able person to superintend and control the bonds , sell
them under such rulesas may be prescribed,receiveany
money obtained thereon, hold and disbursethe same
under the order of the court, and , whenever required,
make a report of his acts to the court.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 393
§ 5. The court shall require the person so designated
and appointed to give bond with good securityfor the
safe-keeping of said money , and the faithfuldischarge of
allhis dutiesin relationto said bonds. The presiding
judge shallcause a record to be made of allbonds that
may be issued under this act, showing the amount and
date thereof .
§ 6. The said bonds shall be made payable to bearer,
and at such places as the court may direct, and the pro-
ceeds thereof shallbe appropriated and used only forthe
purpose of purchasing gravel roads in Henderson county ,
for the maintenance , extension of same , and for the con-
structionand building of such other gravel roads in said
county as said court may deem necessary and expedient .
§7 . Such roads, when purchased or built by said
county ,except as hereinafterdirected,shall be governed
and conducted under such rules and regulations as to
toll, & c., as is now prescribed by law regulating the
same , and the proceeds and revenues from the tolls of
said roads shallbe appropriated exclusivelyby said court
to the maintenance of said roads, and for the payment of
the intereston said bonds as it fallsdue ,and from any
surplus revenue derived therefrom, shall provide a sink-
ing fund to pay offand liquidatethe bonds at maturity.
§8. Should a majorityof thepeople of the county vote
againstsaidproposition,itshallbe the duty of the pre-
sidingjudge to submit said questionat any subsequent
general election,upon the petitionof thirtyor more of
the tax-payers of said county ; but no election shall be
held until notice by publication shall have been given ,
as provided in section second of thisact.
§9 . The county court,the presidingjudge,and a ma-
jorityof the justicesof the peace being present and con-
curring, may ,in their discretion, reduce the tollon the
roads so purchased or built under this act, if the tolls
now fixedby law shallprove more than sufficientto pay
the interest on the bonds issued .
§ 10. The said court is authorized and empowered ,
at any time , whenever in its discretion, it may deem

394 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
itnecessary and expedient,to levy and cause to be col-
lected, as other county revenues are now collected, an
ad valorem tax of not exceeding twenty -fivecents on the
one hundred dollar'sworth of property subject to taxa-
tion forcounty purposes ,which said amount so collected,
togetherwith thetollsof saidroads as may be purchased
or built, shall be used for the purpose of paying off the
interestand principalof saidbonds.
§ 11. The county court shallappoint some suitableand
discreet person to superintend and manage said roads,
receive, collect, and account for the tolls, hold and dis-
burse the same under the order of said court , and when-
ever required, make a report of his acts to said court.
Said person so appointed as such superintendent shall
give bond , with good security, for the safe-keeping of
said money ,and for the faithfuldischarge of allhis duties
in relation to said roads as superintendent thereof, and
may be removed at any time under the orders of said
court when ,in their discretion, they may think proper so
to do .
§ 12. Such person so appointed shallreceive a reason-
able compensation as such superintendent,to be paid out
of the revenues and tolls of said roads .
§ 13. This act shalltake effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 11, 1882 .

CHAPTER 951 .
AN ACT to authorizethe sale of the Newtown and Leesburg Turnpike
Road .
WHEREAS ,It is represented that James Sudduth made
himself liable,while acting as a directorand president of
the Newtown and Leesburg Turnpike Road Company ,for
the debts of said road due for its construction , and that
he paid the same , and that at the May term , 1868,of the
Harrison circuit court, in an action wherein he was
plaintiffand said company was defendant ,he recovered
a judgment against said road for the sum of three thou-
sand nine hundred and sixty-five dollars and sixty-one

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 395
cents ($3,965.61),with interestfrom the 6thday of May ,
1867 ,at six per cent, per annum , and that an order was
made at the November term , 1868,of said court in said
case ,placing said road in the hands of a receiver,who
was directedto collectthe tollsof said road,keep itin
repair, and pay the net earnings of said road on the debt
aforesaid ; and whereas , said receiver has made annual
reports to said court of the sums collected and paid out
by him ; and whereas ,it is represented that the aggre-
gate sum paid on the debt aforesaidis only one thousand
fivehundred and seventy-sixdollarsand forty-nine cents
($ 1,576.49),said sum not being one half the intereston
said debt for said time ; and whereas , it is represented
that itis expensive to keep said case in court and pay a
receiver'ssalary; and whereas ,itisrepresented that said
judgment isthe only debt against said road , and that the
stockholders of said road have not held an election for
officersof said company for eight years or more ; there-
fore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . The owner of the judgment aforesaidhaving given
notice to the stockholdersof said company of hisintended
applicationforat leastsixty(60) days,by publicationof
a notice in three newspapers ,one of which shallbe pub-
lished in each of the counties of Scott, Bourbon , and
Harrison ,may make application in the court and action
aforesaid for the sale of the property and franchises of
said company . Said applicationshallbe by amended
petition in said case,which may be filedin the clerk's
officeof said court at any time , and it shallbe the duty
of the applicant to make the persons defendants to said
amended petition who were ,so far as he can ascertain,
lastelected president and directorsfor said company ,and
to have them served with summons for at leastten days
before the firstday of the term of said court at which he
expects to apply for a sale of said road .
§2 . Any one or more of the officersor stockholders of
said company may contest said application, and if, from

396 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
1
thepleadingsand proof,on a hearingof saidapplication,
the court shall determine that said corporation has not
preserveditsorganizationby the regularelectionof offi-
cers,and that the tollsof said road are not sufficientto
pay the debt and interestaforesaid, and that said sale
will not be injuriousto the stockholdersor the public,and
that said sale, if made , will be fair, just, and equitable
to said Ferguson and the various stockholders in said
road , itshallhave jurisdictionand power to order a sale
of the propertyand franchisesof said road forthe pur-
pose of paying said debt,and for the distributionof the
surplus proceeds ,if any there be ,among the stockholders
pro rata .
§3 . Said sale shall be made at such time and place ,
and on such terms , as the court may determine will best
advance the interestof allconcerned ; but the time ,place,
and terms of sale shall be advertised for at leasttwenty
days by publication in three newspapers ,one of which
shallbe published in each of the counties named in the
first section of this act .
§ 4. The purchaser of said road shall be entitledto all
of the rights,privileges, franchises, and property,secured
to said company by itscharter, or acquired by itby con-
tract or condemnation , and he shall be subject to allthe
restrictions, limitations, and liabilitiesimposed by the
charter or the general laws of the State relativeto turn-
pike roads or companies ,and fora violationthereof shall
be subject to allthe pains and penaltiesnow provided by
law for the government of turnpike companies .
§ 5. The proeeeds arisingfrom said sale shall,so far as
necessaryforthatpurpose,be appliedto the satisfaction
of the debt and interest aforesaid , and the costs of mak-
ing the sale,and iftherebe any surplus,itshallbe dis-
tributedamong the stockholderspro rata.
§ 6. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April11, 1882.

CHAPTER 952 .
AN ACT to amend an act,entitled"An act to empower the county courtof
Grant county to make subscriptionsto the capitalstock of turnpike roads
in saidcounty," approved May 5, 1880 .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That chapter 1461 of the Acts of 1879 , entitled
" An act to empower the county court of Grant county
to make subscriptionsto the capital stock of turnpike
roads in said county ," approved May 5, 1880,be ,and the
same ishereby ,so amended as to authorize the said court
to subscribenot exceedingseven hundred and fifty($750)
dollars to each mile of turnpike hereafter built in said
county .
§2 .That sectionsix(6) of saidactbe soamended as
to require the county judge of said county to submit the
question of subscribingto the capital stock of said turn-
pike roads to the qualifiedvoters of said county , at any
time ,on the written petitionof twenty legalvoters of said
county requesting him so to do , which order submitting
said question shallbe spread upon the order-book of the
Grant county court at its next regular term ,which said
election shallbe held, and the result thereof ascertained
and declared,in the manner provided in the act to which
thisis an amendment : Provided,Said question shall not
be submitted oftener than once in twelve months : And
provided further, That said tax may be levied in any pre-
cinct in which at such election a majority of the legal
votes cast are in favor of such levy, though it failin the
county at large.
§ 3. That sectionnine of said act ishereby repealed.
§ 4. This act to take effectand be in force from its
passage .
Approved April 11, 1882.

CHAPTER 955 .

adjoiningNewport ,Dayton , and Belleview, in Campbell county,to attend
common schoolsin saidci'yand towns .

lic schools in the respective city and towns to which
