CHAPTER 957 .
AN ACT to charter the Altamont Coal Company .
WHEREAS ,There are valuable depositsof coal,iron, and
other minerals and substances, and timbers in Laurel
county, Kentucky , in which the people of that county
and other places have large and valuable interests, which
isof great moment to the public generally; and whereas ,
in order to enable said persons to carry the same to
market , thereby insuring a great public benefit,itis nec-
essary that certain railways, tram -ways , and other im-
provements should be constructed, to the use of which
the general public are to have free access under the stip-
ulations of this act; therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That C. Croake , W. M. Howard , G. D. Brownlie ,
and C. S. Neeld ,their associates,successors, and assigns,
be ,and they are hereby ,created and constituteda body-
corporate,by the name and styleof the "Altamont Coal
Company ," and by thatname and styleshallhave perpet-
ual succession, with power to contract and be contracted
with; to own property, real and personal; to sue and be
sued ;tohave a common seal,and to alterthe same at pleas-
ure;to engage in mining forcoal,iron,and other minerals,
and to build any railroads,tram -roads,or other roads from
the said coal,timber ,or mineral region,or from any point
insame they may desire,to the Knoxvillebranch of the
Louisvilleand NashvilleRailroad,and tohave allpower
necessaryforthe successfulmining and transportingof
the said minerals, and the cutting or sawing and trans-
porting of said timber ,and the prosecution, speciallyand
LOC . L., VOL . H- 26

402 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
generally,of such business,and for the execution of the
power herein granted .
§2 . That said company shall have the power to ac-
quire and hold such real estateby lease,fee-simple,or
otherwise, as it may deem necessary or expedient for its
business. It shall have power to locate, construct, and
operatea railroador branch railroad,tram roads,plank
roads, or any other road ,to or from any of the mines ,
works ,coal, minerals, and timber lands aforesaid, to the
said railroad; and may build bridges when and wherever
necessary for the successfulprosecution of itsbusiness;
and may acquire for said road or roads, and bridges,the
land, timber, and rock necessary for the location and
construction of same by agreement with the owners
thereof; or in default of such agreement ,then the presi-
dent and directorsshall proceed as provided in the Gen-
eral Statutes in cases of turnpike, gravel, and plank
road , chapter 110 .
§3 . Any and all persons shallhave the rightto trans-
port coal, iron,or any other mineral and timber over said
road or roads,bridge or bridges, when completed ; and it
shall be the duty of said company to receive and trans-
port the same ,and for thisit shallnot charge forcoal
more than one quarterof one cent per bushel per mile,
or fraction thereof ; for iron or other ores , not exceeding
twelve and one half cents per ton per mile,or fraction
thereof; forlumber or timber , not exceeding twenty -five
(25) cents per one thousand feet per mile, or fraction
thereof .
§ 4. The capitalstock of said company shallnot ex-
ceed two hundred thousand dollars, to be divided into
shares of one hundred dollarseach,which shallbe per-
sonal property,and indorsedand transferredas may be
prescribedby the by-laws of saidcompany . Books may
be opened for the subscription of stock by any of the
incorporators, but no subscriptionshallbe entered on
thebooks of the company without the consentof a ma-
jority of the incorporators.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 403
§5 . When stock to amount of five thousand dollars
shallhave been subscribed, the incorporators may calla
meeting of the subscribers, at which a president and five
directors shall be elected to serve for two years; and
beforethe expirationof theirterm of service,thepresi-
dent shall give notice,in writing,to the stockholders of
the time and place of their meeting to electsuccessors,
at least five days next before the day of meeting ; and
said president and directorsso elected shall hold their
offices,severally, until their successors are elected. The
said board of directorsmay prescribethe terms of sub-
scription, the time and manner of payment , and how
and when the same may be forfeited; and they may adopt
such by laws as to collectionof stock subscription,and
all other matters pertaining to the business of the com-
pany ,as may be necessary; and the saidboard of direct-
ors shall have the general management of allthe affairs
of said company ,and may exercise allthe power hereby
granted to the corporation.
§6 . They may borrow or obtain by loan such sums of
money ,and on such terms ,as they may deem expedient ,
and may mortgage any or all the property of the com-
pany to secureitspayment : Provided, A majorityof the
stockholders agree thereto They may elect or appoint
a secretary,treasurer, and agents, as they may think nec-
essary, and may fixand pay their salariesand the salary
of the president. They may fillany vacancies occurring
in the board between the elections,as provided above .
§7 . Each share of stock shall entitle the holder to one
vote, which may be voted by himself in person or by
written proxy .
§ 8. All contractsin writing, signed by the president in
the name of the company , shall be binding on the com-
pany . The individual stockholdersshallnot be liablefor
the debts of the company .
§9 . When ten thousand dollars shall have been sub-
scribed,and such portions paid up as the directorsmay
require, the company shall be deemed fully organized ,
and may proceed with itsbusiness,

404 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 10. There shall be no spirituous, vinous , malt , or
intoxicating liquors sold within a distance of two miles
from the store,office,or tipper of the said company ,un-
der a penalty of five hundred dollarsfor each and every
offense .
§ 11. This act takes effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882.

itorsof the same ,and shall be prosecuted to judgment

the amount thereof, from the time of such failure, re-

erty, funds, and prudentialconcerns of said institution

408 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
firstMonday of the succeeding month ,and said election

ings at leastonce a year ,and calledmeetings may be had

whenever deemed necessary. All questionsbefore the

sidered when a lessnumber ispresent than when the vote

rectly,to engage in,or carryon,any otherbusinessthan
that of said institution, without the specialleave of the

§14 . The president and directorsshall keep a record
of theirproceedings, which they shallproduce to the

meetings .

officesunder thischarter,or which may be requiredof
them by the by-laws , rules and regulations, of the cor-
poration.
§ 16. This institutionshall not contract for or receive
a greaterrate of interestthan at the legal rate per annum
forthe loan or use of money ; and intereston promissory
notes,negotiable and payable at the same ,and there dis-
counted , shall be calculated on the true time such notes
have to run .
§ 17. The president and directors shall issue certifi-
cates of stock to the holders thereof for so much as shall
be paid for; and the shares of the capital stock shall be
considered and held in law as personal property, and
assignable and transferable only in such manner and at
such place as the presidentand directorsshall,by their
by laws,prescribe. Certificatesof deposit shallbe oblig-
atory on said institution, and shall be transferable or
assignable when made payable to order.
§ 18. The general meeting of the stockholdersshall be
held annually on the second Monday in January in each
year ,at the time of the annual election,to which meet-
ing the president and directorsshallpresent an accurate
statement of the condition and affairsof the institution;
and general meetings of the stockholders may be called,
as providedin thischarter,or by the presidentand di-
rectors when they deem it desirable, or by any number
of stockholdersthe by -laws shallrequire,
§19 . That the persons above named are hereby con-
stituted commissioners to open books and receive sub .
scriptionsfor the capital stock of said institution, and to
superintend the election of the firstboard of directors,
any three of whom shall be competent to exercise the
powers and perform the dutiesrequiredby thissection.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 411
§20 . The said commissioners shall have power , and
they are hereby authorized and required, on the second
Monday in March next,or at such other time within one
year thereafteras they shalldeem expedient, having
given not less than thirty days ' notice thereof in some
newspaper for the subscriptionof the capitalstock of
said institution, and cause books to be kept open from
10 o'clock A. M. until 2 o'clock P. M. , for at least ten
days , or untilat leastone hundred shares shallhave been
subscribed, when the same may be closed; and if more
than two thousand shares shall have been subscribed by
individuals, companies , and corporations, the commis-
sionersshalldeduct the excess from the largest subscrip-
tion in such manner that no subscriptionshallbe reduced ,
and leave the subscriptionof another larger.
§ 21. That if the whole two thousand shares of capital
stock shall not be taken when the books shall first be
opened ,the president and directorsmay cause the books
to be opened at any time and place they may direct,
givingthirtydays' noticethereofin some newspaper ,and
cause them to be kept open not less than ten days , or
until the whole of the balance of the stock shall have
been taken ; and the president and directorsmay require
such premium on the stock sold at the re-opening of the
books as they shall deem right, and such premium shall
be thepropertyof the institution.
§22 . That when not less than one hundred shares of
the capitalstockshallhave been taken,itshallbe their
duty to give notice in some public newspaper , and ap-
`point a day for the electionof the firstboard of directors,
who shall hold their officesuntil the succeeding annual
election,and not lessthan thirtynor more than sixtydays '
notice shall be given of the time and place of electing
said board ; and at least three of said incorporators shall
act as inspectorsof said election.
§ 23. That the payment of the shares of the capital
stock held by individuals, companies , and corporations
shall be made at the time and in the manner following ,
viz : Five dollars on each share to the commissioners at

412 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the time of subscribing , and five dollars on each share
within ten days after the election of the firstboard of
directors, and five dollars every sixty days thereafter,
untilthe whole amount of said stockispaid: Provided,
That the board shallhave power to prolong the time for
the payment of each installment after one half of the
amount of each share shallhave been paid .
§ 24. Should any of the subscribersto the stock of said
institutionfailor refuse to pay for their stock, as herein
provided ,the president and directors,firstgiving public
notice in two or more newspapers for the space of thirty
days, by resolutionentered on the records, may forfeit
such stock, and proceed ,at such time as they may deem
expedient,tore-sellthe same ; and allpartialpayments
made on any stock which shall be forfeitedshall be so
sold for the benefit of such stockholder .
§ 25. That so soon as ten thousand dollarsof the capi-
tal stock shallhave been subscribed by individuals,com-
panies ,or corporations, as heretofore required,the presi-
dent and directors shall cause some suitable person to
count the money paid in,and to certifythe same to the
president and directors; and when he shall so certify,
and upon such appearing to be the fact, the said institu-
tion isthen hereby authorized to commence business.
§26 . This act shallbe in force from itspassage : Pro-
vided, An organization is effectedwithin twelve months
from its approval by the Governor ; but if no organiza-
tion is effectedwithin the above specifiedtime,thisact
shallbe void. The Legislature reservesthe rightto alter
and amend thisact at pleasure.
Approved April 15, 1882 .

CHAPTER 965 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleof spirituous,vinous, or malt liquors, or the
mixture thereof, in Spring Lick and Caneyville precincts, in Grayson
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be unlawful for any person to sell
or vend any spirituous, vinous , or malt liquors, or the
mixture thereof, in any quantity, within the limitsof
Spring Lick precinctand Caneyvilleprecinct,in Gray-
son county .
§ 2. Itshallbe unlawfulfor the county courtof Gray-
son county , or the trustees of any incorporated town
within either of said precincts,atter the ratificationof
thisact as hereinprovided,to grant or issueany license.
for the sale of spirituous,vinous,or malt liquorswithin

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 417
the territoryabove named . Any license so issued shall
be void .
§3 . That if any person shall violate the provisions of
the firstsection of this act,such person , on conviction
thereof, shallfor each offensebe subject to a fine of not
lessthan twenty-fivedollarsnor more than one hundred
dollars, to be recovered either in the circuit court of
Grayson county, on indictment of a grand jury, or by
warrant before any justice of the peace for Grayson
county ,or other court of competent jurisdiction.
§ 4. This act shallnot be in force in eitherof said pre-
cincts until ratifiedby a majority of the qualifiedvoters
who may vote therein; and forthe purpose of takingthe
senseof thevotersin saidprecincts,a pollshallbe opened
by the officersof election in each of said precincts,who
are appointed by the county court of Grayson county to
hold the regular election on the firstMonday in August ,
1882. In one column of said poll shall be entered the
names of those in favor of the sale of spirituous,vinous ,
and malt liquors,and in the other column shallbe entered
the names of those who are opposed to the sale of spir-
ituous,vinous ,and malt liquors. Those who vote against
the sale of spirituous,vinous ,and malt liquors shall be
construed and counted as in favor of the provisions of
this act . The officers who hold said elections shall cer-
tifythe resultin connection with theirreturns on the
the poll-book as to the other election; and itshall be the
duty of the county judge ,sheriff, and clerk of the county
court,or any two of them ,to compare the vote in each
of said precincts; and ifitappears that a majority of the
qualifiedvoters voting therein,or in eitherof them ,at said
election,are opposed to the sale of spirituous,vinous ,or
malt liquors,then they shallmake a certificateof the fact,
which shall be entered of record on the order -book of
the Grayson county court. If a majority of the voters
shallbe againstthe sale of spirituous, vinous,and malt
liquorsin both of said precincts,then this act shallbe in
forcein both precincts; but ifa majorityof thosevoting
shall only be against the sale of said liquors in one of

said precincts,then as to the precinct so voting this act
shall be in full force. The qualificationof voters shall
be the same as voters at general elections under the laws

Approved April 15 , 1882 .

CHAPTER 979 .
AN ACT toprovidefortheconstructionby thecitiesofCovington,West
Covington ,and Ludlow ,of the River Road in frontof said cities.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the road known as the River Road ,extending
from the cityof Covington ,along the riverfront of West
Covington , to the city of Ludlow , shall be constructed
and put in good order and condition at the expense of
said three cities. The termini of said road shall be West-
ern Row in Covington ,and the Cincinnati Southern Rail-
way bridgein Ludlow .
§2 . That the citycouncil of Covington shall appoint
threecommissioners,and the councilsof West Covington

424 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and Ludlow shall appoint one commissioner each , and
the five so appointed shallconstitutea board whose duty
itshallbe to advertise for sealed proposals to put said
road in thorough repair as a macadamized road ,to such
width and depth of metal , and with such other specifica-
tions, as may be fixed by said board , not to exceed in
width the original width of said road , and to award a
contract therefor to the lowest and best bidder, taking
from him bond with good suretyfor the performance of
hiscontract; and forany breach of saidcontractthe said
commissioners may sue in the name of " The Commis-
sionersof the River Road of Kenton County ."
§3 . The said commissioners ,for the purpose of paying
for said improvement , may make requisitionupon the
councilsof Covington,West Covington, and Ludlow for
theamount necessaryto make such payment ,distributing
the amount required according to the assessed value of
the taxable property in said citiesrespectively,and there-
upon it shall be the duty of the respective councils of
said three citiesto provide for the payment of the sums
so requiredto the contractorwho shall perform said
work . The said commissioners shallhave power to pur-
chase or condemn land necessary to widen said road to
sixty-six feet,such condemnation to be conducted in like
manner with condemnation for railway or turnpike pur-
poses. The said commissioners may employ a civilen-
gineer to plan and supervise said work , and may make
requisitionsfor his pay in like manner with theirrequisi-
tions for pay of the contractor.
§4 . Said commissioners shall receive from their cities
respectivelysuch compensation as shall be fixed by the
councils appointing them respectively; but the same shall
be fixedbefore appointment ,and shallnot be paid until
aftersaid work iscompleted .
§5 . Said commissioners may contractwith any duly
authorized street railway company to give the right of
way over said road for a streetrailway , on such terms as
may be agreed on .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 425
§ 6. After the completion of said road ,so much as nec-
essary of the taxes derived by said citiesfrom real estate
abutting on said road shall be applied to keeping said
road in repair .

Approved April 15, 1882 .

eral law ,except as herein otherwise provided .

426 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 3. Said trustees shall have power to contract for a

years in said district,to be collectedby the sheriffof said
county as other taxes are collected, and the proceeds
applied by the trustees to the furnishing,repairing, and
improvement of such grounds and buildings as the trus-
tees may obtain,by lease or other contract,to be used for
school purposes .
§4 . Said trustees are hereby empowered to receive
and invest any money heretofore collected by taxation
for the purpose of erecting a common school-house in
saiddistrict,and they shallapply the income of any such
fund towards the payment of teachers in said district.
They may also receive any bequest or donation hereto-
foremade ,or which may hereafterbe made forthebene-
fitof saiddistrict,and apply the same as directedby the
donor or devisor,and in the absence of such direction, in
such manner as in theirdiscretionwill best promote the
interest of the districtschool. They may employ any
number of teachers that may be necessary to properly
conduct the school in said district, and the amount that
may be due said districtin each school year ,from the
common school fund of the State, shall be paid upon the
order of the board of trustees to their treasurer , and be
by him applied to the payment of teachers, as the board
may direct. But no teacher shallbe employed by said
trusteeswho is not qualifiedas provided by the general
law .
§5 . Said trusteesmay ,by an order entered upon their
records, submit to the qualifiedwhite voters of said school
districtat the August election, 1882, and not oftener than
once in each year thereafter, at a regular election,the
propositionto vote a tax in such sum as the trusteesmay
direct, not exceeding twenty -five cents on each one hun-
dred dollars' worth of taxable property in said school

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 427
districtbelonging to white persons,to be applied to the
payment of teachers in the districtschool. Such election
shallbe heldby the regularofficersof electionunder the
same rulesprovided by law for other elections; and if, at
any electionheld forthat purpose,as herein provided,a
majority of the legallyqualifiedwhite voters in said dis-
trictshallbe cast in favor of such tax,itshallbe the duty
of the county judge of said county, upon presentation
to him of a certificate to that effect from the officers of
election,to enterupon the order-book of the county court
an orderimposing such tax as may be voted. Said tax
shallbe collectedby the sheriffof said county as other
taxesare directedby law to be collected,and paid to the
treasurer of the board of trustees .
§ 6. For all the purposes of this act said trustees are
created a body -politicand corporate,and they may sue
and be sued ,defend and be defended , in any court of this
Commonwealth , by the name of The Trustees of Com-
mon School DistrictNo. 25,in Oldham county .
§7 . No provision of thisact shallbe construed so as to
affectin anywise the general law in relation to schools
for colored childrenin said district,nor shall any colored
person be subjectto any tax or receiveany benefitcon-
templated by thisact.
§ 8. This act shall take effect from and after the 15th
day of June , 1882.
Approved April 15 , 1882 .
CHAPTER 981 ..
AN ACT to empower the Carter county court to authorizeAndrew Brown ,
or he and his associates,to constructa tram -way upon partsof the public
roads in saidcounty .
WHEREAS , Andrew Brown contemplates building a tram
railroadfrom Olive Hill Station, on the Elizabethtown ,
Lexington , and Big Sandy Railroad , to Cave Branch ,
Buffalo,and Smith'sCreek,in Cartercounty,theroute of
which has,in part,been selected,measured,and marked ;
and whereas ,a portion of said route runs on and with
the county road ; and thisbeing the only practicableway ,

428 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the citizensconsenting have petitioned the county court
to grant the use of such portions of county road as are
necessaryor practicableforthe purpose of buildingsaid
tram -way ; now ,therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county courtof Cartercounty is author-
ized to grant , with the consent of the owners of the land
over which the county road passes,to Andrew Brown , or

struct, and use, upon such parts of the county road as

CHAPTER 983 .
AN ACT to empower the county court of Butlercounty to dispose of the
delinquent listby saleor otherwise.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly ofthe Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. The county court of Butler county,a majority of
the justicesagreeing,may , by order thereof, dispose of
the listof delinquent tax -payers of county revenue by
re-enlistingthem with the sheriffor other collectingofficer
forcollection; or they may receivea part of any delin-
quent taxesin fullsatisfactionof suchtaxes; or theymay
sellsuch list,or part or parts of list,at such times as they
may deem best,to the highest bidder,cash in hand : Pro-
vided,however, That if such sale be ordered,the listmay

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 429
by written or printed notices in at least three public

CHAPTER 984 .
AN ACT to provide for the improvement of the public roads in Hart
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshall be the duty of the judge of the Hart
county court to appoint a county commissioner of roads
for said county, whose duty it shallbe to see that the
roads of thecountyareproperlyworked and kept in good
condition .
§2 . He shall visiteach section of road in the county
within one month after his appointment , and shallgive
such directionto the surveyor of section in regard to the
working of said sections as may , in his judgment , be
necessary to put the road in good condition, and shall
directthepropermodes of drainageand culverts. Drain-
age may be made by trenching, and culvertsmay be
made of plank ,wood ,or stone, as he may direct,and said
directionshallbe specifiedin each case.
§3. He shallsecureas a compensation forhisservices
one dollarperday foreach day whileactuallyengaged
in the duties of his office; but he shall not be allowed
pay formore than one hundred and twenty (120) days in
any year,and shallhold his officefortwo years,unless
forgood and sufficientcause he shouldbe removed by the
judge of the county court,in which court the judge of
the county courtshallappoint a successor.

430 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 4. He shall, before enteringupon the dutiesof his
office, enter into bond with the county court in the sum
of fivehundred dollars,with good security,to faithfully
discharge the duties of his office, and shallimmediately ,
afterbeing qualified,enter upon the dutiesof his office.
He shall, at his earliestconvenience ,visitand give each
surveyor of roads in Hart county specificdirections as
to the mode of working his said section of road .
§ 5. If any road surveyorin the county of Hart should
failor refuse to work his section of road , as prescribed
by said commissioner of roads,the said commissioner of
roads shallpresent said overseer to the grand jury of said
county at its next term of circuitcourt,and he shallbe
fined for each offense not less than twenty -five dollars,
nor more than fiftydollars,for such failure.
§ 6. Surveyor or overseers of roads shallgive,in writ-
ing,to each hand within the road -working age on his
section of road ,a notice of the time and place of the
working of said road ; and on a failure to attend said
road -working ,in person or by proxy , with the necessary
implements for working said roads, shall be fined as pro-
vided in the General Statutes for a failure to work roads .
Each hand shall be required to work faithfullyeight
hours for each day , but no hand shallbe requiredto
work more than two days in any one week .
§7 . Said overseers of roads,each ,shall hold his office
for two years, and shalleach be entitledto a compensa-
tion of one dollar per day for each day said overseer of
roads is engaged in notifyinghands , and in superintend-
ing the working of his said section of road ,to be paid
out of the county levy of said county . Said amount
shallnot be paid until said overseer shall have made his
settlement with the judge of the county court,and shall
have accounted forany toolsand implements belonging
to the county for working the roads : Provided, That he
shallnot be entitledto pay for giving notices but one day
for each road -working .
§ 8. It shall be lawful for each surveyor of roads to
impressinto use wagons ,plows,and teams of hishands,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 431
forhauling and plowing upon said roads,and for each
day said two -horse wagon and team , or two -horse plow
and team shall be so used or worked ,said hand owning
said team and wagon or team and plow shallbe allowed
forsaid team and wagon or plow one dollarand fifty
cents ($ 1.50) for each day said team and wagon or plow
shallbe worked ,to be paid out of the county levy, each
day being recorded at eight hours .
§ 9. It shall be the duty of the judge of the Hart
county courtto cause to be purchased,forthe use of said
county roads, three sets of road-working implements,
consistingof 3 sledges,5 picks,3 rock augers or drills,
6 spades,6 shovels,and 3 crowbars, for the purpose of
working on said roads; and he shall, upon application,
let any surveyor of road ,when not in use,have one set
of said toolsfor road-working ,but he shallnot be allowed
to have said tools longer than four weeks at any one
time ,and he shall then return said tools to said judge .
The judge of the Hart county court shall in no case
permit the overseers of roads to have possession of said
implements without firsttaking a receipt therefor; and
upon a failure to return said tools within one month
thereafter,the said judge of the Hart county court shall
proceed against the overseer by warrant , as in other civil
cases,forthe recovery of similaramounts .
§ 10. The overseers of roads shall not be entitled to
compensation for servicesuntil they have accounted for
thepropertyin theirpossessionbelongingto the county.
§ 11. All acts or parts of acts for working the roads of
Hart county in conflictwith this action hereby repealed .
§ 12. This act shall take effect and be in force from its
passage . 1
Approved April 15,1882.
CHAPTER 985 .
AN ACT for the benefitof the Swiss colony in Bernstadt, Laurel county .
WHEREAS , A censusof the pupilchildren,now fifty-five
(55) in number ,of theSwiss colony at Bernstadt,Laurel

432 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
county , could not be taken in time to enable the com-
missioner of common schools to lay off a special district
for theirbenefit; and whereas ,the said children are not
sufficientlyacquainted with the English language to be
profited by attending the neighboring districtschools;
therefore ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the commissioners of common schoolsishere-
by authorized to lay off,under the directionof the Super-
intendent of Public Insrtuction, a special school district
in said county ,embracing all the pupil children of said
colony , and to appoint a board of trusteesforsaid district.
§2 . The course of instruction for the common school
of said districtshall be the same as that prescribed for
the common schools of the State,but may be conducted
through the media of the French and German languages :
Provided, That the English language be daily and duly
taughtto everypupil of the school; but nothing in this
act shall be construed to authorize the conduct of any
other common school in the Commonwealth except
through the exclusive medium of the English language .
§3 . The text-books for said school shall be prescribed
by the State Board of Education , and the teacher of said
school must duly obtain a county certificateor a State
certificatebefore entering upon the discharge of his
duties .
§4 . That when said school shall have been taught ac-
cording to law and the provisions of this act,the com-
missionersshallduly report the same tothe Superintendent
of Public Instruction, as every common school is now re-
ported, for approval and for payment .
§ 5. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage,and be
in force until the regular meeting of the next General
Assembly .
Approved April 15 , 1882 .

CHAPTER 988 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Kentucky and IndianaCar TrustCompany .

of Kentucky :
§1 . That there ishereby created a body -corporate and
politic,to be known as the " Kentucky and Indiana Car
Trust Company .'
§2 . That said corporation shall have the power and
capacity to contract and be contracted with, sue and be
sued,and to have and use a common seal,and alterthe
same at pleasure; and shallbe indued with allthe powers
and authoritiesincident to corporations generally.

erty or assets belonging to said corporation; and may

CHAPTER 990 .
AN ACT tochartertheWhitleyCoalCompany .
WHEREAS , There are valuable deposits of coal, iron,
and other minerals and substances , and the finest of
timbers,along and on the waters of the Clear Fork of

436 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the Cumberland river, in Whitley county, Kentucky , in
which the people of that county and other places have
large and valuable interest, which is of great moment
to the public generally; and whereas ,in order to enable
saidpersons to carrythe same to market, therebyinsur-
ing a great public benefit, it is necessary that certain
railways,tram -ways ,and other improvements should be
constructed,to the use of which the general public are to
have freeaccessunder the stipulationsof thisact; there.
fore ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
J
§1 . That C. Crooke , J. B. Speed , J. R. Crooke , Geo .
W. Calbert, W. P. Crooke , C. S. Nield, and J. P. Byrne ,
their associates, successors, and assigns,be , and they are
hereby,createdand constituteda body -corporate,by the
name and style of the Whitley Coal Company ," and
by that name and styleshallhave perpetual succession,
with power to contract and be contracted with,to own
property, real and personal,to sue and be sued ,to have
a common seal, and to alter the same at pleasure; to
engage in mining for coal,iron, and other minerals ,'and
to build any railroads, tram -roads,or other roads,from
the said coal, timber , or mineral region, or from any
point in same they may desire, to the Clear Fork river,
or to the Knoxville branch of the Louisville and Nash-
villeRailroad,and to have allpower necessaryfor the
successful mining and transporting of the said miner-
als,and the cutting or sawing and transporting of said
timber,and the prosecution, speciallyand generally, of
such business,and for the execution of the power herein
granted .
§2 . That said company shall have the power to ac-
quire and hold such real estate by lease,fee-simple, or
otherwise, as it may deem necessary or expedient for its
business. It shall have power to locate,construct, and
operate a railroad or branch railroads,tram -roads,plank
roads, or any other road,to or from any of the mines,
works ,coal, mineral , and timber lands aforesaid, to the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 437
said riveror to the said railroad; and may build bridges
when and wherever necessaryforthe successfulprose-
cution of itsbusiness; and may acquire for said road or
roads and bridges the land , timber ; and rock necessary
for the location and construction of same by agreement
with the owners thereof ; or in default of such agree-
ment , then the presidentand directorsshallproceed as
provided in the General Statutesin cases of turnpike,
gravel, and plank roads, chapter 110.
§3 . Any and all persons shallhave the rightto trans-
portcoal,iron,or any othermineraland timberover said
road or roads,bridge or bridges,when completed ; and it
shall be the duty of said company to receive and trans-
port the same ,and for thisit shallnot charge for coal
more than one quarter of one cent per bushel per mile,
or fractionthereof; for iron or other ores,not exceeding
twelve and one half cents per ton per mile,or fraction
thereof; for lumber or timber , not exceeding twenty -five
(25) cents per one thousand feet per mile, or fraction
thereof .
§4 . The capital stock of said company shall not ex-
ceed five hundred thousand dollars, to be divided into
shares of one hundred dollarseach, which shallbe per-
sonal property,and indorsedand transferredas may be
prescribedby the by-laws of saidcompany . Books may
be opened for the subscriptionof stockby any of the
incorporators, but no subscriptionshallbe entered on
the books of the company without the consent of a ma-
jorityof the incorporators.
§5 . When stock to the amount of ten thousand dollars
shallhave been subscribed,the incorporators may calla
meeting of the subscribers, at which a president and five
directorsshallbe electedto serve for two years; and
before the expiration of their term of service,the presi-
dent shallgive notice,inwriting,to the stockholdersof
the time and place of their meeting to electsuccessors,
at leastfivedays next beforethe day of meeting; and
said presidentand directorsso electedshallhold their
offices,severally, until their successors are elected. The

438 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
said board of directorsmay prescribe the terms of sub-
scription, the time and manner of payment , and how
and when the same may be forfeited; and they may adopt
such by -laws as to collectionsof stock subscription, and
all other matters pertaining to the business of the com-
pany ,as shallbe necessary; and the said board of direct-
ors shall have the general management of all the affairs
of said company ,and may exercise allthe power hereby
granted to the corporation.
§6 . They may borrow or obtain by loan such sums of
money ,and on such terms,as they may deem expedient,
and may mortgage any or all the property of the com-
pany to secureitspayment : Provided, A majorityof the
stockholdersagree thereto. They may elector appoint
a secretary,treasurer, and agents, as they may think nec-
essary, and may fixand pay their salariesand the salary
of the president. They may fillany vacancies occurring
in the board between the elections, as provided above .
§7 . Each share of stock shallentitlethe holder thereof
to one vote,which may be voted by himselfin person
or by written proxy .
§ 8. All contractsin writing,signedby the presidentin
the name of the company , shall be binding on the com-
pany . The individualstockholdersshallnot be liablefor
the debts of the company .
§9 . When twenty thousand dollarsshallhave been sub-
scribed, and such portions paid up as the directorsmay
require, the company shallbe deemed fullyorganized,
and may proceed with itsbusiness.
§ 10. There shall be no spirituous, vinous , malt , or
intoxicating liquors sold within a distance of two miles
from the store,office,or tippleof the saidcompany ,un-
der a penalty of five hundred dollarsfor each and every
offense .
§ 11. This act takes effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882 .

C. Marshall , W. S. Reeves , W. J. Puckett, W. T. Shelton,
G. W. Reeves, R. B. Smith, Henry Elliott, T. N. Kim-
brough , J. S. Petrie, and their successors, be , and are
hereby,createda body-politic,to be known by the name ,
style, and titleof the " Supreme Assembly Protection
Union of America ," and by such name and titleshall
have perpetual succession, and be capable in law of
suingandbeingsued,pleadingandbeingimpleaded,and"
of purchasing, leasing, holding, granting, and receiving
in itscorporatename property,real,personal,and mixed ,
andof institutinggrandand subordinateassembliesasit
may see fit,under such laws,rulesand regulations,as the
corporation may enact,not in conflictwith the laws of
this State and the United States .
いい
§2 . The objectsof the corporationshallbe to unitein
one fraternalbody allacceptablewhitepersons;topro-
mote morality; to rendermaterialaidtoitsmembers ; to
encourage each other in business; to assisteach other in
obtaining employment ; to diffuse among itsmembers a
knowledge of the laws pertaining to the preservation of

-442 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
lifeand health ; to establish a fund from which a member
(who has complied with the laws of thisorder),when he
or she reaches a prescribed age , or sooner in case of
death , a stated sum ,not exceeding two thousand dollars,
shallbe paid to his or her family, or as he or she may
direct ; and to establish a fund for the benefit of white
persons under the age of sixteenyears,from which they
shall receive,on arrival at the age of twenty -one years,
or sooner in case of death , a sum not to exceed one
thousand dollars .
§ 3. The said supreme assembly shall have a common
sealforthe making and deliveringof allfegal acts and
proceedings ,the same to break or alterat pleasure.
§ 4. The saidsupreme assembly may provide forhold-
ing itsquadrennial meetings at such time and place as a
majority of itsvoting members may select.
§5 . The private property of members of the corpora-
tion shallbe exempt from the corporate debts.
§6 . The said supreme assembly shall provide for the
elections of such officersas it may deem necessary to
transact the business of the corporation , and to further its
objects, who shall hold officeuntil their successors are
duly elected,qualified, and installedinto office.
§7 . The said supreme assembly shallhave power to
create, by assessment upon its members , hold, and dis-
burse the funds named in the objects of the corporation
forpromoting benevolence,under such regulationsas it
may deem necessary to adopt , and said funds shall be
exempt from execution,and shallunder no circumstances
be liableto seizure or appropriation by any legal or equi-
table process forany debt or debts of itslivingor deceased
members ,and said funds shallbe exempt from the laws ,
rulesand regulations governing the Insurance Bureau ot
this State .
§ 8. This act to be in fullforce, and to take effectfrom
-and afteritspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 443
CHAPTER 995 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe town of Hecla .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1. That the following boundary of land , situated in
Hopkins county , to-wit : Beginning at the west end of
bridge across Stewart's creek , near the shaft of Hecla
coal mines ; thence north sixty-nine degrees west fifty-five
poles and twenty feet; thence south eighty degrees west
eighteen poles twenty -four feet; thence south forty-two
degrees west sixty-eight poles twenty -four feet; thence
south eighteen degrees east twenty-nine poles twenty-
eight feet; thence south seventy-four degrees east sixty-
fourpoles two feet; thence north forty-one degreeseast
sixty-eight poles twenty -four feet; thence south fifty-six
degrees east eight poles; thence north thirty-fivedegrees .
east seven poles; thence north eight degrees east twenty-
five poles to the beginning ,be , and the same is hereby ,
incorporated as the town of Hecla , and said town is
established by that name , and with corporate limits
aforesaid. Thos .D. Roberts ,W. R. Dayal ,James Priest,
Sampson Platt, and Sam . R. Carmack are hereby ap-
pointed trusteesfor said town , and shallhold their office
untilthe firstSaturday in March , 1883, or untiltheirsuc-
cessors may be elected and qualified. It shall be the
duty of said trusteesto give notice,by public advertise-
ment , at three public places in said town , for at least ten
days before the said firstSaturday in March , 1883, of the
time and place of the electionfor trusteesof said town ;
and on the said firstSaturday in March ,1883,the male
citizensof said town ,over twenty -one years of age ,who
are naturalized and have resided in said town for sixty
days next preceding the day of election,may vote for five
male citizens of said town for trustees to serve one year
from and after that date; and untiltheirsuccessorsmay
be elected and qualified, the five persons voted for and
having the largest number of votes shall be declared
duly elected. The trusteesshallappointa clerkand two
judges to hold the election. They shallbe firstsworn to

444 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
faithfullyand impartiallyhold, conduct, and certifythe
same to the trustees, who shall filethe poll-books and
certificatesof qualificationand resultin theiroffice,and
the same shallbe kept and preserved by the board of
trustees, from year to year , as part of theirrecords. The
electionshallbe advertised,and held on the firstSaturday
in March in each succeeding year , as directed above .
Each trustee shall, before he enters upon the duties of
his office,take an oath to faithfullyand impartially exe-
cute his duties as trustee of said town .
§2 . The trusteesshallelectone of their number presi-
dent of the board of trustees. He shall preside at all
theirmeetings,and vote only when there may be a tie
vote as between the others ; he shall sign all orders and
proceedingsof the board; theyshallappointa clerkof
the board of trustees, who shall be sworn , and receive
for his services such compensation as the board may
allow; and he shall be the keeper of the record and
papers of the board of trustees; he shall be subject to
removal at any time by said board . The trustees are
empowered to pass such by -laws , and enact such regula-
tions, as may , in theirjudgment , be necessary for the
good government and well being of said town , not in
conflict with the Constitution and laws of the United
States and State of Kentucky , and to fillall vacancies
that may occur before the next election.
§3 . Said trusteesshall have the power to levy a poll.
tax upon each male citizenof saidtown who may have
resided there for sixty days of two dollars, and an ad
valorem tax upon each one hundred dollars' worth of
property within the town subjectto taxation for State
purposes,not exceedingthirtyfivecents,to be appliedto
the purpose of repairingthe streets,alleys,and the im
provement of said town ; and all finesand forfeituresfor
the violation of the laws and ordinances of said, town ,
and forbreaches of the peace and riotscommitted within
the boundary of said town , shallbe collectedand paid
over to the treasurer of said town , to be used by said
trusteesforthe improvement of saidtown . The clerkof

LAWS OF KENTUCKY , 445
the board of trustees shall listthe taxable property and
tithes in said town each year, between the firstday of
March and the firstday of April, and make out a full
listthereof in a book and return the same to the trustees ,
and the same shallbe preserved and filedby the trustees
as part of the records of said town . The clerk shall
have the power to swear each party assessed, and if any .
person may be aggrieved by the assessment of said clerk,
either as to the value of property or as to his listof poll..
tax,or on any other account ,such person may , at any
time after such assessment , and before the firstday of
August ,apply to said board of trustees and make com-
plaint, and have the judgment of the trusteesupon the
complaint . If the trusteessustain the complaint ,in whole
or in part,the listshall be corrected accordingly; if the
complaint be overruled, a record of that fact shall be
made .
§ 4. There shall be a full and complete record of all
theiractskept by the said board of trustees, and the
same shallbe open and subject to the inspection of any
citizen of said town under all reasonable requests, and
the clerk of the board shall give and deliverto any one
demanding the same a certifiedcopy of any order or
paper or record, or part of record in said office,and the
same shall be attested by the clerk; said clerk shall be
allowed the same fees for said copies as are now allowed
county court clerks for likeservices.
§ 5. Afterthe firstday of May of each year,the board
of trustees shall fix the rate of ad valorem tax to be col-
lected for that year , and the amount of poll-tax, and
shall show in their records the rate so ordered to be col-
lected. The clerk shallthereupon make out a listof the
taxes against each person so assessed as aforesaid, and
the amount of estate upon which it isfounded , and shall
deliver it to the marshal of said town , and take his re-
ceipt therefor,and fileitwith the records of said board .
It shallbe the duty of said marshal to collectthe same ,
and he ishereby vested with allthe powers and authority
to do so by levy and distraint, and to the same extent

446 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
that sheriffs have and are vested with to collect the State
revenue and county levy,and pay itover to the treasurer
of said board on or before the firstday of October there-
after,and for failingto do so said treasurer may ,in the
name of said trustees, in their corporate capacity, insti-
tute a motion against said marshal and his securities in
the Hopkins quarterly or circuitcourts,or in the court of
a justiceof the peace of saidcounty having jurisdiction
of the amount claimed , and recover judgment for the
part so not paid, and ten per cent. damages thereon
besides cost,and proceed to collectthe same by execu-
tion,and without the right of replevy : Provided,however,
That no judgment shallbe rendered against any of said
defendants without a written notice of at least ten days :
And provided further, That if the marshal shall,between
the firstday of October and the firstday of November ,
make out and swear to a listof said taxes uncollected ,
and that he has made close and diligentsearch for prop-
erty of the delinquenton which to levy and make the
amount of taxes due ,and failed to find any ; that he has
applied to said delinquent,if residing in said town , and
demanded payment ; and if he does not know of any
property of said delinquentout of which to make the
taxes,he shallstand relieved from liabilityto that extent
on his receipt.
§ 6. The clerk of said board shall re-list and hand to
said marshal all the delinquent listsfor collection, from
time to time , as the trustees may direct, and take his
receipttherefor; and said marshal and his securitiesshall
be held responsiblefor the same .
§7 . The board of trustees shall elect a treasurer of
theirboard ,who shallexecute a bond with good security,
to be approved by the board. The bond shallbe in the
nature of a covenant to the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
conditioned that the treasurer shallfaithfullyperform all
the duties of said office,and pay over all moneys that
may come to his hands as treasurer as the board of trus-
tees may order and direct; and for violation of any of
the provisions of said board ,the trustees, or any one else

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 447 .
injuredthereby,may have and maintain an actionin the
name of the Commonwealth , and recover whatever may
be justlydue ,in any court having jurisdiction.
§ 8. The board of trustees may appoint a marshal for
said town , whose appointment shallbe entered of record
in their office; and upon a certifiedcopy thereof being
produced to the clerkof Hopkins county,he shall,at a
regular or called term of the Hopkins county court,ad-
minister to said marshal the same oaths taken by con-
stables, and thathe willfaithfullyperform alldutiesof
marshal of said town according to law ; and the said
marshal shall execute bond with good security in said
court, as constables are required to do before entering
upon the duties of theiroffice, with the furthercovenant
that he willperform allthe dutiesof marshal of said town
according to law . The bond shall be attested by the
clerk , and filed in said clerk's office,all of which shall be
put upon the recordsof said court. The marshal shall
pay the clerk,as his fee for said service, one dollar.
The marshal and his securities on said bond shall be
liable thereon to the trustees of said town , and to
any and every one, as constablesand their securi-
ties are liable on their bonds , and the like penalties
and liabilities,actionsand recoveries, may be had there-
on . The marshal so appointed and qualifiedshallhold
his officeuntilthe firstSaturday in March , 1883, and
untilhis successor shallhave been chosen and qualified,
and shall have the same authorities, powers ,and juris-
diction that constables of Hopkins county have , and
shallbe under thesame responsibilitiesand liabilitiesfor
illegallevy and distraint,or other malfeasance , misfeas-
ance ,or nonfeasance in office. On the firstSaturday in
March , 1883, at the same time trustees are elected as
aforesaid, there shall be by the same voters elected
marshal for said town ,and on the same day in every
year thereafter,who shallhold his office,afterbeing qual-
ifiedand giving bond as aforesaid,tillthe next regular
election, and untilhis successor may be chosen and qual-
ified. He shall be entitledto be qualifiedand execute
a

448- LAWS OF KENTUCKY
bond as aforesaid,in the county court,upon the produc-
tion of a certificate from the board of trustees of said
town ,showing hiselection'; and upon hisbeing qualified
and giving bond ,he shall have the same powers , and
be under the same liabilities, as hereinbefore enumerated
and set out for the marshal to be appointed .
1 ,
§ 9. The marshal shall have the same fees and com-
missions for his services, in executing process and per-
forming officialduties, that are allowed by law to
constables ; and for collecting taxes in said town he shall
have and receive such commission and compensation as:
may be allowedto him by the board of trustees. Itshall
be the specialduty of saidmarshal to executeallprocess
issued for an alleged violation of the by -laws and ordi-1
nances of said town , and to receive executions and other
process in favor of said trustees,and serve and account
for the same as constables are required to account for
warrants or processput into theirhands to execute or
account for .
§ 10. The board of trustees of the town of Hecla shall
be a body -corporate and politic, and shallbe known and
designated by the corporate name of " The Trustees of
the Town of Hecla ;" and in that name may sue and be
sued ,and have allthe rights and privilegesnecessary to
consummate the purposes of theirappointment ; and for
any infractionof the by -laws or ordinances of said town
enacted by said trusteesa warrant may be issued by any
justice of the peace ,and served and returned for trial
before a justiceof the peace, and every jurisdictionis
hereby conferred upon such justice necessary to carry
out said by -laws , and to collectfines and forfeituresfor
their violation .
§11 . All the officersof said town shall be citizens'
thereof, and at the time of their election or appointment
tooffice shallhave had a residence therein at leastsixty
days next before such election or appointment ,and shall
be twenty -one years of age .
§ 12. The trusteesof said town shallhave no authority
to grant any licensefor coffee-houses or drinking saloons !
in said town .
1

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 449
§ 13. Said board of trustees shall cause a well -defined
plat of said town to be made out, and it,together with
the boundary of said town ,shallbe recorded by the clerk
of the Hopkins county court at the expense of said trus-
tees .
§ 14. On the said firstSaturday in March , 1883, at the
same time that the trustees are elected , there shall be
elected by the same voters of said town a police judge ;
who shallhold his officefor four years, and untilhis suc-
cessor shall have been commissioned and qualified. On
the same day of the month ,every fourth year thereafter,
the voters of said town shall elect a police judge with
like qualifications required above . The clerk of the
board of trustees shall give a certificateto the person
receiving the highest number of votes for police judge ,
and upon the same being presentedto the Governor of
Kentucky he shall issue a commission to said officer.
He shall by virtue of his officebe a conservator of the
peace,and shall,before he entersupon the dischargeof
his duties,be commissioned and sworn to faithfullyand
impartiallyexecuteallthe dutiesof policejudge of said
town , and shall take such other oaths as are administered
to justices of the peace . Said police judge shall have
the same powers , authorities, and jurisdictionof justices
of the peace in civil, penal , and criminal causes , and
except when there is a vacancy , he shallhave exclusive
jurisdictionwithin the limits of said town of all infrac-
tionof theby -laws and ordinancesof said town , and of
riots and breaches of the peace committed within the
limitsthereof; and all warrants or process in civilcases
issuedby him shallbe returnedfortrialbeforehim un-
less the defendant otherwise directs,by written indorse-
ment on the warrant or process. He shallhave the
power to administer oaths,render judgments ,and award
executions the same that justices now have . He shall
also have power to fine for contempt any sum not ex-
ceeding fivedollars, and imprison not exceeding eight
hours . He shall also have power to issue compulsory
process against parties or witnesses, to issue summons

450 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and subpœnas for parties and witnesses in any case
pending before him , and order a jury to try any case
pending beforehim when requestedby the parties. He
shallorder the arrestof any drunken or disorderlyperson
within the limitsof said town ,and upon a drunken per-
son being brought before him he may order him to be
confineduntilhe is sober,and upon a disorderlyperson
being broughtbeforehim ,he may requireof him secu-
ritytokeep thepeace,and be of good behavior for any
period not exceeding one year ; the bond shall be to the

five hundred dollars,and he shall keep a record of all
hisactsasjudge ofsaidtown ; an actionmay be brought
upon said bond for a violationthereof by the trusteesof
said town in any court having jurisdictionof the amount
in theircorporate capacity,and the fine,if any assessed,
shall be paid over to the treasurer of the town for its

at any time before the firstSaturday in March , 1883,
may selecta policejudge with allthe qualificationshere-
inbeforenamed ,and upon theircertificateto that effect
being presentedto the Governor of Kentucky , he shall
issue a commission to said officer, who shall hold his
officeuntilthe electionon the firstSaturday in March , or
untilhissuccessorshallhave been duly elected,commis-
sioned, and qualified. The succeeding police judge , as
soon as he shall have been commissioned and sworn ,
shallbe entitledto allthe records, books ,and papers of
hispredecessor. The fees of said policejudge shallbe
as follows,to-wit: fora warrant forriot,rout,or breach
of the peace,one dollar; forwarrant for breach of any
of the by -laws or ordinances of said town , fiftycents ;
forswearing a jury,and presidingat the trialin a penal
case,or fortrying same without a jury,one dollar; for

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 451
taking a recognizance, fiftycents; for each judgment ,
twenty-fivecents,and forallotherfeesthe same as now
allowed justicesof the peace. He shallhave the same
power to enforce hisjudgments thatjusticesof the peace
have ,and the same power to enforce the collectionof his
fees. He shallhold a quarterlycourtfor the trialof all
civilcausespending beforehim on the firstSaturdaysin
February ,May , August ,and November .
§ 15. The board of trustees shall have the power to
make and adopt a common corporate seal,and alter the
same at pleasure.
§ 16. This act shalltake effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 15, 1882 .
CHAPTER 996 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the Odd Fellows' Temple Association, of
Lexington,Kentucky .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the charterofthe Odd Fellows''Temple Asso-
ciationbe amended so that at the regular meeting of the
stockholderson the firstSaturday in July, 1882 , and on
the firstSaturday in July annuallythereafter,there shall
be held an election of directors for said association , to
serve for one year , or until their successors are duly
elected .
§2 . Only five directorsshall be chosen , and in the
electionsthereof each stockholdermay cast as many votes
as number of shares of stock such stockholder may own
in said association ; but no one shall be declared elected
a directorwho ,at such election,has not received a major-
ityof the votes that the stockholdersin said association,
under thisamendment ,are authorized to cast.
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882.

452 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 997 .
AN ACT to amend an act,entitled"An act establishinga new charter for
thecityof Louisville," approvedMarch 3d,1870.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. Furniture of the value of three hundred dollars,
when owned and possessed by bona fide residentsof said
city, who are housekeepers and the heads of families,
shallnot be subjectto taxation by said city.
§2 . Stock of corporations engaged in and created for
manufacturing and commercial purposes , and conducting
business in said city,shall not be liable to taxation by
said city.
§3 . All persons who , in good faith, have their resi-
dence in said city for the greater part of any year for or
in which any tax may or shall be levied by it, shall be
deemed a resident of said city.
§ 4. This act shall take effectand be in force from
and after its passage .
Approved April 15, 1882 .
CHAPTER 998
AN ACT forthe benefitof J. C. Revill, clerkof the Owen circuitcourt,
allowing him the further time of two years to distrain for and collect his
uncollected fee-bills.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That J. C. Revill, clerk of the Owen circuitcourt,
be , and he is hereby , allowed the further time of two
years in which todistrainfor and collecthis uncollected
fee-bills,subject,however ,to the same pains and penal-
tiesas now provided by law for the issualand collection
of illegalfee-bills,& c.
§ 2. This act shalltake effectitspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882 .

CHAPTER 999 .
AN ACT to fix the term of officeof the keeper of the poor -house in Todd
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1. That thekeeper of the poor-housein the county
of Todd shall be elected for a term of four years, said
electionto be held in the same manner and by the same
officersas now requiredby law .
§ 2. Nothing in this act shall affectthe term of the
present incumbent ,but his successor shallbe elected for
a term of four years, and thereafterthe terms shall be
four years,instead of one , as now provided .
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afterthe expira-
tionof theterm of the presentincumbent.
Approved April 15, 1882 .
CHAPTER 1000 .
AN ACT to extend the corporate limits of the town of Irvine, Estill
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the corporatelimitsof the town of Irvine,
Estillcounty,be,and the same are hereby,extended,as
follows: Beginning at a point on the north sideof the
Kentucky river,where the upper line of said town strikes
same ; thence up saidriverwith the north margin thereof
to a point oppositethe mouth of StationCamp creek;
thencea straightlineto the highestpeak of Mount Mi-
nerva ; thence a straightline to the northeast corner of
the corporate limitsof said town .
§2 . This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882.

454 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1001 .
AN ACT toprescribethe pay of justicesof the peace in Daviess county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That justicesof the peace in the county of Daviess
may be allowed as compensation,while servingas mem-
bers of the county court,a sum not exceeding three dol-
lars($3) per day,to be paid out of the county revenue.
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882.
CHAPTER 1002 .
AN ACT for the benefitof the Harrodsburg and Duncansville Turnpike
Road Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the president and board of directors of the
Harrodsburg and DuncansvilleTurnpike Road Company
be ,and the same ishereby ,authorized to erect a tollgate
on the three consecutive miles completed on the west end
of said company's road line, and collecttollsthereat:
Provided ,That no greater rate of tollshall be collected
than that authorized by General Statutes.
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882 .
CHAPTER 1003 .
AN ACT forthebenefitofG. W. Hunter, ofNelsoncounty.
WHEREAS , G. W. Hunter , of Nelson county , at great
expense and trouble, pursued and arrested one Virgil
McKee ,charged with horse-stealing; and whereas , said
McKee died before conviction, and said Hunter has
received nothing for effectinghis arrest; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly ofthe Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Auditor of Public Accounts be ,and he is
hereby ,directedto draw his warrant on the Treasurer for

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 455
the sum of fiftydollars,to be paid to G. W. Hunter , out
of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appropri-
ated .
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882 .
CHAPTER 1004 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleor giftof spirituous,vinous, or malt liquors,
or the mixtures thereof, within two miles of the Grange cross-roads, in
the county of Wayne .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That hereafteritshallnot be lawful for any person
or persons to sellor give to any person ,in any way what-
ever,or in any quantity whatever , spirituous, vinous , or
malt liquors,or the mixtures thereof, within two miles of
the Grange cross-roads,in the county of Wayne .
§2 . Any person who shall violate the provisions of
this act shall,upon conviction thereof, be fined the sum
of fiftydollarsfor each offense,to be recovered by indict-
ment .
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882.
CHAPTER 1005 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled" An act to incorporatethe Owings-
villeand Big Sandy Railroad Company ," approved April 8, 1880.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act,entitled" An act to incorporatethe
Owingsvilleand Big Sandy RailroadCompany ," approved
April 8, 1880, be , and the same is hereby , amended ,by
striking out the twelfth section thereof , and that said
sectionbe repealed .
§2 . This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 15, 1882 .

456 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1006 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Bowling Green and Mitchell'sFerry Turnpike-
Road Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That D. B. Campbell , Jno . O. Robinson , W. C.
Brashear,D. H. Phillips,and E. B.Seeley,and theiras-
sociates and successors, be created a body -politic and
corporate, under the name and style of the " Bowling
Green and Mitchell'sFerry Turnpike Company ," forthe
purpose of constructinga turnpike from the citylimits
of Bowling Green , on the road known as the Mitchell's
Ferry Road ,to a point on said road near where itcrosses
Barren river, estimated to be about five miles. That the
capitalstock of said company shall not exceed ten thou-
sand dollars, and be in shares of fiftydollarseach .
§2 . That when sufficient stock has been subscribed to
build one half of said road, the stockholders may organ-
ize and commence work ; and that when one half of said
road is completed , they may erect a toll-gate not less
than a half mile from the citylimitsof Bowling Green ,
and charge such tollsas are now providedforby law for
passing on ,turnpikes five miles in length,in the propor-
tion that said road completed bears to fivemiles.
§3 . There shall be but one toll-gate on said road ,and
said company shallbe empowered to construct said road
as is now provided for by law under the General Statutes-
of the State .
§4 . This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882
CHAPTER 1007 .
AN ACT to authorizethe Ohio county courtto levy and collectan ad
valorem tax for road and general county purposes.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Ohio county court shall be , and is here-
by , authorized to levy and collectan annual tax of not

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 457
exceeding fifteen cents on each one hundred dollars'
worth of propertysubjectto taxationforStatepurposes.

CHAPTER 1011 .
AN ACT toprovide fora stock law forJeffersonand Oldham counties.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . From and after the firstday of January , 1883, it
shallnot be lawfulfor any person or personswithin the
counties of Jefferson, outside of the city of Louisville,
and Oldham to permit their stock of any description to
run at large offtheirown premises or the premises of
which they may be in possession. Any person or persons
so offending shallbe liableto any person or persons dam-
aged by such stock; and for the firstoffense,shallpay for
alldamage committed ; for the second offense, shall pay
double the amount of damage committed ; and for the
thirdor any subsequent offense,shallpay fourtimes the
amount of damage committed,and the partyinjuredshall
have a lienupon such stock for allsuch damage , and all
costs of enforcing the same recoverable before any justice
of the peace or other courtof competent jurisdictionin
Jeffersonand Oldham county : Provided,That the person
or personsso injuredshallnotifythe owner or owners,or
persons having charge of said stock, of theirhaving tres-
passed upon him or them . When the partiesare thus
notified,they offerto pay ,according to the provisions of
thisact, forthe damage committed ,which ,if the person
or persons injuredrefusesto accept,and bringssuit,and
failsto recover more than the amount tendered,he shall
pay allcost.
§2 . The provisionsand penaltiesof thisactshallapply
to allpersonswho may be drivingstockfrom one place
to another in Jefferson and Oldham counties .
§3 . This act shall be submitted to the qualifiedvoters
of Jefferson,outsideof the cityof Louisville,and Oldham
counties,by the voting precincts, on the firstSaturday in
June, year 1882; and each and every precinctwhere-
in a majority of allthe votes cast at said election shall
be in favor of thislaw ,then thislaw shallbe in fullforce
and effectin such precinct or precincts; but in all pre-
cincts wherein a majority shallvote against the law ,then

460 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
in all such precinctsit shall be null and void : Provided ,.
That ifany stock owned in a precinctor precinctsthat
does not adopt thislaw shallcommit depredationswithin
a precinct or precinctswhich shallhave adopted thislaw ,
then and in that event the owners of such stock shall be
liableforallthe pains and penaltiesprovidedforin this
act ,
§4 . Itshallbe the duty of the officersof the election
to be held on the firstSaturday in June , 1882,to open a
poll at each voting precinct in said counties, outside the
cityof Louisville,and Oldham counties,and to propound
to each voter who may vote at said electionthe question,
" Are you for or against a stock law ? " and registerhis
vote in accordance with his response to said question, if
he desires to vote thereon . The vote shall be certified
and returned, examined and compared , as they are
required to be in electionsfor State and county officers;
and if itshallappear by the certificateof the examining
board that a majority of those voting voted in favor of a
stock law ,then the provisions of thisact shalltake effect
in each and every precinct wherein a majority of the
votes cast shall be in favor of a stock law , the provis-
ions of this act shall take effect and be in force in each
precinctwherein a majorityhave voted for a stocklaw ,
from and after the firstday of January , 1883. It shall
be the duty of the examining board to make out a cer-
tificate, under oath, of the votes cast in each precinct for
and against the stock law , and deliver the same to the
presidingjudge of the county courtsof said counties,
who shall cause the same to be entered of record by
the county court clerk of each county herein named .
§ 5. It shallbe the duty of the sheriffsof said counties,
at leastthirtydays before the day upon which this law is
to be voted upon ,to advertise, in at leastthree public
places in each voting precinctoutside of the cityof Louis-
villeand Oldham counties ,that a vote will be taken on the
firstSaturday in June ,1882 ,for or against the stock law .
§6. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 15, 1882...

CHAPTER 1019 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof the coloredschoolsin Petersburg,Kentucky .
WHEREAS , It is represented to the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky that Samuel Youell ,
colored,died intestatein the year 1872, in the town of
Petersburg ,county of Boone ,State of Kentucky ,owning
at the time of his death lots No. 172 and No. 173 in said
town , without heirs entitled to the same ; and whereas , a
petitionfrom sundry citizensof the town of Petersburg
has been presented to thisGeneral Assembly ,praying for
the appropriation of said property to the use and for the
benefitof the colored schools in said town ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That lots No. 172 and No. 173 in the town of Pe-
tersburg, county of Boone , State of Kentucky ,formerly
the property of said Samuel Youell , deceased , now the
propertyof the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,by escheat,
be ,and the same are hereby , appropriated to the use and
for the benefit of the colored schools of said town .
§2 . That S. G. Botts,R. Randall , and W. H Terrill,
be ,and they are hereby , made and appointed trustees, in
whom the titleto said lots is hereby vested for the pur-
poses aforesaid; and they shallhave power and authority
to leasethe same from year to year,and apply the rents
and profitsaccruing therefrom in aid of said schools; or
they may ,in theirdiscretion,use one or both of said lots
for said school purposes ; or they may selland convey
one or both of them , and reinvest the proceeds of the
sale in other real estate for school purposes , as aforesaid;
or in some other safe investment , and apply the income
therefrom to the education of the colored children in said
town .
§3 . Before they shall enter upon the discharge of the
trustherein imposed upon them ,they shallexecute before
the county judge of Boone county a bond ,withapproved
security,for the faithfulperformance of the obligations
of saidtrust; and they shallsettletheiraccounts annually

with said judge ,who shall order the same to record in
the officeof the county court clerk.

sioned by the refusal to qualify,death , or resignation of
any one or all of said trustees, and shallfor good cause
remove them , or either of them , and appoint others suit-
able to the trust: Provided, The State shall in no case

senger , Ticket, and Freight Association, and in such

Directors .
any other company or corporation wishing to contract

ciationby electingone of theirnumber president. They

ment of the company . They may borrow money , and

est of the company ,and to secure the payment thereof,

CHAPTER 1023 .
AN ACT to authorizethe Pendleton county court to levy an ad valorem tax
to build a court-house in said county, and prescribethe mode of col-
lecting .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of the county of Pendleton ,
a majority of the justices of said county being present
and concurring therein,is hereby authorized to levy an
ad valorem tax of not exceeding twenty -fivecents upon
one hundred dollars' worth of taxable propertyin said
county , in each year ,until a sufficientamount shall be
raised to build a court-house in said county , at a cost
of not exceeding fiftythousand dollars.
§2 . If the said county court levies the said tax , the
levy must be made at the same time , and in like manner ,
that the county levy is made under the laws of the Com-
monwealth .
§3 . It shall be the duty of the sheriffto collectthe
taxes levied under the provisions of this act by the said
county court; but before proceeding to collectsaid taxes,
he must execute the county levy bond , as required by the
laws of this Commonwealth ; and he and his sureties
therein shallbe responsiblefor alltaxes collectedby said
sheriffunder the provisions of thisact,in the same man-
ner ,and to the same extent,that they are liablefor county
tax collectedby sheriffs,and in additionthereto,ten per
centum damages thereon fora failureto pay over to said
county court,or the agent thereof, after demand . The
saidsheriffshallreceiveforcollectingsaidtaxessixper
centum on the amount collected .
§4 . This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 15,1882 .
