§2 . That the capitalstock of thiscompany shallbe
any sum to be fixed at any regular meeting of the board
of directors, not less than twenty thousand dollars and
not more than one hundred thousand dollars,to be divided
intosharesof onehundreddollarseach; and everystock-
holder shall be entitledto vote on allquestions affecting
said company , at its meetings ,and in the elections of
directors and other officers, as many votes as he may
hold shares of stock .
§3 . That as soon as one hundred shares are subscribed,
said company may organize, and proceed to take steps
to open books for subscriptionof stock,and to do such
other things as may be necessary to perfectthe organiza-
tion of the company ; but the election of officersshall
not takeplaceuntiltwenty thousand dollarsin stockshall
have been subscribed .
§4. The transferof stock may be made by any stock-
holder or his legal representatives, subjectto such restric-
tion as the board of directors shall, from time to time ,
make and establish, subject, however , to the general
laws .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 473
§5 . That the principal place of business shall be at
or near Winchester , Kentucky , with branch officesand
yards at such other places as may be necessary and con-
venient, and the affairsof said company shall be man-
aged by a board of five directors, all of whom shall be
stockholders . Within one month after the amount of
two hundred shares shall be subscribed for, and on the
second Monday in January in each year thereafter, the
stockholders shall hold a meeting for the election of di-
rectors, and the directors elected shall continue in office
until others have been chosen to succeed them in the
trust, and have accepted the same ; and in case of a va-
cancy in the board of directors, it shall be filledby the
directors from among the stockholders for the remainder
of the year .
§6 . The board of directors, immediately upon their Officers.
election, shall elect a president of said company , who
shall be a stockholder ; also a treasurer and a secretary ,
who shalllikewisebe stockholders,or they may combine
the duties of treasurer and secretary in one office, and
electone person to fillsaid office,or they may dispense
with one or both of said offices,but neither the president,
treasurer nor secretary shall be members of the board ,
and the president shall have no vote in meetings of the
board , but shall, when present,preside over the same .
§7 . Other corporationsmay subscribefor and hold
stock in said company ,and such corporation,in the same
way and manner as provided in the case of natural per-
sons, shall be entitledto vote in its affairsand elections.
§ 8. The business of thiscompany shall be the buying
and selling,keeping ,guarding ,feeding,loading and un-
loading,shipping, receiving, and handling for compensa-
tion, hire, and on commission , of horses, cattle, mules ,
sheep,hogs , and other livestock, and the buying ,selling,
forwarding ,and shipping of feed stuffs,agriculturalpro-
duce , and grains of allkinds.
§ 9. Said company ,in addition to the real estate neces-
sary for the location of theiryards, may buy and acquire
such real estate for laying such railway tracks, siding

474 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
switches,and turn-outs as may be necessary to connect
the yards of said company by railway communication
with such railroadsas now exist,or as may be hereafter
constructed .
§ 10. That the president and board of directorsshall,
once in every year at least, make a full report on the
stateof the company and its affairsto a general meet-
ing of the stockholders; and the board of directors may
enact by -laws for the regulation of the duties and powers
of the officersand agents of the company as provided
herein,and make all and needful rules and by -laws for
the regulation of electionsof officersand appointment of
agents and servants, and define and limit their powers
and compensation ; but no such by -laws shall be incon-
sistentwith the general laws of this State, or with the
Constitution of this State or of the United States . Said
company may take from its officers, agents, and em-
ployes bond , with adequate security, forthe faithfulper-
formance of their duties, and payment of all moneys
thatmay come to theirhands.
§ 11. Said company may issue bonds ,bearing interest
at not exceeding six per cent.per annum ,to an amount
not exceeding one half the value of the real estate of
said company , and secure the same by mortgage upon
the real property of the company ; and said company
shall, by its president and board of directors, annually
declare and make such dividends as they may deem
proper of the net profit arising from the resources of
said company ,afterdeducting the expenses current and
probably contingent, and shall divide the same among
the stockholdersof the said company in proportionto
their respectiveshares.
§ 12. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882 .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 475
CHAPTER 1025 .
AN ACT to authorizethe Boone county court to take stock in turnpikesin
said county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the judge of the Boone county court shall,
by an order of record entered at a regular or special term
of said court, submit to the qualified voters of Boone
county , at the general election on the firstMonday in
August , 1882,the question, " Whether or not they are in
favor of said county court taking stock to the amount of
seven hundred and fiftydollars per mile in turnpikes in
said county , for the purpose of constructing and com-
pleting the same ." Said order shall be published in the
Boone County Recorder,a newspaper publishedat Bur-
lington,in Boone county , at least two weeks before said
election .
§2 . It shallbe the duty of the officersof said election,
in said county ,to open pollsat the several voting places
in saidcounty for the purpose of takingthe senseof the
qualifiedvoterson saidproposition,and they shallrecord
under the head of " For the Turnpike Tax " the names
of thosevotingforsaidproposition,and underthehead
of " Against the Turnpike Tax " the names of those vot-
ing againstsaid proposition; and they shallpropound to
each voter the question, " Are you for or against the
county court taking stock in turnpikes in the county to
the amount of seven hundred and fiftydollarsper mile ? "
§ 3. Itshallbe theduty of the officerswho are required
by law to compare and certifythepoll-books at saidelec-
tion to compare the vote on said proposition, and certify
the result thereof to the county judge at the time they
certifythe returns of the general election.
§4 . If the certificateof said officersshallshow that a
majority of the votes cast on said proposition, at said
election,were cast in favor of said proposition,it shall
be the duty of the Boone county court,composed of the
judge thereof and a majority of the justicesof the peace
for said county , at their next court of claims, and an .

476 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
nually thereafter,to take stockin turnpikes in said county ,
for and on behalf of said county , which shall hereafter
be incorporatedby the Boone county court, or by the
Legislature of the State,for the purpose of constructing
and completing the same , to the amount of seven hun-
dred and fiftydollarsper mile : Provided,That no part of
said subscriptionshallbe paid to any officerof any turn-
pike company ,except upon the completion of each mile
of turnpike,or upon the courtbeing satisfiedthatmeans
are in the hands of the company to insure the completion
thereof .
§ 5. It shallbe the duty of said county court,composed
as aforesaid,at theircourt of claimsin each year after
the adoption of said proposition,to levy an ad valorem
tax upon all the taxable property of Boone county ,not
to exceed twenty -fivecents on each one hundred dollars'
worth of property, for the purpose of paying for said
stock: Provided, That one levyonlyforthatpurpose shall
be made in any one year : And provided further, That
whenever any person shall have heretofore taken stock
in any turnpikein said county,which has been or may
be completed without county aid,no tax for the purposes
herein provided for shall be collected from him untila
tax equal to fiftyper cent. of the amount paid by him for
such turnpike stock shall be collected from others who
have paid nothing for such stock.
§6 . The tax aforesaidshall be collectedby the sheriff
of said county in the same manner , and under the same
officialresponsibility,as is requiredin the collectionof
the State revenue ,and he shall be allowed fiveper cent.
of the amount collected as commissions for the collection
thereof; and he shallpay the amount collected,lesshis
commissions ,to such officeror officersof turnpike com .
panies as may be designated by order of said county
court .
§ 7. This act shalltake effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 15, 1882.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 477
CHAPTER 1026 .
AN ACT to amend an act , entitled "An act to amend an act, entitled 'An
act to incorporatethe Masonic Mutual BenefitAssociationof Maysville.'"
Be itenactedby the General Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act, entitled" An act to amend an act,
entitled" An act to incorporate the Masonic Mutual Ben-
efitAssociation of Maysville,Ky .," approved March 6th,
1878 , be amended as follows : Amend section two of
amendment so that the local board of directors,in form-
ing classes " A ," " B ," and " C ," shall not be limited to
two thousand members in class "A ," one thousand in
class " B ," and seven hundred in class " C ;" but that the
number of members in class "A " shall be unlimited , but
thatwhen the membership in saidclassshallreach two
thousand or over,the beneficiaryfund shallbe limited to
two thousand dollars, and the excess that may be col-
lected from the members ,upon the death of a member ,
over and above the two thousand dollars,shallbe covered
back intothe treasury,and when such excess thus covered
back into the treasury shallbe sufficientto pay an assess-
ment of or lossof two thousand dollars,then no assessment
shall be made upon the membership ,but the local board
shall pay the loss,and notify the membership upon the
next assessment, The local board shall have power to
make the same change in classes " B " and " C ," making
the membership in each unlimited,but confining the ben-
eficiaryfund to one thousand dollarsin class " B ," and to
seven hundred dollars in class " C ," and regulating excess
of assessments,and assessments and notice,as in class"A. "
§2 . Should all or any of the classes, after the benefi-
ciary fund had reached itsrespective limits,i. e., $ 2,000
in class "A ," $ 1,000 in class B ," and $ 700 in class " C ,"
decrease in membership below 2,000, 1,000, and 700 ,
respectively,thenthe associationshallonlypay one dol.
lar to the beneficiaries for each surviving member of the
associationbelonging to the respectiveclasses.
§3 . The localboard of directorsshallhave the power
to grade the membership of the three classes, and of each

478 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
class according to age ,and to grade the initiationfee as
itmay deem bestforthe interestof theassociation,and
may grade and regulate the amount of assessments in all
or any of the classes, and controlthe entire matter as to
the amount of the fees and assessments,according to the
age of the member or applicant for membership .
§ 4. This actshallbe in forcefrom and afterJune 15th,
1882 : Provided, That itis accepted and approved by the
association at its annual meeting in June , 1882; and all
provisionsin originalactand amendments incorporating
thisassociationthat may be in conflictwith the provisions
of thisamendment ,are hereby repealed.
Approved April 15 , 1882.
CHAPTER 1027 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof oldand disabledfiremen of the cityof Louisville..
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the general council of the cityof Louisville,
or any other authority that may now or hereafter have
controlor management of the LouisvilleFire Depart-
ment , have fullpower and authorityto retire, on pay
amounting to one half the sum receivedas fullpay at
the time of retirement, any member of said firedepart-
ment who may ,by reason of old age or permanent disa-
bility,received by accident while in the discharge of duty .
§2 . No member of the firedepartment shallbe entitled
to the benefit of thisact,unless it can be clearlyshown
that the member applying forsuch benefit,ifpermanently
disabled, such disabilitywas caused by accident while
the party was free from the influence of intoxicating
liquors,and had been for a period of twenty years next
preceding his application for retirement an active mem-
ber of the present firedepartment .
§3 . This act shall in no way apply to any employe
of the firedepartment ,except those who do actual fire
service .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 479
§ 4. The salariesof allretiredfiremenshallbe paid in
monthly installments,on the regularmonthly pay -rollsof
the firedepartment ,under the head of retired member ,
and to be provided for out of the firedepartment appro-
priation.
§ 5. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 15, 1882 .
CHAPTER 1028 .
AN ACT relatingto the Old State Road and Ripple Creek Turnpike Road
Company , in Campbell county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the circuit or chancery court of Campbell
county ishereby authorized and fullyempowered to cause
to be sold the said Old State Road and Ripple Creek
Turnpike,with allitsproperty,rights,interests,franchises,
privileges, and appurtenances ; and with the proceeds of
said sale, afterpayment of costs and expenses ,to pay the
debts of said Old State Road and Ripple Creek Turnpike
Road Company , according to their priority,if any pref-
erence isfound ,and in full,ifthe funds are sufficient; and
todistributethe surplus,if any,among the stockholders,
in proportion to the amount paid upon the stock held by
them respectively; but if not sufficientto pay the debts
of the company ,then the fund to be paid to the creditors
pro rata, after paying priorities; said sale to be made at
the court-house door in Newport or Alexandria,upon a
day to be fixedby the court.
§2 . That the purchaseror purchasersunder said sale
may organize a new company ,by the name of Old State
Road Turnpike Company ,and by that name shallhave
all the rights, privileges, powers , and franchises of the
said Old State Road and Ripple Creek Turnpike Road
Company under its charter and amendments thereto.
Saidnew company shallnot be subjectto any debts
or liabilitiesof said Old State Road and Ripple Creek
Turnpike Road Company . Said purchaseror purchasers

480 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and successors may issue so much stock, in shares of
twenty -fivedollars each ,to be paid upon such terms as
may be fixed upon , as they may deem necessary for the
completionand keeping up and operatingsaidroad; and
may issuebonds of the company for like purposes ; and
may call a meeting of the stockholders,and hold an elec-
tion for fivedirectors,one of whom shall be president,to
be chosen by the board , and which directorsshall hold
theirsaid officefor one year ,and until their successors
shall be elected and assume their duties . Said board
shallappoint a secretary and treasurer, and such other
officersand agents as they may deem necessary, and fill
allvacanciesoccurringinthe board or any officeof the
company . Each share of stock shall entitle the owner to
one vote in allelectionsby the stockholders.
§3 . The capitalstock of said company shallnot exceed
sixtythousand dollars,and its bonded indebtedness shall
not exceed fivethousand dollars. Said company may
contract and be contracted with, sue and be sued , and
shall have perpetual succession; and when organized ,
shallhave and possess all the rights,privileges,powers ,
and franchisesof the said purchaser or purchasers of said
road and property, etc., at any such sale.
§ 4. This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved April 15 , 1882 .

AN ACT forthe benefitof John M. Headley , of Fayette county.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth

he ishereby , authorized to peddle and sellgoods , wares ,
and merchandise ,other than spirituous,vinous , or malt
liquors, without taking out a licenseso to do .

Approved April 15, 1882 .

CHAPTER 1030 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Greenup Nickell,of Rowan county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

ed Greenup Nickell to collect his uncollected fee-bills
as justice of the peace and examiner for the county of
Rowan .

CHAPTER 1031 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the cityof Covington .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That for the more efficientcollection of delinquent
school taxes in the cityof Covington ,the school board of
said cityis hereby authorized to elect an additionalcol-
lector,to be known as " Special Collector;" and the said
board shallhave the power to placein the hands of said
special collectorfor collectionsuch delinquent tax-bills
as they may see proper, and for the collectionof such
taxes shallpay said collectorsuch compensation as they
may deem reasonable and just.
§2 . Said collector,before entering upon the discharge
of his duties , shall execute bond to said board in such
form and amount , and with such securities, as said board
may direct, which bond shall be approved by the said
board , and filed with itsclerk.
§ 3. Said collectorshallbe under the same laws , rules
and regulations as govern the regular collectorof said
board ,and shallhave the same powers to enforce collec-
tions .
§4 . This act shall take effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved April 15,1882.

CHAPTER 1032 .
AN ACT to amend an act to incorporateCapitalLodge , No. 6 , I. O. O. F ..
approved March 4, 1850 .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
That an act to incorporateCapital Lodge ,No. 6,I.O.
O. F., approved March 4, 1850, be, and the same is,
amended as follows :
§ 1..That W. S. Dehoney ,Ira Julian, W. L. Pence ,and
E. Whitesides are hereby constituteda body -politicand
corporate,to be known by the name of CorporateCom-
mittee of Capital Lodge ,No 6 ,of the Independent Order
of Odd Fellows,and by thatname shallhave perpetual
succession, and a common seal, with power to change
and alterthe same at pleasure; and by that name may
sue and be sued in any court whatever ,and contract and
be contracted with .
§2 . That said corporatecommittee may purchase or
receive,by deed of gift,deed of trust, or otherwise,any
real and personal property,not exceeding in value thirty
thousand dollars,and hold the same in fee-simple for the
use and benefitof said lodge .
§3 . That said corporate committee shall have power
and authority, out of the proceeds of any real and per-
tonal estatewhich shallhereafterbelong to said corporate
committee ,to create and establisha permanent fund , to
be called the widows ', orphans ', and school fund , the
principaland interestof which may be appropriated to
purposes of benevolence , as connected with the charities
bestowed by said order on widows ,orphans ,and the edu-
cationof orphans: Provided,however,That no partof the
principalor interestof saidfund shallbe appropriatedto
any such purposes,except by and upon the approval of
said lodge .
§ 4. That on the firstregular meeting night in April of
each year ,the position on said corporate committee of
the committeeman who has served the longest time there-
on shallbecome vacant ; and in that case,or in case of a
vacancy in said corporate committee by death ,resigna

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 483
tion,or removal from Franklin county,said lodge shall
torever hereafterhave power to electa member or mem-
bers of said order, belonging to said lodge,resident in
Franklin county,to fillsuch vacancy or vacancies,who
shallhave allthe power and privilegeswith which the
foregoingcommittee are investedby thisact. Said elec-
tion to be held at such time and in such manner as said
lodge shall direct.
§5 . That when ,by death ,resignation, or removal from
the county of Franklin ,a majority of the whole member-
shipof said corporatecommittee,as allowed in the first
sectionof thisact,shall have been vacated for one year
and a day ,then and in that case the fee-simple titleto all
real and personal property which may have vested in
said corporate committee by virtue of this act,shallvest
in said lodge, and said corporate committee shallforfeit
allpower and authorityconferred by thisact.
§6 . And that allmoneys arisingthere from saidcor-
porate committee shallbe paid immediately over to the
treasurer of said lodge ,and his receipt taken for same :
Provided, That the treasurer shall, before receiving the
funds, give bond , good and acceptable to the lodge, in
the sum of ten thousand dollars , and in case he shall fail
to give the said bond ,his election shall be declared null
and void; and the treasurerof said lodge to keep separate
accounts of corporate committee and lodge funds, and
out of saidfunds the said corporatecommittee to have
power to redeem bonds and pay the intereston same ,with
the approval of the noble grand and secretary of said
lodge .
§7 . That saidcorporate committee shallhave power to
ordain any and allrules and regulationsfor theirgovern-
ment and conduct as they may deem necessary, not in
conflictwith the provisionsof thisact.
§ 8. That allacts or partsof actsin conflictwith the
provisions of thisact are hereby repealed.
§ 9. This act to take effectand be in force from and
afterits passage .
Approved April 15, 1882..

CHAPTER 1034 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Joel F. Reed , of Estillcounty.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Auditorof PublicAccounts be,and he is
hereby ,directedto draw his warrant on the Treasury , in
favor of Joel F. Reed , forthe sum of fiftydollars,payable
out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appro-
priated,being for services rendered the State in attempt-
ing to collectdebt on execution in favor of the State,
from the Franklin circuitcourt, against I.N. Culton and
his sureties, as sheriffof Jackson county .
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .

CHAPTER 1035 .
AN ACT to prevent the saleof spirituous,vinous,and malt liquorswithin
a radiusof threemiles of Berea College, in the county of Madison .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshallhereafterbe unlawfulforany person
or personsto sell,vend,exchange with,orgivetoanother,
in any quantity whatever ,any spirituous,vinous ,malt ,or
other intoxicatingliquors, within a radius of three miles
of Berea College,in the county of Madison .
§ 2. Any person guiltyof a violationof the provisions
of the firstsection of thisact shall,on conviction thereof,
before a justiceof the peace , or upon an indictment by
grand jury,be fined not lessthan fiftynor more than one
hundred dollars .
§3 . This act shallnot apply to physicians who ,in good
faith and for medicinal purposes ,furnish or cause to be
furnished to another any such liquors,nor to a druggist
who in good faith, on the prescriptionof a regular physi-
cian,sellsor furnishesto anotherany of such liquors.
§ 4. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882.

CHAPTER 1036 .
AN ACT for the benefit of J. W. Sutherland , tax collector of Daviess
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the further time until after the firstday of
July, 1882, is hereby given to J. W. Sutherland , tax
collectorof Daviess county, in which to pay into the
Treasury the revenue of said county for the year 1881 :
Provided, The sureties of said collectoron his revenue
bond for said year of 1881 shall, in open court, in the
county court of said county ,in writing filedin the county
court clerk's office, consent to the extension of time .
§ 2. This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882 .
CHAPTER 1037 .
AN ACT to prohibit the selling, vending, or giving of any spirituous,
vinous, or malt liquors,or any mixture thereof, in the town of Lovelace-
ville, in Ballardcounty ,or within two milesthereof.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshall not be lawful for any person or per-
sons to,eitherdirectlyor indirectly,sell,vend ,or give any
spirituous,vinous ,or malt liquors,or a mixture thereof,
within the corporate limitsof the town of Lovelaceville,
in Ballardcounty,or withintwo milesthereof.
§2 That for any violation of this act the person or Fine.
persons so offending shall be fined not lessthan twenty-
five nor more than fiftydollars for each offense, to be
recoveredby warrant issuedby the policejudge of said
town or a justice of the peace of said county . Such
warrant shall be issued in the name of the Common-
wealth ,and fullpower ishereby given to any such officer
to try any violationof thisact that may occur within
said corporate limits,or within two miles thereof,and the
same proceedingshallbe had upon a judgment rendered,
as hereinbefore provided for,as if rendered by a circuit
or a criminal court for a similar offense .

486 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§3. Ifthepolicejudge or marshalof saidtown,ora
magistrate or constable of said county ,having knowledge
or information of a violation of the first and second sec-
tions of this act, shall willfullyneglect or failto exe-
cute the dutiesrequiredof them respectively,theyshall,
upon indictment of a grand jury,and conviction in the
circuitcourt,be fined not less than fiftynor more than
one hundred dollars,or imprisonment in the county jail
not lessthan thirtydays nor more than three months for
each offense,or both so finedand imprisonedat thedis-
cretion of the jury .
§ 4. The provisions of this act shallnot apply to wine
to be used onlyforsacramentalpurposes.
§5 . All fines collected under this act,except such as
may be collectedfrom officersfailingto perform the duties
prescribed in this act, shall be paid into the treasury of
said town .
§6 . This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 15, 1882 .
CHAPTER 1038 .
AN ACT for the benefitof James D. McConnell , an infunt, of Barren
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That James D. McConnell , of Barren county , be ,
and he ishereby ,authorized to contract and be contracted
with, selland dispose of any property he may own or
acquire,the same as if he was now twenty -one years of
age , and there is conferred upon him all the rightsand

Approved April 15, 1882 .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 487
CHAPTER 1039 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof the BaptistChurch at Hartford, Kentucky .
WHEREAS , The Baptist Church at Hartford ,Kentucky ,
hold titleto a lot of ground in said town ,conveyed to
them by the judge of the Ohio county court,by order of
the Ohio county court,made and entered on the records
of said court, and are desirous of sellingthe same to use
the proceeds of sale in building a church on another lot,
contractedforby them forthatpurpose; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That when so directedby the BaptistChurch at
Hartford , Kentucky ,by order made and entered on the
minutes at any church meeting of said church,the clerk
of saidchurch ishereby authorizedto sell,eitheratpub-
licor privatesale,the lot mentioned in the preamble of
this act. He shall report said sale to the church at the
next church meeting after said sale, and if said sale is
ratifiedby said church meeting, said clerk shall, upon
payment of thepurchase-money ,convey saidlotby deed
to the purchaser ,which shall invest him with a good and
sufficient title to same .
§2 . The money arising from said sale shall be used
solelyto aid in building a new Baptist church in Hart-
ford,Ky .
§3 . This act shall be in force from and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 15, 1882 .

AN ACT to incorporatePleasantHill Lodge , No. 535, of Free and Accepted
Masons ,in Clay county.

of Kentucky :
§ 1.That themaster,wardens,and members of Pleasant
Hill Lodge ,number fivehundred and thirty-five,of Free
and Accepted Masons ,in Clay county,Kentucky ,be,and
they are hereby,made and constituteda body-politicand

corporate, under the name and style of Pleasant Hill

to hold or dispose of and convey the same at pleasure.
§3 . That serviceof notice or process on the master or
secretaryof said lodge shall be sufficientnotice to said
corporation.
§4 . That saidcorporationshallhave power to pass all
such by -laws ,rules and regulations,not inconsistentwith
the Constitution of this State and the United States , and
laws passed in pursuance thereof, and of the constitution
and laws and edictsof the grand lodge of the State of
Kentucky , as said corporation may from time to time
deem expedient forthe control, management ,and preser-
vation of the property, interest, and concerns of the
lodge and corporation.
§5 . The management of the affairsof said corpora-
tion shallbe ,and is hereby ,invested and intrustedto the
presentmaster and the presentjuniorand seniorwardens ,
and theirsuccessorsin officeas trusteesthereof; a major-
ity of whom ,by and with the consent of a majority of
the lodge,shall have all power to make contracts per-
taining to the real or personal estate of said lodge; and
in conveying real estate,allof said trusteesshalljoin in
the conveyance .
§ 6. Said corporation shalltake possession of, and hold
and dispose of any real or personal estate that the said
lodge now has or may have ,in the same manner that is
hereinprovidedforallfutureestatesacquiredby them .
§7 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 15 , 1882 .

CHAPTER 1041 .
AN ACT to authorizethe cityof Carlisle, in Nicholas county, to re-issue
and duplicatecertain bonds of the town of Carlislein payment of its
subscriptionto the Maysville and Lexington Railroad, Northern Di-
vision .
WHEREAS , Seventeen (17)bonds,beingnumbers ten (10)
to twenty-six (26), inclusive,for $500 each,bearing six
per cent interest,and payable in 1889,issuedby Carlisle,
in Nicholas county , when a town ,in payment of itssub-
scriptionto the Maysvilleand Lexington RailroadCom-
pany ,Northern Division,under the provisionsof an act
so empowering it,approved March 1, 1869 (said bonds
being the property of ElizabethB. Marshall), were, in
May , 1880,stolen, and have not since been discovered or
recovered ; now , therefore ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the board of common council of said city of
Carlislebe , and they are hereby , authorized and empow-
ered, in their discretion,and on such terms as they may
thinkproper,to re-issueand duplicatesaid bonds,num-
bersten (10) to twenty-six (26), inclusive,as they were
originallyissued,under the act aforesaid,and to deliver
said duplicatesto said Elizabeth B. Marshall .
§2 . When said bonds are issued,they shall be in ver-
biage ,dates,and terms identical,as near as may be ,with
the stolen bonds at the time of their larceny, with the
coupons attached for the interestdue and to become due ,
bearing the same rate of interest, and payable on the
same dates and in like manner .
§3 . This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved April 15 , 1882.

CHAPTER 1043 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Householders' Mutual BenefitAssociation of
Kentucky .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That A. B. Whayne , Albert Leet , Ed . Starks, J. Incorporators.
W. Blythe,J. A. Collins,R. M. Bolinger,and J. P. Ty-
ler, and their associates and successors , be , and are here-
by , created a body -corporate, under the name of the Style.
Householders ' Mutual Benefit Association of the State of
Kentucky , with power to sue and be sued ,contract and
be contracted with , with the right to acquire and hold
property,realand personal; withpower todisposeof the
same for benevolent purposes; and may make such by-
laws and regulations as are necessary to carry out the
intentions and purposes of this act of incorporation,not
inconsistentwith this charter and the general laws of
the land ; and may have a corporate seal,renewable and
changeable at pleasure, and may have perpetual succes-
sion .
§2 . The principalofficeof thisassociationshallbe in Principaloffice.
the town of Fulton ,Kentucky ,and the persons named in
sectionone of thisact shall have power ,and are hereby
authorized to electthe firstboard of directorsat any time
after the passage of this act, and from which number
they shall elect the officersof the association ,viz : a pres-
ident,vice president, secretary, and treasurer, and such
otherofficersas theymay deem requisiteforthe manage .
ment of the business of the association. They shallhold
theirofficesforlife,or during good behavior. The board
of directors of this association shall consist of not less
than seven nor more than thirteen, at leastfiveof whom
shallbe residentsof Fulton county ,Kentucky ; and said
directors shall constitute a local board , who shall conduct
and manage the affairsof the association, and a majority
of whom shallconstitutea quorum for the transactionof
any and allbusiness,and may proceed at pleasure to or-
ganize fully. The board of directorsmay fixthe salaries
of theirofficers, and prescribein the by -laws theirduties,

49+ LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and the amounts and conditions of such bonds as they
may require of them ,and all other acts within the pur-
view of thischarter,and not inconsistentwith the general
law . Should a vacancy occur in the board of directors,
the remaining members shall have power to fillit, and
for such purpose may vote in person or by proxy .
§3 . The board of directorsmay give to the members
of this association such assistance, in case of damage
or destructionof theirproperty,in such manner as their
constitutionand by-laws may requireor prescribe; and
to enablethe directorsto createa fund forthispurpose,
they may charge a membership fee, annual fees, and
assessmentfees. Upon the payment of membership fees,
each member shall receive a certificateof membership ,
signed by the president and secretary,which shallentitle
the holder thereof to all the benefits of the association
so long as he,she,or they keep theirannual and assess-
ment feespaid ,as required by the by -laws of the associ-
ation .
§4 . The board of directorsmay establishagencies and
localofficersanywhere in Hickman , Fulton, Calloway,
Graves, Ballard,Marshall,McCracken , Livingston,Cald-
well, Lyon ,Crittenden,and Trigg counties,for the pur-
pose of transactingthe business of thisassociation,which
shall be freefrom tax required of insurance companies ;
shall not be required to make any deposit or report, and
shall not be subject to the insurance laws of this Com-
monwealth .
§5 . The president and directorsshallcause to be made
annually a fullreport of the business of this association,
subject at alltimes to the inspection of itsmembers .
§6 . The general welfare of society, not individual
profit,is the objectfor which this charter isgranted ,and
hence the members are not stockholders in the legal sense
of theterin,and no dividendsor profitsshallbe divided
among the members .
§7 . The private property of the members of thisasso

ciation shall not be liable for any debt of this associa-
tion .
§8 This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .

CHAPTER 1044 .
AN ACT toamend an act incorporating the town of Farmers , Rowan
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the limitsof said town be extended as fol-
lows : Beginning at the house where Hiram Brain now
lives,thence due west to Licking river; thence up said
riveras itmeanders to a pointthreehundred (300) yards
above the saw -millof Collier, McClanahan & Co .;thence
a straightline to Mrs. Alcey D. Clack's house , includ-
ing said house ; thence a straightline to J. B Kring's
house , including said house ; thence a straight line to
Robert Nickell'shouse , including said house ; thence a
straight line to house where Isaac Hardin now lives,
includingsaidhouse; thencedue northto Triplettcreek;
thence down said creek as it meanders to the beginning .
§2 . That the police judge shall hold his courts in
Farmers at such times as the town council shall,by ordi-
nance ,prescribe. Said court shallhave exclusive juris-
dictionwithin the limitsof said town of all prosecutions
for a breach of the ordinances of said town , and concur-
rent jurisdictionwith the quarterly court and courts of
justicesof the peace within the town districtof all civil
suits where the matter in controversy does not exceed
one hundred dollars, exclusive of interest and costs ; and
shallhold his court to try allcivilcases second Saturday
in each month .
§3 . All persons convicted of misdemeanors or viola-
tion of the ordinances of said town , and adjudged to
pay a fine,indefaultof payment ,shallbe kept at labor
at such work as the policejudge may prescribe, until

490 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
said fine and costsare paid ,at the rate of two dollarsper
day ; and itshallbe lawful for the policejudge to pre-
scribe what task shall constitute or represent a day's
work .
§4 The town council are hereby vestedwith power
and controloverthe squares,streets,and alleysin said
town , or which may be hereafter opened . They shall
have all necessarysidewalks constructed, improved, or
repaired in said town , and shall assess the cost thereof
against the owner or owners of the lot fronting upon
sidewalks thus constructed, improved , or repaired ; and
the cost thereofshallbe a lienupon saidlot,and they
shallhave fullpower to enforce said lien.
§5 . This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage ; and allactsor parts of acts in conflictherewith are
hereby repealed.
Approved April 15, 1882
CHAPTER 1045 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the Owensboro and Nashville Railway
Company .
WHEREAS , Several of the counties along the line of the
railroadproposed to be constructedby the Owensboro
and Nashville Railway Company from Owensboro , in
Daviess county ,to Adairville,in Logan county ,subscribed
large sums of money to aid in its construction, and are
now being heavilytaxed on account thereof; and whereas ,
itis represented to thisGeneral Assembly that said com-
pany isnot in good faithexercisingthe powers conferred
by its charter for the purpose of constructing said road ,
and does not intend to complete it; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That if the Owensboro and Nashville Railway
Company shall failto build and complete the railroad
from Owensboro , in Daviess county , to Adairville, in
Logan county , within two years from the date of the
passage of thisact,it shallforfeitall itschartered rights

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 497
and privileges, whether conferred by the original charter
of the Owensboro and RussellvilleRailroad Company ,
and the amendments thereto,or by any articlesof asso-
ciation under the general law , and it shallcease to exist
as a corporation.
§2 . Any one or more freeholdersand tax-payers resid-
ing in any county ,taxed to aid in the construction of said
railroad,may filea petitionin the courthaving jurisdic-
tion of the subject matter in any county through which
the said railroadhas been or isproposed to be constructed,
making said company a defendant thereto, and alleging
the fact that it has failed to complete said railroad, as
herein required; and it shall be the duty of said court,
upon being satisfiedthatsaidcompany has failedto com
plete said road within the time prescribed, to render a
judgment of forfeitureas requiredby thisact,and shall
direct the sale of all the property,franchises, and priv-
ilegesof the Owensboro and Nashville Railway Company ,
upon such terms of creditas itmay deem proper .
§3 . The purchaser or purchasers shallbe the successor
to the Owensboro and Nashville Railway Company ,and
entitledto all its property, franchises,rights, and privil-
eges, subject to the obligations and restrictionsimposed
by itscharter,but shall not be responsible,or in any way
liablefor itsdebts or contracts, and shallgive bond , with
good and sufficientsurety,for the payment of the pur-
chase money .
§ 4. The purchaser or purchasers shall also, in their
individual capacity,give bond to the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , with sufficientsurety,for the use and benefit
of the successor to the Owensboro and Nashville Rail-
way Company ,obligatingthe purchasersto complete the
said railroad to Adairville,in Logan county , within two
years from the date thereof. For a failure to comply
with said obligation,suit may be brought by any one or
more treeholdersand tax -payers in said counties for the
use and benefitof saidcompany ,and the extentof the
LOC . L., VOL . II- 32

498 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
recovery shallbe a sum sufficientto complete said road..
and which sum shallbe so applied.
§5 . A fraudulentmisapplicationof any sum of money
recovered upon said bond to the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky,by any officerof said company for whose use and
benefit the recovery was had , shall be a felony,deemed
to have been committed in the county in which suit may
have been institutedto recover on said bond , and the
person or persons guiltythereofshall,upon conviction,
be punishedby confinement in the penitentiarynot less
than one nor more than three years.
§6 . The proceeds of the sale of the property and fran-
chises of the Owensboro and Nashville Railway Com-
pany shallbe applied,firstto the payment of the costsof
suit, including a reasonable attorney'sfee to be fixed by
the court; secondly,to the payment of debts, if any ,due
from or owing by said company ,according to their legal
priority. The balance ,if any ,shallbe distributedamong
the stockholdersin proportionto the amount of stock
owned by them respectively.
§ 7. This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas--
sage .
Approved April 15, 1882.
CHAPTER 1046 .
AN ACT to authorizea certain definedportionof Garrard county to sub--
scribe to the capital stock of the Lancaster and Buckeye Turnpike
Road Company in saidcounty,and to provideforthe payment of such
subscription .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That allthat portion of Garrard county embraced
in the following described boundary , viz: Beginning at a
pointin the centerof the Lancaster and Buckeye Turn-
pike Road where said road crosses the division line
between precinctsnumbers two and three in said county ;
thence with said division line to Long Branch ; thence
down Long Branch with its meanders to Back creek
>

LAWS OF KENTUCKY 499
at the mouth of Long Branch ; thence down Back creek
with its meanders to Paint Lick creek , at the mouth of
Back creek; thence down Paint Lick creek, and with the
boundary lineof precinctnumber two ,to thebeginning,
be , and ishereby ,authorized and empowered to subscribe
three thousand dollarsto the capitalstock of the Lancas
terand Buckeye Turnpike Road Company .
§2 . That said subscription shall be made upon the
books of said company ,in the name of said defined por-
tion of Garrard county , by the judge of the Garrard
county court,whenever a majorityof the qualifiedvoters
of said defined portion of Garrard county shall; in writ-
ing , request him so to do .
§3 . When said subscriptionismade as provided in this
act,the same shallbe bindingupon saiddefinedportion
of Garrard county; and the taxablepropertyof saidde-
fined portion of Garrard county subject to taxation for
State revenue shallbe bound for the payment thereof.
§4 . The judge of the Garrard county court shall, after
said subscription is made as aforesaid,issuein the name
of said defined portion of Garrard county six bonds ,in
the sum of five hundred dollars each ,one of which bonds
shall be made payable annually until all are paid ; the
firstone being made payable on January the first, 1883,
and one on each succeeding firstday of January there-
after,untilallare paid. Said bonds shallbear interestat
the rate of six per cent.per annum from date untilpaid ,
and shallbe negotiated by the treasurerof Garrard county
for any sum not lessthan theirpar value .
§ 5. It shallbe the duty of the Garrard county court,
annually ,to assess an ad valorem tax upon the taxable
propertywithin said definedportionof Garrard county
sufficientto meet the interestupon the bonds , and to pay
the bonds as they respectivelyfalldue , and to pay the
costs of assessment,levy,and collectionof said tax .
§6 . It shall be the duty of the assessor of Garrard
county , afterthisact goes into effect, in the year 1882,at
the time he is requiredto returnhis books or listsof
assessment of property for State purposes and county

500 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
taxation,to return to the Garrard county court a sepa-
rate listof the property, and the value thereof,and the
persons owning same within said defined portion of
Garrard county ; and for each listso returned he shallbe
entitledto two cents,to be paid by the treasurer of Gar-
rard county out of the tax levied and collected under
and by virtue of this act ; and the assessor shall continue
to return such separate listeach succeeding year until
the said bonds are paid . The duties of the board of
supervisors, of the clerk, and of the county court of Gar-
rard county ,in regard to said separate list,shall be the
same as now prescribed by law in regard to the tax-book :
Provided, The clerk of the Garrard county court shall
make but one copy of said separate list, which copy he
shall deliver to the sheriffof Garrard county on or be-
fore the firstday of June in each year ,and shalltake the
sheriff'sreceiptfor the same .
§7 . It shall be the duty of the sheriff of Garrard
county ,after he shall receive the copy of said separate
list, to collect the tax provided for in this act in the same
manner that he collects the State revenue , and he shall
have the same powers of distraint,levy,and sale,and all
necessary powers in the collectionof said tax , that he
possesses in regard to the collection of State revenue ;
and he and his suretiesshall be responsible for the col-
lection and proper accounting for of said tax upon his
officialbond as sheriff; and he shall be entitled to receive
the same commissions for collecting said tax as he is
entitled to receive for collecting the State revenue , and
shallpay allmoneys collectedunder thisact to the treas-
urer of Garrard county ,who shallappropriate the same
to the payment of the said bonds and the interestthereon,
and the costs of assessment ,levy, and collectionof said
tax . Said treasurershallreceive such compensation out
of the funds in his hands as the Garrard county court
may allow him , not exceeding one per cent. of the
amount or amounts disbursed by him .
§ 8. Each person paying any of the tax herein pro-
vided for shall,to the extent and amount of the tax paid ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 501
become stockholders in said company ,and be entitledto
share in any dividend declared on the stock of said com-
pany . The sheriffshall execute and deliver to each
person paying any tax a receiptfor the amount thereof,
which receiptshallbe assignable,and when the receipts
shall amount to fiftydollars or more , shall entitlethe
holder, upon presentation to the proper officersof the
company ,to certificatesof stock in said company at the
rate of one share for each fiftydollars,and every multiple
of fiftydollars.
§ 9. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved April 15, 1882 .
CHAPTER 1047 .
AN ACT to amend an act,entitled"An act to amend an act to incorporate
the Louisville, Harrodsburg , and Virginia Railroad," approved March
6th , 1869 .

of Kentucky :

cinct, in Henry county," approved April 1, 1880, shall

502 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .

of Kentucky :

are hereby changed,so as to includethe farm of Philip

CHAPTER 1050
AN ACT toamend the charterof the Warren Deposit Bank .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shallbe lawful for the Warren Deposit
Bank to reduce its capitalstock to the sum of one hun-
dred thousand dollars at any time after January 1, 1883:
Provided, At a called or regular meeting of the stock-
holders of said bank a majority of the stock in said bank
shallbe voted to reducethe capitalstockto one hundred
thousanddollars. The bank may purchase the outstand-
ing stock in case of a reduction of the capital, or may

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 503
CHAPTER 1051 .
AN ACT to establishan additionalvoting place in the Marion precinct,in
Crittenden county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That an additionalvoting place be ,and the same is
hereby ,created and establishedin precinct number one ,
in Crittenden county ,said place of voting to be at such
house in the town of Marion ,or such part of the court-
house as may be designated by the Crittenden county
court , by an order entered of record in said court at some
regular term to be held at least ten days before a regular
or specialelection; and said place of voting,when des-
ignated by the county court,shall be known as polling
place number two in said electiondistrict,and the voting
place now provided by law shall be known as polling
place number one .
§2 . It shall be the duty of the county court of said
county to appoint officersof electionto take the vote at
said pollingplaces numbers one and two at the same time
thatofficersof electionare appointed in the other pre-
cincts of said county .
§ 3. All voters in said election district number one liv-
ing on the west side of the Princeton and Ford's Ferry
road are required to vote at polling place number one in
said precinct, and those living on the east side of said
road shallvote at polling place number two in said pre-
cinct .
§4 . The sheriff of Crittenden county is required to
cause fiftynoticesto be printedin large type,tobe paid
for as other election expenses , and cause said notices to
be posted through said electionprecinct, at leastten days
before the next regular election,informing the citizensof
the provisions of this act .
§ 5. This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 15, 1882.

CHAPTER 1052 .
AN ACT for the benefit of William T. Love , former sheriffof Hardin
county .
WHEREAS , It appears that William T. Love , former
sheriffof Hardin county , has fully paid the amount of
his indebtedness to the State and county for revenue ,
county levy,and other dues and taxes, and stillretains
unpaid tax receiptsand fee-billsagainstvarious parties
in said county ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That William T. Love , former sheriffof Hardin
county ,be ,and he is hereby ,granted the furthertime of
two years in which to collect,in the manner indicated by
law for the collection of taxes and fee -bills stilldistrain-
able,all taxes, county levy, and fee-billsin his hands as
such ex -sheriff.
§2 . Before he shall proceed to levy, distrain, or sell
for any of such dues ,he shall,in the county court of said
county , execute a bond , with sufficientsureties, to be
approved by the court and attestedby the clerk,which
bond shall be given to the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
and shallindemnifyany person enjoinedby reason of any
illegallevy or distraintagainst any lossthey may thereby
sustain,and said bond shallhave the same force and effect,
and be proceededupon in thesame manner ,as theofficial
bond of the sheriff, and said Love and his sureties shall
be subject to likepenalties thereon .
§3 . Nothing in thisact shallbe construed to affectany
property held by innocent purchasers,and in good faith
without notice, bought from parties against whom the
tax list or fee -bill is held .
§ 4. This act to be in force from and afteritspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882 .

CHAPTER 1054 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe town of Olympia , in Bath county.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
ARTICLE I.
§1 . That the corporate limitsof the town of Olympia Boundary.
shall include all the territorywithin a boundary of one
square mile,having for itscenter the front door of office
of the present Lexington and Big Sandy depot ,the north
and south lines of the square to run parallel with the
Lexington and Big Sandy Railroad .
§2 . That the said town is hereby laid off into four
wards as follows,to wit: The territorylying east of the
Slate Furnace and Olympian Springs public road,and
south of the Big Sandy Railroad , constituting one quar-
ter of said square ,shallbe known as the firstward ; the
quarter lying west of the said Slate Furnace and Olym-
pian Springs road , and south of the said railroad,shall
be known as the second ward ; the quarter of said square
lying on the west side of the Olympian Springs road,
and north of the said railroad shall be known as the
third ward ; and the remaining quarter, lying to the east
of said Olympian Springs road, and north of the said
railroad ,shall be known as the fourth ward .
ARTICLE II.
§1 . That the prudential,fiscal,and municipalpowers Council.
of the said town shallbe vested in a board of councilmen ,
to consist of a mayor and four councilmen ,to be known
and styledthe Council of Olympia , which council and its
successorsshallbe a body-politicand corporate; and by
said name shallbe capable of contracting and being con-
tracted with, suing and being sued ,and of doing allacts
which a body -politic, having perpetual succession, can ,
in the exerciseof itspowers,lawtullyand rightfullydo.
Said council, under said name and style, is hereby in-
vested with allclaims,titles,or interestin and to allrights
of action, assets,and property held by or for the use of
said town .

policejudge, marshal, clerk, and attorney. The clerk
shallbe ex officiotreasurerand assessor,and the marshal
shallbe ex officiocollectorof taxes.
§3 . The firstelection under this act for mayor and
councilmen shallbe on the firstSaturday in August , 1882.
The persons then elected shall continue in officetillthe
thirdSaturday in January ,1883,and untiltheirsuccessors
shall duly qualify. The mayor and councilmen chosen
on the third Saturday in January , 1883, shall hold their
officesfor one year , and until their successors qual-

officers. Each ward shallelectone councilmen,and may

zen of this Commonwealth , over the age of twenty one
years,and unless he has been a resident of the county
one year ,and of the town sixtydays next preceding his

zen of this Commonwealth ,over the age of twenty -one

sixty days next before his election or appointment ,nor
unless he has been a regular licensed attorney for two
years. No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof coun-
cilman other than a male person of the age of twenty-
one years, and been a citizen of the county one year ,
and of the ward from which he is elected sixty days
preceding said election. Nor shall any one be eligi-
ble to any of the officesmentioned in this connection
who is the vendor of malt or vinous or spirituousliquors,
by license or otherwise,and no person shallbe granted
such license during the term of officefor which he may
have been elected or appointed ,although he may have
resigned .
§ 5. No person shallhave a right to vote in any of the
said electionsother than a male citizen over the age of

vious to said electionday .

ARTICLE III.
Powers and Duties of Officers.
§19 . That every person electedor appointed to an
executive, judicial, or other officeunder this act, shall,

before he enters on the duties of his office,take and sub-

§27 . He shall procure evidence in cases affectinghis

now have , and be subjectto the same liabilities,and have ,

§36 . Said town is to have authority to suppress the

CHAPTER 1055 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe selling, lending, or givingspirituous, vinous,
or malt liquorsin Port Roval precinct, in Henry county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. Itshallbe unlawful forany person to sell,lend,or
give to anotherperson in Port Royal precinct,in Henry

512 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
county ,any spirituous,vinous ,or malt liquors,or mixture
of either .
§2 . Any person violating the firstsection of this act
shall be fined sixtydollars,and each selling,lending , or
giving shallconstitutea separate offense.
§3 . Persons violating this act may be prosecuted by
warrant in the name of the Commonwealth before a
magistrate, or upon indictment of a grand jury
§ 4. It shall be the duty of the officersof the general
electionto be held in Port Royal ,in August ,1882,to open
a poll, and to ask each person voting whether he votes
for or against prohibitingthe saleof liquorin Port Royal
precinct; and if a majority of those voting upon this
question vote in favor of prohibiting the sale of liquor in
said precinct, then this act shall take effect and be in
force from and after said election .
Approved April 15, 1882 .

CHAPTER 1056
AN ACT to incorporatethe Winchester and Lexington Telephone Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Jas. H. Beauchamp , Jno. R. Allen, T. В.
Wood ,J. H. Stuart, W. M. Beckner , A. L. Haggard , and
M. C. Alford,and theirassociates,successors,and assigns,
be , and they are hereby , created a body -corporate and
politic, under the name and style of " The Winchester
and LexingtonTelephoneCompany ;" and by thatname
shallhave perpetual succession,with power to adopt and
use a common seal,to change the same at pleasure; to
contract and to be contracted with,to sue and be sued ,
and to have all the rights and privileges of a natural
person .
§2 . The capitalstockofsaidcompany shallnot exceed
the sum of fifteenthousand dollars,divided into shares of
twenty -fivedollars each ; and a book for the subscription
of stock in said company may be opened at any time
afterthe passage of thisact by the persons named herein,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 513
or any two of them , which book shall remain open until
at least fiftyshares shall be subscribed, when an organ-
ization may be effected, as is hereinafter provided ; and
aftersaid organization,itshallremain open at the option
of the company .
§3 . When fiftyshares shall have been subscribed,the
said subscribers, or a majority of them , may meet for the
purpose of electing a president and five directors to
manage theaffairsof the company . Personshaving sub-
scribed as stockholders shall be entitled to one vote for
each share of stock, and may cast the same in person or
by proxy upon any question that may come before said
president and board of directors, and upon allelections.
The president and board of directors firstelected shall
hold their offices until their successors are elected and
qualified,and a president and fivedirectorsshallannually
thereafterbe elected on the second Monday in January ,
in each year, who shallhold theirofficesforone year ,and
until their successors shall be elected and qualified.
§4. No person not a stockholderin saidcompany shall
hold any officetherein, nor shall any stockholder who
has not paid allcallsmade by the president and board of
directors on the stock subscribed or held by him , vote at
any electionor in any matter .
§ 5. The privatepropertyof the stockholdersshallnot
be subject to,nor in any way be liable for,the debts of
the company .
§ 6. The businessof saidcompany shallbe to erectPurposes.
and maintain and operate a lineof telephone from Lex-
ington,in Fayette county ,to Winchester ,in Clark county ,
and to points between said towns and in the said coun-
ties of Fayette and Clark , and between points in said
counties, and from Lexington or Winchester to Rich-
mond ,in Madison county , and from Winchester to Mt.
Sterling,in Montgomery county,and from Lexington to
any town or towns in any county adjoining Fayette
county, and between and to any points between any
such towns ; and the privilege hereby granted shall be
exclusive, and shallcontinue for twenty years .

7. The president and board of directorsmay ,if they
deem best,electa person to serve for a period not longer
than a year at a time , as a secretary and treasurer,who
shallbe a stockholder, and he shallgive bond for the
faithfulperformance of his duties, and he may at any
time be removed at the pleasure of the board .
§ 8. The president may call meetings of the board of
directors at any time , as he may think proper . Two of
the directors, with the president,or three directors,in the
absence of the president, shall constitutea quorum for
the transaction of business. The board of directorsmay
establishsuch by -laws for the government of the com-
pany as they may deem proper ,not inconsistentwith law .
The company ,and the engineers,surveyors,chain-carriers,
and employes thereof, shallhave power and authority to
enter upon the lands and inclosuresand public roads
through and over which it may be proper to run said
line; and if said company failto agree with the owner
or owners of any land over which they propose to run
said line,they may ,in the county court of the county in
which said land lies,proceed to establishtheirsaid line;
and all the provisions of the General Statutes applicable
to acquiring the right of way for roads,so far as applica-
ble,ismade part hereof. Any person who shallwillfully
or maliciously injure, obstruct, or destroy any of the
property of said company , shallbe confined in the county
jailnot lessthan three and not more than twelve months .
§ 9. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882 .

CHAPTER 1059 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe sale, loan, or gift,of spirituous,vinous, malt,and
otherintoxicatingliquorsin the town of Tattletown,in Mercer county
or within one mile thereof
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

malt ,and other intoxicating liquors,in the town of Tat-
tletown ,in Mercer county ,or within one mile thereof,be ,
and the same ishereby ,prohibited.

tion one of thisact shallbe guiltyof a misdemeanor ,and

offense,recoverable before a justiceof the peace for said
district, or by indictment by a grand jury in Mercer
county .

submitted to the qualifiedvoters of the boundary to be

§ 4 This act shallnot apply to any person who may

licenseshallexpire.

CHAPTER 1060 .
AN ACT authorizingand fixingthe time of an electionto regulatethe sale
of spirituous, vinous,or malt liquorsin the town of Clayville, in Web-
ster county .
WHEREAS , A vote taken on the 2d day of November ,
1880 , under an act , entitled " An act to regulate the sale
of spirituous, vinous, or malt liquorsin this Common-
wealth ," approved January 26th, 1874, the sale of spir-
ituous,vinous ,or malt liquorswas prohibitedin the town
of Clayville, in the county of Webster , under the pro-
visions of the said act; and whereas ,doubts exist as to
the legalityand correctness of said electionor vote, and
another vote being desired; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly ofthe Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That another election shall be held in and for the
town of Clayville,in the county of Webster ,on Saturday ,
the third day of June , 1882, according to, and under the
provisions of, an act, entitled " An act to regulate the
sale of spirituous,vinous,or malt liquorsin thisCommon-
wealth ," approved January 26th, 1874 ,title" Crimes and
Punishments ," and by the same officersand in the same
way and manner , and under the same regulations, as
directedand requiredby the provisionsof the saidact of
January 26th, 1874, and return of the poll or vote at said
election shall be made and compared as required by the
provisions of the said act.
§ 2. If it shallappear a majority of the legal votes
cast at said electionwere in favor of the sale of spirit-
uous , vinous, or malt liquorsin said town , the same shall
be certifiedto the county court of said county of Web

518 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ster, and entered upon the record of said court; and
thereupon the provisionsof the said act of January 26,
1874,shallbe repealed as to said town of Clayville,and
spirituous,vinous ,or malt liquors may thereafterbe sold
in said town in the way and manner ,and under the same
restrictionsand penalties,as fixedby law .
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882.

rules and by -laws for the government of its officers,
agents, and employes, and for the management of its
affairs; and by said name are authorized to do whatever
may be done by a natural person ,not inconsistentwith
the Constitutionand laws of the State of Kentucky , and
the Constitution and laws of the United States .
§2 . Said corporation is hereby authorized to make a
canal from some point on the Ohio river,above the falls
thereof, to some point on said river below the fallsthere-
of, with the necessary branches ,locks,dams ,basins,res-
ervoirs, & c., for the purpose of conducting a sufficient
quantity of the water of said river around or through
the city of Louisville, on the Kentucky side, to float
steam-boats, coal-boats, and all kinds of water -craft
and articles around the falls of said river, and for the
furtherpurposese of supplyingmotive power tomachinery,
manufactories , and the arts along and near said canal ,
and at and near its termini .
§3 . Said corporationishereby authorizedto acquire
and hold,in fee-simple or otherwise,a sufficientquantity
of land for said canal and its branches , locks, dams ,

520 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
basins, conduits, and reservoirs, and for such shops,
offices, warehouses , wharves ,bridges, roads,& c.,as may
be used by said corporationor itsagents or employes
in the conducting of its business ; and when the owner
or claimant of any land necessary to said corporation
cannot agree with said corporation as to the price to be
paid therefor, then the same may be condemned under
the power of the State to acquire private property for
public use on compensation being made therefor, such
taking or condemnation to be by writ of ad quod damnum
sued out from the Jefferson county court, which court
shallhave jurisdictionto trythe same by the intervention
of a jury,and from the verdict and judgment of which
an appeal shalllieto the Court of Appeals of Kentucky .
As far as practicable,the proceedings upon said writ in
said court shallconform to the provisions for the writ of
ad quod damnum in the one hundred and tenth chapter of
the General Statutes of Kentucky .
§4 . The capital stock of said corporation shall be
six million dollars, divided into sixty thousand shares
of one hundred dollars each . When one sixth of said
stock is subscribed, the incorporatorsherein named , or
one or more of them ,shallcall the stockholderstogether
at some suitableplace in the city of Louisville, by notice
published at leastten days before said meeting ,in one or
more of the dailypapers of saidycity,forthe purpose of
organizing and electingdirectorsas herein provided . At
said meeting , and at all meetings of the stockholders,
each share of stock shall entitle the holder to one vote
upon allquestionsto be voted upon by stockholders.
Said incorporators may designate and appoint one or
more of their number to receive said subscriptions.
§5 . The affairsof said corporation shallbe managed
and conducted by seven directors, four of whom shall
constitute a quorum for the transaction of business.
Said directors shall be annually elected by the stock-
holders, and each one of them shall be a stockholder-
After the firstelectionunder the callof the incorporators
the directorsshall be electedby the stockholders at their

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 521
annual meeting ,to begin on the firstMonday in May of
each year ; and the directorsso elected shall hold office
for one year , and untiltheirsuccessors are chosen .
§6 . The officersof saidcorporationshallbe a presi-
dent , vice president, secretary, treasurer, and engineer ;
the duties and responsibilitiesof each of whom shall be
prescribedand regulatedby by-laws. Each of saidoffi-
cers shall receive such compensation for his services as
may ,from time to time , be fixedby resolutionof the said
directors, and such bond and securitymay be required of
each of saidofficersby the directorsas in theirjudgment
may insurethe faithfuldischargeof duty,and the safety
of the money and the property of said corporation.
§7 . Said corporation is hereby authorized to charge
the same tolls for the passage of boats, water -craft,
and articlesthrough said canal that the Louisville and
Portland canal was authorized to charge in its charter,
adopted by the Kentucky Legislature, January twelfth,
eighteen hundred and twenty -five; and said corporation
ishereby further authorized to charge such a reasonable
rent and compensation for the use of water in moving
machinery in manufacturing , and in the mechanical and
industrialarts, and for wharfage , storage, dockage ,& c.,
as may be agreed upon between said corporation and the
partiesmaking use of said water and privileges.
§8 . The right is hereby given to said corporation to
carry its canal along any part of the bed of Beargrass
creek, Paddy's Run , or other natural water -course that
itmay be practicableto follow in itscourse around the
cityof Louisville; and the rightis hereby given to said
corporation to carry said canal and its branches across
any street, alley, railway ,turnpike road ,or stream that
may liein itscourse: Provided, That such street,alley,
railway , turnpike road, or stream shall be bridged or
tunneled by said corporation, so as to interfereas little
as possiblewith passage thereon .
§9 . And be it further enacted, That if the one -sixth of
the capitalstock required to be subscribed before organi-
zation shallbe subscribed, and the said corporation shall

-522 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
be organized,and begin work in good faithon saidcanal
within five, and complete the same in fifteen years
afterthepassage of thisact,then the said corporation,
by thisact created,shallhave the exclusive right to con-
structa canal around and within the cityof Louisville
from some pointabove the fallsof the Ohio riverto some
pointbelow saidfalls,on the Kentucky side,within ten
miles of the route that may be adopted by said corpora-
tion, for the period of fiftyyears from and afterthe time
when such work of construction shall have begun , as
herein provided .
§ 10. This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 15 , 1882 .

except thatthe electionof officersforsaidcompany need
