CHAPTER 1066 .
AN ACT to providefor working the dirtroads in Campbell county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1.The countyjudge oftheCampbellcountycourt
shall divide all of the roads in said county into and
number the districts; and as often as may be neces-
sary appoint a surveyor in each district, whose duty it
shall be to superintend the roads therein,and see that the
same is worked and kept in such repair as the agreement
with the contractorthereon with the county judge re-
quires. No surveyor shall be allowed ,unless by consent
of the court,to resign such place under two years,unless
he remove from the district. Any surveyor may be re-
moved at any time by said court.
§ 2. The clerk of the county court shall, within ten
days after the appointment of a surveyor of a road ,de
liver a copy of the order to the sheriffof the county ,
containing a fulland complete descriptionof the district,
and take his receipt therefor. The sheriffshall, within
fifteendays thereafter, delivera copy of the order and a
descriptionof the districtto the surveyor,and to return
the original to the clerk'sofficeof the Campbell county
court, with the time of the service indorsed thereon ,
which shall be evidence of the facts stated therein . The

524 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
clerk shallpost up in his office,once in every year , a list
of the names of all the surveyors in the county , desig-
nating the districtof each surveyor. The clerk or sheriff
failingto perform the duties prescribed in this section
shall be fined five dollars for each neglect .
§3 . Each surveyor of a public road shallbe furnished
with a copy of the contractforthe working of the roads in
hisdistrict. He shallreportto the countyjudge any fail-
ure on the part of the contractorthereon. He shallcertify
to thecounty judge when the contracthas been properly
complied with,which shallbe prima facieevidenceof the
fact. He shallbe exempt from jury servicesand from
polltax forroad purposesonlyduring hiscontinuancein
said office. For a failureto discharge any of his duties
prescribed in thisact,said surveyor shall be fined there-
for not exceeding twenty -five dollarsfor each offense, to
be recovered by warrant in the name of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , and the quarterly court shall have
exclusive jurisdictionof all proceedings against survey-
ors of roads .
§ 4. The county judge shall,at the court-house in the
town of Alexandria ,on the firstMonday in April in each
year , and such other times as he shallfind necessary,let
out the working and keeping in repair of the public
roads in Campbell county to the lowest and best bidder
(reservingthe rightto rejectany or allbids) for such
lengthof time as he may choose (not exceeding hisown
term of office), afterduly advertisingby posting two
printedadvertisementsin the most public placesin each
district to be so let, and one on the court-house door at
Alexandria ; said noticesto be posted at leastfifteendays
before the lettingof said contracts.
§5 . Each contractor shall execute to the Common-
wealth of Kentucky a bond , with good security, to be
approved by the county judge , in double the amount he
isto receive from his contract,forthe faithfulperformance
of said contract ; and said bond shall be filed at the next
regular term of the Campbell county court afterthe let-
ting,which shallbe notice of record,and the bonds kept
1

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 525
on fileby the clerk of the court in his officein bundles
marked " Contractor's Bonds ," and certifiedcopies of
said bonds shall be competent as evidence ; and the
county judge shall deliver to each surveyor a copy of
the contract of each contractor in each surveyor's dis-
trict. Any contractor who shallfail,in whole or in part,
to complete his contract within the time , and in the man-
ner specifiedtherein,or who ,having contracted to keep
any part of the public roads in good order for traveling
or hauling , or any bridge or culvert in good repair,and
who shallfailto do so,shall,for every failure,be deemed
guilty of a misdemeanor , and ,upon conviction,shall be
fined in any sum not to exceed one hundred dollars.
§ 6. The county judge may provide work on the county
roads of the county for such persons convicted of penal
offenses as may , by order of the proper authority, be
required to work on such roads, or to work out fines
assessed against them on the roads or streetsunder the
provisionsof general or speciallaws,and,ifnecessary,
may employ some suitableperson to oversee and guard
them while at work .
§7 . The county judge shalllet out the construction or
repairing of the bridges in Campbell county ,not included
in the agreement of any contractoron roads,or the levy
court may , in their discretion, appoint a special com-
missioner to let out any bridge or bridges, and provide
forhiscompensation therefor; and such county judge or
specialcommissioner shallbe held liableforany failure
to take sufficientbond in regardto any bridgeso letby
them ,respectively,and the court may requirebond , with
security,from the specialcommissioner .
§8. That alldelinquentroad tax-payers of Campbell
county shallbe requiredto work out theirtaxes at such
times,on roads in theirseveraldistricts,as the surveyor
thereofmay require, for which work said delinquents
shall have credit for whatever amount may be agreed
upon per day by the surveyor and contractor of the road
districtin which said work is done ,and the contractor
shallplace to the creditof the county the amount of work

526 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
performed by saiddelinquent. For failingor refusingto
comply with the ordersof saidsurveyorswhen notified,
allsuch delinquentsare made liableto the punishments
now imposed by law for persons failingor refusingto
work on roads .
§ 9. On applicationof the surveyor, it shallbe the
duty of the sheriffof the county to furnishsaid survey-
ors of the differentdistrictsof the county with a listof
the delinquent road tax -payers in their respective dis-
tricts .
§ 10. That the courts of justicesof the peace in and
for Campbell county shall have jurisdictionconcurrent
with the quarterlycourtof allproceedingsagainst road
contractors under this act; and it shall be the duty of
any justiceof the peace or county judge,upon hisown
knowledge ,or upon complaint of any surveyor, or upon
information under oath of any citizen, that any part of
the public road is out of repair, to issue his warrant
against the delinquent contractor,returnable to his own
or some other justices' court, or to the Campbell quar-
terlycourt; and upon the execution and return thereof,
such proceedings shall be had as are had in other Com-
monwealth cases. In proceedings under this act, the
defendant shall have no right to claim his district, and
both the Commonwealth and defendant shall have the
rightof appeal to the Campbell criminal court.
§ 11 It shall be the duty of the county attorney to
prosecute allwarrants under this act, and for such prose-
cutions he shallreceive twenty -five per cent, of the fines
recovered, and upon his failureto prosecute, the court
may appoint some attorney in his stead,who shallreceive
said commission for his services . All fines assessed under
this act shall be collected as other fines, and shall inure
to the benefitof the road fund of Campbell county , and
shallbe paid into the county treasury of said county .
§ 12. Itshall be unlawful for the county judge ,county
attorney, sheriff, collectorof the revenue , justice of the
peace,or any surveyor,tobecome,directlyor indirectly,
interestedin any contract for working roads or building

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 527
or repairing bridges, and itshall be unlawful for either
of said officersto buy or become interestedin any claim
growing out of said work or contract. For a violation
of thissection, eitherof said officersshallbe guilty of a
misdemeanor ,and ,upon indictmentand convictionthere-
of,shallbe finedin any sum not to exceed fivehundred
dollars. This section shall be given in charge to the
grand jury by the judge of the Campbell county criminal
court .
§13 . Any person who shall willfullyor negligently
injure, destroy, or obstructany of the public roads or
bridges,or any of the culverts or ditches on said road ,
he shall be fined in any sum not to exceed two hundred
dollars,to be collectedin like manner as the finespre-
scribedherein for surveyors, and shall also be liable in
double damages to the county, or any person aggrieved
or injured,to be recovered in any court having jurisdic-
tion of the amount . It shall be the duty of the survey-
ors to report promptly to the county judge any violation
of this section .
§ 14. The county court,a majority of the justicesagree-
ing,shall, on some regular county court day , assessa tax
of not more than fifteen cents on each one hundred dol-
lars' worth of property assessed for revenue purposes ,
and a capitation tax not exceeding two dollarson each
person in said county required by the general law to
work on roads .
§ 15. The ad valorem and capitationtax provided for in
section fourteen of this act shall be collected as other
revenue due the county is collected, and under the same
obligationsby the collectingofficer,and shallbe paid into
the county treasuryby said collectingofficer, taking the
treasurer'sreceipt therefor, which shall be evidence of
the facts stated therein .
§ 16. When any contractor shall have completed his
contract on any road or bridge,he shallreport the facts,
in writing,to the surveyor of the districtin which said
road or bridge is located. It shall then be the duty of
saidsurveyor to inspectsaid work ,and ifperformed in

528 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
accordance with the contract,he shallcertifythe same to
the county judge ,who shall then give to said contractor
an order on the county treasurerfor the amount specified
in the contract .
§ 17. Chapter ninety-four of the General Statutes is.
hereby adopted and made part of this act,except when it
comes in conflictwith this provision of this act; and all
other road laws for Campbell county are hereby repealed.
§ 18. This act shallnot apply to Newport , Dayton ,
Belleview ,Alexandria , California, Highland district, and
Hayfield district,corporations in said county , except that
the authorityto levy said tax shallapply to said districts
of Highland and Hayfield .
§19 . This act shall take effect and be in force from
and afterthe firstday of April, 1883.
Approved April 15, 1882.

CHAPTER 1075 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled" An act to incorporatethe Cynthiana
and Millersburg Turnpike Company ."

of Kentucky :
WHEREAS , It is represented that the bridge on the line
of the Cynthiana and Millersburg pike, over Hinkston
creek,in Bourbon county ,is very much out of repair,and
that it may take three thousand five hundred dollarsto
repair or rebuildthe same , and that the company has no
funds on hand with which to do said work ; for remedy
whereof ,it is enacted :

road be ,and they are hereby ,authorized to borrow a sum
of money sufficientto build or repair said bridge, not ex-
ceeding three thousand fivehundred dollars,and to pledge
said road and itsfranchisesfor the payment of the same
by executing a mortgage on said road and itsfranchises
for the payment thereof. They may bind the company
for intereston the sum they borrow at any rate not ex-
ceeding eight per cent. per annum , payable annually .
§2 . If said president and directorsdetermine to borrow
the money for the purpose aforesaid,and do borrow it,
they may execute a mortgage in the name of the com-
pany ,by itspresidentand treasurerand advisorymem-
ber of said corporation; and when executed by them ,
and recorded in the county clerks officesat Paris and
Cynthiana,shallbe effectualforthe purpose of pledging
said road and its tranchises for the payment of the sum
so borrowed .
§3 . The officers named in the second section of this
act are authorized to proceed at once to have the work
done on said bridge necessary to make itsafe, eitherby
repairing or rebuilding,and to that end shall advertise
and let to the lowest bidder the work to be done .
§4. This actshalltake effectfrom itsapproval.
Approved April 15, 1882 .

CHAPTER 1076 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe New Schooland Hall Companies College, at
Trenton ,Todd county , Kentucky .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That E. G. Sebree,G. E. Garth, J. S. Dickinson,
C. W. Ware , J. D. Gillis, and theirsuccessorsin office,
be, and they are hereby,constituteda body-politicand
corporate,by the name and style of the Trustees of the
New School and Hall Companies College, at Trenton,
Todd county,Kentucky ,and by thattitleto plead and be
impleaded; to acquire,to hold,and to convey property,
real,personal, and mixed , and to have and to use a com-
mon seal .
§2 . Be itfurtherenacted, That a majorityof the trus-
tees in officeshall, at all times, constitutea quorum , and
may meet at such placesas they may selectfortransact-
ing business. The said trustees,in conjunction with the
stockholders of the said college, shall, by vote, fillall

act the business of the college, and shallprescribe their

sary to insure a faithfulperformance of the same ; said

after its passage .

CHAPTER 1077 .
AN ACT to authorizethe Auditor,with the consent of the Attorney Gen-
eraland Governor , to compromise with B. F. Gibson ,latesheriffof Hen-
derson county,and his sureties,for the revenue of said county for1878,
collectedby saidGibson,and not paid to the Auditor.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts , with the
assent of the Governor and Attorney General ,be , and he
ishereby , authorized and empowered to make such com-
promise or other arrangement with B. F. Gibson , late
sheriffof Henderson county ,and his sureties,for the rev-
enue for the year 1878, collectedby said Gibson , and not
accounted forto the Auditor ,as in his judgment is to the
best interestof the State. He shall have the right to
give said parties reasonable time to pay the amounts
agreed upon in any such compromise , to receivecash,
take theirnotes or bonds ,payable to the Commonwealth ,
bearing legalinterestfrom date ,and to be due at such time
or times as may be agreed upon ,not exceeding two years
from the date thereof; and such notes or bonds may be
secured by mortgage or deeds of trustupon unencum-
bered real estateor personal property, or by personal
security; or if no such mortgage or deed of trustor per-.
sonal securitycan be obtained,or when the same is in-
sufficientto secure the amount agreed upon as aforesaid,
he may accept the assignment or transfer of any note,
bond , bill, or chose in action , and the amount realized

536 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
thereon shallbe appliedto the payment of the demand
the same was intended to secure . But the Common-
wealth shall not be held or required to exercise more
than reasonable diligencein the collectionof any of the
aforesaid evidences of debt, and only the net amount
realized thereon shallbe credited upon such demand .
§2.All mortgagesand deeds of trusttaken by the
Auditor in pursuance of this act shallbe recorded in the
same manner and at thesame place as though executed
to private individuals ; and all costs attending the exe-
cutionand recordingthereofshallbe paid by the parties
executing the same .
§3 . All actions and proceedings to enforce the collec-
tion of any such bonds ,notes,bills,or choses in action,
or to enforce the lien by any such mortgage , or to sell
any property embraced by any such deed of trustas may
have been accepted by the Auditor in pursuance of this
act, shallbe the same as presented by law in similar
actions or proceedings between individuals,except that
the Franklin circuitcourt,at eitherof the fiscalor regu-
lar terms,shallhave jurisdictionthereof,concurrent with
the circuit court of the Commonwealth of the counties
where the lands may lie, embraced in any mortgages or
deeds of trustexecuted by the parties aforesaid.
§ 4. The provisions of chapter 44 of the General Stat-
utes shallnot apply to any assignment or conveyance or
deed of trustmade and executed under the provisions of
this act,but all such shall be valid and enforceable against
all and every person ,except such as in good faith held
liens upon the property so assigned or conveyed at the
time of such assignment , conveyance , or transfer was
made .
§5 . Any compromise under this act with any one or
more of the suretiesof said B. F. Gibson ,shall not oper-
ate to release any other of said suretieswho may failor
refuse to make compromise and settlement as herein
provided .
§6. As many suitshave been institutedand are pend-
ing in the Henderson circuitcourt against said B. F.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 537
Gibson and his sureties to enforce the collection of the
judgment rendered against them in favor of the Com-
monwealth , thisact shallin nowise affectthe compensa-
tion of the attorneys prosecuting said suits on behalf of
the State,but they shallreceivethe percentage agreed
upon of all amounts realized by compromise under this
act .
§7 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1882 .

CHAPTER 1080 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Alton and Camdensville Turnpike Road Com-
pany , in Anderson county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That a company be ,and the same ishereby ,created
a body -politicand corporate,under the name and styleof
the Alton and Camdensville Turnpike Road Company ,in
Anderson county,Kentucky ,and by said name and style
shall have perpetual succession, and the power to con-
tractand be contracted with, sue and be sued ,plead and
be impleaded,have and use a common seal,and allother
powers given by the general law to turnpikeroad com-
panies .
§2 . That said company is hereby empowered to con-
structa turnpike road on the macadamized plan from

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 541
some point on the Hardinsville and Crab Orchard Turn-
pike Road ,between the dwelling-houses of Major John
Draffin and Wm . Wilson , situated each about one half
of a mile from the town of Alton , in Anderson county ,
by the most practicableand available route to the Cam-
densville Turnpike Road , leading from the town of
Lawrenceburg to Camdensville and Van Buren ; said
road to pass near the farm of Jno . W. Major and Jeffer-
son Young .
§3 .Jas B. Catlett,JeffersonYoung ,Jas. Cole,John W. Commissioners.
Major , Alvin Major , and Merit Young are hereby ap-
pointed commissioners , any or all of whom may open
books for the subscriptionof stock to said company ,the
stock to be in shares of twenty -fivedollars each , and the
obligation of subscribersthereto shallbe binding upon
said subscribers , and in substance as follows : " We ,
whose names are hereto subscribed and affixed,promise
to pay to the Alton and Camdensville Turnpike Road
Company the several amounts set opposite our names at
such times and in such sums as required by the board of
directorsof said company ."
§4 . Whenever the sum of one thousand dollars shall
be subscribed in stock,said commissioners ,or any three
of them, may call a meeting of such subscribers, to be
held at such time and place as they may designate ; and
at such meeting a president,five directors, and a treas-
urer shallbe elected for the period of one year ,and until
their successors are elected and qualified. The stock-
holders may change the time of their annual meetings ,a
majority of the voters concurring .
§5 . The capitalstock of said company shallnot exceed Capitalstock.
twenty thousand dollars, to be divided into shares of
twenty -fivedollarseach . Subscriptions may be made to
said company in rock, timber,or work upon said road,
and the amount and value of such subscriptionsshallbe
expressed in the subscription, and shallbe binding , and if
they are not furnished to the company on demand , the
value as expressedmay be recoveredfrom the subscribers
in money .

§ 6. The said president and directorsshallhave power
to passby-laws,rulesand regulationsfortheirown gov-
ernment of the affairsof said company and itsofficers
that they may deem necessary,not inconsistentwith this
act or with the Constitution of this State or the Constitu-
tion of the United States.
§7 . The width and grade of said road shallbe deter-
mined by said president and directors,as alsothat portion
to be covered by metal or stone, and the thickness there-
of; and said presidentand directorsshalldetermine when
and in what manner said road ,or any part thereof, shall
be let out for construction .
§8 . That said company shallhave the power to acquire
the right of way and quarries, and land for toll-houses
and toll-gate purposes , or for any other purpose neces-
sary in the construction of said road,by contract or agree-
ment with the parties, or by writ of ad quod damnum ,as
provided by law , in the event they cannot agree ; and
may charge tollat the rate fixedby law for fivemiles, or
at the same rate for any fractionalpart thereof; and said
company is authorized to erect a gate and collecttoll
when one half of said road is completed and ready for
travel , or when two and one half miles thereof is com-
pleted .
§9 . Whenever it is shown to the satisfactionof the
Anderson county court, including the justices of the
peace for said county, or a majority thereof, that the
subscriptionsto said road are sufficientto build and con-
structthe same for the distance of fiveconsecutive miles,
said court shall subscribe one thousand dollars to each
mile, which by said court shall be considered as a part of
the subscription: Provided, That a majorityof allthe
qualified voters in Anderson county , at some general
election, shall have voted said tax of one thousand dol-
lars to the mile to said road .
§ 10. The county judge of Anderson county is hereby
authorized and directed,at the next general election,at
each of the voting places in said county ,to cause a poll
to be opened and the vote to be taken on the question of
1

the county court subscribing one thousand dollars to the
mile , as set out in section nine of this act ; and if a ma-
jority of the legalvotes are cast in favor of said subscrip-
tion, which factmust be shown by the certificateof the
personsauthorizedby law to compare the poll-books in
electionsfor county officers,the county court,the justices
of the peace being present,shalllevy a tax upon the tax-
able propertyin Anderson county sufficientto pay the
subscription aforesaid of one thousand dollars to the
mile: Provided, They only levy a tax for fivemiles in
any one year. If a majorityof the votesare not castin
favor of the tax at the firstelection when it is submitted ,
the county judge is directed hereby to submit the ques-
tion , or cause it to be submitted , in the manner above
stated, at each general election for the space of five
years: Provided, The company of said road,in writing,
request it.
§11 . The sheriffof the county shall,when said tax is
levied , collect the same , and account for it in his settle-
ment with the county court,in the same manner as he
does for the county levy, and receive the same fees for
collecting; and his suretiesshallbe liablefor the turnpike
tax as well as the county levy.
§ 12. The county court shall cause to be paid to said
company as much as four hundred dollars to each one
half of a mile of said road, as soon as itiscompleted and
receivedby the court'scommissioner,who shallbe ap-
pointed by the court,with the justicesof the peace , or a
majoritythereof,concurringin said appointment; and if

while the road isbeing constructed as much as sixty per
cent. of the whole sum subscribedby the court: Pro-
vided,The work done on said road amounts to as much
as eighty per cent,of the whole amount of work neces-
sary for itscompletion ; and when the whole of any one
mile is completed and received by the county court and
justicesof the peace of said county , the county court
shallorder that the whole subscriptionto the mile shall
be paid over to said company .

width .

CHAPTER 1081 .

of Kentucky :

or work to construct a turnpike road from or near the

creek, on the line between the counties of Washington
and Anderson , of a like width of grade and thickness
metal as is required by the charter to which this is an
amendment .
§2 . That it shall be lawful, and the county judge of
Anderson county is hereby directed, to authorize and
compel , at any regular or general electionin the year
1882, at the regular voting precincts in said county , to
cause the proper officersof said electionto open a poll
to take the sense of the qualifiedvoters at each of said
precincts,whether they willappropriateto saidcompany
the sum of $ 1,000 dollars to the mile to construct said
road or not; and upon comparison by those authorized to
compare the vote in the general electionsin thisState, if
it is found that a majority of the qualifiedvoters of the
county have been castforthe appropriationof one thou-
sand dollars,per mile,then the county judge , with the
justicesof the peace for said county, shalllevy an ad
valorem tax upon thetaxablepropertyinAnderson county
sufficienttocompletesaidroad: Provided,Itshallappear

to the satisfactionof the county judge and justicesof the
peace of Anderson county that there are private subscrip-
tions enough ,together with the one thousand dollars to
the mile,to complete said road from and to the points
mentioned in the firstsection of thisact,in the manner
and stylerequiredin the originalcharter,to which thisis
an amendment .
§3 . When the levy is made ,the money collectedmen-
tioned in the foregoingsection of this act, the same
shallbe paid over to the board of the company mentioned
in the firstsectionof thisact,when the road iscompleted
and received by the county court of Anderson county .
§ 4. The tax imposed and required to be collectedby
the thirdsectionof thisactshallbe collectedby thesheriff
of Anderson county ,and he shallaccount for the same in
his settlement with the county court. He shallhave the
same fees forcollectingthe turnpike tax as is allowed by
law for collecting the State revenue .
§5 This act extends the time for five years from its
passage,allowingthe company of saidFox Creek,John .
sonville,and Burnt Church Turnpike Road to receivesub-
scriptionsto said road,and they may reportthe amount
of subscriptionto build said road at any time during the
fiveyears ; and if they are sufficient, with the one thou-
sand dollars to the mile, in the opinion of the county
court,to build and complete said road ,then the county
judge shall, with the concurrence of a majority of the
justices of the peace of said county , direct the money
voted by the county to be paid over,as requiredin sec-
tion three of this act .
§ 6. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage..
Approved April 15, 1882 .

ernment of itsofficers,members , agents, and employes;
and may alter or amend or change the same ,from time
to time,under such rules as they may establishfor that
purpose .
§ 5. The officersof thisassociationshallbe president,
vice president, secretary, assistant secretary, treasurer,
and such other officers, agents and employes , as the
board of managers may deem necessary and convenient
forthe proper prosecution of the business of the associa-
tion; allof whom shallbe appointed by the board of man-
agers at such time ,and for such terms,as they may fixin
the by -laws of the association,whose pay or compensa-
tion shallalsobe fixedby the board of managers .
§6 . If any of the officers, agents or employes , shall
unlawfullyappropriateany of the funds of said associa-
tion to his own use,or shallwillfullyfailto make correct
entries,or knowingly make falseones,on the books,ac-
counts , or reports of said association, with intent to
defraud the associationor any member thereof,or to con-
ceal the improper appropriation of funds ,the person or
personsso offendingshallbe deemed guiltyof felony,
and on conviction thereof shallbe imprisoned in the pen-
itentiaryfor not less than one year nor more than six
years,at the discretionof the jury.
§7 . The association shall have its principal officeor
Principaloffice. place of business in the city of Hopkinsville ,county of
Christian,State of Kentucky ; and may establishagencies

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 551
or branch officesin any of the citiesor towns in the
United States and in the Dominion of Canada .
§ 8. The associationishereby authorized to receive,by
gift,purchase , or otherwise,and to own , control,use,and
enjoy any and all real and personal property or estate
necessary and convenient for properly prosecuting the
particularbusiness of the said association,and to selland
convey the same when the interestsof the association
require it; but nothing in this charter shall be construed
as conferringbanking privilegeson said association.
§ 9. The charter of said associationshallbe perpetual ,
and the said association is hereby declared not to be a
lifeinsurance company in the sense and meaning of the
general lifeinsurance laws of this Commonwealth ,and
shallforever be exempt from the provisions of said gen-
eral insurance laws .
§ 10. The associationbeing of a benevolent character,
it shall be free from all license fees .
§ 11. The privatepropertyof themembers of saidasso-
ciation shall not be liable for the debts of the association ,
nor fordebts of any of itsofficers.
§ 12. This act shall take effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 17 , 1882.
CHAPTER 1087 .

county .

of Kentucky :

scribedand provided for in thisact.

552 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
keeping untilthe owner of such stockshallhave redeemed
them by the payment of all fees and costs incident to
said procedure .
§3 . That whenever any stockshallbe found trespass.
ing upon the inclosure of any person ,the owner of such
stock shallbe liablefor alldamages done ,and for allfees
and costsincident to the prosecution of such trespass, re-
coverablebefore any officerhaving competent jurisdic-
tion,and the party injured shall have a lien upon such
stock for alldamages ,costs,and fees.
§4 . That the officer shall be entitled to the following
fees for taking up stock running at large or trespassing
upon the lands of any one : For each horse,jack,mule ,
or coltover sixmonths old,one dollar; for each bull,cow ,
steer,heifer,or calf over six months old, fiftycents; for
each sheep or hog over three months old, twenty-five
cents,and all expenses incurred by him in impounding
and taking care of the same : Provided, The owner of
said stock is not known to be in immediate pursuit of
same .
§5 . That the officermaking the seizure shalladvertise
allstock seized by him (if not redeemed within fivedays
afterthe seizureof same ) forsalein the precinctten days
before selling. The sale shall be made on a credit of
threemonths,the purchaser giving bond,with approved
security,payable to the officermaking the sale,who shall
be entitled to receive the same commission and fees as
are allowed by law for sales under execution for debt ;

residue, afterall expenses are paid ,to the owner of said

bond .
§ 6. That this act shall be submitted to the qualified
votersof Berlinprecinctat the next August election,and
itshallbe the duty of the officersof saidelectionto open

pared , as they are required in other elections, and if it

take effect; but if the examining board report to said
judge that a majority voted against said stock law ,this
shall not be in force .

CHAPTER 1096 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act to incorporatethe town of
Osceola, in Green county.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the police judge of Osceola shallhave juris-
diction in said town and town district of civil causes to
the same extent that justicesof the peace now have , or
may hereafterhave , in this State; and shallhave the
same jurisdictionof crimes and misdemeanors committed
in said town and town districtas two justices of the
peace now have ; and shall have fulljurisdictionwithin
said town of alloffenses committed against the by -laws
of said town ; and shall have exclusive power to enter
judgments and issue executions for allfinesand penalties
forsuch offenses; he shallhave power to grant attach-
ments and injunctions in the same extent that two jus-
ticesof thepeace have ,or thatthe county judge has; it
shallbe the duty of the policejudge to keep a recordof
his proceedings ,copies of which shall be evidence to the
same extent and for the same purposes that copies of
justices' recordsnow are; he shallhave power to issue
subpœnas for witnessesor other processto compel the
attendance of witnessesbefore him ,and to punish all
contempts against his authorityby finenot exceeding
five dollars in each case; he shall have the power to
order the marshal or other officerto summon a jury in
cases cognizable before him , where a jury is required
by law; he shallhave power to take and certifydepo.
sitionsas justicesof the peace and examiners now have ,
which shallbe allowed to be read as depositions are now
allowed to be read which are taken and certifiedby such
justicesor examiners ; he shallbe allowed and entitledto
the same fees as county judges and justicesare allowed
forsimilarservices,and he shallhave power to collectin
the same way thatjusticesnow collecttheirfees;he shall
have power to appoint a special deputy marshal ,in the
absence of the town marshal ,to execute a warrant of ar-
restfor a criminal offense; he shall hold a regular court

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 561
in the town of Osceola ,for the purpose of trying allcivil
process that may be returned before him , on the fourth
Saturday in the months of March ,June,September,and
December in each year ,which may be changed ,however ,
by the county judge to some other days in saidmonths ,
when he may deem proper . Appeals from alljudgments
rendered by said police judge in civilcases shallbe
allowed to any party,under the same rulesand regula-
tions, and to the same tribunals, as appeals are or may
be allowed from judgments of justices of the peace in
like cases .
§2 . The town marshal of said town shall execute a
bond such as is required to be executed by constables
of the State of Kentucky ; and he shallhave the same
powers and perform the same duties, and be liable to
the same penalties,that constables are now by law or
may hereafter be authorized to perform or may be sub-
jected to.
§3 . That when a vacancy shall occur in the officeof
town marshal of said town ,the policejudge of said town
shallfillsaid vacancy by appointment,which appointee
shall continue in office until the next election for town
officers .
§ 4. This act to take effectfrom and after itspassage .
Approved April 17, 1882.

gift,purchase ,or otherwise, and to own ,control,use,and
enjoy any and allreal and personalpropertyor estate
necessary and convenient for properly prosecuting the
particular business of the association, and to sell and
convey the same when the interestof the associationre-
quire it; but nothing in thischarter shall be construed as
conferringbanking privilegeson said association.
§9 The charter of the said association shall be per-
petual; and said associationis hereby declared not to be
a lifeinsurance company in the sense and meaning of the
general lifeinsurance laws of this Commonwealth , and
shallforever be exempt from the provisions of said gen-
eral insurance laws .
§ 10. This association being of a benevolent character ,
it shall be free from all license fees .
§11 . The private property of members shall not be
liablefordebtsof the association,nor forthedebtsof any
of its officers .
§ 12. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 17 , 1882 .

CHAPTER 1105 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the town of Beach Grove , in McLean
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the charter of the town of Beech Grove , in
McLean county , be so amended as to authorize the
trustees of said town to grant coffee-house license to
any applicant for the sale of spirituous,vinous ,or malt
liquors, in any quantity; but such applicant shall be a
person of good character, and a bona fide resident of
McLean county .
§2 . Before any license shall be granted to any person
tokeep a coffee-house,as providedin sectionone of this
act,the applicant thereforshall execute bond with good
security,in such penalties as the trustees of said town
may prescribe,conditioned that he keep an orderlyhouse ,
and obey the laws of the State.
§3 . The trusteesare authorized to charge and collect
a tax of not less.than twenty -five nor more than one
hundred dollarson each license issued under the provis-
ions of this act , and no license shall be issued until the
feesare paid: Provided, That the applicantshallpay the
State tax required by law before any license shall be
issued .
§4 . That said trusteesmay grant to any person or per-
sons a license to open a bowling or ten-pin alley who
may have complied with the laws of the State made and
provided in such cases; and said trusteesshallfixthe tax
for the same in any sum not lessthan twenty -fivenor more
than one hundred dollars. Said tax must be paid to said

568 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
trusteesbefore any licenseshallbe issued,and the license
of the State must be first obtained .
§5 . Said board of trustees are vested with power to
license and tax, and to provide by ordinance for licensing
and taxing, within the limits of said town , all shoot-
ing-galleries, all auction sales, shows , and exhibitions;
and shallby ordinance enforce the taking out said license
by fixingfinesand penaltiesagainst allpersons failingor
refusingto do so: Provided, No license shall be granted
for any games origaming inconsistentwith the laws of
this Commonwealth . All shows which exhibit within a
half mile of said town shallpay tax therein provided for.
§ 6. Itshallbe the duty of the board of trusteesto open
and keep the sidewalks and streetsin repair; and so far
as the money obtainedby taxation,fines,and otherways ,
collectedin pursuance of judgments of policecourtfor
a violation of by -laws and ordinances of said town , all
such sums shallbe held by the trustees, and shallbe used
for the above purposes , and for other improvements that
may ,from time to time ,be necessary; and said trustees
shall elect a supervisor of streets, who shall certifyall
male residentsof said town between the ages of sixteen
and fiftyyears to work said streets and alleys of said
town , not exceeding two days in each month , and all of
said residentsare exempt from working any road outside
of thecorporatelimitsof saidtown ; and any personfail-
ing to work said streetsand alleys,when properly noti-
fied,shallbe finedtwo dollarsfor each day he so fails,
to be collected as other fines .
§ 7. This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage.
Approved April 17, 1882.

CHAPTER 1107 .
AN ACT to authorize the construction and maintenance of a court -house in
Newport , in Campbell county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1. The judge of the circuitcourtin Campbell county,
shall,by an order of said court, appoint three commis-
sioners , resident of the districthereinafter described , who
shall hold their officesat the pleasure of said judge .
Before entering upon the discharge of their duties,said
commissioners shall execute a satisfactorybond , to be
approved by saidjudge ,for the faithfuldischarge of their
duties, and the proper disbursement of all moneys im-
posed upon by and coming into theirhands under the

-570 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
provisionsof thisact. They shallreceivea compensa-
tion for theirservices,to be fixed by said judge , not ex-
ceeding $ 100 each per annum .
"
§2 . The following described territoryshallconstitutea
separate district, to be styledthe " Court -house District,"
forthe purposes statedin thisact,as follows: Beginning
at a point on the Ohio river,in the center of Four Mile
creek,where the same empties into the Ohio river; thence
with said creek as it meanders to the bridge on the Twelve
Mile Turnpike ,crossingsaid creek; thence with the county
road leading from said bridge, in the direction of the
Alexandria turnpike, to a point where a road known as
Winter's lane intersects said county road ; thence with
said Winter's lane road to the Alexandria pike ; thence ,
crossing said pike, and with the same to a point where
the county road intersectssaid pike on the west side
thereof,just south of the Licking Baptist Church ; thence
with said county road to the Licking turnpike; thence,
crossingsaidturnpikewith saidcounty road,to the Lick-
ing river, near the mouth of Pool's creek; thence with
Licking riverto the Ohio river; thence up the Ohio river
to the mouth of Four Mile creek .
§3 . Said commissioners shallhave power to construct
and maintain , on the site where the court -house now
stands, in the cityof Newport , a suitablecourthouse
at a cost not exceeding $ 50,000. For that purpose said
commissioners shall issue bonds , with interestcoupons
attached,in denominations of $ 500 each ,bearing interest
at the rate of five per cent per annum , payable semi-
annually. Said bonds shallbe numbered from 1 to 100,
and shall be issued as tollows : The first four shall be
payable in one year ; the second four in two years , and
so on in the same order to the end of the issue. They
shallbe made payableat the officeof thecitytreasurer
of the cityof Newport , and shall be exempt from taxa-
tion for allpurposes except State tax . For the purpose
of paying the interest,and redeeming said bonds as they
mature,said commissionersshallannuallylevy a tax on
the real and personal property in said district, not ex-
,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 571
ceeding twelve cents on the $ 100 on theStatevaluation
thereof , which shall be a lien thereon . It shall be the
duty of the sheriffof Campbell county to collectsaid tax
in the same manner and at the same time that he collects
the State revenue ,and he shallreceive the same compen-
sation for his services as for the collection of said State
revenue . Before entering upon the discharge of the
duties imposed upon him by this act, said sheriffshall
execute to said commissioners a good and sufficientbond ,
to be approved by the judge of the circuitcourt of Camp-
bell county , for the faithfuldischarge of his duties. He
shall pay said moneys so collectedby him to the city
treasurer of the city of Newport ,who is hereby author-
ized to receive the same . Said treasurer shall disburse
said moneys so as aforesaid received,as directed by said
commissioners . Before said treasurer shall enter upon
the discharge of the dutieshereby imposed upon him ,he
shall execute a like bond in likemanner ,and to be ap-
proved in the same way ,as the sheriffisrequiredto do .
§ 4. Out of the proceeds of said levy and collections,
said commissioners shallfirstpay the interestdue on said
bonds ; second, the cost of maintaining said court-house ,
includingthe salariesof the county judge and county
attorney, as pro ratal in the proportion that saiddistrict
bears to the whole county ; third, the necessary expenses
incurredin carryingintoeffecttheprovisionsof thisact.
§ 5. Said sheriff and treasurer shall each make an an-
nual report of theirproceedings to the commissioners
aforesaid ; and said commissioners shall make an annual
reportof theirproceedings,includingthe reportsof said
sheriffand treasurer,to the judge of the circuitcourt in
Campbell county .
§6 . The citizensliving within the districtabove de-
scribed shall hereafter be exempt from the payment of a
poll-tax, and the property within said districtshallbe
exempt from all taxation except for State revenue ,for
county roads,for taking care of the poor,courtand jail
expenses, and the Highland district, and the citiesof
Newport and Dayton and the town of Bellevue,forthe
purposes now authorizedby latv.

572 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§7 . All deeds , mortgages ,leases, and conveyances for
the sale,transfer,pledge,or leaseof propertywithinsaid
district,shallbe recorded in the city of Newport , and for
allproperty outside of said districtsaid deeds ,mortgages ,
leases, and conveyances shallbe recorded at Alexandria .
§8 . For thepurposesof collectingthetaxesheretofore
authorized to be levied, said sheriffis hereby invested
with all the powers now conferred upon him by law in
the collectionof the State revenue ; and for any delin-
quency in the payment of said tax so levied, he shall
proceed by levy, distraint,and sale of property, as now
authorized by law ; and the purchaser of property at such
sale shall acquire and be invested with the same right
and titlethat purchasers at such sales for delinquent
State revenue are now invested with .
9. The county clerk of Campbell county shall, ex
officio,act as clerk for said commissioners , and shallkeep
a complete record of their proceedings,in a book which
shall be and remain as a part of the archives of his
office .
§10 All acts and parts of acts in conflictwith the pro-
visionsof thisact are hereby repealed.
§1 . This act shall take effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage ,but the bonds herein provided for shallnot be issued,
nor shallsaid tax be levied or collected,untilthe question
shall have been submitted to the voters residing within
said district at the next regular August election , and a
majority of the voters voting at said election shallhave
voted in favor of said issue and said levy and collection
of said tax .
§ 12. Said districtshallprovide for and take care of its
own poor ,and all persons within said districtwho are
held to answer to the criminal court , or who are com-
mitted to jail upon any criminal or penal charge , shall
be held to said criminal court , and committed to the jail
in the cityof Newport ; and on allsimilarcases outside of
said districtsuch persons shall be held to courts at Alex-
andria ,
§13 . Said commissioners are hereby constituteda body-
corporateand politic,under the name and styleof the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 573
Commissioners for the Court -house District;" and as
such shall have perpetual succession; may contract and
be contracted with; may sue and be sued ,and may plead
and be impleaded ,in the courts of thisCommonwealth .
§ 14. For the purpose of ascertainingthe willof the
voters of said district, the county clerk shall, for each
voting precinct in said district, and for the Cold Spring
districtcause to be made on each poll-book two separate
columns , one headed , " For the Court -house Tax ," and
the other, " Against the Court-house Tax ." The judges
at said election shall ask each voter how he votes upon
said proposition, and the clerk shall record his vote as
given : Provided, That at the Cold Spring district, only
such persons shall vote upon the above proposition as
actually reside within the boundaries of the districthere-
in established .
§ 15. The same board now authorized by law to count
and compare the vote at said electionshall,at the same
time and place ,and in the same manner ,count and com-
pare the vote hereby authorized to be taken . It shall
make out and certify, in writing, to the judge of the
Campbell circuitcourt, at Newport , the result of said
election. If,by said report,itshallappear that a majority
of the voters voting at said election,in said district, are
in favor of said tax , said circuitjudge shallat once ap-
point said commissioners , as hereinbefore provided for.
Approved April 17, 1882 .
CHAPTER 1109 .
AN ACT authorizingthe Carter county court to levy an ad valorem tax in
said county,and the parts of Boyd and Elliottcountiestaken from Carter,
to pay a debt due George R. McKee .
WHEREAS , By an act approved March 20th, 1880, the
county court of Carter county was authorizedto levy a
tax not exceeding twenty cents on each one hundred
dollars' worth of taxable property in the county of Car-
ter, and those parts of Boyd and Elliottcounties taken
from Carter,as shown by the assessor'slistsforthe year
1880 ,and providing for the collectionof the same ,for the

574 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
purpose of paying Hon .George R. McKee an attorney's
fee. The tax was levied ,but not collected because of the
failureof the county court to obtain a collectoras pro-
vided for in said act; for remedy whereof ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Carter county be , and it
ishereby ,authorized and empowered to levy a tax of not
exceeding twenty cents on each one hundred dollars'
worth of taxable propertyin the county of Carter,and
those parts of Boyd and Elliottcounties taken from Car-
tercounty , as may be shown by the assessor'slistsfor the
year 1882,to pay George R. McKee the sum of twenty-
five hundred dollars, and the interestthat has or may
accrue thereon as heretofore allowed by the order of the
Carter county court.
§2 . The Carter county court may levy said tax at its
May term , 1882,and if enough shall not be realized by
the collectionsfrom such levy,may make a similar levy
at the May term, 1883, sufficientto pay any balance in
full of said debt and interest .
§3 . That the tax leviedpursuant to thisact shallbe
collectedby the sheriffof Carter county within said
county ,and by the sheriffsof the counties of Boyd and
Elliott,each within theirrespectivecounties,in the parts
thereof taken from the county of Carter.
§4 . That the Carter county court shall, immediately
after the levy of the tax as herein authorized, cause a
certifiedcopy of itsorderlevyingsuch tax to be delivered
to the respectivesheriffsof said counties. The said sher-
iff'sshallhave the same power to collectsaidtax by dis-
traint or otherwise as provided by law in the collection
of Staterevenue tax,and shallpay the same over to the
person or persons designated by the Carter county court
to receive the same ; and the same shallbe due and pay-
able at the same periods of time and in likemanner as
State revenue tax. For any failureto make payment as
required,the sheriffin default,and his sureties,shallbe
liableon the sheriff'sgeneral bond for the amount due ,
(

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 575
and twenty per cent. damages thereon; and the same
may be recovered by an action in the Carter circuitcourt
by the receiverhereinafterprovided,by action on the bond
of the sheriff in default .
§5 . The county court of Carter county shallappoint a
receiver, to whom the sheriffsshallpay the tax collected;
and take from such receiver bond with good personal
suretyforthe safe-keeping and payment of the moneys
received by him ,and the disbursement thereof as herein
provided ; and may allow such receiver a reasonable
compensation ,not exceeding three per cent, on the sum
received and disbursed , and in case of suits against sher-
iffs,the reasonable attorneys' fees that may be incurred.
§6 . The county court shall, by its order, direct the
receiverto pay over the money ,when received by him , to
George R. McKee , or to his order or assigns, until the
debt to him ,with itsinterest,is extinguished; and if any
sum shallremain in excess,the same shallbe paid by the
receiver, upon order of the Carter county court, on the
bonds or interest thereon of the railroad debt of Carter
county ; but the debt and interestof McKee shallbe first
paid , and itshallnot be lawful to apply the funds raised
by the tax hereby authorized until the said debt and in-
terestisfullypaid.
§7 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 17, 1882.

CHAPTER 1119 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Heywood Academy of Louisville, Kentucky .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
"
§1 . That Rev. John H. Heywood ; Dr. T. S. Bell,
Gavin H. Cochran , Galen T. Thurman , Dr. David P.
Middleton , trustees,and their associates and successors,
be , and they are hereby ,created a body -politicand cor-
porate , by the name of the Heywood Academy of
Louisville, Kentucky ;" with perpetual succession; for
the purpose of educating young ladies; and in the name
of " The Heywood Academy " shall be capable of con-
tracting and being contracted with , of suing and being
sued , of pleading and being impleaded , answering and
defending ,in all courts of law and equity; may have a
common seal, and alter or renew the same at pleasure ;
and shall have power to adopt such by -laws ,rules and
regulations, for their government , and for the manage-
ment of said academy , as they may ,from time to time ,

stitution and laws of this State or of the United States .

and own or control such personal property, as may be

donations and bequests, and sell, transfer, and convey

of Kentucky :

for white childrenin said city,approved February 2 ,1882,
be ,and the same is hereby , amended by strikingout the
words " for white children in said city."

of said act,be ,and the same ishereby ,strickenout .

ham , A. J. Claypool , John M. Wilkins ,J. A. Mitchell,T.
J. Smith , and H. E. Jenkins,having qualifiedand organ-
ized under said act as the board of trusteesof said public
schools, said organization is hereby ratified, and said
trusteesaretoserveuntiltheirsuccessorsare duly elected
and qualified .
§ 4. That section twelve of the act to which this is an
amendment is hereby amended by strikingout the words
" twenty -fivethousand ," occurring afterthe words " shall
not exceed ," and before the words " the said bonds ," and
insertingin lieuthereof the words " thirty-fivethousand ,"
and also by striking out the words " Provided, That no
propertybelongingto coloredpersons shallbe subjectto
any taxationprovidedforin thisact;" and alsoby strik-
ing out the words " ten cents," occurring after the words
" shall not exceed ," and before the words " on each one
hundred dollars," and insertingin lieu thereof the words
" twelve cents ."
§5 . Section thirteen of the act to which this act is an
amendment ishereby amended by strikingout the words ,
" a certifiedcopy of said order or minute shall be pub-
lishedin the Bowling Green newspapers for at least
twenty days preceding said election,such publication to
continue until the time of said election," and in lieu
thereof these words shallbe inserted: " said electionmay
be held ten days afternoticeof the time and places of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 583
voting shallhave been given by publicationin any one
of the newspapers publishedin Bowling Green," and also
by strikingout the words," immediatelyqualifiedand ."
§ 6. Section fourteen of the act to which this act is
an amendment is hereby amended by strikingout the
words " twenty cents," occurring afterthe words " not ex .
ceeding ," and before the words , " upon each one hundred
dollars," and insertingin lieuthereof the words " twenty-
eight cents," and also amended by strikingout the proviso
in the lastclause of said section,which provisoisin the
followingwords , to-wit: " That the property of colored
persons shallnot be taxed under thisact."
§7 . The board of trustees of said schools shallestab-
lishand maintain separate school buildingsfor white and
colored children,and no white childshallbe permitted to
attendthe schoolsprovided for coloredchildren,and no
colored child shall be allowed to attend the schools pro-
vided for white children; and untilsuitablebuildings can
be erectedor purchased,saidboard of trusteesmay lease
for one or more years any building or part of a building
forthe purpose of carryingon any ofthe schoolsprovided
for herein .
§8 . The board of councilmen may make the firstlevy
of taxes for the payment of the interestor principalof
the bonds to be issued, and for defraying the current ex-
penses of said schools,upon the same propertyand the
same pollsassessedby the cityfor the general revenue of
saidcityduring the year 1882,and the levy provided for
herein may be made at any time within ten days after
the board of councilmen shall have been notified that a
majorityof the tax-payers voting have voted in favor of
the issualof the bonds provided for herein.
§ 9. All laws or parts of laws in conflictwith thisact
be , and the same are hereby ,repealed.
§ 10. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
afterits passage .
Approved April 17, 1882.

on propertystoredwith it or given to itscustody,upon

upon ,in writing,between itand the parties,persons,and

pany , in Washington county .

body -corporateand politic,under the name and styleof

Prather,J. J. Reddecks , and Isaac Lynch , or any three of

Officers .
§ 4. That no person shall be eligibleto the office of
president or directorwho is not the owner of one or more
shares of stock in said road ; and at an election of said
officers,each stockholder shallbe entitledto one vote for
each share of stock so owned ,and may be cast in person
or by written proxy .

of appointing a treasurer, gate-keepers, and all other

officersor agents which may be deemed necessary to
effectthe purpose of this act, and to remove them at

and allotherofficersor agentsappointedby them to exe-
cute bond and securityin such sums as they may require,
conditioned for the faithfulperformance of the duties
incumbent on them as such .

nanner and proportion,and at such times,as shallbe by
them required under the law incorporating the company ,
to be collected as other debts . Witness our hands this

CHAPTER 1123 .
AN ACT authorizingthe county court of Bracken county to levy a tax for
bridges .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Bracken county ,a ma-
jorityof thejusticesbeingpresentand concurringtherein,
may at any time levy an ad valorem tax of not more
than ten centsin one year on the $ 100 worth of taxable
property,for the purpose of erecting bridges over creeks
on public roads in said county .
§2 . The sheriffof said county shall collect any levy
thus made in the same way as other leviesof said county ,
and shallbe responsibleon his officialbond executed for
the collectionof the county levy,and allowed the same
compensation as he is now allowed by law for the collec-
tion of the county levy.
§ 3. That said levy, when collected, shall be paid by
the sheriffon the order of the county court,and any sur-
plus remaining in the hands of the sheriffat the next
regular term of the court of claims shall be paid over
to the treasurerof the sinkingfund of saidcounty,and
be applied to the payment of the county debt .
§ 4. This act shall be in force from itspassage .
Approved April 19, 1882 .
CHAPTER 1124 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the town of Carrollton .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the charter of the town of Carrollton, in
Carrollcounty,be,and the same is hereby,so amended
as to authorize and empower the board of trustees for

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 591
said town to purchase and receive titleto a lot of ground
in said town , or within three miles of the corporate limits
of said town , and erect thereon suitablebuildings for the
isolationand taking care of allpersons in said town who
may have been exposed to, or who are afflictedwith
cholera,yellow fever, small pox , or other dangerous and
infectious diseases .
§2 . That the trusteesof said town are hereby author-
ized to take all necessary steps,under the supervision of
the board of health for Carrollcounty,to prevent the
outbreak and spread of cholera,yellow fever,small-pox ,
and other infectiousand dangerous diseasesin said town ,
and to provide for those who are unable to provide for
themselves medicines , medical attention, food, nursing ,
and such other attentions as may be necessary for the
care of those who have been exposed to or are afflicted
with said diseases .
§3 . That the trustees of said town shall have power
to isolateallpersonswho have been exposed to saiddis-
eases , and are authorized to enact such laws as will be
effectualforthat purpose .
§ 4. That the trusteesof said town are hereby author-
ized to appropriate out of the revenue of said town such
sums of money as may be necessary to execute the powers
given them by the firstand second sectionsof thisact.
§5 . This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 19, 1882.
CHAPTER 1125 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Kentucky StreetRailway Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That George H. Moore , J. F. Waller , and J. S.
Phelps,theirassociatesand successors,be,and are here-
by ,created a corporation, under the name and styleof
the " Kentucky Street Railway Company ," and by said
name may have perpetualsuccession, may sue and be
sued,plead and be impleaded,have and use a common

592 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
seal,alterthe same at pleasure;contractand be contract-
ed with,hold and acquire,by leaseor purchase,realand
personalproperty,and disposeof same by sale,lease,or
mortgage .
§ 2. Said corporation shallhave the power to construct,
maintain, and operate singleor double track railway,
with all necessary and convenient side-tracks, curves ,
turn-tables, turn-outs, depots, stables, shops, and other
needed structures, and said railway may begin at the
intersectionof Fifteenth and Kentucky and build along
and through Kentucky street east to Hancock street,
thence north on Hancock street to Main street; also
from the intersectionof Fifteenth and Kentucky streets
northon Fifteenthto Main street,with the privilegeof
extending and building along and over above -named
streetsand others upon which there is no railroad,as the

nect and run over the lines of the Louisville City Rail-
way , or other streetrailroadcompanies in the city of

§3 . Any and all of the railway or extensions thereto
must be builtunder the supervision of the city engineer ,
and operated according to ordinances of general council
now regulatingthe management of one or the other street
railroadsnow operated in Louisville.
§ 4. The capitalstock of said company shallbe one
hundred thousand dollars, and may be increased from
time to time as the majority of stockholders may direct,
and shall be divided into shares of one hundred dollars
each . Said corporation shall have the right to com-
mence business whenever twenty -five thousand dollars
of bona fide subscription shall be received, the same to
be paid as required by board of directors. The said cor-
porators or board of directors,or eitherof them ,may open
books to receive subscription to the capital stock of said
company ,and such books of subscriptionmay be opened
and subscriptionreceivedat such time and place, and
upon such notice thereof, as any two corporators may
deem proper .
1

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 593
§5 . The stockholders representing a majority of the
stocksubscribedformay have the power to electthe first
board of directors,who shall serve until the 1st Monday
in June ,1882 ,at which time said stockholders shall annu-
allymeet and elect their board , and transact any other
business for the good of the corporation,and may change
the date of annual meeting if a majority of the stock-
holders so decide . The board of directors shall elect
from their number a president, and may appoint such
other officersto manage the affairsof the corporation
as may be necessary ; and in case of death or resigna-
tion of any member of the board , the vacancy may be
filledby balance of the directors; said board to make
such by -laws as the good of the corporation may de-
mand .
§6 . If the line or linesof this corporation traverse or
infringe upon any private property, the same shall be
compensated for to the owner , and if no agreement can
be arrived at,the said corporation may proceed to con-
demn the same by writ of ad quod damnum according to
the law regulating such proceedings .
§7 . This corporationmay acquire the rightof way
from other corporations, lease or be leased ; and may ,
with consent of general council of cityof Louisville,
enter into such contractswith other streetrailways as
may be agreed upon by both corporations .
§ 8. Unless the constructionof this railway is com-
menced within three years afterthe passage of thisbill,
itbecomes null and void; but such portion may be built
as the corporation may select,and the same extended as
the growth of the business demands .
§ 9. This act to take effecton and afteritspassage .
Approved April 19, 1882.

§2 . The said society shall also have power to shoot
and fish upon ,and use for itssaid corporate purposes ,the
premises owned ,rented,or leasedby them , at alltimes
not inconsistent with the game and fish laws of this
Commonwealth .
§3 . No member shallbe personally responsibleforany
liabilityof said society; but the property of the society
as a corporation, alone shallbe liablefor alljust claims
against the corporation.
§4 . The county judge of said county may appoint
such police as he may deem necessary to police and keep
order on saidpremises : Provided,however, That saidpolice
is recommended by a majority of the stockholders: And
provided further, That said corporation shall defray all
the expenses of such appointment , and pay all compen-
sation to such police for his services.
§ 5. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 19, 1882.
CHAPTER 1131 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act to incorporatethe Rockcastle
Mining and Lumber Company ," approved February 24,1882 .
WHEREAS , The Rockcastle Mining and Lumber Com-
pany is authorizedand empowered , by itscharterap-
proved February 24, 1882,to build a railroad, with the
principal object of transporting to market the product of
a large boundary of mineral and timber lands, owned by
certainof the corporatorsnamed therein,in the counties
of Martin , Floyd , and Johnson ; and whereas , the build-
ing of said road and the operation of mines willdevelop
a districtof great mineral wealth , invite emigration to
a sparselysettledregion, affordemployment to a large
number of people , and add to the value of contiguous
property, and thereby to the State revenue ; now , there

598 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
fore, to encourage the building of a railroad under the
charter aforesaid ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That if said corporation shall build a railroad
under the act referredto in the preamble , the depots,
round -houses, and machine shops shall be exempt from
all State and local taxationfor a period of fiveyears
from the completion of the firstsection of ten miles;
and each section of ten miles shall be in like manner
exempt from all State and local taxation for a period of
fiveyears from the time when each section or part of
section of ten miles shallhave been completed .
§2 . This act to take effect and be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved April 19 , 1882 .
CHAPTER 1132 .
AN ACT to authorize J. W. Elwards , late treasurer of the State House of
Reform for JuvenileDelinquents,topay over certainmoneys in his hands
to the State Treasurer .
WHEREAS , By act of the General Assembly of Ken-
tucky, approved April 21, 1873, the institutionknown as
the State House of Reform for Juvenile Delinquents was
changed to the Fourth Lunatic Asylum ; and whereas ,
upon the said twenty -firstday of April, 1873 , there re-
mained , and stillremains , an unexpended balance in the
hands of J.W. Edwards , of Louisville,the then treasurer
of said institution, to the credit of said institution ; and
whereas ,thisamount of money has never been paid over
by saidEdwards ,becausetherewas no person authorized
to receive the same ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the said J. W. Edwards be , and is hereby ,
authorized and directed to pay over to the Treasurer of
Kentucky the two hundred dollars,more or less, or what-
ever amount of money is now in his hands as treasurer

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 599
aforesaid; and said Treasurer shallreceiptto said Ed-
wards forthe amount so paid .
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .

CHAPTER 1133 .
AN ACT for the benefitof Samuel Biggerstaff, late sheriffof Madison
county .
WHEREAS , Samuel Biggerstaff, late sheriffof Madison
county ,in his settlements as sheriffwith the Auditor and
county court,was by mistakecharged with and paid into
the State Treasury more than he actually collected for
the State and county ,as shown by the originalassessor's
book of said county,and more than he owed the State;
and whereas ,more than two years have elapsed since
said mistake then made ; now ,therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of the State be , and he ishere-
by , authorized and empowered to re-stateand settlethe
accounts of Samuel Biggerstaff, as sheriffof Madison
county , and allow to said Biggerstaffcreditfor alldelin-
quent exonerations, and all such other creditsto which
he is entitledin the opinion of the Auditor .
§2 . When the Auditor has made the settlements pro-
vided for in section one of thisact,the Auditor is author-
ized and directed to draw his warrant in favor of Samuel
Biggerstaff, on the Treasurer ,for the amount found due
upon that settlement, payable out of any moneys not
otherwise appropriated.
§3 . That it shall be lawful for the county court of
claims of Madison county to appropriate a sufficientsum
to reimburse Samuel Biggerstaff for the excess he has
paid to the county as sheriff,when the said court shall
have become satisfied of the amount of said excess .
And the said Biggerstaff shall be permitted to return
an additional listof delinquent, including non -residents,
removals , exonerations, and those wrongfully assessed;

600 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and it shall be the duty of said county court of claims
to allow said listwhen it is satisfied of the correctness of
said claim .
§ 4. This act shalltake effectand be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved April 19, 1882.
CHAPTER 1134 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof J. T. Gospell, of Grayson county.
WHEREAS , J. T. Gosnell,as trusteeof thejury fund of

son county,paid tojurorsof the Grayson quarterlycourt

of Kentucky :

hereby ,directedto draw his warrant on the Treasury in
favor of said J. T. Gosnell for the sum of seven dollars,
payable out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise
appropriated .
§2 . This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved April 19, 1882.

CHAPTER 1137 .

Kentucky .

of Kentucky :

of Parkland ,Kentucky ,shall be held every two years in

CHAPTER 1140 .
AN ACT toincorporatetheStandardMining,Manufacturing,and Exploring
Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Geo W. Robson ,sr.,G. R. Harms , and Geo .
W. Robson , jr., their associates,successors, and assigns,
be ,and they are hereby ,constituteda body -corporate and
politic,by the name and styleand titleof " The Standard
Mining , Manufacturing , Refining , and Exploring Com-
pany ;" and by that name and styleare hereby made as
capable in law as natural persons to contract and be con-
tracted with,sue and be sued ,to plead and be impleaded ,
in thisCommonwealth and elsewhere ,in allcourts of law
and equity; to have a common seal, and to ordain,estab-
lish, and put in execution all such by -laws ,ordinances,
and regulations,as shall seem necessary and convenient
for the government of said corporation,not being contrary
to the laws of this State or of the United States . The
capital stock of said company shall be fifteenhundred
thousand dollars,in shares of fivedollarseach , and may
be increased or diminished by a vote of a majority of the
stockholders at any time upon a notice of ten days . Said
company may ,however ,commence operations as soon as
three thousand dollarsof said stock be subscribed, and
fiftyper cent.thereofispaid in,and the remaining stock,
capitalstock,may be issuedby the board of directorsof
thesaidcompany at such time as may seem to them best.
§2 . The said company shallhave power to purchase,
lease, negotiate for,and hold as much real estate, not ex-
ceeding in value the paid-up capital,as they may deem
necessary for the prosecution of theirbusiness,to-wit : to
explore, mine for gold, silver, iron, lead, coal, and all

604 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
other minerals; also to bore for saltwater ,petroleum ,oil,
or other products, and in the manufacture of the same ,
or eitherof them ,on alllands now owned ,or which may
be owned ,by them ,or eitherof them ; to purchase , own ,
or build flator steam -boats,roads, bridges, right of way ,
and all necessary buildings and machinery for the suc-
cessfulprosecution of said business; to hold all mining
rights,and dispose of any or allof the above named ma-
terials,lands,& c., named in thisinstrument .
§3 . For conducting the affairsof said company , a
board of five directors shall be chosen ; said board shall
choose one of itsmembers who shall be the president of
said company ; and said board shallserveforone year,
or untiltheir successors are elected and qualified. The
president and directorsshall have power to appoint all
officers,agents,and laborers under them ,or remove them
at pleasure. In all electionseach share shallentitlethe
holder to one vote ; stockholders may vote by proxy .
The stock of said company shall be deemed personal
estate, and shall be non -assessable. In the absence of
the president, a president pro tem may be appointed to
The officeof said company shall be located in
Newport , Kentucky .
act .
§ 4. Books forthe subscriptionof stock may be opened
at any time or place,and closed at pleasure : Provided,
That the stock at no time shall exceed the maximum
amount herein named .
§5. That itshallbe lawfulforthe presidentand direct-
ors of said company , from time to time , and at alltimes,
toborrow suchsums of money asby them shallbe deemed
expedient forthe use of said company ,and on such terms
as they may choose ,and to pledge or mortgage allor any
part of the estate, effects, & c., that may be owned by
them , or any of them ,for the re-payment of said sums of
money so borrowed at such time as may be agreed upon :
Provided , That no banking powers are conferred herein.
§6. In the event of a failureon the part of any stock-
holderto pay up his stock,the share of such stockholder

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 605
may be forfeitedby the presidentand directors,or they
may ,if they so elect,sue and recover the same .
§7 . The Legislature reservesthe rightto alter,amend ,
or repeal thischarter at pleasure.
§ 8. This act shall take effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 19, 1882 .

CHAPTER 1141 ,
AN ACT to incorporatethe CrescentHill Railway Company .

of Kentucky :
,
§1 . That Thomas H. Hayes ,George K. Speed ,J. H.
Lindenberger , Thomas S. Kennedy , John T. G. Galt,
James Harrison , Charles R. Long ,D. M. Rodman ,T. G.
Truman , Joseph Raymond , J. Wash . Davis, George M.
Rogers, Wm . Mahl , W. C. Priest, John Shanks , R. H.
Hoskins , Henry Chenowith , J. W. Osborne , Samuel E.
English, and Joseph B. Gathright, and theirsuccessors,
be, and they are hereby, createda body -corporateand
politic,by the name of the Crescent Hill Railway Com-
pany ; and by thatname shallhave perpetualsuccession
contract and be contracted with, sue and be sued ; may

or double track railway,by animal or steam power , com-
mencing at any point in the cityof Louisville to which
said corporationmay obtain the consent of the mayor
and council of the city of Louisville; thence along any
streetof said cityto which the said mayor and council
may consent ; thence along or over the Louisville and
ShelbyvilleTurnpike,or over itsdirtroad,with the con-
sent of said turnpike company , or over the land lying

606 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
to the Crescent Hill reservoir and beyond ,not exceeding

ter 94 , General Statutes; with power to locate its track

§2 . The capital stock of said corporation shallbe ten

eral meeting called for the purpose ,to not exceeding fifty

rules and regulations for the government of its affairs

restrictions,exceptas herein provided,granted and pro-
vided in the act to incorporate the Beargrass Railway
Company , approved January 27th, 1866 , and the act to

§ 5. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 19, 1882.
CHAPTER 1142 .
AN ACT to amend an act , entitled "An act to charter the Lexington and
Carter County Mining Company ."
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the board of directorsof the Lexington and
Carter County Mining Company may declare and distrib-
ute dividends in stock among the stockholders of said
company ,from time to time,as they may thinkthe inter-
est of the company require; and may add to the capital

to time , in theirjudgment , may advance the interestof

hundred thousand dollars .

Approved April 19, 1882 .

60
§1 . That so much of the county of Fayette as is con-

declared to be , a city, and the inhabitants thereof are

ington," with power to govern themselves by such
ordinances and resolutionsfor municipal purposes as
they may deem proper ,not to conflictwith the charter
nor the Constitution of thisState,nor the Constitution of

poses,by purchase or otherwise; to hold the same and all

prove , sell,and convey , rent or lease the same , and to

as such , by that name ,shall be capable in law of con-
tracting and being contracted with, of suing and being

being answered , into all courts and places, and in all
matters whatsoever ; and may have and use a corporate
seal, and change ,alter, and renew the same at pleasure.
§2 . The fiscal,prudential,and municipal concerns of
said city, with the government and control thereof,shall
be vested in one principalofficerto be calledthe mayor ,
and one city council,consistingof twelve persons,to be
denominated the board of councilmen ,who shall be elected
the firstSaturday in March next in the way hereinafter
prescribed, all of whom shall hold their officesfor two
years,and untiltheirsuccessors shallbe duly elected and
qualified; all of said officersshall have resided in said
citytwo years next precedingtheirelection,shallbe male

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 609
citizensof the United States and of this State,bona fide
residents of the ward for which they may be chosen ,
housekeepersor owners of real estatein said city,hold
no other civiloffice,nor be directly or indirectlyinter-
estedin any contractwith saidcityor in any application
therefor,or a candidateforor hold any officeor employ-
ment for pay in any company or corporation which holds
or is an applicant for any contract with said city, the
terms,rates,or prices of which are subject to modifica-
tion or enforcement by the board of councilmen , nor be
in arrears to the cityfor money collected or held without
a settlement or quietus therefor. Stockholders in other
corporations may be eligible, but shall not vote on or
interferedirectlyor indirectlywith any matter or ques-
tion affectingany contractbetween such company and
the city,or affecting any right or duty under any such
contract- and allsaid officersshallhave attained the age
of twenty -four years . The absence of any of the fore-
going qualificationsshallrender a person ineligibleeither
as mayor or councilman , and if occurring after election
or during his term of office, shallwork a forfeitureof his
office, and the board of councilmen shallso declare, pro-
ceed to fillthe vacancy as hereinafter prescribed.
Wards .
§3 . That itshallbe the duty of said mayor and coun-
cilmen that shallbe firstelected under this act to lay off
the said city into four wards , for the purposes herein
named , as nearly equal as possible as to number of
inhabitants, improvements , and permanent boundaries ,

cilmen , to be members of the board of councilmen ; but
the mayor and councilmen may , in their discretion,
increase the number of said wards to not exceeding
twelve , with at alltimes an equal number of councilmen
from each ward ; and it shallbe the duty of said mayor
and councilmen , from time to time , to alter the bounda-
ries of the wards so as to equalize the number of the
inhabitantsin each as nigh as may be; but such altera-

610 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
tion shallnot be made except on a general census of all
the inhabitantsbeing previouslytaken.
City Elections.
§4 . That on the firstSaturday in March ,in 1882, and
