tants over twenty -one years of age of said city, who at
the time being are citizens of the State of Kentucky ,
and inhabitantsof said city, and bona fide residents

at least sixtydays prior to the election, and who shall

shallbe publishedin one or more of the public newspa-
pers printed in said city, with the names of the ward

claimingthe same the listof the qualifiedvotersrespect-
ively; and on closing the pollsat the hour mentioned in
theirwarrant ,they shall declare the same , and the three
persons having the highest number of qualifiedvotes

shall be declared as duly elected councilmen in each
ward . The vote given for mayor shall be added up by

isteredby the clerk of the county court or any justice of

§5 . That in the event of the death , resignation,ineli-
gibility, or refusalto act of the mayor ,the councilmen
shallproceed to electone of their own number to supply
his place ,and the person so elected shallbe the mayor of

is sworn into office; and the mayor and the councilmen

books shall have been returned ; and if not sooner re-

taking the oath required by the Constitutionand execut-
ing the bonds requiredby thisact, except the recorder

and clerk of recorder's court,who shall enter upon the

ber following theirelection.

chief of the firedepartment ,and such engineers, firemen ,

men shallhold theirofficesfor the term of one year , but

fixthe salariesor compensation for such officers,but the

the term for which he may be elected. The mayor and
councilmen and said other officersof said cityshall,be-
fore entering upon the duty of their respective offices,
each take the oath prescribedfor officersby the Constitu-
tion of Kentucky ,which oath may be administered to the
mayor electby any judge of any court of record in this
Commonwealth ,or by any justice of the peace of Fay-
ettecounty ; and the mayor , afterhe is sworn into office,
shall administer the said oath to each of the councilmen
and said other officers,or the same may be administered
by any justiceof the peace of Fayette county, and an
entry shallbe made on the records of the board that said
oaths have been duly administered.
§ 8. That the mayor and councilmen thus chosen and
qualified shall form one board , and shall sit and act to-
gether as one body ; and at allmeetings the mayor , if
present,shallpreside,but in his absence the board may
elect a chairman for the time being . The said board

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 613
shall choose a clerk,who shall be sworn to the faithful
discharge of the duties of his office, which he shallhold
for one year ,and untilanother shallsucceed him ,remov-
able,however , at the pleasure of the board , and shallbe
denominated " The Clerkof the City," whose duty itshall
be to keep a journal of the proceedings of the board , to
signallwarrantsissuedby them ,and to do allactsin his
said capacity that may be reasonably required of him ,
and to deliverover allpapers and books intrustedto him
to his successor in officeimmediately on such successor
being chosen and qualified,or whenever he shallbe there-
to required by the mayor and councilmen . In allmeet-
ings of the board six councilmen , with the mayor , or in
the absence of the mayor ,seven councilmen ,shallconsti-
tute a quorum to do business; but in cases of levying the
taxes,or in the electionof any officerof the citygovern-
ment ,or the passing of any by -law or ordinance ,seven
councilmen shall concur , or when six councilmen shall
be found voting in the affirmative, the mayor may be
called upon to vote; and in case of his voting in the
affirmative,the measure shallprevail. The board shall
meet in some public place to be provided for that pur-
pose , and theirdeliberationsand acts shallbe public.
Streets- Opening of.
§ 9. That the mayor and councilmen may receive con-
veyances or the consent ,in writing, of the proprietorsof
lots of land within the city for the purpose of locating
new streetsor alleys, or extending any of the streets or
alleys of said cityto the limitsthereof; and on receiving
the same may directsuch streetsor alleysto be extended
or opened ; and when they shalldeem itproper to have
any new streetor alley opened ,or any of the streetsor
alleysof saidcityextended in part,or to the limitsthere-
of,to petitionthe Fayette circuitcourt or the Lexington
recorder'scourt, statingthe streetor alleythey wished
opened or extended , and the names of the owners , if
known , of the lots and lands through which they desire
to have such streetor alley opened or extended , and the
width thereof ; and thereupon said court shall order a

614 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
summons to issuefor such owner to appear on some con-
venient day of that or some subsequent term to show
cause why such street or alley should not be opened or
extended ,which summons shall be executed , or the re-
turn of no inhabitant or known agent ,and no one appear-
ing the court,shallenter up a warning order against such
non -resident owner , and appoint an attorney to defend
for such owner ,and proceed in the same manner as now
provided by the Civil Code of Practicein such cases on
such owner ,if in the county ; if not, on his agent ,if one
is known , and on the return of the summons executed , or
the return of no inhabitant or known agent ,and no one
appearing ,the court shall order the streetor alleyto be
opened or extended , and if any one or more of such per-
sonsshallappear,they shallhave a rightto claim a writ
of ad quod damnum , which the court shallaward to the
proper officer,to be executed as other writs of that na-
ture for opening roads, and on the return of the writ
executed , the court shall order the mayor and council-
men to pay the damages assessed, and shall order the
street or alley to be opened or extended . The mayor
and councilmen shallhave full power and authority to
cause allstreetsor alleysto be opened and cleared of all
obstructionsby the inflictionof suitablefines and penal-
ties: Provided, however, That the mayor and council of
said cityshallhave power ,at any time before the final
decree or order,to dismiss theirpetition,which shallnot
prevent their again institutingproceedings for opening
such streetor alley at any time afterone year from said
dismission .
Paving and Turnpiking of.
§ 10.That the mayor and councilmen shallhave full
power and authorityto cause and procure allthe streets
and alleys in the said city now established,or hereafter
tobe established,tobe paved orturnpikedat the costand
expense of the lot-owners fronting such streetsor alleys;
and a petition,in writing,of the owner or owners of a
greaterpart of the ground frontingon any square shall
be sufficientto authorize a contract for the paving or

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 615
turnpikingthe streetor alleyin such square: Provided,
however, That the mayor and councilmen,by theirunani-
mous consent in council,may cause any streetor alleyin
any square in said cityto be paved or turnpikedat the
cost and expense of the owners of lots or parts of lots
frontingsuch streetor alley, without any petitionor con-
sent; and when the paving or turnpiking shallbe com-
pleted,they shallapportion the costsand expenses equally
on the lot-holders, and a lien is hereby given on the lots
and parts of lotsfor the same ,which costs and expenses
may be listedand collectedas other taxes by the citycol-
lector,who shallhave authorityto selland convey the
lotsand parts of lotsfor the same under the by -laws and
regulationsof the mayor and councilmen : Providedalso,
however, That the owner of any lot, or any part of lot,
soldforpaving or turnpiking,who has not consented,in
writing, forthat purpose ,shallhave five years to redeem
the same on paying the purchase money , with ten per
centum interestper annum , with allthe taxes and levies
that may have subsequently accrued ; and those who have
consented in writing may redeem it any time within one
year on the payment of the like interest: Provided, That
infantsshall have one year after arriving at fullage on
like terms .
Sidewalks Paved and Curbed and Streets Graded .
$ 11. That the mayor and councilmen shallhave power
and authority to cause and procure the sidewalks to be
curbed and paved with stone or brick, and to apportion
the costs and expenses on the owners of lots fronting
thereon, and a lienisgiven on the lotsforthe same ; and
the mayor and councilmen shall have the likeauthority
and power to cause and procure the streetsand alleys
now established, or hereafter to be established, within
saidcity,to be filled,leveled,and graduatedforthe pur-
pose of carrying off the water , or preparatory for paving
or turnpiking,at the cost and expense of the lot-owners
fronting the same ,and a lien is hereby given on the lots
and parts of lots for such costs and expense ; and the
costsand expense accruingunder the provisionsof this

616 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
section shall be listed and collected in like manner as
that of paving and turnpiking, and the collectorshall
have the likeauthorityto selland convey the same ,as is
provided for in the previoussection,and subjectto re-
demption in one year from the day of sale; and in cases
of infants,in one year afterthey arriveat fullage,by
paying ten per centum interestthereon.
What - When StreetsAlready Paved ,Etc.
§ 12. That if any part of a street, alley,or sidewalk ,
in any square,shallhave been paved at the expense of
any one or more owner or owners of lotsin said square ,
before such order shall have been made ,he ,she, or they
shallnot be requiredto pay anything,unlesshis,her,or
theirportion of costs and expenses of grading ,paving , or
turnpiking, assessed as above provided, and including
that already paved ,shallexceed the amount of costs and
expenses by them paid or incurred for the part so paved :
Provided, however, That nothing herein contained shall
preventthe mayor and councilrequiringsidewalksto be
repaved at the expense of the owner of the lot fronting
such sidewalk .
Taxation .
§ 13. The said mayor and councilmen shallhave the
power and authority to assess,levy, and collecttaxes on
such realand personalestate, includingstocks, bonds,
money ,and choses in actionof every kind whatsoever,
not exempt from taxationby law,as they may designate;
but such taxation shallbe uniform on every description
of property assessed; but from the value of stocks,bonds ,
money ,and choses in actionthat may be designatedfor
taxation there shall be deducted the amount of the bona
fide indebtedness of the owner thereof.
Licensesand SpecificTaxes.
§ 14. The mayor and board of councilmen shallalso
have power and authorityto cause allstoreswithin the
city to be rated- first,second ,third,and fourth rate- levy
and collecta tax on each,not exceeding one hundred

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 617
dollarsper year ; and when storesare taxed according to
theirrate,the goods therein shall not be valued and in-
cluded in the assessments of property for taxation, it
being hereby intended to give to said mayor and council
the righteitherto ratethe stores,groceries,and so forth,or
make them pay the ad valorem tax assessed as upon other
property; but in the event of the councildetermining to
tax said stores,groceries,and so forth,by rates,then they
shall likewise be assessed as other property, for the pur-
pose of ascertaining and enforcing the payment of the
firetax , not exceeding ten cents on the one hundred dol-
lars,providedforin thischarter. Said mayor and council
shall likewise have power ,by ordinance ,to provide for
the taxing or rating of any store, grocery , and so forth,
which may be commenced or opened at any time subse-
quent to the day fixedfor the annual assessment , and all
itinerant merchants or vendors : Provided , That in no case
shallthe tax levied upon such be lessthan the ratable
amount ,as compared with the other assessment,in propor-
tion to the time the same iskept open . Said mayor and
councilmen shall also have the right to tax auctioneers
in saidcity(in additiontothe taxesnow imposed by law)
notexceedingone per centum upon allpublicand private
sales of goods ,wares ,or merchandise , and to require them
to take out licenses,with such restrictionsand requisitions
as may be necessary to enforce said tax; but no tax shall
be required for salesof decedents by executors, adminis-
trators , or curators .
§ 15. That it shall and may be lawful for the mayor
and councilof the cityof Lexington to pass such ordi-
nance or ordinances as may by the said mayor and coun-
cil be deemed expedient for imposing and collecting a
tax upon playing cards retailedin said city,to be applied
in lesseningthe expenses of said city: Provided, The
same shallnot exceed fiftycentson thedeck or pack
§ 16. That the mayor and board of councilmen shall
have the rightto tax and license,and shall,by ordinance ,
provide for the licensesfor the following businesses,pro- .
tessions, and employments , with adequate penalties for

doing businesswithout the required license: For any
grocer,confectioner,victualer,hawker ,huckster,peddler,
orany otherretailer,exceptof spirituous,vinous,or fer-
mented liquors,or any other merchant or dealer in dry
goods , clothing,drugs , medicines ,wares , or merchandise
of any kind ,not more than one hundred dollars; for any
theatricalexhibition, lecture,concert, show ,or perform-
ance in the city for pay , such sum as the mayor and
board of councilman may prescribeby ordinance or reso-
lution: Provided, That lectures by resident professors
and literaryor charitableentertainments may be allowed

or charge; foreach vehiclerunning in saidcityforprofit
or hire,including milk wagons ,coal wagons or carts,ice

or other vehiclesused by any one in the business of sell-
ing and delivering any of the above named articles, not
lessthan two nor more than thirtydollars; for every lot-
tery officeor agency therefor, not less than one hundred
nor more than five hundred dollars: Provided, That such

dred dollars; for taverns or hotels,public boarding -houses ,
or places of publicresortor entertainmentwherein no
malt,fermented,vinous,or spirituousliquorsare retailed,
not less than ten dollarsnor more than fiftydollars; for

house,notlessthan tendollarsnor more than one hundred

dollars; foreach intelligenceoffice,claim agent,commer-
cial agent ,street broker ,pawnbroker ,express company ,
or agency therefor,bank exchange or broker's office,tele-
graph office,telephone office,insurance officeor agency
therefor , not less than ten dollars nor than two hundred
and fiftydollars; foreach dealer in wood ,lumber ,and coal,

nor more than two hundred and fiftydollars;foreach attor
ney and counselorat law ,physician,surgeon or dentist,
master builder, architect,or photographer , not less than
fivedollarsnor more than fiftydollars; foreach veterinary

dollars nor more than twenty -five dollars; for each gift

not less than fiftydollars nor more than five hundred
dollars: Provided, That such license shall not authorize
the operation of any gift enterprisenot otherwise made
lawful. For any otherbusinessoremployment not herein
named , the mayor and councilmen may , by ordinance ,
designate and fix the rate ,and cause a license therefor to
be taken as herein provided ,and may grade and classevery
business,profession,or craftherein provided tobe licensed,
and fix the rate of license at or within the minimum and
maximum herein provided ; and the mayor and board of
councilmen shallhave power to grade and class allestab-
lishments wherein malt ,vinous ,or spirituousliquors are
retailed, and fix the rate of license therefor according to
certain limits or districtsof the city wherein the same
may be situated,such limits or districtshaving been first
duly laid out and fixed by ordinance . No license shall
be granted fora longer periodthan one year,and before
the granting or issuing of any license the tax required
therefor shall be paid into the city treasury, and no
license shall be taken as granted untilregularlyissued;
but when a license is issued , it shall bear date with the
commencement of the store or other establishment , or
business or thing licensed; or, if a renewal of license,
shallbe dated from the day of the expiration of the pre

620 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
vious license. Any of the unexpired time of licenses
herein permitted, which shallnot be lessthan six months ,
may be transferredby the holder, with the assent of the
mayor and board of councilmen . The mayor and board
of councilmen shallalsohave power ,whenever in their
opinion the interestsof said cityshallrequire it,to levy
and collecttaxes on dogs , or other domestic animals ,in
any sum not exceeding ten dollars on each dog or other
animal .
§ 17. A tax shall be levied on each male inhabitant of
twenty -one years of age and upwards , except paupers ,
inhabitingsaidcity,at the rationot exceeding one dollar
and fiftycents each .
§ 18. The countylevyforthe county of Fayette hereto-
fore collected from the inhabitants within the boundaries
of said cityshallbe discontinued.
Payment of Taxes.
§19. The mayor and board of councilmenshallhave
power to provideby law that tax-payers shallpay their
taxes to the treasurerby a day to be fixedby ordinance ,
and from and after such day add to allunpaid taxes the
usual collector'scommission forcollectingtaxes; and they
may affix and enforce penalties for the failure to pay
taxes to the collector,which penalty shallnot in any case
be remitted, except by the unanimous consent of the
mayor and board of councilmen .
Assessor and Assessments .
§20 . The city assessor may , with the advice and con-
sentof the board of councilmen,appoint an assistant,if
deemed necessary,and shalltake in a listof allthe taxa-
ble inhabitants within said city,separately in each ward ,
and affixagainst each the amount of his, her, or their
real estate within said city, and also the true and just
value of such personal estateof each of said inhabitants,
whether in goods ,stocks,manufactories ,or other property
that may be designatedfor taxationby the said mayor
and councilmen , which list shall be made on the oath of
the party; or if the party refuse to give in a list, and
1

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 621
swear to the same , the amount shall be assessed from the
best information such assessorpossesses; and in each of
the several wards of the said citysaid listsshallbe ex-
tended to include allmales over twenty -one years of age ,
taverns,groceries,victualers,retailers, and confectioners,
and houses of public resort, and all other business,pro-
fessions, and employments hereinbefore designated to be
licensed or taxed ; and when said listsshallbe completed
and returned to the board of mayor and councilmen ,they
shallgive a reasonable notice that any inhabitant of said
citymay examine the same ; that if any one should feel
aggrieved thereby in an excess of valuation,application
may be made to the mayor and councilmen,and on proof
being made to their satisfaction, the valuation may be
changed : Provided, The applicationis made within the
time which may be prescribed by them ,afterwhich time
no abatement or change shallbe made . The mayor and
councilmen shall proceed to lay and levy the taxes for
the current year from estimates previously submitted to
the board by the mayor : Provided, The same shallnot
exceed seventy-five cents on each hundred dollars for
such valuation; and for the general expenses of said city
government , such as the police salaries, the support of
the poor ,and all charges for the well ordening and gov-
erning of said citythroughout ,the estimates shallbe pre-
sented by the mayor .
Collector .
§ 21. When the said taxes shallbe made out and fixed,
as herein provided , or the day forpaying the same to the
treasurer shall have passed , if any such be fixed, bills
shall be placed in the hands of the city collector,with
their warrant to collect the same . The power of the
collectorof the citytax shall be the same as to the dis-
training, advertising, and sellingproperty, as may ,from
time to time,be granted by law to the sheriffin the col-
lection of the State revenue and county levy ; and for
failure to pay taxes on real estate, the same remedies by
damages and interest for sale of the real estate shall exist
as in the collectionof the State revenue ,except that all

622 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
persons may purchase at said sales,and thatitshallbe
the duty of the collectorto make allreturns to the mayor
and board of councilmen ,which , in the sale of lands for
the State revenue ,the sheriffis required to make to the
Auditor of the Commonwealth ; and that the mayor and
board of councilmen shallperform allthe duties and have
allthe powers to make salesof lands which are prescribed
tobe performedby,and are given to,the Auditorin the
collectionof the State revenue . Bond ,with good surety,
in an adequate penalty, shall be taken of said collector,
payable to the cityof Lexington,and he or they shallbe
removable at the pleasure of the mayor and councilmen .
The citycollectorand his suretiesshallbe liableto judg-
ment by motion in the recorder'scourtforfailingto col-
lectand pay over any sums put in hishands for collection
at the time stipulated, with ten per centum interestand
costs; and fivedays'noticeof such motion shallbe suffi-
cient,and there shallbe no replevin or valuation of prop-
erty on executions issuing on such judgments . A lien
shall exist on the real estate of said collector and his
suretiesfor the payment of all sums placed in his hands
for collection. And the city collectoris authorized to
levy upon any real estatein the cityof Lexington owned
by a person owing taxes to the cityfor the amount of the
unpaid taxes of such person and in the hands of the col-
lectorfor collection,and proceed to selland convey the
same , or so much thereof as may be necessary to pay
said taxes ,in the same manner that sheriffsare author-
ized to selllands under executions of fi.fa ,but with the
right of redemption for the same time and upon the same
terms as hereinbefore given . The lien on the real estate
of the tax -payer for taxes shall commence on the 10th
day of January in the year in which the taxes are as-
sessed, and may be enforced by the levy and sale herein-
before provided for.
§ 22. It shall be the duty of the citycollectorto con-
tinue the collectionof citytaxes and other citydues until
his successor shall be duly elected and qualified, and the
collector'sbook for that year placedin the hands of his

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 623
successor; and he must settlehis accounts ,from time to
time , as the mayor and board of councilmen shall re-
quire .
Fire Tax .
§23 . That in addition to the taxes before provided ,the
mayor and council of the cityof Lexington shallhave the
authority in each year to levy an additional ad valorem
tax on the property within said city, not exceeding ten
cents on each one hundred dollars of assessed property,
which shall be collected in the same manner as other
taxes are by law collectedin said city, and that the fund
arising therefrom shall be kept by the treasurer of said
citydistinctand separate from the other funds, and shall
be expended only in discharge of debts now existing
againstsaidcityforthe purchaseof fireapparatus,and
providing facilitiesfor obtaining water in time of fire, or
for such other appropriations as may be made by resolu-
tions of the mayor and board of councilmen for similar
purposes .
School Tax .
24. That in addition to the taxes before provided ,the
mayor and council of the cityof Lexington shallhave the
authority in each year to levy an additionalad valorem
tax of any sum not exceeding fifteencents on the one
hundred dollarsof assessedproperty within said city,
which shall be collected in the same manner as other
taxes are by law collectedin said city, and that the fund
arising therefrom shall be kept by the treasurer of said
citydistinctand separate from the other funds, and shall
be expended only under the direction and management
of the board of councilmen forthe supportand mainte-
nance of the public cityschools.
Sinking Fund .
§25 . That in addition to the taxes heretofore provided ,
the mayor and board of councilmen shall,ineach year,
levy an additionalad valorem tax,not exceeding five
cents on the one hundred dollarsof assessedproperty
within said city, which shallbe collectedin the same

624 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
manner as other taxes are by law collectedin saidcity;
and itshallbe the duty of the mayor and board of coun-
cilmen to create a sinking fund ,which shallbe sufficient
to pay the semi -annual intereston the bonded debt of
the city; and the moneys constitutingsaid sinking fund ,
togetherwith the five-centtax hereinbeforeprovidedfor,
shallbe kept by the treasurerdistinctfrom the other
moneys of the city,and shallonly be used for the pay-
ment of the interestand principalof saidbonded debt,
and they shall,from time to time ,increase said sinking
fund to an amount sufficientto pay off allof said bonded
debt as the same shall,from time to time , mature .
Accounts .
§ 26. The mayor and councilmen shallannuallypub-
lishan account of all the moneys received and paid out
by theirorderduringtheyear they arein office.
Borrowing Money .
§27 . That said mayor and councilmen shall have the
power to borrow money on the creditof the corporation,
and pledge any of the corporation property for the re-
demption of the same ,or to pledge any part of the future
taxes of the city for the payment of the principal and
intereston said loan: Provided, That the interestpaid in
no case shallexceed six per centum per annum , and that
the amount of debt so created shall not in the aggregate
exceed twenty thousanddollars; and no mayor and board
of councilmen shall have power to increase the present
debt of the city.
Watchmen .
§28 . That the mayor and councilmen shall have the
power and authorityto determine that so many watchmen
shallbe elected,eitherfor day or night, as,in theirdiscre-
tion,they shalljudge best for the safety and advantage of
the city; and such watchmen shallbe conservators of the
peace, and vested with power to apprehend allfelons,
gamblers,rioters,and breakersor disturbersof the peace,
vagrants,persons of evilfame and of riotous,disorderly
conduct,and carrythem, with such evidenceas they may

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 625
possess,before the judge of the court, to be dealt with ac-
cordingto law; and ifsuch apprehensionisin the night,
then to the watch -house for safe-keeping untilthe morn-
ing. They shallalsobe vestedwith the power and duty
of patrollers.
Inspectorsof Grain ,Flour, Whisky ,Etc.
§ 29. They are authorized and empowered to appoint
inspectorsof grain, flour, whisky, tobacco, beef, pork,
butter,and lard for exportation, to provide against frauds
thereon, and to make such by -laws as shall be needful to
carrythe same intoeffect,and to punish breachesthere-
of .
Health Officerand Regulations.
§30. They are vestedwith the power and authorityto
appoint a health officer, to establishregulations which
may be necessary and proper to prevent the introduction
of small-pox , and eradicate such disease when it shall
have made its appearance in said city: Provided, That
nothing herein shall be construed to give any power to
establish any regulation inconsistentwith the Constitu-
tion and laws of the State,or to prevent the Legislature
from changing , by law , any regulation which may be
made by said corporation on thissubjector in relationto
inspectors .
Buildings.
§31 . The board or councilmen shall have power , with
the concurrence of three fourths of the councilmen elect,to
prohibitthe erection or alterationof any building in the
city,or to regulate the size and height thereof, whenever
they,in theirdiscretion,shall believe that said buildings
would endanger or seriouslyand materially injure valu-
able and permanent improvements already erected; and
shallhave power to enforce the observance of such pro-
hibitionor regulationby ordinance inflictingfineor other
penalty forthe breach thereof.
Fire Department.
§32 . They shallhave the power to organize a firede-
partment fortheextinguishmentof firesthatmay happen
LOC . L., VOL .II- 40

626 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
within saidcity; to provideengines and other apparatus
forthatpurpose,and toappointa suitablenumber of able-
bodied men , not exceeding twenty to each engine,who
shallbe exempted from servingon juriesand doing mili-
tiaduty in time of peace during the time they are attached
to said engine ,the said engine -men to be appointed for
any term that the mayor and councilmen shall ordain,
and be discharged by them at pleasure ; and the said
mayor and councilmen shall make all necessary by -laws
to punish any inhabitant of said cityfor non -attendance ,
or disorderlyor improper conduct during the time of fire.
Poor and Work -houses .
§33 . They shallhave the power to erect and procure
a suitablebuildingor buildingsfor a poor-house and
work -house , in which the indigent poor shall be com-
fortablyprovided for at the expense of said corporation,
to be regulated and managed by suitablepersons to be
appointedby the mayor and councilmen forthatpurpose,
and in apartments distinctfrom the infirm and indigent ;
or to make ,from time to time,with the Fayette county
court, or other suitablepersons or associations,contracts
for the proper care and maintenance of such infirm and
indigentpoor of the city. Provisionsshallbe made for
confiningcommon beggars,vagrants,and allpersonswho
may be sentenced for short periods of confinement by
the recorder's court for breaches or disturbances of the
peace,or forpettyoffenses,where theyshallbe employed
in such labor,and made to perform such tasks as shallbe
directedby the ordinance of the mayor and councilmen .
Persons committed to the work -house shall be sent there
by warrantsof the Lexington citycourt,and discharged
by the overseeron the expirationof the time forwhich
they were sentenced . A committee of the board of coun-
cilmen or special overseers of the poor shall be estab-
lishedby the board , who shallplace the poor persons in
the poor -house at their discretion; but the whole estab-
lishment shallbe under the control and immediate care,
and subjectto the frequent visitationof, the mayor and
councilmen . All by -laws and ordinances to carry this

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 627
articleintoeffectiveoperation,not repugnant to the laws
of thisState or the United States, are to be made and
enforced by said corporation.
§ 34. That the mayor and councilmenof the cityof
Lexington shall have authority to lease the city poor-
house and work -house for a period not exceeding five
years at a time ,upon such conditions and with such re-
strictionsas they may see proper to prescribe, and to
contract for the keeping and maintaining of the inmates
thereof; butsuchleasingshallnotpreventthemayor and
councilmen from exercising supervision over the lessee
so as to enforce a proper provision for,and treatment of,
such inmates .
§ 35. The mayor and board of councilmen may require
thatany or allof the personsconfinedin the work-house
shall be employed outside the limitsof said work -house
at labor in cleaning and repairing the streets, sewers ,or
other public property of the city.
§36 .The keeper of the poor and work -houses shalltake
his officesubject to such contract in regard to his duties
and compensation as the mayor and board of councilmen
shall, by ordinance ,provide for: Provided, however, That
any contractso providedforshallnot be alteredto affect
any keeper afterhe shallhave been elected.
Animals Running at Large, and Other Nuisances.
§37 . They shallhave power ,whenever the public con-
venience or safety may require it, to prohibithogs ,cattle,
horses,and other descriptionsof animals ,from running at
large in the streets, lanes, alleys, commons , and other
public places in said city, and to require and compel the
abatement and removal of all nuisances within the limits
of said city,under such regulations as shallbe prescribed
by ordinance .
Tippling-houses,etc., Suppressed.
§38. They shallhave power to suppressby ordinances,
with suitablepenalties,alltippling-houses,bawdy -houses,
disorderlyhouses,and houses of ill-fame ,all retailingof
spirituousliquorswithoutlicenses,and allriots,disorders,

628 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
astrologers, fortune-tellersand clairvoyants, breaches of
the peace,and disturbersof the peace and tranquilityof
the said city.
Gunpowder .
§39 . They shall have the power to erect and procure
one or more suitablebuildings as powder magazines ; to
provide suitable carriages for the conveyance of gun-
powder , and to pass by -laws to prohibitthe introduction
of gunpowder intosaidcity,with penaltiesforbreaches
thereof, in addition to the forfeitureof the powder so
introduced .
Playing Cards .
§ 40. They shallalsohave the power to impose such
restrictionsby ordinance upon the sale or other disposi-
tion of playing -cards by retailin said cityof Lexington ,
as will prevent the vending or trading of the same to
minors; and if deemed necessary, require bond , with
good security, from all retailvenders of playing cards,
upon such terms and conditionsas they may ordain,and
monthly or quarterlyaccountsto be renderedto the city
clerk .
By -laws and Ordinances .
§ 41. That the mayor and couneilmen shall have full
power to pass all needful ordinancesand by-laws for
carryingintoeffectallthe powers herein granted, and
executing allthe provisions of thischarter, with suitable
penalties for the infraction of the same , not exceeding
fiftydollars, except in cases of disturbances of religious
worship ,riots,and breaches of the peace ,where the pen-
alty may be one hundred dollars.
§42 . The validityof the city ordinances and by -laws
may be triedby writ of prohibitionfrom the Fayette cir-
cuit court, with the rightof appeal to the Court of Ap-
peals.
§ 43. Should the judge of the recorder'scourt decide
against the validityof any ordinance or by -law ,the said
decision,with the ordinance or by -law ,shall,on request
of the cityattorney,be certifiedon the record, and the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 629
cityof Lexington shallhave the rightof carryingsaid
decisionto the Court of Appeals by appeal or writ of
error .
§ 44. All penalties for violationsof the ordinances and
by -laws shallbe sued for by warrant , in the name of the
cityof Lexington ,in the recorder'scourt, and judgment
may be given as well forthe costs as the penalties.
§ 45. The mayor and councilmen shall cause all the
by -laws and ordinances passed by them to be fairlyre-
corded in the journal of theirproceedings,and publish
the same in one or more newspapers printed within said
city.
SubordinateOfficers.
§46 . That the mayor and councilmen shallappoint or
provide for the election by the people of all necessary
subordinate officersto carry the foregoing provisions of
this into full effect, such as overseers of the poor and
work -houses,and any otherthatmay be required,and to
qualify them for the faithfuldischarge of their several
duties, and require bond and securitiesof them for the
same ; and they shall have power to pass general ordi-
nances directing the mode and manner of summoning
jurors and grand jurors for the recorder'scourt.
Free Schools .
§47 . The mayor and councilmen shallhave power and
authority to establishone or more free schools in each
ward of said city,and may receive donations of real and
personal estate; to erect the necessary buildings, and to
provide the revenue for their maintenance by a tax on
the ward in which said school or schools shall be estab-
lished. But they may establish one city school for the
entire city,and upon itsbeing made a free school, may
provide the revenue for itsmaintenance by a tax on the
whole city.
Mayor .
48. That the mayor of said city shall be the chief
executive officerof said corporation,and it shall be his
duty tosee thatalllaws and ordinancesof the cityshall
be duly executed and put in force. Itshallbe his duty

630 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
to see that allthe officersappointed by the mayor and
board of councilmen shall discharge theirrespective du-
ties faithfullyand efficiently,and upon a failure of any
one of said officersso to do the mayor shallreportthe
delinquent to the council.
§49 . He shall have the care and superintendence of
allthe publicpropertyof saidcity,and under the direc-
tion and with the consent of the council he shall make
all contractson behalf of the cityfor publicworks and
improvements ,and see that they shall be conducted and
completed according to contract.
§ 50. He shall be superintendent of the market -house
and of the citywork house and prison, and visitorof the
cityschools,and perform such other dutiesas a majority
of the board of councilmen may think proper to impose
upon the chief executive officerof the city.
§51 . He shall keep the public seal of said city, and
shallkeep an officein some convenientplace in saidcity,
and he shallhave power to administeroaths. He shall
have power to summon a meeting of the board of coun-
cilmen at any time ,and so often as he may deem itadvis-
able,notwithstandingthey may have adjournedto a more
distantday . He shall,ifpresent,presideat allmeetings
of the board , except when his own salary may be the
subject of legislation; and in case of a tieon any ques-
tion he shallgive the casting vote,but he shallnot other-
wise have a vote in the board,exceptin the casesherein-
before specified.
§52. Itshallbe his duty from time to time to commu-
nicate to the board of councilmen all such information
and recommend all such measures as may tend to the
improvement of the finances,the police,health,security,
cleanliness,comfort , and ornaments of said city.
§ 53. That whenever the mayor of the city shall be
advised of any assemblage of persons anywhere in the
county of Fayette for the purpose of gaming or other
unlawful objects, it shalland may be lawful for said
mayor ,with the power of the county (ifrequired) to pro-
ceed to the place of such rendezvous or unlawful assem

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 631
blage ,with his marshal or other officeror officers, and to
arrestallpersonsso unlawfullyassembled,and forthwith
to bring them before any justiceof the peace of the
county ,to be dealt with according to law .
§54. That the mayor of saidcityshallhave power and
authority to examine witnesses under dedimuses from the
court within this or any other of the United States or
foreign country, and shall be entitledto receive a fee of
one dollar for each witness so examined , and in all cases
where he shall affix the seal of the corporation to any
document by request,he shallbe entitledto receivethe
sum of one dollar. He shall also have power and au-
thority to take depositionsin said cityin allother cases
where justicesof the peace are now authorized to take
the same .
§ 55. That themayor of saidcityshallbe compensated
for hisservicesby a salary of not exceeding one thousand
dollars,payable monthly out of the funds of the corpora-
tion,to be fixed by the board of councilmen ,exclusive of
the mayor , and said salary shallbe exclusive of allfees
which he may receive for certifyingdocuments of a pub-
licor private nature,or for services rendered to individ-
ualswhich he may be calledupon to perform or execute
in his officialcapacity : Provided, That the salary of no
officer shall be increased during the time for which he
may have been elected or appointed .
§ 56. That the mayor shallhave power , by and with
the adviceand consentof a majorityof allthecouncilmen
elected,at a called or stated meeting of the council, to
remitany fine,penalty,or forfeitureincurredor imposed
by any law or ordinauceof the city,and dischargefrom
the work -house any person thereinconfined. But in all
cases of remission of fines or discharge from imprison-
ment ,the same shallbe made conditioned on the offender
paying the costsof the prosecution, and if not so condi-
tioned,said costs shallbe paid out of the citytreasury.
§57. The mayor shallhave power ,by and with the
unanimous advice and consent of the police committee
of theboard of councilmen,to remit,in writing,any fine
or penaltyimposed by any ordinanceof said city,when

632 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the offenderis imprisonedin the work -house,and to dis-
charge from the work -house any prisoner confined there-
in. The written order of remission or release shall be
reported to and filedat the next regular meeting of the
mayor and board of councilmen .
§ 58. The mayor shalldismiss any member of the
policefor intermeddlingwith, or directlyor indirectly
taking part in,elections,further than to vote , for inten-
tional neglect of duty ,or for wanton breaches of law or
ordinance ,or the rulesfor the government of the police,
or for such other cause as he may deem sufficient. Any
policeman so dismissed may appeal to the police com-
mittee of the board of councilmen , and if the action of
the mayor be not sustained,the committee may restore
such policeman to duty until the next regular meeting of
the board of councilmen .
City Court.
§ 59. The city court of Lexington , heretofore estab-
lishedby law ,shallhave and possessconcurrentlywith
the Fayette circuitcourt,jurisdictionof all inquests of
idiocy or lunacy ,and of no other cases.
§ 60. The judge of said citycourt shall be elected as
now prescribed by law , and shall have the power to try
writs of lunacy , and none other; but in the absence of
the clerk of said court,said writs may be issued by the
judge. For his serviceshe shall receive the sum of
twenty -fivedollarsper annum salary, and where he shall
issue said writs,like fees as are now allowed by law to
the clerk .
§61 . The clerkof the citycourt shallbe electedas now
prescribedby law ,and shallonlyhave the power to issue
writs of lunacy and all other powers exercised by clerks
of the circuitcourtsat the trialof inquestsof lunacy and
idiocy,and none other,and forhisservicesshallreceive
like tees now allowed circuit clerks .
§ 62. The marshal of the citycourt shall be elected as
now prescribedby law,but shallexerciseand perform no
powers or dutiesexcept to execute theprocessesof the
citycourt,forwhich he shallreceive the fees now allowed

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 633
by law ,and he shall not receive any fees or salary for
any other service whatsoever .
Recorder's Court .
§ 63. There shallbe establishedin saidcitya court,to
be called " The Recorder's Court of the City of Lexing-
ton," which shallbe held by the recorder,who shallreceive
forhisservicessuch sum asthe mayor and board of coun-
cilmen shall fix: Provided ,It shall not exceed one thou-
sand dollarsper annum ,his fees excepted .
§ 64. Said courtshallhave exclusiveoriginaljurisdic-
tion in all prosecutions for violationsof the ordinances
of said city,and jurisdictionconcurrentwith the circuit
court of all pleas of the Commonwealth arising within
thelimitsof thecityof Lexington,exceptcasesof felony;
and said court shall have power and authority to take
recognizances from persons charged with offenses cog-
nizable before said court to appear and answer the same
that the circuitcourts have ,and the likepower to enforce
a compliance with the same ; and as to committing crimi-
nal offenders and sending them on for trial, said court
shall have the power of two justices of the peace of
Fayette county .
§ 65. Said court shall, concurrently with the Fayette
circuitcourt,exercise the power and jurisdictionwhich
isgiven herein in regard to the opening and extension of
streetsin said city,and similar proceedings shallbe had
in such cases in said court to those had in the circuit
court .
§66 . Said court shall have jurisdictionof allcases of
vagrancy and petitlarcenyarisingwithin said city,and
the justicesof the peace of Fayette county are hereby
requiredtomake therecognizancesof allpersonscharged
with being vagrants or with petitlarceny within the
city of Lexington ,returnable to the said recorder'scourt
insteadof to the circuitcourt; and upon convictionof
vagrancy or petitlarceny,the person so convictedshall
be sentencedto labor in the citywork -house fornot less
than three nor more than six months ; presentment or
indictment by a grand jury shallnot be necessary in cases

634 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of vagrancy orpetitlarceny,but the person charged with
eitherof these offenses may be arrestedon warrant and
triedby the recorder, and if found guilty,convicted by
him ; women may be so triedand convictedon charge of
being vagrants .
§ 67. The jurisdictionof the recorder of the city of
Lexington shallextend to allcases of riots, routs,or un-
lawfulassemblieswithinthecounty ofFayette,as herein-
after provided. Whenever said judge shall be advised,
or shall receive information on oath of any rout or un-
lawful assemblage of the people for the purpose of gam-
ing,or any unlawfulpurpose whatever,itshallbe lawful
for him to issue his warrant to arrest all such offenders ,
directedto the sheriffor any constable of Fayette county ,
or to the policeof the city,and if no officershouldbe at
hand to serve said warrant ,then to such discreet person
as he (said judge ) shall appoint ,returnable before some
justice of the peace of the county ,on which warrant it
shallbe the duty of the officeror otherperson to arrest
and bring before the said justiceof the peace of the
county of Fayette all persons who shall be found so
assembled,to be dealtwith by the justicesaccordingto
the law; and itshallbe the duty of the citizensof the
cityand county to attend the officeror other person,if
they should be thereunto summoned ,to aid and assistin
arrestingthe persons so found guiltyof infractingthe
law . That the recorder shall be entitled to and receive
a fee of two dollars in each case of vagrancy and petit
larceny at the trialof which he may preside,to be allowed
by the circuitcourt,and paid by the Commonwealth as
other fees are paid .
§ 68. Said court shall be holden at such places as the
mayor and council shall designate, or they failing to
designate a place, at such place as the judge shall
select; and thejudge shallhave power tofixsuch time
for the holding of his court as, in his discretion, the
cases coming before him for trialmay seem to him to
require. Said court shallbe a court of record, and shall
have a seal,which shallbe provided by the mayor and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 635
council,and shall be in the custody of the clerk of said
court,to be used and affixed by him as the seals of cir-
cuit courts are .
§69. Ifshallbe the duty of the clerk of the saidcourt
to keep a record of the proceedings of said court; to issue
alloriginalprocessforbringingpartiesbeforesaidcourt,
and executionsand precepts in itsjudgments ; and to
perform allthedutieswhich usuallypertainto the office
of clerk of a court in this Commonwealth . He shall
charge and receive the same fees which are allowed to
the clerk of the Fayette circuitcourt for like services;
and when he shallperform servicesforpayment whereof
no provision is made by law ,he shallcharge and receive
such fees as shallbe adjudged reasonable,and be allowed
by the judge of this court. The process of said court
may run into every county of thisCommonwealth ; and
when to be executed out of the Lexington citylimits, it
may be directedto the sheriffof any county,who shall
be entitled to the same fee for executing the same as for
executing like process from the circuitcourts; and the
chief of police and sheriffsare authorized and directed
to take bail upon the execution of such process in all
cases in which bail is required on like process issuing
from the circuit court .
§70 . All penaltiesfor breaches of the ordinances and
by -laws of the city shall be sued for by warrant in the
name of the city, and be for its use .
§71 . All persons convicted in the said recorder'scourt
shallbe confinedinthecitywork-houseatlaboruntilthe
fines assessed against them and the cost of prosecution
are discharged, at fiftycents per day , instead of being
confined in jail.
§72. That a returnof "not found" on a capiasprofine,
and of " no propertyfound" on a fi.fa.,issuedon the
same judgment in the said court,shall authorize an at-
tachment out of chancery in favor of the Commonwealth
or the cityagainst the choses in action and effectsof the
defendant or defendants , in the same manner that the
return of no property authorizes an attachment in chan-
cery or judgments in the circuitcourts.

636 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§73 . That all persons committed by the said court for
defaultof surety for good behavior or to keep the peace ,
and all others whom the city of Lexington is bound to
maintain when committed to the jailof Fayette county ,
shallbe confined in the citywork -house ,and they may be
compelled to labor as many days in each week ,at fifty
cents per day , as may be necessary to defray the reasona-
ble cost of their board ,to be , from time to time ,deter-
mined by the mayor and board of councilmen.
§74 . The judge of the said court shallhave power and
authority to examine witnesses under the dedimuses from
any court within this or any of the United States or
foreign country ,and shall be entitledto receive a fee of
one dollarfor each witness examined ; he shallalso have
power and authorityto take depositionsin saidcityinall
other cases where justices of the peace are now author-
ized to take the same : Provided,That said judge , forthe
performances of such duties and the exercise of such
powers as come withinthe jurisdictionof justicesof the
peace for the county of Fayette,shallbe entitledto de-
mand and receive the same fees, and no more , as are
allowed by law to justicesof the peace,exceptthe salary
hereinprovidedfor,and he shallhave the rightto issue
fee-billsand collectthem in like manner as justices of
the peace now have .
§ 75. That allrecognizancestaken by thejudge of the
recorder'scourt when the day is given for the appear-
ance of the party,exceptin cases in which he has juris-
diction to try the matter ,shall be for appearance in the
Fayette circuitcourt,and all recognizances ,except those
above excepted, shall be returned to the clerk of the
said court under the hand of the said judge , and shallbe
docketed as recognizances taken and returned by magis-
trates. The said judge on the examination of criminal
offenders shall make out a statement of the evidence , and
shall recognize the witnesses, and shall return the state-
ment of the evidence and the recognizance to the clerk
of the Fayette circuitcourt.
§ 76. That no judgment for costs shall be rendered in
favor of the cityfor a greater amount than the fine,unless

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 637
the judge of the recorder'scourt shallbe of opinionthat
a judgment for fullcosts should be rendered against the
offender,and shallso certifyon the record.
§77 . That whenever the fees of the chief of police or
clerk are dischargedby confinement in the work -house,
they shallbe paid out of the citytreasury.
§78 . That the cityof Lexington shall be allowed out
of finescollectedon prosecutionsin the name of the
Commonwealth in the recorder'scourt,where the same
exceeds five hundred dollars heretofore given to the city
to pay to the clerk of the recorder'scourt as compensa-
tion for his servicesin criminal cases,fortydollars.
§79 . The attorneyof the city of Lexington shallbe
entitled to the like fees for the like services as are allowed
by law to the prosecuting attorneys in the circuitcourts
of this Commonwealth .
§ 80. The judge of the recorder'scourt shall have con-
current originaljurisdictionwith justicesof the peace in
all civil cases in said city, where , by the laws of this
State ,justicesof the peace are,or shallbe , authorized to
hear and determine , or in any manner have power to
act; and all persons aggrieved by his decision in such
cases shallhave the right of appeal to the Fayette circuit
court on the same terms and conditions, and subject to
the same restrictionsand limitations, as he or she might
or could have had if such judgment had been rendered
by a justiceof the peace in Fayettecounty. Allprocess
in civilcases shall be directed to the chief of police of
saidcity,and executedby himselfor deputy,and he shall
be entitled to receive the same fees for the like services
which constables are entitled to receive, and have the
same power and authoritywithinsaidcity; and for such
civil servicesthe said judge shall be allowed to receive
likefees with justicesof the peace, and collectablein
like manner .
§ 81. The mayor and board of councilmen shallhave
power to fix and regulate witness fees in the said re-
corder's court : Provided , That said fees shall not be in-
creasedbeyond thosenow allowedby law .

638 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 82. No one shall be eligibleto the office of recorder
unless a citizenof thisState, and shallhave been a resi-
dent of the cityof Lexington for at leastone year , and a
practicingattorneyfortwo years precedinghis election.
§ 83. That , in the absence of the recorder of the city
of Lexington at any sessionof the recorder'scourt,or if
in any case itmay not be proper forhim to sit,the mayor
of the city may act in his stead, and in the absence of
both the recorder and mayor ,or if in any case itmay not
be properforeitherof them to sit, the members of the
bar presentmay electone of theirown number to act as
recorderpro tem.,and themayor or suchpersonso elected,
as the case may be,shallhave the same judicialauthority
and exercisethe same judicialpowers forthe time being
as ispossessedand exercisedby the recorderunder the
charter .
Chief of Police.
§84. In the month of August of each year the mayor
and board of councilmen shall elect a chief of police.
who shall enter on the duties of his office on the first
Monday of September following, and remain in office
untilhis successor is elected and qualified.
§ 85. The chief of police may ,by and with the advice
and consent of the mayor and board of councilmen ,ap-
point a deputy, who shallexerciseall the powers and
perform allthe dutiesrequiredof saidchiefof police.
§ 86. The chiefof policeshall,by himself or with his
deputy,attend allthe sessionsof thecounciland of the
recorder'scourt,execute the orders thereof, and preserve
order thereat; he shall, by himself or with his deputy ,
execute all orders emanating from the recorder's court
directed to him; he shallcollectthe fees of the judge and
the clerk of the recorder's court, and of the mayor , if
required. He and his deputy shallexecute bond ,with
sufficientsurety,in an adequate penalty,before the mayor
and councilmen ,to the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,con-
ditioned that they willfaithfullydischarge all the duties
of theiroffice,and to pay over allsums of money that
may come intotheirhands to the persons entitledthereto;

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 639
and a lien shallexiston the lands of the chief of police,
hisdeputy,and theirsureties,from the time of executing
bond , for all sums of money that shall come into their
hands . He shall be entitled to receive the same fees for
the like services which sheriffsare entitled to receive ,and
shallhave thesame power to collectthem . He and his
deputy , and allothers to whom the process of the record-
er's court shall be directed and come for execution , shall
be bound to execute and return the same within the time
prescribedby law for sheriffsto executeand returnsimi-
lar process,and on their failure they and their sureties
shallbe liableto the same finesand penaltiesthat sheriff's
are, as also for not paying over moneys collectedon exe-
cutions,and for making illegalcharges ,for falsereturns,
and other illegalacts; and said courtshallhave power
and jurisdictionto hear and determine motions against
them and their sureties for failures in like manner as the
circuitcourt has power and jurisdictionto hear and de-
termine motions against defaulting sheriffs, or to proceed
by fineand imprisonment to enforcethe due execution
and return of process as other courts of record; and for
theirservicesthe chiefof policeand his deputy shall
receive such fees or salary as the mayor and board of
councilmen may ,by ordinance,direct.
§ 87. Itshallbe the duty of the chiefof policeto col-
lect all fees due to the clerk of the recorder's court when
fee-billsthereforshall be placed in his hands for collec-
tion,in likemanner as is prescribedby law in relation
to clerks' feesof circuitand county courts,and he shall
receive the same compensation for receiving, collecting,
and paying over said feeswhich is allowed sheriffsfor
likeservices; and he shall be liable to motion in the re-
corder'scourt,in case of delinquencyor breach of official
duty ,in likemanner as sheriffsare in the circuitcourt.
And the clerk shallbe liableto the same finesand penal-
ties for illegalcharges or any other illegalacts as the
clerks of the circuitcourts,to be recovered in the same
manner before the said recorder's court .

640 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Salaries .
§ 88. The mayor and board of councilmen shall have
power , from time to time,to fix,change, and alterthe
salariesand fees of all the officersof the city, and to fix
the compensation of those holding said offices, either by
salaryor by fees; and wherever a fixedsalaryshallbe
attached to any officefor which fees are hereinbefore pro-
vided for,allsuch feesshallrevert and be for the use and
benefitof the city: Provided, That the salary or fees at-
tached to any officeshall not be increased beyond those
now allowed by law ; and the salary or compensation of
no officershall be increased or diminished during his term
of office .
Collectorsof Revenue .
§89 . No collectorof revenues of the city shall, after
the expirationof two successiveterms of office,be re-eli-
gible,or act as deputy for a succeeding term .
Exemptions .
§ 90. The mayor and board of councilmen shall have
power to exempt from allcityand municipal tax the
libraryand allthe property,realand personal,belonging
to the Lexington Library Association, or to the Orphans '
Home ,or to any other benevolent or charitableinstitution
in the city of Lexington , which may be designated by
ordinance or resolution, and shall have the power to
exempt from municipal taxation,for a term not to exceed
ten years,allmanufactories employing fiftyhands or over
in said city.
§ 91. That themayor and board of councilmen of the
cityof Lexington are hereby authorizedand empowered
to issue the bonds of said cityin any amount not exceed-
ing twenty-fivethousand ($25,000) dollars, in sums of
not less than one hundred nor more than one thousand
dollars,bearing interest at a rate not exceeding six per
cent.per annum from theirdate,and payable thirtyyears
thereafter, but redeemable at any time after ten years
therefrom, at the option of the city,the intereston said
bonds to be payable semi-annuallyat such place as the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 641
mayor and board of councilmen shallby ordinance ap-
point. Said mayor and board of councilmen shall first
apply the proceeds arisingfrom the sale of said bonds to
the payment of the present floatingdebt of the city, and
the remainder of said proceeds ,if there be any ,shallbe
held as a fund to be appliedto the payment of the bonds
of the cityissuedto the Lexington and Big Sandy Rail-
road Company .
§ 92. That at the election to be held on the firstMon-
day in August ,1882 , for county officers,or at a special
electionto be held at any time previous thereto that the
mayor and board of councilmen may appoint,aftergiv-
ing notice for the length of time now required by law ,
there shall be opened at each voting place within the city
of Lexington a pollfor the purpose of taking the sense
of the qualifiedvoters of said cityupon the question of
the adoption or rejectionof thischarter; and it shallbe
the duty of the mayor and board of councilmen to ap-
point the inspectors to conduct said election, who shall
be qualified as now provided by law to give notice of
the same ,to provide the necessarypoll-books,and to do
all the acts and things in the manner and form as now
provided by law in the case of electionsfor city officers.
It shall be the duty of said inspectors to cause two col-
umns to be opened in said poll-books,in one of which
shall be registered the names of all those voting for the
adoption of this charter, and in the other column the
names of all those voting against its adoption ; and it
shall be the duty of said inspectors, or one of them , to
propound to each person offeringto vote the question,
" Do you vote in favor of the adoption of the new char-
teror againstit?" and hisname shallbe trulyenteredin
the proper column of the poll-book according to his
answer and vote. Said polls shall be compared and

CHAPTER 1146 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act to establishan academy in
Boone county," approved January 8th, 1814, and an act amendatory
thereof, approved March 3d , 1842 .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act, entitled" An act to establishan
academy in Boone county," approved January 8th, 1814,
and an act,entitled" An act to change the name of the
Boone County Academy , and for other purposes," ap-
proved March 3d, 1842,be amended so thathereafterthe
trustees of said academy , now known by the name of
" Morgan Academy ," shallhave power and authorityto
lease for school purposes forwhite childrenany of the
rooms of the academy building which may not be occu-
pied , and used at any time by authority of said trustees
as an academic school; and theyshallapplythe rentsand

646 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
profitsaccruing therefrom to repairing the academy build-
ing,and the fencing around the academy grounds .
§2 . The trusteesof said academy shallhave power
and authorityto levy a poll-tax on each patron of the
academy , not exceeding fivedollarson each patron in
any one year, for the purpose of repairingand keeping
in repairthe lot fencing, and making the necessaryre-
pairs on the academy building, which tax the trustees
aforesaidshallhave power to collectby suit in any court
of competent jurisdictionin Boone county .
§3 . Said trustees shall settletheir accounts annually
with the judge of the Boone county court, showing in
said settlement the amount of money received by them
from allsources during the past year , for what purposes
the same is expended , and the balance on hand at the
time of settlement. The clerk of the Boone county court
shall record the settlements of said trustees in the settle-
ment book of his office as a guardian's settlement is re-
corded ; but neither judge nor clerk of said court shall
be allowed to charge any fee for making and recording
said settlements .
§ 4. Itshallbe the duty of saidcountyjudge to require
the settlements with said trustees to be made as herein
provided; and forthatpurpose he shall,by a ruleof his
court, compel their attendance, and shallenforce the
observance of the rule by a fine of ten dollarson each
trustee for each failure to observe the same , and such
finesshallbe appliedforthe benefitof the academy .
* § 5. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 19 , 1882 ,

CHAPTER 1147 .
AN ACT to authorizethe county court of Lincoln county to make an ap-
propriationto aid in the constructionof a turnpikeroad in saidcounty .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Lincoln county ,a ma-
jorityof thejusticesof the peace of said county agree

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 647
ing thereto,be,and they are hereby,empowered to take
stockto the amount of eighthundreddollarsper mile,
and make an appropriationto pay forsame ,to aid in the
constructionof a turnpike road from the town of Stan-
ford to the villageof Preachersville,in said county.
§2 . The beginning point of said turnpikemay be at
the town of Stanford,and shallrun thence by the shortest
and most practicableroute to Preachersville,crossingthe
turnpike road leading from Stanford to Crab Orchard , at
a point not exceeding three and a half miles from Stan-
ford; or itmay begin at a pointupon said turnpike not
exceeding three and a half miles from Stanford,and run
thence by the shortest and most practicable route to
Preachersville .
§3 . To enable said county court to pay the subscrip-
tion and appropriation herein provided for, said court
may levy an ad valorem tax sufficientto pay therefor:
Provided, That said subscriptionand appropriationshall
not be paid to the board of directorsor other officersof
the company constructing said road until a sufficient
amount of stock,includingthe stockhereinprovidedfor,
has been paid , or secured to be paid, to complete the
building of said road .
§ 4. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 19, 1882.
CHAPTER 1148 .
AN ACT to authorize the Pendleton county court to appoint a collector to
collectthe unpaid taxes and fee-billsdue Minor Colvin, late sheriffof
Pendleton county,forthe benefitof Geo .B. Dance , W B. Shipp , Jack-
son Ducker ,and Louis Lovelace, his sureties.
WHEREAS , Minor Colvin qualifiedas sheriffof Pendle-
ton county in January, 1877,executed bond,with W. B.
Shipp, Jackson Ducker, Louis Lovelace, and Geo . B.
Dance , as his sureties; and whereas ,the said Minor Col-
vin failedto pay allthe county levytaxes collectedby
him for said county,and was sued by saidcounty in the
Pendletoncircuitcourt,and a judgmentrenderedagainst
him and his suretiesfor over thirty-sixhundred ($3,600)

648 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
dollars; and whereas,the saidDance ,Shipp,Ducker ,and
Lovelace were compelled to pay and securein payment ,
and have never been reimbursed in the payment of the
aforesaid judgment ; and whereas ,there yet remains un-
paid to said Colvin taxes and fee-bills; therefore,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be lawful for the judge of the Pen-
dletoncounty courtto appoint a suitableperson,a citizen
of said county ,a collectorto collectthe taxes and fee-
billsdue and unpaid to the saidColvin; and the said
collectorshallhave power and authorityto collectsaid
taxes and fee-bills, and shall have all the remedies of
collecting the same that sheriffs have under the existing
law : Provided, Said collector,before he proceeds to act
under and by virtue of the appointment herein provided
for, shall execute bond in the county court of Pendleton
county,with approved security,for the faithfulperform-
ance of hisduty,and he and his suretiesshallbe liable
on saidbond forany malfeasanceor misfeasanceas sher-
iffsare under the existinglaws ; and any person aggrieved
thereby shallhave the same remedy as are allowed against
sheriffsfor seizure or sales: Provided, That before the
appointment of said collector, the said Colvin consents
thereto,eitherin writing or in open court.
§2 . The said collectorshallpay over to the said Shipp ,
Dance , Ducker , and Lovelace ,all amounts collectedby
him ,and said collectorshallhave the same power to exe-
cute receiptsfor said unpaid taxes and fee-billsas the
said Colvin would have were he himself collectingthe
same .
§3 . The furthertime of one year isgiven the said col-
lector from the passage of this act in which to restrain
and collect all due and uncollected fee -bills and taxes
coming to the said Colvin as sheriffof Pendleton county ..
§4 . This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved April 19, 1882.

CHAPTER 1154 .
AN ACT for the benefitof Mrs. HarriettF. Walston , of Calloway county,

Kentucky ,isthe mother of twelve livingchildren; there-
fore ,

CHAPTER 1155 .
AN ACT to regulatethe selling, loaning, or giving away of spirituous
vinous,or malt liquors,or any mixture of the same , in Bullittsvillevoting
precinct,and within threemiles of the BullittsvilleChristianChurch , in
Boone county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe unlawful for any person to sell,
loan,or give away any spirituous,vinous ,or malt liquors,
or any mixture of the same , within the boundaries of the
Bullittsvillevoting precinct,and within three miles of the
BullittsvilleChristianChurch,in Boone county,and any

654 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
personwho shallsell,loan,or giveaway any such liquors,
or any mixture of the same , within said bounds , shall,
upon indictmentand conviction,be finednot lessthan
fiftynor more than one hundred dollarsforeach offense.
§2 . The provisionsof this act shall not apply to any
druggistwho , in good faith, shallsellsuch liquorsfor
medicinalpurposes,on a prescriptionmade and signed
by a regularpracticingphysician; but no druggistshall
sellsuch liquorsexcept on such prescription; and no
physicianshallmake or signany such prescriptionunless
thepersonforwhom itis made isactuallysick,and such
liquorsare necessarilyrequiredas a medicine,which fact
shallbe statedin the prescription; and any physician
who makes or signs,or permitshis name to be signedto
any prescriptionfor such liquors,except as provided for
in thisact,shallbe guiltyof a violationof thisact,and
upon indictmentand convictionshallbe finednot less
than twenty -five nor more than fiftydollars for each
offense .
§3 . No prescriptionshallbe good forthe obtainingof
any such liquorsmore than one time ,and shallbe taken
up and filedaway by theperson to whom itispresented,
and may be used as evidence in trialon any indictment
under this act .
§4 . The owner or owners of such liquors sold by a
clerk or agent , or given away by such clerk or agent,
contraryto theprovisionsof thisact,shallbe considered
guilty of a violationof this act,and shall be subject to
the penaltiesherein imposed in section one of thisact.
§5 . Any person who shall falsely represent his own
conditionoftheconditionof anotherpersontoany physi-
cian,and therebyobtain a prescriptionfor such liquors,
shall be guiltyof a violation of this act, and upon an
indictment and conviction shall be fined not less than ten
nor more than fiftydollars; and ifany personshallforge
the name of any physicianto any such prescription,he
shallbe fined,upon indictmentand conviction,two hun-
dred dollars,and imprisoned in the county jailnot less
than six months .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 655
§ 6. This act shall be submitted to the qualifiedvoters
within the above mentioned bounds the firstMonday in
August , 1882, and it shall be lawful for any qualified
voter within the above mentioned bounds to vote upon
thispropositionat Bullittsville. Itshallbe the duty of
the clerk,or one of the judges of said election,to pro-
pound to each voter the following question: " Are you for
or against the sale of spirituous,vinous,or malt liquors? "
The clerkshallkeep a recordof the votes cast,and if a
majorityof the votes upon thispropositionare against
the saleof spirituous,vinous, or malt liquors, then this
act shall take effect and be in force .
Approved April 19, 1882.
CHAPTER 1156 .
AN ACT to regulatethe allowance of claims against Livingston county,
and making itthe duty of the county attorneyto appeal from same where
it isnot made according to law .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That hereafter all claims against the county of
Livingston ,to be allowed or passed on by the court of
levy and claims,shall be made out by the claimant and
filedwith the county attorney of said county at leastten
days before the commencement of the term of said court;
and no claim shallbe considered or allowed by said court
unless so filed; but this act shallnot apply to the pay of
the officersof the courtfor servicesrenderedby them ,or
any of them , during the term of said court.
§2 . That hereafterwhen any claim shallbe presented
by any one ,if the amount be twenty -five dollars or up-
wards ,and any part of said claim shallbe allowed by said
court,the county of Livingstonshallhave the right of
appeal to the circuitcourt,and thence to the Court of
Appeals , without executing bond ; and it shall be the
duty of the county attorney to take such appeal when-
ever any partof such claimhas been improperlyallowed.
Approved April 19, 1882.

CHAPTER 1157 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the city of Newport ,Campbell county,
providing forsewerage in said city.

of Kentucky :
§1 . That the board of councilmen of the city of New-
port, Campbell county , shall have full power to build
sewers in said cityupon the petitionof the owners of a
majority of frontfeet abutting upon the square or squares
of the street, alley, lane , road , avenue , or thoroughfare
under which the sewer is to be built . Said sewers to be
built in such manner and with such materials as the said
board of councilmen may deem fit and proper , at the
exclusivecostand expenseof saidowners of the property
abuttingon the streetunder which the same may be built,
to be apportionedpro rataby saidboard accordingto the
number of frontfeet owned by them respectively, by
assessmentand levyof a specialtaxata rateper lineal
foot of the real estate abutting on that portion of the
street,& c.,sewered ,and shallbe due and payable within
thirty(30) days afterthe completionof the work and the
acceptance thereofby said board . Said cost and expense
shallattach, exist, and continue a lien against said real
estate from the time said contract for building said sewer
is awarded untilpaid; and if payment thereof be not
made within(30) thirtydays afterthe acceptanceof the
work , the contractor in whose favor said lien shall exist
may enforce the same , with interestthereon, in any court
having jurisdictionthereof. Errorsin theproceedingsof
said board shallnot exempt said owners from payment ,
after the work has been done as required by ordinance
or contract; but said board or the court in which suit
may be pending shall make all necessary corrections,
rules and orders,and do justice to allparties concerned ;
but in no event ,if the ordinance providing for said con-
struction of sewer has been passed as required in this
section, shallthe citybe liablefor said construction. An
estimate of the work required to be done in the construc-
tionof the sewer ,including the tearing up and re-making

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 657
of the street, shall be firstascertained before the ordi-
nance providing thereforshallbe passed ; and the expense
thereof,including the cost of intersectionsadjoining,shall
be added thereto and distributedpro rata thereon, and
fixedbefore any contract shallbe entered into by the city
for said construction of sewer , & c. Said estimate and
-expenseshallbe embraced in,and form part of,saidcon-
tract,and the tax hereinbefore provided shallnot exceed

mittee of improvements shallfurnish said board of coun-
cilmen ,at theirnext meeting thereafter,with a certificate
that said sewer is completed and accepted ; and if said

CHAPTER 1162 .

for the purpose of constructing a macadamized or gravel
turnpike road in the county of Washington , from the
Bloomfield and SpringfieldTurnpike Road at T. S.
Grundy's ,down said creek to the turnpike road from
Springfieldto Bardstown, at or near where the road
down said creek now intersectsat Loyd Thompson's .

thousand dollars,to be divided into shares of fiftydollars
each .

books for the subscriptionof stock aforesaidat such

times and in such places as any three of said commis-
sioners may deem expedient .

cessors are elected and qualified. The time and place

ileges and franchisesincident to like corporations; shall

dain all such by -laws ,rules and regulations, as may be
