CHAPTER 1171 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the cityof Dayton ,in Campbell county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 That section twelve of the act of the General As-
sembly of Kentucky , approved March 7, 1876, entitled
"An act to revise and amend the charter of the cityof
Dayton , in Campbell county," be amended as follows:
Said tax-billsshallbe each for half-yearly payments ,due
and payable , the firsthalf on or before the twentieth day
of June , and the second half on or before the twentieth
day of December in each and every year ,instead of the
fifteenthday of July and the fifteenthday of January in
each year as heretofore. The city clerk shall, within
twenty days after said bills are ordered to be made out ,
deliverthe same to the citytreasurerfor collection,taking
his receiptfor the gross amount of the taxes contained in
said bills. The treasurer,upon receiptthereof,shallpost
noticesin each ward of said citythat the tax-billsfor the
current year are in hands forcollection,and that payment
will be required to be made , one half on or before the
twentieth day of June , and the other half on or before
the twentieth day of December as aforesaid, and that in
detaultof payment , the prescribedpenalty will be at-
tached thereto. Payment of alltax-billsshallbe officially
indorsed by the treasurer, and all tax-billsdue on the
twentiethday of June remainingin hishands unpaid on
the firstMonday of July,and thosedue on the twentieth
day of December remainingunpaid on the firstMonday
of January,shallbe indorsedby him " delinquent," and
withinfivedays thereafterreturnedto thecityclerk,who
shallthereuponentera creditthereforupon thetreasurer's

668 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
receipt,and then add to such tax-billssuch penalty as may
be prescribed by ordinance , not exceeding fifteenper
cent., and re-deliverthe same to the treasurer, who shall
receipt therefor; and all delinquent tax-billsshall,with
the penalty thereon,be added to the next semi -annual
tax-billon the same property,and shallconstituteone of
the items thereof, and shallso continue to be added until
paid; and a lienin favor of the cityshallexistand con-
tinue upon said property for alltaxes,penalties, and cost
untilthe same be fullypaid. The citymay , at any time
afterthe said semi -annual tax-billsbecome due ,give them
into the hands of a delinquent tax-collectorappointed by
said council, under such terms as said council shalldeter-
mine ,or said billsmay be committed to the attorney of
said cityfor collection,by suitor otherwise,in the Camp-
bell chancery or circuitcourt,and enforce the lienhereby
retained as other liens are enforced ; or in the mayor's
court of said cityfor personal judgment , as the said
council shall determine .
§ 2. That the following lands be , and they are hereby ,
added to, and made part of, said cityof Dayton , and
subject to allthe laws and regulations of said cityexcept
as hereinafter provided , the following described lot or
parcelof land,viz: beginningin a southerlylineof Fair-
fieldavenue ,north 48 degrees east 21.46 feetfrom the
northeasterly corner of lot No. 1 of Gilmore's second
additionto said city; thence with the southerlyline of
Fairfield avenue , north 485 degrees east 569.54 feet;
thence with another line of Fairfieldavenue , north 524
degrees east 147.66 feet,to a corner of Joseph Taylor's
addition; thence with a line of said Taylor'saddition,
south 41 degrees east 365 feet,to the southerly line of
Gardner street; thence with the southerly line of Gard-
ner street extended south 51 degrees west 216.33 feet, to
the easterlyline of Vine street; thence with the easterly
line of Vine street, south 414 degrees east 105 feet;
thence south 48 degrees west 503.54 feet; thence north
414 degrees west 470 feetto the place of beginning ,con-
taining7.24 acres; the said land being subdividedinto

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 669
lots, streets, and alleys,the lots being numbered from 25
to 77,inclusive: Provided, That the said cityof Dayton
shalltax saidlotsonly as they are sold by Mrs. E. T.
Gilmore ,the owner thereof,commencing with 1882 ; and
alllots remaining unsold by the said Mrs. E. T. Gilmore
shallnot be taxed by said city untilthe year 1884, when
all of said lots shall be taxed by the said city as other
city property, whether remaining the property of Mrs.
E. T. Gilmore or in the hands of others .
§3 . That the city council of the cityof Dayton may
appoint an attorney,who shallbe called the cityattorney,
to serve for not more than one year . Said attorney shall
be the legal adviser of said citycouncil; shallprosecute
and defend for said cityin allcases in which said cityis
a party; attend allmeetings when requested to do so by
the president of council or chairman of the law com-
mittee; shallgive written opinions when required; shall
receive such compensation and in such manner as council
shall determine , said salary to be fixed at the same time
as the salariesof other cityofficersare fixed. He shall
give bond to said cityfor the payment of allmoney due
said citycollectedby him ; he shall examine the mayor's
docket annually ,and report,in writing,the amount found
due therein due said city. All laws and parts of laws in
regard to the duties of city attorney are repealed by this
act .
§4 . That the city council of the city of Dayton are
hereby authorized and empowered to issue the bonds of
said cityto any amount not exceeding in the aggregate
the sum of one hundred and twenty -fivethousand dollars.
Said bonds or their proceeds are to be used exclusively
and for no other purpose than to pay offand fund the
present outstanding bonds and bonded indebtedness of
said city. Said bonds shall be of the denomination of
$ 20 , $ 50,$ 100,$ 500, and $ 1,000 each , as said city coun-
cilshalldetermineby ordinance; they shallbe numbered
serially, commencing with number one ; they shall be
made payable thirtyyears afterdate, with the option of
saidcityto pay them offat any time aftertwo yearsfrom

670 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
theirdate,bearing interestfrom their date at the rate of
six per cent, per annum ,payable semi-annually,the in-
terestas it matures,and the principalwhen due,to be
made payable at the officeof the citytreasurerof the city
of Dayton ,tobe designatedin saidbonds. Interestcou-
pons shall be attached for each semi -annual installment
of interest,the said coupons to be numbered seriallyfrom
one to sixty,inclusive,and each coupon to bear the serial
number of the bond to which it is attached . Each of said
bonds shallbe signed by the president of the citycouncil
of said city, and countersigned by city clerk, and shall
have the seal of the city atfixedthereto. Each of said
interestcoupons shallbe signed by the cityclerk. Said
bonds and coupons shallbe made payable to bearer, and
shallpass by delivery. Said bonds so issued may be
placedby thecitycouncilin the possessionof a board of
commissioners , to consist of the president of the city
council, the city treasurer, and city clerk of the city of
Dayton , and said board shallhave the right to dispose of
said bonds ,or any portion of them , at not less than their
par value , and with the proceeds to pay off and redeem
any of the present outstanding bonds of said city, or they
may exchange any of said bonds for any of the present
outstanding bonds , at not lessthan theirpar value. Said
board shallreportinwritingto saidcitycouncilquarterly,
and oftenerif required by said council,the number and
amount of the bonds disposed of by them , and the num-
ber,character,and amount of outstandingbonds paid or
taken in exchange , and they shalldeliver to said council
the bonds so paid or exchanged , and the same shall be
canceledand destroyed. The citycouncilshallcause the
said refundingbonds to be lithographedand made into
book form,with stub attached,showing the number and
amount of bond issued,to whom , whether for money or
bond redeemed , and if bond , the issue, number , series,
and amount thereof. Any person who shalluse or ap-
propriate any of the bonds herein mentioned ,or any of
their proceeds , otherwise than in the manner herein
directed, shall be deemed guilty of embezzlement , and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 671
upon conviction, punished as provided by statute in
cases of embezzlement . 1
§5 . That sectionten and section twenty -seven of the
act of the General Assembly of Kentucky ,approved March
7th,1876,entitled" An act to reviseand amend the charter
of the cityof Dayton ,in Campbell county ," be so amended
that the treasurerof the city of Dayton shallexecute a
bond as treasurerof the cityofDayton ,and as custodian
of the sinking fund of said city, in the sum of $ 10,000,
instead of, as heretofore in said act ,$ 25,000 as treasurer ,
and $ 10,000 as custodian of the sinking fund; all other
portions and provisions of said section are continued in
full force and effect .
6. That the school board of the cityof Dayton are
hereby authorized and empowered to elect a treasurer
for said school board , who shall be the custodian of all
moneys due the schoolsof the cityof Dayton ,both the
money coming from the city of Dayton due said schools,
and that due said schools from the common school fund
of the State of Kentucky . Said treasurershallbe elected
at the firstmeeting of the school board in the month of
March in each and every year ,to serve for one year . He
shallexecutebond to the cityof Dayton ,with good and
sufficientsureties,in the sum of $ 5,000,to be approved
by the citycouncilof said city. It shallbe the duty of
the said treasurerto inspectthe books of the citytreas-
urer once in each month , and see the amount of money
collecteddue said schools; collectand receiptto said city
treasurer for the same , and collect and receipt for the
money coming from the State of Kentucky due said
schools. He shall perform such other dutiesas said
schoolboard may from time to time direct He may be
removed by said city council for cause . Said school
board shall distributeand pay out the money in the
school fund of said cityfor the purpose of maintaining
the schools of Dayton , and their orders for money shall
be drawn on the treasurerof the school fund of the city
of Dayton . He shall receive such salary as the city
council may determine , not to exceed the sum of $ 100
per annum .

672 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 7. This act shall take effectand be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved April 20 , 1882.

Green county , near their mill in said county, to facilitate
their business , a boom ,
§2 . But if, in the judgment of the county judge of
Green county ,itwillbetter serve the interestof the peo-
ple interestedin the use of said river,the saidWright &
Brother may be compelled to keep erected near the bank ,
on either side, a section of the boom higher than fifteen
inches; but there shall not be less than fiftyfeet space
between said sections .
§ 3. Said Wright & Brother shall, within thirty days
from the passage of thisact,make a bond , with sufficient
security, before the county judge of Green county, in
which they shallcovenant and agree with the Common-
wealth of Kentucky that they willpay to any person any
damage they may sustain by the erection of said boom ,
and any person aggrieved may sue on said bond in any
courthavingjurisdictioninGreen county; but thisshall
not prevent suit by parties aggrieved in courts having
jurisdictionin other counties.
§4 . That said Wright & Brother shallnot raftor float
any logs of any kind on said river without firstmaking
the same in a manner plain to be seen; and that said
Wright & Brother shall be liable to indictment in the
Green circuitcourtfortaking and convertingany logsor
other property not their own to their own use , and if
found guiltyof this offense, shall be fined in a sum of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 673
not less ten nor more than one hundred dollars , at the
.discretion of the jury , and shall be liableto pay to any
personwhose property may be taken and converted as
above set forthdouble itsvalue. They shall, at the
request of any person desiring to raft or drive logs or
lumber down said river, conduct the same over said
boom , or cause it to be done , and ,in addition,shall be
liableto pay such personsany damage sustainedin the
passingof saidlogs or lumber over saidboom .
§ 5. That said Wright & Brother shall remove the ob-
structionsin the riveroccasioned by the building of the
boom in ninety days from the passage of thisact.
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 20, 1882.

of the " Covington Light Guards ," a volunteercompany
in the State Guard ,at the rental of $ 400 per annum , and

four months and five days ,to-wit: from the firstday of
January,1879,tothe fifthday of May ,1881,and thereis
due to said Drexelius the sum of $ 518,and it has been
judiciallydetermined that the county of Kenton cannot
by law be coerced to pay the same ; now ,therefore,

of Kentucky :

elius, of Covington , Kentucky , the sum of $ 518 out of

and the Auditor is hereby authorized and directed to

paring such metals and timber for market , and transport-

CHAPTER 1180 .
AN ACT providing for the improvement of the Fountain Ferry Road in
Jeffersoncounty, from the western boundary of the cityof Louisvilleto
the bank of the Ohio river,at the costof the property benefitedthereby.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . WHEREAS , A large majority of persons owning
property within a distance of eight hundred feet on
eithersideof the Fountain Ferry Road ,from the western
boundary lineof the cityof Louisville to the Ohio river,
have petitioned thisGeneral Assembly to pass an act to
enable the county court of Jefferson county to improve
said Fountain Ferry Road by taxing or assessing the
costsof such improvement upon the real estatebenefited
thereby ; now ,in accordance with the prayer of said peti.
tion, it is enacted that the land embraced within and
described by the boundary line,beginning at a point in
the western boundary line of the city of Louisvillein the
centre of the Fountain Ferry Road ,and measuring 800
feetnorth,and 800 feetsouth along the western boundary
of said city; thence westwardly in two parallellines,
each 800 feet from the center of said Fountain Ferry
Road to the bank of the Ohio river,is hereby created a
tax districtforthe improvement of said road , and W. R.
Ray ,J. F. Pelle,Joseph Schulte, J. W. Stine, and Henry
Knodler are hereby appointed commissioners ,who ,or a
majority of whom , shall appoint a competent engineer ,
with power to remove him at pleasure,and fixhis com-
pensation by the month , who shall, under the said com-
missioners,superintend the work hereinafterprovided for.
§2 . The said commissioners shall advertisefor pro-
posals to construct the necessary bridges and culverts,
and to grade and macadamize said Fountain Ferry Road
of such width as they may deem advisable, and to the

684 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
best interestof the property, and shall award the con-
tractfordoing said work to the lowest and best bidder.
§3 . The party to whom the contract is awarded shall
enter into a written contract with the said commissioners ,
with good security,tobe approved by saidcommissioners,
for the full and faithfulcompletion of the work accord -
ing to the specificationswhich shallhave been prepared
by the engineer ,which contract and specificationsshall
be filedin the Jeffersoncounty court.
§ 4. Upon the completion of as much as one thousand
lineal feet of the work according to the contract, the
engineer shallgive public notice of the factby one inser-
tionof a noticein one of thedailynewspapers published
in the cityof Louisville, and name in said notice a day
on which he and the commissioners aforesaid will attend .
and inspect the part of the work reported as completed ,
and on the day named said engineer and said commis-
sioners,or a majority of the same ,shall attend and ex-
amine the work ; and it ismade the duty of allpersons-
owning real property in said districtto attend and ex-
amine said work on said day , and if said work has not
been done according to the contract and specifications,it
shallnot be accepted ; but if, on inspection,itbe found ,by
said engineer and a majority of said commissioners , that
theimprovement has been made in accordance and com-
pliance with the contract and specifications,then itshall
be accepted ,and said engineer shallgive like notice,and
have like inspection for or upon each section of one
thousand feet, as the same shallbe completed ; and when
each of saidsectionsshallhave been accepted,the said
engineer and commissioners shallat once report the same
in writing as completed ,and made in accordance with
the contract,which shall be finaland conclusive on that
subject .
§ 5. Upon such reportbeing made ,itshallbe theduty
of said Jeffersoncounty court to cause to be ascertained
the names of owners of each and every square foot of
land within the tax district described in the first section
hereof, and to assessthe entirecost of said improvement

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 685
of said road ,including the compensation allowed the en-
gineer,and other fees and costs,equally upon each square
foot of land within said district, and a lien shall exist
for,the same on said property,and said court shallappor-
tion said cost of said improvement to the owners of real
estate within said district according to the number of
square feetowned by each person respectively, exclusive
of roads , lanes , or other avenues in said district.
§6 . The orders of said county court, by which the
cost of said improvement is apportioned to the property-
owners respectively,shall bear interestat the rate of six
per cent.per annum untilpaid,and suitsmay be brought
for the enforcement of the lien hereinbeforegiven in the
Louisville chancery court, which court shall have full
power and authorityto correct any and all errorsin the
apportionment or proceedings of the said commissioners ,
engineer, or county court,to the end that each square foot
of land withinthe boundary atoresaid(publicroads only
excepted ) shallbe caused to bear an equal part of the
cost of said improvement . If any of the liensgiven shall
be enforced by judgment of the court, and sale made
thereunder, the defendant, his heirs, or personal repre-
sentatives,shallhave the rightto redeem the same at any
time within one year from the date of such judgment ,by
paying to the plaintiffor his representative the amount
of thejudgment and costs, with ten (10) per cent.interest
thereon from the date of such judgment ; but if not re-
deemed within the one year ,the titleto such land shall
vest absolutelyin the purchaser .
§7 . After said Fountain Ferry Road is improved as
herein provided, it shallbe a public road, and shall be
thereafterkept in repairby Jeffersoncounty as such.
§ 8. Chapter 248 ,entitled"An act authorizing the im-
provement of the Fountain Ferry Road , in Jefferson
county,Kentucky," approved February 23d,1882,ishere-
by repealed.
§ 9. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved April 21, 1882.

686 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1181 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe ExcelsiorBuilding and Saving Association,of
Covington .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
Incorporators .
§1 . That George G. Perkins, O. P. Schmidt , J. H.
Mersmann ,K. F. Benndorf,Jas.Orr,jr.,M. C. Mosch ,P.
P. McVeigh ,John Mabus ,Dr. J. S. Cassidy,J. L. Hacka-
thorn,Max Herbst , Thos . W. McLaughlin , W. S. Nock ,
A. R. Mullins, JuliusL. Bristow, Dr. Chas. Kearns ,
Hy . C. Hallam , George H. Davison ,J. W. Oglesby , J.
Style.
Purposes .
P. Taylor, and John I.Howe , and theirassociates,suc-
cessors, and assigns, be , and they are hereby ,created a
body-corporateand politic,under the name of the "Ex-
celsiorBuilding and Saving Association;" with power to
sue and be sued ,to contract and be contracted with , to
have a common seal, and the same to break or alter at
pleasure. The object of the associationis to enable its
members to aggregate theirweekly savings and the in-
come from the same into a common fund for distribution,
as itincreases,in shares of equal sums,to the members
forbuilding or purchasing homes or other property.
§2 . The place of business of the association shall be
Placeofbusinessin the cityof Covington,Kenton county,and itshallhave
and keep an officein saidcity,and hold meetings thereat
for the transaction of the business of the association at
leastonce every week .
§3 . The association,by successive organizations,may
distributeamong its members series of shares in any
number , firstcompleting under each separate organiza-
tion the fullpayment of all the shares subscribed, when
such organization shall terminate . After the firstorgan-
ization of the association , and after it has accumulated
and paid to itsmembers the amount due on the share or
shares held by each ,the incorporators or shareholders,or
any of them , and their associates, may organize again
forthe likepurpose of accumulating and saving for its
members sums of money in shares; and the same may be
followed with successive re-organizations without num

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 687
ber; but the rightsand privilegesgranted by this act
shallcease at the end of thirtyyears.
§4 . The capitalstock of the associationunder each Capitalstock.
successive organization shall consist of not more than
fivethousand shares,but may be of a less number . At
the firstorganization under thischarter the shares shall
not be less than four hundred dollars nor more than one
thousand dollarseach . Any one may become a member
of the association by subscribingfor one or more shares
of the capitalstock,and paying an initiationfeeof not
less than one nor more than two dollars for each share
subscribed. Each member shall pay a weekly contribu-
tion or deposit of not less than one nor more than two
dollars for each share held , and until all the shareholders
are paid in full; and each shareholder shallbe entitledto
a sum of money equal to the fullamount of his share or
shares . At each successive re-organization of the asso-
ciation, the amount of the shares, not exceeding one
thousand dollars each , and the weekly payments on
same , and all fees,fines, and penalties shall be as deter-
mined by the shareholders subscribingthereunder.
§ 5. Members may have theirshares cashed in advance
by paying intereston the same in weekly installments
to the end of the organization . Members who shall re-
ceive advances on theirshares shallexecute securityby
mortgage on unencumbered real estate of the assessed
value at the time of the advance , on the books of the
assessorof the cityin which itis located, of at leastone
fourth more than the sum advanced , or on unencumbered
real estate,ifthe same be not in a city, of an assessed
value on the books of the county assessor equal to the
sum advanced . The association shall pot make advance- .
ments in excess of these values,and it shall not in any
case make advancements in excess of three -fourths the
actual value of property offeredas securityfor same .
Where permanent improvements have been placed on
property subsequent to assessment ,a proper proportion
of the increased value of such may be counted in making
advancements . It shall be the duty of the directorsto

688 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
refuse full advancements where excessive assessments
are made ,or where there isreasonable probabilityof suit
at law being necessaryto recoversame ,or for any other
good cause appearing to them . Directors voting for
advancements made in excess of values derived from the
assessor'sbooks shall be individuallyresponsible to the
association for any loss arising therefrom . Mortgages
made by members to secureadvancements shallremain
in force untilall shares are paid in full, or the amount
covered by the mortgage is repaid the association, and
shallsecure the payment of the weekly contributionsor
deposits,the intereston the amount advanced ,the pay-
ment of fines and premiums ,and of assessments, taxes,
and fireinsurance on the property mortgaged Should a
member receiving an advance failto pay one or more
of said enumerated liabilitieswithin sixtydays after the
same shall be due , then the whole sum received and
owing by such member shall become due and payable ,
and the directorsmay forecloseitsmortgage and enforce
the payment of the same .
§ 6. The order in which members shall have advances
on theirsharesshallbe determined by the directorsat
some regularmeeting, publiclydrawing not more than
ten (10) names from among those desiring advances .
Those whose names are drawn shall be entitled to their
advances in the numerical order in which they are drawn ;
or the order may be determined by the directorsoffering
in an open meeting , during the business hours thereof,
advances toshareholdersatpublic auction,and the priv-
ilege of having precedence in such advances shall be
sold to the highest bidder. The premium offeredby the
successfulbidder shallbe in excess of the weekly interest
required to be paid on advances , and said premium shall
be paid at the time the advance is made . The members
of the association or the directors shall determine at the
time of each organization the mode by which shares
shallbe advanced , and the mode chosen shallremain un-
changed to the end of the organization .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 689
§7 . The directors may render the shares of members
transferable, and prescribe the mode of making such
transfers. The association may acquire and transfer
property of all kinds, and have and possess the same
powers in such matters as private individualsnow enjoy .
The directors may establishby laws ,and make allrules
and regulations deemed expedient for the management
of the affairsof the association. They may impose fines
against the members for failureto pay weekly contri-
butions and for other causes ; and all fines, penalties,
forfeitures, and premiums shall be appropriated to the
payment of the expenses and losses of the association,
and shall not be counted as interest or usury, or recov-
ered,set off, or pleaded as a credit or counter-claim in
any proceeding institutedor begun after the expiration
of sixtydays from the payment of the same .
§ 8. No indebtedness,exceptin the proper and pruden-
tial conduct of the affairs and business of the same , shall
be incurred by the association,and the private property
of members shall be exempt from the debts of the asso-
ciation .
§ 9. Members may withdraw from the association,and
have the weekly contributions and deposits paid in re-
tunded under such regulations as the directorsshallpre-
scribe .
§ 10. The affairs of the association shall be conducted Directors.
by nine directors,who shallbe elected by the shareholders
at each regular organization, and continue in officeto
the end of the organization. The directors shall electa
president, vice president, and treasurer from their num-
ber, and a secretary from their number or from among
the members ; and the secretary and treasurer shall be
removable at any time by the directors.
§ 11. Directors shallfillany vacancy in the board ,and vacancy.
-directors and other officersand agents of the association
may be removed by the vote of the majority of allshare-
holders at any regular meeting , and after one month's
noticepostedprominentlyin the officeof the association,
LOC . L., VOL . II- 44

690 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
or published in any newspaper generally read and circu-
lated in the city of Covington .
§ 12. It shallbe the duty of the president to preside at
President-dutiesall meetings of the association and directors, sign all
orders issued by the directors,attend to securing the exe-
cution and recording of all mortgages , and , with the
treasurer,to execute all releases, and otherwise look to
the best interests of the association . He shall receive
a fee of two dollars for each mortgage executed to the
associationand leftfor record by him ,to be paid by the
mortgagor . He shall also view each piece of property
offeredas securityfor an advance ,and report if the same
be of the assessed value , or if it has suffered in value ,
and any other fact bearing upon its worth as security.
The directorsmay appoint a committee to act with the
president in viewing and reporting on the value of prop-
erty. In the absence or inabilityto act of the president,
the vice president shallperform his duties.
Secretary.
Treasurer .
§ 13. Itshallbe the duty of the secretaryto be present
and keep a strictrecord of the doings of each meeting
of the association and of the directors, and to have his
record -book at each meeting . He shall keep an accurate
account between each member and the association, and
sign with the president allorders issued by the directors
on the treasurer, and shallsafelykeep his records and all
books and papers belonging to the association, except
mortgages,deeds,and policiesof insurance,and deliver
the same to his successor. He shall, at all times, have
the financialcondition of the association ready for exhi-
bition or examination , and shall report the same four
times a year at regular meetings . He shallexecute bond ,
with good securitysatisfactoryto the directors, in the
sum of one thousand dollars,for the faithfulperformance
of his duties, and shall receive pay fixed by the direct-
ors .
§ 14. Itshallbe the duty of the treasurerto receiveall
money paid to the association,and pay allordersissued
by the directorsand countersignedby the presidentand
secretary, and keep a record of the same . He shall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 691
safelykeep all deeds, mortgages, powers of attorney,
contracts, bonds, notes, policiesof insurance, and like
documents belonging to the association; give bond in
the sum of three thousand dollars, with good sureties
approved by the directors, for the faithfulperformance
of his duties, and deliverto his successorall papers,
effects,and moneys in his charge. All money received
by the treasurershallbe by him depositedin some sol-
vent bank in the cityof Covington,and he shallmake
hischeck forany amount paid out by him ,and hisbooks
and bank account shall,at alltimes,be subject to exami-
tion by the directorsand officersof the association. He
shall make quarterly reports to the association of the
state of his accounts .
§ 15. The directorsshallchoose and fix the pay of an Attorney.
attorney-at-law to examine deeds and other papers , to
examine and report on titles,and the assessed value of
and taxes due on property offered as security, to draft
all mortgages , deeds , and other papers , and give all
needed advice , and to attend to all suits at law for the
association .
§ 16. If money shall accumulate in the hands of the
treasurer, the directors, in their discretion, may appor-
tionthe same among shareholders who have not drawn ,
determining the order by lot,and requiring interest.
§ 17. The directors may , in their discretion, advance
to a member having the right to draw a portion of the
money paid in by such member , reserving enough for
interest,dues, and probable fines, expenses, and losses;
and in such cases the association shall have a superior
lien on the shares and interests of such members .
§ 18. Married women and minors may hold shares in
the association; and money paid by them on theirshares,
or by others for them , and the increase thereon,shallbe
for their own use, and ,in the case of a married woman ,
under her separate control.
§19 . The association shall be exempt from State and
municipal and other taxes, but the shares of members ,,

692 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
when drawn ,shall be subject to the laws taxing surplus
when the same shallapply .
§20 . This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Became a law by reason of the failureof the Governor to return the
same within ten days .

CHAPTER 1182 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Lexington Savings Association.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That J. W. Spencer , Paul Conlon ,G. R. King , and
J. H. Mulligan , be , and they are hereby , constituted a
body -corporate, under the name and styleof the Lexing-
ton Savings Association; and by that name may sue and
be sued , implead and be impleaded ; shall have succes-
sion; have a common seal, and alteror change the same
at pleasure; may acquire and hold real and personal
estate,and selland convey the same ; and shallpossess
such other powers as are consistent with the objects of
the association .
§2 . The officersof the associationshallbe a president,
vice president,solicitor,secretary,assistantsecretary, and
treasurer,and nine (9) directors; and until the firstgen-
eral electionof officers
shallbe president,
secretary ,
hereinafter provided for,
vice president,
assistant secretary ,
solicitor,
treas-
urer, and the other corporators above named shall be
directors . In elections for officers and transaction of
other business,each member present at the meeting shall
be entitledto one vote: Provided,That women and infants,
members of the association,may vote by proxy ; married
women and infantsby their husbands and guardians;
otherwomen by any member of the associationselected
by them ,in writing,to act forthem .
§3 . The capital stock of said association shallbe four
hundred thousand dollars, to be divided into shares of
four hundred dollars each , and to be paid by the stock-
holders in weekly installmentsof one dollar each week ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 693
for each share of stock,untilsaid installments, with their
net accumulations , shall amount to said sum of four
hundred dollars for each share, or,at the expiration of
six years from the firstelection of officers, whether said
shares have amounted to said sum or not,there shallbe a
distributionthereof to the stockholders, and a liquidation.
of its affairs; and after that, its corporate powers and
existenceshallonly continue to settleup its unsettled
business and affairs.
§ 4. The corporators above named shallopen books for
the subscription of said stock, and may adopt ,so far as
ithas progressed,the subscriptionof saidstockin antici-
pation of this act; and whenever three hundred shares
shall be subscribed,said association may go into opera-
tion Other sharesmay subsequentlybe subscribed,until
the whole stock is taken , the subscribers thereto being
requiredto pay the back installmentscorrespondingwith
those paid by the originalsubscribers, with interestat
such rate,not exceeding sixper cent.,as the association
may prescribein itsby-laws : Provided, That no stock-
holder shallacquire or hold at any time more than
shares of said stock . Said stock shall be transferable on
the books of said association as may be provided in its
by -laws .
§5 . The object of said association being to enable its Purposes
members ,by theirsavings and accumulations ,to acquire
homes and otherproperty,itsbusinessshallbe to loan its
capitalstock as paid in,and the accumulation thereof,
only upon mortgages and liens upon real estate,ample
in amount to secure the same ,preference being given in
all cases to members of said association ; and said loans
shallbe made at such rate of interest, not exceeding six
per cent., as may be agreed by the parties thereto, to-
gether with such premiums thereforas may be offeredby
the partiesto whom the loansmay be made .
§ 6. As soon as practicable, after the association is
ready to go into operation,as provided in section four of
thisact,the corporators shallcalla meeting of the stock-
holders, giving reasonable notice of time and place of

694 LAWS OF KENTUCKY
such meeting ,for the electionof officers,who shallserve
for one year ,and until their successors are elected and
enter upon the discharge of theirrespectiveduties. An-
nually thereafter, at such time as may be fixed by the
by -laws to be adopted by the stockholders, the officers
shallbe elected; and said stockholdersshallhave author-
ityto pass and make any by -laws for the government of
said corporation,not inconsistentwith thischarter or the
laws of this State, or the Constitution of the United
States .
By -laws .
§ 7. Said corporation may provide in itsby -laws what
penaltiesshallattach for failureto pay the installments
of stock at the times fixed therefor, and shall have full
power to enforce such penalties, whether the same shall
be by fine or forfeitureof such shares. It may also pro-
vide upon what terms members desiring to do so may
withdraw from the association, and it may also direct
what officersshall be required to execute bonds , with
security, for the faithfulperformance of theirduties, and
the amount of penalty in said bonds ; and said bonds ,
when executed ,shall be fullybinding upon the obligors
thereto; and may requirethe payment quarterly,in ad-
vance , of five cents per week on each share, to meet
current expenses , and also fix an initiationfee of one
dollar on each share .
§ 8. The officersand directorsmay adopt any rulesfor
conducting the business of the association not in conflict
with thisact,theby laws of saidassociation,or the laws
of Kentucky or the Constitutionaforesaid.
§ 9. Said corporation shall have power to issue and
sellor hypothecate itsbonds, with coupons attached,for
any sum not exceeding the capitalstock actuallypaid up ,
and make the same payable at such time and place as
may be deemed most advisable; but such bonds shallnot
bear a rateof interestexceeding six per cent.per annum ,
to be paid annually or semi-annually ,as said corporation
may direct: Provided, however, That no bonds shallbe
so issued without the consent of the stockholders; but if
saidassociationshallissueany bonds under theprovisions

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 695
of thisact,they shall be liableto the same rate of taxa-
tion as other bonds of like character ,but the shares of
stock shall not be liable to taxation .
§ 10. In addition to the mortgage or other lien or secu-
ritywhich may be given by a stockholder to secure any
loan which may be created,a firstlien is given to the
association upon the interest and stock of such share-
holder in said association to secure it in said lien or
indebtedness ; and inasmuch as the object of this asso-
ciation is to enable persons of limited means to secure
homesteads for themselves and theirfamilies,itis hereby
enacted that any accumulations ,not exceeding one thou-
sand dollarsin amount ,of any stockholder,in the funds
of thisassociation, may be held and regarded as a home-
stead right, and shall be exempt from seizure or sale
under execution,attachment, or judgment of any court
according to the act to exempt homesteads from sale for
deht, approved February 10th, 1866 ; but this exemption
shall be in lieu of any other homestead exemption ,but
thisexemption shallnot prevailas againstthe lienheld,
if any , by this association. The board of directors of
said association is authorized to invest, from time to
time,any partof the money of the associationthatmay
be on hand in excess of the amount required for the time
for loans and retirement of stocks , in bank stocks or
bonds of any kind,and may loan any part of such money
to stockholders, upon the security of their stock in the
associationas collateral,tosecurepayment of such loan
or loans respectively; and may loan any part of such
money to persons not stockholders,upon personalsecu-
rityor collaterals,without requiring a mortgage upon real
estate .
§ 11. That this act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
This act became a law by reasonof the Governor declining to approve
the same or return within ten days .

CHAPTER 1183
AN ACT to incorporatethe town of PleasurevilleDepot,in Henry county..
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the boundary of land embraced in the sur-
vey , and bounded as follows: Beginning at a stone in
conjunctioncornersat J.P. Bryant'slot,J.R. Lyle and
J. H. Ladd's line, running in course of J. H. Ladd's and
J.R. Lyle and J.P. Bryant'slinewest 77 polesto a stone;
thence south 91 poles to a stone 40 poles west of Shelby-
villedirt road ; thence east 70 poles to a stone; thence
north 102 poles to a stone in J. H. Ladd's and J. R.
Lyle's line; thence with said J. H. Ladd's and J. R.
Lyle's line 14 poles to the beginning , containing by
measurement 48 acres and 88 square poles,be , and the
same is hereby ,establishedas a town ,by the said name
of Pleasureville; and W. A. Lawrence , J. P. Bryant , J.
T. Campbell , J. R. Moore , B. E. Kitson are hereby ap-
pointed trusteesof said town , and shall hold their office
until the firstSaturday in July, eighteen hundred and
eighty-two , and until their successors are elected and
qualified, and enter upon the discharge of theirduties of
their office. On the said firstSaturday in July,eighteen
hundred and eighty-two ,the said parties named as trus-
tees shallcause an election to be held in said town by
the qualifiedvotersresidingthereinfor thirtydays prior
thereto for fivetrustees,a policejudge ,and town marshal
of said town ,who shallhold theirofficesfor one year,
and until their successors shall be inducted into office;
and on the firstSaturday in July of each year , the trus-
tees of said town shall cause a similar election for said
officeto be held; at time and place of each electionshall
be advertisedfor at leastten days priorthereto by written
or printed notices,posted at three or more public places
in said town . The board of trustees shall appoint two
of their number to hold each election, who shall certify
the poll-book thereof; and the board of trustees shall,
within three days aftereach election,examine and com-
pare the poll-book ,and declare by order on their records

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 697
the person or persons electedto each office. The poll
books of such electionshallbe deposited and safelykept
in county clerk'sofficein Henry county .
§2 . The board of trusteesof said town ,and theirsuc
cessorsin office, are hereby created a body-politicand
corporate,by the name and styleof The Board of Trus-
tees of the Town of Pleasureville, and by that name may
sue and be sued ,plead and be impleaded ,contract and
be contractedwith,in allcourtsand placesin thisCom-
monwealth ; may have and use a corporate seal, and
break or altersame at pleasure. The prudentialand
financial affairs of said town shall be invested in and
controlled by the board of trustees. They shall elect
one of their number chairman , who shall sign all orders
and proceedings of the board . They shall keep a full
and correct record of all the proceedings of the board ,
and shallhave charge of allrecords and papers of said
board . The board of trusteesshall have power to adopt
such by -laws and ordinances for the government of said
town , and for the preservation of the peace and good
morals thereof as they may deem proper ,not inconsistent
with the Constitution and laws of this State or of the
United States,and may enforcesame by proper penal-
ties. They shallhave charge of the streetsand alleys.
and public property of said town , as designated by said
plat or which may hereafterbe established.
§3 There is established in said town a court , to be
known as the police court of Pleasureville,to be held by
the police judge of said town , elected as hereinbefore
provided , who shall be commissioned by the Governor ,
and shallbe known as the police judge of Pleasureville.
He shallbe a conservator of the peace ,and shallhave all
the powers of a justiceof the peace in the issuing of war-
rants for the arrest of persons charged with criminal or
penal offices; and shall have the power of a single jus-
ticein examining trials. The said court shallhave juris-
dictionfor the trialof allpersons upon the charges of the
violationsof the by -laws or ordinances of said town .
cases in which the proceeding is for a violation of the
In

698 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
by -laws or ordinances of said town , the process shall be
directed to the town marshal of Pleasureville, and in all
other cases to the sheriff,constable,or marshal , and in all
cases shall run in the name of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky . All finesfor breach of the by-laws or ordi-
nances of said town shall be for the benefit of said town ,
and shallbe paid to the town treasurer,or to such per-
son as may be by said board of trustees authorized to
receive the same .
§4 . The town marshal of said town shallexecute bond ,
with good and sufficientsurety, to be approved by the
board of trustees, which bond shall be to the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , and shall embrace similar stipula-
tions and conditions as bonds required by law to be
executed by constables, and upon which suit may be
institutedfor any breach thereof by any person injured
thereby. The said marshal shall exercise same powers
and duties as required by law of constables in all pro-
ceedings in said police court for breach of the by -laws
and ordinances of said town , and shall have all the
powers of a constable in making arrestsupon charges of
breach of the criminal or penal laws ; and shall collect
alltaxes which he may be ordered by the board of trus-
teesto collect; and for thatpurpose shallsimilarpowers
as allowed by law to sheriffsin making distraintfor such
taxes; and he shallpay same over as may be directed
by board of trustees.
§ 5. The term of officeof the trustees and of the town
marshal shall begin immediately upon the certificateof
theirelectionby the board of trustees,and of the police
judge upon being commissioned by the Governor ; and
each of said officersshall,before some officerauthorized
to administer oaths, take the oath required by the Consti-
tution of this State,before entering upon the discharge
of their duties .
§6 . The board of trustees shall have power to grant
coffee-house license,with privilegeof sellingspirituous,
vinous , or malt liquors by drink or otherwise in said
town , and to charge and collect a tax therefor, the
1

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 699
amount of such tax to be fixed by order of the board ,
and ,when collected, shall be for the use of said town ;
but no privileges shall be exercised under such license
untilthe tax upon licenserequired to be paid to the State
shallhave been paid.
§7 . The board of trusteesshallhave the power to levy
upon the property in said town a tax of not exceeding
fiftycents upon each one hundred dollarsin value in any
one year ,and a poll-tax of not exceeding one dollar and
fiftycents upon each tithable in said town in any one
year ,which tax shall be collectedby the town marshal ;
and,with a view to the levyingof such tax, the board
of trusteesmay appoint an assessor to take a listof the
taxablepropertyand tithablein saidtown ,and theymay
make such allowance to the assessor and town marshal
for theirservicesas they may deem just.
§ 8. The board of trustees may , with the funds of said
town , grade , pave , and improve the streets, sidewalks ,
alleys, and publicproperty of said town as they may
deem best .
§ 9. The police judge of said town shallbe entitledto
the same fees as a justice of the peace for similar ser-
vices,and the town marshal shallbe entitledto the same
fees as a constable for similar services .
§ 10. The board of trustees shallcause the survey and
plat of said town to be recorded in officeof clerk of
Henry county court, and shallpay for same out of town
treasury .
§11 . The board of trustees may appoint a treasurer,
who shall execute bond ,with surety to be approved by
the board, and whose term of officeshallbe one year,
and may be removed by the board at pleasure. Nothing
in thisact shall be construed to allow any person to hold
the officeof policejudge or trusteewho may sellspiritu-
ous ,vinous ,or malt liquorson the submission of the local
option law .
§ 12. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 21, 1882.

of Kentucky :

hereby , authorized and directed to convey to said Corne--
lius E. Roe ,by deed in the name of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky , a certain tract of land lying on Licking
river, in Rowan county , that was heretofore sold and

monwealth ,the sum of two hundred dollars,with six per
cent. interestthereon from the 1st day of January , 1878,

CHAPTER 1187 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act regulatingthe city court of
Louisville,and the remuneration of itsofficers," approved February 21 ,
1880 .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That sectionten of an act, entitled" An act regu-
lating the citycourt of Louisville and the remuneration
of its officers," approved February 21, 1880,be ,and the
same is hereby , amended as follows: The judge of the
city court of Louisville shall appoint an interpreterfor
said court for a term of three years . The said interpreter
shallreceive an annual salary of six hundred dollars,to
be paid in monthly installments out of the citytreasury.
He shall attend said court during its sessions , and be sub-
ject to itsorders. For a failureto perform his duties,the
court may remove him , and appoint another to fillhis
place .
§ 2. The interpreterprovided for herein shallbe ap-
pointedby saidcourt on the firstMonday in September ,
1882 , and he shall hold his officefor the term of three
years from the date of his appointment .
§ 3. All acts and parts of acts in conflictherewith are
hereby repealed.
Approved April 21, 1882.

CHAPTER 1188 .
AN ACT to empower the board of commissioners of Kenton county to issue
and sellthe bonds of the county to raisemoney to pay itsindebtedness,
and to levy a tax to pay the same .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the board of commissioners of Kenton
county be,and are hereby,empowered to issueand sell
the bonds of Kenton county to an amount sufficientto
raisea fund to pay offthe presentindebtednessof the
county,of such denomination as theymay prescribe,pay-
able at any bank in the cityof Covington they may des-
ignate, at such times as they shall provide , not less than
one nor exceeding ten years afterthe date thereof. Said
bonds shall bear a rate of interestnot exceeding six per
cent. per annum , to be paid semi -annually , for which
coupons shall be attached to said bonds . None of said
bonds ,however , shallbe sold for lessthan par value .
§2 . That said commissioners shallannually levy an ad
valorem tax on the property of the said county ,outsideof .
the city of Covington ,by the laws of the State subject
to taxation for revenue purposes , sufficientto pay the
intereston said bonds and principalof said bonds as
the same shall fall due , which tax shall be collected and
accounted forby the sheriffor collectorof said county ,as

§3 . This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 21, 1882.

CHAPTER 1159 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Lewisburg and Ludlow Turnpike Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That a company ishereby created,under the name
and styleof the " Lewisburg and Ludlow Turnpike Com-
pany ," which shallbe a body-politicand corporate,for
the purpose of constructing a turnpike road between the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 703
city of Covington and the cityof Ludlow ,both in Ken-
ton county .
§2 . Capital stock of said company shallbe in shares of Capitalstock.
twenty -fivedollarseach , and shallnot exceed eight thou-
sand dollars .
§3 . Books of subscriptionshallbe opened by W. P. Commissioners..
Devon ,George Bauer ,S. C. Hart ,and M. Kress , who are
hereby appointed commissioners to take subscriptionsto
the capitalstock of said company at such time and place
as they may deem proper. They shall insert in the sub-
scriptionbook an obligation as follows : " We , whose
names are hereunto subscribed,severallypromise to pay
to the president,directors,and company of the Lewisburg
and Ludlow Turnpike Company the sum of twenty-five
dollarsforeach and every share set oppositeour names ,
in such manner and at such times as may be required
by them under the law incorporating said company "
§4 . When the sum of five thousand dollars shall have
been subscribed to the capital stock,it shallbe the duty
of the commissioners named to give notice of a meeting
of shareholders forthe purpose of electingthree directors
of said company , in which election one vote shall be
allowed for each share of stock subscribed, and may be
castpersonallyorby proxy. The saiddirectorsso elected
shallelectone of themselves to act as presidentand treas-
urer, and such other officersas may be necessary. The
said directorsshall serve one year , and until their suc-
cessors are elected. A majority of the board may be
competent to do business.
§ 5. So soon as said company is organized by the elec-
tionof directors,they shallbe a body-corporateand pol-
itic,under the name and styleaforesaid, and have allthe
privilegesof such; and generally do and perform allacts
lawfulfora corporationto effectthe objectforwhich
said corporationwas created; and of holding, by gift,
purchase,or otherwise,anythingnecessaryfor the prose-
cution of their work or object of thiscorporation.
§ 6. As soon as said company is organized,they shall
be authorized to receive the written consent of any of the

704 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
land-holders through which or near whose land the road
may pass of the right of way or grant of material for the
construction of said road , or for keeping same in repair ;
which consent, when given , shall be valid and binding
according to the terms of same . If, in any instance,they
cannot agree with the owner or owners of lands for the
rightof way or for material necessary for the construc-
tion of same , or if the owner or owners are incompetent
in law to convey ,the president shall apply to the county
court for a writ of ad quod damnum to assess the damages
which the owner or owners thereof may sustain by the
constructionof said road over the land ,or the taking of
material necessary for construction; and upon payment
or tender of the damages assessed ,it shall be lawful for
the company to open and make said road , and do allthe
work pertaining thereto.
§ 7. Said corporation shallfix and regulate the grade ,
width , and metal of said road , shall possess all the
powers , authority,rights, and privileges, and may do all
acts and things necessary to enable said company to
build said turnpike road, erection of gates, collectionof
tolls, appointingofficersand employes,and repair and
operating same . They shall have power ,when one half
mile of said road is made , to erect a gate at or near its
eastern terminus , and collecttollfor any distance trav-
eled on said road at one half rates that Covington and
Lexington Turnpike Company are permitted to charge
for five miles traveled, and double such rates for wagons
hauling sand , moulding , sandstone , and heavy material,
other than farm products : Provided,That when such tolls
so collected shall exceed cost of keeping said road in
repair, and pay to stockholders dividends of six per cent.
per annum ,then such rates of toll shall be reduced to
produce such cost of repair, maintenance , and six per
cent, per annum dividend. When road is completed to
terminal points,a gate may be placed at each end of said
road ,but tollwillbe collectedat one gate only.
§ 8. This company may occupy the public highway
known as county road number seventy-eight,as originally

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 705
surveyedand laidout,and such portionof same as may
notbe requiredinthe buildingof turnpikeshallbe dis-
continued ,and revertto originalowners .
Approved April 21 , 1882 .
CHAPTER 1190 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof William Shotwell,of Whitley county.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be , and he is
hereby ,directed to draw his warrant on the Treasurer in
favor of William Shotwell , of Whitley county , for the
sum of fiftydollars, payable out of any money in the
Treasury not otherwise appropriated, being compensa-
tion to said Shotwell for himself and guard for con-
veying Lurana Hodge ,who was adjudged a lunatic by
the county court of Laurel county,from London ,Laurel
county, to the Eastern Lunatic Asylum at Lexington,
where said lunatic was refused admission on account of
the overcrowded condition of said asylum ; said Shotwell
having paid the expenses of himself,guard , and lunatic
from London ,in Laurel county ,to Lexington , and back
to Laurel county , and having heretoforereceived nothing
therefor .
§2 . This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after itspassage .
Approved April 21, 1882.

CHAPTER 1193 .
AN ACT to incorporatethePleasantRun TurnpikeRoad Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That a company be ,and is hereby ,incorporated,
for the purpose of constructing a turnpike road from the
cityof Ludlow ,Kenton county ,Kentucky , up the Pleas-
ant Run Valley ,to a point near the crossing of the Cin-
cinnati Southern Railway with the county road known
as the Buttermilk pike,and from thence to some point in Style.
the interior,not exceedingten(10) miles,with the power
to construct a branch road from said main road ,up the
west fork of Pleasant Run creek, through the lands of
D. C. Collins,to Joseph Cleveland's lands,and such other
branch roads as they may deem to be forthe convenience
of the public travel.
§ 2. The capitalstock of said company shallbe divided Capital.
intosharesof fifty($50)dollarseach,and shallentitlethe
holder thereof to one vote for each share of stock held in
allthe affairsof said company , and to all the rights and
privilegesof other stockholders. Said stock shallnot be
assignable, except by consent of the board of directors,
and no dividends shall ever be declared or paid on said
stock .
§ 3. The saidPleasantRun Turnpike Road Company
shall have all the privileges and franchises incident to
corporations; and under that name shall have perpet-
ual succession, with power to contract and be contracted
with ,sue and be sued ,plead and be impleaded , in any
court of law or equity; shall be capable of holding
their capital stock, and may increase the same to any
amount forthe objectsherein contemplated, not, how-
ever, to exceed in amount the actual cost of the con-
structionof said turnpikeroad and branch roads; and
may take and hold,by purchase or gift,allsuch lands,
tenements, hereditaments, real or personal property, as
may be necessaryfor the prosecutionof theirwork or
the objectsthereof; and to do any act or thing which a
corporationmay lawfullydo to effectthe objectsforwhich

thesaidcorporationis hereby created. They shallhave
the rightto condemn land forthe right of way ,toll-gate
lots,and material to construct said road and branch roads
under a writ of ad quod damnum as provided by law ,if the
same cannot be obtained by giftor purchase .

§ 4. That M. H. Bentley, H. Berkenkamp , William
Ramler , Fred . Reinhart, jr., John L. Cleveland , D. C.
Kennedy , and Joseph Cleveland, and such others
they may appoint , are appointed commissioners to re-
ceive subscriptionsof stock to said company , and they
may each open books and receive subscriptionsof stock,
at any time or place, on the following written condi-

dollarsforeach share of stockin saidcompany setoppo-
siteour names respectively,in such manner and propor-
tion, and at such times and place, as shallbe required by
said company ." The said subscriptionsmay be for the
main road or branches .
§5 . When a majority of the commissioners aforesaid
who act as such think a sufficient amount of stock has
been taken to justifya commencement of work on said
road , they shall call a meeting of the stockholders, by
written or printed notices,posted in at leastthree of the
most public places along the route of said turnpike road ,
fivedays previous to the time set for said meeting , and
hold an electionfor a presidentand four directors(who
must be stockholders in the main road),to serve for one
year , and until their successors are elected. The presi-
dent and directors shall appoint one of their number
treasurer, who shall execute bond , with approved secu-
rity, conditioned for the faithfulperformance of the duties
of his office,and that he will account for and pay over
allmoneys received by him as treasurer of said turnpike
road company . Said bond shall be payable to the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky ,for the use of the saidPleasant
Run Turnpike Road Company . The securitieson said
bond shall have the same qualifications as securities on the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 715
revenue bonds of the sheriff, and the same lien shall attach
to the property of said treasurer and his securitiesas to

shallbe recorded in the county clerk'sofficeat Covington ,

pike company shall, within five days after itsexecution,
filesaid bond in said county clerk'sofficefor record; and
upon a failureto do so,he shall be fined in a snm not

§ 6. The said treasurer shall receive allmoneys pay-
able to said company ,and pay out the same as directed

pose ,which shallbe open at alltimes for the inspection

ing, and managing said turnpike road and branches ;

feetin width and nine (9) inchesin depth,of well-broken

(10) feetin width and nine (9) inches in depth on the
" branch roads." They shallfixthe grade ,locate bridges,
and contract for the building of the same ; and do such
other acts as are necessary and proper .
§ 8. That said turnpike company may locatetheirmain
road or branch roads on any county road ,and where said
county roads have been macadamized ,they may allow
the citizens who contributed to macadamize the said
roads a reasonable compensation as stock in the said
main or branch road ,

716 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Toll -gates.
§9 . That when said company have completed two or
more miles of said turnpikeroad,they may place toll-

such rates of tollas willbe necessary to keep said road
and branches in good repair,and pay allnecessaryex-
penses of said road , and no more ; not,however ,in any
event,to exceed the rates as fixedby the General Statutes.
The president of said turnpike road shall make a state-
ment of the receiptsand expenditures on account of said
road and branch roads every six months ; said statement
shallbe sworn to by said president,and a copy postedat
each toll-gate on said road .
§ 10. The stockholders who own land along the line of
said road or branches in Kenton county , who subscribe
and pay for two (2) sharesof the capitalstockin said
company , shallbe exempt from the Kenton county road
tax of six(6) centson the one hundred dollars,but shall,
with such others(living on the line of said road as the
county court may direct), work out the per capita road
tax(ifliabletherefor) on saidturnpikeroadorbranches
under the directionof the president and directorsof said
road , and said turnpike company shall be liablefor fail-
ing to keep said road and branches in repair,the same
as surveyors of county roads : Provided, That nothing in
this act shall be so construed so as to deprive the said
town of West Covington of the right or control of any
street in said town .
§ 11. This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 21, 1882.

AN ACT to allow oral pleadings in justices courts in Calloway , Graves ,
and Ballard counties .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the pleadings in justices' courts in Calloway ,.
Graves , and Ballard counties shall be oral and without
verification, yet, before any summons shall issue, the
plaintiffshall filewith the justice the account or the
written contract , or a short written statement of the
facts on which the action is founded ; in all sums exceed-
ing fiftydollarsa tax of fiftycents shall be paid by the
plaintiff,and ifthe claim of the plaintiffislessthan fifty
dollars and the set-off or counter -claim of the defendant
isgreater than fiftydollars,then a like sum of fiftycents
shallbe paid by the defendant to the justice,who shall
reportand accountforsuch sums so paid by theplaintiff

or defendant , as the case may be ,to the trustee of the
jury fund, as in casesof finescollectedby justicesof the
peace .
§2 . This act shalltake effectand be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved April 21, 1882.

ulationand territoryas may be practicable; and it shall

the firstMonday in August , 1882,electfor each ward two

succeeding year the qualifiedvoters of each ward shall
electfor their respective wards one town trustee each ,

annual town election,to be held on the firstMonday in

town trustees, a presidentof the school board,a town
treasurer, and a town clerk, who shallserve for one year .

hereof shall take the oath of office, and be installed on

amend the charter of the town of West Covington ,Ken-

provement . Said cost and expense shall, when the work
is completed and accepted by the trustees, be equally

CHAPTER 1204 .

and Tennessee rivers, in Trigg county, from trespassers.

of Kentucky :

be lawful for any person , not a residentor land-owner in

land and Tennessee rivers,to deer hunt ,or hunt foxes or
other wild animals or game , with hounds or other dogs ,

724 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
upon the lands within said territory,except at such times
and places and in such manner as the residentsor land-
owners of said part of Trigg county shalldesignate.
§2 . That after the firstday of May , 1882, it shall not
be lawful for any person ,not a residentor land -owner in
that portion of Trigg county lying between the Cumber-
land and Tennessee rivers,to herd or turn loose at large
upon the uninclosed lands any horses,mules ,or cattle, for
the purpose of pasturing,grazing , or feeding same upon
the said uninclosed lands within said territory, or know-
ingly permit same done , without the written consent of
the residents or land -owners within said boundary first
had and obtained .

sectionsone and two of this act, or eitherof them , upon

judge or any justiceof the peace for said county .

Approved April 21 , 1882.

CHAPTER 1209 .
AN ACT to authorize the voters within two miles of the town of Christians-
burg to vote upon the saleof spirituous, vinous,or malt liquors.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the officersof election, at the next regular
August election, 1882, for State and county officers,shall
open a pollin the town of Christiansburg,Shelby county ,
to take the sense of the qualifiedvotersfor or against the
sale of spirituous, vinous; or malt liquors, or any admix-
ture thereof , within said town or within a radius of two
miles thereof .
§2 . That at said election it shall be the duty of the
judges of the same to propound to each voter,resident
within said town or within a radius of two miles thereof,
the question: "Are you for or against the sale of spiritu-
ous ,vinous ,or malt liquors? " and to record the vote of
each voter " for" or against " as he may respond .
§ 3. That none but bona fidemale residentsover twen-
ty-one years of age , within said town or said radius of
two miles, shallbe deemed qualifiedvoters on said ques-
tion of the sale of spirituous,vinous ,or malt liquors, and
the judges of said election shall allow none others to
vote on said question .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 727
§4 . That should a majority of said qualifiedvoters at
said election vote against the sale of spirituous,vinous ,
or malt liquorswithin the aforesaidboundaries ,then said
judges and clerk of said electionshallcertifythe same to
the county judge of Shelby county , who shall enter itof
record; and thereafteritshallbe unlawful for said county
judge to grant a licenseor licensesto sellspirituous,
vinous ,or malt liquors,or any admixture thereof, to any
" tavern," " merchant " " druggist," or any one whomso-
ever within the aforesaid boundaries ; and any licenseor
licenses so granted shall be null and void .
§ 5. Any person violating the provisions of this act
shallbe punished in the same manner as persons are now
punished , under the general laws of the State, for like
offenses
§ 6. This act shall not apply to persons now selling
saidliquorsin saidboundary under licensetherefor,until
the expiration of said license or licenses: Provided,how-
ever, That this act shallnot apply to any person or per-
sons living in any districtor boundary in which the sale
of liquorsare now prohibitedby law ,and that nothing in
this act shall repeal any law in force in any part of said
radius of two miles from the town of Christiansburg .
7. This act to take effectfrom and after its passage .
Approved April 21 , 1882 .

ated and constituteda body -politicand corporate,by the

name and style of the BreathittCoal ,Iron, and Lumber

cession; may sue and be sued ,plead and be impleaded ,

ing; and may purchase , hold , sell, and convey personal

hearing as to any proprietor, when the process has been

on a day fixedfor his appearance,and the court shall
have jurisdictionto confirm the report if no traverse is
filed; and in case a traverse is filed, to have the same
triedby a jury and to give judgment upon the reportor
findingof the jury,and orderthe payment of the money
and the execution of the grant in accordance with the
report,and tohave the grant executedby a commissioner
appointed by the court,and to order itto be recorded in
the county clerk'sofficeof the county; and eitherparty
may appeal from the judgment of the court.
§ 7. The commissioners or jury,in estimating the value
of the lands proposed to be taken ,shall not be confined
to the actual value,but may take into consideration any
consequential damage that may result to the adjoining
proprietorsof the land, and also the advantages and dis-
advantages the proposed road willbe to such lands .
§8 . That upon the affidavitof the engineer of said
company ,made and filedbefore the county judge of any

734 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
county through which the proposed road may pass,that
at any point more than one hundred feet is necessary
properly to construct and operate said road ,said company
may acquire the rightto so much land as may be neces-
saryforthatpurpose,and in the manner provided in the
preceding section.
§9 . The said company may also, for the purpose of
constructingand maintaining such line or linesof railor
tramway , occupy or use any turnpike or plank road,
street,or other public way or ground ,or any part thereof,
upon such terms and conditions as may be agreed upon
between saidcompany and the municipaland othercor-
porations,personsor publicauthoritiesowning or having
charge thereof; and in case it shall be necessary to pro-
vide a new road , street, or other ground ,in place of that
so used and occupied, they may acquire the necessary
land, and cause the necessary improvement to be made
thereon. If no agreement can be made for the right to
use or occupy any road , street, or ground that may be
necessary, the company may take and appropriate said
rightsinthe manner providedin the next section.
§ 10. The appropriationsauthorizedin the preceding
sectionshallbe made in the manner ,and subjectto the
same rights of traverse, writ of error, and appeal pro-
vided by law for taking private property for the use of
turnpike and plank road companies ,except that the peti-
tion shall be filedin the circuitcourt of the county in
which such turnpike,plank roads,streets,or otherpublic
ways or grounds may lie; and if a continuous portion of
the same lyingin more than one county is sought to be
used or occupied,the proceedingsmay be institutedin
the circuitcourtof any county in which any part of such
continuous portion may lie; and the damages shall be
assessed for the whole of such portion, whether lying in
the county wherein the proceedings are institutedor in
other counties ; the writ shall be directed to the sheriff of
the county in which the petitionis filed.
§ 11. If during the constructionor afterthe completion
of said line of railway ,it shall be found necessary by

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 735
said trusteesto change the locationor grade ,or substitute
other works or conveniences for those originallydesig-
nated or constructed, or to provide additionalside-tracks,
or other appendages ,forthe proper management and op-
eration of said railway or tramway ,the company may
make such changes and provide such additionalappend-
ages, not departing from the general route originallyse-
lectedby them; and forthe purpose aforesaidmay acquire
and enterupon ,takeand appropriate,suchlands orrights
of way as may be necessary,in the mode hereinbefore
prescribed.
§ 12. Whenever ,along the route selected by said com-
pany , there shallbe a railway already constructed, or
rightsof way or depot or other grounds acquired there-
for, which railroad, right of way , or grounds can be
adopted as part of the said line, it shall be lawful for
persons,companies ,or corporations owning the same to
sellto the said company the said railroad,rightsof way ,
or grounds,or any parts thereof,upon such terms and
conditionsas may be agreedupon betweenthiscompany
and the parties owning such railroads,rightsof way , or
grounds : Provided ,That no such agreement shall be bind-
ing upon the stockholdersof any such company or corpo-
ration unless a majority in interestof said stockholders,as
shown by the books ofsuch company or corporation,shall
ratifythe same in person or by proxy , at a meeting to be
held at the place of holding the election of directors, to
be called afternotice given of the object of the meeting ,
in the manner provided for notice of such elections
§ 13.No bridge or otherwork shallbe so constructed
as to interruptor impair the navigation of such streams
as are naturally navigable,or as may have been declared
so by law ,or may hereafterbe so declared navigable.
§ 14. In the event of the purchase or giftof any right
of way for such railor tramway or ways by said com-
pany of any individualor corporation,therightsgranted
thereby shall be forfeited,and the land or right of way
granted shall revert to the grantor, unless such rail or
tramway be builtover it within seven years from such
purchase or gift.

736 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 15. Said company may , by said corporators, their
associates and successors , elect a board of directors, to
consistof seven (7) persons,at leastfourof whom shall
be residentsof thisState, and allof whom shallbe stock-
holders in the company , to be chosen annually by ballot
of the shareholders , and to serve until their successors are
elected, and a majority of the stock shallbe necessary to
a choice .
§ 16. The board of directorsmay make such by -laws ,
rules and regulations, as they may deem necessary; shall
direct the time and place of the election of officers, the
period for which they are to continue in office; may re-
quire bond and suretiesof any of such officers; may em-
ploy agents and factorsto discharge certain duties under
such penalties as may be fixedby said board of directors:
Provided, That such by -laws , rules and regulations, be
approved by a vote of two- thirdsof the stock at a regu-
lar or special meeting of said company .
§ 17. The capital stock of said company shallbe five
hundred thousand dollars, and shall not be increased,
except with the approval of the owner of two -thirds of
the stock expressed by a vote at a regular or special
meeting ,but may be so increased at the pleasure of two-
thirds in interest of said shareholders .
§ 18. The president and directorsmay issue bonds not
to exceed in amount the paid-up stock of said company ,
to bear interestnot to exceed six per cent per annum ,
and running not to exceed thirtyyears , and may sellthe
same for the purpose of raising money to carry on the
operationsof the company ; and they may pledgethe
property and stock of the company to secure payment of
said bonds and the interest thereon : Provided , Said com-
pany shallnot issuebonds to exceed ten thousand dollars
without the approval of two-thirdsof the stock at a reg-
ular or specialmeeting of said company .
§19 . The board of directorsmay ,if in theirjudgment
it may become necessary, make assessments upon the
stockholdersof said company to raisefunds for current
expenses not to exceed twenty -five hundred dollars in

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 737
any one year ; and such assessments shallbe paid within
sixtydays afterthe receiptof noticethereof,for which
assessments, when paid, said stockholdersshallreceive
certificates,and when such certificatesare presented to
the board of directors in sums of one hundred dollars, or
any multiplethereof, saidboard shallissue stockto the
parties surrendering such certificates.
§20 . Henry Worthington ,J. T. Sullivan,W. P. Devon ,
and F. A. Prague ,of Covington ,Kentucky ; J.M. Frazee ,
of Mason county , Kentucky ; Nicholas Patterson, of Cin-
cinnati, Ohio ; and A. C. Marshall ,of Dayton , Ohio , are
hereby appointed to compose the firstboard of directors
of said company ,and shallcontinue in officeone year
from and afterthe passage of thisact.
§21 . The board of directors shall elect one of their
number as president,and such otherofficersas they shall
deem necessary may be selectedfrom the board or from
the other shareholders. A majority of the directorsshall
constitutea quorum necessary to electofficersand trans-
act business .
§22 . The president and directors may receive stock
subscriptiousfrom partiesin or out of the State,payable
in money or in any real or personalpropertywithinthe
county of Breathitt,or any county or countiesadjoining
Breathitt,upon such terms as may be agreed upon ,and
may issue stock certificatesto such parties to the agreed
value of such property,and such actionshallbe binding
on the company and the party so subscribing.
§23 . The board of directors may , at any time , when
authorizedby the stockholders,at a regular or special
meeting ,provide for the constructionof railor tramways
under the provisions of this act,the opening of streams
for navigation, the mining , transportation, and sale of
coal or other minerals , the cutting , sawing , manufacture ,
transportation, and sale of timber, and the cultivationof
land and the marketing of the productsthereof.
§24 . Any shareholder may vote by proxy ,the authority
to act being in writing, and each share shall count one
LOC . L., VOL . II- 47

738 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
vote. The books of the company shall at all times be
open forthe inspectionof any shareholder. The private
property of shareholdersshallnot be liablefor any of
the debts of the company .
§ 25. No transferof stock shallbe binding upon the
company until entered upon itsbooks ,and no such entry
shallbe made tillsuch stock and all assessments against
itare fullypaid up , or secured to the satisfactionof the
board of directors .
§ 26. The capitalstock of the company shallbe divided
into shares of one hundred dollars each , and shall be
signed by the president and such other officeras the
board of directorsmay designate for that purpose .
§27 . The rollingstock,and road-bed,shops,depots,and
stationsof any railroad or tramway builtunder the pro-
visions of this act shall be exempt from taxation for five
years from and afteritspassage ,together with such per-
sonal propertyas may be necessaryfor the equipment
and management of said road .
$ 28 . The board of directors may , when empowered
by a vote of two thirdsin interestof the stockholders, at
a regularor specialmeeting,hypothecatebonds or stock,
or other property of the company ,forthe purpose of rais-
ing money to erectimprovements upon theirlands,or any
part thereof, or to carry on any mining , manufacturing ,
or agriculturalbusinessupon said lands; but the debt
thereby incurred shall not at any time exceed fiftythou-
sand dollars, exclusive of interest.
§29 . The Legislature reserves the right to amend or
alterthischarter at pleasure,by general laws fixingrates
of freight and passenger transportation, and requiring
the company to conform in every respect to the general
laws of this Commonwealth .
§30 . This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Became a law by the failureof the Governor to returnwithin ten days .
