CHAPTER 1216 .
AN ACT for the benefit of John Wallace , late clerk of the Boone circuit.
court .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :

sage of thisact be granted to John Wallace ,late clerk of
the Boone county circuitcourt,forthe collectionof fee-
bills,and that during the said two years he shall have
power todistrainforthe collectionof same : Provided,He

CHAPTER 1217 .
AN ACT to incorporate the Vanceburg , Quincy and Springville Turnpike
Road Company ,of Lewis and Greenup counties.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That a company is hereby formed and created,
by the name and style of the Vanceburg , Quincy and
SpringvilleTurnpike Road Company , and that George T.
Halbert , Isaac H. Voirs,A. P. Frizzel,Leonidas Darragh ,
A. I. Yancy , A. F. Moore , W. W. Agnew , and B. F.
Branham , and their successors in office,be , and they are
hereby , appointed and created a body -corporate and pol-
itic,under the name and styleaforesaid, for the purpose
of building and operating a turnpike road from Vance .
burg , in Lewis county , up the Ohio river bottom to
Springville, in Greenup county . The capital stock of
said company shallbe fiftythousand dollars, and each
share of stock shall be twenty -five dollars . Said com-
pany shall have power to sue and be sued , contract
and be contracted with,hold, enjoy,possess,convey ,and
dispose of real and personal estate, not exceeding fifty
thousand dollars,and to do all and every other act and

740 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
1
i
thing whatsoever within the object and scope of their
incorporationwhich a body-corporateor politicmay law-
fully do .
§2 . The officersof said company shallconsistof a
president,treasurer, and three directors,the treasurer to
act and perform the duties of secretary; and the per-
sons named in the firstsectionof thisact as incorporators
shall, as soon as they meet and organize,elect one of
theirnumber as president,and one as secretaryand treas-
urer,who shall so act until their successors are elected
and qualified. The president, treasurer, and directors
shallbe chosen annually by the stockholdersin such man-
ner as they may providefor in theirby-laws,who shall
hold and perform the duties of respective officesuntil
theirsuccessorsshallbe elected and qualifiedas such.
§3 .The saidcompany may ,by appropriate proceedings,
as in cases of roads and passways , or turnpike,gravel,
and plank roads, under the General Statutes,cause to be
condemned for the use of said turnpike road the right of
way , sitesfor toll-gates, residence of keepers , and for
such other purposes as said company may need land for
the use of said road . They may also in likemanner con-
demn stone-quarries, gravel-beds, and all necessary and
proper materialto buildand keep said turnpikeroad in
repair, and all necessary bridges on same . The said
turnpike road shall commence at the east side of the fill
or culvert over Slate branch , being the corporate bound-
ary of Vanceburg , and thence with the county road as far
as practicableup theOhio riverbottom to the county line
between Lewis and Greenup counties. They shallmake
proper provision for public travel over the present road
during the constructionof said turnpike road , whenever
the same may conflictor interfere. The said turnpike
road may be located by said company or by commission-
ers under its supervision,and when said road is finished
a distance of two and one half miles,the company may
erecta toll-gate and charge half toll,being governed by
the ratescharged on the Vanceburg ,Salt Lick,Tollesboro
and Maysville Turnpike Road .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 741
§ 4. That the persons named in the firstsectionof this
act as incorporators, are hereby appointed commissioners
to receive subscriptions of stock to said turnpike road
company ,any two of whom may act,who may meet at
such places and times as they may deem proper for the
purpose of taking subscriptions to said turnpike road
company ,who shall also procure one or more books for
that purpose , and the subscribers of stock shall sign an
obligationin substance or words as follows: " We ,whose
names are hereunto subscribed,do promise and agree to
pay to the Vanceburg ,Quincy and SpringvilleTurnpike
Road Company the sum of twenty-fivedollarsfor each
share of stock set opposite our respective names ,in such
proportions and at such times as shall be determined by
the president and directors of said company ."
§5 . That as soon as the sum of two thousand dollars
shall be subscribed in stock to said company ,it shallbe
the duty of said company to give notice to those persons
who have subscribed stock to said company on their
books of a meeting of stockholders at the clerk's office
of the Lewis county court,in Vanceburg ,for the purpose
of choosing the officersof said company . Each stock-
holder shall be entitled to one vote for each share of stock
owned by him or her.
§ 6. That so soon as the officersare elected,and the
company organized , said company shall possess all the
authority, rights, and privileges, and may do allacts and
thingsnecessaryto enablethe saidcompany to buildsaid
turnpike road , erection of gates, collection of tolls, ap-
pointing all necessary engineers,surveyors, and agents ,
as shallbe needed for the successfulbuilding,occupation ,
use, and operating of said road , and forkeeping same in
repair .
§7 . The said presidentand directorsshallfixand reg-
ulate the elevation and grade of said road ,the width or
part thereof to be covered with stone or gravel, and the
width or part thereof to be elevated and graded , and left
uncovered. They shalldesignatethe place forthe erec-
tion of gates,not exceeding one for every five miles of

742 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
said road : Provided, The width of said road shall not
exceed fiftyfeet, and the part covered with stone or
gravel shallnot be lessthan sixteen feetwide and twelve
inches deep ,and the part graded not less than twenty-
two feet .
§ 8. The president and directorsshalldelivera certifi-
cate, signed by the president, and countersigned by the
treasurer,to each stockholderforthe sharesby him held,
which certificate shall be transferable on the books of
saidcompany accordingto the orderof such stockholder;
but no share shall be transferred until all calls or arrear-
ages thereon are paid .
§9 . That the president of the company or board may
call meetings of the board at such times and places as
may be deemed proper , and a majority of the directors
shall think necessary for the transaction of business .
They shallkeep a record of itsproceedings ,to be entered
in a book to be procured by them for that purpose , and
each day's proceedings shallbe signed by the presiding
officerafterbeing approved by the board .
§ 10. The president and directorsmay callon the sub-
scribersof stock for any portion of theirstock subscribed,
not exceeding one fourth of the totalamount at one time ,
nor on shorter periods than three months after the first
call. The firstcall of one fourth may be call for, and
payment enforced, at any time aftersaid board is organ-
ized, untilallthe stock subscribed shallbe paid in by the
subscribers of stock .
§ 11. That in order to enable said company to build
said turnpike road as speedily as possible, and equalize
theburthensthereof,thereisherebyleviedon allproperty,
real and personal, subject to taxation for State revenue
purposes , lying and being within two miles thereof from
itscommencement at Vanceburg to itsterminus at Spring-
ville, and between the Ohio river and a parallel line
thereto from said termini as aforesaid, the sum of fifty
cents upon each one hundred dollars' worth of taxable
property as aforesaid each year , commencing with the
year 1882,and continueuntilsaidroad isbuiltand paid

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 743
for,which tax as aforesaid shall be assessed and collected
as hereinafterprovided ,and shallbe expended in building
said road as collectedeach year .
§ 12. That said company may appoint an assessor to
assess the said property owned by persons subjectto tax-
ation as aforesaidfor the building and completion of said
road . The assessment shall be returned to the May or
June term of the Lewis county courtin each year,which
shallbe subject to revision and correction by the Lewis
county court; and said county court shallcause the same
to be made out and listedwith the sheriffof Lewis county
for collection . Said sheriff shall receive and collect said
taxes,and pay the same over to the treasurerof said turn-
pike road company on or before the firstday of Decem-
ber of each year , and for any default of said sheriffunder
this act , he and his sureties on his officialbond shall be
liableto suit or suitsfor the recovery thereof. Said suit
or suitsshall be brought in the name of the president of
said company ,for the use of said company ,in the Lewis
circuitcourt. The judgment shall be for the amount of
taxes collected by said officer, and which might have
been collectedby due diligence, with the same interest
and costs and damages now allowed by law against
sheriffsfor failureto collectand pay over the county levy
and revenue . The clerk of the Lewis county court shall
be allowed such fees for what he may be required to do
under thisact as allowed to him for similar servicesper-
formed by him for the county ,to be paid by the company
out of any funds in the hands of theirtreasurer.
§13 . All persons who pay taxes under thisact towards
the building of said road shall be stockholders in said
company forthe amount of taxes they shallthus pay ,
and proper certificatesthereof shall be delivered to them
therefor whenever they shallhave paid an amount equal
to twenty-fivedollars,and so on untilthe road iscom
pleted .
§14 . That it shall be the privilege of each person
through whose land said road may run , and who may
have subscribed stock,to work out the value of his said

744 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
stock on the road -bed through his land , at the estimated
price for labor done , or he shall have the preference at
the lowest bid therefor; and allsubscribersof stock shall
have the privilegeof working out their stock subscribed
by them , under the directionof the president and direct-
ors of the road ,at the customary prices for labor. They
must ,however , work under the directionof the president
and directors, or theirauthorized agents.
§ 15. That whenever the resources of said company are
such that, with the aid of the county subscriptionof one
thousand dollars per mile, as authorized by acts hereto-
fore passed ,itwill be enabled to build its road one mile
or more ,and when the same shallhave been actuallylaid
out and located by a competent engineer, and the right
of way secured, and proof of these facts satisfactorily
made known to the Lewis county court,said court shall
be authorized to make the subscriptionof one thousand
dollarsper mile,and so on for each mile so made and
ready for travelby the public, untilthe entireroad is
completed .
§ 16. That persons who shallsubscribestock to said
company and shallhave paid the same eitherin money
or work as aforesaid,shallnot be compelled to pay the
specialtaxesleviedby sectionelevenof thisactuntilthe
entireamount of taxes assessed against them respectively
shallequal the amount which they may have subscribed
and paid in stock as aforesaid; theirsubscriptionsshall
be considered and treated as taxes paid in advance ,and
all the tax-payers being stockholders to the amount of
taxesso paid by them towards the buildingof saidroad,
the intentionbeing to equalize allpersons along the route
of saidroad subjectto the payment of taxesthereon; and
thetaxes againstpersons who may have subscribedand
paid theirstock as aforesaidshallbe canceled,and not
listedfor collectionuntilequalized as aforesaid.
§ 17. The county court of Lewis county ,in addition to
the subscriptionof one thousand dollarsper mile,shall
subscribe stock in one half the cost of building a bridge
over Kinniconick on the line of said road , and shallissue

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 74
the bonds of said county for that purpose upon the same
terms as other bonds authorized to be issued by said
county for building bridges; and upon the subscription
and issualof said bonds by the county for the building of
said road and bridge,the county shall become a stock-
holderin said company to the amount thus subscribed.
The stockof Lewis countyshallbe representedand voted
at any electionfor officers.& c., of said company by the
county judge of saidcounty. That to pay the subscrip-
tionsthusmade by saidcounty judge to the capitalstock.
of said company of one thousand dollars per mile,said
county judge is hereby authorized and required to issue
the bonds of Lewis county ,payable at not longer date
than five years, bearing interest at six per cent. per
annum , principal and interestpayable at such place or
places as the county judge may direct. Said bonds shall
be delivered to the president of said company ,who shall
execute a receiptfor same in the name of the company ;
and upon the delivery of said bonds to said president,
the county of Lewis shall become a stockholder in said
company to the amount of one thousand dollarsper mile .
The officersof said company shall execute a bond in the
Lewis county courtupon the deliveryofthe county bonds
to the president of the company ,for the faithfulexpendi-
ture of said bonds to the legitimate constructionof said
road , as provided for by act of General Assembly , April
9 , 1873 .
§ 18. The compensation of the president and directors
shallbe fixedby the company in their by -laws , and shall
not exceed two dollars per day for the president and one
dollar per day for the directors for each day's service
actuallyperformed by them respectively; and afterthe
road isfinished,the same may be wholly discontinued or
reduced to a nominal consideration .
§19 . The company shall have the power and right to
pass by -laws prohibiting " dead -locking " on said road ,
trespassingor injuriesdone to the same ,and the evading
of paying tollsfor travel done thereon, and to recover
damages or finesthereforby proper proceedingsin the

746 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
name of its president before a magistrate of the district
in which the same shall have been committed or done .
§20 . That allacts and parts of acts in conflictwith this
act are hereby repealed.
§21 . This act shallbe in force from and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 21 , 1882.
CHAPTER 1218 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleof spirituous, vinous,or malt liquors,or any
mixture thereof, at or within two miles of Cany Fork BaptistChurch , in
Barren county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 1220 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof William M. Combs ,of Breathittcounty .
WHEREAS , The Commonwealth of Kentucky , through
A. H. Quillin, agent of the Auditor , did, on the fourth
day of October,1869,sellone stableand lotas the prop-
erty of the State, and at said sale C. C. Hanks became
the purchaser thereof,at the price of three hundred dol-
lars, said property being in the town of Campton , Wolfe
county; and C. C. Hanks ,aftergivingbond and approved
surety for payment of said bid, transferred the bid to
Wm . M. Combs , of Breathitt county ; and it appearing
from the records in the Auditor's office that there has been
$ 192.56 paid upon said bond ; and aftersaid payment
said stableand lot was recovered by Thomas B. Keeton ,
and losttosaid Combs ,by decisionof the Court of Ap-
peals, the State never having had any titlethereto ; there.
fore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be , and he is
hereby ,directedto draw his warrant upon the Treasury
for the sum of one hundred and ninety-two dollars and
fifty-six cents ($ 192.56), payable to Wm . M. Combs out
of any moneys not otherwiseappropriated.
§2 This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage.
Approved April 21 , 1882.
CHAPTER 1221 .
AN ACT toincorporatetheOhio County TurnpikeCompany .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That there be created a body -politic,by the name
of the Ohio County Turnpike Company ,which shallhave

748 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
perpetual succession, a common seal, may sue and be
sued,contract and be contracted with,and subject to the-
provisions hereinafterprovided in thisact.
§ 2. The company aforesaidshallhave power and au-
thorityto constructturnpike roads in Ohio county by such
routes as may be adopted by the president and directors
of said company .
§3 . The capitalstock of saidcompany shallnot exceed
fiftythousand dollars, in shares of twenty -five dollars
each , and each share in said company shallbe entitledto
one vote .
§ 4. That J. E. Pendleton , H. D. McHenry , S. E. Hill,
W. L. King ,E. C. Hubbard ,J. P. Barnard , and Thomas
Stevens , and S. K. Cox , any three of whom may act, are
appointed commissioners to open books and receive sub-
scriptionsto said capitalstock at any time and place,and
until the sum of one thousand dollars shall be subscribed .
As soon as the sum of one thousand dollars shall be sub-
scribedto the capital stock of said company ,a meeting
of said subscribers to said stock shall be held , and there
shall be elected a president, vice president, secretary,
treasurer , and five directors to manage the affairs of
said company , who shall hold theirofficesuntil the first
regular electionprovided for in thisact.
§5 The officersand directorsprovided for shall hold
their officesfor one year , or until their successors are
electedand qualified, and the firstregular electionfor the
officers and directors aforesaid shall be held on the first
Monday in January , 1883, and annually thereafter.
§ 6. That said company shall have all the rights,
powers,and authoritythat are or shallby generallaws
be conferred upon turnpike companies .
§7 . The treasurer,or any other person authorized by
said company to collect,handle , or disburse funds,shall
enter intogood and sufficientbonds,to be approved by
the board of directors of said county ,to faithfullypay
over and account for all sums that may come to his
hands .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 749
§ 8. Vacancies in said officesor directors shall be filled
by the board of directorsuntilthe next succeeding an-
nual election .
§9 . That said company may acquire, by deed ,gift,or
conveyance,or by writsofad quod damnum ,rightsof way ,
ground fortoll-houses (not exceeding one acre at each
house), rock, timber, quarries, and other material, as
may be necessary to build said roads, not exceeding 30
feet wide , as said company may determine .
§ 10. It shall be the duty of the directorsof said road
to lay before the stockholders, at the annual elections, a
fair statement of the conditions of the company , with a
detailedstatement of allreceiptsand expenditures.
§ 11. That when said company shallconstruct one mile
of road ,they shall be allowed to charge and collect one
half the toll allowed by law until they shall finishtwo
and one half miles of road , when they shall as allowed
to charge and collectthree fourthsof the sum allowed
by law until they shall finishfive miles of road , where
they shallbe allowed to charge and collectthe fullrates.
§ 12. The court of claims of Ohio county shall be au-
thorized to subscribe,for and on behalf of said county ,
shares in said company ,not exceeding $ 1,500per mile for
each mile of road constructed,and said subscriptionshall
not be paid untilthe presidentof saidcompany shallstate
under oath, and filethe same in the clerk'sofficeof Ohio
county ,that said mile or miles of said roads have been
finished ..
§ 13. This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved April 21, 1882 .
CHAPTER 1222 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof theMiltonand Hunter'sBottom TurnpikeRoad
Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be lawful for the Milton and Hunter's
Bottom Turnpike Road Company toerecta toll-gateupon

750 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
its road , and to collect tolls for travel thereon when said

utes as the length of said road shall bear to the distance

CHAPTER 1223 .
AN ACT concerning the Paint Lick, Walnut Meadow ,and Big Hill Turn-
pike Road Company .
WHEREAS ,It has been represented that the Paint Lick ,
Walnut Meadow , and Big Hill Turnpike Road Company
have constructed in part their road from the town of
Paint Lick toward the Big Hill, a distance of about five
miles, and yet said road is involved in debt,and the re-
ceiptsin tollsdo not more than keep it in repair; there-
fore ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That on a petitionin equityplead in the Madison
common pleascourtby the creditoryof saidcompany ,to
which the president and treasurer shall be made parties
as representativesof the stockholders,power and author-
ity are hereby conferred on said court to decree a ale of
same , and a distributionof the proceeds thereof among
those who own stock in said road in proportion to the
amount paid by them respectively; or said court may ,in
itsdiscretion, upon equitable terms , adjudge a lease of
said road for a term of years to discharge the indebted-
ness of the company , and have the extension of the road.
§2 . That Madison county , being interestedas a stock-
holder in said road,must be a partyto said action,and
may be brought before the court by serviceof process on
the county judge of said county .
§ 3. This act to be of force from itspassage .
Approved April 21 , 1882 .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 751
CHAPTER 1224 .
AN ACT to provide forrebuildingthe bridge acrossGreen riverat Rio ,in
Hart county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That there is hereby incorporated a bridge com-
pany ,by the name and titleof " Rio Bridge Company ,"
for the purpose of constructing a bridge of imperishable
material across Green river,on the turnpike road which
leads from Bardstown to Nashville, Tennessee, by the
way of Glasgow , Kentucky . The capitalstock of said
company shallbe twenty thousand dollars,to be divided
into shares of one hundred dollars each .
§2 . The books for subscriptionof stock shallbe opened
by H. B. Lane , W. B. Craddock , Jas. R. Armour , W. W.
Bowling , and Will. Adair , or any three thereof, at Rio ,
Hart county,on the firstSaturday in May ,1882,and at
such other times and places as any three of said commis
sioners may designate by written or printed advertise-
ments firstposted up in three public places ten days .
The subscribers of stock shall sign their names to a writ-
ing in said book as follows : We ,whose names are hereto
subscribed,promise to pay to the president and directors
of Rio Bridge Company the sum of one hundred dollars
for each and every share of stock attached to our names ,
at such time and place as they may direct. Witness our
honors this day of — , 188-. That saidcompany
may , and are hereby permitted to,build a bridge upon ,
and to use the abutments ,and to erect a pier in said river
if they consider the same necessary.
§ 3. So soon as one thousand dollars of stock is sub-
scribed, the stockholders shall be convened in Rio , in
Hart county, upon fivedays' public notice; and under
the supervision of not less than three of said commis-
sioners, an election shall be held, each share of stock
casting one vote for president and three directors, each
of whom must be a stockholder in said company . They
shall hold their office until the firstSaturday in May ,
1883, when an electionshallbe held in said Rio for their

752 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
successors; and every firstSaturday in May in each year
a new election shall be held to fillsaid office: Provided ,
however , Each of said officersshall take and subscribe an
oath , which shall be recorded in the books of said com-
pany ,that they will,to the best of theirjudgment and
ability, faithfullyperform all the duties of theirsaid
offices; and they shallcontinue in office, and perform the
duties thereof, untiltheir successors are duly elected and
qualified. The said president and directors,when duly
qualified, shallappoint a treasurer and a clerk,who shall
also take a like oath ,to be recorded as aforesaid ; and the
treasurershall execute a bond , with good and sufficient
security, to be approved by the board , in a sum of at
least ten thousand dollars, conditioned that he will faith-
fullyand trulyperform all the duties of said office, and
pay over all moneys which shall come to his hands as
such , in obedience to the order of said board ; and for a
violationof his bond he may be proceeded against, with
his securities,by motion or suit in the circuitcourt of
any county of thisCommonwealth , as sheriffsmay now
be proceeded against for failingto pay over moneys col-
lected on executions .
§4. The said company shall be a body corporate and
politic,under the name and style of " Rio Bridge Com-
pany ," and in that name may sue and be sued,plead and
be impleaded,and may have and use a common seal,and
shall have perpetual succession .
§5 Said company shall be allowed , and are author-
ized to charge the following tolls,to wit : For each foot-
man , five cents; each horse and rider, ten cents; each
led or loose horse , jacks , jennies , or mules , five cents ;
each head of loose cattle,four cents; each head of sheep ,
hogs , or goats , two and one half cents ; each one horse
buggy and horse, twenty -five cents; each wagon , buggy ,
or carriage with two horses attached,thirtyfive cents;
each wagon with four horses attached, fiftycents ; each
wagon with three horses attached,forty-fivecents; each
wagon with five horses attached, fifty-five cents; each
wagon with six horses attached, sixty-five cents: Pro

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 753
vided, however, That when a wagon or other vehicle is
drawn by oxen , mules , or other stock,that said stock be
rated as a horse in this tariff; each cart with one horse
attached , fifteen cents ; each cart with two horses at-
tached , twenty cents .
§ 6. The said president and board of directors shall
employ a bridge-keeper , who shall take an oath, to be
recordedin the books of the company ,that he willtruly
and faithfullydischargethe dutiesof his position, and
willtrulyand honestlyaccountforand pay to saidcom-
pany monthly all moneys received by him for tolls. He
shall execute a bond for the sum of one thousand dollars,
with good and sufficientsecurity,payable to the president
and directors aforesaid, conditioned upon the faithful
performance of his duties; and for a violationof his bond
he and his securitiesshall be liableby motion or suit in
the circuitcourt, and may be proceeded against in like
manner as sheriffsfor a failureto pay over money col-
lected upon executions .
§7 . The said company may make contracts with resi-
dent citizensof Hart county to pass over said bridge at
such price per year or fractionalpart of a year as the
company may deem just and proper .
§ 8. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 21, 1882.

CHAPTER 1226 .

lege in South Carrollton .

head , W. T. Stiles,E. B. Smith , Rol E. Humphrey ,W.
H. Whitson ,S. J. Rhoads ,E. M. Coleman ,Jos. B. Brown ,

Whitmer ,J. S. Hill,J. K. Sullivan,Jas. C. Moorman , of
Muhlenburg county ,State of Kentucky ,desiring to fur-

CHAPTER 1227 .

CHAPTER 1228 .
AN ACT for the benefit of Joshua F. Bullitt .
WHEREAS , Joshua F. Bullittbecame bound as surety
for the Kentucky River Navigation Company , upon a
note to the Commissioners of the Sinking Fund ,dated No-
vember 19, 1872, for $ 3,801.80for rentsdue upon a lease
of the State'simprovements upon the Kentucky river;
and whereas , on the 13th of February , 1880, said Com-
missionersobtained a judgment of the Franklin circuit
court on said note for the amount thereof , with interest
from said 19th of November , 1872, on which judgment
said Bullitt,on the 21st of May , 1880 ,paid $ 3,400,leav-
ing a balance of over $ 2,400 stilldue ; and whereas ,in
view of the factsconcerning said suretyship, as set forth
in the memorial submitted by said Bullittto this General
Assembly ,it appears that he is equitably entitledto the
relief therein asked for ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

liabilityon saidjudgment .

erty was all sold, and left him indebted to the State the

ous , vinous , or malt liquors to any person other than

CHAPTER 1238 .
AN ACT to authorizeGlasgow Lodge , No. 65, Independent Order of Odd
Fellows , to issne bonds .

of Kentucky :

Odd Fellows,be,and the same is hereby, authorizedto
issuebonds ,not exceeding ten thousand dollarsin value,
to aid saidlodgein the purchaseof a lotorlotsin thecity
of Glasgow ,and the erection thereon of a hall building
to be known as Odd Fellows ' Hall .
§ 2. Said bonds shallbear six per cent.interest,paya-
ble annually in the city of Glasgow , and shall have ten
years to run ,but redeemable at the pleasure of the lodge,
afterthe expiration of two years from the date of each
bond .
§3 . Said bonds shallbe signedby the noblegrand and
attestedby the secretary, under the corporate seal of this
lodge,and may be issued,at the discretionof the lodge,
in sums not less than twenty -five dollars nor more than
five hundred dollars, and all the property of this lodge
subject to execution shall be bound for the payment of
both principal and interestof the same .
§ 4. Said lodge shallhave fullpower to prescribethe
form of the bonds ,to make allarrangements necessary for
carryingintoexecutionthe provisionsof thisact,and to
create a sinking fund out of the rents and revenues
accruing to the lodge for the purpose of paying off the
interestand principal of said bonds at maturity,or sooner
if deemed advisable .
§5 . Said lot or lotswhen so purchased ,and said hall
buildingwhen so erected,so faras they shallbe used for
the charitablepurposes of thislodge, shallbe forever
freeand exempt from taxationforany purposewhatever.
§ 6. This act to take effectfrom its passage .
Approved April 22 , 1882..

CHAPTER 1242 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Leo Hayden , of Lincoln county .
WHEREAS , Fontaine F. Bobbitt taught the common
school in districtNo. 36 ,in Rockcastle county ,Kentucky ,
during the present school year , and said school has been
so reported to the Superintendent ,and the money paid to
thecounty commissioner; yet,upon some doubt or tech-
nicality, the schoolcommissioner of Rockcastlecounty,
as to whether or not said school was taught according to

CHAPTER 1243 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the town of Barbourville , in Knox county ,
approved 2d February, 1871, and an amendment thereto, approved 19th
February , 1874 .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That so much of an act,entitled"An act to reduce
into one the acts concerning the town of Barbourville,"
fixingtheboundary of saidtown ,and forotherpurposes,
as authorizes the trustees of said town to grant a license
to taverns,groceries,coffee-houses ,confectioners,retailers
of spirits,vinous,or malt liquors,or restaurants,be , and
the same are hereby ,repealed.
§2 . That an act, entitled"An act to amend an act to
reduce into one allacts in relationto Barbourville," ap-
proved 19th February , 1874 ,be so amended that it shall
not be lawful for the trusteesof said town to fixthe price
or tax for coffee-house , or any other licenseto sellliquors
at any sum in their discretionnot less than one hundred
dollars, and not exceeding fivehundred dollars; and that
all provisions in either of said acts herein named that
authorizes the granting of a license to any one by any
officeror trusteesto vend or give or sellardent spiritsof
any kind ,or any mixture thereof, be ,and the same are
hereby ,repealed. Any licenseissued in violationof this
act shall be void .
§3 . That it shall not be lawful for the county judge ,
or any one acting as county judge,of Knox county,to
grant a licenseto any one or more persons to vend or sell
spirituousor malt liquors,or any mixture thereof,in the

§4 . This act shall take effectfrom its passage .
Approved April 22 , 1882 .

AN ACT to establisha ferryat a pointone mileor more below theBurks-

of Kentucky :

ferry from his land in Cumberland county across the

villeferry,not to exceed one mile below the line on said
river when the exclusive right of the Burksville ferry
ends ; and he or his lessees or assigns may use either
steam ,horse,flat-boats, or skiffsfor the crossing of per-
sons, stock, or freight. He and his assigns or lessees
shall have the exclusive rightto ferry and crosspersons ,
stock, and freightfor one mile below the line on said
river where the said Burksville ferry has the exclusive
privilegeon said river; and any person ferryingpersons ,
stock, or freightwithin said bounds shall be subject to a
fineof twenty dollars,recoverable by warrant before any
justice of the peace ,county judge , or by indictment,one
half for the use of the keepers of the ferry and the other
for the use of the Commonwealth .
§2 . Said Hunter is hereby authorized to condemn by
writ of ad quod damnum a right of way from the Burks-
villeand Neeley's Ferry road to said ferry,and from said
ferryto the Burksville and Albany road , not exceeding
fortyfeet wide .
§3 . This act shall take effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 22, 1882.

CHAPTER 1249 .
AN ACT to amend an act , entitled " An act to prevent stock from running
at largein the county of Henderson ."
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. It shall not be lawful for any person or persons
within the county of Henderson to permit their stock of
any descriptionto run at large off theirown premises of
which they may be in possession of.
§ 2. Any person or persons so offending shall be liable
to any person or persons for any damage done by such
stock, and the parties so damaged shall have a lien on
such stock forall such damage and cost of enforcing the
same .
§ 3. And the person or persons so damaged shallhave
the authorityby this act to drive such trespassing stock
in his or her lot, and there keep them untilthe damages
has been assessed by the justicesof theirrespective dis-
trict .
§ 4. And if the owner of such trespassing stock does
not pay the damage assessedby said justicesat the time,
:

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 767
they shalladvertise,and in ten days' noticesellso many
of such stock as willpay the damage and cost.
§ 5. The justicesof the peace shallbe paid one dollar
each for their services in all such cases .
§ 6. It shallbe the duty of the judge of the county
court of Henderson to cause the sense of the people or
the qualified voters of Henderson county to be taken
upon thisact,at the generalelectionheldin saidcounty,
and forthat purpose there shallbe opened upon the poll-
books , at each voting place in the differentprecincts of
said county ,a column headed " stock law , another col-
umn headed " against the stock," and the question shall
be asked ," Are you in favor or against the stock law ? "
and should a majority of allthe legal votes cast at said
election be in favor of the stock law , then this act shall
be in fullforce from January the 1st, 1883 .
§ 7. The said judge of the county court of Hender-
son shall in like manner , as often submit this act to the
qualifiedvotersof said county at any subsequent gen-
eral election, when requested in writing by as many as
twenty resident voters, who are tax -payers of said
county , should not a majority of votes be cast therefor
in first submission .
§8 . All acts and parts of acts in conflictwith thisact
are hereby repealed.
Approved April 22, 1882 .

CHAPTER 1254 .
AN ACT for the benefit of James S. Darnell .
WHEREAS , The Legislature of Kentucky heretofore,to-
wit,by an act approved May 6th, 1880,provided that if
any person should arrest a person guilty of stealing a
horse or mule , and secure the indictment and conviction
of such person ,that the person so securing the arrestand
convictionshallbe entitledto a reward of fiftydollars
therefor; and whereas , James S. Darnell, of Franklin
county , Kentucky , did arrest one L. B. Hagerman ,
charged with stealing a horse in Anderson county ,Ken-
tucky , from J. C. Waterfill, November 7th, 1881, and
delivered said Hagerman to the jailer of said county ,
and said Hagerman was indicted for horse-stealingby a
court of competent jurisdictionin said county of Ander-
son ; and whereas ,the said Hagerman was granted bail,
which he forfeitedand escaped trialand conviction,but
without any fault of said Darnell ; and whereas , the
Commonwealth of Kentucky took a forfeitureof said
bail bond of said Hagerman for the sum of $ 300 , and
said Darnell is likelyto lose the reward of $ 50 because
the court released the prisoner on bail; now ,therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be directed
to draw his warrant in favor of said James S. Darnell
for the sum of fiftydollars,to be paid out of any money
in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated.
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 22, 1882.

§3 . The capitalstock of said company shallbe divided

struct said road and toll-houses , at which toll may be
collectedat the rates now allowed by general law .

scribed to justifythe beginning of said road ,the commis-

Bardstown and New Haven pike.

authorized to subscribe to the capital stock of said com-
pany in an amount not exceeding seven hundred and fifty
dollarsper mile,should they deem it,in their discretion,

cuted as such writs are now issued and executed by law

ceedings shall be had ,except the damages shall be paid

CHAPTER 1259 .

county of Spencer.

of Kentucky :

peace in the county of Spencer shallhave jurisdiction
concurrentwith the circuitand quarterlycourtsof said
county in allactions and proceedings for the recovery of
money and personalproperty(except in actionsor pro-
ceedings for injury to person or character),where the
matter in controversy, exclusive of interest and costs,
does not exceed one hundred dollars in value . The
pleadings therein shall be oral and without verifica-
tion; but before any warrant or summons shallissue,the
plaintiffshall filewith the justice the account , written
contract , or a short written statement of the facts on
which the action is founded .
§2 . Where the plaintiff'sdemand exceeds fiftydollars,
exclusive of interestor costs,he shall pay a tax of fifty
cents when he brings his suit,and when the defendant
pleads a set-offor counter-claim which exceeds fiftydol-
lars(exclusiveof interestor costs),he shallat the time he

peace as now provided by law .

§4. Thisacttobe in forcefromand afteritspassage.
Approved April 22, 1882.

CHAPTER 1263 .
AN ACT to amend an act to regulatethe saleand giving away of spirituons,
vinous,or malt liquorsin the cityof Mayfield,Graves county, or within
one mile of said city.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act to regulate the sale of and giving
away of spirituous,vinous,and malt liquorsin the city
of Mayfield , Graves county , or within one mile of said
city,approved February 19, 1878,be amended as follows:
That all of section two of said act, after the word
" wholesale ," in the third line of said section , and also
allof sectionthree,section four,and section five of said
act, are hereby repealed.
§2 . This act shall take effect and be in force from
and afterJune 1,1882,but shallnot affectany licensed

druggist untilthe expiration of any licensewhich he may
hold to sellspirituousliquorsat the date of the approval
of this act .
Approved April 22, 1882 .
CHAPTER 1264 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Geo . W. Weller,of Bullittcounty.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be ,and he is
hereby,directedto draw hiswarrant on the Treasury in
favor of Geo . W. Weller, of Bullittcounty, for the sum
of twenty -five dollars,which sum of twenty -five dollars
is hereby appropriated to said Weller out of any money
in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated.
§2 . This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved April 22, 1882.
CHAPTER 1265 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof the Walton and Frazee Turnpike Road Company ,
in Mason county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the officers and directors of the Walton and
Frazee Turnpike Road ,and allthoseowning or residing
on lands situatedwithin one half mile of said road , shall
be required and compelled to work their road tax out on
said turnpike as the officersmay direct,being governed
by the same laws as now govern surveyors of county
roads; and allsuch persons are hereby exempted from
working road tax on any other road .
§2 . This act to be in fullforce from itspassage .
Approved April 22,1882 .

No. 14,Pendleton county,forthe schoolyear ending June
30th, 1882 ; therefore,
Be itenactedbytheGeneralAssemblyoftheCommonwealth

hereby authorized to draw his draft on the Auditor of
Public Accounts in favor of the commissioner of common
schoolsfor said county for the sum of fifty-four dollars
and thirty-four cents ($54.34), on which shallbe paid

school year ending June 30th, 1882 .

Approved April 22 , 1882.

CHAPTER 1268 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Glasgow , Edmonton and BurksvilleTelephone
Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That E. R. Beauchamp ,J. C. Muncie ,Jos. U. Rog-
ers,John A. Murray ,John Ritter, W. G. Hunter , and E.
Emmons , and theirassociates,successors, and assigns,be ,
and they are hereby,createda body-corporateand politic,
under the name of the " Glasgow ,Edmonton and Burks-
villeTelephone Company ;" with power under such name
to contract and be contracted with, sue and be sued ; to
have perpetual succession, and a common seal,and such
other powers as herein granted .
§2 . The saidcompany shallhave power and authority
to construct,maintain, and operatea telephonelinefrom

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 801
Glasgow ,in Barren county ,to Burksville, in Cumberland
county .
§3 . Said company may acquire rightsof way for said
line in the same manner as now provided by law of this
Commonwealth forthe acquisitionof rightsof way by
turnpike road companies .
§ 4. Said company may fixthe rate of chargesforthe
transmission of messages over said line,and may build
branch lines from said main line .
§5 . The capitalstock of said company shallbe ten capitalstock.
thousand dollars,to be divided into shares of twenty -five
dollars each .
§ 6. The businessof said company shallbe managed Directors.
by a board of five directors,to be chosen annually, upon
the firstMonday in July each year , by a vote of the
stockholders , at which election each share shall be enti-
tled to one vote .
§ 7. The board of directors shall choose one of their Officers.
number president,whose duties shallbe those incident to
said office. They shall have power to appoint a treas-
urer,and such other agents as they may deem necessary
forthe interestsof the business,and may pay them such
-compensation as they may deem reasonable . Said com-
pany may acquire and hold all propertynecessary to
enablethem to carryout the purposes and provisionsof
this act .
§8 . Said company may organize and begin the con-
struction of said main line when one thousand dollars of
the capitalstock shallhave been subscribed for and paid
in. Each of said incorporators are authorized to solicit
and take subscriptionsof stock to said company . When
one thousand dollarsin stock shallhave subscribed, then
the incorporatorsshallcalla meeting of the stockholders,
at which time a board of directors shall be elected to
serve tillthe firstregular election, and until their suc-
cessorsare elected and qualified.
§ 9. Said company may , by its board of directors,
make allby-laws,rulesand regulations,necessaryforthe
LOC . L., VOL . II- 51

802 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
proper carrying out of the provisions of this act, not
inconsistent with the Constitution and laws of this State
and of the United States .
§ 10. Said company may establishbranch lines and
offices along or from said main line,and may contract
with any other telephone company or telegraph company
for the transmission or receiving of messages over its
linesto any other point or points.
§ 11. If any person or persons shall willfullyor mali-
ciouslyinjure,obstruct,or destroyany of thepropertyof
saidcompany ,he shallbe confinedin thecounty jailnot
less than three nor more than twelve months .
§ 12. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 22 , 1882 .
CHAPTER 1269 .
AN ACT to regulateofficialadvertisingin Jeffersoncounty .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That it shall be the duty of the county judge of
Jefferson county , in the month of July of each year , to
hear such competent proof as may be offeredby affidavit
or orally,and from such proof to determine which news-
paper has the largestbonafide circulationwithin such
county ; and when he shallhave heard sufficientevidence
to determine the same , he shall make an order on the
records of his court designating such newspaper as the
one in which shallbe published allofficialadvertisements
now required by law to be published.
§2 . It shall be the duty of all clerks,constables, mas-
ter and special commissioners , marshals , sheriffs, and
collectors,and allother officerswho ,by law or order of
any court held in such county ,may be required to adver-
tise sales,notices,and other matters,to insertand pub-
lish the same in the newspaper selected in accordance
with the firstsection of this act ; and no advertisement .
inserted in any other paper shall be taxed as costs, or
charged for,unless insertedalso in the paper so selected

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 803
as above,and speciallyorderedto be so publishedin such
other paper by the court, or the proper authority having
jurisdictionover such proceeding: Provided, That in no
case shallthe amount charged or paid forsaid advertis-
ing exceed the regular advertising rates of said paper ;
and alladvertisements specifiedin this act shall be set
up as solid matter, printed without unnecessary display.
§3 . In determining the paper having the largestcircu-
lation in said connty ,as provided in the firstsection of
thisact,the countyjudge shalltake the average bonafide
circulationof such paper within such county for a period
of sixtydays precedingthe firstof July of each year,not
including,however , any issue or editionprinted or dated
on Sunday , nor the weekly issue, if the paper be both
daily and weekly .
§ 4. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 22 , 1882.

CHAPTER 1272 .
<AN ACT to authorizethe Butlercounty court of claimsto levy a per capita
and ad valorem tax forthe purpose of creatinga road and bridge fund for
saidcounty,and to govern the expenditureof the same .
Be itenactedbytheGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the court of claims of Butler county be ,and
they are hereby , authorized and empowered to levy a
special tax of not exceeding one dollar per capita on
each person subjectto pay county levy,and twenty-five
cents on each one hundred dollars' worth of taxable prop-
erty in said county per annum ,which tax,when collected,
shallconstitutea special road and bridge fund for said
county .
§2 . Said tax shallbe levied by the court of claims of
said county at the same time and in the same manner as
isthe county levy, and shall be collectedby the sheriffof
the county ,who ,together with the securitieson hiscounty
levy bond ,shall be responsiblefor the faithfulcollection
and disbursement of same ,which it shall be the duty of
the sheriffto pay over on the order of the county court;
and the sheriffshallbe allowed compensation for collect-
ing the same as now allowed by law forcollectingcounty
levy; but he shall not have compensation as if the said
tax was a separate fund ,but as if same was part of the
county levy.
i
§3 . That the fund raised by thistax shallbe kept sep-
arate and apart from all other county funds, and shall
be used only for the purpose of buildingand repairing
bridges on public highways , and for building, altering,
and repairing public highways within said county : Pro-
vided,however, That nothing in thisact shallbe construed
as changing the law with reference to the power and
duty of the county court to open , establish, or change
roads at the cost of the person moving for same , or to
dictate terms with reference to such motions : And pro-
vided further, That nothing herein shall be construed as
depriving the court of claims from levying a poll-tax as
now authorized by law ,in addition to this tax, for any
and allpurposes that it may now be levied for.

808 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 4. That forthe purpose of carrying out this act,the
said court of claims ishereby authorized and empowered
toappointone or more commissionersof publichighways
and bridges as seems to them best,which commissioner or
commissioners may be given power over the whole county ,
or only over a certain districtof such size and boundary
as to the court may seem best;and the court may contract
with the said commissioner or commissioners as to their
compensation and duties,and shall take bond from the
said commissioner or commissioners , with good and suffi-
cient sureties, for the faithfulperformance of the said
commissioners ' duties , and for the faithful and full dis-
bursement of all money that may come to his hand as
commissioner as aforesaid .
§ 5. Itshallbe the duty of allcommissioners appointed
under this act to exercise a general oversight of all roads-
and bridges within his district; to report any needs of
same to the court of claims ; to report any overseer fail-
ing to do his duty to the grand jury; to hirehands, and
have same under his oversight; to work on any road or
bridge in the county when so ordered by the court of
claims, and to do allwork on same that may be ordered
by said court; to superintendthe buildingof allnew
bridges; to immediately repair any bridge damaged by
accident; to move the county court for the alteration,
change ,or discontinuance of any road when in his judg-
ment thepublicinterestdemands it; to oppose any alter-
ation,change ,or discontinuance of any public highway
when in his judgment same would be detrimental to the
public,and to expend any money that may come to his
hands for the improvement of the roads and bridges of
his county ; but he shallmake no expenditure exceeding
fiftydollarsforany one purpose or priceof work ,except.
on the order of the court of claims,unless the cost of the
same be lessthan fiftydollars,when the expendituremay
be made on the order of the county judge alone.
§ 6. Every road commissioner appointed under thisact
shall, before enteringupon the duties of his office, in
open court take an oath that he will well and faithfully

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 809
discharge the duties of said officewithout favor or par-
tialityto any individual.
§7. Every road commissioner appointedunder thisact
shall,in additionto being liableon hisbond ,be also liable
to indictmentby the grand jury of the county forwillful
neglect of duty , malfeasance or nonfeasance in office,
and upon convictionthereofin the circuitcourtbe fined
in a sum not exceeding $ 500 .

duty or liabilitythat may vest under by existing law , or

done by the road hands of the community under existing
law ; but thisstatuteshall be construed as in allrespects
supplemental to the statutesnow in force.
§ 9. This act shallonly apply to Butler county .
§ 10. This act shallbe submitted to a vote of the peo-
ple of Butler county on the firstMonday in August , 1882,
legal notice thereof having been posted at each voting
precinctin said county at leastthreeweeks before said
August election; said notices to statehow much tax may
be levied annually on each one hundred dollars' worth of
property,and alsoon each poll; and ifa majorityof the.
voters voting on that day in said county vote for the bill,
it is then to become a law ; otherwise it is to be null and
void .
Approved April 22, 1882.

CHAPTER 1275 .
AN ACT to establishthe dividing linebetween the countiesof Wayne and
Whitley in thisState.
WHEREAS , Doubts have arisen as to the true location of
the linedividing said counties; for remedy whereof ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the line between the counties of Wayne and
Whitley ,in this State,shallbe as follows: Beginning in
the centre of the Big South Fork of Cumberland river,
where the line between Pulaski and Whitley counties
strikessaid stream oppositethe mouth of Rock creek;
thence up the said Big South Fork with itsmeanderings
to the Kentucky and Tennessee State line; and that all
of the territorywhich lies south and east of said line
which was embraced in Wayne county by the provisions
of the act, entitled "An act for the erection of a new
county out of the counties of Pulaski and Cumberland ,"
approved December 18, 1800,shallbe considered,and is

816 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
now declared to be, part of Whitley county: Provided,
however, That thisact shallnot affectthe validityof any
patentto land lyingin saidterritory,whether the survey
upon which said patent was issued and restswas made
on a warrant issuedby the county courtof eitherWayne
or Whitley county; but said patent or patents, where
otherwiseregularlyissued, shallbe and are declared
valid and sufficient .
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 22, 1882.

ceive the highest number of votes,shallbe deemed and

CHAPTER 1281 .

near Leon Station , in Carter county .

of Kentucky :

istaken out of said boom by the owner ,purchaser , or by

CHAPTER 1284 .

of Kentucky :

monwealth of Kentucky ,in such sums as theydeem suffi-
cient, for the faithfulperformance of the contract by said

CHAPTER 1290 .

of Kentucky :

Kentucky , and D. C. Jameson and Solomon Ellis,of Gal-
latin county , Kentucky ,the present trustees of " Sparta
Institute," an institutionof learning situatedin the town
of Sparta , Owen county ,Kentucky , and their successors
in office,be ,and they are hereby , created a body -politic

stitute,and by thatname shallhave perpetualsuccession,

said " institute;" to sue and be sued ,plead and be im-
pleaded,in any of the courtsof thisCommonwealth ; to
acquire, by gift, grant, devise, purchase , or otherwise,
any real or personal property for the use of said " insti-
tute;" to sell and dispose of the same at pleasure,ac-

personal estateas they may deem proper forthe benefit

ness, to electthe presidentor principalprofessorsand

gether with the trustees,shall have power to expel such

of Kentucky :

Washington County Turnpike Road Company be, and
the same is hereby,amended so as to permit the stock-
holders of itsbranch road , or the board thereof, which
branch runs through the town of Johnsonville to the
Burnt Church,to transactitsown business,with allthe

rights and privileges, so far as applicable, granted by
the act of incorporation aforesaid; the right to sue and
be sued under the name and style of the Board of the
Branch Road of the Fox Creek and Washington County
Turnpike Road Company .

pike Road Company shallnot be responsiblefor the con-
tractsof the branch road aforesaid, nor the branch road.
responsible for the Fox Creek and Washington County
Turnpike Road Company's debts or contractshereafter
made or entered into .
§3 . That the liabilitiesnow existing,ifany ,are not in
anywise changed by this act.
§ 4. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 22, 1882 .
CHAPTER 1292 .
AN ACT for the benefitof the city of Frankfort.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. The cityof Frankfort having a large floatingdebt,.
now ,for the purpose of funding the same ,and to provide
forthe payment thereof, said cityishereby authorized
to issue itscoupon bonds in sums not exceeding seventy
thousand dollarsin the aggregate ,for any period not ex-
ceedingtwenty years,bonds and interestpayable at such
place as may be fixed by ordinance,bearing interestnot
exceeding six per cent.per annum , payable semi -annu-
ally; saidbonds to be in denominations of not lessthan
$ 100 nor more than $ 1,000. Said bonds shallbe issued
before the board of councilmen in open session, and a
registerof such issuemade upon itsjournal. They shall
be signed by the mayor and countersigned by the clerk,.
and bear the seal of said city,and the coupons shallbe
signed by the clerk alone . Of said bonds , $ 35,000 shall
bearsixper cent.interest,be speciallyregisteredasissued,
and delivered in payment of that amount due from the
saidcityforthe fund collectedby itfrom the lottery,and .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 841
so invested as required by law for the benefit of the
Frankfort cityschool,and when so issued,to be delivered
to the board of sinking fund commissioners for safe-keep-
ing. The remaining $ 35,000 of said bonds may be sold
and disposed of by the board of councilmen upon such
terms and in such manner as may be deemed best by the
board of sinking fund commissioners for the interest of
said city,but not below the par value thereof.
§2 . And whereas ,there has been no sinking fund cre-
ated forthe purpose of retiringthe presentindebtedness
of saidcity; now ,to provideforthe prompt payment of
the interestand principalat maturity, not only of the
presentbonded indebtednessof saidcity,but alsoof the
bonds hereby authorized to be issued, a sinking fund is
hereby created therefor,to consistfirstof allmoneys col-
lectedand receivedby saidcityfrom alllicensesof every
description,other than such part thereof as by law the
cityisrequiredtopay intotheStateTreasury;and second,
a sum to be by the board of councilmen of said city
leviedand collectedas taxesin saidcityannually,as will
be sufficient, when added to the amount realized from
licenses, to pay the interest and retirethe principal of
said bonded indebtedness as it matures ; and all officers
of said city whose duty it is to collect or receive any
part of the said funds so set apart to said sinking fund ,
shall,as fastas the same is received,pay the same over
to the board of sinking fund commissioners , first, how-
ever,obtaining a permit from the clerk of said city for
said payments , which permit shallbe dated, give the
amount of the payment to be made ,and the source from
whence the money to be paid was collectedor received.
Said collectingofficershall not, however , receive credit
for such payment until he returns to the cityclerk the
receiptof the board of sinking fund commissioners there-
for .
§3 . A board of sinking fund commissioners is hereby
establishedfor said city,to consistof the cashiersof the
three banks now established in said city and the mayor
of said city. If at any time the cashier of eitherof said

842 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
banks shall refuse to act as a member of said board ,the
board of councilmen shalldeclare his place in said board
vacant,and proceed by ballotto electa suitableperson
to fillthe same . No person ,except the mayor ,who isin
any way connected with the citygovernment or any
officethereof, or who is not a residentof said cityand
has residedtherein four years,shall be eligibleto a posi-
tion in said board . Said board shall electa president,a
secretaryand treasurerfrom itsmembers . It shallbe the
duty of said board to receive from the proper collecting
officersof said city,and receiptto the same therefor, all
moneys and dues of every kind belonging to the sinking
fund of saidcity,and properlyinvestin good and solvent
interest-bearing securities,and use the same in the proper
payment of the interest and retirement of the present
bonded indebtedness of said city,including that author-
ized by thisact: Provided,however,That in the retirement
of said bonded indebtedness,inasmuch as the $ 35,000 of
bonds hereby authorized to be issued in payment of so
much of the lottery school fund is in the nature of an
investment forthe benefit of the cityschool,that shallbe
the lastto be paid off, but the interest thereon shallbe
promptly paid to the use of said school, as provided by
law .
§ 4. The board of councilmen of the city of Frankfort
shallhave power to define the duties of the marshal of
the cityof Frankfort,appoint a receiveror collectorof
taxes and other dues to said city,and remove said receiver
or collector; and when said receiver or collector shall be
so appointed and sworn into officebefore said board ,and
a record made thereof on itsjournal, and he shallhave
given good bond for the faithfuldischarge of his duties,
to be approved by the board of councilmen ,he shallhave
the same powers and duties,and be under the same re-
sponsibilitiesthereforfor the collectionof taxes and other
dues to said city, the advertising,sale,and redemption of
property, and conveyance of same when sold to enforce
payment of taxes,as are now by law conferred and im-
posed upon the marshal of the city of Frankfort, or may
hereafterbe conferred upon said marshal or collector.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 843
§ 5. All laws or parts of laws in conflictwith thisact
are hereby repealed.
§ 6. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved April 22, 1882.
CHAPTER 1293 .
AN ACT to change the county linebetween the countiesof Pendleton and
Bracken so as to detach the farm of A. Aulick from Bracken and add the
same to Pendletoncounty.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the line between the counties of Bracken
and Pendleton be changed so as to begin on the North
Fork at the mouth of Charlie's branch , and running
thence northward up said branch to Shoewalter's line;
thence northwestardly with A. F. Aulick's line to the
line between said counties as now established ,the change
transferring a portion of the farm of A. F. Aulick from
the county of Bracken to the county of Pendleton .
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 22 , 1882 .

CHAPTER 1295 .
AN ACT authorizingthe county court of Boone county to appointcommis-
sionersto constructturnpike roads in certaindistrictsin saidcounty,and
to levy a tax on the property lying in said districtsforthatpurpose .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That when a majority of the owners of land, ex-
clusive of minors and persons under legal disabilities,
lyingin the county of Boone and withinone of eitherof
theroad districtsas describedin sectionthirteen(13) of
thisact,shallpresenta petitionto the county court ask-
ing it, then the said court shall appoint three discreet
freeholdersresiding in said districtto lay out,establish,
and construct a turnpike road in said county between
the points named in said petition; and said commission-
ers, under such name as said court in its order shall fix
upon , shallbe a body-politicand corporate, with full
power to lay out and construct such macadamized road ,
and for this purpose may contract and be contracted
with,sue and be sued,and may acquireby gift,purchase,
or condemnation such property, real or personal,or right
of way , as may be necessary for the construction, repair,
and operation of said road or roads.
§2 . That it shall be the duty of said commissioners ,
within a reasonable time after their appointment , to
cause a map to be made of said districtand the line
of the proposed road to be surveyed , and return to the
county courtsaidmap and survey,with the names of all
personsowning land in and residingin saiddistrict,and
the number of acres owned by each individual in said
district, together with the assessed value of the lands
lyingin said district,and the amount of personalprop

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 845
erty owned by each person residingin the same as is
shown by the assessor'sbooks of said county for the
preceding year . They shall also present a statement in
detail,with fulland complete estimates of the entire cost
of constructing said road or roads .
§3 . That at the next regular term of the county court.
afterfilingsaid report,the said court shall,by an order
duly entered on itsorder-book ,levy a tax on allthe prop-
erty, real and personal,in said district,in a sum sufficient
to build said road in accordance with the estimates re-
ported by the commissioners as the proposed cost of said
road , after deducting therefrom the amount of or value
of any donation , private subscription, or county aid,
which said commissioners may have received or be enti-
tled to, for the purpose of building said road : Provided,
however, That the tax to be leviedto produce saidsum
may be levied and collected in one ,two ,or three years,
as the said petitionersmay request and direct in their
said petitionto the court.
§ 4. That from the date of the order making the same
said levy shallconstitute a lien on the property assessed
as provided in thisact, and the respectiveowners thereof
shallbe bound forthe payment of the same ; and in case
any one bound to pay said levy shallfailto do so,when
due ,and afterten days'noticegiven the saiddelinquent
tax-payer by the aforesaid commissioners , the commis-
sionersmay place the same in the hands of the sheriffof
Boone county for collection,who shall then proceed to
collectsaid tax from said delinquent,and shallhave the
power to enforce its collection by the same means as
other taxes are to be collected,and shallreceive the same
fees forcollectingsaiddelinquent tax as are now received
by said sherifffor collectingother taxes.
§ 5. The said commissioners shall appoint one of their
number to be treasurer, who shall receive and receiptfor
all money or moneys due and coming to said commis-
sionersfrom allsources whatever ,and shallpay the same
out on theirorder,taking and preserving vouchers for all
such payments . But beforehe shallenterupon the dis

846 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
charge of his duties,he shallexecute a bond to the Com-
monwealth , with sureties approved by the county court,
worth at leastdouble the amount of money coming into
his hands ,conditioned for the faithfulperformance of the
duties required of him as treasurer aforesaid under the
provisions of this act; and he shall account for and pay
over on the order of the said commissioners allmoney or
moneys coming into his hands as treasureron the expi-
ration of his term of officeor resignation of the same ,
and suitmay be brought upon hisbond by any person or
persons who have sufferedany injury by reason of any
wrongful act or acts of said treasurer .
§6 . The said commissioners shall,before entering on
theirduties,execute before said court a joint bond ,with
sureties, conditioned in like manner as that of the treas-
urer,which may be sued on in the same manner ,and they
shallbe required to make , at least once in each year , at
such times as the county court may appoint ,a settlement
or report to said court,showing in detail all their acts
and transactions,including the business and transactions
of their treasurer; and said court shall have power to
remove , and it shall be the duty of the court to remove
any or all said commissioners for neglect, misconduct ,
or incompetency , or on a petition of a majority of the
tax -payers resident in said road district.
§7 . That the provisions of chapter one hundred and
ten (110) of the General Statutes in all matters relating
to condemnation of right of way , lands for toll-houses ,
& c.,or for the material required for the construction of
said road , together with such laws as have been enacted
since the adoption of the General Statutes,shallapply to
and govern all proceedings for condemnation under this
act .
§ 8. That no land or property that has been assessed
under thisact for the purpose of constructing one turn-
pike road shall be liablefor an assessment for any other
road proposed to be builtunder the provisions of thisact;
and the property of any person lying within the bounda-
riesof the proposed road district, where the said person
!

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 847
shallresideon another existingand completed turnpike
road,shallonlybe requiredunder thisact to pay a tax
fixed at one fourth the rate paid by the other property-
owners of said road districtnot lying on any completed
turnpikeroad : Provided,however,That the propertyhere-
in fixed at such reduced taxation shallallliein one body ,
and the owner must resideupon the same .
§9 . Assessments shall become due and payable , one
half in six months ,and one half in twelve months ; but if
the same shall be placed in the hands of the sherifffor
collection, because of a refusal to pay to the commis-
sioners or the treasurer, the whole amount due in one
year shall then be due and payable from the time the
same shallbe placed in the hands of the officerfor col-
lection .
§ 10. On applicationto said court in writing of a ma-
jorityof persons residingin said road district,said court
shall make an order allowing the erection of gates and
taking of tollon said road in accordance with the pro-
visionsof the General Statutes; and all persons that are
taxed under this act for the construction of said road
shallhold stock thereinto the extent of the tax paid ; and
forthepurposesof determiningthe dividends,shouldany
arise from the gate receipts on said road , after keeping
the same in repair, the whole amount of tax paid for the
constructionof the road shall be assumed as its capital
stock: Provided,however, That if the same is made a toll
road , no person shallpass upon the same free of tolls.
§ 11. Any person holding a dower interest in lands
lying in either of said districtsshall only be held liable
to pay a tax upon the value of the lifeestate,and those
holding the fee-simple in the same property shallbe liable
for such an additionalsum as will equal,when added to
the tax paid by the person holding the dower interest,the
whole amount to be paid upon said propertyunder this
act .
§ 12.That itshallbe the duty of the clerkof said
county to preservein his officeallreports,settlements,
bonds, maps , and other papers that may be filedwith

-848 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
him as required by this act,and for his servicesshall be
paid such fees as shallbe reasonable,and may be allowed
by the court.
§ 13. Roads constructed under this act shallbe divided
into two classes ; those that are not less than one mile nor
more than fivemiles in length,and those that are not less
than fiveand not more than (10) ten milesin length; and
the first named class , those not less than one nor more
than fivemiles in length,shallnot have laidoffas a road
district, a districtwith territorymore than one mile on
each sideof thelineof the proposed road,and one fourth
of a mile from the termini thereof ; the second class,
those of not less than five nor more than ten miles in
length, shall be alone entitledto a territoryof two miles
on each side of the proposed line of said road , and one
half mile from the termini thereof as a road district set
out in the first section of this act .
§14 . The court shall fix the compensation of the treas-
urer,which shallnot exceed two dollarsper day for each
entireday's servicerendered by him under thisact.
§ 15. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and after itspassage .
Approved April 22, 1882 .
