CHAPTER 1296 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Nepton , Ewing Station and Helena Turnpike
Road Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That a company ishereby created,under the name
of the Nepton ,Ewing Station and Helena Turnpike Road
Company , for the purpose of constructing an artificial
road, upon the McAdam plan, from Nepton , Fleming
county,to a pointon what was known as the Elizaville
and Helena Turnpike , at or near McGros Springs ; then to
follow the old road -bed of what was known as the Eli-
zavilleand Helena Turnpike,to a pointon the Mayslick
and Helena Turnpike,on or near the lands of Richard

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 849
Wells, in Mason county; and from Nepton , Fleming
county ,to a point on the Elizavilleand Fairview Turn-
pike,at or near Ewing Station,Fleming county .
§ 2. The capitalstock of said company shallbe fifteenCapitalstock.
thousand dollars, to be increased or diminished as the
board of directors may determine , to be divided into
shares of fiftydollarseach .
§3 . A book for the subscription of stock in said com-
pany shall be opened at any time and place,under the
direction of any two or more of the following named
commissioners : A. G. Slicer, A. J. Allen,E. Bell,Robert
Hildreth , and Frank Lander .
§4 . The subscribersfor stock shall sign the following
obligation,which shallbe written in a book kept for such
purpose : " We , whose names are hereto subscribed,sev-
erallypromise to pay to the president and directorsof the
Nepton ,Ewing Station and Helena Turnpike Road Com-
pany the sum of fiftydollarsfor each and every share of
stock set opposite our names , in such manner and at
such times and places as shall be required by the presi-
dent and directorsof said company ," which subscriptions
may be collectablein the proper courts.
§5 . That when two thousand dollars shall have been
subscribed to the capital stock of said company ,it shall
be the duty of said commissioners , or one or more of
them ,to give notice of a meeting of the stockholders of
said company , to meet at Nepton , for the purpose of
electing officers; said notice to be put up at three or
more of the most public placeson saidproposed road at
leastfive days previous to said meeting ,at which meet-
ing at leasttwo of said commissioners shall be present,
who shall proceed to take the vote,by ballot or other-
wise, in person or by proxy,of said stockholders, each
stockholderhaving one vote for each share of stock held,
for a president and four directors, who shall hold their
officesfor one year ,and until their successors shall be
duly elected and qualified. The time and place of elec-
tionof officersafterthe firstelectionshallbe fixedby
the presidentand directors,a majorityof whom shallbe
LOC . L., VOL . II- 54

850 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
competent to do business. The presidentand directors,
before they enter on the duties of theiroffices, shalltake
an oath before some justiceof the peace that they will
faithfullydischarge the duties of the respective offices
without favor according to their best judgment . They
shallappoint a treasurer and such other officersas they
may deem necessary,who shallhold said officeor offices
until his or their successors shall be appointed ,but re-
movable by and at the discretionof the president and
directors. The treasurershallgive such bond and secur-
ity as the board of directorsmay require,the obligation
to be made payable to them , conditionedthat he will
faithfullydischarge the duties of treasurerof said com-
pany ,and thathe will,when calledon,pay the amount
of money in his hands to the order of said board , and
that he will perform the duties required of him by the
by -laws of said company .
§6 . The president and directors, when elected and
qualified, shall be a body -corporate,in fact and in law .
by the name of the "Nepton ,Ewing Station, and Helena
Turnpike Road Company ;" and shall have perpetual
succession, and allthe privilegesand franchises incident
to a corporation; and shall be capable of taking and
holding their said capital stock, and the increase and
profitsthereof; and of purchasing , taking,and holding ,
to them and their successors and assigns,and of selling,
transferring,and conveying, in fee-simple or otherwise,
allsuch lands,tenements , and estate,real or personal, as
shall be necessary to them in the prosecution of their
work ; and to sue and be sued ,plead and be impleaded ,
defend and be defended , in any of the courts of this
Commonwealth , or any other place whatever ; and to do
allor every other thing which a body -politicor corporate
may do .
§7. The saidcompany shallhave power to locateand
lay out said road ,receive releasesin writing of any per-
son or personswillingto grant the rightof way forsaid
turnpike,the use of rock and timber forthe same without
charge ,which being so given shall be binding to all in-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 851
tentsand purposes; and may take the steps authorized
by the generallaw of the Stateto condemn theland over
which said road is to run ,and stone and other materials
from adjacent lands.
§ 8. The company shall have power and authority, in
the construction of said road , to fix the width and man-
ner of construction,its grade , and whatever may apper-
tain to such construction: Provided, however, The width
of said road shallnot be less than thirtyfeet,except in
heavy cuts, and the width of metal not lessthan twelve
feet.
§ 9. The company shallhave power to contractforthe
making of any part of said road when the amount of
stock subscribed shall be sufficient to make three miles
of said road ; may designate the place for the toll-gates,
and charge lawful tollson allcompleted road .
§ 10. No person but a stockholder shallhold any office
in thiscompany . Vacancies shallbe filledby the remain-
ing portion of the board by appointment untilthe regular
annual election .
§ 11. If any stockholdershallneglector refuseto pay
his proportion of stock for thirtydays after the time set
forthe payment thereof,every such stockholder,in addi-
tion,shallpay at the rate of one per cent.per month upon
the installments so called, from the time the call was
made ,from every delay of such payment ; and ifpayment
be delayed six months after the time set for payment ,
such delinquent may ,by order of the board of directors,
forfeithis share or shares to the corporation, together
with allthat may have been paid thereon; and the presi
dent,by order of the board , shall sellthe said share or
shares at public auction,having given ten days ' notice :
Provided, The same shall bring the balance due on said
share or shares: And provided, That no stockholder shall
be entitledto the rightsand privilegesof the corporation
who is a delinquent as to the requirements of the presi-
dent and directors .
§ 12. When said road from Nepton to the Mayslick and
Helena Turnpike shallbe completed,itshallbe lawful

852 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
for the company to put up one fullgate where itsofficers
may direct,and collecttollsthereat, notwithstanding said
part of the road may lack a fractionof five miles in
length; and when said road shallbe completed from
Nepton to the Elizavilleand Fairview Turnpike , it shall
be lawful forthe company to put up one half gate where
itsofficersmay direct, and collecttollsthereat, notwith-
standing said part of the road may lack a fractionof two
and one half milesin length.
§ 13. That if any person, with intentto defraud the
company , shallpass through any private bars or gate, or
along over any grounds or lands near or adjoiningthe
said road ,so as to purposely avoid paying toll,or shall
practiceany device to avoid the just payment of toll,
such person or persons thus offending shall, for every
such offense,forfeitand pay to the company the sum of
five dollars,recoverable before any justice of the peace
in like manner as other debts of equal amounts ,in the
name of the said company .
§ 14. The president shall, at the end of six months
from the completion of the road or any part thereof, and
every twelve months thereafter,make out a balance -sheet
of the accounts , assets, receipts, and disbursements of
thecompany ,forthe inspectionof the stockholders,and
make dividends of the clear profits,if any ,and pay the
same to the shareholders .
§ 15. The presidentand directorsshallrequirebond ,
with good security,from toll-gatherers for the faithfuldis-
charge of their duties .
§ 16. It shallbe lawfulforthe company to make a set
of by -laws not inconsistentwith thisact and the Consti-
tution and laws of Kentucky .
§ 17. That the private property of stockholders shall
not be liable for the debts and contracts of said com-
pany .
§ 18. That whenever the proper court of county, or
either or both of them , shall be satisfied that a sufficient
amount of stock has been subscribed in good faith by
responsiblepersonsto complete said turnpikeroad, in-
cludingthe county subscriptionhereinafterprovidedfor,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 853
or thatportion of said road lying in theirrespectivecoun-
ties,then it shall be the duty of such court to subscribe,
on behalf of their respective counties, such an amount
of stock in said road company as is now authorized by
existing laws authorizing the county courts or presiding
judges (as the case may be) of said counties to subscribe
stock in turnpike road companies , and levy the necessary
tax to pay the same ,or authorize itspayment out of any
funds that may now be on hand in either of said counties
not otherwise appropriated.
§19 . All laws relating to what was known as the
Elizavilleand Helena Turnpike Road Company are here-
by repealed .
§20 . This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 22, 1882.

CHAPTER 1303 .
AN ACT for the benefit of Louis Lee , former sheriff of Ballard county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Louis Lee ,former sheriffof Ballard county ,
be , and he is hereby , allowed the further time of two
years , from and afterthe passage of this act, to collect
and distrainfor all taxes,both revenue and county , and
fee billsdue him , and shall be entitledto all the rights
and privilegeswhich were allowed him by law in collect-
ing said taxes and fee -bills at the time they were due :
Provided ,however, Said sheriffshall be subject to all the
pains and penaltiesnow provided and imposed by law
from issuing,distrainingfor, and collectingillegaltaxes
and fee -bills.
§ 2. This act to take effectfrom and after itspassage .
Approved April 22 , 1882 .
CHAPTER 1305 .
AN ACT to authorize the Carroll county court to take stock in turnpike
roads in said county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the sheriffof Carrollcounty shall,on the fifth
day of June , 1882,open a poll-book at the several voting
placesin saidcounty,and take the senseof the qualified
voters of said county as to whether the Trimble county
court shallsubscribean amount equal to one half of the
costofconstructionof each mileofturnpikeroad hereafter
builtin saidcounty by any company now incorporated,
or that shallhereafterbe incorporated, under the laws

856 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of thisState,not to exceed ,however ,an average of eight
hundred dollars per mile to any road constructed under
this act ; said election to be conducted and held , returns
thereof made to, and the result declared by , the same
officers, under the same penalties, as now provided by
law .
§ 2. If, upon due return and comparison of the pollsof
said election, it shall appear that a majority of those
voting are in favor of said county court making the sub-
scriptionaforesaid,then it shallbe the duty of the Car-
rollcounty court, a majorityof the justicesof the peace
in said county being present,to subscribe to the capital
stock of each and every turnpike company now organ-
ized, or hereafter to be organized , in said county as
aforesaid. But no bonds of said county shallbe issued,
nor shall any part of said subscription be paid over to
such company or any person whatever,except as each
mile of turnpike is completed , or untilsaid court shallbe
fullysatisfied,in open court,that the company construct-
ing the road are fullyable to complete itwith the aid of
such subscription.
§3 . Said court shallhave the power and authority,and
they are required,to issue the bonds of said county in
such denomination and forms as said court may ,by its
orders, direct,bearing interest at a rate not exceeding
eight per cent. per annum ,to run twenty years or less, at
the court'soption.
§ 4. When a turnpikecompany has been organizedas
aforesaid,itshallbe the duty of the countyjudge of said
county , upon the request of the directors of said com .
pany , to convene the said justicesfor the purpose of
carrying out the provisions of thisact.
§5 . When the bonds hereinbefore provided for are
issued, it shall be the duty of said court to annually
levy and cause to be collected, as other county revenues-
are now collected,an ad valorem tax of not exceeding
twenty -five cents on each one hundred dollars' worth
of all the property subject to taxation in said county ,
which said amount so collected shall be used for the pur

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 857
poses of paying offthe interestand principalof said
bonds .
§6 . If a majority of the votes cast at said electionin
the entire county is against the ratificationof this act,
then the same to be the law only in such precincts as
shallshow a majority of the voters therein at said elec-
tion to be in favor thereof ; and in that event the taxes
aforesaidshall only be levied and expended in such pre-
cinctsas shallcast a majority of the votes polled for the
ratificationof this act. The sheriffshall give ten days '
notice of the time and object of said electionby printed
notices posted in at leastthree public places in the neigh-
borhood of each voting place, and by at leasttwo inser-
tions in a newspaper published in said county .
§ 7. If a majority of the voters of any precinct or pre-
cinctsin said county is against the ratificationof thisact.
the county court may ,by an order duly entered upon its
order-book , again submit itto the qualifiedvoters of such
precinct or precincts at any general election in said
county .
8. This act shalltake effectfrom its passage .
Approved April 22 , 1882 .

CHAPTER 1308 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled" An act to incorporatethe town of
Monterey , in Owen county,Kentucky ."
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That thecharterof thetown of Monterey,in Owen
county,be so amended that the limitsof said town be
extended so as to embrace and include the following
boundary , viz: To run in a northwesterly directionfrom
the present limitsof said town ,with the Owenton and
Monterey Turnpike Road . so as to include the lands of
Mrs. L. J. Calvert, on said turnpike; and thence so as
to include in said town the residence of F. H. Duvall ;
thence in a southerly direction, with her line,to the base
of the hill; thence with the meanders of the base of the
hill,so as to include the residence of J. K. Fuller, and
on to the Monterey and Byrne'sLanding Turnpike Road ;
thence with the old lines of said town around to the be-
ginning,on the Owenton and Monterey Turnpike Road
first named herein .
§2 . The trusteesof said town shall have the right to
build, construct,and maintain a work -house or place of
confinement ,in which to keep ,confine, and restrainall
personswho may hereafterbe triedand found guiltyby
or beforethe policejudge of saidtown ,forany violations
of the ordinances or by -laws of said town ; and any per-
son,except females, found guiltyof a violationof the
ordinances or by -laws of said town , and adjudged to
pay a finetherefor,shall(unlessthe same ispaid or re-
plevied), be confined in said work -house for a term of
days which , at the rate of two dollarsper day , will sat-
isfythe whole fineimposed on the person so found guilty
and adjudged to pay a fine for a violation of the ordi-
nances or by -laws of said town ; or may be compelled to
work on the streetsor other public works of said town ,
under the supervision of the marshal thereof, untilthe
tine be liquidated, at the rate of two dollarsper day ,and
may be locked up and confined in said work -house at
nightuntilhistime shallhave expired. All finesor pen-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 859
altiesassessedor imposed by or before the policejudge
of said town for a violationof the ordinances or by -laws
of said town ,shall go and inure to the benefit of said
town ,to be expended for the use and benefitof said town ,
as the board of trusteesthereof may see proper . It shall
be the duty of the said police judge to see that the pro-
visionsof thissection are strictlyenforced.
§3 . In order to enable the trustees of said town to
build and maintain a work -house , as set forth in the
foregoing section,and to build pavements , and otherwise
improve the streetsof said town , they shallhave the
rightto levy and collecta poll-tax of not exceeding $ 2.50
on and from each and every male inhabitant of said town
over the age of twenty -one years; but no such poll-tax
shallbe levied or collectedoftener than once in any one
year .
§ 4. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved April 22 , 1882 .

ical interests .

wise , to be used forthe purposes of said corporation,any

ally, on the second Monday in May , a president, vice

gether with three others to be named , shallconstitutethe

hundred thousand dollars,to be divided in shares of one

§ 9. Be it further enacted, That whenever ,in the con-
structionof said road , it shall be necessary to intersect

870 . LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
any otherestablishedroad or way ,itshallbe the duty of
said company so to construct their road across such road
or way as not to impede the passage of persons or prop-
erty along the same more than the nature of the case
may make necessary ; or where it shall be necessary to
pass throughthe lands of any person,it shallalsobe its
duty to provide for such person proper wagon -ways
across said railroad from one part of the land to another .
§ 10. Be itfurther enacted, That the General Assembly
reserves the right,at any time hereafter, or by amend-
ments to thischarter,or by general laws , to regulate the
rates fortransportationof freightand passengers on said
road , and to amend and repeal thischarter.
§ 11. Be it furtherenacted, That said corporation shall
have power to unite or consolidate its railroad to any
other railroad now constructed, or which may hereafter
be constructed, upon such terms as may be mutually
agreed upon between the companies so connecting or
consolidating; and shall have power ,from time to time ,
to borrow such sums of money as may be necessary for
the purposes of said company ,and at any rate of inter-
est not exceeding seven per cent,per annum ,and to issue
and dispose of itsbonds ,in denominations of not lessthan
one hundred dollarsfor any amount so borrowed ,and to
mortgage itscorporate property,real or personal, and its
franchises,or convey the same by deed of trustto secure
the payment of the money so borrowed ,or any other debt
of saidcompany : Provided, That saidcompany shallnot
have the power or authority to buy , lease, acquire, or
consolidate with any other company or parallelrailroad.
§ 12. Be itfurtherenacted, That said company shall
have fullpower and authorityto receivesubscriptions
to its capital stock, and donations in money , labor, or
personal property .
§ 13. This act shalltake effectand be in forcefrom and
afterthe date of itspassage .
Approved April 22 1882.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 871
CHAPTER 1315 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled " An act to reduce into one the several
acts in relationto the town of Bentou , in Marshall county," approved
March 13 , 1878 .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That section eleven of said act be so amended
that said town of Benton shall have a lien for its taxes
on all the property situated in said town , both real and
personal, which is subject to taxation for town purposes .
The lien hereby given shall attach upon deliveryto the
collector the tax -book of the town , and shall continue
untilthe taxes due thereon shallbe fullypaid . Nor shall
removal from the town limits, or a sale or assignment of
such property,dischargeor defeatthe lienso declared.
But such property shall be subject to distress,levy, and
sale for town taxes in the same manner and to the same
extent that similar property is liable to be sold for the
State revenue tax,and when so soldshallbe conveyed
to the purchaser in the same time and in the manner ,
and may be redeemed in the same time and manner as if
sold for State taxes .
§2 . The collectorshall have the powers of a sheriff,
and in the execution of his duties under this act shall be
governed by the laws prescribingthe dutiesof sheriffsin
similar cases .
§3 . This act shall take effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved April 22, 1882 .

AN ACT to amend an act, entitled " An act to amend the charter of the
Goshen and Sligo Turnpike Road Company , and for the benefitof certain
subscribers thereto ."
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That section3 of the aforesaidact,approved Feb-
ruary 18th,1882,be,and the same ishereby,repealed.

872 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1319 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled" An act to incorporatethe CentralPas--
senger Railroad Company ."
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth-
of Kentucky :
§1 . That whereas, the Central Passenger Railroad
Company has builtand operates linesof railroadsin the
county of Jeffersonand cityof Louisville,which are now
being operated forthe accommodation of passenger travel
only; therefore,for the furtherconvenience of the public,
the Central Passenger Railroad Company is hereby au-
thorized and empowered to carry freightand parcels by
horse power over itsdifferentlines,and charge a reason-
able compensation for same : Provided, That hereafter,
should the city council of Louisville desire to regulate
the rate and character of freight,and running of the cars
over said road ,itmay do so,and provided said road shall
not be compelled to carry freight.
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 22,1882 .
CHAPTER 1320 .
AN ACT to empower the court of claims of Lewis county to levy and col--
lect an ad valorem tax to build a bridge .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the court of claims of Lewis county be , and
is hereby , authorized and empowered to levy and collect
an ad valorem tax of not exceeding ten cents on each
one hundred dollars' worth of property in said county ,
said tax to be levied and collected annually untila suffi-
cient sum iscollectedto build a bridge over Kinniconick ,
at Blakenship'supper fordof saidstream.
§2 . The sheriffof said county shallcollectand pay

officialbond ,and shall have the same power to enforce
the collection of said tax as for State revenue .
§ 3. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 22, 1882..

Feland moneys due him on delinquent listsfor1876 and 1877, as late

ity of the justices concurring, be , and they are hereby ,

CHAPTER 1322 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof J. E. Ratcliff, sheriffof Pike county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That J.E. Ratcliff,sheriffof Pike county ,have the

874 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1323 .
AN ACT to enlargethe boundary of the town of Barbourville.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the corporate limitsand boundary of the town
of Barbourville be , and same is,as follows: Beginning at
the mouth of the Tan -yard branch ,where it empties into
Cumberland river; thence with the meanders of said branch
up same ,to where a straightline will strikethe branch
running into LittleRichland creek,crossingeast of J.W. S.
Hinkle's pond ; thence down same branch to LittleRich-
land creek; thence down said creek to Big Richland
creek; thence down said Big Richland creek to Cumber-
land river; thence up saidriverto the beginning .
§2 . That the bridge crossingthe Tan -yard branch east
of Barbourville shall be kept up ,one half by said town ,
and one half by the county court of Knox county ; and
the bridge across Richland creek,on the London road ,
on the leading down the river, shall be kept up by the
county court as heretofore .
§3 . That all streets and roads within the above de-
scribed boundary shall be kept in repair by said town ,
and the militia thereof are exempt from working on any
county roads .
§ 4. That the farm lands included in said boundary
shallnot be taxed for town purposes .
§ 5. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 22,1882 .
CHAPTER 1325 .
AN ACT toincorporatethe Caneyville and Fallsof Rough Turnpike Road
Company .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the persons who may subscribe stock for the
purpose of constructinga turnpikeroad from the railroad
depot in the town of Caneyville,in Grayson county ,to
the Falls of Rough creek,in the same county ,be a body

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 875
politicand corporate, under the name and styleof the
Caneyvilleand Fallsof Rough Turnpike Road Company ,
and by that name may sue and be sued,and have per-
petual succession.
§2 . Said road shall be under the control and manage-
ment of fivedirectors,one of whom shallbe chosen presi-
dent by said directors,and another of whom shall,in like
manner ,be chosen treasurer and secretary of said com-
pany . The directorsshallbe electedannually by the
stockholderson the firstMonday in March ,
§ 3. The capital stock of said road company shall be
twenty thousand dollars, or such sum as may be neces-
sary to finish the road ,to be divided into shares of fifty
dollars each ,and each stockholder shall be entitled to one
vote for each share held by him or her.
§ 4. The width of said road shallnot be lessthan thirty
feet,and the width of the macadamized part shallnot be
less than fourteen feet wide .
§5 . Whenever three miles of said road is completed ,
the company shallhave the rightto erect a toll-gate and
collecttollsfrom persons using and traveling said road ,
the tolls to be collected for the distance traveled ; and
when fivemiles are completed ,a gate shallbe established
and full tolls collected . The same tolls shall be collected
as are fixed and authorized to be collected on such roads
by the General Statutes of the State of Kentucky .
§ 6. Any grade the board of directorsreceive shallbe
legal.
§ 7. Itshallbe lawful for the president and directorsto
borrow money or obtain the same by the issue and sale of
bonds for the purpose of completing and improving said
road ,and in liquidating the debt thereon ; and they may
pledge,mortgage,or selland disposeof allor any part
of the estate,privileges,and income of said company to
secure the payment of money so obtained : Provided,The
money so obtainedshallnot exceed fiftyper cent.of the
capital stock of said road : And provided, That the inter-
est paid on the bonds so issued,or the money so obtained ,
shallnot exceed eight per cent.per annum .

876 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§8 . The rightto condemn land for road ,rock forbuild-
ing and keeping in repairsaid road ,and for toll-gate lots,
said lotsnot to exceed one acre, shallbe governed by the
provisions of chapter one hundred and ten of the General
Statutes .
§ 9. So soon as three thousand dollars shall be sub-
scribed the corporators may give notice,in writing,to the
stockholders, and proceed to organize by the election of
fivedirectors,as provided in sectiontwo of thischarter.
Before entering upon the duties of their office, said
president and directors shall take an oath before some
officerauthorized to administer oaths forthe faithfulper-
formance of their lawful duties .
§ 10.That N. B. Mahuren , W. C. McDaniel , Warren
Purcell, Wm : T. Robertson ,L. B. Beauchamp , D. S. Car-
roll, Hop . Gary , S. W. Bond , Geo . Brack, Eli Bozorth,
George Lahue ,Compton Young , and L. Green be , and
they are hereby , appointed commissioners to open books
for the subscription of stock, at such time and place as-
any three or more of said corporators may deem expedi--
ent .
§ 11. That upon the qualificationof the presidentand
directorsthey shall designate the location of said road ,
and ,at the expense of the company , survey the route of
said road ,the majority of said officersconcurring . That
after said qualification,election, and survey ,said presi-
dent and directorsshallproceed to constructsaidroad,or
to let it out for construction , under such contracts as they
may see fit. The stockholdersshallpay theirsubscrip-
tion in calls of such amounts and at such times as the
board of directorsmay orderon theirbooks.
§ 12. If any stockholdershallfailto pay the stocksub-
scribedby him ,or any part thereof,he may be excluded
from the rightof voting his shares of stock, at the option
of said president and directors; and said company may
sue forthe same in any court of thisCommonwealth hav-
ing jurisdictionthereof.
§ 13. That ifany person shallgo around or turn offof
said road with intent to avoid the payment of toll,he or

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 877
they so offending shall be fined ten dollars,to be recov-
ered by warrant before any justice of the peace ,for the
use of the road ; and said judgment shallbe collectedas
other judgments or finesare collected.
§14 . The president and directorsshall have power to
reduce the rates of toll in case of persons or families
having frequent occasion to pass through the gate, and
instead thereof may charge a reasonable annual tollor
compensation for such persons or families. All persons
making these annual contracts shall be reported to the
treasurer .
§ 15. This act to take effect from and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 22 , 1882.
CHAPTER 1326 .
AN ACT to amend an act directingthe county levy court of Jefferson
county to lay offsaidcounty,outside the corporate limitsof the cityof
Louisville, intotwelvejustices'districts,approved March 2d , 1882.

have been laid off into twelve districts, as provided in

or county court of said county shallhave no power to
alter or change said districts, nor to increase or diminish

sage .
Approved April 22, 1882 .

CHAPTER 1327 .
AN ACT for the benefit of Samuel Watts and others, trustees of a colored
school in Madison county.

of Kentucky :

son Tevis ,trusteesof a colored districtschool in Madison
county ,No. 12,shallbe permitted to erect a school-house
for the education of the colored children in their district
on a pieceof ground conveyed to them by W. C. Peyton
for that purpose , although the point where said school-
house is built is less than one mile from the Silver Creek
Academy , known as the Blythe school-house, where a

18 of the statuteapproved February 23 ,1874,as prohibits
the same , is as to them repealed .
§2 . This act to be of force from itspassage .
Approved April 22 , 1882 .
CHAPTER 1328 .
AN ACT to incorporate the Lexington Car Works Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Thomas Bradley , J. W. Appleton , Alex .
Pearson , F. M. Lowe , Joe Clark, H. H. Keller, J. E.
Keller,B. R. Hutchcraft, and J. H. Hopson ,be , and are
hereby , incorporated as a body -politic and corporate,
under the name and style of the Lexington Car Works
Company ,and under that name have perpetual succes-
sion; may sue and be sued,contract and be contracted
with ,have a common seal, and alter the same at pleas-
ure ; and may acquire and hold such real and personal
property and estate as they may deem necessary in the
prosecution of their business, and may sell and convey
the same at will .
§2 . The businessof saidcompany shallbe to erect,in
or near the city of Lexington ,suitable buildings,works ,
shops,factories, & c., for the construction and manufac

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 879
ture of railroad and other cars , and the manufacture of
railroadcars,street cars,locomotives ,flats, and all such
vehiclesand machinery and farming implements as they
may choose to manufacture .
§3 . The capitalstockof saidcompany shallconsistof
one hundred thousand dollars, which may be from time
to time increased by the board of directors to five hun-
dred thousand dollars, which stock shall be in shares of
one hundred dollars each , and shall be transferable by
the holder thereof, in person or by attorney, on the books
of the company .
§ 4. The business of said company shallbe managed
by a board of five directors, who shall elect from their
number a president,and may appoint such otherofficers
and agents as they may ,from time to time,deem neces-
sary and proper, and fix theircompensation and remove
such officersor agents atpleasure. Allvacanciesin said
board shall be filledby the remaining directors,and the
persons thus appointed shall remain in officeuntil the
next general election .
§ 5. The persons named in the firstsection of thisact,
or any three of them ,may ,at any time,open books for
the subscriptionof stockin said company , which sub-
scriptionsshall be binding upon the subscribersthereto;
and when as much as fiftythousand dollars shall have
been subscribed, said corporators shallcalla meeting of
stockholders,by ten days ' notice published in one of the
newspapers published in the city of Lexington , at which
meeting five directorsshallbe elected,by whom the busi-
ness of the company shall be managed . Said directors
shallhold theirofficefor the term of one year ; and on the
same day annually thereafter as that on which the first
election is held the regular annual election shall there-
after occur. If, for any cause , an election shall not be
held on that day ,the board of directorsshall, as soon as
practicable thereafter,designate another day for the same ,
of which due notice,as hereinbeforeprescribed,shallbe
given . At all elections or other meetings of the stock-
holders of said company , each stockholder shallbe enti

880 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
tled to one vote for each share of stock he holds on which
allcallsshallhave been paid ,which vote may be cast in
person or by written proxy .
§ 6. Said board of directorsshalladopt by -laws for the
management of theirbusiness, and may alterthe same
from time to time ; prescribe the duties and fix the com-
pensation and terms of officeof all such officersand
agents as they may appoint ,and make allrules and regu-
lations necessary in the conduct of their business,not in
conflict with the Constitutions and laws of this Common-
wealth and of the United States .
§7 . The subscriptionsto the capitalstock of said com-
pany shall be paid in in such installments,and at such
times,as the board of directorsmay prescribe; and the
subscribersto said stock shallincur no individualliability
beyond the amount of their subscription, and the private
property of the stockholders shall not be liable for the
debts of the company .
§ 8. Said company shall have power to issue and sell
its bonds ,in such form and in such amounts , payable
at such times and places as it may deem best,not ex-
ceeding , in the aggregate , sixty thousand dollars, and
bearing interest at a rate not exceeding per cent .
per annum , and may mortgage its property,rights, and
franchisesto secure the payment of said bonds ; and any
purchaser at any decretal sale made to foreclose such
mortgage shallbecome fullyinvestedwith allthe prop-
erty , rights, and franchises so sold .
§ 9. Said company may purchase, take, and hold, in
fee-simple or for any less estate,any lands necessary for
the objectsand purposes of said company ; and may
acquire the rightto lay railways, tram-ways , or other
tracks across any lands where it may be necessary to lav
the same to connect the works and principalbuildingsof
said company with the nearest existingrailroads.
10. This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved April 22, 1882 .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 881
CHAPTER 1329 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act to incorporate the town of
Pineville , in Bell county ."
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the corporatelimitsof the town of Pineville
be ,and the same is,enlarged as follows: Beginning on
the south side of Pineville, on the top of the Pine
Mountain bluff, at the present corporate line,one half
mile from the court-house ; thence southwardly down
a branch through to Mansfield to Clear creek, cross-
ing said creek opposite where John F. Haskins now
lives; thence same directionto the top of a ridge; thence
east to the top of the ridge; thence a straight line to
and crossing Cumberland river to the old Renfrow still-
house; thence northeastup the still-house branch to the
present lineof the corporation, on top of the Pine Moun-
tain,on the north side of the Cumberland river.
§ 2. This act shallbe in force from and afteritsitspas-
sage .
Approved April 22 , 1882 .
CHAPTER 1330 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Hampton College,in the city of Louisville,
Jeffersoncounty,State of Kentucky .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That L. D. Hampton , M. G. Bartlett, and M. Η .
Hampton are hereby constituteda body-corporate,by the
name of " The Hampton College," to be locatedin the
city of Louisville, Jeffersoncounty, Kentucky , and by
that name they and theirsuccessors shallhave perpetual
existence as a corporation; they shall constitutethe first
board of trustees, with power to fillvacancies as they
may occur from time to time , and to elect from their
number a president, secretary,and treasurer. The said
corporationshallhave power to sue and be sued,to con-
tractand be contracted with ,to make and use a common
LOC . L., VOL . II- 56

882 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
seal,and to alterthe same at pleasure; to purchase,take,
and hold,by gift,grant,or devise, and to dispose of any
real or personal estate .
§2 . The objectand purpose of saidcorporationishere-
by declared to be the education of daughters by a regular
collegiatecourse of instruction.
§3 . The capital stock of said college shall be not less
than twenty -fivethousand dollarsnor more than seventy-
five thousand dollars ,divided into shares of one hundred
dollars each , to be paid in as follows: twenty per cent.
in cash,the balance in such installmentsas may be fixed
by the trustees.
§4 . In the electionof trustees,and in any transaction
requiringthe actionof stockholders,the holdersof stock
shall be entitled to one vote for each share of stock , and
a majority of stock shallbe required to constitutea quo-
rum , and a majority of votes cast shallelector decide.
§ 5. The corporation shallhave power to borrow money ,
issue and sellbonds , and secure the same by mortgage or
deed of trust : Provided , That at no time shall the sums
borrowed ,or for which bonds are executed , exceed one
half of the capitalstock subscribed.
§ 6. That said college shallbe entitledto allthe rights,
privileges, and immunities enjoyed by other collegesin
thisCommonwealth ; that so long as the collegebuildings
and grounds are held foreducationalpurposesthey shall
be exempt from taxation; that it shallhave power to
confer any and alldegrees that may be lawfullyconferred
by any college or university in the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , and attestthe same by a diploma under the
seal of the college,signed by the president, and attested
by the secretary.
§7 . The corporation may adopt by -laws , and make
any other rules for the management of the college,and
to carry out its object, not inconsistentwith this act,or
with the Constitution and laws of this State and the
United States .
§ 8. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
afteritspassage .
!
Approved April 22, 1882.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 883
CHAPTER 1331 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the cityof Covington.
Be itenactedbytheGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . The mayor of the cityof Covington shallbe the
head of the policeof the said city,and the councilshall
prescribesuitablefinesand penaltiesfor disobedienceof
his orders controllingthe police.
§2 . The mayor shall, within thirtydays from and after
the firstday of January next succeeding his electionand
qualification,nominate to the city council for confirma-
tion, a suitable person to act as chief of police of said
cityfor the term of four years ,and untilhis successor is
qualified; and the citycouncilshallby ordinancefixthe
salary to be paid to the chief of police, which salary
shallnot be increased or diminished within one year from
the passage of any ordinance fixingthe same .
§3 . The mayor shall, within the time prescribedin the
foregoing section , also nominate to the council for con-
firmationso many suitablepersons to serveas policemen
as the council may direct,and from and out of the per-
sonsso nominated and confirmed,themayor shallappoint
two persons to serve as lieutenants of police, and the
council shallfixthe salary to be paid the lieutenantsand
policemen , which salary shall not be increased or dimin-
ished within one year from the time of fixing the same .
§ 4. No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof chief of
police unless he be at least twenty -four years old ; he
must be of good moral character, sober and sagacious ,
and must have been a citizenand residentof Covington
for at leastthree years prior to his appointment .
§ 5. The chief of police,lieutenants,and policemen so
nominated and confirmed shallbe commissioned by the
mayor ,which commission shallbe a warrant of authority.
§6 . The chief of police shallnot,directlynor indirectly,
purchase ,nor in any way obtain control of,the claims of
any policeman for his pay or any part thereof. If he do
so the mayor shallat once dismiss him from office.

884 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 7. The council shallprescribe the uniform to be worn
by the policemen , and shall prescribe the duties of the
chief of police and the policemen . It shall be the duty
of the mayor and chief of police to prescribe rules gov-
erning the force,which rules shallbe reported to council.
The entirepolice force shallbe under the directionof the
chiefof police,who shallactunderordersofthemayor .
§ 8. The mayor may dismiss,or authorize the chief of
police to dismiss,any member of the police force for in-
termeddling with or taking part in elections furtherthan
to vote; for intentionallyfailingto perform any of his
duties as a policeman ; for wanton breaches of law or
ordinance , or for any cause which he may deem suffi-
cient. The mayor or chief of police may suspend , for a
period not exceeding ten days , any member of the force,
for good cause , which suspension shallcause loss of pay
for the period of suspension . A suspension for a period
of more than ten days of any member of the force by
the mayor or chief of police shallbe reported to council,
with the reasons therefor .
§ 9. It shallbe the duty of the chief of police,in per-
son or by some officerof the force,to attend the meet-
ings of the city council, and execute the orders of the
same . He shall summon the members of council to
attend the sessions thereof when required by the presi-
dent of council,or by any four members of council call-
ing a special meeting of that body in the absence of the
president,and he shallreceive no fees therefor.
§ 10. From and afterthe firstday of January, 1883,
the officeof marshal of the city of Covington shall be
abolished,and the duties of said officein relationto the
executionof processes, & c., from the mayor's court of
Covington ,except for the collectionof fines, which shall
be collectedby the chief of police,shallbe performed by
the sheriffof Kenton county,who shallreceivethe same
fees thereforas for likeservices performed by him in the
circuit court .
§ 11. The city council of the city of Covington shall
have power ,by resolutionand by a vote of three fourths

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 885
of the members -elect thereof, including the president,to
cause the improvement or repair of the sidewalks of any
street,lane, alley, market -space ,or public square within
said city, afterhaving given the owner or owners of the
property abutting thereon a copy of said resolution,for
not lessthan ten days ; and in case of refusal or failure
of said owner or owners to have said work done in the
manner and within the time prescribed by said resolution,
said citymay have said work done at the cost and ex-
pense of said owner or owners in front of whose lot or
lotssaid improvement or repairs shall have been made ,
which costs and expense shallattach and existin favor of
said cityfrom the time of the passage of said resolution
untilpaid,which saidlienmay be enforcedby saidcity
in the Kenton chancery court whenever the price of such
improvement or repairs shall exceed ten dollars. Upon
the completion of said improvements or repairs, the cost
and expense shallbe ascertained,and apportionedupon
and among the owners of the lotsand partsof lotsfront-
ing or abutting the same , according to the amount of
work done in front of the respectivelotsor parts of lots.
The service of the notice of the passage of the resolu-

CHAPTER 1338 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Covington ElectricLight Company .

of Kentucky :
§1 . That Horace W. Woodruff , William B. Moore ,jr.,
Wm . H. Glore , and their associates and successors and
assigns, shallbe ,and are hereby created,a body -politic
and corporate by the name of the Covington Electric
Light Company ,with power ,and in that name ,to con-
tractand be contracted with,to sue and be sued ,to plead
and be impleaded ,to answer and defend in all courts
and places whatsoever , as a natural person ; and to have
and use a common seal, and to change , renew ,or alter
the same at pleasure; and to ordain and put in execu-
tion such by -laws ,rules and regulations,for the govern-
ment of itsaffairsas it may deem expedient and proper ,
but the same may be altered or amended at any regular
or calledmeeting by a majority vote of the paid-up ,
after a motion to that end shall have been entered at
any regular or called meeting held not less than thirty
days before: Provided,however, That such by -laws ,rules
and regulations,shall not be inconsistentwith the Con-
stitution of this State or the United States .
§2 . That the capital stock of said company shall be
fixed by the corporation aforesaid,but shall not exceed
one hundred and fiftythousand dollars, divided into
shares of one hundred dollars each . When the sum of

890 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
five thousand dollars is subscribed and paid up , said
company shallhave authorityto organize and commence
business . The shares of said stock shall be and trans-
ferable on the books of the company , according to the
by -laws ; but the company shall hold a lien upon the
shares of any stockholderwho may be indebted to it; and
such shares shall not be transferable without the consent
of the president and directors,until such debt shall have
been paid or discharged . Each share shall entitlethe
holderto one vote,in person or by writtenproxy,in all
electionsof officersand at all meetings of said company
forany purposes .
§3 . That said company shall have the right,through
itsdirectors, to take from itsofficersor agents ,bonds for
the faithfuldischarge of duty , and for the safety and
securityof itsfunds and property.
§ 4. There shallbe elected at each annual meeting of
the stockholdersa president, a vice president,a treasurer,
and five directors,and this number of directors may ,by
a vote as hereinabove provided , be increased to nine,
who shallhold theirofficeforone year,and untiltheir
successors are elected, a majority of whom shall consti-
tute a quorum for the transaction of business. Vacan-
cies that may occur in the term of any directoror other
officermay be filledby the board . The annual election
afterthe firstone ,which may take place at any time after
organization,shallbe held at the officeof the company ,
in thecityof Covington,on the second Monday in Janu-
ary of each year . At all meetings of the directory they

§ 5. That the business of said company is limited to

said city, with light and motive power , generated by
electricity, steam , or other artificialmeans , or to the
furnishingand supplying either said light, power , or
heat, and may charge thereforsuch price and compen-
sation as may be agreed upon between said company
and the individuals or corporations contracting with it;

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 891
and in aid of its object, it may acquire and hold such
real estate as may be necessary for the location of its
works, and the carryinginto effectthe purposes of its
organization, and the body hereby incorporated shall
have the exclusive privilegeof conducting the business
above described, within and adjacent to said city, for the
term of twenty -fiveyears ,but a non -user of the privilege
of this act of incorporation for five years shall work a
forfeiture .
§ 6. Said company shallhave the privilege,subjectto
the regulations of the city authorities,to lay itspipes and
mains ; to erectitspoles, posts, and wires through and
along any streetor highway in the cityaforesaidor adja-
cent thereto as it may seem fit: Provided, It in no way
permanently obstructsthe use of the same to the public
or any individual; and itis hereby required to place and
put in repairany streetor highway through which its
line of pipes,wire, & c., may pass; but it is allowed to
temporarilyobstructfor a reasonabletime in laying its
line, and repairing the same .
§ 7. The private property of stockholders shallnot be
diable fordebts of the corporation.
§ 8. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 22, 1882.
CHAPTER 1339 .

892 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
sonal,or mixed ,to the extent and for the purpose herein--
aftermentioned . They may have and keep a common

§ 2. The authorizedcapitalof said company shallbe
three hundred thousand dollars,to be subscribed in shares
of one hundred dollars each ; and when one hundred
shares of said stock shall have been subscribed and paid
up ,the company may organize and commence business.
§ 3. The business of said company shall be the manu-
facturingand sellingof allkinds of telegraph,telephone,
electriclight, and electricalgoods ; the manufacturing ,
operating,and sellingof electricalmachinery to produce
power or lightby electricity; the lightingof houses,vil-
lages, towns , and citiesby electricityor electriclight,

erty,both real and personal ,which may be necessary for
the efficientprosecution and carrying on of said busi-
ness, and may selland dispose of the same at pleasure,.
with the rightto erect and maintain poles forthe support

and alleysof the villages,towns , and citiesof thisCom-
monwealth , and over and along the public or county
roads of this Commonwealth ,upon such terms and con-
ditionsas may be agreed upon by said corporationand
the municipal ,corporate,or other authoritieshaving charge
of same .
§ 4 : The affairsof said corporation shallbe under the
management and direction of a board of directors, con-
sistingof not less than five stockholders, one of whom
shallbe chosen presidentby said board. Said board of
directors may be increased to not more than nine in
number by the vote of the stockholders at any meeting
called for the election of directors . The said board of
directorsshallbe electedby the stockholdersat such time
as may be agreed upon at a meeting for that purpose
called, of which meeting a notice in writing shall be

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 893
given or mailed to each stockholder. Each share of
stock shall be entitled to one vote . After the first elec-
tion,the directorsshallbe elected annually , at such times
as theby -laws of the company may fix; and the direct-
ors so elected shallhold theirofficefor one year ,and until
their successors are elected and qualified .
§5 . Said board of directorsshall have the power to
make ,alter, and amend by -laws for the government of

conflict with the laws and Constitution of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , or the Constitution of the United
States. They shall have power to fix and determine
the number of officers, agents, and employes of said
company , to fix, appoint , and pay the salaries and
wages of said officers and employes , and to make
and prescribe such rules and regulations for the carry-
ing on of the business of said company as they may
deem proper ; and they may take from any and all offi-
cers, employes , and agents bonds , with security, condi-
tioned for the faithtulperformance and discharge of their
respectiveduties, and to account for allmoney and prop-
ertyof the company which may come intotheirhands .
§6 . The stock of said company shallbe transferable
only on the books of the corporation. Any person wish-
ing to retirefrom said company may do so by a saleand
transfer of his stock as herein provided ; but no such
stockholder shall have the right to demand or obtain a
divisionof the propertyof saidcompany . The company
shallhave and retain a lienupon the shares of any stock-
holder for any indebtednessof such stockholderto the
company untilsuchindebtednessisfullypaidand satisfied;
and no sale or transfer of any stock by such stockholder
shall avoid or vacate the lien of said company thereon
untilsaidindebtednessisfullydischargedand paid. The
privateor individualpropertyof the stockholders,includ-
ing the originalincorporators,shallnot be held liablefor
the debts or liabilitiesof said corporation .
§7. The saidcompany shallhave thepower to borrow
money or create indebtedness to an amount not exceed-

894 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
:
ing itsauthorized capitalstock for the purpose of carry-
ing on its business; may issue its bonds or execute
promissory notes or billsof exchange therefor, payable
at such times and places as may be determined upon by
said board of directors, and may mortgage its property
as security for the same .
§ 8. The principal officeof said company shall be at
Ludlow , Kentucky , but said company may establish
agencies and officesfor the transaction of business at
such other places,either within or without the State of
Kentucky ,as said board of directors may ,from time to
time ,determine upon .
§ 9. This act shall take effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved April 22, 1882 .

CHAPTER 1349 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the LouisvilleWater Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be the duty of the LouisvilleWater
Company to furnishwater to the public firecisternsand
public fireplugs or hydrants of the city of Louisville for
fireprotectionfree of charge .
§2 . The sinking fund of the cityof Louisville being
the owner of the stockof the LouisvilleWater Company ,
and said water company ,by virtuethereof, isthe prop-
erty of the city of Louisville, therefore the Louisville

916 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Water Company ishereby exempted from the payment
of taxes of all kinds of whatever character, State, mu-
nicipal, and special.
§ 3. This act shalltake effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 22, 1882.
CHAPTER 1351 .
AN ACT to amend the road law of Greenup county , and to repealchapter
67 of SessionActs of 1879'-80,approved March 24,1880 .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the road tax forGreenup county is hereby
fixed at ten cents on each one hundred dollars in value
of taxable property assessed for State revenue purposes .
§2 . That chapter sixty-seven of SessionActs of 1879
and 1880,entitled" An act to amend the road law of
Greenup county," approved March 24th, 1880, be, and
the same ishereby ,repealed; and sectionsfifteen,seven-
teen,and eighteen of chapter eleven hundred and thirty-
nine of Session Acts of 1871 , entitled " An act to amend
and reduce into one the several acts in relation to the
road law of Greenup county," are hereby re-enacted in
fullforceand effect,exceptas amended by the firstsec-
tionof thisact; and alllaws and partsof laws in conflict
with thisact are hereby repealed.
§3 . This act shall take effectand be in force from its
passage .
Approved April 24 , 1882.

within the limitsof said town ,and to require bonds to

policejudge shallhave jurisdictionof allviolationsof
the laws ,rules and regulations, of said town .

CHAPTER 1354 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the town of Camdenville , in Anderson
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the charter of the town of Camdenville , in
Anderson county ,be so amended as to authorize the trus-
tees of said town to levy a tax upon the taxable property
within the limitsof said corporation sufficientto build a
lock-up for the purpose of confiningthose who may be
adjudged by any proper or competent court guiltyof a
breach or violation of the town ordinances or by -laws ,
where a fineis the penalty of said breach or violation; or
guilty of a violationof the said statutelaws of thisState,
subjectingthe offenderto a fineor imprisonment in a
county jail, within the limitsof said town .
§2 . That the police judge of said town shallhave the
power to order any person offending or violating the
laws , as set out in sectionone of thisact,to be confined
at hard labor,at the rate of one dollar and fiftycents per
day ,on the streetsof saidtown ,untilthe fineimposed
for such violationis fullypaid offand discharged; and
the marshal of said town shall have the control of said

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 933
offender,with power to keep him or her confinedin the
lock-up when not at work on the streetsand pavements
of said town .
§3 . This act shall take effectand be in force from its
passage .
Approved April 24 , 1882.
CHAPTER 1355 .
AN ACT to regulatethe taking up of property found adriftor running
looseupon Licking riverin the absence of the owner .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the taker-up of boats,skiffs, canoes ,fleetsof
timber, rafts, platforms, and loose logs found afloator
adrift on the Licking river in the absence of the owner ,
shall be entitledto charge the following prices, to-wit:
For each coal or other boat of the value of one hundred
dollarsor more ,five dollars; if of the value of fiftydol-
lars or more ,two dollars; if of the value of twenty dol-
larsor more ,one dollar; if of the value of ten dollarsor
more ,fiftycents; for each skiffor canoe , fiftycents; for
each fleetof timber , fifteendollars; for each raftof not
lessthan fortylogs,fivedollars; for each platform of not
lessthan twenty logs,two dollars; tor each platform of
not less than ten logs, one dollar; for each loose log,
twenty -fivecents. The taker-up shall have a lien upon
the property taken up by him for his charges , and may ,
afterhe has advertisedthe time and place of sale in two
publicplacesin the county or countieswhere the prop-
erty may be located, by written or printed notice, for
twenty days ,giving descriptionof the property, and any
marks thereon,cause the same to be sold by the sheriff
or any constablefor cash in hand ,to pay said charges
and thecostof sale,and alsoa reasonablecompensation
to the taker-up,not exceeding two dollarsper day, for
caring for each fleetof timber; for each raftper day,
twenty -fivecents; for each boat per day , if of the value
of fiftydollarsor more , twenty-fivecents per day ; and

934 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the titleof the propertyso sold shallvest absolutelyin
the purchaser at said sale. The officermaking the sale
shall have the same fees and commissions as he is en-
titledto by law for sales of similar property where sold
under execution .
§ 2. It shallbe the duty of the officermaking the sale
to pay to the taker-up his legal charges ,after deducting
his commission ; and if there be an excess,he shall pay
said residue to the clerk of the county court,and take his
receipt therefor; and if the owner does , within one
year from the sale,appear in the county court of said
county and establish his claim to the satisfaction of the
court, the money deposited with said clerk shall be or-
dered to be paid to the owner ,and the court may compel
its payment by rule and other proper orders against the
clerk and his securities; otherwise, the money shall be
paid by the clerk to the common school commissioners of
the county , and shall constitute a part of the common
school fund of said county . For failing to perform his
duties under thisact,the officermaking the sale shall be
liableupon his officialbond .
§3 . That ifany taker-up shallconceal,change,oblit-
erate,or make any new mark upon the thingtaken up ,
or shallremove the propertyfrom the main riveror its
tributarieswhere found,with a view to preventtheowner
from findingit,he shallfor each offense be fined not less
than fiftynor more than two hundred dollars,in a pro-
ceeding by warrant in the name of the Commonwealth
against such person , and may be recovered before any
justice of the peace , police or county judge , or other
court having jurisdiction.
§ 4. If any taker-up of any such property as described
in thisactshallknowingly allow said propertytaken up
by him to get aground ,he shallnot be allowed to collect
or receiveany compensation fortaking up or caringfor
same .
§5.That any claimantof such property,ifthecounty
court should adjudge that he is not the owner thereof,shall
be entitledto an appeal to the circuitcourtdirectlyfrom
i

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 935
such judgment ,when the amount in controversy shallbe
twenty dollars or more . This act shall in nowise affect
the rightsof the Licking River Lumber and Mining Com-
pany , or in wise shall the provisions thereof apply to
said LickingRiver and Lumber Company ,nor to Pendle-
ton county .
§ 6. All laws in conflictwith the provisions of this act
are hereby repealed.
§ 7. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage ..
Approved April 24, 1882.

CHAPTER 1358 .
AN ACT to authorizeand legalizethe saleand inclosureof part of the oid
jail lot in the town of Nicholasville .
That whereas ,it is represented to us that that part of
the public grounds in the town of Nicholasville,known
as the old jail lot,has ,by the uses to which it has been
directed, become a nuisance ; and whereas , the county
court of Jessamine county , and Jessamine county ,many
years since abandoned and ceased to use said lot, and
have ,by an order of the county court of said county sit-
ting as a court of claims ,released to the said board of
trusteesof said town all the rightof said court and said
county to allthat part of said lot lying west of a linerun-
ning from the southeast corner of the Presbyterian Church ,
parallelwith West streetto main Cross street; and where-
as , the title to said lot of ground being in said board of
trustees,said board made a deed of conveyance of sixty
feet of the southern end of said lot to Ben .P. Campbell
and Lovo .R. Campbell , and fortyfeet of the northern
end of said lot to the Presbyterian Church in Nicholas-
ville(said two parcels of ground lying adjoining the lot
of Ben .P. Campbell and said church ,and being the same
partof saidlotreleasedby the saidcounty court),and re-
quired, as part of the consideration of said conveyances ,
that said purchasers would curb , pave ,grade ,and inclose
the said parcels of land so purchased by them ; now ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the said action of said board of trustees,in
sellingand conveying said ground to said purchasers, is
hereby legalized; and it shall be lawful for said pur

942 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
chasers to curb,pave , grade ,and inclose the same ; and
said deeds of conveyance , so made by said board , shall
have the same force and effect, and shall so effectually
pass the titleto said property to the said purchasers , as
if there had been an act of this Legislature specially
authorizing said board of trusteesto selland convey said
ground in the manner and form in which said sales and
conveyances were made .
§2 . This act to take effect from and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 24 , 1882.
CHAPTER 1360 .

of Kentucky :
§1 . That when the board of trustees for the town of
Carrolltonshalldeem itexpedientto open or widen, in

posed to be opened or widened . Said court shall there-
upon appoint three citizens of said town to view and
report the advantages and disadvantages that will result
from opening or widening the streetsor alleyas proposed
in the motion .
§2 . The viewers shall also report the names of the
parties in possession, and the names of the owners of
the street or alley proposed to be opened or widened .
Upon report of the viewers being filed, summons shall
issueagainst the owners and partiesin possessionto
show cause ,if any , against the motion .
§ 3. If any of the owners of said streetsor alleysare
non -residents of this State, a warning order shall be
made ,warning the non -residentsto show cause,ifany ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 943
against the motion at the next regular monthly term of
the court .
§ 4. The court may order a writ of ad quod damnum ,
directed to the sheriffof Carrollcounty , who shall sum-
mon and empanel a jury of twelve freeholders,citizens
of the town of Carrollton,to meet on the ground described
in the motion ,to assess the damages . Reasonable notice
of the time and place of the meeting of the jury shallbe
given to the owners ,if in Carroll county ,and to the par-
tiesin possession.
§ 5. The jury, after being duly sworn , shall view the
street or alley, or parts thereof sought to be opened or
widened ,and ascertainwhat willbe a just compensation
for the ground proposed to be taken ,and the additional
fencing which will be necessary to fence the same , and
the damages to the residue of the tractbeyond the pecu-
liar benefits which will be derived to such residue from
the opening or widening the streetor alleyproposed to
be opened or widened .
§ 6. If the jury shallnot be sworn on the day specified,
or one shall be sworn and disagree, and shall be dis
charged , the officershall execute the writ on such other
day as he may appoint,noticethereofbeing given to the
parties interested. If the inquest cannot be completed
in one day ,the officershalladjourn the jury from day to
day until its completion . When completed , it shall be
signed by the jurors,and returned by the officer, together
with the writ,to the clerk of the Carroll county court.
§ 7. If the county court shall,upon the reportof the
viewers ' inquest and other evidence ,determine to open
or widen such street or alley, as recommended by the
viewers ,the board of trustees shall pay or tender to the
owners of the lands thus condemned the amount of the
damages assessed, and the legal costs of the procedure
before the court shall proceed to open or widen such
streetor alley.
§ 8. Appeals may be taken from the judgment of the
county court in these cases to the Carrollcircuitcourt,in
the same manner that appeals are taken in road cases,as
provided by chapter 94 of the General Statutes.

§ 9. That the board of trustees for said town shall,
before any town election, appoint suitable persons as
officersof said election,to-wit : two judges ,a clerk,and
a sheriff. The officersof said election shall perform
similarservices,and be entitledto like pay as officersat
State elections .
Approved April 24, 1882.

CHAPTER 1362 .
AN ACT authorizingthe county of Daviess to re-fund the bonds issuedby
itto pay itssubscriptionto the capitalstock of the Owensboro and Rus-
sellvilleRailroad Company .
WHEREAS , The county court of Daviess county , under
an act chartering the Owensboro and RussellvilleRail-
road Company , duly passed by the General Assembly of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky , submitted to the quali-
fiedvoters of said county the propositionwhether or not
said county court should, on behalf of said county ,sub-
scribe $ 250,000 to the capital stock of said railroadcom-
pany ; and whereas , it was determined by the vote so
taken that said county court should subscribe the said
sum to the capitalstock of said railroadcompany ; and
whereas ,said county court at its July term ,held in the
cityof Owensboro ,in the month of July,1868,did,pur-
suant to the resultof said vote,appoint Geo .W. Triplett,
W. B. Tyler, and E C. Berry a committee ,for and in
behalf of said county court,to make said subscriptionof
$250,000to the capitalstock of said railroadcompany ,
and to prepare and have issued bonds of said county to
pay off said subscriptionand to meet the callsmade by
said railroad company upon said county , which bonds
were to bear interestat the rate of six per cent. per
annum ,and to falldue as follows: $ 50,00 in five years ,
$ 50,000 in ten years,$ 75,000 in fifteenyears ,and $ 75,000
in twenty years,which bonds ,with interestcoupons ,were
issued accordingly; and whereas , the county court,in its
order appointing said committee , reserved to itselfthe
rightto call in and pay off said bonds at any time after
fiveyears from the time they issued, which time of five
years has now elapsed; now , in order to enable said

956 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
county court to callin and pay offsaid bonds, or any
part of them thatmay at any time remain unpaid,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of the county of Daviess
may ,and itis hereby authorizedto,issuebonds of said
county in any amount necessary to realize funds suffi-
cient with which to redeem allor any part of itsoutstand-
ing bonds, issuedto pay itssubscriptionto the capital
stock of the Owensboro and Russellville Railroad Com-
pany .
§2 . Said bonds shall bear interest at the rate of five
per cent. per annum , and shall have interest coupons
attachedthereto,and shallbe made payable at such times
as the said county court may by its orders fix and deter-
mine , and shallin allother respectsbe issued, signed,
and attested as said county has issued, signed, and at-
tested its outstanding bonds ,which by this act itis au-
thorized to redeem or refund .
§3 . Said county court may appoint a committee to
execute said bonds in behalf of said county court, and
may provide for their sale : Provided, That none of said
bonds shall be sold for less than their face value .
§4 . Whenever saidcounty court shalldesireto callin
all or any part of the said outstanding bonds , it shall
make a call designating what classor classes,number or
numbers ,of said bonds it desires to take in, which call
shallbe entered upon the records of the county court in
full; said callshallstatethe time when it will pay offand
take in said bonds ,and also the place, which shallbe the
place at which said bonds or their interestcoupons are
made payable ; and said callshallbe published in at least
one newspaper published in said county for at leastthirty
days beforethe day fixedin said callforthe payment of
the bonds therein designated.
§ 5. When the said county court has made a call, as
provided by thisact,itshallpay offallthe bonds called
in and presented, which bonds shallbe registeredand
burned as now required by said county court; and itmay

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 957
exchange the bonds hereby authorized to be issued for
the bonds to be redeemed , if the same shall be agreed
upon between the said county court,and itscommittee,
and the holders of said bonds : Provided, Said new bonds
shall be given and received at not less than their par
value .

court shallhave no power to use said fund for any other
purpose than hereinnamed ; and no funds obtainedunder
theprovisionsof thisactshallbe subjecttoattachment or
garnishment ,and no bonds declared illegalby the courts
of this State shall be paid out of any funds obtained

are hereby repealed.

sage .

CHAPTER 1370 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof C. Vallandigham , late sheriffof Grant county.
WHEREAS .C. Vallandigham ,latesheriffof Grant county ,
has fully settled his accounts with the Auditor for all
revenue chargeable to him forthe years 1877, 1878, 1879,
and 1880; and whereas,he has paid to the Auditor the
assessmentson property improperly listed, and also on
propertywhich was twice listed,and forwhich no credit
has been allowed him in settlements; therefore,

968 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That C. Vallandigham ,late sheriffof Grant county ,
shallbe given untilthe firstday of August ,1882,to make
out and report to the Auditor of State a fulland complete
listof allpropertyimproperlyand twice listedfor said
county upon which he has paid the revenue ; and the
Auditor isdirected to creditand refund to him allmoney
improperly collectedfrom him on property twice listedor
improperlylisted.
§2 . That C. Vallandigham ,late sheriffof Grant county .
shallhave the same right to advertiseand sellany prop-
erty or real estate upon which the tax for any of said
years is unpaid as sheriffsnow have, and to purchase
the same for the Auditor as sheriffs now do ; but before
the said Vallandigham proceeds to act under any of the
provisions of this act,he willhave at leasttwo- thirdsof
sureties on his bond to consent to his acting under the
provisions of this act, and that they willremain respon-
sible therein for any injury that any person or persons-
may sustain by said acts of Vallandigham , and to the
same extent as under the originalbond .
§3 . This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 24, 1882.

CHAPTER 1373 .
AN ACT to authorize the county court of Carroll county to build a free
bridge acrossthe Kentucky river.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Carroll county , a ma-
jorityof her justicesconcurring, is hereby authorized
and empowered to build a bridge across the Kentucky
river, so as to connect Carrollton and Prestonville at a
point near high street,in Carrollton.
§2 . Said bridge shall be so constructed that the dis-
tance between the bottom of the road -way and the river
shallnot be lessthan fiftyfeetat low water, with its
piers so located as not to obstruct the navigable channel ,

972 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and provided with sloping cut-waters on their upper
sides,so as to prevent the accumulation of draft.
§3 . In order to provide for the funds necessary to
build said bridge, the county court of Carroll county , a
majority of her justices concurring , is authorized and
empowered ,whenever her majority of the justicesthereof
shall so determine ,to issue bonds of the county of Car-
roll,not exceeding seventy-five thousand dollars,in such
amount as they may think best,saidbonds not to bear a
greater interestthan eight per cent., and payable twenty
years from date of issue; and in orderto pay the interest,
and provide a sinking fund for theirredemption, said
county courtisauthorizedto levy a tax upon the assess-
ablepropertyin saidcounty sufficientto pay the interest
as it accrues, and to pay offthe bonds within twenty
years .
§ 4. Said bridge shallbe so constructed so as to furnish
free and safe passage for vehicles and foot passengers ,
there being a double track for each , and for all purposes

condition as applies to any other public highway , save
that itshallbe kept light with suitablelightsevery night,
without regard to the regulations of the aforesaid towns
of Carrollton or Prestonville .

bridge from injury, and affixpenalties for rapid driving,
defacing, or otherwise injuring the structure, as other
public property of the county .
§ 6. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 24, 1882 .

may be paid in work on said road , subject to such regu-
lationsas the board of directorsmay establish.
§4 . When the sum of two thousand dollars shall have
been subscribed to the capital stock,itshallbe the duty
of the commissioners named above to give notice,in such
manner and at such time as they may think proper , of a
meeting of the stockholders,at such time and place as

president and five directors. One vote shallbe allowed Presidentanddi-
for each share of stock,and the president and directors
shallcontinue in officefor one year ,and untiltheirsuc-
cessors are qualified. The time and place of election,
after the firstelectionshallbe fixedby the president and
directors,and a majorityof the board shallbe competent
to do business .
rectors .
§ 5. So soon as the company is organized by an elec-
tion of officers, the president and directors shall be a
body -politic and corporate, in fact and in law , by the
name and styleof the " Mt. Vernon and Berea Turnpike Style.
Road Company ," with the privilegesand franchises of a

988 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Powers .
thiscorporation. They shallhave power to contractand

to effectthe objectforwhich said corporationwas cre-
ated .

and width of said road , and its covering with stone or
gravel; may designate the place for toll-gates; fix the
ratesof toll,and regulate and charge the same ; but such
ratesshallnot exceed those fixedby general law . They

pleted at eitherend of said road ,or at one or both ends
of the same ,to erect a gate or gates for each two miles
of said road completed,and collecttolls,and apply the
same to the completion of the road .
§ 7. It shallbe lawful for the officersand employes of
the company , with their tools and appliances,to enter
upon the lands over and contiguous to which the intended
road shallpass,having firstgiven notice to the owners or
occupants thereof; and they shall have power to take
and receive the rightof way over and through the lands
where said road may be located; and if,in any instance,
they cannot procure the right of way over and through
the lands from the owners thereof where said road shall
be located,by an agreement with the owner or owners
of such lands through which said road is to pass ,then
and in that event, the president of the road shall apply
to the county court of Rockcastle or Madison counties,
as the casemay be,fora writof ad quoddamnum to assess
the damages which may be sustained in consequence of
saidroad passingover the saidlands,and upon the pay-
ment or the tender of the damages assessed by the ver-
dict of a jury under said writ of ad quod damnum ,itshall
be lawful for the company to open and make said road
through said lands,and do allthe work necessaryto be
done in making said road; and the party or owner of
such lands as may be given damages for the right of way
for said road may take stock in said road to the amount

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 989
of the damages given him by the jury or by agreement ,
instead of the pay or money for the same .
§8 . The president and directors may appoint annu-
ally such officersas they deem necessary, with such
compensation as they may deem just and right. The
treasurer shall give bond , with good security, in such
sum as the president and directorsmay deem right and
proper,conditionedto pay over allmoneys in his hands
to the order of the board of directors .
§ 9. This act to take effectfrom and afterits passage .
Approved April 24 , 1882 .

forthe year 1883 .

CHAPTER 1395 .
AN ACT for the benefitof Nathaniel W. Deacon ,latecollectorof the State
revenue forthe county of Nelson.
WHEREAS , Nathaniel W. Deacon ,collectorof the State
revenue for the county of Nelson for the year 1881, has
departed this life without having collected all of said
revenue ,and no person having qualified as his successor,
and doubts having arisen as to authorityof his suretiesor
deputies to collectthe same ; for remedy whereof ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the sureties of said Nathaniel W. Deacon
on his bond for the collection of the State revenue for
the county of Nelson forthe year 1881,be,and they are
hereby , authorized to collectthe uncollected State taxes
for said county for the year 1881,and in doing so they
shallhave allthe power and authoritythatsheriffsmay
have in enforcingthe payment of the same ,and shallbe

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 993
responsible for all their acts and doings as sheriffsnow
