they shallhave power to appoint deputies to collectthe
same ; but before any person shallproceed to collectthe
same ,he shallexecute bond in the county court the same
as sheriffsare required to execute for the collection of the
revenue,and take the constitutionaloath,exceptthathe
may execute bond and take the oath at any time ; and
any person so qualified shall have all the powers con-
ferred,and be subjectto allthe penaltiesinflictedby law
upon sheriffs or collectors in this Commonwealth in re-
gard to the collection of the State revenue .
§ 2. That the said suretiesshallhave untilthe firstday
of October ,1882,to pay said uncollectedtaxes,and taxes
unaccounted for .
§3 . This actto take effectand be in forcefrom itspas-
sage .
Approved April 24 , 1882 .

CHAPTER 1408 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe CapitalGas and ElectricLight Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Pat. McDonald ,J. W. Pruett, L. Tobin,A.
Duvall ,J. Stoddard Johnston ,D. Glanton ,and theirasso-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1001
ciatesand successors,be ,and they are hereby ,declared a
body -corporateand politic,by and under the name and
styleof " The Capital Gas and ElectricLight Company ;"
and by such name to have perpetual succession; to con-
tractand be contracted with ,sue and be sued ,plead and
be impleaded ,in any court of law or equity of thisCom-
monwealth ; they may have a common seal, and shall
have power to change or alterthe same at pleasure.
§2 . The capitalstock of said company shallbe fifty
thousand dollars,to be increased by said company as they
may think best for the purposes under thisact; and the
stock shallbe divided into shares of fiftydollarseach .
§3 . The above named persons, and their associates,
afterthe passage of this act,may open books in the city
of Frankfortfora subscriptionof stock,and may receive
subscriptionsfrom individuals and corporations; and the
city of Frankfort,if it sees proper ,is hereby authorized
to take stock in said company .
§4 . It shallbe the duty of the company , afterthe sum
of ten thousand dollars shall have been subscribed ,to call
a meeting of the stockholders, afterfirstgiving ten days '
notice in some newspaper published in Frankfort of said
meeting; and the stockholderspresentshallproceed to
elect a president and four directors to govern and con-
duct the affairsof said company ; each officershallbe a
stockholder in saidcompany ; said officersshallhold their
officesforone year; saidofficersshallreportannuallyto
the stockholders. Preceding the election of officers,the
president shallgive ten days ' notice thereof by an adver-
tisement in a newspaper published in Frankfort .
§ 5. Said company may purchase,or in any legalman-
ner acquireand receive,and hold and use,any property
of any description,real or personal,which may be neces-
sary or proper for the erectionof such machinery ,houses ,
fixtures, or things necessary or requisiteto make and
supply the cityof Frankfort and itssurroundings with gas
or electric light.
§ 6. Said company shallhave power and authorityto
lay their pipes,of every necessary kind ,by and with the

1002 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
consent of the board of council of said city, in and
through any of the streetsof Frankfort,or alleysthereof,
or along the line of any road or thoroughfare ; and for
that purpose to take up the pavements and replace the
same ; and said company shallbe responsibleto the city
for any damage which may arise therefrom ,or any un-
reasonabledelay in replacingthe same . Said company
shallfurnishgas-lightor electriclightto any person on
such terms as the company and such person may agree
upon ; and any such contract shall be obligatory, and en-
forceablein any proper court in thisCommonwealth .
§7 . Said company shallconsistof a president and four
directors, who shall have power and authority to pass
such by -laws and rules for the government of said com-
pany and the transaction of itsbusiness as they may deem
necessary,not inconsistentwith the Constitutionand laws
of the United States or the State of Kentucky .
8. That if any person or persons shall willfully,by
any means whatever,injureor destroyany portionof the
gas-pipes,electriclights,or fixtures, lamps ,lamp -posts,
burners,or any part of the property, works , or machinery
of said company , or shallwillfullyopen a communication
into the streetor other pipes of said company ,or let on
or consume gas afterit has been turned off and stopped
by the company ,such person or persons shallbe liableto
the company for any and alldamages occasioned by such
act or acts,and shallfurthermore be subject to indictment
as fora misdemeanor ,and,upon convictionthereof,shall
be finedin any sum ,at the discretion of a jury , not ex-
ceeding one thousand dollars,or be imprisoned at hard
labor not exceeding one year ,or both so fined and im-
prisoned,at the discretionof a jury ; but thissectionshall
not be so construed as to change the law as to arson,or
willfullyburning the houses or propertyof the company .
§9 . The said board of management and control shall
have power to appoint allofficersand agents, and employ
all such workmen , upon such terms and conditions as
will operate forthe best interestsof the company .
§ 10. The said company shallfurnish gas to consumers
at a price not to exceed three dollars per thousand cubic

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1003
feet,and electriclights at such price as may be agreed
upon .
§ 11. The said Capital Gas and Electric Light Com-
pany shallhave authorityto put up lamp -posts,electric
lights, fixtures,& c., along the street mains as they are
extended . The said company is to keep the lamps in
order, furnish gas , and light and extinguish the same .
Each illuminating lamp -light shall have an illuminating
power of not less than twelve sperm candles, and the
time of burning shall be from the dawn of twilight in
the evening until the dawn of day in the morning ; and
said gas and electriclightsshall be furnished to the city
at a reasonable price per light per annum as may be
agreed upon .
§ 12. Should said cityof Frankfort,by a vote of coun-

CHAPTER 1409 .
AN ACT to authorizethe people in districtNo. 7 (Gratz district), in Owen
county , to vote a tax on their property in aid of turnpike roads in said
district .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be , and is hereby , made lawful for
thepeople of districtNo. 7 (Gratz district),in the county
of Owen , to vote on the question whether they will im-
pose a tax of not exceeding one dollar on each one hun-
dred dollars' worth of propertyin said district, and a
poll-tax of two dollars on each qualifiedvoter in said
district, for the purpose of aiding in building five turn-
pikes roads in said district, and bridges on said roads,
to-wit : One running from Springport,by way of Squires-
ville,to the Owenton and Gratz Turnpike Road , at or
near Kewessen's shop ; one from Ball'sLanding , on the
Kentucky river,up Mill creek,the nearest and most prac-
ticable route, by the way of Clay Lick, to the town of
Gratz ; one to run from the mouth of Savenu creek,at or
near the residence of S. S. Rowlett ,by way of Andrew
Wainscott's farm , to intersect the Owenton and Gratz
Turnpike Road , at or near W. B. Walken's residence;
one to run from the Owenton and Gratz Turnpike , near
Pleasant Home ,by way of John Schoolen's; and thence
down the ridge to intersect the road here mentioned
leading from Ball's Landing to Gratz , at or near the
residence of Robert Thomas ; and the other to run the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1005
nearest and most practicable route from the town of
Gratz to intersectthe road leading from mouth of Savenu
to the Owenton and Gratz Turnpike Road at W. B. Wal-
ken's. Said ad valorem and poll-tax,ifvoted for by a
majority of the qualified voters of said district, to be
assessed for three successive years .
§2 . On the firstMonday in August , 1882,there shall
be opened a poll at the town of Gratz , in said district.
and an election shall be held , which election shall be
presided over and conducted by two judges , a sheriff,
and a clerk, appointed by the Owen county court at
some regular term of said county court, between the
date of the passage of this act and the firstMonday in
August , 1882 , which said officersshall be firstsworn to
faithfullydischarge the duty of holding said election, and
making returnsthereof; and at saidelectionalllegalvoters
of saiddistrictmay vote ; and to each legal voter offering
to vote thereat,there shall be propounded the question,
" Do you vote for or against the turnpike tax ? " and his
vote shall be recorded with his name in accordance with
hisdirection; and on thenext day thereafterthe saidpoll-
book shallbe returned,properly certifiedby said officers,
to the clerkof the Owen county court; and the presiding
judge of Owen county , the clerk of the Owen county
court, and the sheriffof Owen county ,who are hereby
constituted a board for that purpose ,shall compare the
said poll-book ,and shall certifythe resultto the Owen
county court,and the said certificateshallbe entered of
record in the order -book of said court .
§ 3. If a majority of the votes cast at said election are
in favor of said tax , it shall then be the duty of the sheriff
of Owen county to collectfrom each owner of property
in said districta tax of not exceeding one dollar,as here-
inafterprovided,on each one hundred dollars' worth of
propertyin said district,which tax,when collected,shall
be paid over by him to a treasurer thereof, who shall be
appointed by the Owen county court, and who shall be
required to give sufficientbond ,suretiestherefor, by said
court,and who shallreceive no compensation as treasurer
for his services .

1006 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 4. That ifitbecomes the duty of the sheriffof Owen
county to collectsaid tax, he shallbe allowed at the rate
of six dollars for each one hundred dollars and fraction
thereof collectedby him , to be paid out of the fund so
collected; but he shallbe required by the county court of
Owen county to execute bond , with good sureties,for the
faithfulperformance of his duty to collectand properly
pay over the said taxes.
§5 . There shallbe posted at fivepublic places in said
district, ten days before said election, notices that the
said electionwillbe held, and of the rate of taxation pur-
posed to be rated on , which notices shall also show the
amount of poll-tax to be voted on , and shallbe signed by
the presidingjudge of the Owen county court,and posted
by the sheriffor his deputies; and when said election is
held,the rate of taxation,ifa majority shallvote in favor
thereof, shallbe fixedat the same rateas thatnamed in
said notices .
§6 . The taxes and money so collected, afterdeducting
sheriff'scommission for collecting,shall be appropriated
and paid forthe construction of and building and repair
of the fiveturnpike roads,and the bridges thereon,named
in the firstsection of thisact, and for no other purpose ,
and shall be divided pro rata on and among the several
roads named herein according to the number of miles
and fractionsof miles of road to be built on any of said
roads; and said tax to be collected, beginning with the
year 1882,and at the same time for said year that the
sheriffof Owen county collectsthe taxes for said year
for State revenue and other purposes .
§7 . Before the treasurerappointed by the court as pro-
vided in thisact shallpay over any part of saidmoney
to or for the use of said names , each of said roads shall
be incorporated,as now required by law , and shallelect
a president,secretary, and treasurer therefor; and then
the treasurerappointed by the county court as aforesaid
shallpay to the president of any of said roads theirpro-
portionof saidmoney . But beforepaying the same ,the
treasurerappointedby the county courtshallrequireand

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1007
take of the presidents of said roads his bond , with good
security,that he will faithfullyand properly apply the
saidmoney to the buildingand constructionof the road
of which he ispresident,and to the building of necessary
bridges thereon .
§ 8. This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 24 , 1882 .

into shares of one hundred dollars each , and the entire

collectedat the rates allowed by general law .

scribed tojustifythe beginning of said road ,the commis-

theirsuccessors are qualified.

or more than thirtyfeet; the metal or macadamized part
shallnot be lessthan sixteen feet; the steepness or grade

able to procure the rightof way or material for the con-
structionof their road , they are authorized and empow-
ered to apply to the county court for a writ of ad quod
damnum ,and have the right of way and material for the

ors to appoint one of their number treasurer, who shall

sponsibleforallmoneys of the company subjectto the

larsper mile,should they deem it in theirdiscretionnec-

porationmay begin businesswhen fivethousand dollars

Officers .
a general hotel and watering -place business, including

CHAPTER 1422 .
AN ACT toamend thecharterof the Chicago, St. Louis, and New Orleans
Railroad Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Chicago , St. Louis , and New Orleans
Railroad Company may , for the purpose of reducing its
grades or enabling itto operate itsrailroad in this State
more economically ,change its present line of railroad at
such points and for such distance as they may from time
to time deem best,not exceeding in any one place more
than two miles from itspresent located line,and shallhave

be in force from and afteritspassage .

CHAPTER 1425 .
AN ACT to amend an act,entitled" An act to provide for organizingand
establishinga system of public schoolsin Winchester," approved April
4th, 1873.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . The toll-houses on the Winchester and Brownboro ,
on the Winchester and Lexington Turnpike Roads , and
the residence of the keeper of the cemetery near Win-
chester, and the toll-house on theMt. SterlingTurnpike ,
and such other houses within one mile of said school as
may make application to the board of trusteesfor admis-
sion,and will agree to bear theirproportion of the taxes
imposed to keep up said school,be,and they are hereby,
added to the school districtdescribed in the act,entitled
" An act to provide for organizing and establishinga sys-
tem of public schools in Winchester ," approved April 4th,
1873 , and made subject to the provisions of said act.
§2 . The board of trusteesof Winchester public schools
are authorizedand empowered to levythe tax provided

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1029
forin saidact,not exceeding the amount adopted by the
people at the election held for that purpose under the
provisions of said act, and including the poll-tax now
authorized by law ; and the board of councilmen of said
city shallno longer have power to levy said tax.
§3 . Said tax so levied by said board of trustees of
Winchester public schools shallbe assessed and collected
as is now provided by law for the assessment and col-
lectionof the taxes of said cityof Winchester , with like
remedies and penalties, and at a cost not to exceed the
expense of collectingthe taxes of Winchester when said
act to provide for organizing and establishingsaid school
was approved .
§ 4. It shall be the duty of the collectorof revenue
in said cityto pay over,at the end of each month ,the
amount in his hands collected for said schools , after de-
ducting the compensation to which he may be entitledby
law ; and for a failure to do this,he may be sued on his
bond in any court of competent jurisdictionby said board
of trustees of Winchester public schools, and shall for-
feithis compensation for collectingsaid taxes during the
month of the end of which he so fails.
§ 5. It shallbe, and is hereby made , the duty of the
board of councilmen of the city of Winchester to keep
in proper and comfortable repair the building in which
the public schools of Winchester are kept , and the
grounds belonging to same ,and to provide furnitureand
fuel for same ; and if said board of councilmen shall fail
to so provide , when required to do so in writing by the
chairman of the board of trustees of the public schools
of Winchester ,then said last named board may contract
forhaving same done , and the party or partiesrepairing
said building or grounds ,or furnishingfuel or furniture
for said schools, may recover for same , of said city of
Winchester ,by action in a court of competent jurisdic-
tion, when he or they have had a written contract for
same with the board of trusteesof the publicschoolsof
Winchester .
§6. This actshallbe inforcefrom and afteritspassage.
Approved April 24, 1882.

1030 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1426 .
AN ACT toprohibitthesale, loan,or giftof spirituous, vinous, malt,and
other intoxicatingliquors within the corporate limits of the town of
Athens , in Fayette county .
Be itenactedbytheGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the sale, loan, or giftof spirituous,vinous,
malt,and otherintoxicatingliquorsin a lessquantitythan
a half barrel, within the corporate limits of the town of
Athens , in Fayette county, be, and the same ishereby ,
prohibited .
§2 . Any person who shallviolatethe provisionsof the
firstsection of this act shallbe guiltyof a misdemeanor ,
and , on conviction thereof, shall be fined fiftydollars
for each offense, recoverable before the town judge of
Athens , or by indictment of a grand jury in Fayette
county: Provided,That nothing in thisact shallprohibit
a bona fide druggist from fillingprescriptionsof regular
practicingphysicians: And provided, That thisact shall
not apply to any person who may now have license to
sellliquorsin saidtown untilsaid licenseshallexpire.
3. This act shall not take effect until it shall be
submitted to the qualified voters of Athens at a gen-
eral electionin Fayette county,and a majorityof said
votersshallvote in favor thereof; and upon its adop-
tion aforesaid,it shall be and remain in full force and
effect .
Approved April 24, 1882
CHAPTER 1427 .

cession, under the name of the Latonia Agricultural and Style.

and fiftythousand dollars, divided into shares of one

cordance with the by -laws upon thissubject to be adopted

ble personallyforany debtsor liabilitiesof the society.

1032 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§4 . The firstmeeting of the society shall be held in
the cityof Covington at such time as the person named
in the firstsections hereof, or a majority of them , shall
determine, and give due notice of by advertisementin
one or more of the public newspapers printedin said
city,at which meeting there shall be elected,after the-
acceptance of thisact of incorporation,a board of direct-
ors consistingof a president,a vice president,a treasurer,
a secretary,and fivedirectors. The regular annual elec-
tion shall be held in the city of Covington on the first
Monday in March next,and in each year thereafter, or
upon some other day in each year thereafterthatthe
board of directorsmay fix and direct. At any regular
annual meeting it may change the number of directors.
Ifno electionbe made at the appropriatetime,theboard
of directors may call a meeting of the society for that
purpose. The presidentand directorsshall hold their
officesuntil the next regular meeting of the society,and
untiltheirrespectivesuccessorsare elected. Each holder
of stock shall be entitled to one vote in the election of

upon allquestionsthat may be proposed to be voted upon
for each and every share of stock he may hold. Called
meetings may be held at any time by order of the board
of directorsupon notice published,two weeks forspecified
objects. At regularmeetings,or meetings held by ad-
journment over from regular meetings ,the power of the
society over it shall be absolute,except as controlled by
thischarter and the law of the land . At called meetings

1034 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1428 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof A. P. Boyers , of Harrison county .
WHEREAS ,A. P. Boyers captured and returned to the
Warden of the Penitentiary Ben . Renson , a convict who
escaped from the custody of Warner , Tabler & Co .; and
whereas ,said Warner , Tabler & Co. refused payment of
the reward required by law , because said convict was
received by the Warden of the Penitentiary; and where-
as, said Warden and the Penitentiary Commissioners
refused payment because said convict escaped from said
Warner ,Tabler & Co .;and said Boyers isthus denied all
remedy ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts draw his war-
rant on the Treasurer of the Commonwealth in favor of
said A. P. Boyers for the sum of sixty-six and one half
dollars for the capture and return of said convict .
§2 . This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 24 , 1882.
CHAPTER 1429 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof M. C. Rowland , latesheriffof Marshall county.
WHEREAS , M. C. Rowland was sheriff of Marshall
county in the years 1874, 1875, and 1876 ,and paid over
to the State allthe revenue for the years 1874 and 1875 ,
and by his kindness and indulgence , which caused him
to be unable to pay the revenue for the year 1876, a
judgment was obtained against the said Rowland and his
securitiesfor five thousand six hundred and fifty-three
dollars and twenty -eight cents, upon which execution
issued and levied on all the property that said Rowland
and his securities owned , and was sold under said execu-
tion,the proceeds of which was applied to the payment of
said judgment ,leaving due and unpaid on said judgment
about two thousand dollars; and whereas ,by an act of
the Legislature the securities of said Rowland have been

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1035
released; and whereas ,the said M. C. Rowland has noth-
ing out of which the State can get anything more ,and
his health being so extremely bad that there is no proba-
bilityof hiseveraccumulatingany propertyinthe future;
therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the said M. C. Rowland be ,and he is hereby ,
released from all liabilities to the State as sheriff as afore-
said .
§ 2. This act to take effectand be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved April 24, 1882 .
CHAPTER 1430 .
AN ACT forthe benefitSamuel P. Thomas ,sheriffof Hancock county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyoftheCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That further time until the firstday of October ,
1882 ,be extended and given to Samuel P. Thomas ,sheriff
of the county of Hancock , to collect and pay into the
Treasury of the State the revenue due from said county
for the year 1881: Provided, The suretiesof the said
sheriff on his revenue bond for said year shall first
appear in the Hancock county court and give their
consent and agreement to the indulgence and extension
given said sheriffby this act; and same shall be noted
of recordin said court,and a certifiedcopy thereoffor-
warded to the Auditor by the clerk of said court.
§2 . That no suitor proceedings against said sheriffand
his suretiesforthe payment or collectionof the revenues
from said county for the year 1881 shall be instituted
untilafterthe firstday of October , 1882 .
§ 3. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 24, 1882.

1036 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1431 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof John N. Menifee, of Lincoln county .
WHEREAS , Sarah Jane Stapletonwas ,by a judgment of
the Lincoln circuitcourt held in Stanford, at itsApril
term , 1881,decared an idiot,and made a State charge at
seventy-fivedollarsper annum ,and at the October term
of said court an allowance of thirty-seven dollars and
fiftycents was made by said court, and an order entered
directingthe Auditor to draw his warrant on the Treas-
urer for the above sum of thirty-seven dollars and fifty
cents in favor of John N. Menifee , to whom said claim
had been assigned; said claim was presented to the Aud-
itor, who refused to issue his warrant for the payment of
same ,from the fact that the age of the idiotwas not set
out in the judgment declaring her an idiot; therefore,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of theCommonwealt h
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be ,and he is
hereby ,ordered and directed to draw his warrant on the
Treasurer for the sum of thirty-seven dollars and fifty
cents ($37.50) in favor of John N. Menifee , sheriffof Lin-
coln county ,on account of Sarah Jane Stapleton, a pau-
per idiot,and the Treasurerishereby orderedto pay the
same out of any unexpended funds in the Treasury .
§2 . This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 24 , 1882..
CHAPTER 1432 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Nashville, Chattanooga and St. Louis Railroad
Company .
WHEREAS , The Nashville, Chattanooga and St. Louis
Railroad,duly incorporatedunder the laws of the State
of Tennessee ,has acquired by purchase the old Hickman
and Obion RailroadCompany ,which company had been
incorporatedunder the laws of Kentucky ,and saidHick-
man and Obion Railroad is now being operated through
Fulton county , running to the town of Hickman , Ken

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1037
tucky,by thesaid Nashville,Chattanooga and St.Louis
Railroad ; now ,therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the said Nashville,Chattanooga and St. Louis
Railroad Company be,and the same is hereby,incorpo-
rated under the laws of this State , and is clothed with
all the right, privileges, and powers ,and subject to like
restrictions as are embraced in the charters of the Hick-
man and Obion Railroad Company in Kentucky ,and the
Nashville , Chattanooga and St. Louis Railroad Company
in Tennessee : and shall have the power to extend the
track down the streetsof the city of Hickman , by and
with the consent of the citycouncil of said city of Hick-
man .
Approved April 24 , 1882 .

ney ,or by the company , the court shallforthwith order a
jury to be empaneled to trythe questionofcompensation
and damages , unless,for good cause shown ,time is given

CHAPTER 1438 .
AN ACT to prevent the sale, gift, or furnishing intoxicatingliquors, ale,
or beer within common schooldistrictNo. 24,in Hickman county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe unlawful to sell, give,or furnish,
in any way or quantity, any intoxicating liquors, or ale
or beer, of any kind , to any person , within common
school districtNo. 24,in Hickman county , and any per-
son so offending shall be fined not less than twenty dol-
lars nor more than sixty dollars for each offense, to be
recoveredby indictmentby the grand jury,or by warrant
before the countyjudge or any justiceof the peace for
said county .
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage,and all
laws in conflictwith this act are hereby repealed.
Approved April 24, 1882.

CHAPTER 1439 .
AN ACT to amend the revisedcharter of the cityof Paducah ,Kentucky ,
February 11th, 1871,and an act amendatory thereto, approved February
26th, 1878 .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the revised charter of the city of Paducah ,
Kentucky ,approved February 11th,1871,and the amend-
atoryact thereto,approved February 26th,1878,be,and
the same is hereby,so amended that the officeof city
marshal of Paducah is abolished, to take effectat the
expiration of the present term of said citymarshal .
§ 2. That hereafter the city council shall have the
power to electan assistantchiefof police,whose duties
of officeshallbe the same as heretoforeprescribedby
law for citymarshal ,and who shall be under the same
rules and regulations as to competency and qualifications
as now prescribedby the citycharter. That said city
councilshallhave the power to regulateby ordinance
the dutiesof said assistantchiefof police; and for his
compensation the citycouncil shall, in additionto the
fees now allowed by section eight of an act to amend
the revisedcharterof the cityof Paducah ,approved Feb-
ruary 26th,1878,have the power to allow said assistant
chiefofpolicea sum not to exceed fiftydollarsper month ,
to be paid out of the citytreasuryin such manner as the
councilshallprescribeby ordinance.
§3 . That thecitycouncilshallhave the furtherpower ,
by a two -thirdsvote of allthe members composing the
entirecouncilthat the cityisentitledto electunder its
presentcharter,forgood cause,to remove said assistant
chiefof police,and allthe powers heretoforegiven by
the revised charter to the citymarshal shall be vested in
said assistantchief of police. Said assistantchief of
police shallbe electedfor the term of two years, and
qualifyat the same time,and executebond the same as
now prescribedby the charterand ordinancesforcouncil
clerk .
§4 . Be itfurtherenacted, That the city council shall
have the power to allow the mayor of the cityof Padu

1054 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
cah a salary of not less than fivehundred nor more than
twelve hundred dollarsper annum ,to be paid out of the
citytreasuryas the councilmay prescribeby ordinance ;
and allacts or parts of acts in conflictwith this section
are hereby repealed.
§5 . Be itfurtherenacted,That the citycouncil shall have
thepower to allow the cityattorneyof Paducah ,in addi-
tion to the thirtyper cent. now allowed by the charter , a
sum not to exceed four hundred dollars per annum , to be
paid out of the citytreasury in such manner as the coun-
cilmay prescribeby ordinance ; and allacts and parts of
acts in conflictwith thisact are hereby repealed.
§ 6. Be it further enacted, That the city council shall
have the power , and in itsdiscretion,if itsees proper , to
allow the council clerk of the city of Paducah a salary
not to exceed the sum of one thousand dollarsper annum ,
to be paid out of the citytreasury in such manner as the
council may by ordinance prescribe; and when said sal-
ary is allowed ,the same shallbe in lieu of all fees,per-
quisites,and allowances as now prescribed by the revised
charter and amendatory acts thereto; and all acts and
parts of acts in conflictwith thisact are hereby repealed .
§7 . Be it further enacted, That all ordinances under
thisact shallbe passed and carriedby a majorityof all
the members composing the entirecouncil the cityisenti-
tled to elect under its charter; and it shall require the
same number of votes to elect said assistant chief of
police as itdoes to pass an ordinance under thisact.
§ 8. This act to take effectfrom and after itspassage .
Approved April 24, 1882 .

CHAPTER 1444 .

magisterialdistrictin Carrollcounty in which the town of Carrolltonis

of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe unlawful for any person to sellany
spirituous,vinous , or malt liquors within the bounds of
the magisterial districtin Carroll county in which the
town of Carrollton is situated; and any person who sells
any such liquorswithin the same shall,upon conviction,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1069
be fined the sum of not less than twenty -five nor more
than one hundred dollars for each offense .
§2 . The provisions of thisact shall not apply to any
manufacturer or wholesale dealer who ,in good faithand
in the usual course of trade, sellsby wholesale , nor to
persons sellingunder license heretoforegranted tillsuch
licenseexpires,nor to druggistswho sellfor medicinal
purposes on a prescriptionmade and signedby a regular
practicingphysician; but no physicianshallmake or sign
any such prescription except the person for whom it is
made is actuallysick, and such liquor is absolutely re-
quired as a medicine ; and any physician who makes or
signs any prescriptionfor such liquors, except as pro-
vided for in this act,shall be guilty of a violationof this
act,and ,on conviction, fined twenty -fivedollarsfor each
offense .
§3 . This act shall take effect from its passage , and
remain in force only so long as the vote by which a ma-
jorityof the voters of the said town of Carrollton voted
against the sale of liquors, under the local option law ,
stands unreversed by any vote hereafter taken .

CHAPTER 1454 .
AN ACT to organize and establisha public schoolin the town of Madison-
ville, Kentucky .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That allthe territorynow embraced within the
limits of the town of Madisonville, or which may here-
after be embraced within the limits of said town , shall
be ,and is hereby ,incorporated as a school district,which
shall be under the controland management of a board of
trustees appointed by the board of councilmen of said
town as hereinafter directed .
§ 2. The board of councilmen of said town shall, at
theirfirstregular meeting afterthe adoption of this act
by the voters of said town authorized to vote as herein-
afterprovided ,and at theirfirstregular meeting in June
of each year thereafter, appoint fivesuitablemale white
persons,residentvoters of said town ,as school trustees;
and the persons so appointed shall constitute and be
styled" The Board of Trustees of the Madisonville Pub-
licSchool ;" and by that name shallbe known as a body-
corporateand politic; and may sue and be sued,contract
and be contracted with ; may buy ,sell, and convey real
and personal estate; and the titleto allschool property in
said districtshall vest in said school board and their suc-
cessors forever .

1074 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§3 . The saidtrusteesso appointed shalltake an oath
faithfullyto discharge theirduties, and shall,at any time
within one month after their appointment ,meet and or-
ganize . The chairman of the board of councilmen of
said town shall be president of said board . In case of
his absence or inabilityto act,a majority of said board
shall elect one of their number as chairman to preside
and discharge the duties of chairman pro tempore.
§ 4. In the appointment of the board of trusteesof the
Madisonvillepublic schoolit shallbe unlawful for the
board of councilmen to select more than one member of
same from any one religiousdenomination or pursua-
sion .
§ 5. The said board of the public school shall appoint
some suitable person as secretary and treasurer, who
shallhold his officefor two years,and untilhis successor
is appointed and qualified; and shallperform such duties
as generallypertain to the positionof secretaryand
treasurer,and allsuch duties as may be prescribedby
the said school board
§ 6. All vacancies in said school board shall be imme-
diatelyfilledby the board of councilmen of said town by
the appointment of some person with the qualifications
as hereinbefore prescribed; but no person shall, at the
same time , be a member of the board of councilmen and
a member of said school board , except the chairman ..
The removal of a member from said town shall vacate
his position.
§7 . The controland management of the publicschool
of the town of Madisonville,and the propertyand funds
belonging thereto, or which may accrue in any way to it,
or for its establishment, management , or maintenance
under this act or otherwise , shall be vested in the said
school board and their successors in office.
§8 . The said board of trustees shall have power to
make all by -laws and rules for the government of them-
selvesor theirappointees,and for the dispatchof busi-
ness,and for the management ,control,and government
of the school,and to enforce such by -laws and rules by

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1075
suitablefinesand penalties,to be recoveredin any court
having jurisdictionof the amount . They shallappoint
allteachersand otherpersonsnecessaryto carryon said
school,and prescribeallrulesfortheirgovernment ,and
fix their compensation or salaries, and may dismiss or

prescribe the branches of education to be taught in the
school, grade the classes, and prescribe the necessary
qualificationsfor,and the mode of examination of,pupils
or persons applying for admission to any class.
§9 . A majority of the members of said board shallcon-
stitutea quorum for the transaction of business. They
shallmeet at leastonce in every month , and shallfixby
by -law a regular meeting once in each month ,and may
meet as often as to them may seem necessary for the
transaction of business. A majority of those present
may transact any business except in the making of a
contract, for the purchase or sale of real estate,for the
erection of any building, or for the paying out of money ,
in which casesa majorityof the board must concur,and
the yeas and nays must be polled and recorded in the
journal .
§ 10. Said board shallcause to be kept a regular and
correctjournal of theirproceedings in a well-bound book ,
which at alltimes shallbe open to the inspection of any
member of said board , or any member of the board of
councilmen of said town ,or any committee appointed by
the board of councilmen of said town .
§ 11. The board of trusteesof said public school shall,
at the end of each scholasticyear ,make out and return to
the board of councilmen of said town a written or printed
statement ,in such form as may be required by the said
board of councilmen,showing the number of pupils in
said school,and the grade or class to which they belong ,
and whether the said pupils are within the legal age or
localityto entitlethem to free admission to said school,
and such other information as the board of councilmen of
said town may ,from time to time ,require. It shall be
the duty of said schoolboard of trusteesto do and per

1076 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
form any and all other acts imposed by the common
school law of this Commonwealth on the trustees of the
common schools of this State as modified herein .
§ 12. All white children between the age of six and
twenty years residingwithin the corporate limitsof said
town shallhave equal rightsof admission to the school of
said district; but the board of trustees of said public
schoolmay prescribethe termsand ratesupon which any
other white persons not embraced in the above -mentioned
class may be admitted to said public school.
§13 . No catechism or other form of religious belief
shallbe taughtor inculcatedin,nor shallany class-book
be used or adopted for said school which reflectsupon
any religiousdenomination or politicalpersuasion ; nor
shall any class be so conducted as to interferewith the
religiousor politicalbeliefof parentsor pupils.
§ 14. The secretaryand treasurer,before entering upon
his duties , shall execute a bond to the board of trustees
of the Madisonvillepublicschool,with good and ample
suretiesto be approved of by the said board of trustees,
conditioned for the faithfulperformance of allhis duties,
and to account for and pay over to the proper persons all
money which may come to his hands by virtue of his said
office. The secretary shall attend all meetings of the
board, keep theirrecordsand papers,and perform such
other duties as may be required or prescribedby the said
board of trustees . The treasurer shall receive and dis-
pense the money belonging to the school fund, see that it
isproperlycollected,keep accurateand true accounts in
well-bound books of allhisacts,and perform such other
dutiesas may be prescribedor requiredby said board of
trustees. The said secretary and treasurer shall receive
for his servicesa salary to be fixedby the said board , and
paid at such timesas they may direct.
§ 15. The commissioner for common schools of Hopkins
county shallannually make an estimateof the share or
proportionof theStatecommon schoolfundwhich would
be due to the school district of the town of Madisonville
as hereinbeforedesignated, and shallpay over to the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1077
board of trustees of the Madisonville public school the
amount due said district on said account , which fund
shallbe used only in payment of teachers in said school.
§ 16. The board of trusteesof said public school may
admit into said school white children who are non -resi-
dents of the town of Madisonville,or otherwhite pupils
who are not within the school age ,or entitledto equal
rightsof admissionas hereinbeforeprescribed,upon such
terms and conditions, and upon the payment of such
charges as to them may seem right.
§ 17. The board of councilmen for said town shall
cause to be levied and collected an annual tax at the
rate of not exceeding twenty cents upon each one hun-
dred dollars' worth of the same property owned by white
persons that istaxed by the town for town purposes ; the
assessment and collection to be made at the same time
and in the same manner as required by law for the city
revenue . This tax shall be called and known as the
school tax, and only be payable in money ,and is to be
used for the purpose of defraying the yearly expenses of
the said public school, including repairs on school build-
ings and premises: Provided,That only the propertyof
white persons shallbe taxed under thisact : And provided
further,That said tax in thissection mentioned shall not
be levied untilafterthe school building mentioned in this
charter shallbe completed and paid for.
§ 18. The board of councilmen of said town shallpro-
vide the funds forbuying the necessary grounds ,building
the school buildings; and for that purpose the board of
trusteescreated by this act, and their successors, are in-
vested with allthe property,money ,rights, and creditsof
thetrusteesof oldMadisonvilleSeminary; and the board
of trusteescreated by this act are authorized to selland
convey the said seminary house and lot situated in the
town ofMadisonville,and describedina deed from Joseph.
Fuqua to the trustees of said Madisonville Seminary ,
and apply theproceedsthereof,and any money otherwise
providedby thisact,not otherwisespecificallyappropri-
ated, to the erection of a school building or buildings in

1078 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the town of Madisonville. The said trustees may sell
said seminary house and lot publicly or privately upon
such terms as they may deem advisable, and convey to
the purchaser or purchasers the titlein fee-simple to the
same . When said board of trustees shall have effected a
sale of said seminary lot,it shallbe their duty to report
the same , together with the amount of the proceeds of
same ,to the board of councilmen of said town .
§ 19. It shall be the duty of the board of councilmen
of saidtown,and they are hereby authorized,empow-
ered,and required,to raisean amount by taxation on the
property of said town sufficientto erect a school build-
ing or buildings, and to furnish and equip same in such
manner as is authorized by thisact, and as the board of
trusteesof the public school may deem proper and right;
but said amount so authorized to be raised , when added
to the proceeds of the sale of said seminary lot,shallnot
exceed the sum of fifteen thousand dollars . Said amount
to be raised for the purposes aforesaid shall be divided
into four equal installments,and to be levied for in the
following manner , to-wit : The board of councilmen of
said town shallcause to be leviedand collectedupon
the same property in the said town as is taxed by the
State for common school purposes , and by the town for
municipal purposes , the assessment to be made at the
same time as requiredby law formunicipal purposesfor
the year 1882,a tax to pay the firstinstallmentof said
amount , and the cost of collectingthe same : Provided,
however, That no property owned by negroes shall be so
taxed ; and they shall cause to be levied in the same
manner , and upon the same terms, an amount each year
sufficientto pay each installmentas itfallsdue. Said
installmentsshallbe due on the firstday of January,
afterthe levy is made for its payment , or after it should
have been made as hereinprovided. If itshouldbecome
necessary, in order to make said three last installments
available forthe purposes mentioned in thisact,then the
board of councilmen of said town , in their discretion,
are authorizedand empowered to borrow any amount

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1079
not exceeding the amount of said three lastinstallments,
and forthat purpose may issuebonds or scrip,not less
than ten dollars nor not exceeding in denomination one
hundred dollars,running not longer than the time fixed
for the payment and collectionof said installments,and
bearing interestat any rate not exceeding six per cent.
per annum , payable at the maturity of said bonds or
scrip. The bonds or scrip in this section provided for
shall be made payable to bearer, shall be signed by the
chairman of the board of councilmen ,and attestedby the
clerk of said board of councilmen of said town ,shallpass
by delivery, and shallbe sold or discounted by the board
of councilmen of said town ,or such person as they may
authorize, forthe best price they can get, or paid out for
any building, or any other indebtedness created on ac-
count of said public school building or buildings. The
said board of councilmen shall, at the time the levy is
made to pay said installments,levy an additionalamount
to pay the intereston the installment levied for and all
cost of collecting the same .
§20 . The town assessor shall make the assessments
necessary for the fixing and collectionof all amounts
provided by thisact,under such provisions and rules as
may , from time to time, be prescribed by the board of
councilmen of said town . All said amounts as are here-
in required to be raised shallbe collectedby the marshal ,
or a collectorappointed by the board of councilmen ,by
levy,distress,or sale, in the same manner as the town
revenue is collected; and the officerfor said sums shall,
with his sureties,be responsible upon his officialrevenue
bond forthe faithfulperformance of his duties, with such
penalties as are now prescribedby law for delinquencies.
§ 21. The funds arisingfrom the levies directed to be
made by section twenty of this act, and the bonds or
scripby said section authorized to be issued,shallbe paid
and delivered into the school treasury, or the said bonds
or scripmay be delivered to the board of trusteesof said
public school,and by them used for the said building or
buildings,and the purchase of ground,eitherby cashing

1080 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
them or paying them to contractorsand othersto whom
the said board may be indebted on account of same .
§ 22. All money in the school treasuryshallbe under
the control and management of the board of trustees of
saidpublicschool,and used by them forthe solepurpose
of saidpublicschool,and purchase of propertyand erect-
ing of buildingstherefor. If any of said trusteesor the
treasurer shall, without authority of the board , use or
appropriateany of saidfundsto hisown use,he shallbe
deemed guiltyof embezzlement,and be punished as pro-
vided by the general laws of this State for the crime of
enbezzlement .
§ 23. This act shall not take effect and be in force until
ratifiedby a majorityof the legal white votersvoting
at an electionto be held in said town And for the pur-
pose of taking the sense of the qualified voters of said
town upon the ratificationof thisact, the board of coun-
cilmen shall order an election to be held at the court-
house in Madisonville, on a day to be designated , not.
sooner than fifteendays afterthe order is made . Due
notice shallbe given of said election by publication in a
newspaper in said town (if one be published) for one
insertion,and by writtenor printednoticespostedup at
not less than five public places within the town limits.
The board of councilmen shallappoint two judges and a
sheriff, residents of the town ,to hold said election,and
the clerk of the board of councilmen shall act as clerk
thereof. A poll-book shallbe prepared , in one column of
which shall be entered the names of those voting in favor
of the ratification of this act , and in the other column
shall be entered the names of those voting against the
ratification of this act . The officers of said election
shallcertifythe resultto the board of councilmen ,and if
itappears that a majority of the legal white votersvoting.
at said election are in favor of the ratificationof this act ,
itshallbe the duty of said board of councilmen ,by order
of record upon their order-book ,to declare the resultof
said electionin accordance with the return made by the
officersof said election,whereupon this act shall be in

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1081
fullforce: Provided,however,That no vote shallbe ordered
for the ratificationof this act until the present bonded
debt of said town is reduced below ten thousand dollars .
§24 . That ifthe qualifiedvoters of said town shall be
against the provisions of this act under an election held
pursuant to section twenty -three,then the question may
be re-submitted in the same manner at the expiration of
one year from the firstelection; but not more than two
elections shall be held for the purpose of taking the
sense of the voters upon the ratificationof thisact.
§25 . The expenses of the elections authorized by this-
act shall be paid by the board of councilmen of said
town .
§ 26. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 24 , 1882 .
CHAPTER 1456 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Green River and Tennessee Railway Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That M. O. Allen,R. M. Alexander,W. F. Owsley ,
W. G. Hunter , James Maxey , V. M Grinstead, A. L.
Peterman , E. G. Walker , J. J. Gatewood , B. W. Stone ,
P. H. Leslie, H. Trigg,W. H. Walker , R. B. Dehoney ,
C. W. Thompson , S C. Bell,H. C. Baker,U. L. Taylor,
J. R. Hindman , Sinclair Wheat , Jas. T. Page , W. W.
Jones ,F. D. Rigney ,Geo . S. Stone , John W. Whip ,M.
C. Saufley,J. W. Alcorn ,T. W. Varnon ,their associates,
successors, and assigns, who may become stockholders,
be,and theyare hereby,createda body-politicand corpo-
rate,by the name and styleof the Green River and Ten-
nesseeRailway Company ; and by that name shallhave
perpetual succession, and be capable of suing and being
sued,pleading and being impleaded ,defending and being
defended , in law and equity,in allcourts and elsewhere ,
in likemanner , and as fullyas natural persons may be ;
and said corporation may have and use a common seal,
and set aside and renew or alter the same at pleasure;

1082 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and by said corporate name may contract and be con-
tracted with; and by that name shall be , and are hereby ,
invested with all the powers ,privileges,immunities ,and
franchises of natural persons .
§2 . The capital stock of said company may be any
amount , not exceeding thirty dollars per mile for each
mile of railroadwhich said company shall build or own
in this State or other States , divided into shares of one
hundred dollars each , and may be subscribed for, pur-
chased , owned , and controlled by individuals in and
out of this State, and railroad companies and other cor-
porationsin Kentucky or elsewhere.
§3 . The corporators named in the firstsection, or such
of them as may act,shallconstitutethe firstboard of di-
rectors of said company , and may serve as such until
theirsuccessors are duly elected and qualified. The said
corporators, or a majority of them , shall meet at such
time and place as they may agree upon and designate,and
from their own number , or from such persons as they
may associate with themselves , choose a president, and
appoint such other officersand agents as may be nece :-
sary; they shall have power to adopt such by -laws for
the government of the company ,its officers, agents, and
employes ,in the general conduct and management of the
affairsand businessof the company ,as may be needful
and proper,and alter,amend ,or repealthe same at will.
§ 4. The directors shall have power and authority to
locate and establish a general and branch offices or
agenciesat such place or places as may ,in theirjudg-
ment ,be found necessary.
§5. That the incorporatorsshallprovideintheby -laws
for an annual electionof directors,consistingof seven in
number ,who shallbe stockholders of said company , and
fix the time and place and determine the manner of the
firstand succeeding elections of directors. The directors
succeeding the firstboard shallseverallyhold theiroffices
for one year ,and untiltheir successors are elected and
qualified. Each board of directorsmay fillany vacancy
occurring in their number , and shall choose from their

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1083
own number a president. It shallrequire a majority, in-
cluding the president, to constitute a quorum of the
board of directors for the transaction of business .
§6 . At the electionof directors, and allother meetings
of stockholders in said company , stockholders shall be
entitled to one vote for each share of stock so held ,
which may be castin person or by writtenproxy. Pro-
vision shall be made in the by -laws for annual meetings
of the stockholders , and such called meetings as are nec-
essary .
§7 . The president and directors shall have the man-
agement and control of the property,business,and affairs
of the company ; may appoint all such officers, agents,
and employes as are necessary,prescribetheirduties and
compensation , and take from any one of them bond , with
security, for the faithfulperformance of his duties.
§ 8. Itshallbe lawful forthe president and directorsof
said company to open stock-books for subscription to the
capital stock, at such times and places, and continue
them open for such length of time as may be deemed
necessary. The capital stock of said company is hereby
declared to be personal property, and may be issued, cer-
tified, transferred, and registeredin such manner , and at
such places, as may be ordered and provided by the by-
laws of said company .
§ 9. The said railway company shall be , and are here-
by,authorizedand empowered to locate,construct,build,
and complete a railroad,with such number of tracks and
linesof telegraph as said corporation may desire, and for
that purpose shallbe entitledto acquire titleto a contin-
uous line of one hundred feet in width of land , with such
additionalground as may be necessary for the use of said
railroad,from any point in Taylor , Marion , Boyle , Lin-
coln, and Pulaski,Kentucky ,thence by such route as said
company may select,to any point on the southern bound-
ary line of the State , between the western line of the
county of Allen and the eastern line of the county of
Cumberland in the said State of Kentucky ; and to con .
struct,build, and furnish said railroad with all necessary

1084 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
side-tracks,turn-outs, switches, depots, stations, and all
necessarybuildings,erections,and structuresforthe con-
venient working of said railroadand carrying on its
business; and with allthe rolling-stock of every sort and
kind , and allother machinery , implements ,and property
the company deem necessaryand proper for the proper
prosecutionof itsbusiness; and saidcompany shallhave
power to operate and maintain the same ; and for said
purposes said company , in its corporate capacity, is
hereby vestedwith allthe powers necessaryforpurchas-
ing,acquiringtitleto,holding,selling,and conveying and
transferringallreal, personal,and mixed property said
company may deem necessary to the carrying out of the
objectsof thisact,to the same extent as any natural per-
son or persons, or any other corporationmay exercise
such rightof acquisition, holding,and disposingof his,
their, or itsproperty .
§ 10. That each section of twenty miles of said railway
which may be constructed, with itsappurtenances and
property belonging thereto, shall be ,and are hereby , ex-
empted free from taxation, State,county, or municipal,
for the period of fiveyears from the completion of said
sectionsof twenty miles.
§ 11. Said corporation is hereby authorized by its
agents, surveyors, and engineers, to cause such exami-
nations and surveys of the differentroutes as are neces-
sary to determine which is most suitable for the con-
structionof said railroad, and it shallbe lawful for said
company to enter upon ,take possession of,and use all
such real estate as may be necessary forthe construction,
operation,and maintenance of said railroad,itsdepots,
side-tracks,water-stations,engine-houses,machine-shops,
and otherbuildingsand appendages, necessary for the
use of saidrailroad; and saidcorporationmay alsotake
and use any earth,timber ,gravel, stone,or other materi-
als needed for the construction, maintenance ,and repairs
of saidrailroadand itsappendages and works,and may ,
by itsauthorizedagents, agree with the owner of the
land, earth, timber , gravel, stone, or other materials

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1085
wanted for the purchase of the whole , or the use and
occupation of the real estate,the owners thereofbeing
competent to contract; but if the owners being compe-
tent refuseto contract,or any of them are femes covertor
minors or non compos,or out of the county in which the
propertywanted issituated,applicationmay be made by
the company or its agents to the county court of any
county into or through which it isproposed to construct
said railroad; such court shallappoint three commission-
ers, one of whom shallbe an engineer of said company ,
two of whom shall be residents of the county in which
the property is situated,who shall be sworn to discharge
their duties under this act justly and impartially to the
best of their skilland judgment . It shallbe the duty of
said commissioners to view the land or materials required,
and fixthe amount of compensation to which the owner
or owners may be entitled, and make out and return to
the officeof the county clerk of said county a report,in
writing, particularlydescribing the land and materials
valued , and the amount of damages , if any , assessed.
The clerk shall receive such report and filethe same ,
indorsing thereon the time of itsbeing filed,and if the
owner residesin the county ,he shall forthwith issue a
summons commanding the owner or owners of such land
or materialto appear, and show cause why the report
shallnot be confirmed ,which summons shallbe forthwith
executed by the sheriffor other officerin whose hands it
may be placed . The report shall be docketed in the
county court, and stand for trial at any term of said
court, commencing at any term not less than ten days
after service of the summons , it being understood that
the county judge may act in allcases where the county
court is mentioned in this chapter . If the owner is not
a residentof the county , the court shall,at the firstterm
afterthe reportisfiled,appointan attorneyto defend for
such owner ,who may fileexceptionsat any timenot later
than the followingterm; and upon exceptionsbeing filed
by the owner ,or his or her attorney,or by the company ,
the courtshallforthwithordera jury to be empaneled to

1086 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
trythe questionof compensation and damages ,unlessfor
good cause shown time is given for preparation. If no
exceptionbe filed, the reportshallbe confirmed. The
jury and commissioners shall, in estimating the damages
to any proprietorof land or material, afterfixinga just
compensation therefor,take into considerationthe advan-
tages accruing or which may accrue to said owner from
the constructionof the proposed railroad. Said corpora-
tion shallnot be delayed in progressing with itswork by
the proceedings required in this section, but may enter
upon and use the land and real estateneeded , and take
and use the earth , gravel , stone , timber , and other mate-
rialsnecessary for its use, as well before as after said
inquest of damages , firstnotifying the owner or owners
of the same , and causing a correct account to be kept of
the materials used to date of inquest of damages .
§ 12. That said company shallhave power to purchase
and hold any other road in this or any other State,and
may subscribe stock in or aid in building of any other
railroad in or out of this State, whenever ,in theirjudg-
ment ,it may be to the interestof the said company to
do so. Said company may , in like manner , agree on
terms for consolidating said company with any other
railroad company in or out of this State, upon such
terms or conditions, and under such style and name as
may be agreed upon ; and may lease and operate any
railroad connecting with said railroad or branches , and
upon such terms as may be agreed upon by the com-
panies so uniting, connecting or consolidating, and for
that purpose fullpower is hereby given to said company
to make and execute such contracts with any other com-
pany as will secure the object of such connectionsby
lease or otherwise .
§ 13. That the said company is hereby authorized,from
time to time ,to borrow such sum or sums of money as
may be necessaryforthe completing,equipping,furnish-
ing,operating,and maintaining theirsaid railroad,and
to issuethe bonds of said company at such rateof inter-
est,not exceeding six per centum per annum , and sell

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1087
said bonds at such discount as may be thought for the
benefit of the company ,and to mortgage theircorporate
propertyand franchises,or convey the same by deed of
trust to secure the payment of any debt contracted by
said company for the purposes aforesaid; and any such
bonds that may be sold or disposed of at a lessrate than
par shallbe as valid and binding upon said company , as
if the same were sold for the par value thereof.
§ 14. That copies from the minutes of proceedings of
the board of directors of said corporation, and of the
by -laws adopted by them when certifiedby the secretary
of the corporation under its common seal,shall be re-
ceived in all courts and elsewhere , as evidence of the
organizationof said company and of the rulesand by-
laws governing the same , and of any order made or
action taken by said board of directors, to the same
extent that the originalrecords could be used as evidence,
and no further .
§ 15. It shall be lawful for the president and directors
of said company to receive subscriptions to the capital
stock of said company payable in real estate situated in
Kentucky ,and issuea stockcertificate,and may take a
deed of conveyance in fee-simple; and the real estate
thus acquired may be held,used , enjoyed , and controlled,
or sold or conveyed by said company as by a natural per-
son .
§16 . It shall be lawful for said company to receive,
lease,purchase , or otherwise acquire and own , hold, use,
possess,enjoy, and control all such real estateand per-
sonal property,goods,chattels,and otherthings,as may
be convenient and desirable for constructing, operating,
or equipping said railroadand itsbranches and a line of
telegraph along the same ,or which may be conveyed to
itin payment of any stocksubscription,or purchased by
itin satisfactionof any debt,judgment ,or decree,and
may grant, bargain , sell,and convey the same as a nat-
tural person .
§ 17.The directorsof said company may make con-
tracts,which shallbe signed by the presidentand attested

1088 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
by the secretary, with any corporation, company , indi-
vidualor individuals,forconstructingand equipping said
railroadand branches ,or any part thereof, and pay for
the same , wholly or partially,in the stock,bonds ,or other
property of said company , in such manner and upon
such conditions as may be deemed for the best interests
of said company .
§ 18. That itshallbe lawful for the incorporate author-
itiesof any incorporatedcityor town through which said
railroad shall be located to donate or lease to said rail-
way company , as a rightof way , the right to lay a single
or double track through said cityor town , or any portion
of the same , on any streetor highway that the said rail-
road company shallselectfor that purpose .
§19 . The board of directors of said company shall
have power to establishsuch rates of tollfor the convey-
ance of persons and property on their said railroad and
its branches, not exceeding the rate charged by other
similar railroads in this State .
§20 . Said company shallbe ,and ishereby , authorized
and empowered to constructbranch roads,not exceeding
seventy-five miles in length, from the main line of their
aforesaidrailroad,or from any point or pointsthereon,
and may , under the authorityof any other State or States,
extend the said railroador branches to any point or points
in such State or States ; and in the construction , main-
taining, and operating the said branch roads in this
section authorized, the provisions, powers , rights, and
privileges granted in this act shall extend to such
branches .
§ 21. The Legislature reservesthe rightto regulate the
rate of freightand passage over said road .
§22 . That this act shall take effect and be in force from
and afteritspassage .
Approved April 24, 1882.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1089
CHAPTER 1457 .
AN ACT to charter the Owensboro , Fallsof Rough and Green River Rail-
road Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That R. S. Triplett,Daniel M. Griffith,S. M. Dean ,
M. V. Monarch ,J. D. Powers , of Daviess county ; J. F.
Bennett , of Hancock county ; Lafayette Green , Wm . J.
Dean , and R.G. Robertson ,of Grayson county ; Samuel
E. Hill,of Ohio county ; and George Woosley ,of Edmon-
son county , and their associates, successors, and assigns,
be , and they are hereby ,created and constituteda body-
politic,by the name and styleof the Owensboro , Falls of
Rough and Green River Railroad Company ; and by that
name and style, and for the purposes hereinafter men-
tioned, shall have perpetual succession; be capable in
law to contract and to be contracted with,sue and to be
sued ,to have and use a common seal,the same to alter
at pleasure ; and to have and exercise, in addition to
those hereby expresslygranted, all lawful rightsand
privilegesusual and incidentto such corporations,and
requisiteto carry out the objects and purposes of this
act .
§2 . That the corporators named in the firstsection,or
such of them as may act,shallbe ex officio, and are here-
by constituted,the directorsof said company ,and may
serve as such until their successors are duly elected and
qualified. They ,saidcorporators,or a majorityof them ,
shallmeet at such time and place as they may agree
upon and designate, and from theirown number , or from
such persons as they may associate with themselves ,
choose a president and such other officersand agents as
may be necessary. They shallhave power to adopt such
by-laws for the government of the company ,itsofficers,
agents,and employes ,in the general conduct and man-
agement of the affairsand businessof the company ,as
may be deemed needful and proper,and as may not be
repugnant to the Constitution or laws of thisCommon-
LOC . L., VOL . II- 69

1090 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
wealth . Such by -laws shall have the same force and
effectas if enacted herein ; but the same may be amended
or repealedin accordancewith the provisionsthereof.
§3 . That incase any one of saidcorporatorsshallfail
to attendthe firstmeeting at which a majorityshallas-
semble forthe purpose of organization,notice of the time
and placethereofhaving been given to him by such ma-
jority, or attending,he shallfailor refuse to subscribe the
amount of stock prescribed as a qualificationfor a di-
rector,or to comply with any of the by -laws or regula-
tionsadopted by a majorityof saidcorporators,hisplace
as a corporator and directorshallbe deemed and declared
vacant,and the same filledby some otherperson chosen
by the other corporators. Said corporatorsmay associate
with themselves such number of other persons as they
may desire, upon the terms and conditions prescribed by
them .
§ 4. To continue the successionof said company ,the
firstboard of directorsmay provide in the by -laws for an
annual electionof directors,consistingof not exceeding
nine in number ,prescribe theirqualifications,and fixthe
time and place , and determine the manner of the first
and succeeding election of directors . The directors suc-
ceeding the firstboard shallseverallyhold theiroffices
for one year , and until their successors are elected and
qualified. Each board of directorsmay fillany vacancy
occurring, and shall choose from their own number a
president. Itshallrequirea majority,includingthe pres-
ident,to constitute a quorum of the board of directors.
In the electionof directorsand at allother meetings of the
stockholders in said company ,each share of stock shall
entitlethe holder thereofto one vote,which may be cast
in person or by proxy. Provisionsshallbe made in the
by -laws for annual meetings of the stockholders,and such
called meetings as are necessary .
§ 5. The presidentand directorsshallhave the manage-
ment and controlof the property,business,and affairsof
the company ; may appoint allsuch officers,agents,and
employes as are necessary; prescribetheirqualifications,
1

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1091
duties,and compensation,and takefromany one ofthem
bond ,securityforthe faithfulperformance of hisduty.
§ 6. That the objectsand purposes of the incorpora-
tionof said company are,and itis hereby investedwith
allthe rights,franchises,and authority necessary to con-
structand operatea railwayfrom some pointon the Ohio
river,at or near Owensboro ,through Fallsof Rough ,to
some point on Green river, by such route as may be
deemed practicable.
§ 7. The capitalstock of said company shallbe two
millions of dollars, divided into shares of one hundred
dollars each ; and the same may be subscribed for and
held by individuals, railroad companies , or other cor-
porations. Itshallbe lawfulforthepresidentand direct-
ors of said company to open books for the subscription
to the capital stock, at such times and places, and con-
tinue them open for such length of time , as may be
deemed necessary. They may receivesubscriptionsto
the capital stock,payable in contracts to construct any
part of said road ,or to erect buildings,furnishmaterials,
or render services in carrying on the business of said
company . The capitalstockof said company ishereby
declaredto be personalproperty,subjectto the laws gov-
erning personal property, and free from all and every
claim of curtesy and dower . The stock shall be trans-
ferable and assignable, and the president and directors
shall prescribein the by -laws the manner and the con-
ditionsupon which itmay be transferredand assigned.
§ 8. The presidentand directorsof saidcompany may ,
at theirfirstor any subsequent meetings,determine the }
amount that may be necessary for the construction and
equipment of said road ; and ,from time to time ,sell or
disposeof the stockof said company ,or any part there-
of, to such an amount ; and whenever it shall exceed the
capital stock aforesaid,they may , with the consent of a
majority in value of the stockholders,increase the same
to a sum not exceeding four millions of dollars .
§ 9. They may prescribethe terms of subscriptionto
the capital stock,the time and manner of payment ,and

1092 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
:
the grounds of forfeitureof stock subscribed, but not
paid as required; and may forfeitthe stock of the de-
faultingstockholder or collectthe same by legal proceed-
ings .
§ 10. That saidcompany ,itsofficers,engineers,agents,
and employes may enter upon ,survey ,use, excavate , or
fillup any land which may be required for the siteof said
road or depots, warehouses, machine shops, and other
structuresand buildings necessary and convenient forthe
use of said road ; and may take and use any earth,timber ,
stone,gravel,or other material which may be useful and
necessaryforthe construction,use,orrepairsof the same ;
may cross streams and roads,and change the roads when

pany .

of Kentucky :

" Morganfield and Uniontown Gravel Road Company ,"
for the purpose of constructing and operating a gravel

thousand dollars($ 10,000),with the privilegeof increas

ing itto sixtythousand dollars,to be divided into shares

created commissionersforthe purpose of obtainingsub-
scriptionsto the capital stock of said company , any two
of whom may open books forthatpurpose,at such times
and places as they may deem best. When the sum of

newspaper published in Union county , to meet at the
court-house in Morganfield,forthe purpose of electinga

for each share of stock. The president and directors
shallcontinue in officefor one year , and until theirsuc-
cessors are elected and qualified. The elections there-
aftershallbe held on the firstMonday in May in each

thiscorporation; they shallalso have power to contract

impleaded,in any court of law or equityin thisState;

jects for which this corporation was created, including

erty to secure such loans or bonds .

pany , unless the whole amount of his stock called for

urer, secretary, gate-keepers, and such other officersas
