justicesof the peace now have , and they shall be collect-
able in the same way . From alljudgments renderedby
the policejudge ,appeals may be taken in the same man-
ner that appeals are now taken from justices' courts, and
upon alljudgments rendered by the police judge , unless
the same be paid or replevied,executionsand capiaspro
fine,or on judgments renderedby justicesof the peace,
may issue, and the defendant may be confined in the jail
of Pendletonor Bracken county,under the same restric-
tions that prisoners are now confined by law . All fines
imposed by the policejudge shallbe forthe use and ben-
efitof the trusteesof the town of Lenoxburg . All war-
rants for violationsof the by -laws and ordinances of said.

1108 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
town shall run in the name of the board of trustees of
the town of Lenoxburg , and all other warrants in the
name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky . The police
judge shallexecutebond ,with good security,to theboard
of trusteesof saidtown that he willpromptly and faith-
fullypay over allfinesand moneys coming to his hands
to whomsoever said board may direct. The policejudge
shall hold his court for the trial of civil causes on the first
Saturday in every month ,and hiscourt shallalways be
open for the trialof offenses against the criminal or pe-
nal laws of thisCommonwealth ,or against the by -laws
and ordinances of said town . In the event that the office
of policejudgeshouldfrom any causebecome vacant,it
shallbe the duty of the board of trustees to appoint a
successor, who shallbe commissioned by the Governor to
serve until the next regular election,and until his suc-
cessoriselectedand qualified. The policejudge of said
town shallbe furnishedwith allthe publicactsand doc-
uments thatjusticesof the peace are now furnishedwith.
§5 . The marshal ,before entering upon the duties of
his office,shalltake an oath before some officerauthor-
ized to administer the same that he will faithfullyand
impartiallydischarge the duties of his office,and execute
to the board of trusteesof said town a bond ,with good
and approved security,conditionedthathe willfaithfully
perform his duties as marshal ,and well and trulyaccount
for and pay over allmoneys that may be collected or re-
ceived by him as said marshal. Upon which bond like
remedies may be had as upon bonds executed by con-
stables, sheriffs,and other collectingofficers. It shallbe
the duty of the marshal to attend allcourtsheld by the
policejudge,and serveallprocess,and enforceallexecu-
tions and orders directedto him by the board of trustees,
policejudge,or othercourtsin thisCommonwealth ,and
in the same manner that constables perform likeservices,
and shall receive therefor the same compensation that
constables receive . It shall be lawful for the marshal to
execute process,enforce executions,and serve all other
orders in any part of Pendleton and Bracken counties.
i

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1109
It shall be the duty of the marshal to collectall taxes
and other dues owing to the town ,and pay the same
over on the order of the board of trustees of said town ;
he shall return his tax -books to said board of trustees on
the firstday of September in each year , and the said
board shall, at itsfirstregular meeting in September ,
make a full and final settlement with said marshal . In
the event that a vacancy should occur in the office of
marshal , the board of trusteesshall fillsaid vacancy ,by
appointment , untilthe next regular election,and until a
successor iselectedand qualified.
§ 6. The board of trusteesshallhave power to levy an
ad valorem tax, not to exceed fiftycents on each one
hundred dollars' worth of all such property within the
limits of the town of Lenoxburg as is now subject to
taxation under the present revenue laws of the State.
They shall have power to levy and collect an annual
poll-tax on each free male inhabitant, over the age of
twenty -one years,not to exceed two dollars.
§ 7. The board of trustees shall have power to tax all
shows , theatricalperformances , and exhibitionsof what-
ever name or nature ,in any sum not exceeding ten dol-
lars for each day such show , theatricalexhibition, or
public performance may exhibitwithin said town .
§ 8. The board of trustees of said town shall have
power ,by suitableordinancesor by-laws,with penalties
adequate for the purpose , not, however , to exceed fifty
dollarsfine,to suppress tippling-houses ,gambling -houses ,
and houses of ill-fame ,and to punish any one guiltyof
rioting,disturbingthe public peace ,disorderlyor indecent
conduct or language ,disturbingreligiousworship ,schools,
public speaking or lectures,or any lawful public meeting ,
injury to streets, alleys,or public grounds , shade or orna-
mental trees, cisterns, wells, pumps , churches, school-
houses , or other public buildings or property belonging
to said town , and to pass all such by -laws or ordinances
as shallbe necessaryor proper to carryout any power
given by thischarter,or for the suppression and punish-
ment of all such disordersand irregularitiesas shall be

1110 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
prejudicialto the good orderand quietof saidtown . All
ordinances and by -laws adopted by the board of trustees
shall be written out in fair legible handwriting , and
copies thereofshallbe posted in at leasttwo public
places in said town .
§9 . The board of trusteesshall annually appoint one
town assessor, who shall take in a listof allproperty in
said town taxable under thischarter,and to fixa just and
fairvalue upon allproperty listedfor taxationon the
tenth day of January in that year,which listshallbe
made on the oath of the party; or if any one refuses to
give in a listand swear to it,or failsto do so from any
cause , the list shall be made from the best information
the assessorpossesses. A faircopy of said list, arranged
in alphabeticalorder,shall be completed and returned to
the board of trusteeson the firstSaturday in June in that
year , whereof notice shall be given that said inhabitants
may examine ,or owners may examine the same ; and if
any one shall feelaggrieved thereby in excessive valua-
tion or other error,application may be made to the board
of trustees, and proof being made to theirsatisfaction,
the listmay be corrected. The board of trustees shall
pay the assessora reasonablecompensation for his ser-
vices. Said assessor,before entering upon the discharge
of his duties herein, shallexecute to the board of trustees
a good and sufficientbond ,conditionedthathe willfaith-
fullydischarge his duties, and take an oath in likeman-
ner as assessors of the State revenue . Like remedies
may be had upon the assessor'sbond as upon the bonds
of assessors of State revenue ; and he shall be further
liableto said town for a violationof his duty in any sum
not exceeding one hundred dollars for each violation,to
be ascertained in any court of competent jurisdiction.
§ 10. The board of trusteesshall appoint a clerk of
theirboard,whose dutiesand pay shallbe determined by
the board .
§11 . The board of trusteesmay employ an attorneyto
prosecute persons brought before the policejudge for
offensescommitted within the town , and may agree with

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1111
him as to his compensation . They may also employ an
attorneyto representthem in any suitin which the town
is a party .
§ 12. The board of trusteeshave power ,and are hereby
authorized to procure,by purchase or donation ,suitable
grounds , and erect thereon a station-house , in which
offenders against the by -laws and ordinances of said
town may be confined, in the same manner as offend-
ers are now confined in the jail of Pendleton county.
Said station-house shallbe under the custody and control
of the marshal of said town , who shall receive the same
fees thatjailersnow receive for similarservices.
§ 13. No spirituous, vinous, or malt liquors, or cider,
shallbe sold within the corporate limitsof said town , or
within one mile thereof .
§ 14. The board of trustees are hereby authorized and
empowered to grade ,alter,and improve the streets, alleys,
and sidewalks in said town in any manner they may deem
necessary,and to pay forthe same out of the funds aris-
ing to said town from taxes and fines herein authorized
to be collected .
§15. Allpersons livingwithin the limitsof said town
authorized to vote under the general laws of the State,
and allpersons owning real property within the limitsof
said town , shall be qualified to vote for all officersto be
elected under this charter .
§ 16. In the event that a vacancy should occur in the
board of trustees, said board may fillthe vacancy by
appointment untilthe next regular election, and until
the trusteethen elected shallqualify. The board of trus-
tees may appoint such officersand employes as they may
deem necessary to carry out the powers herein granted .
§ 17. Nothing contained in this charter shall be con-
struedso as to exempt persons residingwithinthe limits
of said town from working on public roads .
§ 18. This actshalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 24 , 1882.

1112 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1469 .
AN ACT to amend an act to incorporatethe Uniontown Mining ,Manufac
turingand TransportationCompany ,approvedFebruary 14, 1866, Ses-
sionActs 1865-'6 , pages 466-8 .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act to incorporate the Uniontown Mining ,
Manufacturing,and TransportationCompany ,approved
February 14,1866 ,Session Acts 1865- '6,pages 466-8 ,be ,
and the same ishereby ,amended as follows: 1.That John
Heath, Frank E. Heath , William P. D. Bush ,Finlay F.
Bush ,and Sidney J.Bush ,or any two or more of them,
and theirassociates,may organize saidcompany with the
same effectas contemplatedin saidact; 2. That section
eight of said act be ,and the same is hereby , amended so
as to read as follows,to-wit: Sec .8. Nothing in thischar-
ter shallbe so construed as to confer any banking privi-
leges upon said company ; and the president or secretary
thereof shallbe a resident of, and keep his officein,this
State .
§2 . That thisactshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 24,1882 .
CHAPTER 1470 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Cumberland River Improvement Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Joseph B. Lovelle, Daniel McMullin, J. H.
Mansir , and W. C. Rodes ,of Louisville,and Henry Lynn ,
of New York , George Goebel , of New York , and R. R.
Stone and G. B. Wilgus, of Lexington, Kentucky , their
associates, successors, and assignees,or such of them as
may acceptthischarter,are herebycreateda body -politic
and corporate,under the name of the Cumberland River
Improvement Company ; and by that name may sue and
be sued,contractand be contractedwith, plead and be
impleaded,and answer and defend as a naturalperson ;
and have and use a corporateseal,and change the same

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1113
at pleasure. The company ishereby authorized and em-
powered to construct and maintain two or more locks and
dams in the Cumberland river; and the firstlock and dam
shallbe located at any point in Whitley county between
Williamsburg and the Falls of the Cumberland river, or
not furtherthan sixteen miles below Williamsburg . The
said company shallerect as many locks and dams at such
points between Williamsburg and Harlan court-house ,in
Harlan county, for the purposes of establishingslack-
water navigation from the location of the firstlock to
Pineville, as may be necessary forthat end .
§ 2. Said company shallhave the right to acquire and
hold such real estatein fee-simple,lease, or donation,
upon eitheror both sidesof the Cumberland river,as it
may deem necessary for itsbusiness,and may erect build-
ings, and construct any improvements thereon which may
be to the interestof said company , includinglocksand
dams , wharves , water -powers , acqueducts , houses , ware-
houses ,depots,lock-houses , machine -shops,elevators,tan-
neries , and factories in connection with the use of the
water -power aforesaid.
§3 . Upon the organizationof said company , it may
proceed to build locks and dams as herein authorized.
The locks shall be of the same size as the one near Frank-
fort,Kentucky , on the Kentucky river,and the said locks
shall be of dimensions sufficientto answer the purposes
of slack -water navigation in said river. And the naviga-
tion thereof to be open to all water -craft and persons
desiring to navigate said river; but the said company ,in
building and maintaining said locks and dams ,shallhave
the right to control the same , and shall have all the in-
come ,revenue ,and profitsthereof,including the tollsand
water -powers ,subject to the restrictionsof this charter,
and the rightsof others to navigate the riverand pass
said locks and dams under such regulations as are pre-
scribedherein,and such as the company may make ,not
inconsistent with this charter . Said locks and dams and
theprivilegeshereingranted shallbe a propertyvesting
in the company forfiftyyears .

1114 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 4. While the rights of others to navigate said river
shallnot be prohibited,nevertheless,in considerationof
the building of said locks and dams ,the company may
charge and collect tollson all persons ,boats,crafts of
every descriptionpassing through said locks,at ratesrea-
sonable and just, and not exceeding the rates on the
Kentucky river,and which were in force for passage of
boats, rafts, crafts, and persons through the locks and
dams which were then owned by the State; and the
Board of Railroad Commissioners shall have the right
to fix and regulate the rates of toll, and the same power
to prevent unjust charges and discriminationsupon said
riverthat it has to prevent and correctthe same on the
railroadsof thisCommonwealth ; and itshallbe the duty
of said Commissioners to enforce the provisions of this
section .
§5 . The object of this charter being to increase and
extend the facilitiesof navigation and commerce , and
develop the resources of the country adjacent and contig-
uous to the territorynamed in this act, and ,at the same
time ,to make water -power for the use of the company ,
and for the commerce and advantage of the public,the
said company ,to reward them for theirwork and capital,
are further authorized to lease said water -power , or to
use the same for the purposes of machinery as they may
choose ; and whatever machinery they may so employ
shall be held by the company as a corporation as afore-
said,or they may lease the same , as also theirsaid locks
and dams and franchises,as the company may elect.
§6 . In building the dams herein authorized,it shall
be lawful for said company to make the top thereof suf-
ficientlywide so that wagons and carriages and vehicles
of all kinds may pass and repass over the same ,or in
connection therewith; they may erect bridges for these
purposesacrossthe Cumberland river,and may demand
and receive reasonable tollstherefor,to be fixed by the
county court of the county where the dam is situated;
printedratesto be kept postedat each end of the bridge;
-and saidcompany ,in chargingand collectingtollsherein

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1115
authorized,shallnot discriminateforor against any per-
son or persons or corporations .
§ 7. Said company shallhave power to locate, con-
struct, and operate railroadsor switches, plank roads,
turnpikes, or common roads , or any or either, to and
from any of the works , or over or upon any of the
property to any of itswharves or landings, or to any
railroad now existing, or which may hereafter be con
structed upon , or within the limits of, any real estate
it may acquire by purchase, lease, or donation,as the
necessitiesof the company may require,with such side-
tracks, turn-outs,and depots thereon as may be deemed
necessary .
§ 8. The capital stock of said company shall not ex-
ceed one million and five hundred thousand dollars, and
which shall be divided into shares of one hundred dollars
each , which shall be personal property, and evidenced
and transferred as may be prescribed by the by -laws of
said company . The corporators named in thisact shall
constitutethe firstboard of directors, and shallserve as
such until their successors are elected and qualified as
the by -laws of the company shallprescribe. They , or
a majority of them , shall have power to open books for
subscriptionsto the capital stock of the company , pro-
vide the terms of the subscription, the time and manner
of payment , and the time and manner of forfeitureof
stock subscribed but not paid for as required by the calls;
and they may forfeitthe stock of any defaulting stock-
holder, or collectthe installments by suit,as they may
elect; they may adopt such by -laws for the regulation
and government of said company ,itsofficersand agents ,
as they may deem expedient, and may alteror amend
the same in such manner as they may prescribein said
by -laws . Whenever a sum sufficient,in the judgment of
the corporators named in this act, shall be subscribed,
and such portion thereofpaid as they may require for the
purpose of constructing one or more locks and dams as
herein authorized, the company shallbe deemed fully
organized,and may proceed with itsbusiness. The pay

1116 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ments to the subscriptionsof the capitalstock may be
made in cash , real estate, materials, or machinery fur-
nished, or in labor and services rendered the company ,
in such manner as the board of directorsmay determine ;
and the subscribersto the capital stock of said company
shallnot be liablefor the debts of said company beyond
the unpaid callsmade on their subscriptionto the shares
in the capital stock respectively.
§ 9. The board of directorsshallhave the general man-
agement of allbusiness of said company ,and may exer-
ciseany and allpowers hereby granted to the corporation.
They may increase the capitalstock of the company from
time to time as they may deem expedient ,by new sub-
scriptionsor otherwise,as herein authorized,to the extent
of the limitnamed in the preceding section of this act.
They may from time to time pay dividendsin stock or
cash from the profitsof theirbusiness,and may distrib-
ute the net receiptsand proceedsamong the stockholders
pro rata,or may retainthem ,to be applied in extending
the operationsof the company . They may borrow money
upon the bonds , with semi -annual interest-bearing cou-
pons attached thereto,or other evidences of debt of the
company ,in such sums and payable at such times and
places,and at such rates of interest,not exceeding eight
per cent. per annum , as they may deem expedient , and
may execute mortgages or deeds of trustupon any or all
of the property and franchisesof the company to secure
the payment of such debts as may ,from time to time,be
created .
§ 10. The principalofficeof said company shallbe
eitherin Williamsburg, Whitley county,or in Louisville,
Jeffersoncounty ,as may be determined upon by the board
ofdirectorsin theirby -laws ,and branch offices,agencies ,
or depots may be established at other points within or
out of thisState, as the board of directorsmay ,from time
so time, determine,with the powers to discontinuethe
same whenever , in theirjudgment , the interestof the
company may so require.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1117
§ 11. The meetings of the stockholdersor directorsmay
be calledand heldattheprincipaloffice,or at any branch
-officeor agency of the company within the State. The
board of directorshereafter to be elected by the stock-
holders shall consist of not less than five nor more than
thirteen stockholders, and may be elected in such man-
ner and for such terms as may be prescribed by the by-
laws . The board of directors shall prescribe by the
by -laws the number necessary to constitutea quorum for
the transaction of business for said board ,and they may
fillany vacancies occurring in said board between the
electionsby the stockholdersfor and during the terms as
may be provided by the by -laws in the electionof direct-
ors at the stockholders' meetings for said purpose . The
board of directorsshallhave power to appoint or elect a
president,vice president,secretary, treasurer, and addi-
tional officersand other agents as they may deem expe-
dient, and may fix their salaries,prescribe their duties,
and the time and manner of theiremployment ,and may
require and take from any of them bonds , with sureties,
for the faithfuldischarge of theirrespectiveduties.
§ 12. That the said company may negotiatewith the
owner or owners of the mill-dams situated on said river
above the falls, whose interests are to be injuriously
affected by the removal of the same , and agree with
them upon the amount of damages or amount to be paid
therefor; but if such agreement cannot be made , they
may apply to the clerk of the county court of the county
in which said mill-dam or dams may be , and sue out a
writ of ad quod damnum to condemn said mill-dams and
mill privileges, and have the damages assessed by a fair
and impartialjury as to the property itmay condemn .
§ 13.The officersofsaidcompany may enterupon any
lands contiguous to the Cumberland river, whenever it
shallbe necessary to do so,in prosecuting their surveys.
They may remove any treeson the bank between high
and low water -mark that may be injuriousto the naviga-
tion. Whenever the possessionof any lands or materials
may become necessary for the construction of said work ,

1118 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the said company may purchase the same ; or, in case-
of disagreement as to the value thereof, may summon
a jury to condemn the same in the manner provided
in the statutes. The said company shallbe liablefor
the overflow of any lands caused by the construction of
their works ; and the damages for such overflow shall be
agreed between the owner of such lands and the com-
pany , or may be assessedby a jury summoned in like
manner ,upon the applicationof eitherparty..
§ 14. If any person or persons navigatingthe waters
of the Cumberland river or its tributaries, within the
influenceof the improvements of said company , shall
failor refuseto pay the tollsfixed by the laws as herein
authorized,the person or persons so offendingshallforfeit
and pay to the use of said company three times the
amount of such tolls,to be recovered by civilaction in
the name of said company . If any officerof a vessel
navigating said waters shall knowingly and willfully
present or cause to be presented to the agents of said
company a falsemanifest of his passengers or of the
articlescomposing the cargo of such vessel,with intent
therebytodefraud saidcompany ,he shall,on conviction
thereof, forfeitand pay to the company a sum not ex-
ceeding fivehundred dollars, to be recovered by civil
suit .
§ 15. The corporation shallendure for the term of fifty
years from the date of its organization; but a failure to
commence the work for which it is created within three
years from the passage of this act,or a non -user of the
franchise for two years at any one time ,shalloperate as a
forfeitureof itsfranchise: Provided,however,That thisact
shall cease to be a law if the work isnot begun in good
faithwithin three years from the passage of thisact,and
pushed to a successfulcompletion, giving slack-water
navigation from the firstlock and dam below Williams-
burg,in Whitley county,to Pineville,in Bellcounty; and
the company must put in as many locksand dams as will
be necessaryfor thispurpose, and if they failto do so,
the failingisa forfeitof thischarter.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1119
:
§ 16. Said company ,before proceeding to work , shall
pay over to the county court of Whitley the sum of
twenty -six hundred and sixty-six dollars, and the same
amount to each of the county courts of Knox and Bell
counties, which amount has been expended in clearing
out the obstructionsand improving the navigation of the
river in said counties .
§ 17. Said company shall be responsibleto all persons
fordamage done by reasonof overflowof water from
locks and dams ,or by cutting timber on river bank and
causing banks to wash ; and during the construction of
saidlocksand dams the company shallnot obstructthe
navigation of said river .
§ 18. The Legislaturehereby reserves the rightto alter
or amend this charter .
§ 19. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 24, 1882.
